This file is a merged representation of a subset of the codebase, containing files not matching ignore patterns, combined into a single document by Repomix.
The content has been processed where line numbers have been added.

================================================================
File Summary
================================================================

Purpose:
--------
This file contains a packed representation of a subset of the repository's contents that is considered the most important context.
It is designed to be easily consumable by AI systems for analysis, code review,
or other automated processes.

File Format:
------------
The content is organized as follows:
1. This summary section
2. Repository information
3. Directory structure
4. Repository files (if enabled)
5. Multiple file entries, each consisting of:
  a. A separator line (================)
  b. The file path (File: path/to/file)
  c. Another separator line
  d. The full contents of the file
  e. A blank line

Usage Guidelines:
-----------------
- This file should be treated as read-only. Any changes should be made to the
  original repository files, not this packed version.
- When processing this file, use the file path to distinguish
  between different files in the repository.
- Be aware that this file may contain sensitive information. Handle it with
  the same level of security as you would the original repository.

Notes:
------
- Some files may have been excluded based on .gitignore rules and Repomix's configuration
- Binary files are not included in this packed representation. Please refer to the Repository Structure section for a complete list of file paths, including binary files
- Files matching these patterns are excluded: third-party/**, out/**, dist/**, benchmarks/**, repomix-output.*, *.tar.gz, test/**, docs/chart.svg, .github/**
- Files matching patterns in .gitignore are excluded
- Files matching default ignore patterns are excluded
- Line numbers have been added to the beginning of each line
- Files are sorted by Git change count (files with more changes are at the bottom)

================
File: common/archive-file.h
================
  1: // This file contains functions to read an archive file (.a file).
  2: // An archive file is just a bundle of object files. It's similar to
  3: // tar or zip, but the contents are not compressed.
  4: //
  5: // An archive file is either "regular" or "thin". A regular archive
  6: // contains object files directly, while a thin archive contains only
  7: // pathnames. In the latter case, actual file contents have to be read
  8: // from given pathnames. A regular archive is sometimes called "fat"
  9: // archive as opposed to "thin".
 10: //
 11: // If an archive file is given to the linker, the linker pulls out
 12: // object files that are needed to resolve undefined symbols. So,
 13: // bunding object files as an archive and giving that archive to the
 14: // linker has a different meaning than directly giving the same set of
 15: // object files to the linker. The former links only needed object
 16: // files, while the latter links all the given object files.
 17: //
 18: // Therefore, if you link libc.a for example, not all the libc
 19: // functions are linked to your binary. Instead, only object files
 20: // that provides functions and variables used in your program get
 21: // linked. To make this efficient, static library functions are
 22: // usually separated to each object file in an archive file. You can
 23: // see the contents of libc.a by running `ar t
 24: // /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.a`.
 25: 
 26: #pragma once
 27: 
 28: #include "common.h"
 29: #include "filetype.h"
 30: 
 31: namespace mold {
 32: 
 33: struct ArHdr {
 34:   char ar_name[16];
 35:   char ar_date[12];
 36:   char ar_uid[6];
 37:   char ar_gid[6];
 38:   char ar_mode[8];
 39:   char ar_size[10];
 40:   char ar_fmag[2];
 41: 
 42:   bool starts_with(std::string_view s) const {
 43:     return std::string_view(ar_name, s.size()) == s;
 44:   }
 45: 
 46:   bool is_strtab() const {
 47:     return starts_with("// ");
 48:   }
 49: 
 50:   bool is_symtab() const {
 51:     return starts_with("/ ") || starts_with("/SYM64/ ");
 52:   }
 53: 
 54:   std::string read_name(std::string_view strtab, u8 *&ptr) const {
 55:     // BSD-style long filename
 56:     if (starts_with("#1/")) {
 57:       int namelen = atoi(ar_name + 3);
 58:       std::string name{(char *)ptr, (size_t)namelen};
 59:       ptr += namelen;
 60: 
 61:       if (size_t pos = name.find('\0'))
 62:         name = name.substr(0, pos);
 63:       return name;
 64:     }
 65: 
 66:     // SysV-style long filename
 67:     if (starts_with("/")) {
 68:       const char *start = strtab.data() + atoi(ar_name + 1);
 69:       return {start, (const char *)strstr(start, "/\n")};
 70:     }
 71: 
 72:     // Short fileanme
 73:     if (const char *end = (char *)memchr(ar_name, '/', sizeof(ar_name)))
 74:       return {ar_name, end};
 75:     return {ar_name, sizeof(ar_name)};
 76:   }
 77: };
 78: 
 79: template <typename Context, typename MappedFile>
 80: std::vector<MappedFile *>
 81: read_thin_archive_members(Context &ctx, MappedFile *mf) {
 82:   u8 *begin = mf->data;
 83:   u8 *data = begin + 8;
 84:   std::vector<MappedFile *> vec;
 85:   std::string_view strtab;
 86: 
 87:   while (data < begin + mf->size) {
 88:     // Each header is aligned to a 2 byte boundary.
 89:     if ((begin - data) % 2)
 90:       data++;
 91: 
 92:     ArHdr &hdr = *(ArHdr *)data;
 93:     u8 *body = data + sizeof(hdr);
 94:     u64 size = atol(hdr.ar_size);
 95: 
 96:     // Read a string table.
 97:     if (hdr.is_strtab()) {
 98:       strtab = {(char *)body, (size_t)size};
 99:       data = body + size;
100:       continue;
101:     }
102: 
103:     // Skip a symbol table.
104:     if (hdr.is_symtab()) {
105:       data = body + size;
106:       continue;
107:     }
108: 
109:     if (!hdr.starts_with("#1/") && !hdr.starts_with("/"))
110:       Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": filename is not stored as a long filename";
111: 
112:     std::string name = hdr.read_name(strtab, body);
113: 
114:     // Skip if symbol table
115:     if (name == "__.SYMDEF" || name == "__.SYMDEF SORTED")
116:       continue;
117: 
118:     std::string path = name.starts_with('/') ?
119:       name : (filepath(mf->name).parent_path() / name).string();
120:     vec.push_back(MappedFile::must_open(ctx, path));
121:     vec.back()->thin_parent = mf;
122:     data = body;
123:   }
124:   return vec;
125: }
126: 
127: template <typename Context, typename MappedFile>
128: std::vector<MappedFile *> read_fat_archive_members(Context &ctx, MappedFile *mf) {
129:   u8 *begin = mf->data;
130:   u8 *data = begin + 8;
131:   std::vector<MappedFile *> vec;
132:   std::string_view strtab;
133: 
134:   while (begin + mf->size - data >= 2) {
135:     if ((begin - data) % 2)
136:       data++;
137: 
138:     ArHdr &hdr = *(ArHdr *)data;
139:     u8 *body = data + sizeof(hdr);
140:     u64 size = atol(hdr.ar_size);
141:     data = body + size;
142: 
143:     // Read if string table
144:     if (hdr.is_strtab()) {
145:       strtab = {(char *)body, (size_t)size};
146:       continue;
147:     }
148: 
149:     // Skip if symbol table
150:     if (hdr.is_symtab())
151:       continue;
152: 
153:     // Read the name field
154:     std::string name = hdr.read_name(strtab, body);
155: 
156:     // Skip if symbol table
157:     if (name == "__.SYMDEF" || name == "__.SYMDEF SORTED")
158:       continue;
159: 
160:     vec.push_back(mf->slice(ctx, name, body - begin, data - body));
161:   }
162:   return vec;
163: }
164: 
165: template <typename Context, typename MappedFile>
166: std::vector<MappedFile *> read_archive_members(Context &ctx, MappedFile *mf) {
167:   switch (get_file_type(ctx, mf)) {
168:   case FileType::AR:
169:     return read_fat_archive_members(ctx, mf);
170:   case FileType::THIN_AR:
171:     return read_thin_archive_members(ctx, mf);
172:   default:
173:     unreachable();
174:   }
175: }
176: 
177: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/cmdline.h
================
 1: #pragma once
 2: 
 3: #include "common.h"
 4: 
 5: namespace mold {
 6: 
 7: template <typename Context>
 8: std::vector<std::string_view>
 9: read_response_file(Context &ctx, std::string_view path) {
10:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
11:   MappedFile<Context> *mf = MappedFile<Context>::must_open(ctx, std::string(path));
12:   u8 *data = mf->data;
13: 
14:   auto read_quoted = [&](i64 i, char quote) {
15:     std::string buf;
16:     while (i < mf->size && data[i] != quote) {
17:       if (data[i] == '\\') {
18:         buf.append(1, data[i + 1]);
19:         i += 2;
20:       } else {
21:         buf.append(1, data[i++]);
22:       }
23:     }
24:     if (i >= mf->size)
25:       Fatal(ctx) << path << ": premature end of input";
26:     vec.push_back(save_string(ctx, buf));
27:     return i + 1;
28:   };
29: 
30:   auto read_unquoted = [&](i64 i) {
31:     std::string buf;
32: 
33:     while (i < mf->size) {
34:       if (data[i] == '\\' && i + 1 < mf->size) {
35:         buf.append(1, data[i + 1]);
36:         i += 2;
37:         continue;
38:       }
39: 
40:       if (!isspace(data[i])) {
41:         buf.append(1, data[i++]);
42:         continue;
43:       }
44: 
45:       break;
46:     }
47: 
48:     vec.push_back(save_string(ctx, buf));
49:     return i;
50:   };
51: 
52:   for (i64 i = 0; i < mf->size;) {
53:     if (isspace(data[i]))
54:       i++;
55:     else if (data[i] == '\'')
56:       i = read_quoted(i + 1, '\'');
57:     else if (data[i] == '\"')
58:       i = read_quoted(i + 1, '\"');
59:     else
60:       i = read_unquoted(i);
61:   }
62:   return vec;
63: }
64: 
65: // Replace "@path/to/some/text/file" with its file contents.
66: template <typename Context>
67: std::vector<std::string_view> expand_response_files(Context &ctx, char **argv) {
68:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
69:   for (i64 i = 0; argv[i]; i++) {
70:     if (argv[i][0] == '@')
71:       append(vec, read_response_file(ctx, argv[i] + 1));
72:     else
73:       vec.push_back(argv[i]);
74:   }
75:   return vec;
76: }
77: 
78: static inline std::string_view string_trim(std::string_view str) {
79:   size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(" \t");
80:   if (pos == str.npos)
81:     return "";
82:   str = str.substr(pos);
83: 
84:   pos = str.find_last_not_of(" \t");
85:   if (pos == str.npos)
86:     return str;
87:   return str.substr(0, pos + 1);
88: }
89: 
90: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/compress.cc
================
  1: // This file implements a multi-threaded zlib and zstd compression
  2: // routine.
  3: //
  4: // zlib-compressed data can be merged just by concatenation as long as
  5: // each piece of data is flushed with Z_SYNC_FLUSH. In this file, we
  6: // split input data into multiple shards, compress them individually
  7: // and concatenate them. We then append a header, a trailer and a
  8: // checksum so that the concatenated data is valid zlib-format data.
  9: //
 10: // zstd-compressed data can be merged in the same way.
 11: //
 12: // Using threads to compress data has a downside. Since the dictionary
 13: // is reset on boundaries of shards, compression ratio is sacrificed
 14: // a little bit. However, if a shard size is large enough, that loss
 15: // is negligible in practice.
 16: 
 17: #include "common.h"
 18: 
 19: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 20: #include <zlib.h>
 21: #include <zstd.h>
 22: 
 23: #define CHECK(fn)                               \
 24:   do {                                          \
 25:     [[maybe_unused]] int r = (fn);              \
 26:     assert(r == Z_OK);                          \
 27:   } while (0)
 28: 
 29: namespace mold {
 30: 
 31: static constexpr i64 SHARD_SIZE = 1024 * 1024;
 32: 
 33: static std::vector<std::string_view> split(std::string_view input) {
 34:   std::vector<std::string_view> shards;
 35: 
 36:   while (input.size() >= SHARD_SIZE) {
 37:     shards.push_back(input.substr(0, SHARD_SIZE));
 38:     input = input.substr(SHARD_SIZE);
 39:   }
 40:   if (!input.empty())
 41:     shards.push_back(input);
 42:   return shards;
 43: }
 44: 
 45: static std::vector<u8> zlib_compress(std::string_view input) {
 46:   // Initialize zlib stream. Since debug info is generally compressed
 47:   // pretty well with lower compression levels, we chose compression
 48:   // level 1.
 49:   z_stream strm;
 50:   strm.zalloc = Z_NULL;
 51:   strm.zfree = Z_NULL;
 52:   strm.opaque = Z_NULL;
 53: 
 54:   CHECK(deflateInit2(&strm, 1, Z_DEFLATED, -15, 8, Z_DEFAULT_STRATEGY));
 55: 
 56:   // Set an input buffer
 57:   strm.avail_in = input.size();
 58:   strm.next_in = (u8 *)input.data();
 59: 
 60:   // Set an output buffer. deflateBound() returns an upper bound
 61:   // on the compression size. +16 for Z_SYNC_FLUSH.
 62:   std::vector<u8> buf(deflateBound(&strm, strm.avail_in) + 16);
 63: 
 64:   // Compress data. It writes all compressed bytes except the last
 65:   // partial byte, so up to 7 bits can be held to be written to the
 66:   // buffer.
 67:   strm.avail_out = buf.size();
 68:   strm.next_out = buf.data();
 69:   CHECK(deflate(&strm, Z_BLOCK));
 70: 
 71:   // This is a workaround for libbacktrace before 2022-04-06.
 72:   //
 73:   // Zlib is a bit stream, and what Z_SYNC_FLUSH does is to write a
 74:   // three bit value indicating the start of an uncompressed data
 75:   // block followed by four byte data 00 00 ff ff which indicates that
 76:   // the length of the block is zero. libbacktrace uses its own zlib
 77:   // inflate routine, and it had a bug that if that particular three
 78:   // bit value happens to end at a byte boundary, it accidentally
 79:   // skipped the next byte.
 80:   //
 81:   // In order to avoid triggering that bug, we should avoid calling
 82:   // deflate() with Z_SYNC_FLUSH if the current bit position is 5.
 83:   // If it's 5, we insert an empty block consisting of 10 bits so
 84:   // that the bit position is 7 in the next byte.
 85:   //
 86:   // https://github.com/ianlancetaylor/libbacktrace/pull/87
 87:   int nbits;
 88:   deflatePending(&strm, Z_NULL, &nbits);
 89:   if (nbits == 5)
 90:     CHECK(deflatePrime(&strm, 10, 2));
 91:   CHECK(deflate(&strm, Z_SYNC_FLUSH));
 92: 
 93:   assert(strm.avail_out > 0);
 94:   buf.resize(buf.size() - strm.avail_out);
 95:   buf.shrink_to_fit();
 96:   deflateEnd(&strm);
 97:   return buf;
 98: }
 99: 
100: ZlibCompressor::ZlibCompressor(u8 *buf, i64 size) {
101:   std::string_view input{(char *)buf, (size_t)size};
102:   std::vector<std::string_view> inputs = split(input);
103:   std::vector<u64> adlers(inputs.size());
104:   shards.resize(inputs.size());
105: 
106:   // Compress each shard
107:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)inputs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
108:     adlers[i] = adler32(1, (u8 *)inputs[i].data(), inputs[i].size());
109:     shards[i] = zlib_compress(inputs[i]);
110:   });
111: 
112:   // Combine checksums
113:   checksum = adlers[0];
114:   for (i64 i = 1; i < inputs.size(); i++)
115:     checksum = adler32_combine(checksum, adlers[i], inputs[i].size());
116: 
117:   // Comput the total size
118:   compressed_size = 8; // the header and the trailer
119:   for (std::vector<u8> &shard : shards)
120:     compressed_size += shard.size();
121: }
122: 
123: void ZlibCompressor::write_to(u8 *buf) {
124:   // Write a zlib-format header
125:   buf[0] = 0x78;
126:   buf[1] = 0x9c;
127: 
128:   // Copy compressed data
129:   std::vector<i64> offsets(shards.size());
130:   offsets[0] = 2; // +2 for header
131:   for (i64 i = 1; i < shards.size(); i++)
132:     offsets[i] = offsets[i - 1] + shards[i - 1].size();
133: 
134:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)shards.size(), [&](i64 i) {
135:     memcpy(&buf[offsets[i]], shards[i].data(), shards[i].size());
136:   });
137: 
138:   // Write a trailer
139:   u8 *end = buf + compressed_size;
140:   end[-6] = 3;
141:   end[-5] = 0;
142: 
143:   // Write a checksum
144:   *(ub32 *)(end - 4) = checksum;
145: }
146: 
147: static std::vector<u8> zstd_compress(std::string_view input) {
148:   std::vector<u8> buf(ZSTD_COMPRESSBOUND(input.size()));
149:   constexpr int level = 3; // compression level; must be between 1 to 22
150: 
151:   size_t sz = ZSTD_compress(buf.data(), buf.size(), input.data(), input.size(),
152:                             level);
153:   assert(!ZSTD_isError(sz));
154:   buf.resize(sz);
155:   buf.shrink_to_fit();
156:   return buf;
157: }
158: 
159: ZstdCompressor::ZstdCompressor(u8 *buf, i64 size) {
160:   std::string_view input{(char *)buf, (size_t)size};
161:   std::vector<std::string_view> inputs = split(input);
162:   shards.resize(inputs.size());
163: 
164:   // Compress each shard
165:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)inputs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
166:     shards[i] = zstd_compress(inputs[i]);
167:   });
168: 
169:   compressed_size = 0;
170:   for (std::vector<u8> &shard : shards)
171:     compressed_size += shard.size();
172: }
173: 
174: void ZstdCompressor::write_to(u8 *buf) {
175:   // Copy compressed data
176:   std::vector<i64> offsets(shards.size());
177:   for (i64 i = 1; i < shards.size(); i++)
178:     offsets[i] = offsets[i - 1] + shards[i - 1].size();
179: 
180:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)shards.size(), [&](i64 i) {
181:     memcpy(&buf[offsets[i]], shards[i].data(), shards[i].size());
182:   });
183: }
184: 
185: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/demangle.cc
================
 1: #include "common.h"
 2: 
 3: #include <cstdlib>
 4: 
 5: #ifndef _WIN32
 6: #include <cxxabi.h>
 7: #endif
 8: 
 9: #include "../third-party/rust-demangle/rust-demangle.h"
10: 
11: namespace mold {
12: 
13: std::string_view demangle(std::string_view name) {
14:   static thread_local char *p;
15:   if (p)
16:     free(p);
17: 
18:   // Try to demangle as a Rust symbol. Since legacy-style Rust symbols
19:   // are also valid as a C++ mangled name, we need to call this before
20:   // cpp_demangle.
21:   p = rust_demangle(std::string(name).c_str(), 0);
22:   if (p)
23:     return p;
24: 
25:   // Try to demangle as a C++ symbol.
26:   if (std::optional<std::string_view> s = cpp_demangle(name))
27:     return *s;
28:   return name;
29: }
30: 
31: std::optional<std::string_view> cpp_demangle(std::string_view name) {
32:   static thread_local char *buf;
33:   static thread_local size_t buflen;
34: 
35:   // TODO(cwasser): Actually demangle Symbols on Windows using e.g.
36:   // `UnDecorateSymbolName` from Dbghelp, maybe even Itanium symbols?
37: #ifndef _WIN32
38:   if (name.starts_with("_Z")) {
39:     int status;
40:     char *p = abi::__cxa_demangle(std::string(name).c_str(), buf, &buflen, &status);
41:     if (status == 0) {
42:       buf = p;
43:       return p;
44:     }
45:   }
46: #endif
47: 
48:   return {};
49: }
50: 
51: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/Dockerfile
================
 1: # This dockerfile creates a reproducible build environment for mold.
 2: #
 3: # $ docker buildx create --use
 4: # $ docker buildx build --platform x86_64,aarch64,arm,ppc64le,s390x -t rui314/mold-builder:latest --push .
 5: 
 6: FROM ubuntu:18.04
 7: ENV TZ=Europe/London
 8: RUN apt-get update && \
 9:   apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends software-properties-common && \
10:   add-apt-repository -y ppa:ubuntu-toolchain-r/test && \
11:   apt-get update && \
12:   apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential wget libstdc++-11-dev zlib1g-dev gcc-10 g++-10 python3 && \
13:   \
14:   mkdir /openssl && cd /openssl && \
15:   wget -O- -q https://www.openssl.org/source/openssl-3.0.7.tar.gz | tar --strip-components=1 -xzf - && \
16:   ./Configure --prefix=/usr/local --libdir=lib && \
17:   make -j$(nproc) && \
18:   make -j$(nproc) install && \
19:   ldconfig && \
20:   mkdir /cmake && cd /cmake && \
21:   wget -O- -q https://github.com/Kitware/CMake/releases/download/v3.24.2/cmake-3.24.2.tar.gz | tar --strip-components=1 -xzf - && \
22:   ./bootstrap --parallel=$(nproc) && make -j$(nproc) && make -j$(nproc) install && \
23:   rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /cmake /openssl

================
File: common/filepath.cc
================
 1: #include "common.h"
 2: 
 3: #include <filesystem>
 4: #include <sys/stat.h>
 5: 
 6: namespace mold {
 7: 
 8: std::string get_realpath(std::string_view path) {
 9:   std::error_code ec;
10:   std::filesystem::path link = std::filesystem::read_symlink(path, ec);
11:   if (ec)
12:     return std::string(path);
13:   return (filepath(path) / ".." / link).lexically_normal().string();
14: }
15: 
16: // Removes redundant '/..' or '/.' from a given path.
17: // The transformation is done purely by lexical processing.
18: // This function does not access file system.
19: std::string path_clean(std::string_view path) {
20:   return filepath(path).lexically_normal().string();
21: }
22: 
23: std::filesystem::path to_abs_path(std::filesystem::path path) {
24:   if (path.is_absolute())
25:     return path.lexically_normal();
26:   return (std::filesystem::current_path() / path).lexically_normal();
27: }
28: 
29: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/filetype.h
================
  1: #pragma once
  2: 
  3: #include "common.h"
  4: #include "../elf/elf.h"
  5: 
  6: namespace mold {
  7: 
  8: enum class FileType {
  9:   UNKNOWN,
 10:   EMPTY,
 11:   ELF_OBJ,
 12:   ELF_DSO,
 13:   MACH_OBJ,
 14:   MACH_EXE,
 15:   MACH_DYLIB,
 16:   MACH_BUNDLE,
 17:   MACH_UNIVERSAL,
 18:   AR,
 19:   THIN_AR,
 20:   TAPI,
 21:   TEXT,
 22:   GCC_LTO_OBJ,
 23:   LLVM_BITCODE,
 24: };
 25: 
 26: template <typename MappedFile>
 27: bool is_text_file(MappedFile *mf) {
 28:   u8 *data = mf->data;
 29:   return mf->size >= 4 && isprint(data[0]) && isprint(data[1]) &&
 30:          isprint(data[2]) && isprint(data[3]);
 31: }
 32: 
 33: template <typename E, typename Context, typename MappedFile>
 34: inline bool is_gcc_lto_obj(Context &ctx, MappedFile *mf) {
 35:   using namespace mold::elf;
 36: 
 37:   const char *data = mf->get_contents().data();
 38:   ElfEhdr<E> &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<E> *)data;
 39:   ElfShdr<E> *sh_begin = (ElfShdr<E> *)(data + ehdr.e_shoff);
 40:   std::span<ElfShdr<E>> shdrs{(ElfShdr<E> *)(data + ehdr.e_shoff), ehdr.e_shnum};
 41: 
 42:   // e_shstrndx is a 16-bit field. If .shstrtab's section index is
 43:   // too large, the actual number is stored to sh_link field.
 44:   i64 shstrtab_idx = (ehdr.e_shstrndx == SHN_XINDEX)
 45:     ? sh_begin->sh_link : ehdr.e_shstrndx;
 46: 
 47:   for (ElfShdr<E> &sec : shdrs) {
 48:     // GCC FAT LTO objects contain both regular ELF sections and GCC-
 49:     // specific LTO sections, so that they can be linked as LTO objects if
 50:     // the LTO linker plugin is available and falls back as regular
 51:     // objects otherwise. GCC FAT LTO object can be identified by the
 52:     // presence of `.gcc.lto_.symtab` section.
 53:     if (!ctx.arg.plugin.empty()) {
 54:       std::string_view name = data + shdrs[shstrtab_idx].sh_offset + sec.sh_name;
 55:       if (name.starts_with(".gnu.lto_.symtab."))
 56:       return true;
 57:     }
 58: 
 59:     if (sec.sh_type != SHT_SYMTAB)
 60:       continue;
 61: 
 62:     // GCC non-FAT LTO object contains only sections symbols followed by
 63:     // a common symbol whose name is `__gnu_lto_slim` (or `__gnu_lto_v1`
 64:     // for older GCC releases).
 65:     std::span<ElfSym<E>> elf_syms{(ElfSym<E> *)(data + sec.sh_offset),
 66:                                   (size_t)sec.sh_size / sizeof(ElfSym<E>)};
 67: 
 68:     auto skip = [](u8 type) {
 69:       return type == STT_NOTYPE || type == STT_FILE || type == STT_SECTION;
 70:     };
 71: 
 72:     i64 i = 1;
 73:     while (i < elf_syms.size() && skip(elf_syms[i].st_type))
 74:       i++;
 75: 
 76:     if (i < elf_syms.size() && elf_syms[i].st_shndx == SHN_COMMON) {
 77:       std::string_view name =
 78:         data + shdrs[sec.sh_link].sh_offset + elf_syms[i].st_name;
 79:       if (name.starts_with("__gnu_lto_"))
 80:         return true;
 81:     }
 82:     break;
 83:   }
 84: 
 85:   return false;
 86: }
 87: 
 88: template <typename Context, typename MappedFile>
 89: FileType get_file_type(Context &ctx, MappedFile *mf) {
 90:   using namespace elf;
 91: 
 92:   std::string_view data = mf->get_contents();
 93: 
 94:   if (data.empty())
 95:     return FileType::EMPTY;
 96: 
 97:   if (data.starts_with("\177ELF")) {
 98:     u8 byte_order = ((ElfEhdr<I386> *)data.data())->e_ident[EI_DATA];
 99: 
100:     if (byte_order == ELFDATA2LSB) {
101:       auto &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<I386> *)data.data();
102: 
103:       if (ehdr.e_type == ET_REL) {
104:         if (ehdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] == ELFCLASS32) {
105:           if (is_gcc_lto_obj<I386>(ctx, mf))
106:             return FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ;
107:         } else {
108:           if (is_gcc_lto_obj<X86_64>(ctx, mf))
109:             return FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ;
110:         }
111:         return FileType::ELF_OBJ;
112:       }
113: 
114:       if (ehdr.e_type == ET_DYN)
115:         return FileType::ELF_DSO;
116:     } else {
117:       auto &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<M68K> *)data.data();
118: 
119:       if (ehdr.e_type == ET_REL) {
120:         if (ehdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] == ELFCLASS32) {
121:           if (is_gcc_lto_obj<M68K>(ctx, mf))
122:             return FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ;
123:         } else {
124:           if (is_gcc_lto_obj<SPARC64>(ctx, mf))
125:             return FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ;
126:         }
127:         return FileType::ELF_OBJ;
128:       }
129: 
130:       if (ehdr.e_type == ET_DYN)
131:         return FileType::ELF_DSO;
132:     }
133:     return FileType::UNKNOWN;
134:   }
135: 
136:   if (data.starts_with("\xcf\xfa\xed\xfe")) {
137:     switch (*(ul32 *)(data.data() + 12)) {
138:     case 1: // MH_OBJECT
139:       return FileType::MACH_OBJ;
140:     case 2: // MH_EXECUTE
141:       return FileType::MACH_EXE;
142:     case 6: // MH_DYLIB
143:       return FileType::MACH_DYLIB;
144:     case 8: // MH_BUNDLE
145:       return FileType::MACH_BUNDLE;
146:     }
147:     return FileType::UNKNOWN;
148:   }
149: 
150:   if (data.starts_with("!<arch>\n"))
151:     return FileType::AR;
152:   if (data.starts_with("!<thin>\n"))
153:     return FileType::THIN_AR;
154:   if (data.starts_with("--- !tapi-tbd"))
155:     return FileType::TAPI;
156:   if (data.starts_with("\xca\xfe\xba\xbe"))
157:     return FileType::MACH_UNIVERSAL;
158:   if (is_text_file(mf))
159:     return FileType::TEXT;
160:   if (data.starts_with("\xde\xc0\x17\x0b"))
161:     return FileType::LLVM_BITCODE;
162:   if (data.starts_with("BC\xc0\xde"))
163:     return FileType::LLVM_BITCODE;
164:   return FileType::UNKNOWN;
165: }
166: 
167: inline std::string filetype_to_string(FileType type) {
168:   switch (type) {
169:   case FileType::UNKNOWN: return "UNKNOWN";
170:   case FileType::EMPTY: return "EMPTY";
171:   case FileType::ELF_OBJ: return "ELF_OBJ";
172:   case FileType::ELF_DSO: return "ELF_DSO";
173:   case FileType::MACH_EXE: return "MACH_EXE";
174:   case FileType::MACH_OBJ: return "MACH_OBJ";
175:   case FileType::MACH_DYLIB: return "MACH_DYLIB";
176:   case FileType::MACH_BUNDLE: return "MACH_BUNDLE";
177:   case FileType::MACH_UNIVERSAL: return "MACH_UNIVERSAL";
178:   case FileType::AR: return "AR";
179:   case FileType::THIN_AR: return "THIN_AR";
180:   case FileType::TAPI: return "TAPI";
181:   case FileType::TEXT: return "TEXT";
182:   case FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ: return "GCC_LTO_OBJ";
183:   case FileType::LLVM_BITCODE: return "LLVM_BITCODE";
184:   }
185:   return "UNKNOWN";
186: }
187: 
188: inline std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, FileType type) {
189:   out << filetype_to_string(type);
190:   return out;
191: }
192: 
193: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/glob.cc
================
  1: #include "common.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <cstring>
  4: 
  5: namespace mold {
  6: 
  7: std::optional<Glob> Glob::compile(std::string_view pat) {
  8:   std::vector<Element> vec;
  9: 
 10:   while (!pat.empty()) {
 11:     u8 c = pat[0];
 12:     pat = pat.substr(1);
 13: 
 14:     switch (c) {
 15:     case '[': {
 16:       // Here are a few bracket pattern examples:
 17:       //
 18:       // [abc]: a, b or c
 19:       // [$\]!]: $, ] or !
 20:       // [a-czg-i]: a, b, c, z, g, h, or i
 21:       // [^a-z]: Any character except lowercase letters
 22:       vec.push_back({BRACKET});
 23:       std::bitset<256> &bitset = vec.back().bitset;
 24: 
 25:       bool negate = false;
 26:       if (!pat.empty() && pat[0] == '^') {
 27:         negate = true;
 28:         pat = pat.substr(1);
 29:       }
 30: 
 31:       bool closed = false;
 32: 
 33:       while (!pat.empty()) {
 34:         if (pat[0] == ']') {
 35:           pat = pat.substr(1);
 36:           closed = true;
 37:           break;
 38:         }
 39: 
 40:         if (pat[0] == '\\') {
 41:           pat = pat.substr(1);
 42:           if (pat.empty())
 43:             return {};
 44:         }
 45: 
 46:         if (pat.size() >= 3 && pat[1] == '-') {
 47:           u8 start = pat[0];
 48:           u8 end = pat[2];
 49:           pat = pat.substr(3);
 50: 
 51:           if (end == '\\') {
 52:             if (pat.empty())
 53:               return {};
 54:             end = pat[0];
 55:             pat = pat.substr(1);
 56:           }
 57: 
 58:           if (end < start)
 59:             return {};
 60: 
 61:           for (i64 i = start; i <= end; i++)
 62:             bitset[i] = true;
 63:         } else {
 64:           bitset[(u8)pat[0]] = true;
 65:           pat = pat.substr(1);
 66:         }
 67:       }
 68: 
 69:       if (!closed)
 70:         return {};
 71: 
 72:       if (negate)
 73:         bitset.flip();
 74:       break;
 75:     }
 76:     case '?':
 77:       vec.push_back({QUESTION});
 78:       break;
 79:     case '*':
 80:       vec.push_back({STAR});
 81:       break;
 82:     default:
 83:       if (vec.empty() || vec.back().kind != STRING)
 84:         vec.push_back({STRING});
 85:       vec.back().str += c;
 86:       break;
 87:     }
 88:   }
 89: 
 90:   return {Glob{std::move(vec)}};
 91: }
 92: 
 93: bool Glob::match(std::string_view str) {
 94:   return do_match(str, elements);
 95: }
 96: 
 97: bool Glob::do_match(std::string_view str, std::span<Element> elements) {
 98:   while (!elements.empty()) {
 99:     Element &e = elements[0];
100:     elements = elements.subspan(1);
101: 
102:     switch (e.kind) {
103:     case STRING:
104:       if (str.empty() || !str.starts_with(e.str))
105:         return false;
106:       str = str.substr(e.str.size());
107:       break;
108:     case STAR:
109:       if (elements.empty())
110:         return true;
111: 
112:       // Patterns like "*foo*bar*" should be much more common than more
113:       // complex ones like "*foo*[abc]*" or "*foo**?bar*", so we optimize
114:       // the former case here.
115:       if (elements[0].kind == STRING) {
116:         for (;;) {
117:           size_t pos = str.find(elements[0].str);
118:           if (pos == str.npos)
119:             break;
120:           if (do_match(str.substr(pos + elements[0].str.size()),
121:                        elements.subspan(1)))
122:             return true;
123:           str = str.substr(pos + 1);
124:         }
125:         return false;
126:       }
127: 
128:       // Other cases are handled here.
129:       for (i64 j = 0; j < str.size(); j++)
130:         if (do_match(str.substr(j), elements))
131:           return true;
132:       return false;
133:     case QUESTION:
134:       if (str.empty())
135:         return false;
136:       str = str.substr(1);
137:       break;
138:     case BRACKET:
139:       if (str.empty() || !e.bitset[str[0]])
140:         return false;
141:       str = str.substr(1);
142:       break;
143:     }
144:   }
145: 
146:   return str.empty();
147: }
148: 
149: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/hyperloglog.cc
================
 1: // This file implements HyperLogLog algorithm, which estimates
 2: // the number of unique items in a given multiset.
 3: //
 4: // For more info, read
 5: // https://engineering.fb.com/2018/12/13/data-infrastructure/hyperloglog
 6: 
 7: #include "common.h"
 8: 
 9: #include <cmath>
10: 
11: namespace mold {
12: 
13: i64 HyperLogLog::get_cardinality() const {
14:   double z = 0;
15:   for (i64 val : buckets)
16:     z += pow(2, -val);
17:   return ALPHA * NBUCKETS * NBUCKETS / z;
18: }
19: 
20: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/integers.h
================
  1: // This file defines integral types for file input/output. We need to use
  2: // these types instead of the plain integers (such as uint32_t or int32_t)
  3: // when reading from/writing to an mmap'ed file area for the following
  4: // reasons:
  5: //
  6: // 1. mold is always a cross linker and should not depend on what host it
  7: //    is running on. Users should be able to run mold on a big-endian
  8: //    SPARC machine to create a little-endian RV64 binary, for example.
  9: //
 10: // 2. Even though data members in all ELF data strucutres are naturally
 11: //    aligned, they are not guaranteed to be aligned on memory. Because
 12: //    archive file (.a file) aligns each member only to a 2 byte boundary,
 13: //    anything larger than 2 bytes may be unaligned in an mmap'ed memory.
 14: //    Unaligned access is an undefined behavior in C/C++, so we shouldn't
 15: //    cast an arbitrary pointer to a uint32_t, for example, to read a
 16: //    32-bits value.
 17: //
 18: // The data types defined in this file don't depend on host byte order and
 19: // don't do unaligned access.
 20: 
 21: #pragma once
 22: 
 23: #include <bit>
 24: #include <cstdint>
 25: #include <cstring>
 26: 
 27: #if !defined(__LITTLE_ENDIAN__) && !defined(__BIG_ENDIAN__)
 28: # if __BYTE_ORDER__ == __ORDER_LITTLE_ENDIAN__
 29: #  define __LITTLE_ENDIAN__ 1
 30: # elif __BYTE_ORDER__ == __ORDER_BIG_ENDIAN__
 31: #  define __BIG_ENDIAN__ 1
 32: # else
 33: #  error "unknown host byte order"
 34: # endif
 35: #endif
 36: 
 37: namespace mold {
 38: 
 39: typedef uint8_t u8;
 40: typedef uint16_t u16;
 41: typedef uint32_t u32;
 42: typedef uint64_t u64;
 43: 
 44: typedef int8_t i8;
 45: typedef int16_t i16;
 46: typedef int32_t i32;
 47: typedef int64_t i64;
 48: 
 49: template <typename T>
 50: static inline T bswap(T val) {
 51:   switch (sizeof(T)) {
 52:   case 2:  return __builtin_bswap16(val);
 53:   case 4:  return __builtin_bswap32(val);
 54:   case 8:  return __builtin_bswap64(val);
 55:   default: __builtin_unreachable();
 56:   }
 57: }
 58: 
 59: template <typename T, int SIZE = sizeof(T)>
 60: class LittleEndian {
 61: public:
 62:   LittleEndian() = default;
 63:   LittleEndian(T x) { *this = x; }
 64: 
 65:   operator T() const {
 66:     if constexpr (sizeof(T) == SIZE) {
 67:       T x;
 68:       memcpy(&x, val, sizeof(T));
 69:       if constexpr (std::endian::native == std::endian::big)
 70:         x = bswap(x);
 71:       return x;
 72:     } else {
 73:       static_assert(SIZE == 3);
 74:       return (val[2] << 16) | (val[1] << 8) | val[0];
 75:     }
 76:   }
 77: 
 78:   LittleEndian &operator=(T x) {
 79:     if constexpr (sizeof(T) == SIZE) {
 80:       if constexpr (std::endian::native == std::endian::big)
 81:         x = bswap(x);
 82:       memcpy(val, &x, sizeof(T));
 83:     } else {
 84:       static_assert(SIZE == 3);
 85:       val[2] = x >> 16;
 86:       val[1] = x >> 8;
 87:       val[0] = x;
 88:     }
 89:     return *this;
 90:   }
 91: 
 92:   LittleEndian &operator++() {
 93:     return *this = *this + 1;
 94:   }
 95: 
 96:   LittleEndian operator++(int) {
 97:     T ret = *this;
 98:     *this = *this + 1;
 99:     return ret;
100:   }
101: 
102:   LittleEndian &operator--() {
103:     return *this = *this - 1;
104:   }
105: 
106:   LittleEndian operator--(int) {
107:     T ret = *this;
108:     *this = *this - 1;
109:     return ret;
110:   }
111: 
112:   LittleEndian &operator+=(T x) {
113:     return *this = *this + x;
114:   }
115: 
116:   LittleEndian &operator-=(T x) {
117:     return *this = *this - x;
118:   }
119: 
120:   LittleEndian &operator&=(T x) {
121:     return *this = *this & x;
122:   }
123: 
124:   LittleEndian &operator|=(T x) {
125:     return *this = *this | x;
126:   }
127: 
128: private:
129:   u8 val[SIZE];
130: };
131: 
132: using il16 = LittleEndian<i16>;
133: using il32 = LittleEndian<i32>;
134: using il64 = LittleEndian<i64>;
135: using ul16 = LittleEndian<u16>;
136: using ul24 = LittleEndian<u32, 3>;
137: using ul32 = LittleEndian<u32>;
138: using ul64 = LittleEndian<u64>;
139: 
140: template <typename T, int SIZE = sizeof(T)>
141: class BigEndian {
142: public:
143:   BigEndian() = default;
144:   BigEndian(T x) { *this = x; }
145: 
146:   operator T() const {
147:     if constexpr (sizeof(T) == SIZE) {
148:       T x;
149:       memcpy(&x, val, sizeof(T));
150:       if constexpr (std::endian::native == std::endian::little)
151:         x = bswap(x);
152:       return x;
153:     } else {
154:       static_assert(SIZE == 3);
155:       return (val[0] << 16) | (val[1] << 8) | val[2];
156:     }
157:   }
158: 
159:   BigEndian &operator=(T x) {
160:     if constexpr (sizeof(T) == SIZE) {
161:       if constexpr (std::endian::native == std::endian::little)
162:         x = bswap(x);
163:       memcpy(val, &x, sizeof(T));
164:     } else {
165:       static_assert(SIZE == 3);
166:       val[0] = x >> 16;
167:       val[1] = x >> 8;
168:       val[2] = x;
169:     }
170:     return *this;
171:   }
172: 
173:   BigEndian &operator++() {
174:     return *this = *this + 1;
175:   }
176: 
177:   BigEndian operator++(int) {
178:     T ret = *this;
179:     *this = *this + 1;
180:     return ret;
181:   }
182: 
183:   BigEndian &operator--() {
184:     return *this = *this - 1;
185:   }
186: 
187:   BigEndian operator--(int) {
188:     T ret = *this;
189:     *this = *this - 1;
190:     return ret;
191:   }
192: 
193:   BigEndian &operator+=(T x) {
194:     return *this = *this + x;
195:   }
196: 
197:   BigEndian &operator-=(T x) {
198:     return *this = *this - x;
199:   }
200: 
201:   BigEndian &operator&=(T x) {
202:     return *this = *this & x;
203:   }
204: 
205:   BigEndian &operator|=(T x) {
206:     return *this = *this | x;
207:   }
208: 
209: private:
210:   u8 val[SIZE];
211: };
212: 
213: using ib16 = BigEndian<i16>;
214: using ib32 = BigEndian<i32>;
215: using ib64 = BigEndian<i64>;
216: using ub16 = BigEndian<u16>;
217: using ub24 = BigEndian<u32, 3>;
218: using ub32 = BigEndian<u32>;
219: using ub64 = BigEndian<u64>;
220: 
221: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/main.cc
================
  1: #include "common.h"
  2: #include "config.h"
  3: 
  4: #include <cstring>
  5: #include <filesystem>
  6: #include <signal.h>
  7: #include <tbb/global_control.h>
  8: 
  9: #ifdef USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC
 10: #include <mimalloc-new-delete.h>
 11: #endif
 12: 
 13: #ifdef __FreeBSD__
 14: # include <sys/sysctl.h>
 15: # include <unistd.h>
 16: #endif
 17: 
 18: namespace mold {
 19: 
 20: std::string mold_version_string = MOLD_VERSION;
 21: 
 22: namespace elf {
 23: int main(int argc, char **argv);
 24: }
 25: 
 26: namespace macho {
 27: int main(int argc, char **argv);
 28: }
 29: 
 30: static std::string get_mold_version() {
 31:   std::string name = MOLD_IS_SOLD ? "mold (sold) " : "mold ";
 32:   if (mold_git_hash.empty())
 33:     return name + MOLD_VERSION + " (compatible with GNU ld)";
 34:   return name + MOLD_VERSION + " (" + mold_git_hash + "; compatible with GNU ld)";
 35: }
 36: 
 37: void cleanup() {
 38:   if (output_tmpfile)
 39:     unlink(output_tmpfile);
 40: }
 41: 
 42: std::string errno_string() {
 43:   // strerror is not thread-safe, so guard it with a lock.
 44:   static std::mutex mu;
 45:   std::scoped_lock lock(mu);
 46:   return strerror(errno);
 47: }
 48: 
 49: // Returns the path of the mold executable itself
 50: std::string get_self_path() {
 51: #ifdef __FreeBSD__
 52:   // /proc may not be mounted on FreeBSD. The proper way to get the
 53:   // current executable's path is to use sysctl(2).
 54:   int mib[4];
 55:   mib[0] = CTL_KERN;
 56:   mib[1] = KERN_PROC;
 57:   mib[2] = KERN_PROC_PATHNAME;
 58:   mib[3] = -1;
 59: 
 60:   size_t size;
 61:   sysctl(mib, 4, NULL, &size, NULL, 0);
 62: 
 63:   std::string path;
 64:   path.resize(size);
 65:   sysctl(mib, 4, path.data(), &size, NULL, 0);
 66:   return path;
 67: #else
 68:   return std::filesystem::read_symlink("/proc/self/exe").string();
 69: #endif
 70: }
 71: 
 72: // mold mmap's an output file, and the mmap succeeds even if there's
 73: // no enough space left on the filesystem. The actual disk blocks are
 74: // not allocated on the mmap call but when the program writes to it
 75: // for the first time.
 76: //
 77: // If a disk becomes full as a result of a write to an mmap'ed memory
 78: // region, the failure of the write is reported as a SIGBUS or structured
 79: // exeption with code EXCEPTION_IN_PAGE_ERROR on Windows. This
 80: // signal handler catches that signal and prints out a user-friendly
 81: // error message. Without this, it is very hard to realize that the
 82: // disk might be full.
 83: #ifdef _WIN32
 84: 
 85: static LONG WINAPI vectored_handler(_EXCEPTION_POINTERS *exception_info) {
 86:   static std::mutex mu;
 87:   std::scoped_lock lock{mu};
 88: 
 89:   PEXCEPTION_RECORD exception_record = exception_info->ExceptionRecord;
 90:   ULONG_PTR *exception_information = exception_record->ExceptionInformation;
 91:   if (exception_record->ExceptionCode == EXCEPTION_IN_PAGE_ERROR &&
 92:       (ULONG_PTR)output_buffer_start <= exception_information[1] &&
 93:       exception_information[1] < (ULONG_PTR)output_buffer_end) {
 94: 
 95:     const char msg[] = "mold: failed to write to an output file. Disk full?\n";
 96:     (void)!write(_fileno(stderr), msg, sizeof(msg) - 1);
 97:   }
 98: 
 99:   cleanup();
100:   _exit(1);
101: }
102: 
103: void install_signal_handler() {
104:   AddVectoredExceptionHandler(0, vectored_handler);
105: }
106: 
107: #else
108: 
109: static void sighandler(int signo, siginfo_t *info, void *ucontext) {
110:   static std::mutex mu;
111:   std::scoped_lock lock{mu};
112: 
113:   if ((signo == SIGSEGV || signo == SIGBUS) &&
114:       output_buffer_start <= info->si_addr &&
115:       info->si_addr < output_buffer_end) {
116:     const char msg[] = "mold: failed to write to an output file. Disk full?\n";
117:     (void)!write(STDERR_FILENO, msg, sizeof(msg) - 1);
118:   }
119: 
120:   cleanup();
121:   _exit(1);
122: }
123: 
124: void install_signal_handler() {
125:   struct sigaction action;
126:   action.sa_sigaction = sighandler;
127:   sigemptyset(&action.sa_mask);
128:   action.sa_flags = SA_SIGINFO;
129: 
130:   sigaction(SIGINT, &action, NULL);
131:   sigaction(SIGTERM, &action, NULL);
132:   sigaction(SIGBUS, &action, NULL);
133: }
134: 
135: #endif
136: 
137: i64 get_default_thread_count() {
138:   // mold doesn't scale well above 32 threads.
139:   int n = tbb::global_control::active_value(
140:     tbb::global_control::max_allowed_parallelism);
141:   return std::min(n, 32);
142: }
143: 
144: } // namespace mold
145: 
146: int main(int argc, char **argv) {
147:   mold::mold_version = mold::get_mold_version();
148: 
149: #if MOLD_IS_SOLD
150:   std::string cmd = mold::filepath(argv[0]).filename().string();
151:   if (cmd == "ld64" || cmd.starts_with("ld64."))
152:     return mold::macho::main(argc, argv);
153: #endif
154: 
155:   return mold::elf::main(argc, argv);
156: }

================
File: common/multi-glob.cc
================
  1: // This file implements the Aho-Corasick algorithm to match multiple
  2: // glob patterns to symbol strings as quickly as possible.
  3: //
  4: // Here are some examples of glob patterns:
  5: //
  6: //    qt_private_api_tag*
  7: //    *16QAccessibleCache*
  8: //    *32QAbstractFileIconProviderPrivate*
  9: //    *17QPixmapIconEngine*
 10: //
 11: // `*` is a wildcard that matches any substring. We sometimes have
 12: // hundreds of glob patterns and have to match them against millions
 13: // of symbol strings.
 14: //
 15: // Aho-Corasick cannot handle complex patterns such as `*foo*bar*`.
 16: // We handle such patterns with the Glob class. Glob is relatively
 17: // slow, but complex patterns are rare in practice, so it should be
 18: // OK.
 19: 
 20: #include "common.h"
 21: 
 22: #include <queue>
 23: #include <regex>
 24: 
 25: namespace mold {
 26: 
 27: std::optional<u32> MultiGlob::find(std::string_view str) {
 28:   std::call_once(once, [&] { compile(); });
 29:   u32 val = UINT32_MAX;
 30: 
 31:   if (root) {
 32:     // Match against simple glob patterns
 33:     TrieNode *node = root.get();
 34: 
 35:     auto walk = [&](u8 c) {
 36:       for (;;) {
 37:         if (node->children[c]) {
 38:           node = node->children[c].get();
 39:           val = std::min(val, node->value);
 40:           return;
 41:         }
 42: 
 43:         if (!node->suffix_link)
 44:           return;
 45:         node = node->suffix_link;
 46:       }
 47:     };
 48: 
 49:     walk('\0');
 50:     for (u8 c : str)
 51:       walk(c);
 52:     walk('\0');
 53:   }
 54: 
 55:   // Match against complex glob patterns
 56:   for (std::pair<Glob, u32> &glob : globs)
 57:     if (glob.first.match(str))
 58:       val = std::min(val, glob.second);
 59: 
 60:   if (val == UINT32_MAX)
 61:     return {};
 62:   return val;
 63: }
 64: 
 65: static bool is_simple_pattern(std::string_view pat) {
 66:   static std::regex re(R"(\*?[^*[?]+\*?)", std::regex_constants::optimize);
 67:   return std::regex_match(pat.begin(), pat.end(), re);
 68: }
 69: 
 70: static std::string handle_stars(std::string_view pat) {
 71:   std::string str(pat);
 72: 
 73:   // Convert "foo" -> "\0foo\0", "*foo" -> "foo\0", "foo*" -> "\0foo"
 74:   // and "*foo*" -> "foo". Aho-Corasick can do only substring matching,
 75:   // so we use \0 as beginning/end-of-string markers.
 76:   if (str.starts_with('*') && str.ends_with('*'))
 77:     return str.substr(1, str.size() - 2);
 78:   if (str.starts_with('*'))
 79:     return str.substr(1) + "\0"s;
 80:   if (str.ends_with('*'))
 81:     return "\0"s + str.substr(0, str.size() - 1);
 82:   return "\0"s + str + "\0"s;
 83: }
 84: 
 85: bool MultiGlob::add(std::string_view pat, u32 val) {
 86:   assert(!is_compiled);
 87:   assert(!pat.empty());
 88: 
 89:   strings.push_back(std::string(pat));
 90: 
 91:   // Complex glob pattern
 92:   if (!is_simple_pattern(pat)) {
 93:     if (std::optional<Glob> glob = Glob::compile(pat)) {
 94:       globs.push_back({std::move(*glob), val});
 95:       return true;
 96:     }
 97:     return false;
 98:   }
 99: 
100:   // Simple glob pattern
101:   if (!root)
102:     root.reset(new TrieNode);
103:   TrieNode *node = root.get();
104: 
105:   for (u8 c : handle_stars(pat)) {
106:     if (!node->children[c])
107:       node->children[c].reset(new TrieNode);
108:     node = node->children[c].get();
109:   }
110: 
111:   node->value = std::min(node->value, val);
112:   return true;
113: }
114: 
115: void MultiGlob::compile() {
116:   is_compiled = true;
117:   if (root) {
118:     fix_suffix_links(*root);
119:     fix_values();
120:   }
121: }
122: 
123: void MultiGlob::fix_suffix_links(TrieNode &node) {
124:   for (i64 i = 0; i < 256; i++) {
125:     if (!node.children[i])
126:       continue;
127: 
128:     TrieNode &child = *node.children[i];
129: 
130:     TrieNode *cur = node.suffix_link;
131:     for (;;) {
132:       if (!cur) {
133:         child.suffix_link = root.get();
134:         break;
135:       }
136: 
137:       if (cur->children[i]) {
138:         child.suffix_link = cur->children[i].get();
139:         break;
140:       }
141: 
142:       cur = cur->suffix_link;
143:     }
144: 
145:     fix_suffix_links(child);
146:   }
147: }
148: 
149: void MultiGlob::fix_values() {
150:   std::queue<TrieNode *> queue;
151:   queue.push(root.get());
152: 
153:   do {
154:     TrieNode *node = queue.front();
155:     queue.pop();
156: 
157:     for (std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> &child : node->children) {
158:       if (!child)
159:         continue;
160:       child->value = std::min(child->value, child->suffix_link->value);
161:       queue.push(child.get());
162:     }
163:   } while (!queue.empty());
164: }
165: 
166: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/output-file-unix.h
================
  1: #include "common.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <fcntl.h>
  4: #include <filesystem>
  5: #include <sys/mman.h>
  6: #include <sys/stat.h>
  7: #include <sys/types.h>
  8: 
  9: namespace mold {
 10: 
 11: inline u32 get_umask() {
 12:   u32 orig_umask = umask(0);
 13:   umask(orig_umask);
 14:   return orig_umask;
 15: }
 16: 
 17: template <typename Context>
 18: static std::pair<i64, char *>
 19: open_or_create_file(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm) {
 20:   std::string tmpl = filepath(path).parent_path() / ".mold-XXXXXX";
 21:   char *path2 = (char *)save_string(ctx, tmpl).data();
 22: 
 23:   i64 fd = mkstemp(path2);
 24:   if (fd == -1)
 25:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << path2 <<  ": " << errno_string();
 26: 
 27:   // Reuse an existing file if exists and writable because on Linux,
 28:   // writing to an existing file is much faster than creating a fresh
 29:   // file and writing to it.
 30:   if (ctx.overwrite_output_file && rename(path.c_str(), path2) == 0) {
 31:     ::close(fd);
 32:     fd = ::open(path2, O_RDWR | O_CREAT, perm);
 33:     if (fd != -1 && !ftruncate(fd, filesize) && !fchmod(fd, perm & ~get_umask()))
 34:       return {fd, path2};
 35: 
 36:     unlink(path2);
 37:     fd = ::open(path2, O_RDWR | O_CREAT, perm);
 38:     if (fd == -1)
 39:       Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << path2 << ": " << errno_string();
 40:   }
 41: 
 42:   if (ftruncate(fd, filesize))
 43:     Fatal(ctx) << "ftruncate failed: " << errno_string();
 44: 
 45:   if (fchmod(fd, (perm & ~get_umask())) == -1)
 46:     Fatal(ctx) << "fchmod failed: " << errno_string();
 47:   return {fd, path2};
 48: }
 49: 
 50: template <typename Context>
 51: class MemoryMappedOutputFile : public OutputFile<Context> {
 52: public:
 53:   MemoryMappedOutputFile(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm)
 54:     : OutputFile<Context>(path, filesize, true) {
 55:     i64 fd;
 56:     std::tie(fd, output_tmpfile) = open_or_create_file(ctx, path, filesize, perm);
 57: 
 58:     this->buf = (u8 *)mmap(nullptr, filesize, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE,
 59:                            MAP_SHARED, fd, 0);
 60:     if (this->buf == MAP_FAILED)
 61:       Fatal(ctx) << path << ": mmap failed: " << errno_string();
 62:     ::close(fd);
 63: 
 64:     mold::output_buffer_start = this->buf;
 65:     mold::output_buffer_end = this->buf + filesize;
 66:   }
 67: 
 68:   ~MemoryMappedOutputFile() {
 69:     if (fd2 != -1)
 70:       ::close(fd2);
 71:   }
 72: 
 73:   void close(Context &ctx) override {
 74:     Timer t(ctx, "close_file");
 75: 
 76:     if (!this->is_unmapped)
 77:       munmap(this->buf, this->filesize);
 78: 
 79:     // If an output file already exists, open a file and then remove it.
 80:     // This is the fastest way to unlink a file, as it does not make the
 81:     // system to immediately release disk blocks occupied by the file.
 82:     fd2 = ::open(this->path.c_str(), O_RDONLY);
 83:     if (fd2 != -1)
 84:       unlink(this->path.c_str());
 85: 
 86:     if (rename(output_tmpfile, this->path.c_str()) == -1)
 87:       Fatal(ctx) << this->path << ": rename failed: " << errno_string();
 88:     output_tmpfile = nullptr;
 89:   }
 90: 
 91: private:
 92:   int fd2 = -1;
 93: };
 94: 
 95: template <typename Context>
 96: class MallocOutputFile : public OutputFile<Context> {
 97: public:
 98:   MallocOutputFile(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm)
 99:     : OutputFile<Context>(path, filesize, false), perm(perm) {
100:     this->buf = (u8 *)mmap(NULL, filesize, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE,
101:                            MAP_SHARED | MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0);
102:     if (this->buf == MAP_FAILED)
103:       Fatal(ctx) << "mmap failed: " << errno_string();
104:   }
105: 
106:   void close(Context &ctx) override {
107:     Timer t(ctx, "close_file");
108: 
109:     if (this->path == "-") {
110:       fwrite(this->buf, this->filesize, 1, stdout);
111:       fclose(stdout);
112:       return;
113:     }
114: 
115:     i64 fd = ::open(this->path.c_str(), O_RDWR | O_CREAT, perm);
116:     if (fd == -1)
117:       Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << this->path << ": " << errno_string();
118: 
119:     FILE *fp = fdopen(fd, "w");
120:     fwrite(this->buf, this->filesize, 1, fp);
121:     fclose(fp);
122:   }
123: 
124: private:
125:   i64 perm;
126: };
127: 
128: template <typename Context>
129: std::unique_ptr<OutputFile<Context>>
130: OutputFile<Context>::open(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm) {
131:   Timer t(ctx, "open_file");
132: 
133:   if (path.starts_with('/') && !ctx.arg.chroot.empty())
134:     path = ctx.arg.chroot + "/" + path_clean(path);
135: 
136:   bool is_special = false;
137:   if (path == "-") {
138:     is_special = true;
139:   } else {
140:     struct stat st;
141:     if (stat(path.c_str(), &st) == 0 && (st.st_mode & S_IFMT) != S_IFREG)
142:       is_special = true;
143:   }
144: 
145:   OutputFile<Context> *file;
146:   if (is_special)
147:     file = new MallocOutputFile(ctx, path, filesize, perm);
148:   else
149:     file = new MemoryMappedOutputFile(ctx, path, filesize, perm);
150: 
151: #ifdef MADV_HUGEPAGE
152:   // Enable transparent huge page for an output memory-mapped file.
153:   // On Linux, it has an effect only on tmpfs mounted with `huge=advise`,
154:   // but it can make the linker ~10% faster. You can try it by creating
155:   // a tmpfs with the following commands
156:   //
157:   //  $ mkdir tmp
158:   //  $ sudo mount -t tmpfs -o size=2G,huge=advise none tmp
159:   //
160:   // and then specifying a path under the directory as an output file.
161:   madvise(file->buf, filesize, MADV_HUGEPAGE);
162: #endif
163: 
164:   if (ctx.arg.filler != -1)
165:     memset(file->buf, ctx.arg.filler, filesize);
166:   return std::unique_ptr<OutputFile>(file);
167: }
168: 
169: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/output-file-win32.h
================
 1: #include "common.h"
 2: 
 3: #include <fcntl.h>
 4: #include <filesystem>
 5: #include <windows.h>
 6: 
 7: namespace mold {
 8: 
 9: template <typename Context>
10: class MallocOutputFile : public OutputFile<Context> {
11: public:
12:   MallocOutputFile(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm)
13:     : OutputFile<Context>(path, filesize, false), perm(perm) {
14:     this->buf = (u8 *)malloc(filesize);
15:     if (!this->buf)
16:       Fatal(ctx) << "malloc failed";
17:   }
18: 
19:   void close(Context &ctx) override {
20:     Timer t(ctx, "close_file");
21: 
22:     if (this->path == "-") {
23:       fwrite(this->buf, this->filesize, 1, stdout);
24:       fclose(stdout);
25:       return;
26:     }
27: 
28:     i64 fd = ::open(this->path.c_str(), O_RDWR | O_CREAT, perm);
29:     if (fd == -1)
30:       Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << this->path << ": " << errno_string();
31: 
32:     FILE *fp = fdopen(fd, "w");
33:     fwrite(this->buf, this->filesize, 1, fp);
34:     fclose(fp);
35:     free(this->buf);
36:   }
37: 
38: private:
39:   i64 perm;
40: };
41: 
42: template <typename Context>
43: std::unique_ptr<OutputFile<Context>>
44: OutputFile<Context>::open(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm) {
45:   Timer t(ctx, "open_file");
46: 
47:   if (path.starts_with('/') && !ctx.arg.chroot.empty())
48:     path = ctx.arg.chroot + "/" + path_clean(path);
49: 
50:   OutputFile<Context> *file = new MallocOutputFile(ctx, path, filesize, perm);
51: 
52:   if (ctx.arg.filler != -1)
53:     memset(file->buf, ctx.arg.filler, filesize);
54:   return std::unique_ptr<OutputFile<Context>>(file);
55: }
56: 
57: } // namespace mold

File: common/output-file.h

================
File: common/perf.cc
================
  1: #include "common.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <functional>
  4: #include <iomanip>
  5: #include <ios>
  6: 
  7: #ifndef _WIN32
  8: #include <sys/resource.h>
  9: #include <sys/time.h>
 10: #endif
 11: 
 12: namespace mold {
 13: 
 14: i64 Counter::get_value() {
 15:   return values.combine(std::plus());
 16: }
 17: 
 18: void Counter::print() {
 19:   sort(instances, [](Counter *a, Counter *b) {
 20:     return a->get_value() > b->get_value();
 21:   });
 22: 
 23:   for (Counter *c : instances)
 24:     std::cout << std::setw(20) << std::right << c->name
 25:               << "=" << c->get_value() << "\n";
 26: }
 27: 
 28: static i64 now_nsec() {
 29: #ifdef _WIN32
 30:   return (i64)std::chrono::steady_clock::now().time_since_epoch().count();
 31: #else
 32:   struct timespec t;
 33:   clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t);
 34:   return (i64)t.tv_sec * 1'000'000'000 + t.tv_nsec;
 35: #endif
 36: }
 37: 
 38: static std::pair<i64, i64> get_usage() {
 39: #ifdef _WIN32
 40:   auto to_nsec = [](FILETIME t) -> i64 {
 41:     return ((u64)t.dwHighDateTime << 32 + (u64)t.dwLowDateTime) * 100;
 42:   };
 43: 
 44:   FILETIME creation, exit, kernel, user;
 45:   GetProcessTimes(GetCurrentProcess(), &creation, &exit, &kernel, &user);
 46:   return {to_nsec(user), to_nsec(kernel)};
 47: #else
 48:   auto to_nsec = [](struct timeval t) -> i64 {
 49:     return (i64)t.tv_sec * 1'000'000'000 + t.tv_usec * 1'000;
 50:   };
 51: 
 52:   struct rusage ru;
 53:   getrusage(RUSAGE_SELF, &ru);
 54:   return {to_nsec(ru.ru_utime), to_nsec(ru.ru_stime)};
 55: #endif
 56: }
 57: 
 58: TimerRecord::TimerRecord(std::string name, TimerRecord *parent)
 59:   : name(name), parent(parent) {
 60:   start = now_nsec();
 61:   std::tie(user, sys) = get_usage();
 62:   if (parent)
 63:     parent->children.push_back(this);
 64: }
 65: 
 66: void TimerRecord::stop() {
 67:   if (stopped)
 68:     return;
 69:   stopped = true;
 70: 
 71:   i64 user2;
 72:   i64 sys2;
 73:   std::tie(user2, sys2) = get_usage();
 74: 
 75:   end = now_nsec();
 76:   user = user2 - user;
 77:   sys = sys2 - sys;
 78: }
 79: 
 80: static void print_rec(TimerRecord &rec, i64 indent) {
 81:   printf(" % 8.3f % 8.3f % 8.3f  %s%s\n",
 82:          ((double)rec.user / 1'000'000'000),
 83:          ((double)rec.sys / 1'000'000'000),
 84:          (((double)rec.end - rec.start) / 1'000'000'000),
 85:          std::string(indent * 2, ' ').c_str(),
 86:          rec.name.c_str());
 87: 
 88:   sort(rec.children, [](TimerRecord *a, TimerRecord *b) {
 89:     return a->start < b->start;
 90:   });
 91: 
 92:   for (TimerRecord *child : rec.children)
 93:     print_rec(*child, indent + 1);
 94: }
 95: 
 96: void print_timer_records(
 97:     tbb::concurrent_vector<std::unique_ptr<TimerRecord>> &records) {
 98:   for (i64 i = records.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
 99:     records[i]->stop();
100: 
101:   for (i64 i = 0; i < records.size(); i++) {
102:     TimerRecord &inner = *records[i];
103:     if (inner.parent)
104:       continue;
105: 
106:     for (i64 j = i - 1; j >= 0; j--) {
107:       TimerRecord &outer = *records[j];
108:       if (outer.start <= inner.start && inner.end <= outer.end) {
109:         inner.parent = &outer;
110:         outer.children.push_back(&inner);
111:         break;
112:       }
113:     }
114:   }
115: 
116:   std::cout << "     User   System     Real  Name\n";
117: 
118:   for (std::unique_ptr<TimerRecord> &rec : records)
119:     if (!rec->parent)
120:       print_rec(*rec, 0);
121: 
122:   std::cout << std::flush;
123: }
124: 
125: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/sha.h
================
 1: #pragma once
 2: 
 3: #include <cstdint>
 4: 
 5: typedef uint8_t u8;
 6: static constexpr int64_t SHA256_SIZE = 32;
 7: 
 8: #ifdef _WIN32
 9: // On Windows, we use Microsoft CNG.
10: 
11: #include <Windows.h>
12: #include <bcrypt.h>
13: #include <ntstatus.h>
14: 
15: inline static BCRYPT_ALG_HANDLE get_sha256_handle() {
16:   static std::once_flag once;
17:   static BCRYPT_ALG_HANDLE alg;
18: 
19:   std::call_once(once, [&] {
20:     BCryptOpenAlgorithmProvider(&alg, BCRYPT_SHA256_ALGORITHM, nullptr, 0);
21:   });
22:   return alg;
23: }
24: 
25: inline void sha256_hash(u8 *in, size_t len, u8 *out) {
26:   BCryptHash(get_sha256_handle(), nullptr, 0, in, len, out, SHA256_SIZE);
27: }
28: 
29: class SHA256Hash {
30: public:
31:   SHA256Hash() {
32:     BCryptCreateHash(get_sha256_handle(), &handle, nullptr, 0, nullptr, 0, 0);
33:   }
34: 
35:   void update(u8 *data, size_t len) {
36:     BCryptHashData(handle, data, len, 0);
37:   }
38: 
39:   void finish(u8 *out) {
40:     BCryptFinishHash(handle, out, SHA256_SIZE, 0);
41:   }
42: 
43: private:
44:   BCRYPT_HASH_HANDLE handle;
45: };
46: 
47: #else
48: // On Unix, we use OpenSSL or the Apple's OpenSSL-compatible API.
49: 
50: #ifdef __APPLE__
51: #  define COMMON_DIGEST_FOR_OPENSSL
52: #  include <CommonCrypto/CommonDigest.h>
53: #  define SHA256(data, len, md) CC_SHA256(data, len, md)
54: #else
55: #  define OPENSSL_SUPPRESS_DEPRECATED 1
56: #  include <openssl/sha.h>
57: #endif
58: 
59: inline void sha256_hash(u8 *in, size_t len, u8 *out) {
60:   SHA256(in, len, out);
61: }
62: 
63: class SHA256Hash {
64: public:
65:   SHA256Hash() {
66:     SHA256_Init(&ctx);
67:   }
68: 
69:   void update(u8 *data, size_t len) {
70:     SHA256_Update(&ctx, data, len);
71:   }
72: 
73:   void finish(u8 *out) {
74:     SHA256_Final(out, &ctx);
75:   }
76: 
77: private:
78:   SHA256_CTX ctx;
79: };
80: 
81: #endif

================
File: common/tar.cc
================
  1: #include "common.h"
  2: 
  3: namespace mold {
  4: 
  5: // A tar file consists of one or more Ustar header followed by data.
  6: // Each Ustar header represents a single file in an archive.
  7: //
  8: // tar is an old file format, and its `name` field is only 100 bytes long.
  9: // If `name` is longer than 100 bytes, we can emit a PAX header before a
 10: // Ustar header to store a long filename.
 11: //
 12: // For simplicity, we always emit a PAX header even for a short filename.
 13: struct UstarHeader {
 14:   UstarHeader() {
 15:     memset(this, 0, sizeof(*this));
 16:   }
 17: 
 18:   void finalize() {
 19:     memset(checksum, ' ', sizeof(checksum));
 20:     memcpy(magic, "ustar", 5);
 21:     memcpy(version, "00", 2);
 22: 
 23:     // Compute checksum
 24:     int sum = 0;
 25:     for (i64 i = 0; i < sizeof(*this); i++)
 26:       sum += ((u8 *)this)[i];
 27: 
 28:     // We need to convince the compiler that sum isn't too big to silence
 29:     // -Werror=format-truncation.
 30:     ASSUME(sum < 01'000'000);
 31:     snprintf(checksum, sizeof(checksum), "%06o", sum);
 32:   }
 33: 
 34:   char name[100];
 35:   char mode[8];
 36:   char uid[8];
 37:   char gid[8];
 38:   char size[12];
 39:   char mtime[12];
 40:   char checksum[8];
 41:   char typeflag[1];
 42:   char linkname[100];
 43:   char magic[6];
 44:   char version[2];
 45:   char uname[32];
 46:   char gname[32];
 47:   char devmajor[8];
 48:   char devminor[8];
 49:   char prefix[155];
 50:   char pad[12];
 51: };
 52: 
 53: static std::string encode_path(std::string basedir, std::string path) {
 54:   path = path_clean(basedir + "/" + path);
 55: 
 56:   // Construct a string which contains something like
 57:   // "16 path=foo/bar\n" where 16 is the size of the string
 58:   // including the size string itself.
 59:   i64 len = std::string(" path=\n").size() + path.size();
 60:   i64 total = std::to_string(len).size() + len;
 61:   total = std::to_string(total).size() + len;
 62:   return std::to_string(total) + " path=" + path + "\n";
 63: }
 64: 
 65: std::unique_ptr<TarWriter>
 66: TarWriter::open(std::string output_path, std::string basedir) {
 67:   FILE *out = fopen(output_path.c_str(), "w");
 68:   if (!out)
 69:     return nullptr;
 70:   return std::unique_ptr<TarWriter>(new TarWriter(out, basedir));
 71: }
 72: 
 73: TarWriter::~TarWriter() {
 74:   fclose(out);
 75: }
 76: 
 77: void TarWriter::append(std::string path, std::string_view data) {
 78:   // Write PAX header
 79:   static_assert(sizeof(UstarHeader) == BLOCK_SIZE);
 80:   UstarHeader pax;
 81: 
 82:   std::string attr = encode_path(basedir, path);
 83:   snprintf(pax.size, sizeof(pax.size), "%011zo", attr.size());
 84:   pax.typeflag[0] = 'x';
 85:   pax.finalize();
 86:   fwrite(&pax, sizeof(pax), 1, out);
 87: 
 88:   // Write pathname
 89:   fwrite(attr.data(), attr.size(), 1, out);
 90:   fseek(out, align_to(ftell(out), BLOCK_SIZE), SEEK_SET);
 91: 
 92:   // Write Ustar header
 93:   UstarHeader ustar;
 94:   memcpy(ustar.mode, "0000664", 8);
 95:   snprintf(ustar.size, sizeof(ustar.size), "%011zo", data.size());
 96:   ustar.finalize();
 97:   fwrite(&ustar, sizeof(ustar), 1, out);
 98: 
 99:   // Write file contents
100:   fwrite(data.data(), data.size(), 1, out);
101:   fseek(out, align_to(ftell(out), BLOCK_SIZE), SEEK_SET);
102: 
103:   // A tar file must ends with two empty blocks, so write such
104:   // terminator and seek back.
105:   u8 terminator[BLOCK_SIZE * 2] = {};
106:   fwrite(&terminator, BLOCK_SIZE * 2, 1, out);
107:   fseek(out, -BLOCK_SIZE * 2, SEEK_END);
108: 
109:   assert(ftell(out) % BLOCK_SIZE == 0);
110: }
111: 
112: } // namespace mold

================
File: common/update-git-hash.cmake
================
 1: # Get a git hash value. We do not want to use git command here
 2: # because we don't want to make git a build-time dependency.
 3: if(EXISTS "${SOURCE_DIR}/.git/HEAD")
 4:   file(READ "${SOURCE_DIR}/.git/HEAD" HASH)
 5:   string(STRIP "${HASH}" HASH)
 6: 
 7:   if(HASH MATCHES "^ref: (.*)")
 8:     set(HEAD "${CMAKE_MATCH_1}")
 9:     if(EXISTS "${SOURCE_DIR}/.git/${HEAD}")
10:       file(READ "${SOURCE_DIR}/.git/${HEAD}" HASH)
11:       string(STRIP "${HASH}" HASH)
12:     else()
13:       file(READ "${SOURCE_DIR}/.git/packed-refs" PACKED_REFS)
14:       string(REGEX REPLACE ".*\n([0-9a-f]+) ${HEAD}\n.*" "\\1" HASH "\n${PACKED_REFS}")
15:     endif()
16:   endif()
17: endif()
18: 
19: # Create new file contents and update a given file if necessary.
20: set(NEW_CONTENTS "#include <string>
21: namespace mold {
22: std::string mold_git_hash = \"${HASH}\";
23: }
24: ")
25: 
26: if(EXISTS "${OUTPUT_FILE}")
27:   file(READ "${OUTPUT_FILE}" OLD_CONTENTS)
28:   if(NOT "${NEW_CONTENTS}" STREQUAL "${OLD_CONTENTS}")
29:     file(WRITE "${OUTPUT_FILE}" "${NEW_CONTENTS}")
30:   endif()
31: else()
32:   file(WRITE "${OUTPUT_FILE}" "${NEW_CONTENTS}")
33: endif()

================
File: common/uuid.cc
================
 1: #include "common.h"
 2: 
 3: #include <random>
 4: 
 5: namespace mold {
 6: 
 7: std::array<u8, 16> get_uuid_v4() {
 8:   std::array<u8, 16> bytes;
 9: 
10:   std::random_device rand;
11:   u32 buf[4] = { rand(), rand(), rand(), rand() };
12:   memcpy(bytes.data(), buf, 16);
13: 
14:   // Indicate that this is UUIDv4 as defined by RFC4122.
15:   bytes[6] = (bytes[6] & 0b00001111) | 0b01000000;
16:   bytes[8] = (bytes[8] & 0b00111111) | 0b10000000;
17:   return bytes;
18: }
19: 
20: } // namespace mold

================
File: docs/bugs.md
================
  1: This is a note about interesting bugs that I met during the
  2: development of the mold linker.
  3: 
  4: ## GNU IFUNC
  5: 
  6: Problem: A statically-linked "hello world" program mysteriously
  7: crashed in `__libc_start_main` function which is called just after
  8: `_start`.
  9: 
 10: Investigation: I opened up gdb and found that the program reads a
 11: bogus value from some array. It looks like `memcpy` failed to copy
 12: proper data there.  After some investigation, I noticed that `memcpy`
 13: did't copy data at all but instead returned the address of
 14: `__memcpy_avx_unaligned` function, which is a real `memcpy` function
 15: optimized for machines with the AVX registers.
 16: 
 17: This odd issue was caused by the GNU IFUNC mechanism.  That is, if a
 18: function symbol has type `STT_GNU_IFUNC`, the function does not do
 19: what its name suggests to do but instead returns a pointer to a
 20: function that does the actual job. In this case, `memcpy` is an IFUNC
 21: function, and it returns an address of `__memcpy_avx_unaligned` which
 22: is a real `memcpy` function.
 23: 
 24: IFUNC function addresses are stored to `.got` section in an ELF
 25: executable.  The dynamic loader executes all IFUNC functions at
 26: startup to replace their GOT entries with their return values. This
 27: mechanism allows programs to choose the best implementation among
 28: variants of the same function at runtime based on the machine info.
 29: 
 30: If a program is statically-linked, there's no dynamic loader that
 31: rewrites the GOT entries. Therefore, if a program is
 32: statically-linked, a libc's startup routine does that on behalf of the
 33: dynamic loader. Concretely, a startup routine interprets all dynamic
 34: relocations between `__rela_iplt_start` and `__rela_iplt_stop`
 35: symbols.  It is linker's responsibility to emit dynamic relocations
 36: for IFUNC symbols even if it is linking a statically-linked program
 37: and mark the beginning and the ending of a `.rela.dyn` section with
 38: the symbols, so that the startup routine can find the relocations.
 39: 
 40: The bug was my linker didn't define `__rela_iplt_start` and
 41: `__rela_iplt_stop` symbols. Since these symbols are weak, they are
 42: initialized to zero. From the point of the initializer function,
 43: there's no dynamic relocations between `__rela_iplt_start` and
 44: `__rela_iplt_stop` symbols. That left GOT entries for IFUNC symbols
 45: untouched.
 46: 
 47: The proper fix was to emit dynamic relocations for IFUNC symbols and
 48: define the linker-synthesized symbols. I did that, and the bug was
 49: fixed.
 50: 
 51: ## stdio buffering
 52: 
 53: Problem: A statically-linked "Hello world" program prints out the
 54: message if executed as `./hello`, but it doesn't output anything if
 55: executed as `./hello | cat`.
 56: 
 57: Investigation: I knew that the default buffering mode for stdout is
 58: line buffering (buffer is flushed on every '\n'), but if it is not
 59: connected to the terminal (i.e. `isatty(2)` returns 0 on
 60: `STDOUT_FILENO`), it automatically switches to full buffering (buffer
 61: is flushed when it becomes full). So, it looks like libc failed to
 62: flush the stdout on program exit for some reason.
 63: 
 64: I traced all function calls using gdb and noticed that `__libc_atexit`
 65: was not called. That function seemed to be responsible for buffer
 66: flushing. I don't know how exactly I found the root cause, but after
 67: spending an hour or two, I found that `__start___libc_atexit` and
 68: `__stop___libc_atexit` have value 0 in my linker's output while they
 69: mark a section containing the address of `__libc_atexit` in GNU ld's
 70: output.
 71: 
 72: So, libc doesn't directly call `__libc_atexit` but instead call all
 73: function pointers between `__start___libc_atexit` and
 74: `__stop___libc_atexit` symbols. libc puts `__libc_atexit` address in
 75: `_libc_atexit` section, expecting that the linker automatically
 76: creates the start and the end marker symbols for the section.
 77: 
 78: There's an obscure linker feature: if a section name is valid as a C
 79: identifier (e.g. `foo` or `_foo_bar` but not `.foo`), the linker
 80: automatically creates marker symbols by prepending `__start_` and
 81: `__stop_` to the section name. My linker lacked the feature.
 82: 
 83: I implemented the feature, and the bug was fixed.
 84: 
 85: ## TLS variable initialization
 86: 
 87: Problem: A statically-linked "hello world" program crashes after
 88: reading a thread-local variable.
 89: 
 90: Investigation: Thread-local variables are very different from other
 91: types of varaibles because there may be more than one instance of the
 92: same variable in memory. Each thread has its copy of thread-local
 93: varaibles. `%fs` segment register points the end of the variable area
 94: for the current thread, and the variables are accessed as an offset
 95: from `%fs`.
 96: 
 97: Thread-local variables may be initialized (e.g. `thread_local int x =
 98: 5;`). The linker gathers all thread-local variables and put them into
 99: `PT_TLS` segment. At runtime, the contents of the segment is used as
100: an "initialization image" for new threads. When a new thread is
101: created, the image is memcpy'ed to the new thread's thread-local
102: variable area. The initialization image itself is read-only at
103: runtime.
104: 
105: It took more than a day to find out that memcpy copies the
106: initialization image to a different place than the thread-local
107: variables reside. That means, thread-local variables had garbage as
108: initial values, and the program crashed when using them.
109: 
110: The problem is that I set a very large value (4096) to the alignment
111: of `PT_TLS` segment. All `PT_LOAD` segments are naturally aligned to
112: the page boundary, so I use the same value for `PT_TLS`, but that was
113: a mistake. When a thread initialization routine sets a value to `%fs`,
114: it first aligns the end of the thread-local variable area address to
115: `PT_TLS` alignment value. So, if you set a large value to `PT_TLS`
116: alignment, `%fs` is set to a wrong place.
117: 
118: I fixed `PT_TLS` alignment, and the bug was gone.
119: 
120: ## stdio buffering (another issue)
121: 
122: I noticed that a dynamically-linked "hello world" program didn't
123: flush its stdout buffer on exit. The cause of the problem was that the
124: executable had more than one DT_NEEDED entry for `libc.so`.
125: 
126: DT_NEEDED entries in `.dynamic` section specify a list of shared
127: object file names which need to be linked at runtime. I added one
128: DT_NEEDED entry for each library specified with the `-l` option.
129: The pitfall is, unlike object files, libraries are allowed to
130: appear more than once in a command line, and the linker has to
131: de-duplicate them before processing. Adding more than one DT_NEEDED
132: entry for the same shared object causes mysterious issues like this.
133: 
134: # Copy relocations and symbol aliases
135: 
136: environ, _environ and __environ point to the same location in libc.so,
137: so when we create a copy relocation for one of the symbols, we need to
138: do that for all of them. Otherwise, they'll end up pointing to different
139: places which causes a very mysterious issue.
140: 
141: # DT_DEBUG and gdb
142: 
143: If you forget to add an entry with type DT_DEBUG to .dynamic, gdb's
144: `info sharedlibrary` command doesn't print out a list of shared
145: libraries loaded to memory. The value of the entry doesn't matter, so
146: it can be just zero. The existence of it is important.
147: 
148: #
149: 
150: __EH_FRAME_BEGIN__ in libgcc/crtstuff.c

================
File: docs/coding-guidelines.md
================
 1: # Coding Guidelines
 2: 
 3: mold is written in C++20, but as is the case with every C++ project,
 4: it has local coding rules. In this document, I'll explain some of them
 5: and try to give justifications for why I chose such rules.
 6: 
 7: ## DOs
 8: 
 9: - Always use `i64` (which is a type alias for `int64_t` in mold) for
10:   integers unless you have a reason not to. For example, even if you know
11:   that a loop counter won't exceed 100, you should stop thinking about it
12:   and just use `i64`.
13: 
14:   Justification: Local variables are usually on CPU registers, so on
15:   64-bit CPUs, there's no performance peanlty on choosing `i64` over
16:   `i32`. Even if a compiler has to spill register values to the stack,
17:   I don't think there's an observable difference between `i32` and
18:   `i64`. Therefore, extra 32 bits are essentially free. On 32-bit CPUs,
19:   they are not free, but that's OK because we are writing mold for modern
20:   computers. mold will still run on 32-bit computers but a bit slowly.
21: 
22:   By always using `i64`, we can eliminate the need to think about the
23:   "right" size for each variable. It also reduces the risk of integer
24:   overflow.
25: 
26:   Exceptions: If you have to allocate a very large number (e.g. millions)
27:   of the same object, its size matters. In that case, use a smaller type.
28: 
29: ## DON'Ts
30: 
31: - Don't use `auto` unless its actual type is obvious in the very narrow
32:   context. Currently, we use `auto` only for lambdas.
33: 
34:   Justification: I think `auto` makes code writing easier but code reading
35:   harder, because readers have to make a guess as to what is the actual
36:   type of `auto`. If you are already familiar with the existing codebase,
37:   you may be able to guess it easily, but that's not always the case.
38:   I want to keep the mold codebase friendly to first-time readers.
39: 
40: - Don't over-use inheritance. In mold, most classes don't have parents,
41:   and even if they do, their class hierarchy is very shallow. Currently
42:   its height is just two (i.e. abstract classes and their implementations).
43: 
44:   Justification: Designing class hierarchies is fun as it feels like
45:   taxonomy, but I don't think that always help writing code. It looks like
46:   simpler class hierarchy makes its code simpler.

================
File: docs/design.md
================
  1: ## Design and implementation of mold
  2: 
  3: For the rest of this documentation, I'll explain the design and the
  4: implementation of mold. If you are only interested in using mold, you
  5: don't need to read the below.
  6: 
  7: ### Motivation
  8: 
  9: Here is why I'm writing a new linker:
 10: 
 11: - Even though lld has significantly improved the situation, linking is
 12:   still one of the slowest steps in a build. It is especially
 13:   annoying when I changed one line of code and had to wait for a few
 14:   seconds or even more for a linker to complete. It should be
 15:   instantaneous. There's a need for a faster linker.
 16: 
 17: - The number of cores on a PC has increased a lot lately, and this
 18:   trend is expected to continue. However, the existing linkers can't
 19:   take the advantage of the trend because they don't scale well for more
 20:   cores. I have a 64-core/128-thread machine, so my goal is to create
 21:   a linker that uses the CPU nicely. mold should be much faster than
 22:   other linkers on 4 or 8-core machines too, though.
 23: 
 24: - It looks to me that the designs of the existing linkers are somewhat
 25:   too similar, and I believe there are a lot of drastically different
 26:   designs that haven't been explored yet. Developers generally don't
 27:   care about linkers as long as they work correctly, and they don't
 28:   even think about creating a new one. So there may be lots of low
 29:   hanging fruits there in this area.
 30: 
 31: ### Basic design
 32: 
 33: - In order to achieve a `cp`-like performance, the most important
 34:   thing is to fix the layout of an output file as quickly as possible, so
 35:   that we can start copying actual data from input object files to an
 36:   output file as soon as possible.
 37: 
 38: - Copying data from input files to an output file is I/O-bounded, so
 39:   there should be room for doing computationally-intensive tasks while
 40:   copying data from one file to another.
 41: 
 42: - We should allow the linker to preload object files from disk and
 43:   parse them in memory before a complete set of input object files
 44:   is ready. To do so, we need
