274:         // next instruction.
275:         *(ul32 *)loc = 0xd503'201f; // nop
276:         break;
277:       }
278: 
279:       i64 val = S + A - P;
280:       if (val < -(1 << 27) || (1 << 27) <= val)
281:         val = get_thunk_addr(i) + A - P;
282:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 27, 2);
283:       break;
284:     }
285:     case R_AARCH64_PLT32:
286:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
287:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P;
288:       break;
289:     case R_AARCH64_CONDBR19:
290:     case R_AARCH64_LD_PREL_LO19:
291:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 20), 1LL << 20);
292:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 20, 2) << 5;
293:       break;
294:     case R_AARCH64_PREL16:
295:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 15), 1LL << 15);
296:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A - P;
297:       break;
298:     case R_AARCH64_PREL32:
299:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 32);
300:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P;
301:       break;
302:     case R_AARCH64_PREL64:
303:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - P;
304:       break;
305:     case R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC:
306:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(G + GOT + A, 11, 3) << 10;
307:       break;
308:     case R_AARCH64_LD64_GOTPAGE_LO15: {
309:       i64 val = G + GOT + A - page(GOT);
310:       check(val, 0, 1 << 15);
311:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 14, 3) << 10;
312:       break;
313:     }
314:     case R_AARCH64_TLSIE_ADR_GOTTPREL_PAGE21: {
315:       i64 val = page(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A) - page(P);
316:       check(val, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
317:       write_adrp(loc, val);
318:       break;
319:     }
320:     case R_AARCH64_TLSIE_LD64_GOTTPREL_LO12_NC:
321:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A, 11, 3) << 10;
322:       break;
323:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G0: {
324:       i64 val = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
325:       check(val, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
326:       write_movn_movz(loc, val);
327:       break;
328:     }
329:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G0_NC:
330:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 15, 0) << 5;
331:       break;
332:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G1: {
333:       i64 val = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
334:       check(val, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
335:       write_movn_movz(loc, val >> 16);
336:       break;
337:     }
338:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G1_NC:
339:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 31, 16) << 5;
340:       break;
341:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G2: {
342:       i64 val = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
343:       check(val, -(1LL << 47), 1LL << 47);
344:       write_movn_movz(loc, val >> 32);
345:       break;
346:     }
347:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_HI12: {
348:       i64 val = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
349:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 24);
350:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 23, 12) << 10;
351:       break;
352:     }
353:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_LO12:
354:       check(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 0, 1 << 12);
355:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 11, 0) << 10;
356:       break;
357:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_LO12_NC:
358:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 11, 0) << 10;
359:       break;
360:     case R_AARCH64_TLSGD_ADR_PAGE21: {
361:       i64 val = page(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A) - page(P);
362:       check(val, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
363:       write_adrp(loc, val);
364:       break;
365:     }
366:     case R_AARCH64_TLSGD_ADD_LO12_NC:
367:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A, 11, 0) << 10;
368:       break;
369:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_ADR_PAGE21:
370:       if (sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
371:         i64 val = page(sym.get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx) + A) - page(P);
372:         check(val, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
373:         write_adrp(loc, val);
374:       } else {
375:         // adrp x0, 0 -> movz x0, #tls_ofset_hi, lsl #16
376:         i64 val = (S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
377:         check(val, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
378:         *(ul32 *)loc = 0xd2a0'0000 | (bits(val, 32, 16) << 5);
379:       }
380:       break;
381:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_LD64_LO12:
382:       if (sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
383:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx) + A, 11, 3) << 10;
384:       } else {
385:         // ldr x2, [x0] -> movk x0, #tls_ofset_lo
386:         u32 offset_lo = (S + A - ctx.tp_addr) & 0xffff;
387:         *(ul32 *)loc = 0xf280'0000 | (offset_lo << 5);
388:       }
389:       break;
390:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_ADD_LO12:
391:       if (sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
392:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx) + A, 11, 0) << 10;
393:       } else {
394:         // add x0, x0, #0 -> nop
395:         *(ul32 *)loc = 0xd503'201f;
396:       }
397:       break;
398:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_CALL:
399:       if (!sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
400:         // blr x2 -> nop
401:         *(ul32 *)loc = 0xd503'201f;
402:       }
403:       break;
404:     default:
405:       unreachable();
406:     }
407:   }
408: }
409: 
410: template <>
411: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
412:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
413: 
414:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
415:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
416:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
417:       continue;
418: 
419:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
420:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
421: 
422:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
423:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
424:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
425:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
426:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
427:     };
428: 
429:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
430:     i64 frag_addend;
431:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
432: 
433:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
434:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
435: 
436:     switch (rel.r_type) {
437:     case R_AARCH64_ABS64:
438:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
439:         *(ul64 *)loc = *val;
440:       else
441:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A;
442:       break;
443:     case R_AARCH64_ABS32: {
444:       i64 val = S + A;
445:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
446:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
447:       break;
448:     }
449:     default:
450:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
451:                  << rel;
452:       break;
453:     }
454:   }
455: }
456: 
457: template <>
458: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
459:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
460: 
461:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
462:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
463: 
464:   // Scan relocations
465:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
466:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
467:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
468:       continue;
469: 
470:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
471:     u8 *loc = (u8 *)(contents.data() + rel.r_offset);
472: 
473:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
474:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
475: 
476:     switch (rel.r_type) {
477:     case R_AARCH64_ABS64:
478:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
479:       break;
480:     case R_AARCH64_ADR_GOT_PAGE:
481:       // An ADR_GOT_PAGE and GOT_LO12_NC relocation pair is used to load a
482:       // symbol's address from GOT. If the GOT value is a link-time
483:       // constant, we may be able to rewrite the ADRP+LDR instruction pair
484:       // with an ADRP+ADD, eliminating a GOT memory load.
485:       if (ctx.arg.relax && sym.is_relative() && !sym.is_imported &&
486:           !sym.is_ifunc() && i + 1 < rels.size()) {
487:         // ADRP+LDR must be consecutive and use the same register to relax.
488:         const ElfRel<E> &rel2 = rels[i + 1];
489:         if (rel2.r_type == R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC &&
490:             rel2.r_offset == rel.r_offset + 4 &&
491:             rel2.r_sym == rel.r_sym &&
492:             rel.r_addend == 0 &&
493:             rel2.r_addend == 0 &&
494:             is_adrp(loc) &&
495:             is_ldr(loc + 4)) {
496:           u32 rd = bits(*(ul32 *)loc, 4, 0);
497:           u32 rn = bits(*(ul32 *)(loc + 4), 9, 5);
498:           u32 rt = bits(*(ul32 *)(loc + 4), 4, 0);
499:           if (rd == rn && rn == rt) {
500:             i++;
501:             break;
502:           }
503:         }
504:       }
505:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
506:       break;
507:     case R_AARCH64_LD64_GOT_LO12_NC:
508:     case R_AARCH64_LD64_GOTPAGE_LO15:
509:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
510:       break;
511:     case R_AARCH64_CALL26:
512:     case R_AARCH64_JUMP26:
513:     case R_AARCH64_PLT32:
514:       if (sym.is_imported)
515:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
516:       break;
517:     case R_AARCH64_TLSIE_ADR_GOTTPREL_PAGE21:
518:     case R_AARCH64_TLSIE_LD64_GOTTPREL_LO12_NC:
519:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
520:       break;
521:     case R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21:
522:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
523:       break;
524:     case R_AARCH64_TLSGD_ADR_PAGE21:
525:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
526:       break;
527:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_ADR_PAGE21:
528:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_LD64_LO12:
529:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_ADD_LO12:
530:       if (!relax_tlsdesc(ctx, sym))
531:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSDESC;
532:       break;
533:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G0:
534:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G0_NC:
535:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G1:
536:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G1_NC:
537:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_MOVW_TPREL_G2:
538:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_HI12:
539:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_LO12:
540:     case R_AARCH64_TLSLE_ADD_TPREL_LO12_NC:
541:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
542:       break;
543:     case R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC:
544:     case R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_LO21:
545:     case R_AARCH64_CONDBR19:
546:     case R_AARCH64_LD_PREL_LO19:
547:     case R_AARCH64_LDST16_ABS_LO12_NC:
548:     case R_AARCH64_LDST32_ABS_LO12_NC:
549:     case R_AARCH64_LDST64_ABS_LO12_NC:
550:     case R_AARCH64_LDST128_ABS_LO12_NC:
551:     case R_AARCH64_LDST8_ABS_LO12_NC:
552:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G0:
553:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G0_NC:
554:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G1:
555:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G1_NC:
556:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G2:
557:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G2_NC:
558:     case R_AARCH64_MOVW_UABS_G3:
559:     case R_AARCH64_PREL16:
560:     case R_AARCH64_PREL32:
561:     case R_AARCH64_PREL64:
562:     case R_AARCH64_TLSGD_ADD_LO12_NC:
563:     case R_AARCH64_TLSDESC_CALL:
564:       break;
565:     default:
566:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
567:     }
568:   }
569: }
570: 
571: template <>
572: void RangeExtensionThunk<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
573:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + output_section.shdr.sh_offset + offset;
574: 
575:   static const ul32 data[] = {
576:     0x9000'0010, // adrp x16, 0   # R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21
577:     0x9100'0210, // add  x16, x16 # R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC
578:     0xd61f'0200, // br   x16
579:   };
580: 
581:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(data));
582: 
583:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
584:     u64 S = symbols[i]->get_addr(ctx);
585:     u64 P = output_section.shdr.sh_addr + offset + i * E::thunk_size;
586: 
587:     u8 *loc = buf + i * E::thunk_size;
588:     memcpy(loc , data, sizeof(data));
589:     write_adrp(loc, page(S) - page(P));
590:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 4) |= bits(S, 11, 0) << 10;
591:   }
592: }
593: 
594: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-i386.cc
================
  1: // i386 is similar to x86-64 but lacks PC-relative memory access
  2: // instructions. So it's not straightforward to support position-
  3: // independent code (PIC) on that target.
  4: //
  5: // If an object file is compiled with -fPIC, a function that needs to load
  6: // a value from memory first obtains its own address with the following
  7: // code
  8: //
  9: //   call __x86.get_pc_thunk.bx
 10: //
 11: // where __x86.get_pc_thunk.bx is defined as
 12: //
 13: //   __x86.get_pc_thunk.bx:
 14: //     mov (%esp), %ebx  # move the return address to %ebx
 15: //     ret
 16: //
 17: // . With the function's own address (or, more precisely, the address
 18: // immediately after the call instruction), the function can compute an
 19: // absolute address of a variable with its address + link-time constant.
 20: //
 21: // Executing call-mov-ret isn't very cheap, and allocating one register to
 22: // store PC isn't cheap too, especially given that i386 has only 8
 23: // general-purpose registers. But that's the cost of PIC on i386. You need
 24: // to pay it when creating a .so and a position-independent executable.
 25: //
 26: // When a position-independent function calls another function, it sets
 27: // %ebx to the address of .got. Position-independent PLT entries use that
 28: // register to load values from .got.plt/.got.
 29: //
 30: // If we are creating a position-dependent executable (PDE), we can't
 31: // assume that %ebx is set to .got. For PDE, we need to create position-
 32: // dependent PLT entries which don't use %ebx.
 33: //
 34: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/i386.pdf
 35: 
 36: #include "mold.h"
 37: 
 38: namespace mold::elf {
 39: 
 40: using E = I386;
 41: 
 42: template <>
 43: i64 get_addend(u8 *loc, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
 44:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 45:   case R_386_8:
 46:   case R_386_PC8:
 47:     return *loc;
 48:   case R_386_16:
 49:   case R_386_PC16:
 50:     return *(ul16 *)loc;
 51:   case R_386_32:
 52:   case R_386_PC32:
 53:   case R_386_GOT32:
 54:   case R_386_GOT32X:
 55:   case R_386_PLT32:
 56:   case R_386_GOTOFF:
 57:   case R_386_GOTPC:
 58:   case R_386_TLS_LDM:
 59:   case R_386_TLS_GOTIE:
 60:   case R_386_TLS_LE:
 61:   case R_386_TLS_IE:
 62:   case R_386_TLS_GD:
 63:   case R_386_TLS_LDO_32:
 64:   case R_386_SIZE32:
 65:   case R_386_TLS_GOTDESC:
 66:     return *(ul32 *)loc;
 67:   default:
 68:     return 0;
 69:   }
 70: }
 71: 
 72: template <>
 73: void write_addend(u8 *loc, i64 val, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
 74:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 75:   case R_386_NONE:
 76:     break;
 77:   case R_386_8:
 78:   case R_386_PC8:
 79:     *loc = val;
 80:     break;
 81:   case R_386_16:
 82:   case R_386_PC16:
 83:     *(ul16 *)loc = val;
 84:     break;
 85:   case R_386_32:
 86:   case R_386_PC32:
 87:   case R_386_GOT32:
 88:   case R_386_GOT32X:
 89:   case R_386_PLT32:
 90:   case R_386_GOTOFF:
 91:   case R_386_GOTPC:
 92:   case R_386_TLS_LDM:
 93:   case R_386_TLS_GOTIE:
 94:   case R_386_TLS_LE:
 95:   case R_386_TLS_IE:
 96:   case R_386_TLS_GD:
 97:   case R_386_TLS_LDO_32:
 98:   case R_386_SIZE32:
 99:   case R_386_TLS_GOTDESC:
100:     *(ul32 *)loc = val;
101:     break;
102:   default:
103:     unreachable();
104:   }
105: }
106: 
107: template <>
108: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
109:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
110:     static const u8 insn[] = {
111:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb, // endbr32
112:       0x51,                   // push   %ecx
113:       0x8d, 0x8b, 0, 0, 0, 0, // lea    GOTPLT+4(%ebx), %ecx
114:       0xff, 0x31,             // push   (%ecx)
115:       0xff, 0x61, 0x04,       // jmp    *0x4(%ecx)
116:     };
117:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
118:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 7) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + 4;
119:   } else {
120:     static const u8 insn[] = {
121:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb, // endbr32
122:       0x51,                   // push   %ecx
123:       0xb9, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // mov    GOTPLT+4, %ecx
124:       0xff, 0x31,             // push   (%ecx)
125:       0xff, 0x61, 0x04,       // jmp    *0x4(%ecx)
126:       0xcc,                   // (padding)
127:     };
128:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
129:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr + 4;
130:   }
131: }
132: 
133: template <>
134: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
135:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
136:     static const u8 insn[] = {
137:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb, // endbr32
138:       0xb9, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // mov $reloc_offset, %ecx
139:       0xff, 0xa3, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *foo@GOT(%ebx)
140:       0xcc,                   // (padding)
141:     };
142:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
143:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 5) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
144:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 11) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
145:   } else {
146:     static const u8 insn[] = {
147:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb, // endbr32
148:       0xb9, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // mov $reloc_offset, %ecx
149:       0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *foo@GOT
150:       0xcc,                   // (padding)
151:     };
152:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
153:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 5) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
154:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 11) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx);
155:   }
156: }
157: 
158: template <>
159: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
160:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
161:     static const u8 insn[] = {
162:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb,             // endbr32
163:       0xff, 0xa3, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // jmp *foo@GOT(%ebx)
164:       0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
165:     };
166:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
167:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
168:   } else {
169:     static const u8 insn[] = {
170:       0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfb,             // endbr32
171:       0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // jmp *foo@GOT
172:       0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
173:     };
174:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
175:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx);
176:   }
177: }
178: 
179: template <>
180: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
181:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
182:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
183: 
184:   switch (rel.r_type) {
185:   case R_NONE:
186:     break;
187:   case R_386_32:
188:     *(ul32 *)loc = val;
189:     break;
190:   case R_386_PC32:
191:     *(ul32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
192:     break;
193:   default:
194:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
195:   }
196: }
197: 
198: static u32 relax_got32x(u8 *loc) {
199:   // mov imm(%reg1), %reg2 -> lea imm(%reg1), %reg2
200:   if (loc[0] == 0x8b)
201:     return 0x8d00 | loc[1];
202:   return 0;
203: }
204: 
205: // Relax GD to LE
206: static void relax_gd_to_le(u8 *loc, ElfRel<E> rel, u64 val) {
207:   static const u8 insn[] = {
208:     0x65, 0xa1, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %gs:0, %eax
209:     0x81, 0xc0, 0, 0, 0, 0, // add $tp_offset, %eax
210:   };
211: 
212:   switch (rel.r_type) {
213:   case R_386_PLT32:
214:   case R_386_PC32:
215:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
216:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
217:     break;
218:   case R_386_GOT32:
219:   case R_386_GOT32X:
220:     memcpy(loc - 2, insn, sizeof(insn));
221:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 6) = val;
222:     break;
223:   default:
224:     unreachable();
225:   }
226: }
227: 
228: // Relax LD to LE
229: static void relax_ld_to_le(u8 *loc, ElfRel<E> rel, u64 val) {
230:   switch (rel.r_type) {
231:   case R_386_PLT32:
232:   case R_386_PC32: {
233:     static const u8 insn[] = {
234:       0x65, 0xa1, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %gs:0, %eax
235:       0x2d, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // sub $tls_size, %eax
236:     };
237:     memcpy(loc - 2, insn, sizeof(insn));
238:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
239:     break;
240:   }
241:   case R_386_GOT32:
242:   case R_386_GOT32X: {
243:     static const u8 insn[] = {
244:       0x65, 0xa1, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %gs:0, %eax
245:       0x2d, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // sub $tls_size, %eax
246:       0x90,                   // nop
247:     };
248:     memcpy(loc - 2, insn, sizeof(insn));
249:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
250:     break;
251:   }
252:   default:
253:     unreachable();
254:   }
255: }
256: 
257: template <>
258: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
259:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
260: 
261:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
262:   if (ctx.reldyn)
263:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
264:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
265: 
266:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
267:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
268:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
269:       continue;
270: 
271:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
272:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
273: 
274:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
275:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
276:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
277:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
278:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
279:     };
280: 
281:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
282:     u64 A = get_addend(*this, rel);
283:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
284:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
285:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
286: 
287:     switch (rel.r_type) {
288:     case R_386_8:
289:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
290:       *loc = S + A;
291:       break;
292:     case R_386_16:
293:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
294:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A;
295:       break;
296:     case R_386_32:
297:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
298:       break;
299:     case R_386_PC8:
300:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 7), 1 << 7);
301:       *loc = S + A - P;
302:       break;
303:     case R_386_PC16:
304:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
305:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A - P;
306:       break;
307:     case R_386_PC32:
308:     case R_386_PLT32:
309:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P;
310:       break;
311:     case R_386_GOT32:
312:       *(ul32 *)loc = G + A;
313:       break;
314:     case R_386_GOT32X:
315:       if (sym.has_got(ctx)) {
316:         *(ul32 *)loc = G + A;
317:       } else {
318:         u32 insn = relax_got32x(loc - 2);
319:         assert(insn);
320:         loc[-2] = insn >> 8;
321:         loc[-1] = insn;
322:         *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
323:       }
324:       break;
325:     case R_386_GOTOFF:
326:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
327:       break;
328:     case R_386_GOTPC:
329:       *(ul32 *)loc = GOT + A - P;
330:       break;
331:     case R_386_TLS_GOTIE:
332:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
333:       break;
334:     case R_386_TLS_LE:
335:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
336:       break;
337:     case R_386_TLS_IE:
338:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A;
339:       break;
340:     case R_386_TLS_GD:
341:       if (sym.has_tlsgd(ctx)) {
342:         *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
343:       } else {
344:         relax_gd_to_le(loc, rels[i + 1], S - ctx.tp_addr);
345:         i++;
346:       }
347:       break;
348:     case R_386_TLS_LDM:
349:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx)) {
350:         *(ul32 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
351:       } else {
352:         relax_ld_to_le(loc, rels[i + 1], ctx.tp_addr - ctx.tls_begin);
353:         i++;
354:       }
355:       break;
356:     case R_386_TLS_LDO_32:
357:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
358:       break;
359:     case R_386_SIZE32:
360:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.esym().st_size + A;
361:       break;
362:     case R_386_TLS_GOTDESC:
363:       if (sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
364:         *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
365:       } else {
366:         static const u8 insn[] = {
367:           0x8d, 0x05, 0, 0, 0, 0, // lea 0, %eax
368:         };
369:         memcpy(loc - 2, insn, sizeof(insn));
370:         *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
371:       }
372:       break;
373:     case R_386_TLS_DESC_CALL:
374:       if (!sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
375:         // call *(%eax) -> nop
376:         loc[0] = 0x66;
377:         loc[1] = 0x90;
378:       }
379:       break;
380:     default:
381:       unreachable();
382:     }
383:   }
384: }
385: 
386: template <>
387: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
388:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
389: 
390:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
391:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
392:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
393:       continue;
394: 
395:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
396:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
397: 
398:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
399:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
400:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
401:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
402:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
403:     };
404: 
405:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
406:     i64 frag_addend;
407:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
408: 
409:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
410:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : get_addend(*this, rel);
411:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
412: 
413:     switch (rel.r_type) {
414:     case R_386_8:
415:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
416:       *loc = S + A;
417:       break;
418:     case R_386_16:
419:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
420:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A;
421:       break;
422:     case R_386_32:
423:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
424:         *(ul32 *)loc = *val;
425:       else
426:         *(ul32 *)loc = S + A;
427:       break;
428:     case R_386_PC8:
429:       check(S + A, -(1 << 7), 1 << 7);
430:       *loc = S + A;
431:       break;
432:     case R_386_PC16:
433:       check(S + A, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
434:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A;
435:       break;
436:     case R_386_PC32:
437:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A;
438:       break;
439:     case R_386_GOTPC:
440:       *(ul32 *)loc = GOT + A;
441:       break;
442:     case R_386_GOTOFF:
443:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
444:       break;
445:     case R_386_TLS_LDO_32:
446:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
447:         *(ul32 *)loc = *val;
448:       else
449:         *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
450:       break;
451:     case R_386_SIZE32:
452:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.esym().st_size + A;
453:       break;
454:     default:
455:       unreachable();
456:     }
457:   }
458: }
459: 
460: template <>
461: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
462:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
463: 
464:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
465:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
466: 
467:   // Scan relocations
468:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
469:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
470:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
471:       continue;
472: 
473:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
474:     u8 *loc = (u8 *)(contents.data() + rel.r_offset);
475: 
476:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
477:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
478: 
479:     switch (rel.r_type) {
480:     case R_386_8:
481:     case R_386_16:
482:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
483:       break;
484:     case R_386_32:
485:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
486:       break;
487:     case R_386_PC8:
488:     case R_386_PC16:
489:     case R_386_PC32:
490:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
491:       break;
492:     case R_386_GOT32:
493:     case R_386_GOTPC:
494:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
495:       break;
496:     case R_386_GOT32X: {
497:       // We always want to relax GOT32X because static PIE doesn't
498:       // work without it.
499:       bool do_relax = !sym.is_imported && sym.is_relative() &&
500:                       relax_got32x(loc - 2);
501:       if (!do_relax)
502:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
503:       break;
504:     }
505:     case R_386_PLT32:
506:       if (sym.is_imported)
507:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
508:       break;
509:     case R_386_TLS_GOTIE:
510:     case R_386_TLS_IE:
511:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
512:       break;
513:     case R_386_TLS_GD:
514:       if (i + 1 == rels.size())
515:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLS_GD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOT32";
516: 
517:       if (u32 ty = rels[i + 1].r_type;
518:           ty != R_386_PLT32 && ty != R_386_PC32 &&
519:           ty != R_386_GOT32 && ty != R_386_GOT32X)
520:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLS_GD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOT32";
521: 
522:       // We always relax if -static because libc.a doesn't contain
523:       // __tls_get_addr().
524:       if (ctx.arg.is_static ||
525:           (ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared && !sym.is_imported))
526:         i++;
527:       else
528:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
529:       break;
530:     case R_386_TLS_LDM:
531:       if (i + 1 == rels.size())
532:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLS_LDM reloc must be followed by PLT or GOT32";
533: 
534:       if (u32 ty = rels[i + 1].r_type;
535:           ty != R_386_PLT32 && ty != R_386_PC32 &&
536:           ty != R_386_GOT32 && ty != R_386_GOT32X)
537:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLS_LDM reloc must be followed by PLT or GOT32";
538: 
539:       // We always relax if -static because libc.a doesn't contain
540:       // __tls_get_addr().
541:       if (ctx.arg.is_static || (ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared))
542:         i++;
543:       else
544:         ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
545:       break;
546:     case R_386_TLS_GOTDESC:
547:       if (!relax_tlsdesc(ctx, sym))
548:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSDESC;
549:       break;
550:     case R_386_TLS_LE:
551:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
552:       break;
553:     case R_386_GOTOFF:
554:     case R_386_TLS_LDO_32:
555:     case R_386_SIZE32:
556:     case R_386_TLS_DESC_CALL:
557:       break;
558:     default:
559:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
560:     }
561:   }
562: }
563: 
564: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-m68k.cc
================
  1: // This file contains code for the Motorola 68000 series microprocessors,
  2: // which is often abbreviated as m68k. Running a Unix-like system on a
  3: // m68k-based machine today is probably a retro-computing hobby activity,
  4: // but the processor was a popular choice to build Unix computers during
  5: // '80s. Early Sun workstations for example used m68k. Macintosh until
  6: // 1994 were based on m68k as well until they switched to PowerPC (and
  7: // then to x86 and to ARM.)
  8: //
  9: // From the linker's point of view, it is not hard to support m68k. It's
 10: // just a 32-bit big-endian CISC ISA. Compared to comtemporary i386,
 11: // m68k's psABI is actually simpler because m68k has PC-relative memory
 12: // access instructions and therefore can support position-independent
 13: // code without too much hassle.
 14: //
 15: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/m68k.pdf
 16: 
 17: #include "mold.h"
 18: 
 19: namespace mold::elf {
 20: 
 21: using E = M68K;
 22: 
 23: template <>
 24: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 25:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 26:     0x2f, 0x00,                         // move.l %d0, -(%sp)
 27:     0x2f, 0x3b, 0x01, 0x70, 0, 0, 0, 0, // move.l (GOTPLT+4, %pc), -(%sp)
 28:     0x4e, 0xfb, 0x01, 0x71, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp    ([GOTPLT+8, %pc])
 29:   };
 30: 
 31:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 32:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 6) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr;
 33:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 14) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 4;
 34: }
 35: 
 36: template <>
 37: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 38:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 39:     0x20, 0x3c, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // move.l PLT_OFFSET, %d0
 40:     0x4e, 0xfb, 0x01, 0x71, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp    ([GOTPLT_ENTRY, %pc])
 41:   };
 42: 
 43:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 44:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 2) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
 45:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 10) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 8;
 46: }
 47: 
 48: template <>
 49: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 50:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 51:     0x4e, 0xfb, 0x01, 0x71, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp ([GOT_ENTRY, %pc])
 52:   };
 53: 
 54:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 55:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 4) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 2;
 56: }
 57: 
 58: template <>
 59: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
 60:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
 61:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
 62: 
 63:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 64:   case R_NONE:
 65:     break;
 66:   case R_68K_32:
 67:     *(ub32 *)loc = val;
 68:     break;
 69:   case R_68K_PC32:
 70:     *(ub32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
 71:     break;
 72:   default:
 73:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
 74:   }
 75: }
 76: 
 77: template <>
 78: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
 79:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
 80: 
 81:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
 82:   if (ctx.reldyn)
 83:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
 84:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
 85: 
 86:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
 87:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
 88:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
 89:       continue;
 90: 
 91:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
 92:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
 93: 
 94:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
 95:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
 96:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
 97:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
 98:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
 99:     };
100: 
101:     auto write16 = [&](u64 val) {
102:       check(val, 0, 1 << 16);
103:       *(ub16 *)loc = val;
104:     };
105: 
106:     auto write16s = [&](u64 val) {
107:       check(val, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
108:       *(ub16 *)loc = val;
109:     };
110: 
111:     auto write8 = [&](u64 val) {
112:       check(val, 0, 1 << 8);
113:       *loc = val;
114:     };
115: 
116:     auto write8s = [&](u64 val) {
117:       check(val, -(1 << 7), 1 << 7);
118:       *loc = val;
119:     };
120: 
121:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
122:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
123:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
124:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
125:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
126: 
127:     switch (rel.r_type) {
128:     case R_68K_32:
129:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
130:       break;
131:     case R_68K_16:
132:       write16(S + A);
133:       break;
134:     case R_68K_8:
135:       write8(S + A);
136:       break;
137:     case R_68K_PC32:
138:     case R_68K_PLT32:
139:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - P;
140:       break;
141:     case R_68K_PC16:
142:     case R_68K_PLT16:
143:       write16s(S + A - P);
144:       break;
145:     case R_68K_PC8:
146:     case R_68K_PLT8:
147:       write8s(S + A - P);
148:       break;
149:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL32:
150:       *(ub32 *)loc = GOT + A - P;
151:       break;
152:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL16:
153:       write16s(GOT + A - P);
154:       break;
155:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL8:
156:       write8s(GOT + A - P);
157:       break;
158:     case R_68K_GOTOFF32:
159:       *(ub32 *)loc = G + A;
160:       break;
161:     case R_68K_GOTOFF16:
162:       write16(G + A);
163:       break;
164:     case R_68K_GOTOFF8:
165:       write8(G + A);
166:       break;
167:     case R_68K_TLS_GD32:
168:       *(ub32 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
169:       break;
170:     case R_68K_TLS_GD16:
171:       write16(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
172:       break;
173:     case R_68K_TLS_GD8:
174:       write8(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
175:       break;
176:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM32:
177:       *(ub32 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
178:       break;
179:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM16:
180:       write16(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
181:       break;
182:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM8:
183:       write8(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
184:       break;
185:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO32:
186:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
187:       break;
188:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO16:
189:       write16s(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
190:       break;
191:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO8:
192:       write8s(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
193:       break;
194:     case R_68K_TLS_IE32:
195:       *(ub32 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
196:       break;
197:     case R_68K_TLS_IE16:
198:       write16(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
199:       break;
200:     case R_68K_TLS_IE8:
201:       write8(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
202:       break;
203:     case R_68K_TLS_LE32:
204:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
205:       break;
206:     case R_68K_TLS_LE16:
207:       write16(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
208:       break;
209:     case R_68K_TLS_LE8:
210:       write8(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
211:       break;
212:     default:
213:       unreachable();
214:     }
215:   }
216: }
217: 
218: template <>
219: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
220:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
221: 
222:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
223:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
224:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
225:       continue;
226: 
227:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
228:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
229: 
230:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
231:     i64 frag_addend;
232:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
233: 
234:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
235:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
236: 
237:     switch (rel.r_type) {
238:     case R_68K_32:
239:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
240:         *(ub32 *)loc = *val;
241:       else
242:         *(ub32 *)loc = S + A;
243:       break;
244:     default:
245:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
246:                  << rel;
247:     }
248:   }
249: }
250: 
251: template <>
252: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
253:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
254: 
255:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
256:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
257: 
258:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
259:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
260:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
261:       continue;
262: 
263:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
264: 
265:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
266:       Error(ctx) << sym << ": GNU ifunc symbol is not supported on m68k";
267: 
268:     switch (rel.r_type) {
269:     case R_68K_32:
270:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
271:       break;
272:     case R_68K_16:
273:     case R_68K_8:
274:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
275:       break;
276:     case R_68K_PC32:
277:     case R_68K_PC16:
278:     case R_68K_PC8:
279:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
280:       break;
281:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL32:
282:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL16:
283:     case R_68K_GOTPCREL8:
284:     case R_68K_GOTOFF32:
285:     case R_68K_GOTOFF16:
286:     case R_68K_GOTOFF8:
287:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
288:       break;
289:     case R_68K_PLT32:
290:     case R_68K_PLT16:
291:     case R_68K_PLT8:
292:       if (sym.is_imported)
293:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
294:       break;
295:     case R_68K_TLS_GD32:
296:     case R_68K_TLS_GD16:
297:     case R_68K_TLS_GD8:
298:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
299:       break;
300:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM32:
301:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM16:
302:     case R_68K_TLS_LDM8:
303:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
304:       break;
305:     case R_68K_TLS_IE32:
306:     case R_68K_TLS_IE16:
307:     case R_68K_TLS_IE8:
308:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
309:       break;
310:     case R_68K_TLS_LE32:
311:     case R_68K_TLS_LE16:
312:     case R_68K_TLS_LE8:
313:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
314:       break;
315:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO32:
316:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO16:
317:     case R_68K_TLS_LDO8:
318:       break;
319:     default:
320:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
321:     }
322:   }
323: }
324: 
325: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-ppc32.cc
================
  1: // This file implements the PowerPC 32-bit ISA. For 64-bit PowerPC, see
  2: // arch-ppc64v1.cpp and arch-ppc64v2.cpp.
  3: //
  4: // PPC32 is a RISC ISA. It has 32 general-purpose registers (GPRs).
  5: // r0, r11 and r12 are reserved for static linkers, so we can use these
  6: // registers in PLTs and range extension thunks. In addition to that, it
  7: // has a few special registers. Notable ones are LR which holds a return
  8: // address and CTR which we can use to store a branch target address.
  9: //
 10: // It feels that the PPC32 psABI is unnecessarily complicated at first
 11: // glance, but that is mainly stemmed from the fact that the ISA lacks
 12: // PC-relative load/store instructions. Since machine instructions cannot
 13: // load data relative to its own address, it is not straightforward to
 14: // support position-independent code (PIC) on PPC32.
 15: //
 16: // A position-independent function typically contains the following code
 17: // in the prologue to obtain its own address:
 18: //
 19: //    mflr  r0        // save the current return address to %r0
 20: //    bcl   20, 31, 4 // call the next instruction as if it were a function
 21: //    mtlr  r12       // save the return address to %r12
 22: //    mtlr  r0        // restore the original return address
 23: //
 24: // An object file compiled with -fPIC contains a data section named
 25: // `.got2` to store addresses of locally-defined global variables and
 26: // constants. A PIC function usually computes its .got2+0x8000 and set it
 27: // to %r30. This scheme allows the function to access global objects
 28: // defined in the same input file with a single %r30-relative load/store
 29: // instruction with a 16-bit offset, given that .got2 is smaller than
 30: // 0x10000 (or 65536) bytes.
 31: //
 32: // Since each object file has its own .got2, %r30 refers to different
 33: // places in a merged .got2 for two functions that came from different
 34: // input files. Therefore, %r30 makes sense only within a single function.
 35: //
 36: // Technically, we can reuse a %r30 value in our PLT if we create a PLT
 37: // _for each input file_ (that's what GNU ld seems to be doing), but that
 38: // doesn't seems to be worth its complexity. Our PLT simply doesn't rely
 39: // on a %r30 value.
 40: //
 41: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/ppc32.pdf
 42: 
 43: #include "mold.h"
 44: 
 45: namespace mold::elf {
 46: 
 47: using E = PPC32;
 48: 
 49: static u64 lo(u64 x)    { return x & 0xffff; }
 50: static u64 hi(u64 x)    { return x >> 16; }
 51: static u64 ha(u64 x)    { return (x + 0x8000) >> 16; }
 52: static u64 high(u64 x)  { return (x >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 53: static u64 higha(u64 x) { return ((x + 0x8000) >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 54: 
 55: template <>
 56: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 57:   static const ub32 insn[] = {
 58:     // Get the address of this PLT section
 59:     0x7c08'02a6, //    mflr    r0
 60:     0x429f'0005, //    bcl     20, 31, 4
 61:     0x7d88'02a6, // 1: mflr    r12
 62:     0x7c08'03a6, //    mtlr    r0
 63: 
 64:     // Compute the runtime address of GOTPLT+12
 65:     0x3d8c'0000, //    addis   r12, r12, (GOTPLT - 1b)@higha
 66:     0x398c'0000, //    addi    r12, r12, (GOTPLT - 1b)@lo
 67: 
 68:     // Compute the PLT entry offset
 69:     0x7d6c'5850, //    sub     r11, r11, r12
 70:     0x1d6b'0003, //    mulli   r11, r11, 3
 71: 
 72:     // Load GOTPLT[2] and branch to GOTPLT[1]
 73:     0x800c'fff8, //    lwz     r0,  -8(r12)
 74:     0x7c09'03a6, //    mtctr   r0
 75:     0x818c'fffc, //    lwz     r12, -4(r12)
 76:     0x4e80'0420, //    bctr
 77:     0x6000'0000, //    nop
 78:     0x6000'0000, //    nop
 79:     0x6000'0000, //    nop
 80:     0x6000'0000, //    nop
 81:   };
 82: 
 83:   static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_hdr_size);
 84:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 85: 
 86:   ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)buf;
 87:   loc[4] |= higha(ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr + 4);
 88:   loc[5] |= lo(ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr + 4);
 89: }
 90: 
 91: static const ub32 plt_entry[] = {
 92:   // Get the address of this PLT entry
 93:   0x7c08'02a6, // mflr    r0
 94:   0x429f'0005, // bcl     20, 31, 4
 95:   0x7d88'02a6, // mflr    r12
 96:   0x7c08'03a6, // mtlr    r0
 97: 
 98:   // Load an address from the GOT/GOTPLT entry and jump to that address
 99:   0x3d6c'0000, // addis   r11, r12, OFFSET@higha
100:   0x396b'0000, // addi    r11, r11, OFFSET@lo
101:   0x818b'0000, // lwz     r12, 0(r11)
102:   0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr   r12
103:   0x4e80'0420, // bctr
104: };
105: 
106: template <>
107: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
108:   static_assert(E::plt_size == sizeof(plt_entry));
109:   memcpy(buf, plt_entry, sizeof(plt_entry));
110: 
111:   ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)buf;
112:   i64 offset = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 8;
113:   loc[4] |= higha(offset);
114:   loc[5] |= lo(offset);
115: }
116: 
117: template <>
118: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
119:   static_assert(E::pltgot_size == sizeof(plt_entry));
120:   memcpy(buf, plt_entry, sizeof(plt_entry));
121: 
122:   ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)buf;
123:   i64 offset = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 8;
124:   loc[4] |= higha(offset);
125:   loc[5] |= lo(offset);
126: }
127: 
128: template <>
129: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
130:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
131:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
132: 
133:   switch (rel.r_type) {
134:   case R_NONE:
135:     break;
136:   case R_PPC_ADDR32:
137:     *(ub32 *)loc = val;
138:     break;
139:   case R_PPC_REL32:
140:     *(ub32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
141:     break;
142:   default:
143:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
144:   }
145: }
146: 
147: template <>
148: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
149:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
150: 
151:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
152:   if (ctx.reldyn)
153:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
154:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
155: 
156:   u64 GOT2 = file.ppc32_got2 ? file.ppc32_got2->get_addr() : 0;
157: 
158:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
159:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
160:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
161:       continue;
162: 
163:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
164:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
165: 
166:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
167:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
168:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
169:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
170:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
171: 
172:     switch (rel.r_type) {
173:     case R_PPC_ADDR32:
174:     case R_PPC_UADDR32:
175:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
176:       break;
177:     case R_PPC_ADDR14:
178:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 15, 2) << 2;
179:       break;
180:     case R_PPC_ADDR16:
181:     case R_PPC_UADDR16:
182:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_LO:
183:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A);
184:       break;
185:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_HI:
186:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(S + A);
187:       break;
188:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_HA:
189:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A);
190:       break;
191:     case R_PPC_ADDR24:
192:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 25, 2) << 2;
193:       break;
194:     case R_PPC_ADDR30:
195:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 31, 2) << 2;
196:       break;
197:     case R_PPC_PLT16_LO:
198:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(G + GOT - A - GOT2);
199:       break;
200:     case R_PPC_PLT16_HI:
201:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(G + GOT - A - GOT2);
202:       break;
203:     case R_PPC_PLT16_HA:
204:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(G + GOT - A - GOT2);
205:       break;
206:     case R_PPC_PLT32:
207:       *(ub32 *)loc = G + GOT - A - GOT2;
208:       break;
209:     case R_PPC_REL14:
210:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 15, 2) << 2;
211:       break;
212:     case R_PPC_REL16:
213:     case R_PPC_REL16_LO:
214:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - P);
215:       break;
216:     case R_PPC_REL16_HI:
217:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(S + A - P);
218:       break;
219:     case R_PPC_REL16_HA:
220:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - P);
221:       break;
222:     case R_PPC_REL24:
223:     case R_PPC_LOCAL24PC: {
224:       i64 val = S + A - P;
225:       if (sign_extend(val, 25) != val)
226:         val = get_thunk_addr(i) - P;
227:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
228:       break;
229:     }
230:     case R_PPC_PLTREL24: {
231:       i64 val = S - P;
232:       if (sym.has_plt(ctx) || sign_extend(val, 25) != val)
233:         val = get_thunk_addr(i) - P;
234:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
235:       break;
236:     }
237:     case R_PPC_REL32:
238:     case R_PPC_PLTREL32:
239:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - P;
240:       break;
241:     case R_PPC_GOT16:
242:     case R_PPC_GOT16_LO:
243:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(G + A);
244:       break;
245:     case R_PPC_GOT16_HI:
246:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(G + A);
247:       break;
248:     case R_PPC_GOT16_HA:
249:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(G + A);
250:       break;
251:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_LO:
252:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
253:       break;
254:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_HI:
255:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
256:       break;
257:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_HA:
258:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
259:       break;
260:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_LO:
261:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
262:       break;
263:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_HI:
264:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
265:       break;
266:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_HA:
267:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
268:       break;
269:     case R_PPC_GOT_TLSGD16:
270:       *(ub16 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - GOT;
271:       break;
272:     case R_PPC_GOT_TLSLD16:
273:       *(ub16 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - GOT;
274:       break;
275:     case R_PPC_GOT_TPREL16:
276:       *(ub16 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - GOT;
277:       break;
278:     case R_PPC_TLS:
279:     case R_PPC_TLSGD:
280:     case R_PPC_TLSLD:
281:     case R_PPC_PLTSEQ:
282:     case R_PPC_PLTCALL:
283:       break;
284:     default:
285:       unreachable();
286:     }
287:   }
288: }
289: 
290: template <>
291: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
292:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
293: 
294:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
295:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
296:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
297:       continue;
298: 
299:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
300:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
301: 
302:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
303:     i64 frag_addend;
304:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
305: 
306:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
307:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
308: 
309:     switch (rel.r_type) {
310:     case R_PPC_ADDR32:
311:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
312:         *(ub32 *)loc = *val;
313:       else
314:         *(ub32 *)loc = S + A;
315:       break;
316:     default:
317:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
318:                  << rel;
319:     }
320:   }
321: }
322: 
323: template <>
324: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
325:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
326: 
327:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
328:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
329: 
330:   // Scan relocations
331:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
332:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
333:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
334:       continue;
335: 
336:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
337: 
338:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
339:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
340: 
341:     switch (rel.r_type) {
342:     case R_PPC_ADDR32:
343:     case R_PPC_UADDR32:
344:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
345:       break;
346:     case R_PPC_ADDR14:
347:     case R_PPC_ADDR16:
348:     case R_PPC_UADDR16:
349:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_LO:
350:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_HI:
351:     case R_PPC_ADDR16_HA:
352:     case R_PPC_ADDR24:
353:     case R_PPC_ADDR30:
354:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
355:       break;
356:     case R_PPC_REL14:
357:     case R_PPC_REL16:
358:     case R_PPC_REL16_LO:
359:     case R_PPC_REL16_HI:
360:     case R_PPC_REL16_HA:
361:     case R_PPC_REL32:
362:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
363:       break;
364:     case R_PPC_GOT16:
365:     case R_PPC_GOT16_LO:
366:     case R_PPC_GOT16_HI:
367:     case R_PPC_GOT16_HA:
368:     case R_PPC_PLT16_LO:
369:     case R_PPC_PLT16_HI:
370:     case R_PPC_PLT16_HA:
371:     case R_PPC_PLT32:
372:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
373:       break;
374:     case R_PPC_REL24:
375:     case R_PPC_PLTREL24:
376:     case R_PPC_PLTREL32:
377:       if (sym.is_imported)
378:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
379:       break;
380:     case R_PPC_GOT_TLSGD16:
381:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
382:       break;
383:     case R_PPC_GOT_TLSLD16:
384:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
385:       break;
386:     case R_PPC_GOT_TPREL16:
387:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
388:       break;
389:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_LO:
390:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_HI:
391:     case R_PPC_TPREL16_HA:
392:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
393:       break;
394:     case R_PPC_LOCAL24PC:
395:     case R_PPC_TLS:
396:     case R_PPC_TLSGD:
397:     case R_PPC_TLSLD:
398:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_LO:
399:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_HI:
400:     case R_PPC_DTPREL16_HA:
401:     case R_PPC_PLTSEQ:
402:     case R_PPC_PLTCALL:
403:       break;
404:     default:
405:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
406:     }
407:   }
408: }
409: 
410: template <>
411: void RangeExtensionThunk<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
412:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + output_section.shdr.sh_offset + offset;
413: 
414:   static const ub32 local_thunk[] = {
415:     // Get this thunk's address
416:     0x7c08'02a6, // mflr    r0
417:     0x429f'0005, // bcl     20, 31, 4
418:     0x7d88'02a6, // mflr    r12
419:     0x7c08'03a6, // mtlr    r0
420: 
421:     // Materialize the destination's address in %r11 and jump to that address
422:     0x3d6c'0000, // addis   r11, r12, OFFSET@higha
423:     0x396b'0000, // addi    r11, r11, OFFSET@lo
424:     0x7d69'03a6, // mtctr   r11
425:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
426:     0x6000'0000, // nop
427:   };
428: 
429:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(plt_entry));
430:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(local_thunk));
431: 
432:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
433:     ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)(buf + i * E::thunk_size);
434:     Symbol<E> &sym = *symbols[i];
435: 
436:     if (sym.has_plt(ctx)) {
437:       memcpy(loc, plt_entry, sizeof(plt_entry));
438:       u64 got = sym.has_got(ctx) ? sym.get_got_addr(ctx) : sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx);
439:       i64 val = got - get_addr(i) - 8;
440:       loc[4] |= higha(val);
441:       loc[5] |= lo(val);
442:     } else {
443:       memcpy(loc, local_thunk, sizeof(local_thunk));
444:       i64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx) - get_addr(i) - 8;
445:       loc[4] |= higha(val);
446:       loc[5] |= lo(val);
447:     }
448:   }
449: }
450: 
451: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-ppc64v1.cc
================
  1: // This file contains code for the 64-bit PowerPC ELFv1 ABI that is
  2: // commonly used for big-endian PPC systems. Modern PPC systems that use
  3: // the processor in the little-endian mode use the ELFv2 ABI instead. For
  4: // ELFv2, see arch-ppc64v2.cc.
  5: //
  6: // Even though they are similiar, ELFv1 isn't only different from ELFv2 in
  7: // endianness. The most notable difference is, in ELFv1, a function
  8: // pointer doesn't directly refer the entry point of a function but
  9: // instead refers a data structure so-called "function descriptor".
 10: //
 11: // The function descriptor is essentially a pair of a function entry point
 12: // address and a value that should be set to %r2 before calling that
 13: // function. There is also a third member for "the environment pointer for
 14: // languages such as Pascal and PL/1" according to the psABI, but it looks
 15: // like no one acutally uses it. In total, the function descriptor is 24
 16: // bytes long. Here is why we need it.
 17: //
 18: // PPC generally lacks PC-relative data access instructions. Position-
 19: // independent code sets GOT + 0x8000 to %r2 and access global variables
 20: // relative to %r2.
 21: //
 22: // Each ELF file has its own GOT. If a function calls another function in
 23: // the same ELF file, it doesn't have to reset %r2. However, if it is in
 24: // other file (e.g. other .so), it has to set a new value to %r2 so that
 25: // the register contains the callee's GOT + 0x8000.
 26: //
 27: // In this way, you can't call a function just by knowing the function's
 28: // entry point address. You also need to know a proper %r2 value for the
 29: // function. This is why a function pointer refers a tuple of an address
 30: // and a %r2 value.
 31: //
 32: // If a function call is made through PLT, PLT takes care of restoring %r2.
 33: // Therefore, the caller has to restore %r2 only for function calls
 34: // through function pointers.
 35: //
 36: // .opd (short for "official procedure descriptors") contains function
 37: // descriptors.
 38: //
 39: // You can think OPD as this: even in other targets, a function can have a
 40: // few different addresses for different purposes. It may not only have an
 41: // entry point address but may also have PLT and/or GOT addresses.
 42: // In PPCV1, it may have an OPD address in addition to these. OPD address
 43: // is used for relocations that refers the address of a function as a
 44: // function pointer.
 45: //
 46: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/ppc64v1.pdf
 47: 
 48: #include "mold.h"
 49: 
 50: #include <algorithm>
 51: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 52: 
 53: namespace mold::elf {
 54: 
 55: using E = PPC64V1;
 56: 
 57: static u64 lo(u64 x)    { return x & 0xffff; }
 58: static u64 hi(u64 x)    { return x >> 16; }
 59: static u64 ha(u64 x)    { return (x + 0x8000) >> 16; }
 60: static u64 high(u64 x)  { return (x >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 61: static u64 higha(u64 x) { return ((x + 0x8000) >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 62: 
 63: // .plt is used only for lazy symbol resolution on PPC64. All PLT
 64: // calls are made via range extension thunks even if they are within
 65: // reach. Thunks read addresses from .got.plt and jump there.
 66: // Therefore, once PLT symbols are resolved and final addresses are
 67: // written to .got.plt, thunks just skip .plt and directly jump to the
 68: // resolved addresses.
 69: template <>
 70: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 71:   static const ub32 insn[] = {
 72:     0x7d88'02a6, // mflr    r12
 73:     0x429f'0005, // bcl     20, 31, 4 // obtain PC
 74:     0x7d68'02a6, // mflr    r11
 75:     0xe84b'0024, // ld      r2,36(r11)
 76:     0x7d88'03a6, // mtlr    r12
 77:     0x7d62'5a14, // add     r11,r2,r11
 78:     0xe98b'0000, // ld      r12,0(r11)
 79:     0xe84b'0008, // ld      r2,8(r11)
 80:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr   r12
 81:     0xe96b'0010, // ld      r11,16(r11)
 82:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
 83:     // .quad .got.plt - .plt - 8
 84:     0x0000'0000,
 85:     0x0000'0000,
 86:   };
 87: 
 88:   static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_hdr_size);
 89:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 90:   *(ub64 *)(buf + 44) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 8;
 91: }
 92: 
 93: template <>
 94: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 95:   ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)buf;
 96:   i64 idx = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx);
 97: 
 98:   // The PPC64 ELFv1 ABI requires PLT entries to be vary in size depending
 99:   // on their indices. Unlike other targets, .got.plt is filled not by us
100:   // but by the loader, so we don't have a control over where the initial
101:   // call to the PLT entry jumps to. So we need to strictly follow the PLT
102:   // section layout as the loader expect it to be.
103:   if (idx < 0x8000) {
104:     static const ub32 insn[] = {
105:       0x3800'0000, // li      r0, PLT_INDEX
106:       0x4b00'0000, // b       plt0
107:     };
108: 
109:     memcpy(loc, insn, sizeof(insn));
110:     loc[0] |= idx;
111:     loc[1] |= (ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 4) & 0x00ff'ffff;
112:   } else {
113:     static const ub32 insn[] = {
114:       0x3c00'0000, // lis     r0, PLT_INDEX@high
115:       0x6000'0000, // ori     r0, r0, PLT_INDEX@lo
116:       0x4b00'0000, // b       plt0
117:     };
118: 
119:     memcpy(loc, insn, sizeof(insn));
120:     loc[0] |= high(idx);
121:     loc[1] |= lo(idx);
122:     loc[2] |= (ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 8) & 0x00ff'ffff;
123:   }
124: }
125: 
126: // .plt.got is not necessary on PPC64 because range extension thunks
127: // directly read GOT entries and jump there.
128: template <>
129: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {}
130: 
131: template <>
132: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
133:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
134:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
135: 
136:   switch (rel.r_type) {
137:   case R_NONE:
138:     break;
139:   case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
140:     *(ub64 *)loc = val;
141:     break;
142:   case R_PPC64_REL32:
143:     *(ub32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
144:     break;
145:   case R_PPC64_REL64:
146:     *(ub64 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
147:     break;
148:   default:
149:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
150:   }
151: }
152: 
153: template <>
154: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
155:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
156: 
157:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
158:   if (ctx.reldyn)
159:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
160:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
161: 
162:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
163:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
164:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
165:       continue;
166: 
167:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
168:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
169: 
170:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
171:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
172:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
173:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
174:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
175:     };
176: 
177:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
178:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
179:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
180:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
181:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
182:     u64 TOC = ctx.extra.TOC->value;
183: 
184:     switch (rel.r_type) {
185:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
186:       apply_toc_rel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
187:       break;
188:     case R_PPC64_TOC:
189:       apply_toc_rel(ctx, *ctx.extra.TOC, rel, loc, TOC, A, P, dynrel);
190:       break;
191:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_HA:
192:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - TOC);
193:       break;
194:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO:
195:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - TOC);
196:       break;
197:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_DS:
198:       check(S + A - TOC, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
199:       *(ub16 *)loc |= (S + A - TOC) & 0xfffc;
200:       break;
201:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO_DS:
202:       *(ub16 *)loc |= (S + A - TOC) & 0xfffc;
203:       break;
204:     case R_PPC64_REL24: {
205:       i64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx, NO_OPD) + A - P;
206:       if (sym.has_plt(ctx) || sign_extend(val, 25) != val)
207:         val = get_thunk_addr(i) + A - P;
208: 
209:       check(val, -(1 << 25), 1 << 25);
210:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
211: 
212:       // If a callee is an external function, PLT saves %r2 to the
213:       // caller's r2 save slot. We need to restore it after function
214:       // return. To do so, there's usually a NOP as a placeholder
215:       // after a BL. 0x6000'0000 is a NOP.
216:       if (sym.has_plt(ctx) && *(ub32 *)(loc + 4) == 0x6000'0000)
217:         *(ub32 *)(loc + 4) = 0xe841'0028; // ld r2, 40(r1)
218:       break;
219:     }
220:     case R_PPC64_REL64:
221:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - P;
222:       break;
223:     case R_PPC64_REL16_HA:
224:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - P);
225:       break;
226:     case R_PPC64_REL16_LO:
227:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - P);
228:       break;
229:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HA:
230:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(G + GOT - TOC);
231:       break;
232:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HI:
233:       *(ub16 *)loc = hi(G + GOT - TOC);
234:       break;
235:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO:
236:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(G + GOT - TOC);
237:       break;
238:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO_DS:
239:       *(ub16 *)loc |= (G + GOT - TOC) & 0xfffc;
240:       break;
241:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_HA:
242:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - TOC);
243:       break;
244:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_HA:
245:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - TOC);
246:       break;
247:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_LO:
248:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - TOC);
249:       break;
250:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_HA:
251:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - TOC);
252:       break;
253:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_LO:
254:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - TOC);
255:       break;
256:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_HA:
257:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
258:       break;
259:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_LO:
260:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
261:       break;
262:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_HA:
263:       *(ub16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
264:       break;
265:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_LO:
266:       *(ub16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
267:       break;
268:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_LO_DS:
269:       *(ub16 *)loc |= (sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - TOC) & 0xfffc;
270:       break;
271:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ:
272:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL:
273:     case R_PPC64_TLS:
274:     case R_PPC64_TLSGD:
275:     case R_PPC64_TLSLD:
276:       break;
277:     default:
278:       unreachable();
279:     }
280:   }
281: }
282: 
283: template <>
284: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
285:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
286: 
287:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
288:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
289:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
290:       continue;
291: 
292:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
293:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
294: 
295:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
296:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
297:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
298:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
299:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
300:     };
301: 
302:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
303:     i64 frag_addend;
304:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
305: 
306:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
307:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
308: 
309:     switch (rel.r_type) {
310:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
311:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
312:         *(ub64 *)loc = *val;
313:       else
314:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A;
315:       break;
316:     case R_PPC64_ADDR32: {
317:       i64 val = S + A;
318:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
319:       *(ub32 *)loc = val;
320:       break;
321:     }
322:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL64:
323:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
324:       break;
325:     default:
326:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
327:                  << rel;
328:     }
329:   }
330: }
331: 
332: template <>
333: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
334:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
335: 
336:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
337:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
338: 
339:   // Scan relocations
340:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
341:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
342:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
343:       continue;
344: 
345:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
346: 
347:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
348:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT | NEEDS_PPC_OPD;
349: 
350:     // Any relocation except R_PPC64_REL24 is considered as an
351:     // address-taking relocation.
352:     if (rel.r_type != R_PPC64_REL24 && sym.get_type() == STT_FUNC)
353:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_PPC_OPD;
354: 
355:     switch (rel.r_type) {
356:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
357:     case R_PPC64_TOC:
358:       scan_toc_rel(ctx, sym, rel);
359:       break;
360:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_HA:
361:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
362:       break;
363:     case R_PPC64_REL24:
364:       if (sym.is_imported)
365:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
366:       break;
367:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HA:
368:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
369:       break;
370:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_HA:
371:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
372:       break;
373:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_HA:
374:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
375:       break;
376:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_HA:
377:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_LO:
378:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
379:       break;
380:     case R_PPC64_REL64:
381:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_HA:
382:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO:
383:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO_DS:
384:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_DS:
385:     case R_PPC64_REL16_HA:
386:     case R_PPC64_REL16_LO:
387:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HI:
388:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO:
389:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO_DS:
390:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ:
391:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL:
392:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_LO_DS:
393:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_LO:
394:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_LO:
395:     case R_PPC64_TLS:
396:     case R_PPC64_TLSGD:
397:     case R_PPC64_TLSLD:
398:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_HA:
399:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_LO:
400:       break;
401:     default:
402:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
403:     }
404:   }
405: }
406: 
407: template <>
408: void RangeExtensionThunk<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
409:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + output_section.shdr.sh_offset + offset;
410: 
411:   // If the destination is .plt.got, we save the current r2, read an
412:   // address of a function descriptor from .got, restore %r2 and jump
413:   // to the function.
414:   static const ub32 pltgot_thunk[] = {
415:     // Store the caller's %r2
416:     0xf841'0028, // std   %r2, 40(%r1)
417: 
418:     // Load an address of a function descriptor
419:     0x3d82'0000, // addis %r12, %r2,  foo@got@toc@ha
420:     0xe98c'0000, // ld    %r12, foo@got@toc@lo(%r12)
421: 
422:     // Restore the callee's %r2
423:     0xe84c'0008, // ld    %r2,  8(%r12)
424: 
425:     // Jump to the function
426:     0xe98c'0000, // ld    %r12, 0(%r12)
427:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr %r12
428:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
429:   };
430: 
431:   // If the destination is .plt, read a function descriptor from .got.plt.
432:   static const ub32 plt_thunk[] = {
433:     // Store the caller's %r2
434:     0xf841'0028, // std   %r2, 40(%r1)
435: 
436:     // Materialize an address of a function descriptor
437:     0x3d82'0000, // addis %r12, %r2,  foo@gotplt@toc@ha
438:     0x398c'0000, // addi  %r12, %r12, foo@gotplt@toc@lo
439: 
440:     // Restore the callee's %r2
441:     0xe84c'0008, // ld    %r2,  8(%r12)
442: 
443:     // Jump to the function
444:     0xe98c'0000, // ld    %r12, 0(%r12)
445:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr %r12
446:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
447:   };
448: 
449:   // If the destination is a non-imported function, we directly jump
450:   // to the function entry address.
451:   static const ub32 local_thunk[] = {
452:     0x3d82'0000, // addis r12, r2,  foo@toc@ha
453:     0x398c'0000, // addi  r12, r12, foo@toc@lo
454:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr r12
455:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
456:     0x6000'0000, // nop
457:     0x6000'0000, // nop
458:     0x6000'0000, // nop
459:   };
460: 
461:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(pltgot_thunk));
462:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(plt_thunk));
463:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(local_thunk));
464: 
465:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
466:     Symbol<E> &sym = *symbols[i];
467:     ub32 *loc = (ub32 *)(buf + i * E::thunk_size);
468: 
469:     if (sym.has_got(ctx)) {
470:       memcpy(loc, pltgot_thunk, sizeof(pltgot_thunk));
471:       i64 val = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.extra.TOC->value;
472:       loc[1] |= higha(val);
473:       loc[2] |= lo(val);
474:     } else if(sym.has_plt(ctx)) {
475:       memcpy(loc, plt_thunk, sizeof(plt_thunk));
476:       i64 val = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - ctx.extra.TOC->value;
477:       loc[1] |= higha(val);
478:       loc[2] |= lo(val);
479:     } else {
480:       memcpy(loc, local_thunk, sizeof(local_thunk));
481:       i64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx, NO_OPD) - ctx.extra.TOC->value;
482:       loc[0] |= higha(val);
483:       loc[1] |= lo(val);
484:     }
485:   }
486: }
487: 
488: static InputSection<E> *get_opd_section(ObjectFile<E> &file) {
489:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file.sections)
490:     if (isec && isec->name() == ".opd")
491:       return isec.get();
492:   return nullptr;
493: }
494: 
495: static ElfRel<E> *
496: get_relocation_at(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec, i64 offset) {
497:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels = isec.get_rels(ctx);
498: 
499:   auto it = std::lower_bound(rels.begin(), rels.end(), offset,
500:                              [](const ElfRel<E> &r, i64 offset) {
501:     return r.r_offset < offset;
502:   });
503: 
504:   if (it == rels.end())
505:     return nullptr;
506:   if (it->r_offset != offset)
507:     return nullptr;
508:   return &*it;
509: }
510: 
511: struct OpdSymbol {
512:   bool operator<(const OpdSymbol &x) const { return r_offset < x.r_offset; }
513: 
514:   u64 r_offset = 0;
515:   Symbol<E> *sym = nullptr;
516: };
517: 
518: static Symbol<E> *
519: get_opd_sym_at(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<OpdSymbol> syms, u64 offset) {
520:   auto it = std::lower_bound(syms.begin(), syms.end(), OpdSymbol{offset});
521:   if (it == syms.end())
522:     return nullptr;
523:   if (it->r_offset != offset)
524:     return nullptr;
525:   return it->sym;
526: }
527: 
528: // Compiler creates an .opd entry for each function symbol. The intention
529: // is to make it possible to create an output .opd section just by linking
530: // input .opd sections in the same manner as we do to other normal input
531: // sections.
532: //
533: // However, in reality, .opd isn't a normal input section. It needs many
534: // special treatments as follows:
535: //
536: // 1. A function symbol refers not a .text but an .opd. Its address works
537: //    fine for address-taking relocations such as R_PPC64_ADDR64. However,
538: //    R_PPC64_REL24 (which is used for branch instruction) needs a
539: //    function's real address instead of the function's .opd address.
540: //    We need to read .opd contents to find out a function entry point
541: //    address to apply R_PPC64_REL24.
542: //
543: // 2. Output .opd entries are needed only for functions whose addresses
544: //    are taken. Just copying input .opd sections to an output would
545: //    produces lots of dead .opd entries.
546: //
547: // 3. In this design, all function symbols refer an .opd section, and that
548: //    doesn't work well with graph traversal optimizations such as garbage
549: //    collection or identical comdat folding. For example, garbage
550: //    collector would mark an .opd alive which in turn mark all functions
551: //    thatare referenced by .opd as alive, effectively keeping all
552: //    functions as alive.
553: //
554: // The problem is that the compiler creates a half-baked .opd section, and
555: // the linker has to figure out what all these .opd entries and
556: // relocations are trying to achieve. It's like the compiler would emit a
557: // half-baked .plt section in an object file and the linker has to deal
558: // with that. That's not a good design.
559: //
560: // So, in this function, we undo what the compiler did to .opd. We remove
561: // function symbols from .opd and reattach them to their function entry
562: // points. We also rewrite relocations that directly refer an input .opd
563: // section so that they refer function symbols instead. We then mark input
564: // .opd sections as dead.
565: //
566: // After this function, we mark symbols with the NEEDS_PPC_OPD flag if the
567: // symbol needs an .opd entry. We then create an output .opd just like we
568: // do for .plt or .got.
569: void ppc64v1_rewrite_opd(Context<E> &ctx) {
570:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
571:     InputSection<E> *opd = get_opd_section(*file);
572:     if (!opd)
573:       return;
574:     opd->is_alive = false;
575: 
576:     // Move symbols from .opd to .text.
577:     std::vector<OpdSymbol> opd_syms;
578: 
579:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->symbols) {
580:       if (sym->file != file || sym->get_input_section() != opd)
581:         continue;
582: 
583:       if (u32 ty = sym->get_type(); ty != STT_FUNC && ty != STT_GNU_IFUNC)
584:         continue;
585: 
586:       ElfRel<E> *rel = get_relocation_at(ctx, *opd, sym->value);
587:       if (!rel)
588:         Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": cannot find a relocation in .opd for "
589:                    << *sym << " at offset 0x" << std::hex << (u64)sym->value;
590: 
591:       Symbol<E> *sym2 = file->symbols[rel->r_sym];
592:       if (sym2->get_type() != STT_SECTION)
593:         Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": bad relocation in .opd referring " << *sym2;
594: 
595:       opd_syms.push_back({sym->value, sym});
596: 
597:       sym->set_input_section(sym2->get_input_section());
598:       sym->value = rel->r_addend;
599:     }
600: 
601:     // Sort symbols so that get_opd_sym_at() can do binary search.
602:     sort(opd_syms);
603: 
604:     // Rewrite relocations so that they directly refer to .opd.
605:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
606:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive || isec.get() == opd)
607:         continue;
608:
