609:       for (ElfRel<E> &r : isec->get_rels(ctx)) {
610:         Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[r.r_sym];
611:         if (sym.get_input_section() != opd)
612:           continue;
613: 
614:         Symbol<E> *real_sym = get_opd_sym_at(ctx, opd_syms, r.r_addend);
615:         if (!real_sym)
616:           Fatal(ctx) << *isec << ": cannot find a symbol in .opd for " << r
617:                      << " at offset 0x" << std::hex << (u64)r.r_addend;
618: 
619:         r.r_sym = real_sym->sym_idx;
620:         r.r_addend = 0;
621:       }
622:     }
623:   });
624: }
625: 
626: // When a function is exported, the dynamic symbol for the function should
627: // refers the function's .opd entry. This function marks such symbols with
628: // NEEDS_PPC_OPD.
629: void ppc64v1_scan_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
630:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
631:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->symbols)
632:       if (sym->file == file && sym->is_exported)
633:         if (u32 ty = sym->get_type(); ty == STT_FUNC || ty == STT_GNU_IFUNC)
634:           sym->flags |= NEEDS_PPC_OPD;
635:   });
636: 
637:   // Functions referenced by the ELF header also have to have .opd entries.
638:   auto mark = [&](std::string_view name) {
639:     if (!name.empty())
640:       if (Symbol<E> &sym = *get_symbol(ctx, name); !sym.is_imported)
641:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PPC_OPD;
642:   };
643: 
644:   mark(ctx.arg.entry);
645:   mark(ctx.arg.init);
646:   mark(ctx.arg.fini);
647: }
648: 
649: void PPC64OpdSection::add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
650:   sym->set_opd_idx(ctx, symbols.size());
651:   symbols.push_back(sym);
652:   this->shdr.sh_size += ENTRY_SIZE;
653: }
654: 
655: i64 PPC64OpdSection::get_reldyn_size(Context<E> &ctx) const {
656:   if (ctx.arg.pic)
657:     return symbols.size() * 2;
658:   return 0;
659: }
660: 
661: void PPC64OpdSection::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
662:   ub64 *buf = (ub64 *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
663: 
664:   ElfRel<E> *rel = nullptr;
665:   if (ctx.arg.pic)
666:     rel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset + reldyn_offset);
667: 
668:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols) {
669:     u64 addr = sym->get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT | NO_OPD);
670:     *buf++ = addr;
671:     *buf++ = ctx.extra.TOC->value;
672:     *buf++ = 0;
673: 
674:     if (ctx.arg.pic) {
675:       u64 loc = sym->get_opd_addr(ctx);
676:       *rel++ = ElfRel<E>(loc, E::R_RELATIVE, 0, addr);
677:       *rel++ = ElfRel<E>(loc + 8, E::R_RELATIVE, 0, ctx.extra.TOC->value);
678:     }
679:   }
680: }
681: 
682: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-s390x.cc
================
  1: // This file contains code for the IBM z/Architecture 64-bit ISA, which is
  2: // commonly referred to as "s390x" on Linux.
  3: //
  4: // z/Architecture is a 64-bit CISC ISA developed by IBM around 2000 for
  5: // IBM's "big iron" mainframe computers. The computers are direct
  6: // descendents of IBM System/360 all the way back in 1966. I've never
  7: // actually seen a mainframe, and you probaly haven't either, but it looks
  8: // like the mainframe market is still large enough to sustain its ecosystem.
  9: // Ubuntu for example provides the official support for s390x as of 2022.
 10: // Since they are being actively maintained, we need to support them.
 11: //
 12: // As an instruction set, s390x isn't particularly odd. It has 16 general-
 13: // purpose registers. Instructions are 2, 4 or 6 bytes long and always
 14: // aligned to 2 bytes boundaries. Despite unfamiliarty, I found that it
 15: // just feels like an x86-64 in a parallel universe.
 16: //
 17: // Here is the register usage in this ABI:
 18: //
 19: //   r0-r1: reserved as scratch registers so we can use them in our PLT
 20: //   r2:    parameter passing and return values
 21: //   r3-r6: parameter passing
 22: //   r12:   address of GOT if position-independent code
 23: //   r14:   return address
 24: //   r15:   stack pointer
 25: //   a1:    upper 32 bits of TP (thread pointer)
 26: //   a2:    lower 32 bits of TP (thread pointer)
 27: //
 28: // Thread-local storage (TLS) is supported on s390x in the same way as it
 29: // is on other targets with one exeption. On other targets, __tls_get_addr
 30: // is used to get an address of a thread-local variable. On s390x,
 31: // __tls_get_offset is used instead. The difference is __tls_get_offset
 32: // returns an address of a thread-local variable as an offset from TP. So
 33: // we need to add TP to a return value before use. I don't know why it is
 34: // different, but that is the way it is.
 35: //
 36: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/s390x.pdf
 37: 
 38: #include "mold.h"
 39: 
 40: namespace mold::elf {
 41: 
 42: using E = S390X;
 43: 
 44: static void write_mid20(u8 *loc, u64 val) {
 45:   *(ub32 *)loc |= (bits(val, 11, 0) << 16) | (bits(val, 19, 12) << 8);
 46: }
 47: 
 48: template <>
 49: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 50:   static u8 insn[] = {
 51:     0xe3, 0x00, 0xf0, 0x38, 0x00, 0x24, // stg   %r0, 56(%r15)
 52:     0xc0, 0x10, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // larl  %r1, GOTPLT_OFFSET
 53:     0xd2, 0x07, 0xf0, 0x30, 0x10, 0x08, // mvc   48(8, %r15), 8(%r1)
 54:     0xe3, 0x10, 0x10, 0x10, 0x00, 0x04, // lg    %r1, 16(%r1)
 55:     0x07, 0xf1,                         // br    %r1
 56:     0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, // nopr; nopr; nopr
 57:   };
 58: 
 59:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 60:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 8) = (ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 6) >> 1;
 61: }
 62: 
 63: template <>
 64: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 65:   static u8 insn[] = {
 66:     0xc0, 0x10, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // larl  %r1, GOTPLT_ENTRY_OFFSET
 67:     0xe3, 0x10, 0x10, 0x00, 0x00, 0x04, // lg    %r1, (%r1)
 68:     0xc0, 0x01, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // lgfi  %r0, PLT_INDEX
 69:     0x07, 0xf1,                         // br    %r1
 70:     0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, // nopr; nopr; nopr
 71:     0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, 0x07, 0x00, // nopr; nopr; nopr
 72:   };
 73: 
 74:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 75:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 2) = (sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx)) >> 1;
 76:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 14) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
 77: }
 78: 
 79: template <>
 80: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 81:   static u8 insn[] = {
 82:     0xc0, 0x10, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // larl  %r1, GOT_ENTRY_OFFSET
 83:     0xe3, 0x10, 0x10, 0x00, 0x00, 0x04, // lg    %r1, (%r1)
 84:     0x07, 0xf1,                         // br    %r1
 85:     0x07, 0x00,                         // nopr
 86:   };
 87: 
 88:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 89:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 2) = (sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx)) >> 1;
 90: }
 91: 
 92: template <>
 93: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
 94:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
 95:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
 96: 
 97:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 98:   case R_NONE:
 99:     break;
100:   case R_390_PC32:
101:     *(ub32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
102:     break;
103:   case R_390_64:
104:     *(ub64 *)loc = val;
105:     break;
106:   default:
107:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
108:   }
109: }
110: 
111: template <>
112: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
113:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
114: 
115:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
116:   if (ctx.reldyn)
117:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
118:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
119: 
120:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
121:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
122:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
123:       continue;
124: 
125:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
126:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
127: 
128:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
129:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
130:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
131:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
132:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
133:     };
134: 
135:     auto check_dbl = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
136:       check(val, lo, hi);
137: 
138:       // R_390_*DBL relocs should never refer a symbol at an odd address
139:       if (val & 1)
140:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": misaligned symbol " << sym
141:                    << " for relocation " << rel;
142:     };
143: 
144:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
145:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
146:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
147:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
148:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
149: 
150:     switch (rel.r_type) {
151:     case R_390_64:
152:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
153:       break;
154:     case R_390_8:
155:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
156:       *loc = S + A;
157:       break;
158:     case R_390_12:
159:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 12);
160:       *(ul16 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 11, 0);
161:       break;
162:     case R_390_16:
163:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
164:       *(ub16 *)loc = S + A;
165:       break;
166:     case R_390_20:
167:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 20);
168:       write_mid20(loc, S + A);
169:       break;
170:     case R_390_32:
171:     case R_390_PLT32:
172:       check(S + A, 0, 1LL << 32);
173:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A;
174:       break;
175:     case R_390_PLT64:
176:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A;
177:       break;
178:     case R_390_PC12DBL:
179:     case R_390_PLT12DBL:
180:       check_dbl(S + A - P, -(1 << 12), 1 << 12);
181:       *(ul16 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 12, 1);
182:       break;
183:     case R_390_PC16:
184:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
185:       *(ub16 *)loc = S + A - P;
186:       break;
187:     case R_390_PC32:
188:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
189:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - P;
190:       break;
191:     case R_390_PC64:
192:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - P;
193:       break;
194:     case R_390_PC16DBL:
195:     case R_390_PLT16DBL:
196:       check_dbl(S + A - P, -(1 << 16), 1 << 16);
197:       *(ub16 *)loc = (S + A - P) >> 1;
198:       break;
199:     case R_390_PC24DBL:
200:     case R_390_PLT24DBL:
201:       check_dbl(S + A - P, -(1 << 24), 1 << 24);
202:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 24, 1);
203:       break;
204:     case R_390_PC32DBL:
205:     case R_390_PLT32DBL:
206:       check_dbl(S + A - P, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
207:       *(ub32 *)loc = (S + A - P) >> 1;
208:       break;
209:     case R_390_GOT12:
210:     case R_390_GOTPLT12:
211:       check(G + A, 0, 1 << 12);
212:       *(ul16 *)loc |= bits(G + A, 11, 0);
213:       break;
214:     case R_390_GOT16:
215:     case R_390_GOTPLT16:
216:       check(G + A, 0, 1 << 16);
217:       *(ub16 *)loc = G + A;
218:       break;
219:     case R_390_GOT20:
220:     case R_390_GOTPLT20:
221:       check(G + A, 0, 1 << 20);
222:       write_mid20(loc, G + A);
223:       break;
224:     case R_390_GOT32:
225:     case R_390_GOTPLT32:
226:       check(G + A, 0, 1LL << 32);
227:       *(ub32 *)loc = G + A;
228:       break;
229:     case R_390_GOT64:
230:     case R_390_GOTPLT64:
231:       *(ub64 *)loc = G + A;
232:       break;
233:     case R_390_GOTOFF16:
234:     case R_390_PLTOFF16:
235:       check(S + A - GOT, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
236:       *(ub16 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
237:       break;
238:     case R_390_GOTOFF32:
239:     case R_390_PLTOFF32:
240:       check(S + A - GOT, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
241:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
242:       break;
243:     case R_390_GOTOFF64:
244:     case R_390_PLTOFF64:
245:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
246:       break;
247:     case R_390_GOTPC:
248:       *(ub64 *)loc = GOT + A - P;
249:       break;
250:     case R_390_GOTPCDBL:
251:       check_dbl(GOT + A - P, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
252:       *(ub32 *)loc = (GOT + A - P) >> 1;
253:       break;
254:     case R_390_GOTENT:
255:       check(GOT + G + A - P, -(1LL << 32), 1LL << 32);
256:       *(ub32 *)loc = (GOT + G + A - P) >> 1;
257:       break;
258:     case R_390_TLS_LE32:
259:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
260:       break;
261:     case R_390_TLS_LE64:
262:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
263:       break;
264:     case R_390_TLS_GOTIE20:
265:       write_mid20(loc, sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT);
266:       break;
267:     case R_390_TLS_IEENT:
268:       *(ub32 *)loc = (sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - P) >> 1;
269:       break;
270:     case R_390_TLS_GD32:
271:       if (sym.has_tlsgd(ctx))
272:         *(ub32 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
273:       else if (sym.has_gottp(ctx))
274:         *(ub32 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
275:       else
276:         *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
277:       break;
278:     case R_390_TLS_GD64:
279:       if (sym.has_tlsgd(ctx))
280:         *(ub64 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
281:       else if (sym.has_gottp(ctx))
282:         *(ub64 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
283:       else
284:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
285:       break;
286:     case R_390_TLS_GDCALL:
287:       if (sym.has_tlsgd(ctx)) {
288:         // do nothing
289:       } else if (sym.has_gottp(ctx)) {
290:         // lg %r2, 0(%r2, %r12)
291:         static u8 insn[] = { 0xe3, 0x22, 0xc0, 0x00, 0x00, 0x04 };
292:         memcpy(loc, insn, sizeof(insn));
293:       } else {
294:         // nop
295:         static u8 insn[] = { 0xc0, 0x04, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00 };
296:         memcpy(loc, insn, sizeof(insn));
297:       }
298:       break;
299:     case R_390_TLS_LDM32:
300:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx))
301:         *(ub32 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
302:       break;
303:     case R_390_TLS_LDM64:
304:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx))
305:         *(ub64 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
306:       break;
307:     case R_390_TLS_LDO32:
308:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx))
309:         *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
310:       else
311:         *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
312:       break;
313:     case R_390_TLS_LDO64:
314:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx))
315:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
316:       else
317:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
318:       break;
319:     case R_390_TLS_LDCALL:
320:       if (!ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx)) {
321:         // nop
322:         static u8 insn[] = { 0xc0, 0x04, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00 };
323:         memcpy(loc, insn, sizeof(insn));
324:       }
325:       break;
326:     default:
327:       unreachable();
328:     }
329:   }
330: }
331: 
332: template <>
333: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
334:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
335: 
336:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
337:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
338:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
339:       continue;
340: 
341:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
342:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
343: 
344:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
345:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
346:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
347:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
348:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
349:     };
350: 
351:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
352:     i64 frag_addend;
353:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
354: 
355:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
356:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
357: 
358:     switch (rel.r_type) {
359:     case R_390_32: {
360:       i64 val = S + A;
361:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
362:       *(ub32 *)loc = val;
363:       break;
364:     }
365:     case R_390_64:
366:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
367:         *(ub64 *)loc = *val;
368:       else
369:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A;
370:       break;
371:     case R_390_TLS_LDO64:
372:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
373:         *(ub64 *)loc = *val;
374:       else
375:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
376:       break;
377:     default:
378:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": apply_reloc_nonalloc: " << rel;
379:     }
380:   }
381: }
382: 
383: template <>
384: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
385:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
386: 
387:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
388:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
389: 
390:   // Scan relocations
391:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
392:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
393:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
394:       continue;
395: 
396:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
397: 
398:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
399:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
400: 
401:     switch (rel.r_type) {
402:     case R_390_64:
403:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
404:       break;
405:     case R_390_8:
406:     case R_390_12:
407:     case R_390_16:
408:     case R_390_20:
409:     case R_390_32:
410:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
411:       break;
412:     case R_390_PC16:
413:     case R_390_PC16DBL:
414:     case R_390_PC32:
415:     case R_390_PC32DBL:
416:     case R_390_PC64:
417:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
418:       break;
419:     case R_390_GOT12:
420:     case R_390_GOT16:
421:     case R_390_GOT20:
422:     case R_390_GOT32:
423:     case R_390_GOT64:
424:     case R_390_GOTOFF16:
425:     case R_390_GOTOFF32:
426:     case R_390_GOTOFF64:
427:     case R_390_GOTPLT12:
428:     case R_390_GOTPLT16:
429:     case R_390_GOTPLT20:
430:     case R_390_GOTPLT32:
431:     case R_390_GOTPLT64:
432:     case R_390_GOTPC:
433:     case R_390_GOTPCDBL:
434:     case R_390_GOTENT:
435:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
436:       break;
437:     case R_390_PLT12DBL:
438:     case R_390_PLT16DBL:
439:     case R_390_PLT24DBL:
440:     case R_390_PLT32:
441:     case R_390_PLT32DBL:
442:     case R_390_PLT64:
443:     case R_390_PLTOFF16:
444:     case R_390_PLTOFF32:
445:     case R_390_PLTOFF64:
446:       if (sym.is_imported)
447:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
448:       break;
449:     case R_390_TLS_GOTIE20:
450:     case R_390_TLS_IEENT:
451:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
452:       break;
453:     case R_390_TLS_GD32:
454:     case R_390_TLS_GD64:
455:       // We always want to relax calls to __tls_get_offset() in statically-
456:       // linked executables because __tls_get_offset() in libc.a just calls
457:       // abort().
458:       if (ctx.arg.is_static ||
459:           (ctx.arg.relax && !sym.is_imported && !ctx.arg.shared)) {
460:         // do nothing
461:       } else if (ctx.arg.relax && !sym.is_imported && ctx.arg.shared &&
462:                  !ctx.arg.z_dlopen) {
463:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
464:       } else {
465:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
466:       }
467:       break;
468:     case R_390_TLS_LDM32:
469:     case R_390_TLS_LDM64: {
470:       bool do_relax = ctx.arg.is_static || (ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared);
471:       if (!do_relax)
472:         ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
473:       break;
474:     }
475:     case R_390_TLS_LE32:
476:     case R_390_TLS_LE64:
477:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
478:       break;
479:     case R_390_TLS_LDO32:
480:     case R_390_TLS_LDO64:
481:     case R_390_TLS_GDCALL:
482:     case R_390_TLS_LDCALL:
483:       break;
484:     default:
485:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": scan_relocations: " << rel;
486:     }
487:   }
488: }
489: 
490: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-sh4.cc
================
  1: // SH-4 (SuperH 4) is a 32-bit RISC ISA developed by Hitachi in the early
  2: // '90s. Some relatively powerful systems were developed with SH-4.
  3: // A notable example is Sega's Dreamcast game console which debuted in 1998.
  4: // Hitachi later spun off its semiconductor division as an independent
  5: // company, Renesas, and Renesas is still selling SH-4 processors for the
  6: // embedded market. It has never been as popular as ARM is, and its
  7: // popularity continues to decline though.
  8: //
  9: // SH-4's most distinctive feature compared to other RISC ISAs is that its
 10: // instructions are 16 bits in length instead of more common 32 bits for
 11: // better code density. This difference affects various aspects of its
 12: // instruction set as shown below:
 13: //
 14: //  - SH-4 has 16 general-purpose registers (GPRs) instead of the most
 15: //    commmon 32 GPR configuration to save one bit to specify a register.
 16: //
 17: //  - Binary instructions such as ADD normally take three register in
 18: //    RISC ISAs (e.g. x ← y ⊕ z where x, y and z are registers), but
 19: //    SH-4's instructions take only two registers. The result of an
 20: //    operation is written to one of the source registers (e.g. x ← x ⊕ y).
 21: //
 22: //  - Usual RISC ISAs have "load high" and "load low" instructions to set
 23: //    an immediate to most significant and least significant bits in a
 24: //    register to construct a full 32-bit value in a register. This
 25: //    technique is hard to use in SH-4, as 16 bit instructions are too
 26: //    small to contain large immediates. On SH-4, large immediates are
 27: //    loaded from memory using `mov.l` PC-relative load instruction.
 28: //
 29: //  - Many RISC ISAs are, despite their name, actually fairly complex.
 30: //    They tend to have hundreds if not thousands of different instructions.
 31: //    SH-4 doesn't really have that many instructions because its 16-bit
 32: //    machine code simply can't encode many different opcodes. As a
 33: //    result, the number of relocations the linker has to support is also
 34: //    small.
 35: //
 36: // Beside these, SH-4 has a delay branch slot just like contemporary MIPS
 37: // and SPARC. That is, one instruction after a branch instruction will
 38: // always be executed even if the branch is taken. Delay branch slot allows
 39: // a pipelined CPU to start and finish executing an instruction after a
 40: // branch regardless of the branch's condition, simplifying the processor's
 41: // implementation. It's considered a bad premature optimization nowadays,
 42: // though. Modern RISC processors don't have it.
 43: //
 44: // Here are notes about the SH-4 psABI:
 45: //
 46: //  - If a source file is compiled with -fPIC, each function starts
 47: //    with a piece of code to store the address of .got to %r12.
 48: //    We can use the register in our PLT for position-independent output.
 49: //
 50: //  - Even though it uses the RELA-type relocations, relocation addends
 51: //    are stored not to the r_addend field but to the relocated section
 52: //    contents for some reason. Therefore, it's effectively REL.
 53: //
 54: //  - It looks like the ecosystem has bit-rotted. Some tests, especially
 55: //    one using C++ exceptions, don't pass even with GNU ld.
 56: //
 57: //  - GCC/SH4 tends to write dynamically-relocated data into .text, so the
 58: //    output from the linker contains lots of text relocations. That's not
 59: //    a problem with embedded programming, I guess.
 60: 
 61: #include "mold.h"
 62: 
 63: namespace mold::elf {
 64: 
 65: using E = SH4;
 66: 
 67: // Even though SH-4 uses RELA-type relocations, addends are stored to
 68: // relocated places for some reason.
 69: template <>
 70: i64 get_addend(u8 *loc, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
 71:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 72:   case R_SH_DIR32:
 73:   case R_SH_REL32:
 74:   case R_SH_TLS_GD_32:
 75:   case R_SH_TLS_LD_32:
 76:   case R_SH_TLS_LDO_32:
 77:   case R_SH_TLS_IE_32:
 78:   case R_SH_TLS_LE_32:
 79:   case R_SH_TLS_DTPMOD32:
 80:   case R_SH_TLS_DTPOFF32:
 81:   case R_SH_TLS_TPOFF32:
 82:   case R_SH_GOT32:
 83:   case R_SH_PLT32:
 84:   case R_SH_GOTOFF:
 85:   case R_SH_GOTPC:
 86:   case R_SH_GOTPLT32:
 87:     return *(ul32 *)loc;
 88:   default:
 89:     return 0;
 90:   }
 91: }
 92: 
 93: template <>
 94: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 95:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
 96:     static const u8 insn[] = {
 97:       0x02, 0xd2, //    mov.l   1f, r2
 98:       0xcc, 0x32, //    add     r12, r2
 99:       0x22, 0x50, //    mov.l   @(8, r2), r0
100:       0x21, 0x52, //    mov.l   @(4, r2), r2
101:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
102:       0x00, 0xe0, //    mov     #0, r0
103:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOTPLT
104:     };
105: 
106:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_hdr_size);
107:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
108:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 12) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
109:   } else {
110:     static const u8 insn[] = {
111:       0x02, 0xd2, //    mov.l   1f, r2
112:       0x22, 0x50, //    mov.l   @(8, r2), r0
113:       0x21, 0x52, //    mov.l   @(4, r2), r2
114:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
115:       0x00, 0xe0, //    mov     #0, r0
116:       0x09, 0x00, //    nop
117:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOTPLT
118:     };
119: 
120:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_hdr_size);
121:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
122:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 12) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr;
123:   }
124: }
125: 
126: template <>
127: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
128:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
129:     static const u8 insn[] = {
130:       0x01, 0xd0, //    mov.l   1f, r0
131:       0xce, 0x00, //    mov.l   @(r0, r12), r0
132:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
133:       0x01, 0xd1, //    mov.l   2f, r1
134:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOTPLT_ENTRY
135:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 2: .long INDEX_IN_RELPLT
136:     };
137: 
138:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_size);
139:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
140:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 8) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
141:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 12) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
142:   } else {
143:     static const u8 insn[] = {
144:       0x01, 0xd0, //    mov.l   1f, r0
145:       0x02, 0x60, //    mov.l   @r0, r0
146:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
147:       0x01, 0xd1, //    mov.l   2f, r1
148:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOTPLT_ENTRY
149:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 2: .long INDEX_IN_RELPLT
150:     };
151: 
152:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::plt_size);
153:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
154:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 8) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx);
155:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 12) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
156:   }
157: }
158: 
159: template <>
160: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
161:   if (ctx.arg.pic) {
162:     static const u8 insn[] = {
163:       0x01, 0xd0, //    mov.l   1f, r0
164:       0xce, 0x00, //    mov.l   @(r0, r12), r0
165:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
166:       0x09, 0x00, //    nop
167:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOT_ENTRY
168:     };
169: 
170:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::pltgot_size);
171:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
172:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 8) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
173:   } else {
174:     static const u8 insn[] = {
175:       0x01, 0xd0, //    mov.l   1f, r0
176:       0x02, 0x60, //    mov.l   @r0, r0
177:       0x2b, 0x40, //    jmp     @r0
178:       0x09, 0x00, //    nop
179:       0, 0, 0, 0, // 1: .long GOT_ENTRY
180:     };
181: 
182:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::pltgot_size);
183:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
184:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 8) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx);
185:   }
186: }
187: 
188: template <>
189: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
190:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
191:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
192: 
193:   switch (rel.r_type) {
194:   case R_NONE:
195:     break;
196:   case R_SH_DIR32:
197:     *(ul32 *)loc = val;
198:     break;
199:   case R_SH_REL32:
200:     *(ul32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
201:     break;
202:   default:
203:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
204:   }
205: }
206: 
207: template <>
208: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
209:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
210: 
211:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
212:   if (ctx.reldyn)
213:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
214:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
215: 
216:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
217:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
218:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
219:       continue;
220: 
221:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
222:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
223: 
224:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
225:     u64 A = get_addend(loc, rel);
226:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
227:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
228:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
229: 
230:     switch (rel.r_type) {
231:     case R_SH_DIR32:
232:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
233:       break;
234:     case R_SH_REL32:
235:     case R_SH_PLT32:
236:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P;
237:       break;
238:     case R_SH_GOT32:
239:       *(ul32 *)loc = G;
240:       break;
241:     case R_SH_GOTPC:
242:       *(ul32 *)loc = GOT + A - P;
243:       break;
244:     case R_SH_GOTOFF:
245:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - GOT;
246:       break;
247:     case R_SH_TLS_GD_32:
248:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
249:       break;
250:     case R_SH_TLS_LD_32:
251:       *(ul32 *)loc = ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
252:       break;
253:     case R_SH_TLS_LDO_32:
254:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
255:       break;
256:     case R_SH_TLS_IE_32:
257:       *(ul32 *)loc = sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT;
258:       break;
259:     case R_SH_TLS_LE_32:
260:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
261:       break;
262:     default:
263:       unreachable();
264:     }
265:   }
266: }
267: 
268: template <>
269: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
270:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
271: 
272:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
273:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
274:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
275:       continue;
276: 
277:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
278:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
279: 
280:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
281:     i64 frag_addend;
282:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
283: 
284:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
285:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : get_addend(loc, rel);
286: 
287:     switch (rel.r_type) {
288:     case R_SH_DIR32:
289:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
290:         *(ul32 *)loc = *val;
291:       else
292:         *(ul32 *)loc = S + A;
293:       break;
294:     default:
295:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
296:                  << rel;
297:     }
298:   }
299: }
300: 
301: template <>
302: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
303:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
304: 
305:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
306:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
307: 
308:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
309:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
310:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
311:       continue;
312: 
313:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
314: 
315:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
316:       Error(ctx) << sym << ": GNU ifunc symbol is not supported on sh4";
317: 
318:     switch (rel.r_type) {
319:     case R_SH_DIR32:
320:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
321:       break;
322:     case R_SH_REL32:
323:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
324:       break;
325:     case R_SH_GOT32:
326:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
327:       break;
328:     case R_SH_PLT32:
329:       if (sym.is_imported)
330:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
331:       break;
332:     case R_SH_TLS_GD_32:
333:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
334:       break;
335:     case R_SH_TLS_LD_32:
336:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
337:       break;
338:     case R_SH_TLS_IE_32:
339:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
340:       break;
341:     case R_SH_TLS_LE_32:
342:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
343:       break;
344:     case R_SH_GOTPC:
345:     case R_SH_GOTOFF:
346:     case R_SH_TLS_LDO_32:
347:       break;
348:     default:
349:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
350:     }
351:   }
352: }
353: 
354: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/arch-sparc64.cc
================
  1: // SPARC is a RISC ISA developed by Sun Microsystems.
  2: //
  3: // The byte order of the processor is big-endian. Anything larger than a
  4: // byte is stored in the "reverse" order compared to little-endian
  5: // processors such as x86-64.
  6: //
  7: // All instructions are 4 bytes long and aligned to 4 bytes boundaries.
  8: //
  9: // A notable feature of SPARC is that, unlike other RISC ISAs, it doesn't
 10: // need range extension thunks. It is because the SPARC's CALL instruction
 11: // contains a whopping 30 bits immediate. The processor scales it by 4 to
 12: // extend it to 32 bits (this is doable because all instructions are
 13: // aligned to 4 bytes boundaries, so the least significant two bits are
 14: // always zero). That means CALL's reach is PC ± 2 GiB, elinating the
 15: // need of range extension thunks. It comes with the cost that the CALL
 16: // instruction alone takes 1/4th of the instruction encoding space,
 17: // though.
 18: //
 19: // SPARC has 32 general purpose registers. CALL instruction saves a return
 20: // address to %o7, which is an alias for %r15. Thread pointer is stored to
 21: // %g7 which is %r7.
 22: //
 23: // SPARC does not have PC-relative load/store instructions. To access data
 24: // in the position-independent manner, we usually first set the address of
 25: // .got to, for example, %l7, with the following piece of code
 26: //
 27: //   sethi  %hi(. - _GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_), %l7
 28: //   add  %l7, %lo(. - _GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_), %l7
 29: //   call __sparc_get_pc_thunk.l7
 30: //   nop
 31: //
 32: // where __sparc_get_pc_thunk.l7 is defined as
 33: //
 34: //   retl
 35: //   add  %o7, %l7, %l7
 36: //
 37: // . SETHI and the following ADD materialize a 32 bits offset to .got.
 38: // CALL instruction sets a return address to $o7, and the subsequent ADD
 39: // adds it to the GOT offset to materialize the absolute address of .got.
 40: //
 41: // Note that we have a NOP after CALL and an ADD after RETL because of
 42: // SPARC's delay branch slots. That is, the SPARC processor always
 43: // executes one instruction after a branch even if the branch is taken.
 44: // This may seem like an odd behavior, and indeed it is considered as such
 45: // (that's a premature optimization for the early pipelined SPARC
 46: // processors), but that's been a part of the ISA's spec so that's what it
 47: // is.
 48: //
 49: // Note also that the .got address obtained this way is not shared between
 50: // functions, so functions can use an arbitrary register to hold the .got
 51: // address. That also means each function needs to execute the above piece
 52: // of code to become position-independent.
 53: //
 54: // This scheme is very similar to i386. That may not be a coincidence
 55: // because the i386 ELF psABI is created by Sun Microsystems too.
 56: //
 57: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/sparc.pdf
 58: 
 59: #include "mold.h"
 60: 
 61: namespace mold::elf {
 62: 
 63: using E = SPARC64;
 64: 
 65: // SPARC's PLT section is writable despite containing executable code.
 66: // We don't need to write the PLT header entry because the dynamic loader
 67: // will do that for us.
 68: //
 69: // We also don't need a .got.plt section to store the result of lazy PLT
 70: // symbol resolution because the dynamic symbol resolver directly mutates
 71: // instructions in PLT so that they jump to the right places next time.
 72: // That's why each PLT entry contains lots of NOPs; they are a placeholder
 73: // for the runtime to add more instructions.
 74: //
 75: // Self-modifying code is nowadays considered really bad from the security
 76: // point of view, though.
 77: template <>
 78: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 79:   memset(buf, 0, E::plt_hdr_size);
 80: }
 81: 
 82: template <>
 83: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 84:   static ub32 insn[] = {
 85:     0x0300'0000, // sethi (. - .PLT0), %g1
 86:     0x3068'0000, // ba,a  %xcc, .PLT1
 87:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 88:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 89:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 90:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 91:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 92:     0x0100'0000, // nop
 93:   };
 94: 
 95:   u64 plt0 = ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr;
 96:   u64 plt1 = ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr + E::plt_size;
 97:   u64 entry = sym.get_plt_addr(ctx);
 98: 
 99:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
100:   *(ub32 *)buf |= bits(entry - plt0, 21, 0);
101:   *(ub32 *)(buf + 4) |= bits(plt1 - entry - 4, 20, 2);
102: }
103: 
104: template <>
105: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
106:   static ub32 entry[] = {
107:     0x8a10'000f, // mov  %o7, %g5
108:     0x4000'0002, // call . + 8
109:     0xc25b'e014, // ldx  [ %o7 + 20 ], %g1
110:     0xc25b'c001, // ldx  [ %o7 + %g1 ], %g1
111:     0x81c0'4000, // jmp  %g1
112:     0x9e10'0005, // mov  %g5, %o7
113:     0x0000'0000, // .quad $plt_entry - $got_entry
114:     0x0000'0000,
115:   };
116: 
117:   memcpy(buf, entry, sizeof(entry));
118:   *(ub64 *)(buf + 24) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 4;
119: }
120: 
121: template <>
122: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
123:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
124:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
125: 
126:   switch (rel.r_type) {
127:   case R_NONE:
128:     break;
129:   case R_SPARC_64:
130:   case R_SPARC_UA64:
131:     *(ub64 *)loc = val;
132:     break;
133:   case R_SPARC_DISP32:
134:     *(ub32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
135:     break;
136:   default:
137:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
138:   }
139: }
140: 
141: template <>
142: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
143:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
144: 
145:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
146:   if (ctx.reldyn)
147:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
148:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
149: 
150:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
151:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
152:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
153:       continue;
154: 
155:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
156:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
157: 
158:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
159:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
160:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
161:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
162:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
163:     };
164: 
165:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
166:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
167:     u64 P = (get_addr() + rel.r_offset);
168:     u64 G = (sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>));
169:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
170: 
171:     switch (rel.r_type) {
172:     case R_SPARC_64:
173:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
174:       break;
175:     case R_SPARC_5:
176:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 5);
177:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 4, 0);
178:       break;
179:     case R_SPARC_6:
180:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 6);
181:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 5, 0);
182:       break;
183:     case R_SPARC_7:
184:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 7);
185:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 6, 0);
186:       break;
187:     case R_SPARC_8:
188:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
189:       *(u8 *)loc = S + A;
190:       break;
191:     case R_SPARC_10:
192:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 10);
193:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 9, 0);
194:       break;
195:     case R_SPARC_LO10:
196:     case R_SPARC_LOPLT10:
197:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 9, 0);
198:       break;
199:     case R_SPARC_11:
200:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 11);
201:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 10, 0);
202:       break;
203:     case R_SPARC_13:
204:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 13);
205:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 12, 0);
206:       break;
207:     case R_SPARC_16:
208:     case R_SPARC_UA16:
209:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
210:       *(ub16 *)loc = S + A;
211:       break;
212:     case R_SPARC_22:
213:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 22);
214:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 21, 0);
215:       break;
216:     case R_SPARC_32:
217:     case R_SPARC_UA32:
218:     case R_SPARC_PLT32:
219:       check(S + A, 0, 1LL << 32);
220:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A;
221:       break;
222:     case R_SPARC_PLT64:
223:     case R_SPARC_UA64:
224:     case R_SPARC_REGISTER:
225:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A;
226:       break;
227:     case R_SPARC_DISP8:
228:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 7), 1 << 7);
229:       *(u8 *)loc = S + A - P;
230:       break;
231:     case R_SPARC_DISP16:
232:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
233:       *(ub16 *)loc = S + A - P;
234:       break;
235:     case R_SPARC_DISP32:
236:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT32:
237:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
238:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - P;
239:       break;
240:     case R_SPARC_DISP64:
241:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - P;
242:       break;
243:     case R_SPARC_WDISP16: {
244:       i64 val = S + A - P;
245:       check(val, -(1 << 16), 1 << 16);
246:       *(ub16 *)loc |= (bit(val, 16) << 21) | bits(val, 15, 2);
247:       break;
248:     }
249:     case R_SPARC_WDISP19:
250:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 20), 1 << 20);
251:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 20, 2);
252:       break;
253:     case R_SPARC_WDISP22:
254:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 23), 1 << 23);
255:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 23, 2);
256:       break;
257:     case R_SPARC_WDISP30:
258:     case R_SPARC_WPLT30:
259:       check(S + A - P, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
260:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 31, 2);
261:       break;
262:     case R_SPARC_HI22:
263:     case R_SPARC_HIPLT22:
264:     case R_SPARC_LM22:
265:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 31, 10);
266:       break;
267:     case R_SPARC_GOT10:
268:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(G, 9, 0);
269:       break;
270:     case R_SPARC_GOT13:
271:       check(G, 0, 1 << 12);
272:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(G, 12, 0);
273:       break;
274:     case R_SPARC_GOT22:
275:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(G, 31, 10);
276:       break;
277:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_HIX22: {
278:       i64 val = S + A - GOT;
279:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val < 0 ? ~val : val, 31, 10);
280:       break;
281:     }
282:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_LOX10: {
283:       i64 val = S + A - GOT;
284:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 9, 0) | (val < 0 ? 0b1'1100'0000'0000 : 0);
285:       break;
286:     }
287:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP_HIX22:
288:       // We always have to relax a GOT load to a load immediate if a
289:       // symbol is local, because R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP cannot represent
290:       // an addend for a local symbol.
291:       if (sym.is_imported || sym.is_ifunc()) {
292:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(G, 31, 10);
293:       } else if (sym.is_absolute()) {
294:         i64 val = S + A;
295:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val < 0 ? ~val : val, 31, 10);
296:       } else {
297:         i64 val = S + A - GOT;
298:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val < 0 ? ~val : val, 31, 10);
299:       }
300:       break;
301:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP_LOX10: {
302:       if (sym.is_imported || sym.is_ifunc()) {
303:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(G, 9, 0);
304:       } else if (sym.is_absolute()) {
305:         i64 val = S + A;
306:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 9, 0) | (val < 0 ? 0b1'1100'0000'0000 : 0);
307:       } else {
308:         i64 val = S + A - GOT;
309:         *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(val, 9, 0) | (val < 0 ? 0b1'1100'0000'0000 : 0);
310:       }
311:       break;
312:     }
313:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP:
314:       if (sym.is_imported || sym.is_ifunc())
315:         break;
316: 
317:       if (sym.is_absolute()) {
318:         // ldx [ %g2 + %g1 ], %g1  →  nop
319:         *(ub32 *)loc = 0x0100'0000;
320:       } else {
321:         // ldx [ %g2 + %g1 ], %g1  →  add %g2, %g1, %g1
322:         *(ub32 *)loc &= 0b00'11111'000000'11111'1'11111111'11111;
323:         *(ub32 *)loc |= 0b10'00000'000000'00000'0'00000000'00000;
324:       }
325:       break;
326:     case R_SPARC_PC10:
327:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT10:
328:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 9, 0);
329:       break;
330:     case R_SPARC_PC22:
331:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT22:
332:     case R_SPARC_PC_LM22:
333:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 31, 10);
334:       break;
335:     case R_SPARC_OLO10:
336:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(bits(S + A, 9, 0) + rel.r_type_data, 12, 0);
337:       break;
338:     case R_SPARC_HH22:
339:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 63, 42);
340:       break;
341:     case R_SPARC_HM10:
342:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 41, 32);
343:       break;
344:     case R_SPARC_PC_HH22:
345:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 63, 42);
346:       break;
347:     case R_SPARC_PC_HM10:
348:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - P, 41, 32);
349:       break;
350:     case R_SPARC_HIX22:
351:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(~(S + A), 31, 10);
352:       break;
353:     case R_SPARC_LOX10:
354:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 9, 0) | 0b1'1100'0000'0000;
355:       break;
356:     case R_SPARC_H44:
357:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 43, 22);
358:       break;
359:     case R_SPARC_M44:
360:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 21, 12);
361:       break;
362:     case R_SPARC_L44:
363:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A, 11, 0);
364:       break;
365:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_HI22:
366:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 31, 10);
367:       break;
368:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_LO10:
369:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 9, 0);
370:       break;
371:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_CALL:
372:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_CALL: {
373:       u64 addr;
374:       if (ctx.arg.is_static)
375:         addr = ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sec->shdr.sh_addr;
376:       else
377:         addr = ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sym->get_addr(ctx);
378: 
379:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(addr + A - P, 31, 2);
380:       break;
381:     }
382:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_HI22:
383:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 31, 10);
384:       break;
385:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_LO10:
386:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 9, 0);
387:       break;
388:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_HIX22:
389:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr, 31, 10);
390:       break;
391:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_LOX10:
392:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr, 9, 0);
393:       break;
394:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_HI22:
395:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 31, 10);
396:       break;
397:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LO10:
398:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - GOT, 9, 0);
399:       break;
400:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LE_HIX22:
401:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(~(S + A - ctx.tp_addr), 31, 10);
402:       break;
403:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LE_LOX10:
404:       *(ub32 *)loc |= bits(S + A - ctx.tp_addr, 9, 0) | 0b1'1100'0000'0000;
405:       break;
406:     case R_SPARC_SIZE32:
407:       *(ub32 *)loc = sym.esym().st_size + A;
408:       break;
409:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_ADD:
410:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_ADD:
411:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_ADD:
412:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LD:
413:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LDX:
414:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_ADD:
415:       break;
416:     default:
417:       unreachable();
418:     }
419:   }
420: }
421: 
422: template <>
423: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
424:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
425: 
426:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
427:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
428:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
429:       continue;
430: 
431:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
432:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
433: 
434:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
435:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
436:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
437:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
438:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
439:     };
440: 
441:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
442:     i64 frag_addend;
443:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
444: 
445:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
446:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
447: 
448:     switch (rel.r_type) {
449:     case R_SPARC_64:
450:     case R_SPARC_UA64:
451:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
452:         *(ub64 *)loc = *val;
453:       else
454:         *(ub64 *)loc = S + A;
455:       break;
456:     case R_SPARC_32:
457:     case R_SPARC_UA32: {
458:       i64 val = S + A;
459:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
460:       *(ub32 *)loc = val;
461:       break;
462:     }
463:     case R_SPARC_TLS_DTPOFF32:
464:       *(ub32 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
465:       break;
466:     case R_SPARC_TLS_DTPOFF64:
467:       *(ub64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
468:       break;
469:     default:
470:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": apply_reloc_nonalloc: " << rel;
471:     }
472:   }
473: }
474: 
475: template <>
476: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
477:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
478: 
479:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
480:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
481: 
482:   // Scan relocations
483:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
484:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
485:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
486:       continue;
487: 
488:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
489: 
490:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
491:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
492: 
493:     switch (rel.r_type) {
494:     case R_SPARC_64:
495:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
496:       break;
497:     case R_SPARC_8:
498:     case R_SPARC_5:
499:     case R_SPARC_6:
500:     case R_SPARC_7:
501:     case R_SPARC_10:
502:     case R_SPARC_11:
503:     case R_SPARC_13:
504:     case R_SPARC_16:
505:     case R_SPARC_22:
506:     case R_SPARC_32:
507:     case R_SPARC_REGISTER:
508:     case R_SPARC_UA16:
509:     case R_SPARC_UA32:
510:     case R_SPARC_UA64:
511:     case R_SPARC_PC_HM10:
512:     case R_SPARC_OLO10:
513:     case R_SPARC_LOX10:
514:     case R_SPARC_HM10:
515:     case R_SPARC_M44:
516:     case R_SPARC_HIX22:
517:     case R_SPARC_LO10:
518:     case R_SPARC_L44:
519:     case R_SPARC_LM22:
520:     case R_SPARC_HI22:
521:     case R_SPARC_H44:
522:     case R_SPARC_HH22:
523:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
524:       break;
525:     case R_SPARC_PLT32:
526:     case R_SPARC_WPLT30:
527:     case R_SPARC_WDISP30:
528:     case R_SPARC_HIPLT22:
529:     case R_SPARC_LOPLT10:
530:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT32:
531:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT22:
532:     case R_SPARC_PCPLT10:
533:     case R_SPARC_PLT64:
534:       if (sym.is_imported)
535:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
536:       break;
537:     case R_SPARC_GOT13:
538:     case R_SPARC_GOT10:
539:     case R_SPARC_GOT22:
540:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_HIX22:
541:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
542:       break;
543:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP_HIX22:
544:       if (sym.is_imported)
545:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
546:       break;
547:     case R_SPARC_DISP16:
548:     case R_SPARC_DISP32:
549:     case R_SPARC_DISP64:
550:     case R_SPARC_DISP8:
551:     case R_SPARC_PC10:
552:     case R_SPARC_PC22:
553:     case R_SPARC_PC_LM22:
554:     case R_SPARC_WDISP16:
555:     case R_SPARC_WDISP19:
556:     case R_SPARC_WDISP22:
557:     case R_SPARC_PC_HH22:
558:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
559:       break;
560:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_HI22:
561:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
562:       break;
563:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_HI22:
564:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
565:       break;
566:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_HI22:
567:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
568:       break;
569:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_CALL:
570:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_CALL:
571:       if (!ctx.arg.is_static && ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sym->is_imported)
572:         ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sym->flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
573:       break;
574:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LE_HIX22:
575:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LE_LOX10:
576:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
577:       break;
578:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP_LOX10:
579:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_OP:
580:     case R_SPARC_GOTDATA_LOX10:
581:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_LO10:
582:     case R_SPARC_TLS_GD_ADD:
583:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_LO10:
584:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDM_ADD:
585:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_HIX22:
586:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_LOX10:
587:     case R_SPARC_TLS_LDO_ADD:
588:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_ADD:
589:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LD:
590:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LDX:
591:     case R_SPARC_TLS_IE_LO10:
592:     case R_SPARC_SIZE32:
593:       break;
594:     default:
595:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": scan_relocations: " << rel;
596:     }
597:   }
598: }
599: 
600: // __tls_get_addr is not defined by libc.a, so we can't use that function
601: // in statically-linked executables. This section provides a replacement.
602: void SparcTlsGetAddrSection::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
603:   ub32 *buf = (ub32 *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
604: 
605:   static const ub32 insn[] = {
606:     0x0300'0000, // sethi  %hi(TP_SIZE), %g1
607:     0x8210'6000, // or   %g1, %lo(TP_SIZE), %g1
608:     0x8221'c001, // sub  %g7, %g1, %g1
609:     0xd05a'2008, // ldx  [ %o0 + 8 ], %o0
610:     0x81c3'e008, // retl
611:     0x9000'4008, // add  %g1, %o0, %o0
612:   };
613: 
614:   assert(this->shdr.sh_size == sizeof(insn));
615:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
616: 
617:   buf[0] |= bits(ctx.tp_addr - ctx.tls_begin, 31, 10);
618:   buf[1] |= bits(ctx.tp_addr - ctx.tls_begin, 9, 0);
619: }
620: 
621: } // namespace mold::elf
