  19: inline const char helpmsg[] = R"(
  20: Options:
  21:   --help                      Report usage information
  22:   -v, --version               Report version information
  23:   -V                          Report version and target information
  24:   -(, --start-group           Ignored
  25:   -), --end-group             Ignored
  26:   -C DIR, --directory DIR     Change to DIR before doing anything
  27:   -E, --export-dynamic        Put symbols in the dynamic symbol table
  28:     --no-export-dynamic
  29:   -F LIBNAME, --filter LIBNAME
  30:                               Set DT_FILTER to the specified value
  31:   -I FILE, --dynamic-linker FILE
  32:                               Set dynamic linker path
  33:     --no-dynamic-linker
  34:   -L DIR, --library-path DIR  Add DIR to library search path
  35:   -M, --print-map             Write map file to stdout
  36:   -N, --omagic                Do not page align data, do not make text readonly
  37:     --no-omagic
  38:   -O NUMBER                   Ignored
  39:   -S, --strip-debug           Strip .debug_* sections
  40:   -T FILE, --script FILE      Read linker script
  41:   -X, --discard-locals        Discard temporary local symbols
  42:   -e SYMBOL, --entry SYMBOL   Set program entry point
  43:   -f SHLIB, --auxiliary SHLIB Set DT_AUXILIARY to the specified value
  44:   -h LIBNAME, --soname LIBNAME
  45:                               Set shared library name
  46:   -l LIBNAME                  Search for a given library
  47:   -m TARGET                   Set target
  48:   -o FILE, --output FILE      Set output filename
  49:   -q, --emit-relocs           Leaves relocation sections in the output
  50:   -r, --relocatable           Generate relocatable output
  51:   -s, --strip-all             Strip .symtab section
  52:   -u SYMBOL, --undefined SYMBOL
  53:                               Force to resolve SYMBOL
  54:   --Bdynamic, --dy            Link against shared libraries (default)
  55:   --Bstatic, --dn, --static   Do not link against shared libraries
  56:   --Bsymbolic                 Bind global symbols locally
  57:   --Bsymbolic-functions       Bind global functions locally
  58:   --Bno-symbolic              Cancel --Bsymbolic and --Bsymbolic-functions
  59:   --Map FILE                  Write map file to a given file
  60:   --Tbss=ADDR                 Set address to .bss
  61:   --Tdata                     Set address to .data
  62:   --Ttext                     Set address to .text
  63:   --allow-multiple-definition Allow multiple definitions
  64:   --apply-dynamic-relocs      Apply link-time values for dynamic relocations (defualt)
  65:     --no-apply-dynamic-relocs
  66:   --as-needed                 Only set DT_NEEDED if used
  67:     --no-as-needed
  68:   --build-id [none,md5,sha1,sha256,uuid,HEXSTRING]
  69:                               Generate build ID
  70:     --no-build-id
  71:   --chroot DIR                Set a given path to root directory
  72:   --color-diagnostics=[auto,always,never]
  73:                               Use colors in diagnostics
  74:   --color-diagnostics         Alias for --color-diagnostics=always
  75:   --compress-debug-sections [none,zlib,zlib-gabi,zstd]
  76:                               Compress .debug_* sections
  77:   --dc                        Ignored
  78:   --dependency-file=FILE      Write Makefile-style dependency rules to FILE
  79:   --defsym=SYMBOL=VALUE       Define a symbol alias
  80:   --demangle                  Demangle C++ symbols in log messages (default)
  81:     --no-demangle
  82:   --enable-new-dtags          Emit DT_RUNPATH for --rpath (default)
  83:     --disable-new-dtags       Emit DT_RPATH for --rpath
  84:   --execute-only              Make executable segments unreadable
  85:   --dp                        Ignored
  86:   --dynamic-list              Read a list of dynamic symbols (implies -Bsymbolic)
  87:   --eh-frame-hdr              Create .eh_frame_hdr section
  88:     --no-eh-frame-hdr
  89:   --exclude-libs LIB,LIB,..   Mark all symbols in given libraries hidden
  90:   --export-dynamic-symbol     Put symbols matching glob in the dynamic symbol table
  91:   --export-dynamic-symbol-list
  92:                               Read a list of dynamic symbols
  93:   --fatal-warnings            Treat warnings as errors
  94:     --no-fatal-warnings       Do not treat warnings as errors (default)
  95:   --fini SYMBOL               Call SYMBOL at unload-time
  96:   --fork                      Spawn a child process (default)
  97:     --no-fork
  98:   --gc-sections               Remove unreferenced sections
  99:     --no-gc-sections
 100:   --gdb-index                 Create .gdb_index for faster gdb startup
 101:   --hash-style [sysv,gnu,both,none]
 102:                               Set hash style
 103:   --icf=[all,safe,none]       Fold identical code
 104:     --no-icf
 105:   --ignore-data-address-equality
 106:                               Allow merging non-executable sections with --icf
 107:   --image-base ADDR           Set the base address to a given value
 108:   --init SYMBOL               Call SYMBOL at load-time
 109:   --no-undefined              Report undefined symbols (even with --shared)
 110:   --noinhibit-exec            Create an output file even if errors occur
 111:   --oformat=binary            Omit ELF, section and program headers
 112:   --pack-dyn-relocs=[relr,none]
 113:                               Pack dynamic relocations
 114:   --package-metadata=STRING   Set a given string to .note.package
 115:   --perf                      Print performance statistics
 116:   --pie, --pic-executable     Create a position independent executable
 117:     --no-pie, --no-pic-executable
 118:   --pop-state                 Restore state of flags governing input file handling
 119:   --print-gc-sections         Print removed unreferenced sections
 120:     --no-print-gc-sections
 121:   --print-icf-sections        Print folded identical sections
 122:     --no-print-icf-sections
 123:   --push-state                Save state of flags governing input file handling
 124:   --quick-exit                Use quick_exit to exit (default)
 125:     --no-quick-exit
 126:   --relax                     Optimize instructions (default)
 127:     --no-relax
 128:   --repro                     Embed input files to .repro section
 129:   --require-defined SYMBOL    Require SYMBOL be defined in the final output
 130:   --retain-symbols-file FILE  Keep only symbols listed in FILE
 131:   --reverse-sections          Reverses input sections in the output file
 132:   --rosegment                 Put read-only non-executable sections in their own segment (default)
 133:     --no-rosegment            Put read-only non-executable sections in an executable segment
 134:   --rpath DIR                 Add DIR to runtime search path
 135:   --rpath-link DIR            Ignored
 136:   --run COMMAND ARG...        Run COMMAND with mold as /usr/bin/ld
 137:   --section-start=SECTION=ADDR Set address to section
 138:   --shared, --Bshareable      Create a share library
 139:   --shuffle-sections[=SEED]   Randomize the output by shuffling input sections
 140:   --sort-common               Ignored
 141:   --sort-section              Ignored
 142:   --spare-dynamic-tags NUMBER Reserve give number of tags in .dynamic section
 143:   --start-lib                 Give following object files in-archive-file semantics
 144:     --end-lib                 End the effect of --start-lib
 145:   --stats                     Print input statistics
 146:   --sysroot DIR               Set target system root directory
 147:   --thread-count COUNT, --threads=COUNT
 148:                               Use COUNT number of threads
 149:   --threads                   Use multiple threads (default)
 150:     --no-threads
 151:   --trace                     Print name of each input file
 152:   --undefined-version         Do not report version scripts that refer undefined symbols
 153:     --no-undefined-version    Report version scripts that refer undefined symbols (default)
 154:   --unique PATTERN            Don't merge input sections that match a given pattern
 155:   --unresolved-symbols [report-all,ignore-all,ignore-in-object-files,ignore-in-shared-libs]
 156:                               How to handle unresolved symbols
 157:   --version-script FILE       Read version script
 158:   --warn-common               Warn about common symbols
 159:     --no-warn-common
 160:   --warn-once                 Only warn once for each undefined symbol
 161:   --warn-shared-textrel       Warn if the output .so needs text relocations
 162:   --warn-textrel              Warn if the output file needs text relocations
 163:   --warn-unresolved-symbols   Report unresolved symbols as warnings
 164:     --error-unresolved-symbols
 165:                               Report unresolved symbols as errors (default)
 166:   --whole-archive             Include all objects from static archives
 167:     --no-whole-archive
 168:   --wrap SYMBOL               Use wrapper function for a given symbol
 169:   -z defs                     Report undefined symbols (even with --shared)
 170:     -z nodefs
 171:   -z common-page-size=VALUE   Ignored
 172:   -z execstack                Require executable stack
 173:     -z noexecstack
 174:   -z execstack-if-needed      Make the stack area execuable if an input file explicitly requests it
 175:   -z initfirst                Mark DSO to be initialized first at runtime
 176:   -z interpose                Mark object to interpose all DSOs but executable
 177:   -z keep-text-section-prefix Keep .text.{hot,unknown,unlikely,startup,exit} as separate sections in the final binary
 178:     -z nokeep-text-section-prefix
 179:   -z lazy                     Enable lazy function resolution (default)
 180:   -z max-page-size=VALUE      Use VALUE as the memory page size
 181:   -z nocopyreloc              Do not create copy relocations
 182:   -z nodefaultlib             Make the dynamic loader to ignore default search paths
 183:   -z nodelete                 Mark DSO non-deletable at runtime
 184:   -z nodlopen                 Mark DSO not available to dlopen
 185:   -z nodump                   Mark DSO not available to dldump
 186:   -z now                      Disable lazy function resolution
 187:   -z origin                   Mark object requiring immediate $ORIGIN processing at runtime
 188:   -z pack-relative-relocs     Alias for --pack-dyn-relocs=relr
 189:     -z nopack-relative-relocs
 190:   -z separate-loadable-segments
 191:                               Separate all loadable segments to different pages
 192:     -z separate-code          Separate code and data into different pages
 193:     -z noseparate-code        Allow overlap in pages
 194:   -z relro                    Make some sections read-only after relocation (default)
 195:     -z norelro
 196:   -z text                     Report error if DT_TEXTREL is set
 197:     -z notext
 198:     -z textoff
 199: 
 200: mold: supported targets: elf32-i386 elf64-x86-64 elf32-littlearm elf64-littleaarch64 elf32-littleriscv elf32-bigriscv elf64-littleriscv elf64-bigriscv elf32-powerpc elf64-powerpc elf64-powerpc elf64-powerpcle elf64-s390 elf64-sparc elf32-m68k elf32-sh-linux elf64-alpha
 201: mold: supported emulations: elf_i386 elf_x86_64 armelf_linux_eabi aarch64linux aarch64elf elf32lriscv elf32briscv elf64lriscv elf64briscv elf32ppc elf32ppclinux elf64ppc elf64lppc elf64_s390 elf64_sparc m68kelf shlelf_linux elf64alpha)";
 202: 
 203: static std::vector<std::string> add_dashes(std::string name) {
 204:   // Single-letter option
 205:   if (name.size() == 1)
 206:     return {"-" + name};
 207: 
 208:   // Multi-letter linker options can be preceded by either a single
 209:   // dash or double dashes except ones starting with "o", which must
 210:   // be preceded by double dashes. For example, "-omagic" is
 211:   // interpreted as "-o magic". If you really want to specify the
 212:   // "omagic" option, you have to pass "--omagic".
 213:   if (name[0] == 'o')
 214:     return {"--" + name};
 215:   return {"-" + name, "--" + name};
 216: }
 217: 
 218: template <typename E>
 219: static i64 parse_hex(Context<E> &ctx, std::string opt, std::string_view value) {
 220:   auto flags = std::regex_constants::optimize | std::regex_constants::ECMAScript;
 221:   static std::regex re(R"((?:0x|0X)?([0-9a-fA-F]+))", flags);
 222: 
 223:   std::cmatch m;
 224:   if (!std::regex_match(value.data(), value.data() + value.size(), m, re))
 225:     Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << opt << ": not a hexadecimal number";
 226:   return std::stoul(m[1], nullptr, 16);
 227: }
 228: 
 229: template <typename E>
 230: static i64 parse_number(Context<E> &ctx, std::string opt,
 231:                         std::string_view value) {
 232:   size_t nread;
 233: 
 234:   if (value.starts_with('-')) {
 235:     i64 ret = std::stoul(std::string(value.substr(1)), &nread, 0);
 236:     if (value.size() - 1 != nread)
 237:       Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << opt << ": not a number: " << value;
 238:     return -ret;
 239:   }
 240: 
 241:   i64 ret = std::stoul(std::string(value), &nread, 0);
 242:   if (value.size() != nread)
 243:     Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << opt << ": not a number: " << value;
 244:   return ret;
 245: }
 246: 
 247: template <typename E>
 248: static std::vector<u8> parse_hex_build_id(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view arg) {
 249:   auto flags = std::regex_constants::optimize | std::regex_constants::ECMAScript;
 250:   static std::regex re(R"(0[xX]([0-9a-fA-F][0-9a-fA-F])+)", flags);
 251: 
 252:   if (!std::regex_match(arg.begin(), arg.end(), re))
 253:     Fatal(ctx) << "invalid build-id: " << arg;
 254: 
 255:   arg = arg.substr(2);
 256: 
 257:   auto fn = [](char c) {
 258:     if ('0' <= c && c <= '9')
 259:       return c - '0';
 260:     if ('a' <= c && c <= 'f')
 261:       return c - 'a' + 10;
 262:     assert('A' <= c && c <= 'F');
 263:     return c - 'A' + 10;
 264:   };
 265: 
 266:   std::vector<u8> vec;
 267:   for (i64 i = 0; i < arg.size(); i += 2)
 268:     vec.push_back((fn(arg[i]) << 4) | fn(arg[i + 1]));
 269:   return vec;
 270: }
 271: 
 272: static std::vector<std::string_view>
 273: split_by_comma_or_colon(std::string_view str) {
 274:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
 275: 
 276:   for (;;) {
 277:     i64 pos = str.find_first_of(",:");
 278:     if (pos == str.npos) {
 279:       vec.push_back(str);
 280:       break;
 281:     }
 282:     vec.push_back(str.substr(0, pos));
 283:     str = str.substr(pos);
 284:   }
 285:   return vec;
 286: }
 287: 
 288: template <typename E>
 289: static void read_retain_symbols_file(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view path) {
 290:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
 291:     MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, std::string(path));
 292:   std::string_view data((char *)mf->data, mf->size);
 293: 
 294:   ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file.reset(new std::unordered_set<std::string_view>);
 295: 
 296:   while (!data.empty()) {
 297:     size_t pos = data.find('\n');
 298:     std::string_view name;
 299: 
 300:     if (pos == data.npos) {
 301:       name = data;
 302:       data = "";
 303:     } else {
 304:       name = data.substr(0, pos);
 305:       data = data.substr(pos + 1);
 306:     }
 307: 
 308:     name = string_trim(name);
 309:     if (!name.empty())
 310:       ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file->insert(name);
 311:   }
 312: }
 313: 
 314: static bool is_file(std::string_view path) {
 315:   struct stat st;
 316:   return stat(std::string(path).c_str(), &st) == 0 &&
 317:          (st.st_mode & S_IFMT) != S_IFDIR;
 318: }
 319: 
 320: template <typename E>
 321: static std::vector<SectionOrder>
 322: parse_section_order(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view arg) {
 323:   auto flags = std::regex_constants::ECMAScript | std::regex_constants::icase |
 324:                std::regex_constants::optimize;
 325:   static std::regex re1(R"(^\s*(TEXT|DATA|RODATA|BSS)(?:\s|$))", flags);
 326:   static std::regex re2(R"(^\s*([a-zA-Z0-9_.][^\s]*|EHDR|PHDR)(?:\s|$))", flags);
 327:   static std::regex re3(R"(^\s*=(0x[0-9a-f]+|\d+)(?:\s|$))", flags);
 328:   static std::regex re4(R"(^\s*%(0x[0-9a-f]+|\d*)(?:\s|$))", flags);
 329:   static std::regex re5(R"(^\s*!(\S+)(?:\s|$))", flags);
 330: 
 331:   std::vector<SectionOrder> vec;
 332:   arg = string_trim(arg);
 333: 
 334:   while (!arg.empty()) {
 335:     SectionOrder ord;
 336:     std::cmatch m;
 337: 
 338:     if (std::regex_search(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re1)) {
 339:       ord.type = SectionOrder::GROUP;
 340:       ord.name = m[1].str();
 341:     } else if (std::regex_search(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re2)) {
 342:       ord.type = SectionOrder::SECTION;
 343:       ord.name = m[1].str();
 344:     } else if (std::regex_search(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re3)) {
 345:       ord.type = SectionOrder::ADDR;
 346:       std::string s = m[1];
 347:       ord.value = std::stoull(s, nullptr, s.starts_with("0x") ? 16 : 10);
 348:     } else if (std::regex_search(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re4)) {
 349:       ord.type = SectionOrder::ALIGN;
 350:       std::string s = m[1];
 351:       ord.value = std::stoull(s, nullptr, s.starts_with("0x") ? 16 : 10);
 352:     } else if (std::regex_search(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re5)) {
 353:       ord.type = SectionOrder::SYMBOL;
 354:       ord.name = m[1].str();
 355:     } else {
 356:       Fatal(ctx) << "--section-order: parse error: " << arg;
 357:     }
 358: 
 359:     vec.push_back(ord);
 360:     arg = arg.substr(m[0].length());
 361:   }
 362: 
 363:   bool is_first = true;
 364:   for (SectionOrder &ord : vec) {
 365:     if (ord.type == SectionOrder::SECTION) {
 366:       if (is_first) {
 367:         is_first = false;
 368:       } else if (ord.name == "EHDR")
 369:         Fatal(ctx) << "--section-order: EHDR must be the first "
 370:                    << "section specifier: " << arg;
 371:     }
 372:   }
 373: 
 374:   return vec;
 375: }
 376: 
 377: template <typename E>
 378: static std::variant<Symbol<E> *, u64>
 379: parse_defsym_value(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view s) {
 380:   if (s.starts_with("0x") || s.starts_with("0X")) {
 381:     size_t nread;
 382:     u64 addr = std::stoull(std::string(s), &nread, 16);
 383:     if (s.size() != nread)
 384:       return {};
 385:     return addr;
 386:   }
 387: 
 388:   if (s.find_first_not_of("0123456789") == s.npos)
 389:     return (u64)std::stoull(std::string(s), nullptr, 10);
 390:   return get_symbol(ctx, s);
 391: }
 392: 
 393: template <typename E>
 394: std::vector<std::string> parse_nonpositional_args(Context<E> &ctx) {
 395:   std::span<std::string_view> args = ctx.cmdline_args;
 396:   args = args.subspan(1);
 397: 
 398:   std::vector<std::string> remaining;
 399:   std::string_view arg;
 400: 
 401:   ctx.page_size = E::page_size;
 402:   ctx.arg.color_diagnostics = isatty(STDERR_FILENO);
 403: 
 404:   bool version_shown = false;
 405:   bool warn_shared_textrel = false;
 406:   std::optional<SeparateCodeKind> z_separate_code;
 407:   std::optional<bool> z_relro;
 408:   std::unordered_set<std::string_view> rpaths;
 409: 
 410:   auto add_rpath = [&](std::string_view arg) {
 411:     if (rpaths.insert(arg).second) {
 412:       if (!ctx.arg.rpaths.empty())
 413:         ctx.arg.rpaths += ':';
 414:       ctx.arg.rpaths += arg;
 415:     }
 416:   };
 417: 
 418:   // RISC-V object files contains lots of local symbols, so by default
 419:   // we discard them. This is compatible with GNU ld.
 420:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>)
 421:     ctx.arg.discard_locals = true;
 422: 
 423:   // It looks like the SPARC's dynamic linker takes both RELA's r_addend
 424:   // and the value at the relocated place. So we don't want to write
 425:   // values to relocated places.
 426:   if (is_sparc<E>)
 427:     ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs = false;
 428: 
 429:   auto read_arg = [&](std::string name) {
 430:     for (std::string opt : add_dashes(name)) {
 431:       if (args[0] == opt) {
 432:         if (args.size() == 1)
 433:           Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << name << ": argument missing";
 434:         arg = args[1];
 435:         args = args.subspan(2);
 436:         return true;
 437:       }
 438: 
 439:       std::string prefix = (name.size() == 1) ? opt : opt + "=";
 440:       if (args[0].starts_with(prefix)) {
 441:         arg = args[0].substr(prefix.size());
 442:         args = args.subspan(1);
 443:         return true;
 444:       }
 445:     }
 446:     return false;
 447:   };
 448: 
 449:   auto read_eq = [&](std::string name) {
 450:     for (std::string opt : add_dashes(name)) {
 451:       if (args[0].starts_with(opt + "=")) {
 452:         arg = args[0].substr(opt.size() + 1);
 453:         args = args.subspan(1);
 454:         return true;
 455:       }
 456:     }
 457:     return false;
 458:   };
 459: 
 460:   auto read_flag = [&](std::string name) {
 461:     for (std::string opt : add_dashes(name)) {
 462:       if (args[0] == opt) {
 463:         args = args.subspan(1);
 464:         return true;
 465:       }
 466:     }
 467:     return false;
 468:   };
 469: 
 470:   auto read_z_flag = [&](std::string name) {
 471:     if (args.size() >= 2 && args[0] == "-z" && args[1] == name) {
 472:       args = args.subspan(2);
 473:       return true;
 474:     }
 475: 
 476:     if (!args.empty() && args[0] == "-z" + name) {
 477:       args = args.subspan(1);
 478:       return true;
 479:     }
 480:     return false;
 481:   };
 482: 
 483:   auto read_z_arg = [&](std::string name) {
 484:     if (args.size() >= 2 && args[0] == "-z" && args[1].starts_with(name + "=")) {
 485:       arg = args[1].substr(name.size() + 1);
 486:       args = args.subspan(2);
 487:       return true;
 488:     }
 489: 
 490:     if (!args.empty() && args[0].starts_with("-z" + name + "=")) {
 491:       arg = args[0].substr(name.size() + 3);
 492:       args = args.subspan(1);
 493:       return true;
 494:     }
 495:     return false;
 496:   };
 497: 
 498:   while (!args.empty()) {
 499:     if (read_flag("help")) {
 500:       SyncOut(ctx) << "Usage: " << ctx.cmdline_args[0]
 501:                    << " [options] file...\n" << helpmsg;
 502:       exit(0);
 503:     }
 504: 
 505:     if (read_arg("o") || read_arg("output")) {
 506:       ctx.arg.output = arg;
 507:     } else if (read_arg("dynamic-linker") || read_arg("I")) {
 508:       ctx.arg.dynamic_linker = arg;
 509:     } else if (read_flag("no-dynamic-linker")) {
 510:       ctx.arg.dynamic_linker = "";
 511:     } else if (read_flag("v")) {
 512:       SyncOut(ctx) << mold_version;
 513:       version_shown = true;
 514:     } else if (read_flag("version")) {
 515:       SyncOut(ctx) << mold_version;
 516:       exit(0);
 517:     } else if (read_flag("V")) {
 518:       SyncOut(ctx) << mold_version
 519:                    << "\n  Supported emulations:\n   elf_x86_64\n   elf_i386\n"
 520:                    << "   aarch64linux\n   armelf_linux_eabi\n   elf64lriscv\n"
 521:                    << "   elf64briscv\n   elf32lriscv\n   elf32briscv\n"
 522:                    << "   elf32ppc\n   elf64ppc\n   elf64lppc\n   elf64_s390\n"
 523:                    << "   elf64_sparc\n   m68kelf\n   shlelf_linux\n   elf64alpha";
 524:       version_shown = true;
 525:     } else if (read_arg("m")) {
 526:       if (arg == "elf_x86_64") {
 527:         ctx.arg.emulation = X86_64::target_name;
 528:       } else if (arg == "elf_i386") {
 529:         ctx.arg.emulation = I386::target_name;
 530:       } else if (arg == "aarch64linux") {
 531:         ctx.arg.emulation = ARM64::target_name;
 532:       } else if (arg == "armelf_linux_eabi") {
 533:         ctx.arg.emulation = ARM32::target_name;
 534:       } else if (arg == "elf64lriscv") {
 535:         ctx.arg.emulation = RV64LE::target_name;
 536:       } else if (arg == "elf64briscv") {
 537:         ctx.arg.emulation = RV64BE::target_name;
 538:       } else if (arg == "elf32lriscv") {
 539:         ctx.arg.emulation = RV32LE::target_name;
 540:       } else if (arg == "elf32briscv") {
 541:         ctx.arg.emulation = RV32BE::target_name;
 542:       } else if (arg == "elf32ppc" || arg == "elf32ppclinux") {
 543:         ctx.arg.emulation = PPC32::target_name;
 544:       } else if (arg == "elf64ppc") {
 545:         ctx.arg.emulation = PPC64V1::target_name;
 546:       } else if (arg == "elf64lppc") {
 547:         ctx.arg.emulation = PPC64V2::target_name;
 548:       } else if (arg == "elf64_s390") {
 549:         ctx.arg.emulation = S390X::target_name;
 550:       } else if (arg == "elf64_sparc") {
 551:         ctx.arg.emulation = SPARC64::target_name;
 552:       } else if (arg == "m68kelf") {
 553:         ctx.arg.emulation = M68K::target_name;
 554:       } else if (arg == "shlelf_linux") {
 555:         ctx.arg.emulation = SH4::target_name;
 556:       } else if (arg == "elf64alpha") {
 557:         ctx.arg.emulation = ALPHA::target_name;
 558:       } else {
 559:         Fatal(ctx) << "unknown -m argument: " << arg;
 560:       }
 561:     } else if (read_flag("end-lib")) {
 562:       remaining.push_back("--end-lib");
 563:     } else if (read_flag("export-dynamic") || read_flag("E")) {
 564:       ctx.arg.export_dynamic = true;
 565:     } else if (read_flag("no-export-dynamic")) {
 566:       ctx.arg.export_dynamic = false;
 567:     } else if (read_flag("Bsymbolic")) {
 568:       ctx.arg.Bsymbolic = true;
 569:     } else if (read_flag("Bsymbolic-functions")) {
 570:       ctx.arg.Bsymbolic_functions = true;
 571:     } else if (read_flag("Bno-symbolic")) {
 572:       ctx.arg.Bsymbolic = false;
 573:       ctx.arg.Bsymbolic_functions = false;
 574:     } else if (read_arg("exclude-libs")) {
 575:       append(ctx.arg.exclude_libs, split_by_comma_or_colon(arg));
 576:     } else if (read_flag("q") || read_flag("emit-relocs")) {
 577:       ctx.arg.emit_relocs = true;
 578:       ctx.arg.discard_locals = false;
 579:     } else if (read_arg("e") || read_arg("entry")) {
 580:       ctx.arg.entry = arg;
 581:     } else if (read_arg("Map")) {
 582:       ctx.arg.Map = arg;
 583:       ctx.arg.print_map = true;
 584:     } else if (read_flag("print-dependencies")) {
 585:       ctx.arg.print_dependencies = true;
 586:     } else if (read_flag("print-map") || read_flag("M")) {
 587:       ctx.arg.print_map = true;
 588:     } else if (read_flag("Bstatic") || read_flag("dn") || read_flag("static")) {
 589:       ctx.arg.is_static = true;
 590:       remaining.push_back("--Bstatic");
 591:     } else if (read_flag("Bdynamic") || read_flag("dy")) {
 592:       ctx.arg.is_static = false;
 593:       remaining.push_back("--Bdynamic");
 594:     } else if (read_flag("shared") || read_flag("Bshareable")) {
 595:       ctx.arg.shared = true;
 596:     } else if (read_arg("spare-dynamic-tags")) {
 597:       ctx.arg.spare_dynamic_tags = parse_number(ctx, "spare-dynamic-tags", arg);
 598:     } else if (read_flag("start-lib")) {
 599:       remaining.push_back("--start-lib");
 600:     } else if (read_flag("start-stop")) {
 601:       ctx.arg.start_stop = true;
 602:     } else if (read_arg("dependency-file")) {
 603:       ctx.arg.dependency_file = arg;
 604:     } else if (read_arg("defsym")) {
 605:       size_t pos = arg.find('=');
 606:       if (pos == arg.npos || pos == arg.size() - 1)
 607:         Fatal(ctx) << "-defsym: syntax error: " << arg;
 608:       ctx.arg.defsyms.emplace_back(get_symbol(ctx, arg.substr(0, pos)),
 609:                                    parse_defsym_value(ctx, arg.substr(pos + 1)));
 610:     } else if (read_flag(":lto-pass2")) {
 611:       ctx.arg.lto_pass2 = true;
 612:     } else if (read_arg(":ignore-ir-file")) {
 613:       ctx.arg.ignore_ir_file.insert(arg);
 614:     } else if (read_flag("demangle")) {
 615:       ctx.arg.demangle = true;
 616:     } else if (read_flag("no-demangle")) {
 617:       ctx.arg.demangle = false;
 618:     } else if (read_flag("default-symver")) {

 621:       ctx.arg.noinhibit_exec = true;

 640:       ctx.arg.sysroot = arg;

 645:       ctx.arg.unique = std::move(*pat);

1007:                    << ": -R as an alias for --just-symbols is not supported";
1008:       add_rpath(arg);
1009:     } else if (read_flag("undefined-version")) {
1010:       ctx.arg.undefined_version = true;
1011:     } else if (read_flag("no-undefined-version")) {
1012:       ctx.arg.undefined_version = false;
1013:     } else if (read_flag("build-id")) {
1014:       ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::HASH;
1015:       ctx.arg.build_id.hash_size = 20;
1016:     } else if (read_arg("build-id")) {
1017:       if (arg == "none") {
1018:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::NONE;
1019:       } else if (arg == "uuid") {
1020:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::UUID;
1021:       } else if (arg == "md5") {
1022:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::HASH;
1023:         ctx.arg.build_id.hash_size = 16;
1024:       } else if (arg == "sha1") {
1025:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::HASH;
1026:         ctx.arg.build_id.hash_size = 20;
1027:       } else if (arg == "sha256") {
1028:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::HASH;
1029:         ctx.arg.build_id.hash_size = 32;
1030:       } else if (arg.starts_with("0x") || arg.starts_with("0X")) {
1031:         ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::HEX;
1032:         ctx.arg.build_id.value = parse_hex_build_id(ctx, arg);
1033:       } else {
1034:         Fatal(ctx) << "invalid --build-id argument: " << arg;
1035:       }
1036:     } else if (read_flag("no-build-id")) {
1037:       ctx.arg.build_id.kind = BuildId::NONE;
1038:     } else if (read_arg("format") || read_arg("b")) {
1039:       if (arg == "binary")
1040:         Fatal(ctx)
1041:           << "mold does not support `-b binary`. If you want to convert a"
1042:           << " binary file into an object file, use `objcopy -I binary -O"
1043:           << " default <input-file> <output-file.o>` instead.";
1044:       Fatal(ctx) << "unknown command line option: -b " << arg;
1045:     } else if (read_arg("auxiliary") || read_arg("f")) {
1046:       ctx.arg.auxiliary.push_back(arg);
1047:     } else if (read_arg("filter") || read_arg("F")) {
1048:       ctx.arg.filter.push_back(arg);
1049:     } else if (read_arg("O")) {
1050:     } else if (read_flag("O0")) {
1051:     } else if (read_flag("O1")) {
1052:     } else if (read_flag("O2")) {
1053:     } else if (read_flag("verbose")) {
1054:     } else if (read_flag("color-diagnostics")) {
1055:     } else if (read_flag("eh-frame-hdr")) {
1056:     } else if (read_flag("start-group")) {
1057:     } else if (read_flag("end-group")) {
1058:     } else if (read_flag("(")) {
1059:     } else if (read_flag(")")) {
1060:     } else if (read_flag("fatal-warnings")) {
1061:     } else if (read_flag("enable-new-dtags")) {
1062:     } else if (read_flag("disable-new-dtags")) {
1063:     } else if (read_flag("nostdlib")) {
1064:     } else if (read_flag("allow-shlib-undefined")) {
1065:     } else if (read_flag("no-allow-shlib-undefined")) {
1066:     } else if (read_flag("no-add-needed")) {
1067:     } else if (read_flag("no-call-graph-profile-sort")) {
1068:     } else if (read_flag("no-copy-dt-needed-entries")) {
1069:     } else if (read_arg("sort-section")) {
1070:     } else if (read_flag("sort-common")) {
1071:     } else if (read_flag("dc")) {
1072:     } else if (read_flag("dp")) {
1073:     } else if (read_flag("fix-cortex-a53-835769")) {
1074:     } else if (read_flag("fix-cortex-a53-843419")) {
1075:     } else if (read_flag("EL")) {
1076:     } else if (read_flag("warn-once")) {
1077:     } else if (read_flag("nodefaultlibs")) {
1078:     } else if (read_flag("warn-constructors")) {
1079:     } else if (read_flag("warn-execstack")) {
1080:     } else if (read_flag("no-warn-execstack")) {
1081:     } else if (read_flag("secure-plt")) {
1082:     } else if (read_arg("rpath-link")) {
1083:     } else if (read_z_flag("combreloc")) {
1084:     } else if (read_z_flag("nocombreloc")) {
1085:     } else if (read_z_arg("common-page-size")) {
1086:     } else if (read_flag("no-keep-memory")) {
1087:     } else if (read_arg("max-cache-size")) {
1088:     } else if (read_arg("version-script")) {
1089:       // --version-script, --dynamic-list and --export-dynamic-symbol[-list]
1090:       // are treated as positional arguments even if they are actually not
1091:       // positional. This is because linker scripts (a positional argument)
1092:       // can also specify a version script, and it's better to consolidate
1093:       // parsing in read_input_files. In particular, version scripts can
1094:       // modify ctx.default_version which we initialize *after* parsing
1095:       // non-positional args, so the parsing cannot be done right here.
1096:       remaining.push_back("--version-script=" + std::string(arg));
1097:     } else if (read_arg("dynamic-list")) {
1098:       ctx.arg.Bsymbolic = true;
1099:       remaining.push_back("--dynamic-list=" + std::string(arg));
1100:     } else if (read_arg("export-dynamic-symbol")) {
1101:       remaining.push_back("--export-dynamic-symbol=" + std::string(arg));
1102:     } else if (read_arg("export-dynamic-symbol-list")) {
1103:       remaining.push_back("--export-dynamic-symbol-list=" + std::string(arg));
1104:     } else if (read_flag("as-needed")) {
1105:       remaining.push_back("--as-needed");
1106:     } else if (read_flag("no-as-needed")) {
1107:       remaining.push_back("--no-as-needed");
1108:     } else if (read_flag("whole-archive")) {
1109:       remaining.push_back("--whole-archive");
1110:     } else if (read_flag("no-whole-archive")) {
1111:       remaining.push_back("--no-whole-archive");
1112:     } else if (read_arg("l")) {
1113:       remaining.push_back("-l" + std::string(arg));
1114:     } else if (read_arg("script") || read_arg("T")) {
1115:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
1116:     } else if (read_flag("push-state")) {
1117:       remaining.push_back("--push-state");
1118:     } else if (read_flag("pop-state")) {
1119:       remaining.push_back("--pop-state");
1120:     } else if (args[0].starts_with("-z") && args[0].size() > 2) {
1121:       Warn(ctx) << "unknown command line option: " << args[0];
1122:       args = args.subspan(1);
1123:     } else if (args[0] == "-z" && args.size() >= 2) {
1124:       Warn(ctx) << "unknown command line option: -z " << args[1];
1125:       args = args.subspan(2);
1126:     } else if (args[0] == "-dynamic") {
1127:       Fatal(ctx) << "unknown command line option: -dynamic;"
1128:                  << " -dynamic is a macOS linker's option. If you are trying"
1129:                  << " to build a binary for an Apple platform, you need to use"
1130:                  << " ld64.mold instead of mold or ld.mold.";
1131:     } else {
1132:       if (args[0][0] == '-')
1133:         Fatal(ctx) << "unknown command line option: " << args[0];
1134:       remaining.push_back(std::string(args[0]));
1135:       args = args.subspan(1);
1136:     }
1137:   }
1138: 
1139:   if (!ctx.arg.sysroot.empty()) {
1140:     for (std::string &path : ctx.arg.library_paths) {
1141:       if (std::string_view(path).starts_with('='))
1142:         path = ctx.arg.sysroot + path.substr(1);
1143:       else if (std::string_view(path).starts_with("$SYSROOT"))
1144:         path = ctx.arg.sysroot + path.substr(8);
1145:     }
1146:   }
1147: 
1148:   // Clean library paths by removing redundant `/..` and `/.`
1149:   // so that they are easier to read in log messages.
1150:   for (std::string &path : ctx.arg.library_paths)
1151:     path = path_clean(path);
1152: 
1153:   if (ctx.arg.shared)
1154:     ctx.arg.pic = true;
1155: 
1156:   if (ctx.arg.pic)
1157:     ctx.arg.image_base = 0;
1158: 
1159:   if (ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file) {
1160:     ctx.arg.strip_all = false;
1161:     ctx.arg.discard_all = false;
1162:   }
1163: 
1164:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
1165:     ctx.arg.is_static = true;
1166: 
1167:   // --section-order implies `-z separate-loadable-segments`
1168:   if (z_separate_code)
1169:     ctx.arg.z_separate_code = *z_separate_code;
1170:   else if (!ctx.arg.section_order.empty())
1171:     ctx.arg.z_separate_code = SEPARATE_LOADABLE_SEGMENTS;
1172: 
1173:   // --section-order implies `-z norelro`
1174:   if (z_relro)
1175:     ctx.arg.z_relro = *z_relro;
1176:   else if (!ctx.arg.section_order.empty())
1177:     ctx.arg.z_relro = false;
1178: 
1179:   if (!ctx.arg.shared) {
1180:     if (!ctx.arg.filter.empty())
1181:       Fatal(ctx) << "-filter may not be used without -shared";
1182:     if (!ctx.arg.auxiliary.empty())
1183:       Fatal(ctx) << "-auxiliary may not be used without -shared";
1184:   }
1185: 
1186:   if (!ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs && !E::is_rela)
1187:     Fatal(ctx) << "--no-apply-dynamic-relocs may not be used on "
1188:                << E::target_name;
1189: 
1190:   if (is_sparc<E> && ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs)
1191:     Fatal(ctx) << "--apply-dynamic-relocs may not be used on SPARC64";
1192: 
1193:   if (!ctx.arg.section_start.empty() && !ctx.arg.section_order.empty())
1194:     Fatal(ctx) << "--section-start may not be used with --section-order";
1195: 
1196:   if (ctx.arg.image_base % ctx.page_size)
1197:     Fatal(ctx) << "-image-base must be a multiple of -max-page-size";
1198: 
1199:   if (ctx.arg.thread_count == 0)
1200:     ctx.arg.thread_count = get_default_thread_count();
1201: 
1202:   if (char *env = getenv("MOLD_REPRO"); env && env[0])
1203:     ctx.arg.repro = true;
1204: 
1205:   if (ctx.arg.shared || ctx.arg.export_dynamic)
1206:     ctx.default_version = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
1207:   else
1208:     ctx.default_version = VER_NDX_LOCAL;
1209: 
1210:   if (ctx.arg.default_symver) {
1211:     std::string ver = ctx.arg.soname.empty() ?
1212:       filepath(ctx.arg.output).filename().string() : std::string(ctx.arg.soname);
1213:     ctx.arg.version_definitions.push_back(ver);
1214:     ctx.default_version = VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED + 1;
1215:   }
1216: 
1217:   if (ctx.arg.shared && warn_shared_textrel)
1218:     ctx.arg.warn_textrel = true;
1219: 
1220:   ctx.arg.undefined.push_back(ctx.arg.entry);
1221: 
1222:   // --oformat=binary implies --strip-all because without a section
1223:   // header, there's no way to identify the locations of a symbol
1224:   // table in an output file in the first place.
1225:   if (ctx.arg.oformat_binary)
1226:     ctx.arg.strip_all = true;
1227: 
1228:   // By default, mold tries to ovewrite to an output file if exists
1229:   // because at least on Linux, writing to an existing file is much
1230:   // faster than creating a fresh file and writing to it.
1231:   //
1232:   // However, if an existing file is in use, writing to it will mess
1233:   // up processes that are executing that file. Linux prevents a write
1234:   // to a running executable file; it returns ETXTBSY on open(2).
1235:   // However, that mechanism doesn't protect .so files. Therefore, we
1236:   // want to disable this optimization if we are creating a shared
1237:   // object file.
1238:   if (ctx.arg.shared)
1239:     ctx.overwrite_output_file = false;
1240: 
1241:   if (version_shown && remaining.empty())
1242:     exit(0);
1243:   return remaining;
1244: }
1245: 
1246: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
1247: 
1248: template std::vector<std::string> parse_nonpositional_args(Context<E> &ctx);
1249: 
1250: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/dwarf.cc
================
  1: // This file contains code to read DWARF debug info to create .gdb_index.
  2: //
  3: // .gdb_index is an optional section to speed up GNU debugger. It contains
  4: // two maps: 1) a map from function/variable/type names to compunits, and
  5: // 2) a map from function address ranges to compunits. gdb uses these
  6: // maps to quickly find a compunit given a name or an instruction pointer.
  7: //
  8: // (Terminology: a compilation unit, which often abbreviated as compunit
  9: // or cu, is a unit of debug info. An input .debug_info section usually
 10: // contains one compunit, and thus an output .debug_info contains as
 11: // many compunits as the number of input files.)
 12: //
 13: // .gdb_index is not mandatory. All the information in .gdb_index is
 14: // also in other debug info sections. You can actually create an
 15: // executable without .gdb_index and later add it using `gdb-add-index`
 16: // post-processing tool that comes with gdb.
 17: //
 18: // The mapping from names to compunits is 1:n while the mapping from
 19: // address ranges to compunits is 1:1. That is, two object files may
 20: // define the same type name (with the same definition), while there
 21: // should be no two functions that overlap with each other in memory.
 22: //
 23: // .gdb_index contains an on-disk hash table for names, so gdb can
 24: // lookup names without loading all strings into memory and construct an
 25: // in-memory hash table.
 26: //
 27: // Names are in .debug_gnu_pubnames and .debug_gnu_pubtypes input
 28: // sections. These sections are created if `-ggnu-pubnames` is given.
 29: // Besides names, these sections contains attributes for each name so
 30: // that gdb can distinguish type names from function names, for example.
 31: //
 32: // A compunit contains one or more function address ranges. If an
 33: // object file is compiled without -ffunction-sections, it contains
 34: // only one .text section and therefore contains a single address range.
 35: // Such range is typically stored directly to the compunit.
 36: //
 37: // If an object file is compiled with -fucntion-sections, it contains
 38: // more than one .text section, and it has as many address ranges as
 39: // the number of .text sections. Such discontiguous address ranges are
 40: // stored to .debug_ranges in DWARF 2/3/4/5 and
 41: // .debug_rnglists/.debug_addr in DWARF 5.
 42: //
 43: // .debug_info section contains DWARF debug info. Although we don't need
 44: // to parse the whole .debug_info section to read address ranges, we
 45: // have to do a little bit. DWARF is complicated and often handled using
 46: // a library such as libdwarf. But we don't use any library because we
 47: // don't want to add an extra run-time dependency just for --gdb-index.
 48: //
 49: // This page explains the format of .gdb_index:
 50: // https://sourceware.org/gdb/onlinedocs/gdb/Index-Section-Format.html
 51: 
 52: #include "mold.h"
 53: 
 54: namespace mold::elf {
 55: 
 56: // The hash function for .gdb_index.
 57: static u32 gdb_hash(std::string_view name) {
 58:   u32 h = 0;
 59:   for (u8 c : name) {
 60:     if ('A' <= c && c <= 'Z')
 61:       c = 'a' + c - 'A';
 62:     h = h * 67 + c - 113;
 63:   }
 64:   return h;
 65: }
 66: 
 67: // Split .debug_info into so-called "compilation units". A .debug_info
 68: // section usually contains one compunit unless it was created by `ld -r`.
 69: // This is for --gdb-index.
 70: template <typename E>
 71: std::vector<std::string_view>
 72: read_compunits(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file) {
 73:   file.debug_info->uncompress(ctx);
 74:   std::string_view data = file.debug_info->contents;
 75:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
 76: 
 77:   while (!data.empty()) {
 78:     if (data.size() < 4)
 79:       Fatal(ctx) << *file.debug_info << ": corrupted .debug_info";
 80:     if (*(U32<E> *)data.data() == 0xffff'ffff)
 81:       Fatal(ctx) << *file.debug_info << ": --gdb-index: DWARF64 not supported";
 82:     i64 len = *(U32<E> *)data.data() + 4;
 83:     vec.push_back(data.substr(0, len));
 84:     data = data.substr(len);
 85:   }
 86:   return vec;
 87: }
 88: 
 89: // Parses .debug_gnu_pubnames and .debug_gnu_pubtypes. These sections
 90: // start with a 14 bytes header followed by (4-byte offset, 1-byte type,
 91: // null-terminated string) tuples.
 92: //
 93: // The 4-byte offset is an offset into .debug_info that contains details
 94: // about the name. The 1-byte type is a type of the corresponding name
 95: // (e.g. function, variable or datatype). The string is a name of a
 96: // function, a variable or a type.
 97: template <typename E>
 98: std::vector<GdbIndexName> read_pubnames(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file) {
 99:   std::vector<GdbIndexName> vec;
100: 
101:   auto get_cu_idx = [&](InputSection<E> &isec, i64 offset) {
102:     i64 off = 0;
103:     for (i64 i = 0; i < file.compunits.size(); i++) {
104:       if (offset == off)
105:         return file.compunits_idx + i;
106:       off += file.compunits[i].size();
107:     }
108:     Fatal(ctx) << isec << ": corrupted debug_info_offset";
109:   };
110: 
111:   auto read = [&](InputSection<E> &isec) {
112:     isec.uncompress(ctx);
113:     std::string_view contents = isec.contents;
114: 
115:     while (!contents.empty()) {
116:       if (contents.size() < 14)
117:         Fatal(ctx) << isec << ": corrupted header";
118: 
119:       u32 len = *(U32<E> *)contents.data() + 4;
120:       u32 debug_info_offset = *(U32<E> *)(contents.data() + 6);
121:       u32 cu_idx = get_cu_idx(isec, debug_info_offset);
122: 
123:       std::string_view data = contents.substr(14, len - 14);
124:       contents = contents.substr(len);
125: 
126:       while (!data.empty()) {
127:         u32 offset = *(U32<E> *)data.data();
128:         data = data.substr(4);
129:         if (offset == 0)
130:           break;
131: 
132:         u8 type = data[0];
133:         data = data.substr(1);
134: 
135:         std::string_view name = data.data();
136:         data = data.substr(name.size() + 1);
137: 
138:         vec.push_back({name, gdb_hash(name), (type << 24) | cu_idx});
139:       }
140:     }
141:   };
142: 
143:   if (file.debug_pubnames)
144:     read(*file.debug_pubnames);
145:   if (file.debug_pubtypes)
146:     read(*file.debug_pubtypes);
147: 
148:   // Uniquify elements because GCC 11 seems to emit one record for each
149:   // comdat group which results in having a lot of duplicate records.
150:   auto less = [](const GdbIndexName &a, const GdbIndexName &b) {
151:     return std::tuple{a.hash, a.attr, a.name} <
152:            std::tuple{b.hash, b.attr, b.name};
153:   };
154: 
155:   auto equal = [](const GdbIndexName &a, const GdbIndexName &b) {
156:     return std::tuple{a.hash, a.attr, a.name} ==
157:            std::tuple{b.hash, b.attr, b.name};
158:   };
159: 
160:   std::sort(vec.begin(), vec.end(), less);
161:   vec.erase(std::unique(vec.begin(), vec.end(), equal), vec.end());
162:   return vec;
163: }
164: 
165: template <typename E>
166: static u8 *get_buffer(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> *chunk) {
167:   if (u8 *buf = chunk->get_uncompressed_data())
168:     return buf;
169:   return ctx.buf + chunk->shdr.sh_offset;
170: }
171: 
172: // Try to find a compilation unit from .debug_info and its
173: // corresponding record from .debug_abbrev and returns them.
174: template <typename E>
175: static std::tuple<u8 *, u8 *, u32>
176: find_compunit(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, i64 offset) {
177:   // Read .debug_info to find the record at a given offset.
178:   u8 *cu = get_buffer(ctx, ctx.debug_info) + offset;
179:   u32 dwarf_version = *(U16<E> *)(cu + 4);
180:   u32 abbrev_offset;
181: 
182:   // Skip a header.
183:   switch (dwarf_version) {
184:   case 2:
185:   case 3:
186:   case 4:
187:     abbrev_offset = *(U32<E> *)(cu + 6);
188:     if (u32 address_size = cu[10]; address_size != sizeof(Word<E>))
189:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unsupported address size "
190:                  << address_size;
191:     cu += 11;
192:     break;
193:   case 5: {
194:     abbrev_offset = *(U32<E> *)(cu + 8);
195:     if (u32 address_size = cu[7]; address_size != sizeof(Word<E>))
196:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unsupported address size "
197:                  << address_size;
198: 
199:     switch (u32 unit_type = cu[6]; unit_type) {
200:     case DW_UT_compile:
201:     case DW_UT_partial:
202:       cu += 12;
203:       break;
204:     case DW_UT_skeleton:
205:     case DW_UT_split_compile:
206:       cu += 20;
207:       break;
208:     default:
209:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unknown DW_UT_* value: 0x"
210:                  << std::hex << unit_type;
211:     }
212:     break;
213:   }
214:   default:
215:     Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unknown DWARF version: "
216:                << dwarf_version;
217:   }
218: 
219:   u32 abbrev_code = read_uleb(cu);
220: 
221:   // Find a .debug_abbrev record corresponding to the .debug_info record.
222:   // We assume the .debug_info record at a given offset is of
223:   // DW_TAG_compile_unit which describes a compunit.
224:   u8 *abbrev = get_buffer(ctx, ctx.debug_abbrev) + abbrev_offset;
225: 
226:   for (;;) {
227:     u32 code = read_uleb(abbrev);
228:     if (code == 0)
229:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: .debug_abbrev does not contain"
230:                  << " a record for the first .debug_info record";
231: 
232:     if (code == abbrev_code) {
233:       // Found a record
234:       u64 abbrev_tag = read_uleb(abbrev);
235:       if (abbrev_tag != DW_TAG_compile_unit && abbrev_tag != DW_TAG_skeleton_unit)
236:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: the first entry's tag is not"
237:                    << " DW_TAG_compile_unit/DW_TAG_skeleton_unit but 0x"
238:                    << std::hex << abbrev_tag;
239:       break;
240:     }
241: 
242:     // Skip an uninteresting record
243:     read_uleb(abbrev); // tag
244:     abbrev++; // has_children byte
245:     for (;;) {
246:       u64 name = read_uleb(abbrev);
247:       u64 form = read_uleb(abbrev);
248:       if (name == 0 && form == 0)
249:         break;
250:       if (form == DW_FORM_implicit_const)
251:         read_uleb(abbrev);
252:     }
253:   }
254: 
255:   abbrev++; // skip has_children byte
256:   return {cu, abbrev, dwarf_version};
257: }
258: 
259: // Estimate the number of address ranges contained in a given file.
260: // It may over-estimate but never under-estimate.
261: template <typename E>
262: i64 estimate_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file) {
263:   // Each CU contains zero or one address area.
264:   i64 ret = file.compunits.size();
265: 
266:   // In DWARF 4, a CU can refer address ranges in .debug_ranges.
267:   // .debug_ranges contains a vector of [begin, end) address pairs.
268:   // The last entry must be a null terminator, so we do -1.
269:   if (file.debug_ranges)
270:     ret += file.debug_ranges->sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>) / 2 - 1;
271: 
272:   // In DWARF 5, a CU can refer address ranges in .debug_rnglists, which
273:   // contains variable-length entries. The smallest possible range entry
274:   // is one byte for the code and two ULEB128 values (each can be as
275:   // small as one byte), so 3 bytes.
276:   if (file.debug_rnglists)
277:     ret += file.debug_rnglists->sh_size / 3;
278:   return ret;
279: }
280: 
281: // .debug_info contains variable-length fields. This class reads them.
282: template <typename E>
283: class DebugInfoReader {
284: public:
285:   DebugInfoReader(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, u8 *cu)
286:     : ctx(ctx), file(file), cu(cu) {}
287: 
288:   u64 read(u64 form);
289: 
290:   Context<E> &ctx;
291:   ObjectFile<E> &file;
292:   u8 *cu;
293: };
294: 
295: // Read value of the given DW_FORM_* form. If a value is not scalar,
296: // returns a dummy value 0.
297: template <typename E>
298: u64 DebugInfoReader<E>::read(u64 form) {
299:   switch (form) {
300:   case DW_FORM_flag_present:
301:     return 0;
302:   case DW_FORM_data1:
303:   case DW_FORM_flag:
304:   case DW_FORM_strx1:
305:   case DW_FORM_addrx1:
306:   case DW_FORM_ref1:
307:     return *cu++;
308:   case DW_FORM_data2:
309:   case DW_FORM_strx2:
310:   case DW_FORM_addrx2:
311:   case DW_FORM_ref2: {
312:     u64 val = *(U16<E> *)cu;
313:     cu += 2;
314:     return val;
315:   }
316:   case DW_FORM_strx3:
317:   case DW_FORM_addrx3: {
318:     u64 val = *(U24<E> *)cu;
319:     cu += 3;
320:     return val;
321:   }
322:   case DW_FORM_data4:
323:   case DW_FORM_strp:
324:   case DW_FORM_sec_offset:
325:   case DW_FORM_line_strp:
326:   case DW_FORM_strx4:
327:   case DW_FORM_addrx4:
328:   case DW_FORM_ref4: {
329:     u64 val = *(U32<E> *)cu;
330:     cu += 4;
331:     return val;
332:   }
333:   case DW_FORM_data8:
334:   case DW_FORM_ref8: {
335:     u64 val = *(U64<E> *)cu;
336:     cu += 8;
337:     return val;
338:   }
339:   case DW_FORM_addr:
340:   case DW_FORM_ref_addr: {
341:     u64 val = *(Word<E> *)cu;
342:     cu += sizeof(Word<E>);
343:     return val;
344:   }
345:   case DW_FORM_strx:
346:   case DW_FORM_addrx:
347:   case DW_FORM_udata:
348:   case DW_FORM_ref_udata:
349:   case DW_FORM_loclistx:
350:   case DW_FORM_rnglistx:
351:     return read_uleb(cu);
352:   case DW_FORM_string:
353:     cu += strlen((char *)cu) + 1;
354:     return 0;
355:   default:
356:     Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unhandled debug info form: 0x"
357:                << std::hex << form;
358:     return 0;
359:   }
360: }
361: 
362: // Read a range list from .debug_ranges starting at the given offset.
363: template <typename E>
364: static std::vector<u64>
365: read_debug_range(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, Word<E> *range) {
366:   std::vector<u64> vec;
367:   u64 base = 0;
368: 
369:   for (i64 i = 0; range[i] || range[i + 1]; i += 2) {
370:     if (range[i] + 1 == 0) {
371:       // base address selection entry
372:       base = range[i + 1];
373:     } else {
374:       vec.push_back(range[i] + base);
375:       vec.push_back(range[i + 1] + base);
376:     }
377:   }
378:   return vec;
379: }
380: 
381: // Read a range list from .debug_rnglists starting at the given offset.
382: template <typename E>
383: static std::vector<u64>
384: read_rnglist_range(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, u8 *rnglist,
385:                    Word<E> *addrx) {
386:   std::vector<u64> vec;
387:   u64 base = 0;
388: 
389:   for (;;) {
390:     switch (*rnglist++) {
391:     case DW_RLE_end_of_list:
392:       return vec;
393:     case DW_RLE_base_addressx:
394:       base = addrx[read_uleb(rnglist)];
395:       break;
396:     case DW_RLE_startx_endx:
397:       vec.push_back(addrx[read_uleb(rnglist)]);
398:       vec.push_back(addrx[read_uleb(rnglist)]);
399:       break;
400:     case DW_RLE_startx_length:
401:       vec.push_back(addrx[read_uleb(rnglist)]);
402:       vec.push_back(vec.back() + read_uleb(rnglist));
403:       break;
404:     case DW_RLE_offset_pair:
405:       vec.push_back(base + read_uleb(rnglist));
406:       vec.push_back(base + read_uleb(rnglist));
407:       break;
408:     case DW_RLE_base_address:
409:       base = *(Word<E> *)rnglist;
410:       rnglist += sizeof(Word<E>);
411:       break;
412:     case DW_RLE_start_end:
413:       vec.push_back(*(Word<E> *)rnglist);
414:       rnglist += sizeof(Word<E>);
415:       vec.push_back(*(Word<E> *)rnglist);
416:       rnglist += sizeof(Word<E>);
417:       break;
418:     case DW_RLE_start_length:
419:       vec.push_back(*(Word<E> *)rnglist);
420:       rnglist += sizeof(Word<E>);
421:       vec.push_back(vec.back() + read_uleb(rnglist));
422:       break;
423:     }
424:   }
425: }
426: 
427: // Returns a list of address ranges explained by a compunit at the
428: // `offset` in an output .debug_info section.
429: //
430: // .debug_info contains DWARF debug info records, so this function
431: // parses DWARF. If a designated compunit contains multiple ranges, the
432: // ranges are read from .debug_ranges (or .debug_rnglists for DWARF5).
433: // Otherwise, a range is read directly from .debug_info (or possibly
434: // from .debug_addr for DWARF5).
435: template <typename E>
436: std::vector<u64>
437: read_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, i64 offset) {
438:   u8 *cu;
439:   u8 *abbrev;
440:   u32 dwarf_version;
441:   std::tie(cu, abbrev, dwarf_version) = find_compunit(ctx, file, offset);
442: 
443:   DebugInfoReader<E> reader{ctx, file, cu};
444: 
445:   struct Record {
446:     u64 form = 0;
447:     u64 value = 0;
448:   };
449: 
450:   Record low_pc;
451:   Record high_pc;
452:   Record ranges;
453:   std::optional<u64> rnglists_base;
454:   Word<E> *addrx = nullptr;
455: 
456:   // Read all interesting debug records.
457:   for (;;) {
458:     u64 name = read_uleb(abbrev);
459:     u64 form = read_uleb(abbrev);
460:     if (name == 0 && form == 0)
461:       break;
462: 
463:     u64 val = reader.read(form);
464: 
465:     switch (name) {
466:     case DW_AT_low_pc:
467:       low_pc = {form, val};
468:       break;
469:     case DW_AT_high_pc:
470:       high_pc = {form, val};
471:       break;
472:     case DW_AT_rnglists_base:
473:       rnglists_base = val;
474:       break;
475:     case DW_AT_addr_base:
476:       addrx = (Word<E> *)(get_buffer(ctx, ctx.debug_addr) + val);
477:       break;
478:     case DW_AT_ranges:
479:       ranges = {form, val};
480:       break;
481:     }
482:   }
483: 
484:   // Handle non-contiguous address ranges.
485:   if (ranges.form) {
486:     if (dwarf_version <= 4) {
487:        Word<E> *range_begin =
488:         (Word<E> *)(get_buffer(ctx, ctx.debug_ranges) + ranges.value);
489:       return read_debug_range(ctx, file, range_begin);
490:     }
491: 
492:     assert(dwarf_version == 5);
493: 
494:     u8 *buf = get_buffer(ctx, ctx.debug_rnglists);
495:     if (ranges.form == DW_FORM_sec_offset)
496:       return read_rnglist_range(ctx, file, buf + ranges.value, addrx);
497: 
498:     if (!rnglists_base)
499:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: missing DW_AT_rnglists_base";
500: 
501:     u8 *base = buf + *rnglists_base;
502:     return read_rnglist_range(ctx, file, base + *(U32<E> *)base, addrx);
503:   }
504: 
505:   // Handle a contiguous address range.
506:   if (low_pc.form && high_pc.form) {
507:     u64 lo;
508: 
509:     switch (low_pc.form) {
510:     case DW_FORM_addr:
511:       lo = low_pc.value;
512:       break;
513:     case DW_FORM_addrx:
514:     case DW_FORM_addrx1:
515:     case DW_FORM_addrx2:
516:     case DW_FORM_addrx4:
517:       lo = addrx[low_pc.value];
518:       break;
519:     default:
520:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unhandled form for DW_AT_low_pc: 0x"
521:                  << std::hex << high_pc.form;
522:     }
523: 
524:     switch (high_pc.form) {
525:     case DW_FORM_addr:
526:       return {lo, high_pc.value};
527:     case DW_FORM_addrx:
528:     case DW_FORM_addrx1:
529:     case DW_FORM_addrx2:
530:     case DW_FORM_addrx4:
531:       return {lo, addrx[high_pc.value]};
532:     case DW_FORM_udata:
533:     case DW_FORM_data1:
534:     case DW_FORM_data2:
535:     case DW_FORM_data4:
536:     case DW_FORM_data8:
537:       return {lo, lo + high_pc.value};
538:     default:
539:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": --gdb-index: unhandled form for DW_AT_high_pc: 0x"
540:                  << std::hex << high_pc.form;
541:     }
542:   }
543: 
544:   return {};
545: }
546: 
547: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
548: 
549: template std::vector<std::string_view> read_compunits(Context<E> &, ObjectFile<E> &);
550: template std::vector<GdbIndexName> read_pubnames(Context<E> &, ObjectFile<E> &);
551: template i64 estimate_address_areas(Context<E> &, ObjectFile<E> &);
552: template std::vector<u64> read_address_areas(Context<E> &, ObjectFile<E> &, i64);
553: 
554: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/gc-sections.cc
================
  1: // This file implements a mark-sweep garbage collector for -gc-sections.
  2: // In this algorithm, vertices are sections and edges are relocations.
  3: // Any section that is reachable from a root section is considered alive.
  4: 
  5: #include "mold.h"
  6: 
  7: #include <tbb/concurrent_vector.h>
  8: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
  9: 
 10: namespace mold::elf {
 11: 
 12: template <typename E>
 13: static bool should_keep(const InputSection<E> &isec) {
 14:   u32 type = isec.shdr().sh_type;
 15:   u32 flags = isec.shdr().sh_flags;
 16:   std::string_view name = isec.name();
 17: 
 18:   return (flags & SHF_GNU_RETAIN) ||
 19:          type == SHT_NOTE ||
 20:          type == SHT_INIT_ARRAY ||
 21:          type == SHT_FINI_ARRAY ||
 22:          type == SHT_PREINIT_ARRAY ||
 23:          (is_arm32<E> && type == SHT_ARM_EXIDX) ||
 24:          name.starts_with(".ctors") ||
 25:          name.starts_with(".dtors") ||
 26:          name.starts_with(".init") ||
 27:          name.starts_with(".fini") ||
 28:          is_c_identifier(name);
 29: }
 30: 
 31: template <typename E>
 32: static bool mark_section(InputSection<E> *isec) {
 33:   return isec && isec->is_alive && !isec->is_visited.test_and_set();
 34: }
 35: 
 36: template <typename E>
 37: static void visit(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> *isec,
 38:                   tbb::feeder<InputSection<E> *> &feeder, i64 depth) {
 39:   assert(isec->is_visited);
 40: 
 41:   // If this is a text section, .eh_frame may contain records
 42:   // describing how to handle exceptions for that function.
 43:   // We want to keep associated .eh_frame records.
 44:   for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : isec->get_fdes())
 45:     for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : fde.get_rels(isec->file).subspan(1))
 46:       if (Symbol<E> *sym = isec->file.symbols[rel.r_sym])
 47:         if (mark_section(sym->get_input_section()))
 48:           feeder.add(sym->get_input_section());
 49: 
 50:   for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : isec->get_rels(ctx)) {
 51:     Symbol<E> &sym = *isec->file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
 52: 
 53:     // Symbol can refer either a section fragment or an input section.
 54:     // Mark a fragment as alive.
 55:     if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag()) {
 56:       frag->is_alive = true;
 57:       continue;
 58:     }
 59: 
 60:     // Mark a section alive. For better performacne, we don't call
 61:     // `feeder.add` too often.
 62:     if (mark_section(sym.get_input_section())) {
 63:       if (depth < 3)
 64:         visit(ctx, sym.get_input_section(), feeder, depth + 1);
 65:       else
 66:         feeder.add(sym.get_input_section());
 67:     }
 68:   }
 69: }
 70: 
 71: template <typename E>
 72: static void collect_root_set(Context<E> &ctx,
 73:                              tbb::concurrent_vector<InputSection<E> *> &rootset) {
 74:   Timer t(ctx, "collect_root_set");
 75: 
 76:   auto enqueue_section = [&](InputSection<E> *isec) {
 77:     if (mark_section(isec))
 78:       rootset.push_back(isec);
 79:   };
 80: 
 81:   auto enqueue_symbol = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 82:     if (sym) {
 83:       if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym->get_frag())
 84:         frag->is_alive = true;
 85:       else
 86:         enqueue_section(sym->get_input_section());
 87:     }
 88:   };
 89: 
 90:   // Add sections that are not subject to garbage collection.
 91:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 92:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
 93:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive)
 94:         continue;
 95: 
 96:       // --gc-sections discards only SHF_ALLOC sections. If you want to
 97:       // reduce the amount of non-memory-mapped segments, you should
 98:       // use `strip` command, compile without debug info or use
 99:       // --strip-all linker option.
100:       u32 flags = isec->shdr().sh_flags;
101:       if (!(flags & SHF_ALLOC))
102:         isec->is_visited = true;
103: 
104:       if (should_keep(*isec))
105:         enqueue_section(isec.get());
106:     }
107:   });
108: 
109:   // Add sections containing exported symbols
110:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
111:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->symbols)
112:       if (sym->file == file && sym->is_exported)
113:         enqueue_symbol(sym);
114:   });
115: 
116:   // Add sections referenced by root symbols.
117:   enqueue_symbol(get_symbol(ctx, ctx.arg.entry));
118: 
119:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.undefined)
120:     enqueue_symbol(get_symbol(ctx, name));
121: 
122:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.require_defined)
123:     enqueue_symbol(get_symbol(ctx, name));
124: 
125:   // .eh_frame consists of variable-length records called CIE and FDE
126:   // records, and they are a unit of inclusion or exclusion.
127:   // We just keep all CIEs and everything that are referenced by them.
128:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
129:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies)
130:       for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : cie.get_rels())
131:         enqueue_symbol(file->symbols[rel.r_sym]);
132:   });
133: }
134: 
135: // Mark all reachable sections
136: template <typename E>
137: static void mark(Context<E> &ctx,
138:                  tbb::concurrent_vector<InputSection<E> *> &rootset) {
139:   Timer t(ctx, "mark");
140: 
141:   tbb::parallel_for_each(rootset, [&](InputSection<E> *isec,
142:                                     tbb::feeder<InputSection<E> *> &feeder) {
143:     visit(ctx, isec, feeder, 0);
144:   });
145: }
146: 
147: // Remove unreachable sections
148: template <typename E>
149: static void sweep(Context<E> &ctx) {
150:   Timer t(ctx, "sweep");
151:   static Counter counter("garbage_sections");
152: 
153:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
154:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
155:       if (isec && isec->is_alive && !isec->is_visited) {
156:         if (ctx.arg.print_gc_sections)
157:           SyncOut(ctx) << "removing unused section " << *isec;
158:         isec->kill();
159:         counter++;
160:       }
161:     }
162:   });
163: }
164: 
165: template <typename E>
166: void gc_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
167:   Timer t(ctx, "gc");
168: 
169:   tbb::concurrent_vector<InputSection<E> *> rootset;
170:   collect_root_set(ctx, rootset);
171:   mark(ctx, rootset);
172:   sweep(ctx);
173: }
174: 
175: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
176: 
177: template void gc_sections(Context<E> &ctx);
178: 
179: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/icf.cc
================
  1: // This file implements the Identical Code Folding feature which can
  2: // reduce the output file size of a typical program by a few percent.
  3: // ICF identifies read-only input sections that happen to be identical
  4: // and thus can be used interchangeably. ICF leaves one of them and discards
  5: // the others.
  6: //
  7: // ICF is usually used in combination with -ffunction-sections and
  8: // -fdata-sections compiler options, so that object files have one section
  9: // for each function or variable instead of having one large .text or .data.
 10: // The unit of ICF merging is section.
 11: //
 12: // Two sections are considered identical by ICF if they have the exact
 13: // same contents, metadata such as section flags, exception handling
 14: // records, and relocations. The last one is interesting because two
 15: // relocations are considered identical if they point to the _same_
 16: // section in terms of ICF.
 17: //
 18: // To see what that means, consider two sections, A and B, which are
 19: // identical except for one pair of relocations. Say, A has a relocation to
 20: // section C, and B has a relocation to D. In this case, A and B are
 21: // considered identical if C and D are considered identical. C and D can be
 22: // either really the same section or two different sections that are
 23: // considered identical by ICF. Below is an example of such inputs, A, B, C
 24: // and D:
 25: //
 26: //   void A() { C(); }
 27: //   void B() { D(); }
 28: //   void C() { A(); }
 29: //   void D() { B(); }
 30: //
 31: // If we assume A and B are mergeable, we can merge C and D, which makes A
 32: // and B mergeable. There's no contradiction in our assumption, so we can
 33: // conclude that A and B as well as C and D are mergeable.
 34: //
 35: // This problem boils down to one in graph theory. Input to ICF can be
 36: // considered as a directed graph in which vertices are sections and edges
 37: // are relocations. Vertices have labels (section contents, etc.), and so
 38: // are edges (relocation offsets, etc.). Two vertices are considered
 39: // identical if and only if the (possibly infinite) their unfoldings into
 40: // regular trees are equal. Given this formulation, we want to find as
 41: // many identical vertices as possible.
 42: //
 43: // Just like a lot of problems with graph, this problem doesn't have a
 44: // straightforward "optimal" solution, and we need to resort to heuristics.
 45: //
 46: // mold approaches this problem by hashing program trees with increasing depth
 47: // on each iteration.
 48: // For example, when we start, we only hash individual functions with
 49: // their call into other functions omitted. From the second iteration, we
 50: // put the function they call into the hash by appending the hash of those
 51: // functions from the previous iteration. This means that the nth iteration
 52: // hashes call chain up to (n-1) levels deep.
 53: // We use a cryptographic hash function, so the unique number of hashes will
 54: // only monotonically increase as we take into account of deeper trees with
 55: // iterations (otherwise, that means we have found a hash collision). We stop
 56: // when the unique number of hashes stop increasing; this is based on the fact
 57: // that once we observe an iteration with the same amount of unique hashes as
 58: // the previous iteration, it will remain unchanged for further iterations.
 59: // This is provable, but here we omit the proof for brevity.
 60: //
 61: // When compared to other approaches, mold's approach has a relatively cheaper
 62: // cost per iteration, and as a bonus, is highly parallelizable.
 63: // For Chromium, mold's ICF finishes in less than 1 second with 20 threads,
 64: // whereas lld takes 5 seconds and gold takes 50 seconds under the same
 65: // conditions.
 66: 
 67: #include "mold.h"
 68: #include "../common/sha.h"
 69: 
 70: #include <array>
 71: #include <cstdio>
 72: #include <tbb/concurrent_unordered_map.h>
 73: #include <tbb/concurrent_vector.h>
 74: #include <tbb/enumerable_thread_specific.h>
 75: #include <tbb/parallel_for.h>
 76: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 77: #include <tbb/parallel_sort.h>
 78: 
 79: static constexpr int64_t HASH_SIZE = 16;
 80: 
 81: typedef std::array<uint8_t, HASH_SIZE> Digest;
 82: 
 83: namespace std {
 84: template<> struct hash<Digest> {
 85:   size_t operator()(const Digest &k) const {
 86:     return *(int64_t *)&k[0];
 87:   }
 88: };
 89: }
 90: 
 91: namespace mold::elf {
 92: 
 93: template <typename E>
 94: static void uniquify_cies(Context<E> &ctx) {
 95:   Timer t(ctx, "uniquify_cies");
 96:   std::vector<CieRecord<E> *> cies;
 97: 
 98:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 99:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies) {
100:       for (i64 i = 0; i < cies.size(); i++) {
101:         if (cie.equals(*cies[i])) {
102:           cie.icf_idx = i;
103:           goto found;
104:         }
105:       }
106:       cie.icf_idx = cies.size();
107:       cies.push_back(&cie);
108:     found:;
109:     }
110:   }
111: }
112: 
113: template <typename E>
114: static bool is_eligible(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec) {
115:   const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = isec.shdr();
116:   std::string_view name = isec.name();
117: 
118:   bool is_alloc = (shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
119:   bool is_exec = (shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) ||
120:                  ctx.arg.ignore_data_address_equality;
121:   bool is_relro = (name == ".data.rel.ro" ||
122:                    name.starts_with(".data.rel.ro."));
123:   bool is_readonly = !(shdr.sh_flags & SHF_WRITE) || is_relro;
124:   bool is_bss = (shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS);
125:   bool is_empty = (shdr.sh_size == 0);
126:   bool is_init = (shdr.sh_type == SHT_INIT_ARRAY || name == ".init");
127:   bool is_fini = (shdr.sh_type == SHT_FINI_ARRAY || name == ".fini");
128:   bool is_enumerable = is_c_identifier(name);
129:   bool is_addr_taken = !ctx.arg.icf_all && isec.address_significant;
130: 
131:   return is_alloc && is_exec && is_readonly && !is_bss && !is_empty &&
132:          !is_init && !is_fini && !is_enumerable && !is_addr_taken;
133: }
134: 
135: static Digest digest_final(SHA256Hash &sha) {
136:   u8 buf[SHA256_SIZE];
137:   sha.finish(buf);
138: 
139:   Digest digest;
140:   memcpy(digest.data(), buf, HASH_SIZE);
141:   return digest;
142: }
143: 
144: template <typename E>
145: static bool is_leaf(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec) {
146:   if (!isec.get_rels(ctx).empty())
147:     return false;
148: 
149:   for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : isec.get_fdes())
150:     if (fde.get_rels(isec.file).size() > 1)
151:       return false;
152: 
153:   return true;
154: }
155: 
156: template <typename E>
157: struct LeafHasher {
158:   size_t operator()(InputSection<E> *isec) const {
159:     u64 h = hash_string(isec->contents);
160:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : isec->get_fdes()) {
161:       u64 h2 = hash_string(fde.get_contents(isec->file).substr(8));
162:       h = combine_hash(h, h2);
163:     }
164:     return h;
165:   }
166: };
167: 
168: template <typename E>
169: struct LeafEq {
170:   bool operator()(InputSection<E> *a, InputSection<E> *b) const {
171:     if (a->contents != b->contents)
172:       return false;
173: 
174:     std::span<FdeRecord<E>> x = a->get_fdes();
175:     std::span<FdeRecord<E>> y = b->get_fdes();
176: 
177:     if (x.size() != y.size())
178:       return false;
179: 
180:     for (i64 i = 0; i < x.size(); i++)
181:       if (x[i].get_contents(a->file).substr(8) !=
182:           y[i].get_contents(b->file).substr(8))
183:         return false;
184:     return true;
185:   }
186: };
187: 
188: // Early merge of leaf nodes, which can be processed without constructing the
189: // entire graph. This reduces the vertex count and improves memory efficiency.
190: template <typename E>
191: static void merge_leaf_nodes(Context<E> &ctx) {
192:   Timer t(ctx, "merge_leaf_nodes");
193: 
194:   static Counter eligible("icf_eligibles");
195:   static Counter non_eligible("icf_non_eligibles");
196:   static Counter leaf("icf_leaf_nodes");
197: 
198:   tbb::concurrent_unordered_map<InputSection<E> *, InputSection<E> *,
199:                                 LeafHasher<E>, LeafEq<E>> map;
200: 
201:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
202:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : ctx.objs[i]->sections) {
203:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive)
204:         continue;
205: 
206:       if (!is_eligible(ctx, *isec)) {
207:         non_eligible++;
208:         continue;
209:       }
210: 
211:       if (is_leaf(ctx, *isec)) {
212:         leaf++;
213:         isec->icf_leaf = true;
214:         auto [it, inserted] = map.insert({isec.get(), isec.get()});
215:         if (!inserted && isec->get_priority() < it->second->get_priority())
216:           it->second = isec.get();
217:       } else {
218:         eligible++;
219:         isec->icf_eligible = true;
220:       }
221:     }
222:   });
223: 
224:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
225:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : ctx.objs[i]->sections) {
226:       if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->icf_leaf) {
227:         auto it = map.find(isec.get());
228:         assert(it != map.end());
229:         isec->leader = it->second;
230:       }
231:     }
232:   });
233: }
234: 
235: template <typename E>
236: static Digest compute_digest(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec) {
237:   SHA256Hash sha;
238: 
239:   auto hash = [&](auto val) {
240:     sha.update((u8 *)&val, sizeof(val));
241:   };
242: 
243:   auto hash_string = [&](std::string_view str) {
244:     hash(str.size());
245:     sha.update((u8 *)str.data(), str.size());
246:   };
247: 
248:   auto hash_symbol = [&](Symbol<E> &sym) {
249:     InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section();
250: 
251:     if (!sym.file) {
252:       hash('1');
253:       hash((u64)&sym);
254:     } else if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag()) {
255:       hash('2');
256:       hash((u64)frag);
257:     } else if (!isec) {
258:       hash('3');
259:     } else if (isec->leader) {
260:       hash('4');
261:       hash((u64)isec->leader);
262:     } else if (isec->icf_eligible) {
263:       hash('5');
264:     } else {
265:       hash('6');
266:       hash((u64)isec);
267:     }
268:     hash(sym.value);
269:   };
270: 
271:   hash_string(isec.contents);
272:   hash(isec.shdr().sh_flags);
273:   hash(isec.get_fdes().size());
274:   hash(isec.get_rels(ctx).size());
275: 
276:   for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : isec.get_fdes()) {
277:     hash(isec.file.cies[fde.cie_idx].icf_idx);
278: 
279:     // Bytes 0 to 4 contain the length of this record, and
280:     // bytes 4 to 8 contain an offset to CIE.
281:     hash_string(fde.get_contents(isec.file).substr(8));
282: 
283:     hash(fde.get_rels(isec.file).size());
284: 
285:     for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : fde.get_rels(isec.file).subspan(1)) {
286:       hash_symbol(*isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym]);
287:       hash(rel.r_type);
288:       hash(rel.r_offset - fde.input_offset);
289:       hash(get_addend(isec.file.cies[fde.cie_idx].input_section, rel));
290:     }
291:   }
292: 
293:   for (i64 i = 0; i < isec.get_rels(ctx).size(); i++) {
294:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = isec.get_rels(ctx)[i];
295:     hash(rel.r_offset);
296:     hash(rel.r_type);
297:     hash(get_addend(isec, rel));
298:     hash_symbol(*isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym]);
299:   }
300: 
301:   return digest_final(sha);
302: }
303: 
304: template <typename E>
305: static std::vector<InputSection<E> *> gather_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
306:   Timer t(ctx, "gather_sections");
307: 
308:   // Count the number of input sections for each input file.
309:   std::vector<i64> num_sections(ctx.objs.size());
310: 
311:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
312:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : ctx.objs[i]->sections)
313:       if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->icf_eligible)
314:         num_sections[i]++;
315:   });
316: 
317:   std::vector<i64> section_indices(ctx.objs.size());
318:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size() - 1; i++)
319:     section_indices[i + 1] = section_indices[i] + num_sections[i];
320: 
321:   std::vector<InputSection<E> *> sections(
322:     section_indices.back() + num_sections.back());
323: 
324:   // Fill `sections` contents.
325:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
326:     i64 idx = section_indices[i];
327:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : ctx.objs[i]->sections)
328:       if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->icf_eligible)
329:         sections[idx++] = isec.get();
330:   });
331: 
332:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)sections.size(), [&](i64 i) {
333:     sections[i]->icf_idx = i;
334:   });
335: 
336:   return sections;
337: }
338: 
339: template <typename E>
340: static std::vector<Digest>
341: compute_digests(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<InputSection<E> *> sections) {
342:   Timer t(ctx, "compute_digests");
343: 
344:   std::vector<Digest> digests(sections.size());
345:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)sections.size(), [&](i64 i) {
346:     digests[i] = compute_digest(ctx, *sections[i]);
347:   });
348:   return digests;
349: }
350: 
351: // Build a graph, treating every function as a vertex and every function call
352: // as an edge. See the description at the top for a more detailed formulation.
353: // We use u32 indices here to improve cache locality.
354: template <typename E>
355: static void gather_edges(Context<E> &ctx,
356:                          std::span<InputSection<E> *> sections,
