357:                          std::vector<u32> &edges,
358:                          std::vector<u32> &edge_indices) {
359:   Timer t(ctx, "gather_edges");
360: 
361:   if (sections.empty())
362:     return;
363: 
364:   std::vector<i64> num_edges(sections.size());
365:   edge_indices.resize(sections.size());
366: 
367:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)sections.size(), [&](i64 i) {
368:     InputSection<E> &isec = *sections[i];
369:     assert(isec.icf_eligible);
370: 
371:     for (i64 j = 0; j < isec.get_rels(ctx).size(); j++) {
372:       const ElfRel<E> &rel = isec.get_rels(ctx)[j];
373:       Symbol<E> &sym = *isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
374:       if (!sym.get_frag())
375:         if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
376:           if (isec->icf_eligible)
377:             num_edges[i]++;
378:     }
379:   });
380: 
381:   for (i64 i = 0; i < num_edges.size() - 1; i++)
382:     edge_indices[i + 1] = edge_indices[i] + num_edges[i];
383: 
384:   edges.resize(edge_indices.back() + num_edges.back());
385: 
386:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)num_edges.size(), [&](i64 i) {
387:     InputSection<E> &isec = *sections[i];
388:     i64 idx = edge_indices[i];
389: 
390:     for (ElfRel<E> &rel : isec.get_rels(ctx)) {
391:       Symbol<E> &sym = *isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
392:       if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
393:         if (isec->icf_eligible)
394:           edges[idx++] = isec->icf_idx;
395:   }
396:   });
397: }
398: 
399: template <typename E>
400: static i64 propagate(std::span<std::vector<Digest>> digests,
401:                      std::span<u32> edges, std::span<u32> edge_indices,
402:                      bool &slot, BitVector &converged,
403:                      tbb::affinity_partitioner &ap) {
404:   static Counter round("icf_round");
405:   round++;
406: 
407:   i64 num_digests = digests[0].size();
408:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> changed;
409: 
410:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, num_digests, [&](i64 i) {
411:     if (converged.get(i))
412:       return;
413: 
414:     SHA256Hash sha;
415:     sha.update(digests[2][i].data(), HASH_SIZE);
416: 
417:     i64 begin = edge_indices[i];
418:     i64 end = (i + 1 == num_digests) ? edges.size() : edge_indices[i + 1];
419: 
420:     for (i64 j : edges.subspan(begin, end - begin))
421:       sha.update(digests[slot][j].data(), HASH_SIZE);
422: 
423:     digests[!slot][i] = digest_final(sha);
424: 
425:     if (digests[slot][i] == digests[!slot][i]) {
426:       // This node has converged. Skip further iterations as it will
427:       // yield the same hash.
428:       converged.set(i);
429:     } else {
430:       changed.local()++;
431:     }
432:   }, ap);
433: 
434:   slot = !slot;
435:   return changed.combine(std::plus());
436: }
437: 
438: template <typename E>
439: static i64 count_num_classes(std::span<Digest> digests,
440:                              tbb::affinity_partitioner &ap) {
441:   std::vector<Digest> vec(digests.begin(), digests.end());
442:   tbb::parallel_sort(vec);
443: 
444:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> num_classes;
445:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)vec.size() - 1, [&](i64 i) {
446:     if (vec[i] != vec[i + 1])
447:       num_classes.local()++;
448:   }, ap);
449:   return num_classes.combine(std::plus());
450: }
451: 
452: template <typename E>
453: static void print_icf_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
454:   tbb::concurrent_vector<InputSection<E> *> leaders;
455:   tbb::concurrent_unordered_multimap<InputSection<E> *, InputSection<E> *> map;
456: 
457:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
458:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
459:       if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->leader) {
460:         if (isec.get() == isec->leader)
461:           leaders.push_back(isec.get());
462:         else
463:           map.insert({isec->leader, isec.get()});
464:       }
465:     }
466:   });
467: 
468:   tbb::parallel_sort(leaders.begin(), leaders.end(),
469:                      [&](InputSection<E> *a, InputSection<E> *b) {
470:                        return a->get_priority() < b->get_priority();
471:                      });
472: 
473:   i64 saved_bytes = 0;
474: 
475:   for (InputSection<E> *leader : leaders) {
476:     auto [begin, end] = map.equal_range(leader);
477:     if (begin == end)
478:       continue;
479: 
480:     SyncOut(ctx) << "selected section " << *leader;
481: 
482:     i64 n = 0;
483:     for (auto it = begin; it != end; it++) {
484:       SyncOut(ctx) << "  removing identical section " << *it->second;
485:       n++;
486:     }
487:     saved_bytes += leader->contents.size() * n;
488:   }
489: 
490:   SyncOut(ctx) << "ICF saved " << saved_bytes << " bytes";
491: }
492: 
493: template <typename E>
494: void icf_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
495:   Timer t(ctx, "icf");
496:   if (ctx.objs.empty())
497:     return;
498: 
499:   uniquify_cies(ctx);
500:   merge_leaf_nodes(ctx);
501: 
502:   // Prepare for the propagation rounds.
503:   std::vector<InputSection<E> *> sections = gather_sections(ctx);
504: 
505:   // We allocate 3 arrays to store hashes for each vertex.
506:   //
507:   // Index 0 and 1 are used for tree hashes from the previous
508:   // iteration and the current iteration. They switch roles every
509:   // iteration. See `slot` below.
510:   //
511:   // Index 2 stores the initial, single-vertex hash. This is combined
512:   // with hashes from the connected vertices to form the tree hash
513:   // described above.
514:   std::vector<std::vector<Digest>> digests(3);
515:   digests[0] = compute_digests<E>(ctx, sections);
516:   digests[1].resize(digests[0].size());
517:   digests[2] = digests[0];
518: 
519:   std::vector<u32> edges;
520:   std::vector<u32> edge_indices;
521:   gather_edges<E>(ctx, sections, edges, edge_indices);
522: 
523:   BitVector converged(digests[0].size());
524:   bool slot = 0;
525: 
526:   // Execute the propagation rounds until convergence is obtained.
527:   {
528:     Timer t(ctx, "propagate");
529:     tbb::affinity_partitioner ap;
530: 
531:     // A cheap test that the graph hasn't converged yet.
532:     // The loop after this one uses a strict condition, but it's expensive
533:     // as it requires sorting the entire hash collection.
534:     //
535:     // For nodes that have a cycle in downstream (i.e. recursive
536:     // functions and functions that calls recursive functions) will always
537:     // change with the iterations. Nodes that doesn't (i.e. non-recursive
538:     // functions) will stop changing as soon as the propagation depth reaches
539:     // the call tree depth.
540:     // Here, we test whether we have reached sufficient depth for the latter,
541:     // which is a necessary (but not sufficient) condition for convergence.
542:     i64 num_changed = -1;
543:     for (;;) {
544:       i64 n = propagate<E>(digests, edges, edge_indices, slot, converged, ap);
545:       if (n == num_changed)
546:         break;
547:       num_changed = n;
548:     }
549: 
550:     // Run the pass until the unique number of hashes stop increasing, at which
551:     // point we have achieved convergence (proof omitted for brevity).
552:     i64 num_classes = -1;
553:     for (;;) {
554:       // count_num_classes requires sorting which is O(n log n), so do a little
555:       // more work beforehand to amortize that log factor.
556:       for (i64 i = 0; i < 10; i++)
557:         propagate<E>(digests, edges, edge_indices, slot, converged, ap);
558: 
559:       i64 n = count_num_classes<E>(digests[slot], ap);
560:       if (n == num_classes)
561:         break;
562:       num_classes = n;
563:     }
564:   }
565: 
566:   // Group sections by SHA digest.
567:   {
568:     Timer t(ctx, "group");
569: 
570:     auto *map = new tbb::concurrent_unordered_map<Digest, InputSection<E> *>;
571:     std::span<Digest> digest = digests[slot];
572: 
573:     tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)sections.size(), [&](i64 i) {
574:       InputSection<E> *isec = sections[i];
575:       auto [it, inserted] = map->insert({digest[i], isec});
576:       if (!inserted && isec->get_priority() < it->second->get_priority())
577:         it->second = isec;
578:     });
579: 
580:     tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)sections.size(), [&](i64 i) {
581:       auto it = map->find(digest[i]);
582:       assert(it != map->end());
583:       sections[i]->leader = it->second;
584:     });
585: 
586:     // Since free'ing the map is slow, postpone it.
587:     ctx.on_exit.push_back([=] { delete map; });
588:   }
589: 
590:   if (ctx.arg.print_icf_sections)
591:     print_icf_sections(ctx);
592: 
593:   // Eliminate duplicate sections.
594:   // Symbols pointing to eliminated sections will be redirected on the fly when
595:   // exporting to the symtab.
596:   {
597:     Timer t(ctx, "sweep");
598:     static Counter eliminated("icf_eliminated");
599:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
600:       for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
601:         if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->is_killed_by_icf()) {
602:           isec->kill();
603:           eliminated++;
604:         }
605:       }
606:     });
607:   }
608: }
609: 
610: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
611: 
612: template void icf_sections(Context<E> &ctx);
613: 
614: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/input-sections.cc
================
  1: #include "mold.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <limits>
  4: #include <zlib.h>
  5: #include <zstd.h>
  6: 
  7: namespace mold::elf {
  8: 
  9: typedef enum {
 10:   NONE, ERROR, COPYREL, DYN_COPYREL, PLT, CPLT, DYN_CPLT, DYNREL, BASEREL, IFUNC,
 11: } Action;
 12: 
 13: template <typename E>
 14: bool CieRecord<E>::equals(const CieRecord<E> &other) const {
 15:   if (get_contents() != other.get_contents())
 16:     return false;
 17: 
 18:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> x = get_rels();
 19:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> y = other.get_rels();
 20:   if (x.size() != y.size())
 21:     return false;
 22: 
 23:   for (i64 i = 0; i < x.size(); i++) {
 24:     if (x[i].r_offset - input_offset != y[i].r_offset - other.input_offset ||
 25:         x[i].r_type != y[i].r_type ||
 26:         file.symbols[x[i].r_sym] != other.file.symbols[y[i].r_sym] ||
 27:         get_addend(input_section, x[i]) != get_addend(other.input_section, y[i]))
 28:       return false;
 29:   }
 30:   return true;
 31: }
 32: 
 33: static i64 to_p2align(u64 alignment) {
 34:   if (alignment == 0)
 35:     return 0;
 36:   return std::countr_zero(alignment);
 37: }
 38: 
 39: template <typename E>
 40: InputSection<E>::InputSection(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file,
 41:                               std::string_view name, i64 shndx)
 42:   : file(file), shndx(shndx) {
 43:   if (shndx < file.elf_sections.size())
 44:     contents = {(char *)file.mf->data + shdr().sh_offset, (size_t)shdr().sh_size};
 45: 
 46:   if (shdr().sh_flags & SHF_COMPRESSED) {
 47:     ElfChdr<E> &chdr = *(ElfChdr<E> *)&contents[0];
 48:     sh_size = chdr.ch_size;
 49:     p2align = to_p2align(chdr.ch_addralign);
 50:   } else {
 51:     sh_size = shdr().sh_size;
 52:     p2align = to_p2align(shdr().sh_addralign);
 53:   }
 54: 
 55:   // Sections may have been compressed. We usually uncompress them
 56:   // directly into the mmap'ed output file, but we want to uncompress
 57:   // early for REL-type ELF types to read relocation addends from
 58:   // section contents. For RELA-type, we don't need to do this because
 59:   // addends are in relocations.
 60:   //
 61:   // SH-4 stores addends to sections despite being RELA, which is a
 62:   // special (and buggy) case.
 63:   if constexpr (!E::is_rela || is_sh4<E>)
 64:     uncompress(ctx);
 65: }
 66: 
 67: template <typename E>
 68: void InputSection<E>::uncompress(Context<E> &ctx) {
 69:   if (!(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_COMPRESSED) || uncompressed)
 70:     return;
 71: 
 72:   u8 *buf = new u8[sh_size];
 73:   uncompress_to(ctx, buf);
 74:   contents = std::string_view((char *)buf, sh_size);
 75:   ctx.string_pool.emplace_back(buf);
 76:   uncompressed = true;
 77: }
 78: 
 79: template <typename E>
 80: void InputSection<E>::uncompress_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 81:   if (!(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_COMPRESSED) || uncompressed) {
 82:     memcpy(buf, contents.data(), contents.size());
 83:     return;
 84:   }
 85: 
 86:   if (contents.size() < sizeof(ElfChdr<E>))
 87:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": corrupted compressed section";
 88: 
 89:   ElfChdr<E> &hdr = *(ElfChdr<E> *)&contents[0];
 90:   std::string_view data = contents.substr(sizeof(ElfChdr<E>));
 91: 
 92:   switch (hdr.ch_type) {
 93:   case ELFCOMPRESS_ZLIB: {
 94:     unsigned long size = sh_size;
 95:     if (::uncompress(buf, &size, (u8 *)data.data(), data.size()) != Z_OK)
 96:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": uncompress failed";
 97:     assert(size == sh_size);
 98:     break;
 99:   }
100:   case ELFCOMPRESS_ZSTD:
101:     if (ZSTD_decompress(buf, sh_size, (u8 *)data.data(), data.size()) != sh_size)
102:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": ZSTD_decompress failed";
103:     break;
104:   default:
105:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": unsupported compression type: 0x"
106:                << std::hex << hdr.ch_type;
107:   }
108: }
109: 
110: template <typename E>
111: static Action get_rel_action(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
112:                              const Action table[3][4]) {
113:   auto get_output_type = [&] {
114:     if (ctx.arg.shared)
115:       return 0;
116:     if (ctx.arg.pie)
117:       return 1;
118:     return 2;
119:   };
120: 
121:   auto get_sym_type = [&] {
122:     if (sym.is_absolute())
123:       return 0;
124:     if (!sym.is_imported)
125:       return 1;
126:     if (sym.get_type() != STT_FUNC)
127:       return 2;
128:     return 3;
129:   };
130: 
131:   return table[get_output_type()][get_sym_type()];
132: }
133: 
134: template <typename E>
135: static void scan_rel(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec, Symbol<E> &sym,
136:                      const ElfRel<E> &rel, Action action) {
137:   bool writable = (isec.shdr().sh_flags & SHF_WRITE);
138: 
139:   auto error = [&] {
140:     std::string msg = sym.is_absolute() ? "-fno-PIC" : "-fPIC";
141:     Error(ctx) << isec << ": " << rel << " relocation at offset 0x"
142:                << std::hex << rel.r_offset << " against symbol `"
143:                << sym << "' can not be used; recompile with " << msg;
144:   };
145: 
146:   auto check_textrel = [&] {
147:     if (!writable) {
148:       if (ctx.arg.z_text) {
149:         error();
150:       } else if (ctx.arg.warn_textrel) {
151:         Warn(ctx) << isec << ": relocation against symbol `" << sym
152:                   << "' in read-only section";
153:       }
154:       ctx.has_textrel = true;
155:     }
156:   };
157: 
158:   auto copyrel = [&] {
159:     assert(sym.is_imported);
160:     if (sym.esym().st_visibility == STV_PROTECTED) {
161:       Error(ctx) << isec
162:                  << ": cannot make copy relocation for protected symbol '" << sym
163:                  << "', defined in " << *sym.file << "; recompile with -fPIC";
164:     }
165:     sym.flags |= NEEDS_COPYREL;
166:   };
167: 
168:   auto dynrel = [&] {
169:     check_textrel();
170:     isec.file.num_dynrel++;
171:   };
172: 
173:   switch (action) {
174:   case NONE:
175:     break;
176:   case ERROR:
177:     error();
178:     break;
179:   case COPYREL:
180:     if (!ctx.arg.z_copyreloc)
181:       error();
182:     copyrel();
183:     break;
184:   case DYN_COPYREL:
185:     if (writable || !ctx.arg.z_copyreloc)
186:       dynrel();
187:     else
188:       copyrel();
189:     break;
190:   case PLT:
191:     sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
192:     break;
193:   case CPLT:
194:     sym.flags |= NEEDS_CPLT;
195:     break;
196:   case DYN_CPLT:
197:     if (writable)
198:       dynrel();
199:     else
200:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_CPLT;
201:     break;
202:   case DYNREL:
203:     dynrel();
204:     break;
205:   case BASEREL:
206:     check_textrel();
207:     if (!isec.is_relr_reloc(ctx, rel))
208:       isec.file.num_dynrel++;
209:     break;
210:   case IFUNC:
211:     dynrel();
212:     ctx.num_ifunc_dynrels++;
213:     break;
214:   default:
215:     unreachable();
216:   }
217: }
218: 
219: template <typename E>
220: static Action get_pcrel_action(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
221:   // This is for PC-relative relocations (e.g. R_X86_64_PC32).
222:   // We cannot promote them to dynamic relocations because the dynamic
223:   // linker generally does not support PC-relative relocations.
224:   constexpr static Action table[3][4] = {
225:     // Absolute  Local    Imported data  Imported code
226:     {  ERROR,    NONE,    ERROR,         PLT    },  // Shared object
227:     {  ERROR,    NONE,    COPYREL,       PLT    },  // Position-independent exec
228:     {  NONE,     NONE,    COPYREL,       CPLT   },  // Position-dependent exec
229:   };
230: 
231:   return get_rel_action(ctx, sym, table);
232: }
233: 
234: template <typename E>
235: static Action get_absrel_action(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
236:   // This is a decision table for absolute relocations that is smaller
237:   // than the word size (e.g. R_X86_64_32). Since the dynamic linker
238:   // generally does not support dynamic relocations smaller than the
239:   // word size, we need to report an error if a relocation cannot be
240:   // resolved at link-time.
241:   constexpr static Action table[3][4] = {
242:     // Absolute  Local    Imported data  Imported code
243:     {  NONE,     ERROR,   ERROR,         ERROR },  // Shared object
244:     {  NONE,     ERROR,   ERROR,         ERROR },  // Position-independent exec
245:     {  NONE,     NONE,    COPYREL,       CPLT  },  // Position-dependent exec
246:   };
247: 
248:   return get_rel_action(ctx, sym, table);
249: }
250: 
251: template <typename E>
252: static Action get_dyn_absrel_action(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
253:   if (sym.is_ifunc())
254:     return IFUNC;
255: 
256:   // This is a decision table for absolute relocations for the word
257:   // size data (e.g. R_X86_64_64). Unlike the absrel_table, we can emit
258:   // a dynamic relocation if we cannot resolve an address at link-time.
259:   constexpr static Action table[3][4] = {
260:     // Absolute  Local    Imported data  Imported code
261:     {  NONE,     BASEREL, DYNREL,        DYNREL   },  // Shared object
262:     {  NONE,     BASEREL, DYNREL,        DYNREL   },  // Position-independent exec
263:     {  NONE,     NONE,    DYN_COPYREL,   DYN_CPLT },  // Position-dependent exec
264:   };
265: 
266:   return get_rel_action(ctx, sym, table);
267: }
268: 
269: template <typename E>
270: static Action get_ppc64_toc_action(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
271:   if (sym.is_ifunc())
272:     return IFUNC;
273: 
274:   // As a special case, we do not create copy relocations nor canonical
275:   // PLTs for .toc sections. PPC64's .toc is a compiler-generated
276:   // GOT-like section, and no user-generated code directly uses values
277:   // in it.
278:   constexpr static Action table[3][4] = {
279:     // Absolute  Local    Imported data  Imported code
280:     {  NONE,     BASEREL, DYNREL,        DYNREL },  // Shared object
281:     {  NONE,     BASEREL, DYNREL,        DYNREL },  // Position-independent exec
282:     {  NONE,     NONE,    DYNREL,        DYNREL },  // Position-dependent exec
283:   };
284: 
285:   return get_rel_action(ctx, sym, table);
286: }
287: 
288: template <typename E>
289: void InputSection<E>::scan_pcrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
290:                                  const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
291:   scan_rel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, get_pcrel_action(ctx, sym));
292: }
293: 
294: template <typename E>
295: void InputSection<E>::scan_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
296:                                   const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
297:   scan_rel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, get_absrel_action(ctx, sym));
298: }
299: 
300: template <typename E>
301: void InputSection<E>::scan_dyn_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
302:                                       const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
303:   scan_rel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, get_dyn_absrel_action(ctx, sym));
304: }
305: 
306: template <typename E>
307: void InputSection<E>::scan_toc_rel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
308:                                    const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
309:   scan_rel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, get_ppc64_toc_action(ctx, sym));
310: }
311: 
312: template <typename E>
313: void InputSection<E>::check_tlsle(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
314:                                   const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
315:   if (ctx.arg.shared)
316:     Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against `" << sym
317:                << "` can not be used when making a shared object;"
318:                << " recompile with -fPIC";
319: }
320: 
321: template <typename E>
322: static void apply_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec,
323:                          Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel, u8 *loc,
324:                          u64 S, i64 A, u64 P, ElfRel<E> *&dynrel,
325:                          Action action) {
326:   bool writable = (isec.shdr().sh_flags & SHF_WRITE);
327: 
328:   auto apply_dynrel = [&] {
329:     *dynrel++ = ElfRel<E>(P, E::R_ABS, sym.get_dynsym_idx(ctx), A);
330:     if (ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs)
331:       *(Word<E> *)loc = A;
332:   };
333: 
334:   switch (action) {
335:   case COPYREL:
336:   case CPLT:
337:   case NONE:
338:     *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
339:     break;
340:   case BASEREL:
341:     if (isec.is_relr_reloc(ctx, rel)) {
342:       *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
343:     } else {
344:       *dynrel++ = ElfRel<E>(P, E::R_RELATIVE, 0, S + A);
345:       if (ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs)
346:         *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
347:     }
348:     break;
349:   case DYN_COPYREL:
350:     if (writable || !ctx.arg.z_copyreloc)
351:       apply_dynrel();
352:     else
353:       *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
354:     break;
355:   case DYN_CPLT:
356:     if (writable)
357:       apply_dynrel();
358:     else
359:       *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
360:     break;
361:   case DYNREL:
362:     apply_dynrel();
363:     break;
364:   case IFUNC: {
365:     if constexpr (supports_ifunc<E>) {
366:       u64 addr = sym.get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT) + A;
367:       *dynrel++ = ElfRel<E>(P, E::R_IRELATIVE, 0, addr);
368:       if (ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs)
369:         *(Word<E> *)loc = addr;
370:     } else {
371:       unreachable();
372:     }
373:     break;
374:   }
375:   default:
376:     unreachable();
377:   }
378: }
379: 
380: template <typename E>
381: void InputSection<E>::apply_dyn_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
382:                                        const ElfRel<E> &rel, u8 *loc,
383:                                        u64 S, i64 A, u64 P,
384:                                        ElfRel<E> *&dynrel) {
385:   apply_absrel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel,
386:                get_dyn_absrel_action(ctx, sym));
387: }
388: 
389: template <typename E>
390: void InputSection<E>::apply_toc_rel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
391:                                     const ElfRel<E> &rel, u8 *loc,
392:                                     u64 S, i64 A, u64 P,
393:                                     ElfRel<E> *&dynrel) {
394:   apply_absrel(ctx, *this, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel,
395:                get_ppc64_toc_action(ctx, sym));
396: }
397: 
398: template <typename E>
399: void InputSection<E>::write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
400:   if (shdr().sh_type == SHT_NOBITS || sh_size == 0)
401:     return;
402: 
403:   // Copy data
404:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>) {
405:     copy_contents_riscv(ctx, buf);
406:   } else {
407:     uncompress_to(ctx, buf);
408:   }
409: 
410:   // Apply relocations
411:   if (!ctx.arg.relocatable) {
412:     if (shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)
413:       apply_reloc_alloc(ctx, buf);
414:     else
415:       apply_reloc_nonalloc(ctx, buf);
416:   }
417: }
418: 
419: // Get the name of a function containin a given offset.
420: template <typename E>
421: std::string_view InputSection<E>::get_func_name(Context<E> &ctx, i64 offset) const {
422:   for (const ElfSym<E> &esym : file.elf_syms) {
423:     if (esym.st_shndx == shndx && esym.st_type == STT_FUNC &&
424:         esym.st_value <= offset && offset < esym.st_value + esym.st_size) {
425:       std::string_view name = file.symbol_strtab.data() + esym.st_name;
426:       if (ctx.arg.demangle)
427:         return demangle(name);
428:       return name;
429:     }
430:   }
431:   return "";
432: }
433: 
434: // Test if the symbol a given relocation refers to has already been resolved.
435: // If not, record that error and returns true.
436: template <typename E>
437: bool InputSection<E>::record_undef_error(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
438:   // If a relocation refers to a linker-synthesized symbol for a
439:   // section fragment, it's always been resolved.
440:   if (file.elf_syms.size() <= rel.r_sym)
441:     return false;
442: 
443:   Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
444:   const ElfSym<E> &esym = file.elf_syms[rel.r_sym];
445:   assert(sym.file);
446: 
447:   auto record = [&] {
448:     std::stringstream ss;
449:     if (std::string_view source = file.get_source_name(); !source.empty())
450:       ss << ">>> referenced by " << source << "\n";
451:     else
452:       ss << ">>> referenced by " << *this << "\n";
453: 
454:     ss << ">>>               " << file;
455:     if (std::string_view func = get_func_name(ctx, rel.r_offset); !func.empty())
456:       ss << ":(" << func << ")";
457: 
458:     typename decltype(ctx.undef_errors)::accessor acc;
459:     ctx.undef_errors.insert(acc, {sym.name(), {}});
460:     acc->second.push_back(ss.str());
461:   };
462: 
463:   // A non-weak undefined symbol must be promoted to an imported
464:   // symbol or resolved to an defined symbol. Otherwise, it's an
465:   // undefined symbol error.
466:   //
467:   // Every ELF file has an absolute local symbol as its first symbol.
468:   // Referring to that symbol is always valid.
469:   bool is_undef = esym.is_undef() && !esym.is_weak() && sym.sym_idx;
470:   if (!sym.is_imported && is_undef && sym.esym().is_undef()) {
471:     record();
472:     return true;
473:   }
474: 
475:   // If a protected/hidden undefined symbol is resolved to other .so,
476:   // it's handled as if no symbols were found.
477:   if (sym.file->is_dso &&
478:       (sym.visibility == STV_PROTECTED || sym.visibility == STV_HIDDEN)) {
479:     record();
480:     return true;
481:   }
482: 
483:   return false;
484: }
485: 
486: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
487: 
488: template struct CieRecord<E>;
489: template class InputSection<E>;
490: 
491: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/linker-script.cc
================
  1: // On Linux, /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so is not actually
  2: // a shared object file but an ASCII text file containing a linker
  3: // script to include a "real" libc.so file. Therefore, we need to
  4: // support a (very limited) subset of the linker script language.
  5: 
  6: #include "mold.h"
  7: 
  8: #include <cctype>
  9: #include <iomanip>
 10: 
 11: namespace mold::elf {
 12: 
 13: template <typename E>
 14: static thread_local MappedFile<Context<E>> *current_file;
 15: 
 16: template <typename E>
 17: void read_version_script(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> &tok);
 18: 
 19: static std::string_view get_line(std::string_view input, const char *pos) {
 20:   assert(input.data() <= pos);
 21:   assert(pos < input.data() + input.size());
 22: 
 23:   i64 start = input.rfind('\n', pos - input.data());
 24:   if (start == input.npos)
 25:     start = 0;
 26:   else
 27:     start++;
 28: 
 29:   i64 end = input.find('\n', pos - input.data());
 30:   if (end == input.npos)
 31:     end = input.size();
 32: 
 33:   return input.substr(start, end - start);
 34: }
 35: 
 36: template <typename E>
 37: class SyntaxError {
 38: public:
 39:   SyntaxError(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view errpos) : out(ctx) {
 40:     std::string_view contents = current_file<E>->get_contents();
 41:     std::string_view line = get_line(contents, errpos.data());
 42: 
 43:     i64 lineno = 1;
 44:     for (i64 i = 0; contents.data() + i < line.data(); i++)
 45:       if (contents[i] == '\n')
 46:         lineno++;
 47: 
 48:     i64 column = errpos.data() - line.data();
 49: 
 50:     std::stringstream ss;
 51:     ss << current_file<E>->name << ":" << lineno << ": ";
 52:     i64 indent = (i64)ss.tellp() + strlen("mold: ");
 53:     ss << line << "\n" << std::setw(indent + column) << " " << "^ ";
 54:     out << ss.str();
 55:   }
 56: 
 57:   template <class T> SyntaxError &operator<<(T &&val) {
 58:     out << std::forward<T>(val);
 59:     return *this;
 60:   }
 61: 
 62:   [[noreturn]] ~SyntaxError() = default;
 63: 
 64:   Fatal<Context<E>> out;
 65: };
 66: 
 67: template <typename E>
 68: static std::vector<std::string_view>
 69: tokenize(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view input) {
 70:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
 71:   while (!input.empty()) {
 72:     if (isspace(input[0])) {
 73:       input = input.substr(1);
 74:       continue;
 75:     }
 76: 
 77:     if (input.starts_with("/*")) {
 78:       i64 pos = input.find("*/", 2);
 79:       if (pos == std::string_view::npos)
 80:         SyntaxError(ctx, input) << "unclosed comment";
 81:       input = input.substr(pos + 2);
 82:       continue;
 83:     }
 84: 
 85:     if (input[0] == '#') {
 86:       i64 pos = input.find("\n", 1);
 87:       if (pos == std::string_view::npos)
 88:         break;
 89:       input = input.substr(pos + 1);
 90:       continue;
 91:     }
 92: 
 93:     if (input[0] == '"') {
 94:       i64 pos = input.find('"', 1);
 95:       if (pos == std::string_view::npos)
 96:         SyntaxError(ctx, input) << "unclosed string literal";
 97:       vec.push_back(input.substr(0, pos + 1));
 98:       input = input.substr(pos + 1);
 99:       continue;
100:     }
101: 
102:     i64 pos = input.find_first_not_of(
103:       "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz"
104:       "0123456789_.$/\\~=+[]*?-!^:");
105: 
106:     if (pos == 0)
107:       pos = 1;
108:     else if (pos == input.npos)
109:       pos = input.size();
110: 
111:     vec.push_back(input.substr(0, pos));
112:     input = input.substr(pos);
113:   }
114:   return vec;
115: }
116: 
117: template <typename E>
118: static std::span<std::string_view>
119: skip(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> tok, std::string_view str) {
120:   if (tok.empty())
121:     Fatal(ctx) << current_file<E>->name << ": expected '" << str
122:                << "', but got EOF";
123:   if (tok[0] != str)
124:     SyntaxError(ctx, tok[0]) << "expected '" << str << "'";
125:   return tok.subspan(1);
126: }
127: 
128: static std::string_view unquote(std::string_view s) {
129:   if (s.size() > 0 && s[0] == '"') {
130:     assert(s[s.size() - 1] == '"');
131:     return s.substr(1, s.size() - 2);
132:   }
133:   return s;
134: }
135: 
136: template <typename E>
137: static std::span<std::string_view>
138: read_output_format(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> tok) {
139:   tok = skip(ctx, tok, "(");
140:   while (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != ")")
141:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
142:   if (tok.empty())
143:     Fatal(ctx) << current_file<E>->name << ": expected ')', but got EOF";
144:   return tok.subspan(1);
145: }
146: 
147: template <typename E>
148: static bool is_in_sysroot(Context<E> &ctx, std::string path) {
149:   std::string rel = to_abs_path(path)
150:                         .lexically_relative(to_abs_path(ctx.arg.sysroot))
151:                         .string();
152:   return rel != "." && !rel.starts_with("../");
153: }
154: 
155: template <typename E>
156: static MappedFile<Context<E>> *resolve_path(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view tok) {
157:   std::string str(unquote(tok));
158: 
159:   // GNU ld prepends the sysroot if a pathname starts with '/' and the
160:   // script being processed is in the sysroot. We do the same.
161:   if (str.starts_with('/') && is_in_sysroot(ctx, current_file<E>->name))
162:     return MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, ctx.arg.sysroot + str);
163: 
164:   if (str.starts_with('=')) {
165:     std::string path;
166:     if (ctx.arg.sysroot.empty())
167:       path = str.substr(1);
168:     else
169:       path = ctx.arg.sysroot + str.substr(1);
170:     return MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, path);
171:   }
172: 
173:   if (str.starts_with("-l"))
174:     return find_library(ctx, str.substr(2));
175: 
176:   if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = open_library(ctx, str))
177:     return mf;
178: 
179:   for (std::string_view dir : ctx.arg.library_paths) {
180:     std::string path = std::string(dir) + "/" + str;
181:     if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = open_library(ctx, path))
182:       return mf;
183:   }
184: 
185:   SyntaxError(ctx, tok) << "library not found: " << str;
186: }
187: 
188: template <typename E>
189: static std::span<std::string_view>
190: read_group(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> tok) {
191:   tok = skip(ctx, tok, "(");
192: 
193:   while (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != ")") {
194:     if (tok[0] == "AS_NEEDED") {
195:       bool orig = ctx.as_needed;
196:       ctx.as_needed = true;
197:       tok = read_group(ctx, tok.subspan(1));
198:       ctx.as_needed = orig;
199:       continue;
200:     }
201: 
202:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = resolve_path(ctx, tok[0]);
203:     read_file(ctx, mf);
204:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
205:   }
206: 
207:   if (tok.empty())
208:     Fatal(ctx) << current_file<E>->name << ": expected ')', but got EOF";
209:   return tok.subspan(1);
210: }
211: 
212: template <typename E>
213: void parse_linker_script(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
214:   current_file<E> = mf;
215: 
216:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec = tokenize(ctx, mf->get_contents());
217:   std::span<std::string_view> tok = vec;
218: 
219:   while (!tok.empty()) {
220:     if (tok[0] == "OUTPUT_FORMAT") {
221:       tok = read_output_format(ctx, tok.subspan(1));
222:     } else if (tok[0] == "INPUT" || tok[0] == "GROUP") {
223:       tok = read_group(ctx, tok.subspan(1));
224:     } else if (tok[0] == "VERSION") {
225:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
226:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
227:       read_version_script(ctx, tok);
228:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, "}");
229:     } else if (tok.size() > 3 && tok[1] == "=" && tok[3] == ";") {
230:       ctx.arg.defsyms.emplace_back(get_symbol(ctx, unquote(tok[0])),
231:                                    get_symbol(ctx, unquote(tok[2])));
232:       tok = tok.subspan(4);
233:     } else if (tok[0] == ";") {
234:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
235:     } else {
236:       SyntaxError(ctx, tok[0]) << "unknown linker script token";
237:     }
238:   }
239: }
240: 
241: template <typename E>
242: std::string_view
243: get_script_output_type(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
244:   current_file<E> = mf;
245: 
246:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec = tokenize(ctx, mf->get_contents());
247:   std::span<std::string_view> tok = vec;
248: 
249:   if (tok.size() >= 3 && tok[0] == "OUTPUT_FORMAT" && tok[1] == "(") {
250:     if (tok[2] == "elf64-x86-64")
251:       return X86_64::target_name;
252:     if (tok[2] == "elf32-i386")
253:       return I386::target_name;
254:   }
255: 
256:   if (tok.size() >= 3 && (tok[0] == "INPUT" || tok[0] == "GROUP") &&
257:       tok[1] == "(")
258:     if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
259:         MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, std::string(unquote(tok[2]))))
260:       return get_machine_type(ctx, mf);
261: 
262:   return "";
263: }
264: 
265: static bool read_label(std::span<std::string_view> &tok,
266:                        std::string label) {
267:   if (tok.size() >= 1 && tok[0] == label + ":") {
268:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
269:     return true;
270:   }
271: 
272:   if (tok.size() >= 2 && tok[0] == label && tok[1] == ":") {
273:     tok = tok.subspan(2);
274:     return true;
275:   }
276:   return false;
277: }
278: 
279: template <typename E>
280: static void
281: read_version_script_commands(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> &tok,
282:                              std::string_view ver_str, u16 ver_idx, bool is_cpp) {
283:   bool is_global = true;
284: 
285:   while (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != "}") {
286:     if (read_label(tok, "global")) {
287:       is_global = true;
288:       continue;
289:     }
290: 
291:     if (read_label(tok, "local")) {
292:       is_global = false;
293:       continue;
294:     }
295: 
296:     if (tok[0] == "extern") {
297:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
298: 
299:       if (!tok.empty() && tok[0] == "\"C\"") {
300:         tok = tok.subspan(1);
301:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
302:         read_version_script_commands( ctx, tok, ver_str, ver_idx, false);
303:       } else {
304:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "\"C++\"");
305:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
306:         read_version_script_commands(ctx, tok, ver_str, ver_idx, true);
307:       }
308: 
309:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, "}");
310:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, ";");
311:       continue;
312:     }
313: 
314:     if (tok[0] == "*") {
315:       ctx.default_version = (is_global ? ver_idx : (u32)VER_NDX_LOCAL);
316:       ctx.default_version_from_version_script = true;
317:     } else if (is_global) {
318:       ctx.version_patterns.push_back({unquote(tok[0]), current_file<E>->name,
319:                                       ver_str, ver_idx, is_cpp});
320:     } else {
321:       ctx.version_patterns.push_back({unquote(tok[0]), current_file<E>->name,
322:                                       ver_str, VER_NDX_LOCAL, is_cpp});
323:     }
324: 
325:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
326: 
327:     if (!tok.empty() && tok[0] == "}")
328:       return;
329:     tok = skip(ctx, tok, ";");
330:   }
331: }
332: 
333: template <typename E>
334: void read_version_script(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> &tok) {
335:   u16 next_ver = VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED + ctx.arg.version_definitions.size() + 1;
336: 
337:   while (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != "}") {
338:     std::string_view ver_str;
339:     u16 ver_idx;
340: 
341:     if (tok[0] == "{") {
342:       ver_str = "global";
343:       ver_idx = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
344:     } else {
345:       ver_str = tok[0];
346:       ver_idx = next_ver++;
347:       ctx.arg.version_definitions.push_back(std::string(tok[0]));
348:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
349:     }
350: 
351:     tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
352:     read_version_script_commands(ctx, tok, ver_str, ver_idx, false);
353:     tok = skip(ctx, tok, "}");
354:     if (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != ";")
355:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
356:     tok = skip(ctx, tok, ";");
357:   }
358: }
359: 
360: template <typename E>
361: void parse_version_script(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
362:   current_file<E> = mf;
363:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec = tokenize(ctx, mf->get_contents());
364:   std::span<std::string_view> tok = vec;
365:   read_version_script(ctx, tok);
366:   if (!tok.empty())
367:     SyntaxError(ctx, tok[0]) << "trailing garbage token";
368: }
369: 
370: template <typename E>
371: void read_dynamic_list_commands(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string_view> &tok,
372:                                 bool is_cpp) {
373:   while (!tok.empty() && tok[0] != "}") {
374:     if (tok[0] == "extern") {
375:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
376: 
377:       if (!tok.empty() && tok[0] == "\"C\"") {
378:         tok = tok.subspan(1);
379:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
380:         read_dynamic_list_commands(ctx, tok, false);
381:       } else {
382:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "\"C++\"");
383:         tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
384:         read_dynamic_list_commands(ctx, tok, true);
385:       }
386: 
387:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, "}");
388:       tok = skip(ctx, tok, ";");
389:       continue;
390:     }
391: 
392:     if (tok[0] == "*")
393:       ctx.default_version = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
394:     else
395:       ctx.version_patterns.push_back({unquote(tok[0]), current_file<E>->name,
396:                                       "global", VER_NDX_GLOBAL, is_cpp});
397: 
398:     tok = skip(ctx, tok.subspan(1), ";");
399:   }
400: }
401: 
402: template <typename E>
403: void parse_dynamic_list(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
404:   current_file<E> = mf;
405:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec = tokenize(ctx, mf->get_contents());
406:   std::span<std::string_view> tok = vec;
407: 
408:   tok = skip(ctx, tok, "{");
409:   read_dynamic_list_commands(ctx, tok, false);
410:   tok = skip(ctx, tok, "}");
411:   tok = skip(ctx, tok, ";");
412: 
413:   if (!tok.empty())
414:     SyntaxError(ctx, tok[0]) << "trailing garbage token";
415: }
416: 
417: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
418: 
419: template void parse_linker_script(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
420: template std::string_view get_script_output_type(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
421: template void parse_version_script(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
422: template void parse_dynamic_list(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
423: 
424: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/lto-unix.cc
================
  1: // This file handles the linker plugin to support LTO (Link-Time
  2: // Optimization).
  3: //
  4: // LTO is a technique to do whole-program optimization to a program. Since
  5: // a linker sees the whole program as opposed to a single compilation
  6: // unit, it in theory can do some optimizations that cannot be done in the
  7: // usual separate compilation model. For example, LTO should be able to
  8: // inline functions that are defined in other compilation unit.
  9: //
 10: // In GCC and Clang, all you have to do to enable LTO is adding the
 11: // `-flto` flag to the compiler and the linker command lines. If `-flto`
 12: // is given, the compiler generates a file that contains not machine code
 13: // but the compiler's IR (intermediate representation). In GCC, the output
 14: // is an ELF file which wraps GCC's IR. In LLVM, it's not even an ELF file
 15: // but just a raw LLVM IR file.
 16: //
 17: // Here is what we have to do if at least one input file is not a usual
 18: // ELF file but an IR object file:
 19: //
 20: //  1. Read symbols both from usual ELF files and from IR object files and
 21: //     resolve symbols as usual.
 22: //
 23: //  2. Pass all IR objects to the compiler backend. The compiler backend
 24: //     compiles the IRs and returns a few big ELF object files as a
 25: //     result.
 26: //
 27: //  3. Parse the returned ELF files and overwrite IR object symbols with
 28: //     the returned ones, discarding IR object files.
 29: //
 30: //  4. Continue the rest of the linking process as usual.
 31: //
 32: // When gcc or clang inovkes ld, they pass `-plugin linker-plugin.so` to
 33: // the linker. The given .so file provides a way to call the compiler
 34: // backend.
 35: //
 36: // The linker plugin API is documented at
 37: // https://gcc.gnu.org/wiki/whopr/driver, though the document is a bit
 38: // outdated.
 39: //
 40: // Frankly, the linker plugin API is peculiar and is not very easy to use.
 41: // For some reason, the API functions don't return the result of a
 42: // function call as a return value but instead calls other function with
 43: // the result as its argument to "return" the result.
 44: //
 45: // For example, the first thing you need to do after dlopen()'ing a linker
 46: // plugin .so is to call `onload` function with a list of callback
 47: // functions. `onload` calls callbacks to notify about the pointers to
 48: // other functions the linker plugin provides. I don't know why `onload`
 49: // can't just return a list of functions or why the linker plugin can't
 50: // define not only `onload` but other functions, but that's what it is.
 51: //
 52: // Here is the steps to use the linker plugin:
 53: //
 54: //  1. dlopen() the linker plugin .so and call `onload` to obtain pointers
 55: //     to other functions provided by the plugin.
 56: //
 57: //  2. Call `claim_file_hook` with an IR object file to read its symbol
 58: //     table. `claim_file_hook` calls the `add_symbols` callback to
 59: //     "return" a list of symbols.
 60: //
 61: //  3. `claim_file_hook` returns LDPT_OK only when the plugin wants to
 62: //     handle a given file. Since we pass only IR object files to the
 63: //     plugin in mold, it always returns LDPT_OK in our case.
 64: //
 65: //  4. Once we made a decision as to which object file to include into the
 66: //     output file, we call `all_symbols_read_hook` to compile IR objects
 67: //     into a few big ELF files. That function calls the `get_symbols`
 68: //     callback to ask us about the symbol resolution results. (The
 69: //     compiler backend needs to know whether an undefined symbol in an IR
 70: //     object was resolved to a regular object file or a shared object to
 71: //     do whole program optimization, for example.)
 72: //
 73: //  5. `all_symbols_read_hook` "returns" the result by calling the
 74: //     `add_input_file` callback. The callback is called with a path to an
 75: //     LTO'ed ELF file. We parse that ELF file and override symbols
 76: //     defined by IR objects with the ELF file's ones.
 77: //
 78: //  6. Lastly, we call `cleanup_hook` to remove temporary files created by
 79: //     the compiler backend.
 80: 
 81: #include "mold.h"
 82: #include "lto.h"
 83: 
 84: #include <cstdarg>
 85: #include <cstring>
 86: #include <dlfcn.h>
 87: #include <fcntl.h>
 88: #include <sstream>
 89: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 90: #include <unistd.h>
 91: 
 92: #if 0
 93: # define LOG std::cerr
 94: #else
 95: # define LOG std::ostringstream()
 96: #endif

100: // Global variables
101: // We store LTO-related information to global variables,
102: // as the LTO plugin is not thread-safe by design anyway.
103: 
104: template <typename E> static Context<E> *gctx;
105: template <typename E> static std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> lto_objects;
106: 
107: static int phase = 0;
108: static std::vector<PluginSymbol> plugin_symbols;
109: static ClaimFileHandler *claim_file_hook;
110: static AllSymbolsReadHandler *all_symbols_read_hook;
111: static CleanupHandler *cleanup_hook;
112: static bool is_gcc_linker_api_v1 = false;

116: template <typename E>
117: static PluginStatus message(PluginLevel level, const char *fmt, ...) {
118:   LOG << "message\n";
119:   Context<E> &ctx = *gctx<E>;
120: 
121:   char buf[1000];
122:   va_list ap;
123:   va_start(ap, fmt);
124:   vsnprintf(buf, sizeof(buf), fmt, ap);
125:   va_end(ap);
126: 
127:   switch (level) {
128:   case LDPL_INFO:
129:     SyncOut(ctx) << buf;
130:     break;
131:   case LDPL_WARNING:
132:     Warn(ctx) << buf;
133:     break;
134:   case LDPL_ERROR:
135:   case LDPL_FATAL:
136:     Fatal(ctx) << buf;
137:   }
138: 
139:   return LDPS_OK;
140: }

164: static PluginStatus
165: add_symbols(void *handle, int nsyms, const PluginSymbol *psyms) {
166:   LOG << "add_symbols: " << nsyms << "\n";
167:   assert(phase == 1);
168:   plugin_symbols = {psyms, psyms + nsyms};
169:   return LDPS_OK;

172: template <typename E>
173: static PluginStatus add_input_file(const char *path) {
174:   LOG << "add_input_file: " << path << "\n";
175: 
176:   Context<E> &ctx = *gctx<E>;
177:   static i64 file_priority = 100;
178: 
179:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, path);
180: 
181:   ObjectFile<E> *file = ObjectFile<E>::create(ctx, mf, "", false);
182:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(file);
183:   lto_objects<E>.push_back(file);
184: 
185:   file->priority = file_priority++;
186:   file->is_alive = true;
187:   file->parse(ctx);
188:   file->resolve_symbols(ctx);
189:   return LDPS_OK;
190: }

195:   return LDPS_OK;

203:   if (file.mf->fd != -1) {
204:     close(file.mf->fd);
205:     file.mf->fd = -1;
206:   }
207:   return LDPS_OK;
208: }

268: static PluginStatus
269: get_symbols_v1(const void *handle, int nsyms, PluginSymbol *psyms) {

272: 
273: // get_symbols teaches the LTO plugin as to how we have resolved symbols.
274: // The plugin uses the symbol resolution info to optimize the program.
275: //
276: // For example, if a definition in an IR file is not referenced by
277: // non-IR objects at all, the plugin may choose to completely inline
278: // that definition within the IR objects and remove the symbol from the
279: // LTO result. On the other hand, if a definition is referenced by a
280: // non-IR object, it has to keep the symbol in the LTO result.
281: template <typename E>
282: static PluginStatus
283: get_symbols(const void *handle, int nsyms, PluginSymbol *psyms, bool is_v2) {
284:   ObjectFile<E> &file = *(ObjectFile<E> *)handle;
285:   assert(file.is_lto_obj);
286: 
287:   // If file is an archive member which was not chose to be included in
288:   // to the final result, we need to make the plugin to ignore all
289:   // symbols.
290:   if (!file.is_alive) {
291:     assert(!is_v2);
292:     for (int i = 0; i < nsyms; i++)
293:       psyms[i].resolution = LDPR_PREEMPTED_REG;
294:     return LDPS_NO_SYMS;
295:   }
296: 
297:   auto get_resolution = [&](ElfSym<E> &esym, Symbol<E> &sym) {
298:     if (!sym.file)
299:       return LDPR_UNDEF;
300: 
301:     if (sym.file == &file) {
302:       if (sym.referenced_by_regular_obj)
303:         return LDPR_PREVAILING_DEF;
304:       if (sym.is_exported)
305:         return is_v2 ? LDPR_PREVAILING_DEF : LDPR_PREVAILING_DEF_IRONLY_EXP;
306:       return LDPR_PREVAILING_DEF_IRONLY;
307:     }
308: 
309:     if (sym.file->is_dso)
310:       return LDPR_RESOLVED_DYN;
311: 
312:     if (((ObjectFile<E> *)sym.file)->is_lto_obj && !sym.is_wrapped)
313:       return esym.is_undef() ? LDPR_RESOLVED_IR : LDPR_PREEMPTED_IR;
314:     return esym.is_undef() ? LDPR_RESOLVED_EXEC : LDPR_PREEMPTED_REG;
315:   };
316: 
317:   // Set the symbol resolution results to psyms.
318:   for (i64 i = 0; i < nsyms; i++) {
319:     ElfSym<E> &esym = file.elf_syms[i + 1];
320:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[i + 1];
321:     psyms[i].resolution = get_resolution(esym, sym);
322:   }
323:   return LDPS_OK;
324: }
325: 
326: // This function restarts mold itself with `--:lto-pass2` and
327: // `--:ignore-ir-file` flags. We do this as a workaround for the old
328: // linker plugins that do not support the get_symbols_v3 API.
329: //
330: // get_symbols_v1 and get_symbols_v2 don't provide a way to ignore an
331: // object file we previously passed to the linker plugin. So we can't
332: // "unload" object files in archives that we ended up not choosing to
333: // include into the final output.
334: //
335: // As a workaround, we restart the linker with a list of object files
336: // the linker has to ignore, so that it won't read the object files
337: // from archives next time.
338: //
339: // This is an ugly hack and should be removed once GCC adopts the v3 API.
340: template <typename E>
341: static void restart_process(Context<E> &ctx) {
342:   std::vector<const char *> args;
343: 
344:   for (std::string_view arg : ctx.cmdline_args)
345:     args.push_back(strdup(std::string(arg).c_str()));
346: 
347:   for (std::unique_ptr<ObjectFile<E>> &file : ctx.obj_pool)
348:     if (file->is_lto_obj && !file->is_alive)
349:       args.push_back(strdup(("--:ignore-ir-file=" +
350:                              file->mf->get_identifier()).c_str()));
351: 
352:   args.push_back("--:lto-pass2");
353:   args.push_back(nullptr);
354: 
355:   std::cout << std::flush;
356:   std::cerr << std::flush;
357: 
358:   std::string self = get_self_path();
359:   execv(self.c_str(), (char * const *)args.data());
360:   std::cerr << "execv failed: " << errno_string() << "\n";
361:   _exit(1);
362: }
363: 
364: template <typename E>
365: static PluginStatus
366: get_symbols_v2(const void *handle, int nsyms, PluginSymbol *psyms) {
367:   LOG << "get_symbols_v2\n";
368:   return get_symbols<E>(handle, nsyms, psyms, true);
369: }
370:

372: static PluginStatus
373: get_symbols_v3(const void *handle, int nsyms, PluginSymbol *psyms) {
374:   LOG << "get_symbols_v3\n";
375:   return get_symbols<E>(handle, nsyms, psyms, false);
376: }

413: static PluginStatus
414: get_wrap_symbols(uint64_t *num_symbols, const char ***wrap_symbols) {
415:   LOG << "get_wrap_symbols\n";

418: 
419: template <typename E>
420: static PluginLinkerAPIVersion
421: get_api_version(const char *plugin_identifier,
422:                 unsigned plugin_version,
423:                 int minimal_api_supported,
424:                 int maximal_api_supported,
425:                 const char **linker_identifier,
426:                 const char **linker_version) {
427:   if (LAPI_V1 < minimal_api_supported)
428:     Fatal(*gctx<E>) << "LTO plugin does not support V0 or V1 API";
429: 
430:   std::string version = mold_version + "\0"s;
431: 
432:   *linker_identifier = "mold";
433:   *linker_version = version.data();
434: 
435:   if (LAPI_V1 <= maximal_api_supported) {
436:     is_gcc_linker_api_v1 = true;
437:     return LAPI_V1;
438:   }
439:   return LAPI_V0;
440: }
441: 
442: template <typename E>
443: static void load_plugin(Context<E> &ctx) {
444:   assert(phase == 0);
445:   phase = 1;
446:   gctx<E> = &ctx;
447: 
448:   void *handle = dlopen(ctx.arg.plugin.c_str(), RTLD_NOW | RTLD_GLOBAL);
449:   if (!handle)
450:     Fatal(ctx) << "could not open plugin file: " << dlerror();
451: 
452:   OnloadFn *onload = (OnloadFn *)dlsym(handle, "onload");
453:   if (!onload)
454:     Fatal(ctx) << "failed to load plugin " << ctx.arg.plugin << ": "
455:                << dlerror();
456: 
457:   auto save = [&](std::string_view str) {
458:     return save_string(ctx, std::string(str).c_str()).data();
459:   };
460: 
461:   std::vector<PluginTagValue> tv;
462:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_MESSAGE, message<E>);
463: 
464:   if (ctx.arg.shared)
465:     tv.emplace_back(LDPT_LINKER_OUTPUT, LDPO_DYN);
466:   else if (ctx.arg.pie)
467:     tv.emplace_back(LDPT_LINKER_OUTPUT, LDPO_PIE);
468:   else
469:     tv.emplace_back(LDPT_LINKER_OUTPUT, LDPO_EXEC);
470: 
471:   for (std::string_view opt : ctx.arg.plugin_opt)
472:     tv.emplace_back(LDPT_OPTION, save(opt));
473: 
474:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_REGISTER_CLAIM_FILE_HOOK, register_claim_file_hook<E>);
475:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_REGISTER_ALL_SYMBOLS_READ_HOOK,
476:                   register_all_symbols_read_hook<E>);
477:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_REGISTER_CLEANUP_HOOK, register_cleanup_hook<E>);
478:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ADD_SYMBOLS, add_symbols);
479:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_SYMBOLS, get_symbols_v1);
480:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ADD_INPUT_FILE, add_input_file<E>);
481:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_FILE, get_input_file);
482:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_RELEASE_INPUT_FILE, release_input_file<E>);
483:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ADD_INPUT_LIBRARY, add_input_library);
484:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_OUTPUT_NAME, save(ctx.arg.output));
485:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_SET_EXTRA_LIBRARY_PATH, set_extra_library_path);
486:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_VIEW, get_view<E>);
487:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_COUNT, get_input_section_count);
488:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_TYPE, get_input_section_type);
489:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_NAME, get_input_section_name);
490:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_CONTENTS, get_input_section_contents);
491:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_UPDATE_SECTION_ORDER, update_section_order);
492:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ALLOW_SECTION_ORDERING, allow_section_ordering);
493:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ADD_SYMBOLS_V2, add_symbols);
494:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_SYMBOLS_V2, get_symbols_v2<E>);
495:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_ALLOW_UNIQUE_SEGMENT_FOR_SECTIONS,
496:                   allow_unique_segment_for_sections);
497:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_UNIQUE_SEGMENT_FOR_SECTIONS, unique_segment_for_sections);
498:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_SYMBOLS_V3, get_symbols_v3<E>);
499:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_ALIGNMENT, get_input_section_alignment);
500:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_INPUT_SECTION_SIZE, get_input_section_size);
501:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_REGISTER_NEW_INPUT_HOOK, register_new_input_hook<E>);
502:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_WRAP_SYMBOLS, get_wrap_symbols);
503:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_GET_API_VERSION, get_api_version<E>);
504:   tv.emplace_back(LDPT_NULL, 0);
505: 
506:   [[maybe_unused]] PluginStatus status = onload(tv.data());
507:   assert(status == LDPS_OK);
508: }
509: 
510: template <typename E>
511: static ElfSym<E> to_elf_sym(PluginSymbol &psym) {
512:   ElfSym<E> esym;
513:   memset(&esym, 0, sizeof(esym));
514: 
515:   switch (psym.def) {
516:   case LDPK_DEF:
517:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_ABS;
518:     break;
519:   case LDPK_WEAKDEF:
520:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_ABS;
521:     esym.st_bind = STB_WEAK;
522:     break;
523:   case LDPK_UNDEF:
524:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF;
525:     break;
526:   case LDPK_WEAKUNDEF:
527:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF;
528:     esym.st_bind = STB_WEAK;
529:     break;
530:   case LDPK_COMMON:
531:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_COMMON;
532:     break;
533:   }
534: 
535:   switch (psym.symbol_type) {
536:   case LDST_UNKNOWN:
537:     break;
538:   case LDST_FUNCTION:
539:     esym.st_type = STT_FUNC;
540:     break;
541:   case LDST_VARIABLE:
542:     esym.st_type = STT_OBJECT;
543:     break;
544:   };
545: 
546:   switch (psym.visibility) {
547:   case LDPV_DEFAULT:
548:     break;
549:   case LDPV_PROTECTED:
550:     esym.st_visibility = STV_PROTECTED;
551:     break;
552:   case LDPV_INTERNAL:
553:     esym.st_visibility = STV_INTERNAL;
554:     break;
555:   case LDPV_HIDDEN:
556:     esym.st_visibility = STV_HIDDEN;
557:     break;
558:   }
559: 
560:   esym.st_size = psym.size;
561:   return esym;
562: }
563: 
564: // Returns true if a given linker plugin looks like LLVM's one.
565: // Returns false if it's GCC.
566: template <typename E>
567: static bool is_llvm(Context<E> &ctx) {
568:   return ctx.arg.plugin.ends_with("LLVMgold.so");
569: }
570: 
571: // Returns true if a given linker plugin supports the get_symbols_v3 API.
572: // Any version of LLVM and GCC 12 or newer support it.
573: template <typename E>
574: static bool supports_v3_api(Context<E> &ctx) {
575:   return is_gcc_linker_api_v1 || is_llvm(ctx);
576: }
577: 
578: template <typename E>
579: ObjectFile<E> *read_lto_object(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
580:   // V0 API's claim_file is not thread-safe.
581:   static std::mutex mu;
582:   std::unique_lock lock(mu, std::defer_lock);
583:   if (!is_gcc_linker_api_v1)
584:     lock.lock();
585: 
586:   if (ctx.arg.plugin.empty())
587:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": don't know how to handle this LTO object file "
588:                << "because no -plugin option was given. Please make sure you "
589:                << "added -flto not only for creating object files but also for "
590:                << "creating the final executable.";
591: 
592:   // dlopen the linker plugin file
593:   static std::once_flag flag;
594:   std::call_once(flag, [&] { load_plugin(ctx); });
595: 
596:   // Create mold's object instance
597:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = new ObjectFile<E>;
598:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(obj);
599: 
600:   obj->filename = mf->name;
601:   obj->symbols.push_back(new Symbol<E>);
602:   obj->first_global = 1;
603:   obj->is_lto_obj = true;
604:   obj->mf = mf;
605: 
606:   // Create plugin's object instance
607:   PluginInputFile file = {};
608: 
609:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf2 = mf->parent ? mf->parent : mf;
610:   file.name = save_string(ctx, mf2->name).data();
611:   if (mf2->fd == -1)
612:     mf2->fd = open(file.name, O_RDONLY);
613:   file.fd = mf2->fd;
614:   if (file.fd == -1)
615:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << file.name << ": " << errno_string();
616: 
617:   if (mf->parent)
618:     obj->archive_name = mf->parent->name;
619: 
620:   file.offset = mf->get_offset();
621:   file.filesize = mf->size;
622:   file.handle = (void *)obj;
623: 
624:   LOG << "read_lto_symbols: "<< mf->name << "\n";
625: 
626:   // claim_file_hook() calls add_symbols() which initializes `plugin_symbols`
627:   int claimed = false;
628:   claim_file_hook(&file, &claimed);
629:   if (!claimed)
630:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": not claimed by the LTO plugin;"
631:                << " please make sure you are using the same compiler of the"
632:                << " same version for all object files";
633: 
634:   // It looks like GCC doesn't need fd after claim_file_hook() while
635:   // LLVM needs it and takes the ownership of fd. To prevent "too many
636:   // open files" issue, we close fd only for GCC. This is ugly, though.
637:   if (!is_llvm(ctx)) {
638:     close(mf2->fd);
639:     mf2->fd = -1;
640:   }
641: 
642:   // Initialize object symbols
643:   std::vector<ElfSym<E>> *esyms = new std::vector<ElfSym<E>>(1);
644: 
645:   for (PluginSymbol &psym : plugin_symbols) {
646:     esyms->push_back(to_elf_sym<E>(psym));
647:     obj->symbols.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, save_string(ctx, psym.name)));
648:   }
649: 
650:   obj->elf_syms = *esyms;
651:   obj->has_symver.resize(esyms->size());
652:   plugin_symbols.clear();
653:   return obj;
654: }
655: 
656: // Entry point
657: template <typename E>
658: std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> do_lto(Context<E> &ctx) {
659:   Timer t(ctx, "do_lto");
660: 
661:   if (!ctx.arg.lto_pass2 && !supports_v3_api(ctx))
662:     restart_process(ctx);
663: 
664:   assert(phase == 1);
665:   phase = 2;
666: 
667:   // Set `referenced_by_regular_obj` bit.
668:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
669:     if (file->is_lto_obj)
670:       return;
671: 
672:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->symbols.size(); i++) {
673:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
674: 
675:       if (sym.file && !sym.file->is_dso &&
676:           ((ObjectFile<E> *)sym.file)->is_lto_obj) {
677:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
678:         sym.referenced_by_regular_obj = true;
679:       }
680:     }
681:   });
682: 
683:   // Symbols specified by the --wrap option needs to be visible from
684:   // regular object files.
685:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.wrap) {
686:     get_symbol(ctx, name)->referenced_by_regular_obj = true;
687: 
688:     std::string_view x = save_string(ctx, "__wrap_" + std::string(name));
689:     std::string_view y = save_string(ctx, "__real_" + std::string(name));
690: 
691:     get_symbol(ctx, x)->referenced_by_regular_obj = true;
692:     get_symbol(ctx, y)->referenced_by_regular_obj = true;
693:   }
694: 
695:   // all_symbols_read_hook() calls add_input_file() and add_input_library()
696:   LOG << "all symbols read\n";
697:   if (PluginStatus st = all_symbols_read_hook(); st != LDPS_OK)
698:     Fatal(ctx) << "LTO: all_symbols_read_hook returns " << st;
699: 
700:   return lto_objects<E>;
701: }
702: 
703: template <typename E>
704: void lto_cleanup(Context<E> &ctx) {
705:   Timer t(ctx, "lto_cleanup");
706: 
707:   if (cleanup_hook)
708:     cleanup_hook();
709: }
710: 
711: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
712: 
713: template ObjectFile<E> *read_lto_object(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
714: template std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> do_lto(Context<E> &);
715: template void lto_cleanup(Context<E> &);
716: 
717: } // namespace mold::elf

  8:   LDPS_OK,
  9:   LDPS_NO_SYMS,
 10:   LDPS_BAD_HANDLE,
 11:   LDPS_ERR,
 12: };
 13: 
 14: enum PluginTag {
 15:   LDPT_NULL,
 16:   LDPT_API_VERSION,
 17:   LDPT_GOLD_VERSION,
 18:   LDPT_LINKER_OUTPUT,
 19:   LDPT_OPTION,
 20:   LDPT_REGISTER_CLAIM_FILE_HOOK,
 21:   LDPT_REGISTER_ALL_SYMBOLS_READ_HOOK,
 22:   LDPT_REGISTER_CLEANUP_HOOK,
 23:   LDPT_ADD_SYMBOLS,
 24:   LDPT_GET_SYMBOLS,
 25:   LDPT_ADD_INPUT_FILE,
 26:   LDPT_MESSAGE,
 27:   LDPT_GET_INPUT_FILE,
