================
File: elf/mapfile.cc
================
  1: #include "mold.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <fstream>
  4: #include <iomanip>
  5: #include <ios>
  6: #include <sstream>
  7: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
  8: #include <unordered_map>
  9: 
 10: namespace mold::elf {
 11: 
 12: template <typename E>
 13: using Map =
 14:   tbb::concurrent_hash_map<InputSection<E> *, std::vector<Symbol<E> *>>;
 15: 
 16: template <typename E>
 17: static std::unique_ptr<std::ofstream> open_output_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 18:   std::unique_ptr<std::ofstream> file(new std::ofstream);
 19:   file->open(ctx.arg.Map.c_str());
 20:   if (!file->is_open())
 21:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << ctx.arg.Map << ": " << errno_string();
 22:   return file;
 23: }
 24: 
 25: template <typename E>
 26: static Map<E> get_map(Context<E> &ctx) {
 27:   Map<E> map;
 28: 
 29:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 30:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->symbols) {
 31:       if (sym->file != file || sym->get_type() == STT_SECTION)
 32:         continue;
 33: 
 34:       if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym->get_input_section()) {
 35:         assert(file == &isec->file);
 36:         typename Map<E>::accessor acc;
 37:         map.insert(acc, {isec, {}});
 38:         acc->second.push_back(sym);
 39:       }
 40:     }
 41:   });
 42: 
 43:   if (map.size() <= 1)
 44:     return map;
 45: 
 46:   tbb::parallel_for(map.range(), [](const typename Map<E>::range_type &range) {
 47:     for (auto it = range.begin(); it != range.end(); it++) {
 48:       std::vector<Symbol<E> *> &vec = it->second;
 49:       sort(vec, [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) { return a->value < b->value; });
 50:     }
 51:   });
 52:   return map;
 53: }
 54: 
 55: template <typename E>
 56: void print_map(Context<E> &ctx) {
 57:   std::ostream *out = &std::cout;
 58:   std::unique_ptr<std::ofstream> file;
 59: 
 60:   if (!ctx.arg.Map.empty()) {
 61:     file = open_output_file(ctx);
 62:     out = file.get();
 63:   }
 64: 
 65:   // Construct a section-to-symbol map.
 66:   Map<E> map = get_map(ctx);
 67: 
 68:   // Print a mapfile.
 69:   *out << "               VMA       Size Align Out     In      Symbol\n";
 70: 
 71:   for (Chunk<E> *osec : ctx.chunks) {
 72:     *out << std::showbase
 73:          << std::setw(18) << std::hex << (u64)osec->shdr.sh_addr << std::dec
 74:          << std::setw(11) << (u64)osec->shdr.sh_size
 75:          << std::setw(6) << (u64)osec->shdr.sh_addralign
 76:          << " " << osec->name << "\n";
 77: 
 78:     if (osec->kind() != OUTPUT_SECTION)
 79:       continue;
 80: 
 81:     std::span<InputSection<E> *> members = ((OutputSection<E> *)osec)->members;
 82:     std::vector<std::string> bufs(members.size());
 83: 
 84:     tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)members.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 85:       InputSection<E> *mem = members[i];
 86:       std::ostringstream ss;
 87:       opt_demangle = ctx.arg.demangle;
 88:       u64 addr = osec->shdr.sh_addr + mem->offset;
 89: 
 90:       ss << std::showbase
 91:          << std::setw(18) << std::hex << addr << std::dec
 92:          << std::setw(11) << (u64)mem->sh_size
 93:          << std::setw(6) << (1 << (u64)mem->p2align)
 94:          << "         " << *mem << "\n";
 95: 
 96:       typename Map<E>::const_accessor acc;
 97:       if (map.find(acc, mem))
 98:         for (Symbol<E> *sym : acc->second)
 99:           ss << std::showbase
100:              << std::setw(18) << std::hex << sym->get_addr(ctx) << std::dec
101:              << "          0     0                 "
102:              << *sym << "\n";
103: 
104:       bufs[i] = ss.str();
105:     });
106: 
107:     for (std::string &str : bufs)
108:       *out << str;
109:   }
110: }
111: 
112: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
113: 
114: template void print_map(Context<E> &ctx);
115: 
116: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/mold-wrapper.c
================
  1: #define _GNU_SOURCE 1
  2: 
  3: #if !defined(__OpenBSD__) && !defined(__FreeBSD__)
  4: #  include <alloca.h>
  5: #endif
  6: #include <dlfcn.h>
  7: #include <spawn.h>
  8: #include <stdarg.h>
  9: #include <stdbool.h>
 10: #include <stdio.h>
 11: #include <stdlib.h>
 12: #include <string.h>
 13: #include <unistd.h>
 14: 
 15: extern char **environ;
 16: 
 17: static char *get_mold_path() {
 18:   char *path = getenv("MOLD_PATH");
 19:   if (path)
 20:     return path;
 21:   fprintf(stderr, "MOLD_PATH is not set\n");
 22:   exit(1);
 23: }
 24: 
 25: static void debug_print(const char *fmt, ...) {
 26:   if (!getenv("MOLD_WRAPPER_DEBUG"))
 27:     return;
 28: 
 29:   va_list ap;
 30:   va_start(ap, fmt);
 31:   fprintf(stderr, "mold-wrapper.so: ");
 32:   vfprintf(stderr, fmt, ap);
 33:   fflush(stderr);
 34:   va_end(ap);
 35: }
 36: 
 37: static int count_args(va_list *ap) {
 38:   va_list aq;
 39:   va_copy(aq, *ap);
 40: 
 41:   int i = 0;
 42:   while (va_arg(aq, char *))
 43:     i++;
 44:   va_end(aq);
 45:   return i;
 46: }
 47: 
 48: static void copy_args(char **argv, const char *arg0, va_list *ap) {
 49:   int i = 1;
 50:   char *arg;
 51:   while ((arg = va_arg(*ap, char *)))
 52:     argv[i++] = arg;
 53: 
 54:   ((const char **)argv)[0] = arg0;
 55:   ((const char **)argv)[i] = NULL;
 56: }
 57: 
 58: static bool is_ld(const char *path) {
 59:   const char *ptr = path + strlen(path);
 60:   while (path < ptr && ptr[-1] != '/')
 61:     ptr--;
 62: 
 63:   return !strcmp(ptr, "ld") || !strcmp(ptr, "ld.lld") ||
 64:          !strcmp(ptr, "ld.gold") || !strcmp(ptr, "ld.bfd");
 65: }
 66: 
 67: int execvpe(const char *file, char *const *argv, char *const *envp) {
 68:   debug_print("execvpe %s\n", file);
 69: 
 70:   if (!strcmp(file, "ld") || is_ld(file))
 71:     file = get_mold_path();
 72: 
 73:   for (int i = 0; envp[i]; i++)
 74:     putenv(envp[i]);
 75: 
 76:   typeof(execvpe) *real = dlsym(RTLD_NEXT, "execvp");
 77:   return real(file, argv, environ);
 78: }
 79: 
 80: int execve(const char *path, char *const *argv, char *const *envp) {
 81:   debug_print("execve %s\n", path);
 82:   if (is_ld(path))
 83:     path = get_mold_path();
 84:   typeof(execve) *real = dlsym(RTLD_NEXT, "execve");
 85:   return real(path, argv, envp);
 86: }
 87: 
 88: int execl(const char *path, const char *arg0, ...) {
 89:   va_list ap;
 90:   va_start(ap, arg0);
 91:   char **argv = alloca((count_args(&ap) + 2) * sizeof(char *));
 92:   copy_args(argv, arg0, &ap);
 93:   va_end(ap);
 94:   return execve(path, argv, environ);
 95: }
 96: 
 97: int execlp(const char *file, const char *arg0, ...) {
 98:   va_list ap;
 99:   va_start(ap, arg0);
100:   char **argv = alloca((count_args(&ap) + 2) * sizeof(char *));
101:   copy_args(argv, arg0, &ap);
102:   va_end(ap);
103:   return execvpe(file, argv, environ);
104: }
105: 
106: int execle(const char *path, const char *arg0, ...) {
107:   va_list ap;
108:   va_start(ap, arg0);
109:   char **argv = alloca((count_args(&ap) + 2) * sizeof(char *));
110:   copy_args(argv, arg0, &ap);
111:   char **env = va_arg(ap, char **);
112:   va_end(ap);
113:   return execve(path, argv, env);
114: }
115: 
116: int execv(const char *path, char *const *argv) {
117:   return execve(path, argv, environ);
118: }
119: 
120: int execvp(const char *file, char *const *argv) {
121:   return execvpe(file, argv, environ);
122: }
123: 
124: int posix_spawn(pid_t *pid, const char *path,
125:                 const posix_spawn_file_actions_t *file_actions,
126:                 const posix_spawnattr_t *attrp,
127:                 char *const *argv, char *const *envp) {
128:   debug_print("posix_spawn %s\n", path);
129:   if (is_ld(path))
130:     path = get_mold_path();
131:   typeof(posix_spawn) *real = dlsym(RTLD_NEXT, "posix_spawn");
132:   return real(pid, path, file_actions, attrp, argv, envp);
133: }

================
File: elf/relocatable.cc
================
  1: // This file implements -r or --relocatable. That option forces the linker
  2: // to combine input object files into another single large object file.
  3: // Since the behavior of the linker when the option is given is quite
  4: // different from that of the normal execution mode, we separate code for
  5: // the feature into this separate file.
  6: //
  7: // The --relocatable option isn't used very often. After all, if you want
  8: // to combine object files into a single file, you could use `ar`.
  9: // However, some programs use it in a creative manner which is hard to be
 10: // substituted with static archives, so we need to support this option in
 11: // the same way as GNU ld does. A notable example is GHC (Glasgow Haskell
 12: // Compiler). GHC has its own dynamic linker which can load a .o file (as
 13: // opposed to a .so) into memory. GHC's module is not a shared object file
 14: // but a combined object file.
 15: //
 16: // There are many different ways to combine object files into a single file.
 17: // The simplest approach would be to just copy all sections from input files
 18: // to an output file as-is with a few exceptions for singleton sections such
 19: // as the symbol table or the string table. That works, but that's not
 20: // compatible with GNU ld.
 21: //
 22: // To be compatible with GNU ld, we need to do the followings:
 23: //
 24: //  - Regular sections containing opaque data (e.g. ".text" or ".data")
 25: //    are just copied as-is. Two sections with the same name are merged.
 26: //
 27: //  - .symtab, .strtab and .shstrtab are merged.
 28: //
 29: //  - COMDAT groups are uniquified.
 30: //
 31: //  - Relocations are copied, but we need to fix symbol indices.
 32: 
 33: #include "mold.h"
 34: 
 35: #include <tbb/parallel_for.h>
 36: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 37: 
 38: namespace mold::elf {
 39: 
 40: // Create linker-synthesized sections
 41: template <typename E>
 42: static void r_create_synthetic_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 43:   auto push = [&]<typename T>(T *x) {
 44:     ctx.chunks.push_back(x);
 45:     ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(x);
 46:     return x;
 47:   };
 48: 
 49:   ctx.ehdr = push(new OutputEhdr<E>(0));
 50:   ctx.shdr = push(new OutputShdr<E>);
 51:   ctx.eh_frame = push(new EhFrameSection<E>);
 52:   ctx.eh_frame_reloc = push(new EhFrameRelocSection<E>);
 53:   ctx.strtab = push(new StrtabSection<E>);
 54:   ctx.symtab = push(new SymtabSection<E>);
 55:   ctx.shstrtab = push(new ShstrtabSection<E>);
 56:   ctx.note_property = push(new NotePropertySection<E>);
 57: }
 58: 
 59: // Create SHT_GROUP (i.e. comdat group) sections. We uniquify comdat
 60: // sections by signature. We want to propagate input comdat groups as
 61: // output comdat groups if they are still alive after uniquification.
 62: template <typename E>
 63: static void create_comdat_group_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 64:   Timer t(ctx, "create_comdat_group_sections");
 65: 
 66:   std::vector<std::vector<Chunk<E> *>> vec{ctx.objs.size()};
 67: 
 68:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 69:     ObjectFile<E> &file = *ctx.objs[i];
 70: 
 71:     for (ComdatGroupRef<E> &ref : file.comdat_groups) {
 72:       if (ref.group->owner != file.priority)
 73:         continue;
 74: 
 75:       Symbol<E> *sym = file.symbols[file.elf_sections[ref.sect_idx].sh_info];
 76:       assert(sym);
 77: 
 78:       std::vector<Chunk<E> *> members;
 79:       for (u32 j : ref.members) {
 80:         const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = file.elf_sections[j];
 81:         if (shdr.sh_type == (E::is_rela ? SHT_RELA : SHT_REL)) {
 82:           InputSection<E> &isec = *file.sections[shdr.sh_info];
 83:           members.push_back(isec.output_section->reloc_sec.get());
 84:         } else {
 85:           InputSection<E> &isec = *file.sections[j];
 86:           members.push_back(isec.output_section);
 87:         }
 88:       }
 89: 
 90:       vec[i].push_back(new ComdatGroupSection<E>(*sym, std::move(members)));
 91:     }
 92:   });
 93: 
 94:   for (std::vector<Chunk<E> *> &vec2 : vec) {
 95:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : vec2) {
 96:       ctx.chunks.push_back(chunk);
 97:       ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(chunk);
 98:     }
 99:   }
100: }
101: 
102: // Unresolved undefined symbols in the -r mode are simply propagated to an
103: // output file as undefined symbols. This function guarantees that
104: // unresolved undefined symbols belongs to some input file.
105: template <typename E>
106: static void r_claim_unresolved_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
107:   Timer t(ctx, "r_claim_unresolved_symbols");
108: 
109:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
110:     if (!file->is_alive)
111:       return;
112: 
113:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
114:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[i];
115:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
116:       if (!esym.is_undef())
117:         continue;
118: 
119:       std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
120: 
121:       if (sym.file &&
122:           (!sym.esym().is_undef() || sym.file->priority <= file->priority))
123:         continue;
124: 
125:       sym.file = file;
126:       sym.origin = 0;
127:       sym.value = 0;
128:       sym.sym_idx = i;
129:     }
130:   });
131: }
132: 
133: // Set output section in-file offsets. Output section memory addresses
134: // are left as zero.
135: template <typename E>
136: static u64 r_set_osec_offsets(Context<E> &ctx) {
137:   u64 offset = 0;
138:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
139:     offset = align_to(offset, chunk->shdr.sh_addralign);
140:     chunk->shdr.sh_offset = offset;
141:     offset += chunk->shdr.sh_size;
142:   }
143:   return offset;
144: }
145: 
146: template <typename E>
147: void combine_objects(Context<E> &ctx) {
148:   compute_merged_section_sizes(ctx);
149: 
150:   create_output_sections(ctx);
151: 
152:   r_create_synthetic_sections(ctx);
153: 
154:   r_claim_unresolved_symbols(ctx);
155: 
156:   compute_section_sizes(ctx);
157: 
158:   sort_output_sections(ctx);
159: 
160:   create_output_symtab(ctx);
161: 
162:   ctx.eh_frame->construct(ctx);
163: 
164:   create_reloc_sections(ctx);
165: 
166:   create_comdat_group_sections(ctx);
167: 
168:   compute_section_headers(ctx);
169: 
170:   i64 filesize = r_set_osec_offsets(ctx);
171:   ctx.output_file =
172:     OutputFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, ctx.arg.output, filesize, 0666);
173:   ctx.buf = ctx.output_file->buf;
174: 
175:   copy_chunks(ctx);
176:   clear_padding(ctx);
177:   ctx.output_file->close(ctx);
178:   ctx.checkpoint();
179: 
180:   if (ctx.arg.print_map)
181:     print_map(ctx);
182: 
183:   if (ctx.arg.stats)
184:     show_stats(ctx);
185: 
186:   if (ctx.arg.perf)
187:     print_timer_records(ctx.timer_records);
188: 
189:   if (ctx.arg.quick_exit)
190:     _exit(0);
191: }
192: 
193: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
194: 
195: template void combine_objects(Context<E> &);
196: 
197: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/subprocess.cc
================
  1: #if !defined(_WIN32) && !defined(__APPLE__)
  2: 
  3: #include "mold.h"
  4: #include "config.h"
  5: 
  6: #include <filesystem>
  7: #include <signal.h>
  8: #include <sys/stat.h>
  9: #include <sys/time.h>
 10: #include <sys/types.h>
 11: #include <sys/wait.h>
 12: #include <unistd.h>
 13: 
 14: namespace mold::elf {
 15: 
 16: #ifdef MOLD_X86_64
 17: // Exiting from a program with large memory usage is slow --
 18: // it may take a few hundred milliseconds. To hide the latency,
 19: // we fork a child and let it do the actual linking work.
 20: std::function<void()> fork_child() {
 21:   int pipefd[2];
 22:   if (pipe(pipefd) == -1) {
 23:     perror("pipe");
 24:     exit(1);
 25:   }
 26: 
 27:   pid_t pid = fork();
 28:   if (pid == -1) {
 29:     perror("fork");
 30:     exit(1);
 31:   }
 32: 
 33:   if (pid > 0) {
 34:     // Parent
 35:     close(pipefd[1]);
 36: 
 37:     char buf[1];
 38:     if (read(pipefd[0], buf, 1) == 1)
 39:       _exit(0);
 40: 
 41:     int status;
 42:     waitpid(pid, &status, 0);
 43: 
 44:     if (WIFEXITED(status))
 45:       _exit(WEXITSTATUS(status));
 46:     if (WIFSIGNALED(status))
 47:       raise(WTERMSIG(status));
 48:     _exit(1);
 49:   }
 50: 
 51:   // Child
 52:   close(pipefd[0]);
 53: 
 54:   return [=] {
 55:     char buf[] = {1};
 56:     [[maybe_unused]] int n = write(pipefd[1], buf, 1);
 57:     assert(n == 1);
 58:   };
 59: }
 60: #endif
 61: 
 62: template <typename E>
 63: static std::string find_dso(Context<E> &ctx, std::filesystem::path self) {
 64:   // Look for mold-wrapper.so from the same directory as the executable is.
 65:   std::filesystem::path path = self.parent_path() / "mold-wrapper.so";
 66:   std::error_code ec;
 67:   if (std::filesystem::is_regular_file(path, ec) && !ec)
 68:     return path;
 69: 
 70:   // If not found, search $(MOLD_LIBDIR)/mold, which is /usr/local/lib/mold
 71:   // by default.
 72:   path = MOLD_LIBDIR "/mold/mold-wrapper.so";
 73:   if (std::filesystem::is_regular_file(path, ec) && !ec)
 74:     return path;
 75: 
 76:   // Look for ../lib/mold/mold-wrapper.so
 77:   path = self.parent_path() / "../lib/mold/mold-wrapper.so";
 78:   if (std::filesystem::is_regular_file(path, ec) && !ec)
 79:     return path;
 80: 
 81:   Fatal(ctx) << "mold-wrapper.so is missing";
 82: }
 83: 
 84: template <typename E>
 85: [[noreturn]]
 86: void process_run_subcommand(Context<E> &ctx, int argc, char **argv) {
 87:   assert(argv[1] == "-run"s || argv[1] == "--run"s);
 88: 
 89:   if (!argv[2])
 90:     Fatal(ctx) << "-run: argument missing";
 91: 
 92:   // Get the mold-wrapper.so path
 93:   std::string self = get_self_path();
 94:   std::string dso_path = find_dso(ctx, self);
 95: 
 96:   // Set environment variables
 97:   putenv(strdup(("LD_PRELOAD=" + dso_path).c_str()));
 98:   putenv(strdup(("MOLD_PATH=" + self).c_str()));
 99: 
100:   // If ld, ld.lld or ld.gold is specified, run mold itself
101:   if (std::string cmd = filepath(argv[2]).filename();
102:       cmd == "ld" || cmd == "ld.lld" || cmd == "ld.gold") {
103:     std::vector<char *> args;
104:     args.push_back(argv[0]);
105:     args.insert(args.end(), argv + 3, argv + argc);
106:     args.push_back(nullptr);
107:     execv(self.c_str(), args.data());
108:     Fatal(ctx) << "mold -run failed: " << self << ": " << errno_string();
109:   }
110: 
111:   // Execute a given command
112:   execvp(argv[2], argv + 2);
113:   Fatal(ctx) << "mold -run failed: " << argv[2] << ": " << errno_string();
114: }
115: 
116: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
117: 
118: template void process_run_subcommand(Context<E> &, int, char **);
119: 
120: } // namespace mold::elf
121: 
122: #endif

================
File: macho/arch-x86-64.cc
================
  1: #include "mold.h"
  2: 
  3: namespace mold::macho {
  4: 
  5: using E = X86_64;
  6: 
  7: template <>
  8: void StubsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
  9:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset;
 10: 
 11:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 12:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *imm(%rip)
 13:   };
 14: 
 15:   static_assert(E::stub_size == sizeof(insn));
 16: 
 17:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++) {
 18:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 19: 
 20:     u64 this_addr = this->hdr.addr + i * 6;
 21: 
 22:     u64 ptr_addr;
 23:     if (ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr)
 24:       ptr_addr = ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.addr + sizeof(Word<E>) * i;
 25:     else
 26:       ptr_addr = syms[i]->get_got_addr(ctx);
 27: 
 28:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 2) = ptr_addr - this_addr - 6;
 29:     buf += sizeof(insn);
 30:   }
 31: }
 32: 
 33: template <>
 34: void StubHelperSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 35:   u8 *start = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset;
 36:   u8 *buf = start;
 37: 
 38:   static const u8 insn0[] = {
 39:     0x4c, 0x8d, 0x1d, 0, 0, 0, 0, // lea $__dyld_private(%rip), %r11
 40:     0x41, 0x53,                   // push %r11
 41:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // jmp *$dyld_stub_binder@GOT(%rip)
 42:     0x90,                         // nop
 43:   };
 44: 
 45:   static_assert(sizeof(insn0) == E::stub_helper_hdr_size);
 46: 
 47:   memcpy(buf, insn0, sizeof(insn0));
 48:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 3) = ctx.__dyld_private->get_addr(ctx) - this->hdr.addr - 7;
 49:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 11) =
 50:     ctx.dyld_stub_binder->get_got_addr(ctx) - this->hdr.addr - 15;
 51: 
 52:   buf += sizeof(insn0);
 53: 
 54:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.stubs.syms.size(); i++) {
 55:     u8 insn[] = {
 56:       0x68, 0, 0, 0, 0, // push $bind_offset
 57:       0xe9, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp $__stub_helper
 58:     };
 59: 
 60:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::stub_helper_size);
 61: 
 62:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 63:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 1) = ctx.lazy_bind->bind_offsets[i];
 64:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = start - buf - 10;
 65:     buf += sizeof(insn);
 66:   }
 67: }
 68: 
 69: template <>
 70: void ObjcStubsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 71:   if (this->hdr.size == 0)
 72:     return;
 73: 
 74:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 75:     0x48, 0x8b, 0x35, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov @selector("foo")(%rip), %rsi
 76:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // jmp *_objc_msgSend@GOT(%rip)
 77:     0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc,             // (padding)
 78:   };
 79:   static_assert(sizeof(insn) == ENTRY_SIZE);
 80: 
 81:   u64 msgsend_got_addr = ctx._objc_msgSend->get_got_addr(ctx);
 82: 
 83:   for (i64 i = 0; i < methnames.size(); i++) {
 84:     ul32 *buf = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset + ENTRY_SIZE * i);
 85:     u64 sel_addr = selrefs[i]->get_addr(ctx);
 86:     u64 ent_addr = this->hdr.addr + ENTRY_SIZE * i;
 87: 
 88:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 89:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 3) = sel_addr - ent_addr - 7;
 90:     *(ul32 *)(buf + 9) = msgsend_got_addr - ent_addr - 13;
 91:   }
 92: }
 93: 
 94: static i64 get_reloc_addend(u32 type) {
 95:   switch (type) {
 96:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_1:
 97:     return 1;
 98:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_2:
 99:     return 2;
100:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_4:
101:     return 4;
102:   default:
103:     return 0;
104:   }
105: }
106: 
107: static i64 read_addend(u8 *buf, const MachRel &r) {
108:   if (r.p2size == 2)
109:     return *(il32 *)(buf + r.offset);
110:   assert(r.p2size == 3);
111:   return *(il64 *)(buf + r.offset);
112: }
113: 
114: template <>
115: std::vector<Relocation<E>>
116: read_relocations(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, const MachSection<E> &hdr) {
117:   std::vector<Relocation<E>> vec;
118:   vec.reserve(hdr.nreloc);
119: 
120:   MachRel *rels = (MachRel *)(file.mf->data + hdr.reloff);
121: 
122:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.nreloc; i++) {
123:     MachRel &r = rels[i];
124:     i64 addend = read_addend(file.mf->data + hdr.offset, r) +
125:                  get_reloc_addend(r.type);
126: 
127:     vec.push_back(Relocation<E>{
128:       .offset = r.offset,
129:       .type = (u8)r.type,
130:       .size = (u8)(1 << r.p2size),
131:       .is_subtracted = (i > 0 && rels[i - 1].type == X86_64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR),
132:     });
133: 
134:     Relocation<E> &rel = vec.back();
135: 
136:     if (r.is_extern) {
137:       rel.target = file.syms[r.idx];
138:       rel.is_sym = true;
139:       rel.addend = addend;
140:     } else {
141:       u64 addr = r.is_pcrel ? (hdr.addr + r.offset + addend + 4) : addend;
142:       Subsection<E> *target = file.find_subsection(ctx, addr);
143:       if (!target)
144:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": bad relocation: " << r.offset;
145: 
146:       rel.target = target;
147:       rel.is_sym = false;
148:       rel.addend = addr - target->input_addr;
149:     }
150:   }
151: 
152:   return vec;
153: }
154: 
155: template <>
156: void Subsection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
157:   for (Relocation<E> &r : get_rels()) {
158:     Symbol<E> *sym = r.sym();
159:     if (!sym || !sym->file)
160:       continue;
161: 
162:     if (sym->file->is_dylib)
163:       ((DylibFile<E> *)sym->file)->is_alive = true;
164: 
165:     if ((r.type == X86_64_RELOC_TLV) != sym->is_tlv)
166:       Error(ctx) << "illegal thread local variable reference to regular symbol `"
167:                  << *sym << "`";
168: 
169:     switch (r.type) {
170:     case X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH:
171:       if (sym->is_imported)
172:         sym->flags |= NEEDS_STUB;
173:       break;
174:     case X86_64_RELOC_GOT:
175:     case X86_64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD:
176:       sym->flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
177:       break;
178:     case X86_64_RELOC_TLV:
179:       sym->flags |= NEEDS_THREAD_PTR;
180:       break;
181:     }
182:   }
183: }
184: 
185: template <>
186: void Subsection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
187:   std::span<Relocation<E>> rels = get_rels();
188: 
189:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
190:     Relocation<E> &r = rels[i];
191: 
192:     if (r.sym() && !r.sym()->file) {
193:       Error(ctx) << "undefined symbol: " << isec->file << ": " << *r.sym();
194:       continue;
195:     }
196: 
197:     u8 *loc = buf + r.offset;
198:     u64 S = r.get_addr(ctx);
199:     u64 A = r.addend;
200:     u64 P = get_addr(ctx) + r.offset;
201:     u64 G = r.sym() ? r.sym()->got_idx * sizeof(Word<E>) : 0;
202:     u64 GOT = ctx.got.hdr.addr;
203: 
204:     switch (r.type) {
205:     case X86_64_RELOC_UNSIGNED:
206:       ASSERT(r.size == 8);
207: 
208:       if (r.sym() && r.sym()->is_imported)
209:         break;
210: 
211:       if (r.refers_to_tls())
212:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tls_begin;
213:       else
214:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A;
215:       break;
216:     case X86_64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR:
217:       ASSERT(r.size == 4 || r.size == 8);
218:       i++;
219:       ASSERT(rels[i].type == X86_64_RELOC_UNSIGNED);
220:       if (r.size == 4)
221:         *(ul32 *)loc = rels[i].get_addr(ctx) + rels[i].addend - S;
222:       else
223:         *(ul64 *)loc = rels[i].get_addr(ctx) + rels[i].addend - S;
224:       break;
225:     case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED:
226:     case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_1:
227:     case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_2:
228:     case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_4:
229:       ASSERT(r.size == 4);
230:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P - 4 - get_reloc_addend(r.type);
231:       break;
232:     case X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH:
233:       ASSERT(r.size == 4);
234:       *(ul32 *)loc = S + A - P - 4;
235:       break;
236:     case X86_64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD:
237:     case X86_64_RELOC_GOT:
238:       ASSERT(r.size == 4);
239:       *(ul32 *)loc = G + GOT + A - P - 4;
240:       break;
241:     case X86_64_RELOC_TLV:
242:       ASSERT(r.size == 4);
243:       *(ul32 *)loc = r.sym()->get_tlv_addr(ctx) + A - P - 4;
244:       break;
245:     default:
246:       Fatal(ctx) << *isec<< ": unknown reloc: " << (int)r.type;
247:     }
248:   }
249: }
250: 
251: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: macho/input-sections.cc
================
 1: #include "mold.h"
 2: 
 3: namespace mold::macho {
 4: 
 5: template <typename E>
 6: OutputSection<E> &get_output_section(Context<E> &ctx, const MachSection<E> &hdr) {
 7:   static std::unordered_set<std::string_view> data_const_set = {
 8:     "__got", "__auth_got", "__auth_ptr", "__nl_symbol_ptr", "__const",
 9:     "__cfstring", "__mod_init_func", "__mod_term_func", "__objc_classlist",
10:     "__objc_nlclslist", "__objc_catlist", "__objc_nlcatlist", "__objc_protolist",
11:   };
12: 
13:   std::string_view seg = hdr.get_segname();
14:   std::string_view sect = hdr.get_sectname();
15: 
16:   if (seg == "__DATA" && data_const_set.contains(sect)) {
17:     seg = "__DATA_CONST";
18:   } else if (seg == "__TEXT" && sect == "__StaticInit") {
19:     sect = "__text";
20:   }
21: 
22:   return *OutputSection<E>::get_instance(ctx, seg, sect);
23: }
24: 
25: template <typename E>
26: InputSection<E>::InputSection(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file,
27:                               const MachSection<E> &hdr, u32 secidx)
28:   : file(file), hdr(hdr), secidx(secidx), osec(get_output_section(ctx, hdr)) {
29:   if (hdr.type != S_ZEROFILL && &file != ctx.internal_obj)
30:     contents = file.mf->get_contents().substr(hdr.offset, hdr.size);
31: }
32: 
33: template <typename E>
34: void InputSection<E>::parse_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
35:   // Parse mach-o relocations to fill `rels` vector
36:   rels = read_relocations(ctx, file, hdr);
37: 
38:   // Sort `rels` vector
39:   sort(rels, [](const Relocation<E> &a, const Relocation<E> &b) {
40:     return a.offset < b.offset;
41:   });
42: 
43:   // Find subsections this relocation section refers to
44:   auto begin = std::partition_point(file.subsections.begin(),
45:                                     file.subsections.end(),
46:                                     [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
47:     return subsec->input_addr < hdr.addr;
48:   });
49: 
50:   auto end = std::partition_point(begin, file.subsections.end(),
51:                                   [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
52:     return subsec->input_addr < hdr.addr + hdr.size;
53:   });
54: 
55:   // Assign each subsection a group of relocations
56:   i64 i = 0;
57:   for (auto it = begin; it < end; it++) {
58:     Subsection<E> &subsec = **it;
59:     subsec.rel_offset = i;
60: 
61:     u32 input_offset = subsec.input_addr - subsec.isec->hdr.addr;
62:     while (i < rels.size() && rels[i].offset < input_offset + subsec.input_size) {
63:       rels[i].offset -= input_offset;
64:       i++;
65:     }
66:     subsec.nrels = i - subsec.rel_offset;
67:   }
68: }
69: 
70: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
71: 
72: template class InputSection<E>;
73: 
74: } // namespace mold::macho

  9: namespace mold::macho {

 11: template <typename E>
 12: static void do_load_plugin(Context<E> &ctx) {
 13:   void *handle = dlopen(ctx.arg.lto_library.c_str(), RTLD_NOW | RTLD_GLOBAL);
 14:   if (!handle)
 15:     Fatal(ctx) << "could not open plugin file: " << dlerror();
 16: 
 17:   ctx.lto.dlopen_handle = handle;
 18: 
 19:   // I don't want to use a macro, but I couldn't find an easy way to
 20:   // write the same thing without a macro.
 21: #define DLSYM(x) ctx.lto.x = (decltype(ctx.lto.x))dlsym(handle, "lto_" #x)
 22:   DLSYM(get_version);
 23:   DLSYM(get_error_message);
 24:   DLSYM(module_is_object_file);
 25:   DLSYM(module_is_object_file_for_target);
 26:   DLSYM(module_has_objc_category);
 27:   DLSYM(module_is_object_file_in_memory);
 28:   DLSYM(module_is_object_file_in_memory_for_target);
 29:   DLSYM(module_create);
 30:   DLSYM(module_create_from_memory);
 31:   DLSYM(module_create_from_memory_with_path);
 32:   DLSYM(module_create_in_local_context);
 33:   DLSYM(module_create_in_codegen_context);
 34:   DLSYM(module_create_from_fd);
 35:   DLSYM(module_create_from_fd_at_offset);
 36:   DLSYM(module_dispose);
 37:   DLSYM(module_get_target_triple);
 38:   DLSYM(module_set_target_triple);
 39:   DLSYM(module_get_num_symbols);
 40:   DLSYM(module_get_symbol_name);
 41:   DLSYM(module_get_symbol_attribute);
 42:   DLSYM(module_get_linkeropts);
 43:   DLSYM(module_get_macho_cputype);
 44:   DLSYM(module_has_ctor_dtor);
 45:   DLSYM(codegen_set_diagnostic_handler);
 46:   DLSYM(codegen_create);
 47:   DLSYM(codegen_create_in_local_context);
 48:   DLSYM(codegen_dispose);
 49:   DLSYM(codegen_add_module);
 50:   DLSYM(codegen_set_module);
 51:   DLSYM(codegen_set_debug_model);
 52:   DLSYM(codegen_set_pic_model);
 53:   DLSYM(codegen_set_cpu);
 54:   DLSYM(codegen_set_assembler_path);
 55:   DLSYM(codegen_set_assembler_args);
 56:   DLSYM(codegen_add_must_preserve_symbol);
 57:   DLSYM(codegen_write_merged_modules);
 58:   DLSYM(codegen_compile);
 59:   DLSYM(codegen_compile_to_file);
 60:   DLSYM(codegen_optimize);
 61:   DLSYM(codegen_compile_optimized);
 62:   DLSYM(api_version);
 63:   DLSYM(set_debug_options);
 64:   DLSYM(codegen_debug_options);
 65:   DLSYM(codegen_debug_options_array);
 66:   DLSYM(initialize_disassembler);
 67:   DLSYM(codegen_set_should_internalize);
 68:   DLSYM(codegen_set_should_embed_uselists);
 69: #undef DLSYM
 70: }
 71: 
 72: template <typename E>
 73: void load_lto_plugin(Context<E> &ctx) {
 74:   std::call_once(ctx.lto_plugin_loaded, [&] { do_load_plugin(ctx); });
 75: }
 76: 
 77: template <typename E>
 78: void do_lto(Context<E> &ctx) {
 79:   LTOCodeGen *cg = ctx.lto.codegen_create();
 80: 
 81:   // Add bitcode files to CodeGen.
 82:   for (const std::string &opt : ctx.arg.mllvm)
 83:     ctx.lto.codegen_debug_options(cg, opt.c_str());
 84: 
 85:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
 86:     if (file->lto_module)
 87:       ctx.lto.codegen_add_module(cg, file->lto_module);
 88: 
 89:   // Mark symbols that have to be preserved. All symbols that are not
 90:   // marked here may be internalized and deleted as an extenrally-
 91:   // visible symbol.
 92:   if (ctx.output_type == MH_DYLIB || ctx.arg.export_dynamic) {
 93:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 94:       if (!file->lto_module) {
 95:         for (i64 i = 0; i < file->mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 96:           MachSym<E> &msym = file->mach_syms[i];
 97:           Symbol<E> &sym = *file->syms[i];
 98:           if (msym.is_undef() && !sym.file->is_dylib &&
 99:               ((ObjectFile<E> *)sym.file)->lto_module)
100:             ctx.lto.codegen_add_must_preserve_symbol(cg, sym.name.data());
101:         }
102:       }
103:     }
104: 
105:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
106:       if (file->lto_module)
107:         for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
108:           if (sym->file == file && sym->visibility != SCOPE_LOCAL)
109:             ctx.lto.codegen_add_must_preserve_symbol(cg, sym->name.data());
110:   }
111: 
112:   if (ctx.arg.entry->file)
113:     ctx.lto.codegen_add_must_preserve_symbol(cg, ctx.arg.entry->name.data());
114: 
115:   // Run the compiler backend to do LTO.
116:   size_t size;
117:   u8 *data = (u8 *)ctx.lto.codegen_compile(cg, &size);
118:   if (!data)
119:     Fatal(ctx) << "lto_codegen_compile failed: " << ctx.lto.get_error_message();
120: 
121:   if (!ctx.arg.object_path_lto.empty()) {
122:     FILE *out = fopen(ctx.arg.object_path_lto.c_str(), "w");
123:     if (!out)
124:       Fatal(ctx) << "-object_path_lto: cannot open " << ctx.arg.object_path_lto
125:                  << ": " << errno_string();
126:     fwrite(data, size, 1, out);
127:     fclose(out);
128:   }
129: 
130:   // Remove bitcode object files from ctx.objs.
131:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
132:     if (file->lto_module) {
133:       file->clear_symbols();
134:       file->is_alive = false;
135:     }
136:   }
137: 
138:   // Add a result of LTO as a new object file.
139:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = new MappedFile<Context<E>>;
140:   mf->name = "<LTO>";
141:   mf->data = data;
142:   mf->size = size;
143:   ctx.mf_pool.emplace_back(mf);
144: 
145:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = ObjectFile<E>::create(ctx, mf, "");
146:   obj->parse(ctx);
147:   obj->is_alive = true;
148:   obj->priority = 100; // regular file starts with priority 10,000
149:   obj->resolve_symbols(ctx);
150:   ctx.objs.push_back(obj);
151: }
152: 
153: #ifdef MOLD_ARM64
154: LTOPlugin::~LTOPlugin() {
155:   if (dlopen_handle)
156:     dlclose(dlopen_handle);
157: }
158: #endif
159: 
160: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
161: 
162: template void load_lto_plugin(Context<E> &);
163: template void do_lto(Context<E> &);
164: 
165: } // namespace mold::macho

  9: enum LTOSymbolAttributes {
 10:   LTO_SYMBOL_ALIGNMENT_MASK = 0x0000001F,
 11:   LTO_SYMBOL_PERMISSIONS_MASK = 0x000000E0,
 12:   LTO_SYMBOL_PERMISSIONS_CODE = 0x000000A0,
 13:   LTO_SYMBOL_PERMISSIONS_DATA = 0x000000C0,
 14:   LTO_SYMBOL_PERMISSIONS_RODATA = 0x00000080,
 15:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_MASK = 0x00000700,
 16:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_REGULAR = 0x00000100,
 17:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_TENTATIVE = 0x00000200,
 18:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_WEAK = 0x00000300,
 19:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_UNDEFINED = 0x00000400,
 20:   LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_WEAKUNDEF = 0x00000500,

 22:   LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_INTERNAL = 0x00000800,
 23:   LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_HIDDEN = 0x00001000,
 24:   LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_DEFAULT = 0x00001800,
 25:   LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_PROTECTED = 0x00002000,

125:   LTOCodeGen *(*codegen_create)();
126: 
127:   LTOCodeGen *(*codegen_create_in_local_context)();
128: 
129:   void (*codegen_dispose)(LTOCodeGen *cg);
130: 
131:   bool (*codegen_add_module)(LTOCodeGen *cg, LTOModule *mod);
132: 
133:   void (*codegen_set_module)(LTOCodeGen *cg, LTOModule *mod);
134: 
135:   bool (*codegen_set_debug_model)(LTOCodeGen *cg, LTODebugModel);
136: 
137:   bool (*codegen_set_pic_model)(LTOCodeGen *cg, LTOCodegenModel);
138: 
139:   void (*codegen_set_cpu)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *cpu);
140: 
141:   void (*codegen_set_assembler_path)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *path);
142: 
143:   void (*codegen_set_assembler_args)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char **args,
144:                                      int nargs);
145: 
146:   void (*codegen_add_must_preserve_symbol)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *symbol);
147: 
148:   bool (*codegen_write_merged_modules)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *path);
149: 
150:   const void *(*codegen_compile)(LTOCodeGen *cg, size_t *length);
151: 
152:   bool (*codegen_compile_to_file)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char **name);
153: 
154:   bool (*codegen_optimize)(LTOCodeGen *cg);
155: 
156:   const void *(*codegen_compile_optimized)(LTOCodeGen *cg, size_t *length);
157: 
158:   unsigned (*api_version)();
159: 
160:   void (*set_debug_options)(const char *const *options, int number);
161: 
162:   void (*codegen_debug_options)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *);
163: 
164:   void (*codegen_debug_options_array)(LTOCodeGen *cg, const char *const *,
165:                                       int number);
166: 
167:   void (*initialize_disassembler)();
168: 
169:   void (*codegen_set_should_internalize)(LTOCodeGen *cg,
170:                                          bool should_internalize);
171: 
172:   void (*codegen_set_should_embed_uselists)(LTOCodeGen *cg,
173:                                             bool should_embed_use_lists);
174: };

176: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: macho/mapfile.cc
================
 1: #include "mold.h"
 2: 
 3: #include <iomanip>
 4: #include <fstream>
 5: 
 6: namespace mold::macho {
 7: 
 8: struct Sym {
 9:   u64 addr;
10:   u32 size;
11:   u32 fileidx;
12:   std::string_view name;
13: };
14: 
15: template <typename E>
16: void print_map(Context<E> &ctx) {
17:   std::ofstream out(ctx.arg.map.c_str());
18:   if (!out.is_open())
19:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << ctx.arg.map << ": " << errno_string();
20: 
21:   out << "# Path: " << ctx.arg.output << "\n"
22:       << "# Arch: x86-64\n"
23:       << "# Object files:\n";
24: 
25:   std::vector<Sym> syms;
26: 
27:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size(); i++){
28:     ObjectFile<E> &file = *ctx.objs[i];
29: 
30:     if (file.is_alive) {
31:       out << "[" << std::setw(3) << i << "] " << file << "\n";
32:       for (Symbol<E> *sym : file.syms)
33:         if (sym && sym->file == &file)
34:           if (!sym->subsec || sym->subsec->is_alive)
35:             syms.push_back({sym->get_addr(ctx), 0, (u32)i, sym->name});
36:     }
37:   }
38: 
39:   sort(syms, [](const Sym &a, const Sym &b) {
40:     return a.addr < b.addr;
41:   });
42: 
43:   out << "# Sections:\n"
44:       << "# Address       Size            Segment Section\n";
45: 
46:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments) {
47:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks) {
48:       if (!chunk->is_hidden) {
49:         out << "0x" << std::hex
50:             << std::setw(8) << std::setfill('0') << chunk->hdr.addr
51:             << "     0x"
52:             << std::setw(8) << std::setfill('0') << chunk->hdr.size
53:             << "      " << std::setw(7) << std::left << std::setfill(' ')
54:             << chunk->hdr.get_segname()
55:             << " " << chunk->hdr.get_sectname() << "\n";
56:       }
57:     }
58:   }
59: 
60:   out << "# Symbols:\n"
61:       << "# Address       Size            File  Name\n";
62: 
63:   for (Sym &sym : syms) {
64:     out << "0x" << std::hex
65:         << std::setw(8) << std::setfill('0') << sym.addr
66:         << "     0x"
67:         << std::setw(8) << std::setfill('0') << 0
68:         << "      ["
69:         << std::setw(3) << std::right << std::setfill(' ') << std::dec
70:         << sym.fileidx
71:         << "] " << sym.name << "\n";
72:   }
73: }
74: 
75: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
76: 
77: template void print_map(Context<E> &);
78: 
79: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: macho/README.md
================
  1: # Mach-O linker overview
  2: 
  3: Mach-O is an executable and linkable file format. It was originally
  4: created for the Mach kernel-based system. Since NeXTSTEP is based on the
  5: Mach kernel and the modern macOS/iOS is a direct descendent of it, Mach-O
  6: is now universally used as an object file for the Apple systems.
  7: 
  8: In this document, I'll explain Mach-O by comparing it with ELF.
  9: 
 10: ## Sections and subsections
 11: 
 12: Section is not an atomic unit of copying in Mach-O. If a section has the
 13: `MH_SUBSECTIONS_VIA_SYMBOLS` flag, it can be split at locations where
 14: global symbols points to. Splitted section is called "subsection".
 15: 
 16: For example, assume your source file contains two function definitions,
 17: `foo` and `bar`. The compiled object file contains only one text section
 18: which consists of two subsections. One subsection starts at where `foo`
 19: refers to, and the other starts at where `bar` refers to.
 20: 
 21: In other words, Mach-O's section is not equivalent to ELF's section;
 22: Mach-O's subsection is.
 23: 
 24: ## Relocations
 25: 
 26: A Mach-O relocation can refer to either a symbol or a section, while an
 27: ELF relocation can refer to only a symbol.
 28: 
 29: ## Dynamic name resolution
 30: 
 31: In Mach-O, dynamic symbols are resolved with a tuple of (dylib's name,
 32: symbol name). That means dynamic symbols must be resolved by their
 33: corresponding dynamic libraries at runtime.
 34: 
 35: This is contrary to ELF in which dynamic symbols are resolved only by
 36: name. In ELF, dynamic symbols can be resolved by any dynamic library as
 37: long as their names match.
 38: 
 39: If a dylib in a special value `BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_FLAT_LOOKUP`, the symbol
 40: name is looked up from all dylibs at runtime. This gives Mach-O the
 41: semantics that are the same as the ELF's flat namespace. This special value
 42: is used in the following rare occasions:
 43: 
 44: 1. If a symbol name specified as an argument for `-U` is missing at
 45:    link-time, the symbol is promoted to a dynamic symbol so that it'll get
 46:    another chance to be resolved at load-time. Since we don't know the
 47:    library name for such symbol, we'll set `BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_FLAT_LOOKUP`
 48:    as a library identifier instead.
 49: 
 50: 2. If `-flat_namespace` is given, all dynamic symbols are exported without
 51:    specifying their corresponding library names. This option gives the
 52:    ELF semantics to Mach-O programs.
 53: 
 54: The other difference between Mach-O and ELF Is that Mach-O's dynamic
 55: symbols are stored in a trie so that symbols' common prefixes are shared
 56: in a file. In ELF, dynamic symbol strings are just a run of NUL-terminated
 57: strings. So, Mach-O's import table is more complicated but compact than
 58: ELF's table.
 59: 
 60: ## Lazy function symbol resolution
 61: 
 62: Imported function symbols are resolved in the same way as ELF but with
 63: slightly different file layout.
 64: 
 65: `__stubs` section contains PLT entries. It reads a function address from
 66: `__la_symbol_ptr` section and jump there.
 67: 
 68: `__la_symbol_ptr` entries are initialized to point to entries in
 69: `__stubs_helper` section. If a PLT entry is called for the first time, the
 70: control is transferred to its corresponding entry in `__stubs_helper`.
 71: It then calls `dyld_stub_binder` with appropriate arguments for symbol
 72: resolution.
 73: 
 74: There's no notion of "canonical PLT" in Mach-O because the compiler always
 75: emit code to load a function pointer value from GOT even for `-fno-PIC`.
 76: In other words, the compiler always assumes that the address of a function
 77: is not known at link-time if the address is used as a value.
 78: 
 79: ## Re-exported libraries
 80: 
 81: Mach-O dylibs can refer to other dylibs as "reexported libraries". If
 82: `libfoo.dylib` refers to `libbar.dylib` as a reexported library, all symbols
 83: defined by `libbar.dylib` become available as if they were defined by
 84: `libfoo.dylib`.
 85: 
 86: The purpose of reexporting is to give developers freedom to reorganize their
 87: libraries while keeping the programming interface the same. For example,
 88: macOS's `libSystem.dylib` provides basic functionalities for macOS apps.
 89: Apple may want to move some features out of `libSystem.dylib` to a new
 90: dylib. They can safely do it by referring to the new dylib as a reexported
 91: library of `libSystem.dylib`. If there's no reexporting feature, they can't
 92: do this without asking the library users to add a new `-l` line to their
 93: build files.
 94: 
 95: ## Thread-local variables
 96: 
 97: If a symbol is of thread-local variable (TLV), the symbol is defined in a
 98: `__thread_vars` section in an input file. A `__thread_vars` entry consists of
 99: a vector of the three word-size tuples that contains
100: 
101: 1. a function pointer to `tlv_get_addr`,
102: 2. an indirect pointer to the TLS block of the Mach-O image, and
103: 3. the offset within the TLS block
104: 
105: at runtime. You can obtain the address of a TLV by calling the function
106: pointer with the address of the tuple as an argument.
107: 
108: TLVs are always referenced by `ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_*` or `X86_64_RELOC_TLV`
109: relocations. For each TLV referenced by these relocations, the linker
110: creates an entry in the linker-synthesized `__thread_ptrs` section and let
111: it refer to the address of its corresponding `__thread_vars` entry.
112: 
113: TLVs are always referenced indirectly via `__thread_ptrs`. If a TLV is
114: defined locally, its `__thread_ptrs` entry refers to a `__thread_vars`
115: entry in the same file. Otherwise, the `__thread_ptr` entry refers to a
116: `__thread_vars` entry in other Mach-O file image. In other words,
117: `__thread_ptrs` is a GOT-like section for TLVs.
118: 
119: A TLS template image consists of `__thread_data` and `__thread_bss` sections.
120: `__thread_data` contains initial values for TLVs. `__thread_bss` specifies
121: only its size. These sections must be consecutive in the output file;
122: otherwise each per-thread block contains unrelated piece of data, resulting
123: in waste of memory.
124: 
125: ## Endianness and bitwidth
126: 
127: Since Mach-O is effectively used only by Apple, and all Apple systems are
128: little-endian nowadays, Mach-O is effectively little-endian only.
129: Likewise, it is 64-bit only since Apple has terminated 32-bit app support.
130: 
131: Apple Watch seems to be using an ILP32 ABI on ARM64, but I don't know the
132: details about it.

================
File: macho/thunks.cc
================
  1: #if MOLD_ARM64 || MOLD_ARM64_32
  2: 
  3: #include "mold.h"
  4: 
  5: #include <tbb/parallel_for.h>
  6: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
  7: 
  8: namespace mold::macho {
  9: 
 10: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
 11: 
 12: // We create a thunk no further than 100 MiB from any subsection.
 13: static constexpr i64 MAX_DISTANCE = 100 * 1024 * 1024;
 14: 
 15: // We create a thunk for each 10 MiB subsections.
 16: static constexpr i64 BATCH_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024;
 17: 
 18: // We assume that a single thunk group is smaller than 100 KiB.
 19: static constexpr i64 MAX_THUNK_SIZE = 102400;
 20: 
 21: static bool is_reachable(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
 22:                          Subsection<E> &subsec, Relocation<E> &rel) {
 23:   if (sym.has_stub()) {
 24:     // If it branches to PLT, the PLT must have already got an address.
 25:     if (ctx.stubs.hdr.addr == 0)
 26:       return false;
 27:   } else {
 28:     // We create thunks with a pessimistic assumption that all
 29:     // out-of-section relocations would be out-of-range.
 30:     if (!sym.subsec || &sym.subsec->isec->osec != &subsec.isec->osec)
 31:       return false;
 32: 
 33:     // Uninitialized subsections in the same output section are
 34:     // out-of-reach.
 35:     if (sym.subsec->output_offset == -1)
 36:       return false;
 37:   }
 38: 
 39:   // Compute a distance between the relocated place and the symbol
 40:   // and check if they are within reach.
 41:   i64 addr = sym.get_addr(ctx);
 42:   i64 pc = subsec.get_addr(ctx) + rel.offset;
 43:   i64 val = addr + rel.addend - pc;
 44:   return -(1 << 27) <= val && val < (1 << 27);
 45: }
 46: 
 47: static void reset_thunk(RangeExtensionThunk<E> &thunk) {
 48:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : thunk.symbols) {
 49:     sym->thunk_idx = -1;
 50:     sym->thunk_sym_idx = -1;
 51:     sym->flags &= (u8)~NEEDS_RANGE_EXTN_THUNK;
 52:   }
 53: }
 54: 
 55: // ARM64's call/jump instructions take 27 bits displacement, so they
 56: // can refer only up to ±128 MiB. If a branch target is further than
 57: // that, we need to let it branch to a linker-synthesized code
 58: // sequence that construct a full 32 bit address in a register and
 59: // jump there. That linker-synthesized code is called "thunk".
 60: template <>
 61: void create_range_extension_thunks(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec) {
 62:   std::span<Subsection<E> *> m = osec.members;
 63:   if (m.empty())
 64:     return;
 65: 
 66:   m[0]->output_offset = 0;
 67: 
 68:   // Initialize input sections with a dummy offset so that we can
 69:   // distinguish sections that have got an address with the one who
 70:   // haven't.
 71:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)1, (i64)m.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 72:     m[i]->output_offset = -1;
 73:   });
 74: 
 75:   // We create thunks from the beginning of the section to the end.
 76:   // We manage progress using four offsets which increase monotonically.
 77:   // The locations they point to are always A <= B <= C <= D.
 78:   i64 a = 0;
 79:   i64 b = 0;
 80:   i64 c = 0;
 81:   i64 d = 0;
 82:   i64 offset = 0;
 83:   i64 thunk_idx = 0;
 84: 
 85:   while (b < m.size()) {
 86:     // Move D foward as far as we can jump from B to D.
 87:     while (d < m.size() &&
 88:            align_to(offset, 1 << m[d]->p2align) + m[d]->input_size <
 89:            m[b]->output_offset + MAX_DISTANCE - MAX_THUNK_SIZE) {
 90:       offset = align_to(offset, 1 << m[d]->p2align);
 91:       m[d]->output_offset = offset;
 92:       offset += m[d]->input_size;
 93:       d++;
 94:     }
 95: 
 96:     // Move C forward so that C is apart from B by BATCH_SIZE.
 97:     c = b + 1;
 98:     while (c < m.size() &&
 99:            m[c]->output_offset + m[c]->input_size <
100:            m[b]->output_offset + BATCH_SIZE)
101:       c++;
102: 
103:     // Move A forward so that A is reachable from C.
104:     i64 c_offset = (c == m.size()) ? offset : m[c]->output_offset;
105:     while (a < m.size() && m[a]->output_offset + MAX_DISTANCE < c_offset)
106:       a++;
107: 
108:     // Erase references to out-of-range thunks.
109:     while (thunk_idx < osec.thunks.size() &&
110:            osec.thunks[thunk_idx]->offset < m[a]->output_offset)
111:       reset_thunk(*osec.thunks[thunk_idx++]);
112: 
113:     // Create a thunk for input sections between B and C and place it at D.
114:     offset = align_to(offset, RangeExtensionThunk<E>::ALIGNMENT);
115:     RangeExtensionThunk<E> *thunk =
116:       new RangeExtensionThunk<E>(osec, osec.thunks.size(), offset);
117:     osec.thunks.emplace_back(thunk);
118: 
119:     // Scan relocations between B and C to collect symbols that need thunks.
120:     tbb::parallel_for_each(m.begin() + b, m.begin() + c,
121:                            [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
122:       std::span<Relocation<E>> rels = subsec->get_rels();
123: 
124:       for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
125:         Relocation<E> &r = rels[i];
126:         if (!r.sym()->file || r.type != ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26)
127:           continue;
128: 
129:         // Skip if the destination is within reach.
130:         if (is_reachable(ctx, *r.sym(), *subsec, r))
131:           continue;
132: 
133:         // If the symbol is already in another thunk, reuse it.
134:         if (r.sym()->thunk_idx != -1) {
135:           r.thunk_idx = r.sym()->thunk_idx;
136:           r.thunk_sym_idx = r.sym()->thunk_sym_idx;
137:           continue;
138:         }
139: 
140:         // Otherwise, add the symbol to this thunk if it's not added already.
141:         r.thunk_idx = thunk->thunk_idx;
142:         r.thunk_sym_idx = -1;
143: 
144:         if (!(r.sym()->flags.fetch_or(NEEDS_RANGE_EXTN_THUNK) &
145:               NEEDS_RANGE_EXTN_THUNK)) {
146:           std::scoped_lock lock(thunk->mu);
147:           thunk->symbols.push_back(r.sym());
148:         }
149:       }
150:     });
151: 
152:     // Now that we know the number of symbols in the thunk, we can compute
153:     // its size.
154:     offset += thunk->size();
155: 
156:     // Sort symbols added to the thunk to make the output deterministic.
157:     sort(thunk->symbols, [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) {
158:       return std::tuple{a->file->priority, a->value} <
159:              std::tuple{b->file->priority, b->value};
160:     });
161: 
162:     // Assign offsets within the thunk to the symbols.
163:     for (i64 i = 0; Symbol<E> *sym : thunk->symbols) {
164:       sym->thunk_idx = thunk->thunk_idx;
165:       sym->thunk_sym_idx = i++;
166:     }
167: 
168:     // Scan relocations again to fix symbol offsets in the last thunk.
169:     tbb::parallel_for_each(m.begin() + b, m.begin() + c,
170:                            [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
171:       for (Relocation<E> &r : subsec->get_rels())
172:         if (r.thunk_idx == thunk->thunk_idx)
173:           r.thunk_sym_idx = r.sym()->thunk_sym_idx;
174:     });
175: 
176:     // Move B forward to point to the begining of the next group.
177:     b = c;
178:   }
179: 
180:   while (thunk_idx < osec.thunks.size())
181:     reset_thunk(*osec.thunks[thunk_idx++]);
182: 
183:   osec.hdr.size = offset;
184: }
185: 
186: } // namespace mold::macho
187: 
188: #endif

================
File: macho/yaml.cc
================
  1: // On macOS, a .tbd file can be used instead of a .dylib to link
  2: // against a dynamic library. A .tbd file is a text file in the YAML
  3: // format, so the linker has to be able to parse a YAML file. This
  4: // file implements a YAML parser.
  5: //
  6: // We implemented a YAML parser ourselves instead of using an external
  7: // library. We didn't want to add an dependency to an external library
  8: // just to read .tdb files.
  9: //
 10: // YAML as a format is very complicated. For example, the official
 11: // YAML specification allows embedding a JSON string into YAML; so it
 12: // is strictly larger than JSON. It has surprisingly many features
 13: // that most users are not aware of. Fortunately, we have to support
 14: // only a small portion of the spec to read a .tbd file.
 15: 
 16: #include "mold.h"
 17: 
 18: #include <optional>
 19: 
 20: namespace mold::macho {
 21: 
 22: enum { STRING = 1, INDENT, DEDENT, END };
 23: 
 24: struct Token {
 25:   u8 kind = 0;
 26:   std::string_view str;
 27: };
 28: 
 29: class YamlParser {
 30: public:
 31:   YamlParser(std::string_view input) : input(input) {}
 32: 
 33:   std::variant<std::vector<YamlNode>, YamlError> parse();
 34: 
 35: private:
 36:   std::optional<YamlError> tokenize();
 37: 
 38:   void tokenize_bare_string(std::string_view &str);
 39: 
 40:   std::optional<YamlError> tokenize_list(std::string_view &str);
 41:   std::optional<YamlError> tokenize_string(std::string_view &str, char end);
 42: 
 43:   std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> parse_element(std::span<Token> &tok);
 44:   std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> parse_list(std::span<Token> &tok);
 45:   std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> parse_map(std::span<Token> &tok);
 46:   std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> parse_flow_element(std::span<Token> &tok);
 47:   std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> parse_flow_list(std::span<Token> &tok);
 48: 
 49:   std::string_view input;
 50:   std::vector<Token> tokens;
 51: };
 52: 
 53: // A tokenizer for YAML. YAML represents blocks by indentation. This
 54: // tokenizer inserts INDENT and DEDENT special tokens before and after
 55: // each indented text block.
 56: std::optional<YamlError> YamlParser::tokenize() {
 57:   std::vector<i64> indents = {0};
 58: 
 59:   auto indent = [&](std::string_view str, i64 depth) {
 60:     tokens.push_back({INDENT, str});
 61:     indents.push_back(depth);
 62:   };
 63: 
 64:   auto dedent = [&](std::string_view str) {
 65:     assert(indents.size() > 1);
 66:     tokens.push_back({DEDENT, str});
 67:     indents.pop_back();
 68:   };
 69: 
 70:   auto skip_line = [](std::string_view &str) {
 71:     size_t pos = str.find('\n');
 72:     if (pos == str.npos)
 73:       str = str.substr(str.size());
 74:     else
 75:       str = str.substr(pos + 1);
 76:   };
 77: 
 78:   auto tokenize_line = [&](std::string_view &str) -> std::optional<YamlError> {
 79:     const char *start = str.data();
 80: 
 81:     if (str.starts_with("---")) {
 82:       while (indents.size() > 1)
 83:         dedent(str);
 84:       tokens.push_back({END, str.substr(0, 3)});
 85:       skip_line(str);
 86:       return {};
 87:     }
 88: 
 89:     if (str.starts_with("...")) {
 90:       while (indents.size() > 1)
 91:         dedent(str);
 92:       tokens.push_back({END, str.substr(0, 3)});
 93:       str = str.substr(str.size());
 94:       return {};
 95:     }
 96: 
 97:     size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(" \t");
 98:     if (pos == str.npos || str[pos] == '#' || str[pos] == '\n') {
 99:       skip_line(str);
100:       return {};
101:     }
102: 
103:     if (indents.back() != pos) {
104:       if (indents.back() < pos) {
105:         indent(str, pos);
106:       } else {
107:         while (indents.back() != pos) {
108:           if (pos < indents.back())
109:             dedent(str);
110:           else
111:             return YamlError{"bad indentation", start - input.data()};
112:         }
113:       }
114:     }
115: 
116:     str = str.substr(pos);
117: 
118:     while (!str.empty()) {
119:       if (str[0] == '\n') {
120:         str = str.substr(1);
121:         return {};
122:       }
123: 
124:       if (str.starts_with("- ")) {
125:         tokens.push_back({'-', str.substr(0, 1)});
126: 
127:         size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(" \t", 1);
128:         if (pos == str.npos || str[pos] == '\n') {
129:           skip_line(str);
130:           return {};
131:         }
132: 
133:         str = str.substr(pos);
134:         indent(str, str.data() - start);
135:         continue;
136:       }
137: 
138:       if (str.starts_with('['))
139:         return tokenize_list(str);
140: 
141:       if (str.starts_with('\'')) {
142:         if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize_string(str, '\''))
143:           return err;
144:         continue;
145:       }
146: 
147:       if (str.starts_with('"')) {
148:         if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize_string(str, '"'))
149:           return err;
150:         continue;
151:       }
152: 
153:       if (str.starts_with('#')) {
154:         skip_line(str);
155:         return {};
156:       }
157: 
158:       if (str.starts_with(':')) {
159:         tokens.push_back({':', str.substr(0, 1)});
160: 
161:         size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(" \t", 1);
162:         if (pos == str.npos || str[pos] == '\n') {
163:           skip_line(str);
164:           return {};
165:         }
166: 
167:         str = str.substr(pos);
168:         continue;
169:       }
170: 
171:       tokenize_bare_string(str);
172:     }
173:     return {};
174:   };
175: 
176:   std::string_view str = input;
177:   while (!str.empty())
178:     if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize_line(str))
179:       return err;
180:   return {};
181: }
182: 
183: std::optional<YamlError> YamlParser::tokenize_list(std::string_view &str) {
184:   const char *start = str.data();
185: 
186:   tokens.push_back({'[', str.substr(0, 1)});
187:   str = str.substr(1);
188: 
189:   while (!str.empty() && str[0] != ']') {
190:     if (size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(" \t\n"); pos) {
191:       str = str.substr(pos);
192:       continue;
193:     }
194: 
195:     if (str.starts_with('\'')) {
196:       if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize_string(str, '\''))
197:         return err;
198:       continue;
199:     }
200: 
201:     if (str.starts_with('"')) {
202:       if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize_string(str, '"'))
203:         return err;
204:       continue;
205:     }
206: 
207:     if (str.starts_with(',')) {
208:       tokens.push_back({',', str.substr(0, 1)});
209:       str = str.substr(1);
210:       continue;
211:     }
212: 
213:     tokenize_bare_string(str);
214:   }
215: 
216:   if (str.empty())
217:     return YamlError{"unclosed list", start - input.data()};
218: 
219:   const char *bracket = str.data();
220:   tokens.push_back({']', str.substr(0, 1)});
221:   str = str.substr(1);
222: 
223:   while (!str.empty() && (str[0] == ' ' || str[0] == '\t'))
224:     str = str.substr(1);
225:   if (str.empty() || str[0] != '\n')
226:     return YamlError{"no newline after ']'", bracket - input.data()};
227:   str = str.substr(1);
228:   return {};
229: }
230: 
231: std::optional<YamlError>
232: YamlParser::tokenize_string(std::string_view &str, char end) {
233:   const char *start = str.data();
234:   size_t pos = str.find(end, 1);
235:   if (pos == str.npos)
236:     return YamlError{"unterminated string literal", start - input.data()};
237: 
238:   tokens.push_back({STRING, str.substr(1, pos - 1)});
239:   str = str.substr(pos + 1);
240:   return {};
241: }
242: 
243: void
244: YamlParser::tokenize_bare_string(std::string_view &str) {
245:   size_t pos = str.find_first_not_of(
246:     "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789_-/.");
247:   if (pos == str.npos)
248:     pos = str.size();
249:   tokens.push_back({STRING, str.substr(0, pos)});
250:   str = str.substr(pos);
251: }
252: 
253: std::variant<std::vector<YamlNode>, YamlError> YamlParser::parse() {
254:   if (std::optional<YamlError> err = tokenize())
255:     return *err;
256: 
257:   std::span<Token> tok(tokens);
258:   std::vector<YamlNode> vec;
259: 
260:   while (!tok.empty()) {
261:     if (tok[0].kind == END) {
262:       tok = tok.subspan(1);
263:       continue;
264:     }
265: 
266:     std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> elem = parse_element(tok);
267:     if (YamlError *err = std::get_if<YamlError>(&elem))
268:       return *err;
269:     vec.push_back(std::get<YamlNode>(elem));
270: 
271:     if (tok[0].kind != END)
272:       return YamlError{"stray token", tok[0].str.data() - input.data()};
273:   }
274:   return vec;
275: }
276: 
277: std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError>
278: YamlParser::parse_element(std::span<Token> &tok) {
279:   if (tok[0].kind == INDENT) {
280:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
281: 
282:     std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> elem = parse_element(tok);
283:     assert(tok[0].kind == DEDENT);
284:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
285:     return elem;
286:   }
287: 
288:   if (tok[0].kind == '-')
289:     return parse_list(tok);
290: 
291:   if (tok.size() > 2 && tok[0].kind == STRING && tok[1].kind == ':')
292:     return parse_map(tok);
293: 
294:   return parse_flow_element(tok);
295: }
296: 
297: std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError>
298: YamlParser::parse_list(std::span<Token> &tok) {
299:   std::vector<YamlNode> vec;
300: 
301:   while (tok[0].kind != END && tok[0].kind != DEDENT) {
302:     if (tok[0].kind != '-')
303:       return YamlError{"list element expected", tok[0].str.data() - input.data()};
304:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
305: 
306:     std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> elem = parse_element(tok);
307:     if (YamlError *err = std::get_if<YamlError>(&elem))
308:       return *err;
309:     vec.push_back(std::get<YamlNode>(elem));
310:   }
311:   return YamlNode{vec};
312: }
313: 
314: std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError>
315: YamlParser::parse_map(std::span<Token> &tok) {
316:   std::map<std::string_view, YamlNode> map;
317: 
318:   while (tok[0].kind != END && tok[0].kind != DEDENT) {
319:     if (tok.size() < 2 || tok[0].kind != STRING || tok[1].kind != ':')
320:       return YamlError{"map key expected", tok[0].str.data() - input.data()};
321: 
322:     std::string_view key = tok[0].str;
323:     tok = tok.subspan(2);
324: 
325:     std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> elem = parse_element(tok);
326:     if (YamlError *err = std::get_if<YamlError>(&elem))
327:       return *err;
328:     map[key] = std::get<YamlNode>(elem);
329:   }
330:   return YamlNode{map};
331: }
332: 
333: std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError>
334: YamlParser::parse_flow_element(std::span<Token> &tok) {
335:   if (tok[0].kind == '[') {
336:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
337:     return parse_flow_list(tok);
338:   }
339: 
340:   if (tok[0].kind != STRING)
341:     return YamlError{"scalar expected", tok[0].str.data() - input.data()};
342: 
343:   std::string_view val = tok[0].str;
344:   tok = tok.subspan(1);
345:   return YamlNode{val};
346: }
347: 
348: std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError>
349: YamlParser::parse_flow_list(std::span<Token> &tok) {
350:   std::vector<YamlNode> vec;
351:   const char *start = tok[0].str.data();
352: 
353:   while (tok[0].kind != ']' && tok[0].kind != END) {
354:     std::variant<YamlNode, YamlError> elem = parse_flow_element(tok);
355:     if (YamlError *err = std::get_if<YamlError>(&elem))
356:       return *err;
357:     vec.push_back(std::get<YamlNode>(elem));
358: 
359:     if (tok[0].kind == ']')
360:       break;
361:     if (tok[0].kind != ',')
362:       return YamlError{"comma expected", tok[0].str.data() - input.data()};
363:     tok = tok.subspan(1);
364:   }
365: 
366:   if (tok[0].kind == END)
367:     return YamlError{"unterminated flow list", start - input.data()};
368: 
369:   tok = tok.subspan(1);
370:   return YamlNode{vec};
371: }
372: 
373: std::variant<std::vector<YamlNode>, YamlError>
374: parse_yaml(std::string_view str) {
375:   return YamlParser(str).parse();
376: }
377: 
378: } // namespace mold::macho
