================
File: elf/main.cc
================
  1: #include "mold.h"
  2: #include "../common/archive-file.h"
  3: #include "../common/cmdline.h"
  4: #include "../common/output-file.h"
  5: 
  6: #include <cstring>
  7: #include <functional>
  8: #include <iomanip>
  9: #include <map>
 10: #include <regex>
 11: #include <signal.h>
 12: #include <sys/stat.h>
 13: #include <sys/types.h>
 14: #include <tbb/global_control.h>
 15: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 16: #include <unordered_set>
 17: 
 18: #ifdef _WIN32
 19: # include <direct.h>
 20: # define _chdir chdir
 21: #else
 22: # include <unistd.h>
 23: #endif
 24: 
 25: namespace mold::elf {
 26: 
 27: // Read the beginning of a given file and returns its machine type
 28: // (e.g. EM_X86_64 or EM_386).
 29: template <typename E>
 30: std::string_view get_machine_type(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
 31:   auto get_elf_type = [&](u8 *buf) -> std::string_view {
 32:     bool is_le = (((ElfEhdr<I386> *)buf)->e_ident[EI_DATA] == ELFDATA2LSB);
 33:     bool is_64;
 34:     u32 e_machine;
 35: 
 36:     if (is_le) {
 37:       auto &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<I386> *)buf;
 38:       is_64 = (ehdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] == ELFCLASS64);
 39:       e_machine = ehdr.e_machine;
 40:     } else {
 41:       auto &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<M68K> *)buf;
 42:       is_64 = (ehdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] == ELFCLASS64);
 43:       e_machine = ehdr.e_machine;
 44:     }
 45: 
 46:     switch (e_machine) {
 47:     case EM_386:
 48:       return I386::target_name;
 49:     case EM_X86_64:
 50:       return X86_64::target_name;
 51:     case EM_ARM:
 52:       return ARM32::target_name;
 53:     case EM_AARCH64:
 54:       return ARM64::target_name;
 55:     case EM_RISCV:
 56:       if (is_le)
 57:         return is_64 ? RV64LE::target_name : RV32LE::target_name;
 58:       return is_64 ? RV64BE::target_name : RV32BE::target_name;
 59:     case EM_PPC:
 60:       return PPC32::target_name;
 61:     case EM_PPC64:
 62:       return is_le ? PPC64V2::target_name : PPC64V1::target_name;
 63:     case EM_S390X:
 64:       return S390X::target_name;
 65:     case EM_SPARC64:
 66:       return SPARC64::target_name;
 67:     case EM_68K:
 68:       return M68K::target_name;
 69:     case EM_SH:
 70:       return SH4::target_name;
 71:     case EM_ALPHA:
 72:       return ALPHA::target_name;
 73:     default:
 74:       return "";
 75:     }
 76:   };
 77: 
 78:   switch (get_file_type(ctx, mf)) {
 79:   case FileType::ELF_OBJ:
 80:   case FileType::ELF_DSO:
 81:   case FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ:
 82:     return get_elf_type(mf->data);
 83:   case FileType::AR:
 84:     for (MappedFile<Context<E>> *child : read_fat_archive_members(ctx, mf))
 85:       if (get_file_type(ctx, child) == FileType::ELF_OBJ)
 86:         return get_elf_type(child->data);
 87:     return "";
 88:   case FileType::THIN_AR:
 89:     for (MappedFile<Context<E>> *child : read_thin_archive_members(ctx, mf))
 90:       if (get_file_type(ctx, child) == FileType::ELF_OBJ)
 91:         return get_elf_type(child->data);
 92:     return "";
 93:   case FileType::TEXT:
 94:     return get_script_output_type(ctx, mf);
 95:   default:
 96:     return "";
 97:   }
 98: }
 99: 
100: template <typename E>
101: static void
102: check_file_compatibility(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
103:   std::string_view target = get_machine_type(ctx, mf);
104:   if (target != ctx.arg.emulation)
105:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": incompatible file type: "
106:                << ctx.arg.emulation << " is expected but got " << target;
107: }
108: 
109: template <typename E>
110: static ObjectFile<E> *new_object_file(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
111:                                       std::string archive_name) {
112:   static Counter count("parsed_objs");
113:   count++;
114: 
115:   check_file_compatibility(ctx, mf);
116: 
117:   bool in_lib = ctx.in_lib || (!archive_name.empty() && !ctx.whole_archive);
118:   ObjectFile<E> *file = ObjectFile<E>::create(ctx, mf, archive_name, in_lib);
119:   file->priority = ctx.file_priority++;
120:   ctx.tg.run([file, &ctx] { file->parse(ctx); });
121:   if (ctx.arg.trace)
122:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace: " << *file;
123:   return file;
124: }
125: 
126: template <typename E>
127: static ObjectFile<E> *new_lto_obj(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
128:                                   std::string archive_name) {
129:   static Counter count("parsed_lto_objs");
130:   count++;
131: 
132:   if (ctx.arg.ignore_ir_file.count(mf->get_identifier()))
133:     return nullptr;
134: 
135:   ObjectFile<E> *file = read_lto_object(ctx, mf);
136:   file->priority = ctx.file_priority++;
137:   file->archive_name = archive_name;
138:   file->is_in_lib = ctx.in_lib || (!archive_name.empty() && !ctx.whole_archive);
139:   file->is_alive = !file->is_in_lib;
140:   ctx.has_lto_object = true;
141:   if (ctx.arg.trace)
142:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace: " << *file;
143:   return file;
144: }
145: 
146: template <typename E>
147: static SharedFile<E> *
148: new_shared_file(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
149:   check_file_compatibility(ctx, mf);
150: 
151:   SharedFile<E> *file = SharedFile<E>::create(ctx, mf);
152:   file->priority = ctx.file_priority++;
153:   ctx.tg.run([file, &ctx] { file->parse(ctx); });
154:   if (ctx.arg.trace)
155:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace: " << *file;
156:   return file;
157: }
158: 
159: template <typename E>
160: void read_file(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
161:   if (ctx.visited.contains(mf->name))
162:     return;
163: 
164:   switch (get_file_type(ctx, mf)) {
165:   case FileType::ELF_OBJ:
166:     ctx.objs.push_back(new_object_file(ctx, mf, ""));
167:     return;
168:   case FileType::ELF_DSO:
169:     ctx.dsos.push_back(new_shared_file(ctx, mf));
170:     ctx.visited.insert(mf->name);
171:     return;
172:   case FileType::AR:
173:   case FileType::THIN_AR:
174:     for (MappedFile<Context<E>> *child : read_archive_members(ctx, mf)) {
175:       switch (get_file_type(ctx, child)) {
176:       case FileType::ELF_OBJ:
177:         ctx.objs.push_back(new_object_file(ctx, child, mf->name));
178:         break;
179:       case FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ:
180:       case FileType::LLVM_BITCODE:
181:         if (ObjectFile<E> *file = new_lto_obj(ctx, child, mf->name))
182:           ctx.objs.push_back(file);
183:         break;
184:       case FileType::ELF_DSO:
185:         Warn(ctx) << mf->name << "(" << child->name
186:                   << "): shared object file in an archive is ignored";
187:         break;
188:       default:
189:         break;
190:       }
191:     }
192:     ctx.visited.insert(mf->name);
193:     return;
194:   case FileType::TEXT:
195:     parse_linker_script(ctx, mf);
196:     return;
197:   case FileType::GCC_LTO_OBJ:
198:   case FileType::LLVM_BITCODE:
199:     if (ObjectFile<E> *file = new_lto_obj(ctx, mf, ""))
200:       ctx.objs.push_back(file);
201:     return;
202:   default:
203:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": unknown file type";
204:   }
205: }
206: 
207: template <typename E>
208: static std::string_view
209: deduce_machine_type(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string> args) {
210:   for (std::string_view arg : args)
211:     if (!arg.starts_with('-'))
212:       if (auto *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, std::string(arg)))
213:         if (std::string_view target = get_machine_type(ctx, mf);
214:             !target.empty())
215:           return target;
216:   Fatal(ctx) << "-m option is missing";
217: }
218: 
219: template <typename E>
220: MappedFile<Context<E>> *open_library(Context<E> &ctx, std::string path) {
221:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path);
222:   if (!mf)
223:     return nullptr;
224: 
225:   std::string_view target = get_machine_type(ctx, mf);
226:   if (target.empty() || target == E::target_name)
227:     return mf;
228:   Warn(ctx) << path << ": skipping incompatible file " << target
229:             << " " << (int)E::e_machine;
230:   return nullptr;
231: }
232: 
233: template <typename E>
234: MappedFile<Context<E>> *find_library(Context<E> &ctx, std::string name) {
235:   if (name.starts_with(':')) {
236:     for (std::string_view dir : ctx.arg.library_paths) {
237:       std::string path = std::string(dir) + "/" + name.substr(1);
238:       if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = open_library(ctx, path))
239:         return mf;
240:     }
241:     Fatal(ctx) << "library not found: " << name;
242:   }
243: 
244:   for (std::string_view dir : ctx.arg.library_paths) {
245:     std::string stem = std::string(dir) + "/lib" + name;
246:     if (!ctx.is_static)
247:       if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = open_library(ctx, stem + ".so"))
248:         return mf;
249:     if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = open_library(ctx, stem + ".a"))
250:       return mf;
251:   }
252:   Fatal(ctx) << "library not found: " << name;
253: }
254: 
255: template <typename E>
256: MappedFile<Context<E>> *find_from_search_paths(Context<E> &ctx, std::string name) {
257:   if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, name))
258:     return mf;
259: 
260:   for (std::string_view dir : ctx.arg.library_paths)
261:     if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
262:         MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, std::string(dir) + "/" + name))
263:       return mf;
264:   return nullptr;
265: }
266: 
267: template <typename E>
268: static void read_input_files(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string> args) {
269:   Timer t(ctx, "read_input_files");
270: 
271:   std::vector<std::tuple<bool, bool, bool, bool>> state;
272:   ctx.is_static = ctx.arg.is_static;
273: 
274:   while (!args.empty()) {
275:     std::string_view arg = args[0];
276:     args = args.subspan(1);
277: 
278:     if (arg == "--as-needed") {
279:       ctx.as_needed = true;
280:     } else if (arg == "--no-as-needed") {
281:       ctx.as_needed = false;
282:     } else if (arg == "--whole-archive") {
283:       ctx.whole_archive = true;
284:     } else if (arg == "--no-whole-archive") {
285:       ctx.whole_archive = false;
286:     } else if (arg == "--Bstatic") {
287:       ctx.is_static = true;
288:     } else if (arg == "--Bdynamic") {
289:       ctx.is_static = false;
290:     } else if (arg == "--start-lib") {
291:       ctx.in_lib = true;
292:     } else if (arg == "--end-lib") {
293:       ctx.in_lib = false;
294:     } else if (remove_prefix(arg, "--version-script=")) {
295:       MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_from_search_paths(ctx, std::string(arg));
296:       if (!mf)
297:         Fatal(ctx) << "--version-script: file not found: " << arg;
298:       parse_version_script(ctx, mf);
299:     } else if (remove_prefix(arg, "--dynamic-list=")) {
300:       MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_from_search_paths(ctx, std::string(arg));
301:       if (!mf)
302:         Fatal(ctx) << "--dynamic-list: file not found: " << arg;
303:       parse_dynamic_list(ctx, mf);
304:     } else if (remove_prefix(arg, "--export-dynamic-symbol=")) {
305:       if (arg == "*")
306:         ctx.default_version = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
307:       else
308:         ctx.version_patterns.push_back({arg, "--export-dynamic-symbol",
309:                                         "global", VER_NDX_GLOBAL, false});
310:     } else if (remove_prefix(arg, "--export-dynamic-symbol-list=")) {
311:       MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_from_search_paths(ctx, std::string(arg));
312:       if (!mf)
313:         Fatal(ctx) << "--export-dynamic-symbol-list: file not found: " << arg;
314:       parse_dynamic_list(ctx, mf);
315:     } else if (arg == "--push-state") {
316:       state.push_back({ctx.as_needed, ctx.whole_archive, ctx.is_static,
317:                        ctx.in_lib});
318:     } else if (arg == "--pop-state") {
319:       if (state.empty())
320:         Fatal(ctx) << "no state pushed before popping";
321:       std::tie(ctx.as_needed, ctx.whole_archive, ctx.is_static, ctx.in_lib) =
322:         state.back();
323:       state.pop_back();
324:     } else if (remove_prefix(arg, "-l")) {
325:       MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_library(ctx, std::string(arg));
326:       mf->given_fullpath = false;
327:       read_file(ctx, mf);
328:     } else {
329:       read_file(ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, std::string(arg)));
330:     }
331:   }
332: 
333:   if (ctx.objs.empty())
334:     Fatal(ctx) << "no input files";
335: 
336:   ctx.tg.wait();
337: }
338: 
339: // Since elf_main is a template, we can't run it without a type parameter.
340: // We speculatively run elf_main with X86_64, and if the speculation was
341: // wrong, re-run it with an actual machine type.
342: template <typename E>
343: static int redo_main(int argc, char **argv, std::string_view target) {
344:   if (target == I386::target_name)
345:     return elf_main<I386>(argc, argv);
346:   if (target == ARM64::target_name)
347:     return elf_main<ARM64>(argc, argv);
348:   if (target == ARM32::target_name)
349:     return elf_main<ARM32>(argc, argv);
350:   if (target == RV64LE::target_name)
351:     return elf_main<RV64LE>(argc, argv);
352:   if (target == RV64BE::target_name)
353:     return elf_main<RV64BE>(argc, argv);
354:   if (target == RV32LE::target_name)
355:     return elf_main<RV32LE>(argc, argv);
356:   if (target == RV32BE::target_name)
357:     return elf_main<RV32BE>(argc, argv);
358:   if (target == PPC32::target_name)
359:     return elf_main<PPC32>(argc, argv);
360:   if (target == PPC64V1::target_name)
361:     return elf_main<PPC64V1>(argc, argv);
362:   if (target == PPC64V2::target_name)
363:     return elf_main<PPC64V2>(argc, argv);
364:   if (target == S390X::target_name)
365:     return elf_main<S390X>(argc, argv);
366:   if (target == SPARC64::target_name)
367:     return elf_main<SPARC64>(argc, argv);
368:   if (target == M68K::target_name)
369:     return elf_main<M68K>(argc, argv);
370:   if (target == SH4::target_name)
371:     return elf_main<SH4>(argc, argv);
372:   if (target == ALPHA::target_name)
373:     return elf_main<ALPHA>(argc, argv);
374:   unreachable();
375: }
376: 
377: template <typename E>
378: int elf_main(int argc, char **argv) {
379:   Context<E> ctx;
380: 
381:   // Process -run option first. process_run_subcommand() does not return.
382:   if (argc >= 2 && (argv[1] == "-run"sv || argv[1] == "--run"sv)) {
383: #if defined(_WIN32) || defined(__APPLE__)
384:     Fatal(ctx) << "-run is supported only on Unix";
385: #endif
386:     process_run_subcommand(ctx, argc, argv);
387:   }
388: 
389:   // Parse non-positional command line options
390:   ctx.cmdline_args = expand_response_files(ctx, argv);
391:   std::vector<std::string> file_args = parse_nonpositional_args(ctx);
392: 
393:   // If no -m option is given, deduce it from input files.
394:   if (ctx.arg.emulation.empty())
395:     ctx.arg.emulation = deduce_machine_type(ctx, file_args);
396: 
397:   // Redo if -m is not x86-64.
398:   if constexpr (is_x86_64<E>)
399:     if (ctx.arg.emulation != X86_64::target_name)
400:       return redo_main<E>(argc, argv, ctx.arg.emulation);
401: 
402:   Timer t_all(ctx, "all");
403: 
404:   install_signal_handler();
405: 
406:   if (!ctx.arg.directory.empty())
407:     if (chdir(ctx.arg.directory.c_str()) == -1)
408:       Fatal(ctx) << "chdir failed: " << ctx.arg.directory
409:                  << ": " << errno_string();
410: 
411:   // Fork a subprocess unless --no-fork is given.
412:   std::function<void()> on_complete;
413: 
414: #if !defined(_WIN32) && !defined(__APPLE__)
415:   if (ctx.arg.fork)
416:     on_complete = fork_child();
417: #endif
418: 
419:   tbb::global_control tbb_cont(tbb::global_control::max_allowed_parallelism,
420:                                ctx.arg.thread_count);
421: 
422:   // Handle --wrap options if any.
423:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.wrap)
424:     get_symbol(ctx, name)->is_wrapped = true;
425: 
426:   // Handle --retain-symbols-file options if any.
427:   if (ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file)
428:     for (std::string_view name : *ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file)
429:       get_symbol(ctx, name)->write_to_symtab = true;
430: 
431:   for (std::string_view arg : ctx.arg.trace_symbol)
432:     get_symbol(ctx, arg)->is_traced = true;
433: 
434:   // Parse input files
435:   read_input_files(ctx, file_args);
436: 
437:   // Uniquify shared object files by soname
438:   {
439:     std::unordered_set<std::string_view> seen;
440:     std::erase_if(ctx.dsos, [&](SharedFile<E> *file) {
441:       return !seen.insert(file->soname).second;
442:     });
443:   }
444: 
445:   Timer t_total(ctx, "total");
446:   Timer t_before_copy(ctx, "before_copy");
447: 
448:   // Apply -exclude-libs
449:   apply_exclude_libs(ctx);
450: 
451:   // Create a dummy file containing linker-synthesized symbols.
452:   if (!ctx.arg.relocatable)
453:     create_internal_file(ctx);
454: 
455:   // resolve_symbols is 4 things in 1 phase:
456:   //
457:   // - Determine the set of object files to extract from archives.
458:   // - Remove redundant COMDAT sections (e.g. duplicate inline functions).
459:   // - Finally, the actual symbol resolution.
460:   // - LTO, which requires preliminary symbol resolution before running
461:   //   and a follow-up re-resolution after the LTO objects are emitted.
462:   //
463:   // These passes have complex interactions, and unfortunately has to be
464:   // put together in a single phase.
465:   resolve_symbols(ctx);
466: 
467:   // "Kill" .eh_frame input sections after symbol resolution.
468:   kill_eh_frame_sections(ctx);
469: 
470:   // Resolve mergeable section pieces to merge them.
471:   resolve_section_pieces(ctx);
472: 
473:   // Handle --relocatable. Since the linker's behavior is quite different
474:   // from the normal one when the option is given, the logic is implemented
475:   // to a separate file.
476:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable) {
477:     combine_objects(ctx);
478:     return 0;
479:   }
480: 
481:   // Create .bss sections for common symbols.
482:   convert_common_symbols(ctx);
483: 
484:   // Apply version scripts.
485:   apply_version_script(ctx);
486: 
487:   // Parse symbol version suffixes (e.g. "foo@ver1").
488:   parse_symbol_version(ctx);
489: 
490:   // Set is_imported and is_exported bits for each symbol.
491:   compute_import_export(ctx);
492: 
493:   // Read address-significant section information.
494:   if (ctx.arg.icf && !ctx.arg.icf_all)
495:     mark_addrsig(ctx);
496: 
497:   // Garbage-collect unreachable sections.
498:   if (ctx.arg.gc_sections)
499:     gc_sections(ctx);
500: 
501:   // Merge identical read-only sections.
502:   if (ctx.arg.icf)
503:     icf_sections(ctx);
504: 
505:   // Compute sizes of sections containing mergeable strings.
506:   compute_merged_section_sizes(ctx);
507: 
508:   // Create linker-synthesized sections such as .got or .plt.
509:   create_synthetic_sections(ctx);
510: 
511:   // Make sure that there's no duplicate symbol
512:   if (!ctx.arg.allow_multiple_definition)
513:     check_duplicate_symbols(ctx);
514: 
515:   // Warn if symbols with different types are defined under the same name.
516:   check_symbol_types(ctx);
517: 
518:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
519:     ppc64v1_rewrite_opd(ctx);
520: 
521:   // Bin input sections into output sections.
522:   create_output_sections(ctx);
523: 
524:   // Add synthetic symbols such as __ehdr_start or __end.
525:   add_synthetic_symbols(ctx);
526: 
527:   // Beyond this point, no new files will be added to ctx.objs
528:   // or ctx.dsos.
529: 
530:   // Handle `-z cet-report`.
531:   if (ctx.arg.z_cet_report != CET_REPORT_NONE)
532:     check_cet_errors(ctx);
533: 
534:   // Handle `-z execstack-if-needed`.
535:   if (ctx.arg.z_execstack_if_needed)
536:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
537:       if (file->needs_executable_stack)
538:         ctx.arg.z_execstack = true;
539: 
540:   // If we are linking a .so file, remaining undefined symbols does
541:   // not cause a linker error. Instead, they are treated as if they
542:   // were imported symbols.
543:   //
544:   // If we are linking an executable, weak undefs are converted to
545:   // weakly imported symbols so that they'll have another chance to be
546:   // resolved.
547:   claim_unresolved_symbols(ctx);
548: 
549:   // Beyond this point, no new symbols will be added to the result.
550: 
551:   // Handle --print-dependencies
552:   if (ctx.arg.print_dependencies)
553:     print_dependencies(ctx);
554: 
555:   // Handle -repro
556:   if (ctx.arg.repro)
557:     write_repro_file(ctx);
558: 
559:   // Handle --require-defined
560:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.require_defined)
561:     if (!get_symbol(ctx, name)->file)
562:       Error(ctx) << "--require-defined: undefined symbol: " << name;
563: 
564:   // .init_array and .fini_array contents have to be sorted by
565:   // a special rule. Sort them.
566:   sort_init_fini(ctx);
567: 
568:   // Likewise, .ctors and .dtors have to be sorted. They are rare
569:   // because they are superceded by .init_array/.fini_array, though.
570:   sort_ctor_dtor(ctx);
571: 
572:   // Handle --shuffle-sections
573:   if (ctx.arg.shuffle_sections != SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_NONE)
574:     shuffle_sections(ctx);
575: 
576:   // Copy string referred by .dynamic to .dynstr.
577:   for (SharedFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos)
578:     ctx.dynstr->add_string(file->soname);
579:   for (std::string_view str : ctx.arg.auxiliary)
580:     ctx.dynstr->add_string(str);
581:   for (std::string_view str : ctx.arg.filter)
582:     ctx.dynstr->add_string(str);
583:   if (!ctx.arg.rpaths.empty())
584:     ctx.dynstr->add_string(ctx.arg.rpaths);
585:   if (!ctx.arg.soname.empty())
586:     ctx.dynstr->add_string(ctx.arg.soname);
587: 
588:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
589:     ppc64v1_scan_symbols(ctx);
590: 
591:   // Scan relocations to find symbols that need entries in .got, .plt,
592:   // .got.plt, .dynsym, .dynstr, etc.
593:   scan_relocations(ctx);
594: 
595:   // Compute sizes of output sections while assigning offsets
596:   // within an output section to input sections.
597:   compute_section_sizes(ctx);
598: 
599:   // Sort sections by section attributes so that we'll have to
600:   // create as few segments as possible.
601:   sort_output_sections(ctx);
602: 
603:   // If --packed_dyn_relocs=relr was given, base relocations are stored
604:   // to a .relr.dyn section in a compressed form. Construct a compressed
605:   // relocations now so that we can fix section sizes and file layout.
606:   if (ctx.arg.pack_dyn_relocs_relr)
607:     construct_relr(ctx);
608: 
609:   // Reserve a space for dynamic symbol strings in .dynstr and sort
610:   // .dynsym contents if necessary. Beyond this point, no symbol will
611:   // be added to .dynsym.
612:   ctx.dynsym->finalize(ctx);
613: 
614:   // Print reports about undefined symbols, if needed.
615:   if (ctx.arg.unresolved_symbols == UNRESOLVED_ERROR)
616:     report_undef_errors(ctx);
617: 
618:   // Fill .gnu.version_d section contents.
619:   if (ctx.verdef)
620:     ctx.verdef->construct(ctx);
621: 
622:   // Fill .gnu.version_r section contents.
623:   ctx.verneed->construct(ctx);
624: 
625:   // Compute .symtab and .strtab sizes for each file.
626:   create_output_symtab(ctx);
627: 
628:   // .eh_frame is a special section from the linker's point of view,
629:   // as its contents are parsed and reconstructed by the linker,
630:   // unlike other sections that are regarded as opaque bytes.
631:   // Here, we construct output .eh_frame contents.
632:   ctx.eh_frame->construct(ctx);
633: 
634:   // Handle --gdb-index.
635:   if (ctx.arg.gdb_index)
636:     ctx.gdb_index->construct(ctx);
637: 
638:   // If --emit-relocs is given, we'll copy relocation sections from input
639:   // files to an output file.
640:   if (ctx.arg.emit_relocs)
641:     create_reloc_sections(ctx);
642: 
643:   // Compute the section header values for all sections.
644:   compute_section_headers(ctx);
645: 
646:   // Assign offsets to output sections
647:   i64 filesize = set_osec_offsets(ctx);
648: 
649:   // On RISC-V, branches are encode using multiple instructions so
650:   // that they can jump to anywhere in ±2 GiB by default. They may
651:   // be replaced with shorter instruction sequences if destinations
652:   // are close enough. Do this optimization.
653:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>)
654:     filesize = riscv_resize_sections(ctx);
655: 
656:   // At this point, memory layout is fixed.
657: 
658:   // Set actual addresses to linker-synthesized symbols.
659:   fix_synthetic_symbols(ctx);
660: 
661:   // Beyond this, you can assume that symbol addresses including their
662:   // GOT or PLT addresses have a correct final value.
663: 
664:   // If --compress-debug-sections is given, compress .debug_* sections
665:   // using zlib.
666:   if (ctx.arg.compress_debug_sections != COMPRESS_NONE)
667:     filesize = compress_debug_sections(ctx);
668: 
669:   // At this point, both memory and file layouts are fixed.
670: 
671:   t_before_copy.stop();
672: 
673:   // Create an output file
674:   ctx.output_file =
675:     OutputFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, ctx.arg.output, filesize, 0777);
676:   ctx.buf = ctx.output_file->buf;
677: 
678:   Timer t_copy(ctx, "copy");
679: 
680:   // Copy input sections to the output file and apply relocations.
681:   copy_chunks(ctx);
682: 
683:   // Some part of .gdb_index couldn't be computed until other debug
684:   // sections are complete. We have complete debug sections now, so
685:   // write the rest of .gdb_index.
686:   if (ctx.gdb_index)
687:     ctx.gdb_index->write_address_areas(ctx);
688: 
689:   // Dynamic linker works better with sorted .rela.dyn section,
690:   // so we sort them.
691:   ctx.reldyn->sort(ctx);
692: 
693:   // Zero-clear paddings between sections
694:   clear_padding(ctx);
695: 
696:   // .note.gnu.build-id section contains a cryptographic hash of the
697:   // entire output file. Now that we wrote everything except build-id,
698:   // we can compute it.
699:   if (ctx.buildid)
700:     ctx.buildid->write_buildid(ctx);
701: 
702:   t_copy.stop();
703:   ctx.checkpoint();
704: 
705:   // Close the output file. This is the end of the linker's main job.
706:   ctx.output_file->close(ctx);
707: 
708:   // Handle --dependency-file
709:   if (!ctx.arg.dependency_file.empty())
710:     write_dependency_file(ctx);
711: 
712:   if (ctx.has_lto_object)
713:     lto_cleanup(ctx);
714: 
715:   t_total.stop();
716:   t_all.stop();
717: 
718:   if (ctx.arg.print_map)
719:     print_map(ctx);
720: 
721:   // Show stats numbers
722:   if (ctx.arg.stats)
723:     show_stats(ctx);
724: 
725:   if (ctx.arg.perf)
726:     print_timer_records(ctx.timer_records);
727: 
728:   std::cout << std::flush;
729:   std::cerr << std::flush;
730:   if (on_complete)
731:     on_complete();
732: 
733:   if (ctx.arg.quick_exit)
734:     _exit(0);
735: 
736:   for (std::function<void()> &fn : ctx.on_exit)
737:     fn();
738:   ctx.checkpoint();
739:   return 0;
740: }
741: 
742: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
743: 
744: template void read_file(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
745: template MappedFile<Context<E>> *open_library(Context<E> &, std::string);
746: 
747: #ifdef MOLD_X86_64
748: 
749: extern template int elf_main<I386>(int, char **);
750: extern template int elf_main<ARM32>(int, char **);
751: extern template int elf_main<ARM64>(int, char **);
752: extern template int elf_main<RV32BE>(int, char **);
753: extern template int elf_main<RV32LE>(int, char **);
754: extern template int elf_main<RV64LE>(int, char **);
755: extern template int elf_main<RV64BE>(int, char **);
756: extern template int elf_main<PPC32>(int, char **);
757: extern template int elf_main<PPC64V1>(int, char **);
758: extern template int elf_main<PPC64V2>(int, char **);
759: extern template int elf_main<S390X>(int, char **);
760: extern template int elf_main<SPARC64>(int, char **);
761: extern template int elf_main<M68K>(int, char **);
762: extern template int elf_main<SH4>(int, char **);
763: extern template int elf_main<ALPHA>(int, char **);
764: 
765: int main(int argc, char **argv) {
766:   return elf_main<X86_64>(argc, argv);
767: }
768: 
769: #else
770: 
771: template int elf_main<E>(int, char **);
772: 
773: #endif
774: 
775: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/tls.cc
================
  1: // This file contains helper functions for thread-local storage (TLS).
  2: // TLS is probably the most obscure feature the linker has to support,
  3: // so I'll explain it in detail in this comment.
  4: //
  5: // TLS is a per-thread storage. Thread-local variables (TLVs) are in a TLS
  6: // so that each thread has its own set of thread-local variables. Taking
  7: // an address of a TLV returns a unique value for each thread. For example,
  8: // `&foo` for the following code returns different pointer values for
  9: // different threads.
 10: //
 11: //   thread_local int foo;
 12: //
 13: // TLV is a relatively new feature. C for example didn't provide the
 14: // official support for it through the keyword `thread_local` until C11.
 15: // TLV needs a coordination between the compiler, the linker and the
 16: // runtime to work correctly.
 17: //
 18: // An ELF exectuable or a shared library using TLV contains a "TLS template
 19: // image" in the PT_TLS segment. For each newly created thread including the
 20: // initial one, the runtime allocates a contiguous memory for an executable
 21: // and its depending shared libraries and copies template images there. That
 22: // per-thread memory is called the "TLS block". After allocating and
 23: // initializing a TLS block, the runtime sets a register to refer to the TLS
 24: // block, so that the thread-local variables are accessible relative to the
 25: // register.
 26: //
 27: // The register referring to the per-thread storage is called the Thread
 28: // Pointer (TP). TP is part of the thread's context. When the kernel
 29: // scheduler switches threads, TP is saved and restored automatically just
 30: // like other registers are.
 31: //
 32: // The TLS template image is read-only. It contains TLVs' initial values
 33: // for new threads, and no one writes to it at runtime.
 34: //
 35: // Now, let's think about how to access a TLV. We need to know the TLV's
 36: // address to access it which can be done in several different ways as
 37: // follows:
 38: //
 39: //  1. If we are creating an executable, we know the exact size of the TLS
 40: //     template image we are creating, and we know where the TP will be
 41: //     set to after the template is copied to the TLS block. Therefore,
 42: //     the TP-relative address of a TLV in the main executable is known at
 43: //     link-time. That means, computing a TLV's address can be as easy as
 44: //     `add %dst, %tp, <link-time constant>`.
 45: //
 46: //  2. If we are creating a shared library, we don't exactly know where
 47: //     its TLS template image will be copied to in terms of the
 48: //     TP-relative address, because we don't know how large is the main
 49: //     executable's and other libraries' TLS template images are. Only the
 50: //     runtime knows the exact TP-relative address.
 51: //
 52: //     We can solve the problem with an indirection. Specifically, for
 53: //     each TLV whose TP-relative address is only known at process startup
 54: //     time, we create a GOT entry to store its TP-relative address. We
 55: //     also emit a dynamic relocation to let the runtime to fill the GOT
 56: //     entry with a TP-relative address.
 57: //
 58: //     Computing a TLV address in this scheme needs at least two machine
 59: //     instructions in most ISAs; first instruction loads a value from a
 60: //     GOT entry, and the second one adds the loaded value to TP.
 61: //
 62: //  3. Now, think about libraries that you dynamically load with dlopen.
 63: //     The TLS block for such library has to be allocated separately from
 64: //     the initial TLS block, so we now have two or more discontiguous
 65: //     TLS blocks. There's no easy formula to compute an address of a TLV
 66: //     in a separate TLS block.
 67: //
 68: //     The address of a TLV in a separate TLS block can be obtained by
 69: //     calling a libc-provided function, __tls_get_addr(). The function
 70: //     takes two arguments; a module ID to identify the ELF file and the
 71: //     TLV's offset within the ELF file's TLS template image. Accessing a
 72: //     TLV is sometimes compiled to a function call! The module ID and the
 73: //     offset are usually stored to GOT as two consecutive words.
 74: //
 75: // The last access method is the most generic, so the compiler emits such
 76: // code by default. But that's the most expensive one, so the linker
 77: // rewrites instructions if possible so that 3) is relaxed to 2) or even
 78: // to 1).
 79: //
 80: // 1) is called the Local Exec access model. 2) is Initial Exec, and 3) is
 81: // General Dynamic.
 82: //
 83: // There's another little trick that the compiler can use if it knows two
 84: // TLVs are in the same ELF file (usually in the same file as the code is).
 85: // In this case, we can call __tls_get_addr() only once with a module ID and
 86: // the offset 0 to obtain the base address of the ELF file's TLS block. The
 87: // base address obtained this way is sometimes called Dynamic Thread Pointer
 88: // or DTP. We can then compute TLVs' addresses by adding their DTP-relative
 89: // addresses to DTP. This access model is called the Local Dynamic.
 90: //
 91: //
 92: // === TLS Descriptor access model ===
 93: //
 94: // As described above, there are arguably too many different TLS access
 95: // models from the most generic one you can use in any ELF file to the most
 96: // efficient one you can use only when building a main executable. Compiling
 97: // source code with an appropriate TLS access model is bothersome. To solve
 98: // the problem, a new TLS access model was proposed. That is called the TLS
 99: // Descriptor (TLSDESC) model.
100: //
101: // For a TLV compiled with TLSDESC, we allocate two consecutive GOT slots
102: // and create a TLSDESC dynamic relocation for them. The dynamic linker
103: // sets a function pointer to the first GOT slot and its argument to the
104: // second slot.
105: //
106: // To access the TLV, we call the function pointer with the argument we
107: // read from the second GOT slot. The function returns the TLV's
108: // TP-relative address.
109: //
110: // The runtime chooses the best access method depending on the situation
111: // and sets a pointer to the most efficient code to the first GOT slot.
112: // For example, if a TLV's TP-relative address is known at process startup
113: // time, the runtime sets that address to the second GOT slot and set a
114: // function that just returns its argument to the first GOT slot.
115: //
116: // With TLSDECS, the compiler can always emit the same code for TLVs
117: // without sacrificing runtime performance.
118: //
119: // TLSDESC is better than the traditional, non-TLSDESC TLS access models.
120: // It's the default on ARM64, but on other targets, TLSDESC is
121: // unfortunately either optional or even not supported at all. So we still
122: // need to support both the traditional TLS models and the TLSDESC model.
123: 
124: #include "mold.h"
125: 
126: namespace mold::elf {
127: 
128: template <typename E>
129: static ElfPhdr<E> *get_tls_segment(Context<E> &ctx) {
130:   if (ctx.phdr)
131:     for (ElfPhdr<E> &phdr : ctx.phdr->phdrs)
132:       if (phdr.p_type == PT_TLS)
133:         return &phdr;
134:   return nullptr;
135: }
136: 
137: template <typename E>
138: u64 get_tls_begin(Context<E> &ctx) {
139:   if (ElfPhdr<E> *phdr = get_tls_segment(ctx))
140:     return phdr->p_vaddr;
141:   return 0;
142: }
143: 
144: // Returns the TP address which can be used for efficient TLV accesses in
145: // the main executable. TP at runtime refers to a per-process TLS block
146: // whose address is not known at link-time. So the address returned from
147: // this function is the TP if the TLS template image were a TLS block.
148: template <typename E>
149: u64 get_tp_addr(Context<E> &ctx) {
150:   ElfPhdr<E> *phdr = get_tls_segment(ctx);
151:   if (!phdr)
152:     return 0;
153: 
154:   // On x86, SPARC and s390x, TP (%gs on i386, %fs on x86-64, %g7 on SPARC
155:   // and %a0/%a1 on s390x) refers past the end of the TLS block for
156:   // historical reasons. TLVs are accessed with negative offsets from TP.
157:   if constexpr (is_x86<E> || is_sparc<E> || is_s390x<E>)
158:     return align_to(phdr->p_vaddr + phdr->p_memsz, phdr->p_align);
159: 
160:   // On ARM, SH4 and Alpha, the runtime appends two words at the beginning
161:   // of TLV template image when copying TLVs to the TLS block, so we need
162:   // to offset it.
163:   if constexpr (is_arm<E> || is_sh4<E> || is_alpha<E>)
164:     return align_down(phdr->p_vaddr - sizeof(Word<E>) * 2, phdr->p_align);
165: 
166:   // On PPC and m68k, TP is 0x7000 (28 KiB) past the beginning of the TLV
167:   // block to maximize the addressable range for load/store instructions
168:   // with 16-bits signed immediates. It's not exactly 0x8000 (32 KiB) off
169:   // because there's a small implementation-defined piece of data before
170:   // the TLV block, and the runtime wants to access them efficiently too.
171:   if constexpr (is_ppc<E> || is_m68k<E>)
172:     return phdr->p_vaddr + 0x7000;
173: 
174:   // RISC-V just uses the beginning of the main executable's TLV block as
175:   // TP. RISC-V load/store instructions usually take 12-bits signed
176:   // immediates, so the beginning of TLV ± 2 KiB is accessible with a
177:   // single load/store instruction.
178:   assert(is_riscv<E>);
179:   return phdr->p_vaddr;
180: }
181: 
182: // Returns the address when __tls_get_addr would be called with offset 0.
183: template <typename E>
184: u64 get_dtp_addr(Context<E> &ctx) {
185:   ElfPhdr<E> *phdr = get_tls_segment(ctx);
186:   if (!phdr)
187:     return 0;
188: 
189:   // On PPC64 and m68k, R_DTPOFF is resolved to the address 0x8000 (32
190:   // KiB) past the start of the TLS block. The bias maximizes the
191:   // accessible range for load/store instructions with 16-bits signed
192:   // immediates. That is, if the offset were right at the beginning of
193:   // the start of the TLS block, the half of addressible space (negative
194:   // immediates) would have been wasted.
195:   if constexpr (is_ppc<E> || is_m68k<E>)
196:     return phdr->p_vaddr + 0x8000;
197: 
198:   // On RISC-V, the bias is 0x800 as the load/store instructions in the
199:   // ISA usually have a 12-bit immediate.
200:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>)
201:     return phdr->p_vaddr + 0x800;
202: 
203:   // On other targets, DTP simply refers to the beginning of the TLS block.
204:   return phdr->p_vaddr;
205: }
206: 
207: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
208: 
209: template u64 get_tls_begin<E>(Context<E> &);
210: template u64 get_tp_addr<E>(Context<E> &);
211: template u64 get_dtp_addr<E>(Context<E> &);
212: 
213: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: macho/arch-arm64.cc
================
  1: #if MOLD_ARM64 || MOLD_ARM64_32
  2: 
  3: #include "mold.h"
  4: 
  5: namespace mold::macho {
  6: 
  7: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
  8: 
  9: static u64 page(u64 val) {
 10:   return val & 0xffff'ffff'ffff'f000;
 11: }
 12: 
 13: static u64 page_offset(u64 hi, u64 lo) {
 14:   u64 val = page(hi) - page(lo);
 15:   return (bits(val, 13, 12) << 29) | (bits(val, 32, 14) << 5);
 16: }
 17: 
 18: // Write an immediate to an ADD, LDR or STR instruction.
 19: static void write_add_ldst(u8 *loc, u32 val) {
 20:   u32 insn = *(ul32 *)loc;
 21:   i64 scale = 0;
 22: 
 23:   if ((insn & 0x3b00'0000) == 0x3900'0000) {
 24:     // LDR/STR accesses an aligned 1, 2, 4, 8 or 16 byte data on memory.
 25:     // The immediate is scaled by the data size, so we need to know the
 26:     // data size to write a correct immediate.
 27:     //
 28:     // The most significant two bits of the instruction usually
 29:     // specifies the data size.
 30:     scale = bits(insn, 31, 30);
 31: 
 32:     // Vector and byte LDR/STR shares the same scale bits.
 33:     // We can distinguish them by looking at other bits.
 34:     if (scale == 0 && (insn & 0x04800000) == 0x04800000)
 35:       scale = 4;
 36:   }
 37: 
 38:   *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 11, scale) << 10;
 39: }
 40: 
 41: static void to_32(ul32 *loc) {
 42:   // The most significant two bits of a load/store instruction indicates
 43:   // the size. Rewrite it to reduce it from 8 to 4 so that it accesses
 44:   // wN register instead of xN register on the ILP32 ABI.
 45:   if constexpr (is_arm64_32<E>)
 46:     *loc = (*loc & 0x3fff'ffff) | 0x8000'0000;
 47: }
 48: 
 49: template <>
 50: void StubsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 51:   ul32 *buf = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
 52: 
 53:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++) {
 54:     static const ul32 insn[] = {
 55:       0x9000'0010, // adrp x16, $ptr@PAGE
 56:       0xf940'0210, // ldr  x16, [x16, $ptr@PAGEOFF]
 57:       0xd61f'0200, // br   x16
 58:     };
 59: 
 60:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::stub_size);
 61:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 62:     to_32(buf + 1);
 63: 
 64:     u64 this_addr = this->hdr.addr + E::stub_size * i;
 65: 
 66:     u64 ptr_addr;
 67:     if (ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr)
 68:       ptr_addr = ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.addr + sizeof(Word<E>) * i;
 69:     else
 70:       ptr_addr = syms[i]->get_got_addr(ctx);
 71: 
 72:     buf[0] |= page_offset(ptr_addr, this_addr);
 73:     buf[1] |= bits(ptr_addr, 11, 3) << 10;
 74:     buf += 3;
 75:   }
 76: }
 77: 
 78: // __stubs_helper contains code to call the dynamic symbol resolver for
 79: // PLT.
 80: template <>
 81: void StubHelperSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 82:   ul32 *start = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
 83:   ul32 *buf = start;
 84: 
 85:   static const ul32 insn0[] = {
 86:     0x9000'0011, // adrp x17, $__dyld_private@PAGE
 87:     0x9100'0231, // add  x17, x17, $__dyld_private@PAGEOFF
 88:     0xa9bf'47f0, // stp  x16, x17, [sp, #-16]!
 89:     0x9000'0010, // adrp x16, $dyld_stub_binder@PAGE
 90:     0xf940'0210, // ldr  x16, [x16, $dyld_stub_binder@PAGEOFF]
 91:     0xd61f'0200, // br   x16
 92:   };
 93: 
 94:   static_assert(sizeof(insn0) == E::stub_helper_hdr_size);
 95:   memcpy(buf, insn0, sizeof(insn0));
 96:   to_32(buf + 4);
 97: 
 98:   u64 dyld_private = ctx.__dyld_private->get_addr(ctx);
 99:   buf[0] |= page_offset(dyld_private, this->hdr.addr);
100:   buf[1] |= bits(dyld_private, 11, 0) << 10;
101: 
102:   u64 stub_binder = ctx.dyld_stub_binder->get_got_addr(ctx);
103:   buf[3] |= page_offset(stub_binder, this->hdr.addr - 12);
104:   buf[4] |= bits(stub_binder, 11, 0) << 10;
105: 
106:   buf += 6;
107: 
108:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.stubs.syms.size(); i++) {
109:     static const ul32 insn[] = {
110:       0x1800'0050, // ldr  w16, addr
111:       0x1400'0000, // b    stubHelperHeader
112:       0x0000'0000, // addr: .long <idx>
113:     };
114: 
115:     static_assert(sizeof(insn) == E::stub_helper_size);
116: 
117:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
118:     buf[1] |= bits((start - buf - 1) * 4, 27, 2);
119:     buf[2] = ctx.lazy_bind->bind_offsets[i];
120:     buf += 3;
121:   }
122: }
123: 
124: // For each _objc_msgSend$<classname>, we create an entry in _objc_stubs
125: // section. Each entry consist of machine code that calls _objc_msgSend
126: // with the address to an interned class name as an argument.
127: template <>
128: void ObjcStubsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
129:   if (this->hdr.size == 0)
130:     return;
131: 
132:   static const ul32 insn[] = {
133:     0x9000'0001, // adrp  x1, @selector("foo")@PAGE
134:     0xf940'0021, // ldr   x1, [x1, @selector("foo")@PAGEOFF]
135:     0x9000'0010, // adrp  x16, _objc_msgSend@GOTPAGE
136:     0xf940'0210, // ldr   x16, [x16, _objc_msgSend@GOTPAGEOFF]
137:     0xd61f'0200, // br    x16
138:     0xd420'0020, // brk   #0x1
139:     0xd420'0020, // brk   #0x1
140:     0xd420'0020, // brk   #0x1
141:   };
142:   static_assert(sizeof(insn) == ENTRY_SIZE);
143: 
144:   u64 msgsend_got_addr = ctx._objc_msgSend->get_got_addr(ctx);
145: 
146:   for (i64 i = 0; i < methnames.size(); i++) {
147:     ul32 *buf = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset + ENTRY_SIZE * i);
148:     u64 sel_addr = selrefs[i]->get_addr(ctx);
149:     u64 ent_addr = this->hdr.addr + ENTRY_SIZE * i;
150: 
151:     memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
152:     to_32(buf + 1);
153:     to_32(buf + 3);
154: 
155:     buf[0] |= page_offset(sel_addr, ent_addr);
156:     buf[1] |= bits(sel_addr, 11, 3) << 10;
157:     buf[2] |= page_offset(msgsend_got_addr, ent_addr + 8);
158:     buf[3] |= bits(msgsend_got_addr, 11, 3) << 10;
159:   }
160: }
161: 
162: template <>
163: std::vector<Relocation<E>>
164: read_relocations(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, const MachSection<E> &hdr) {
165:   std::vector<Relocation<E>> vec;
166:   vec.reserve(hdr.nreloc);
167: 
168:   MachRel *rels = (MachRel *)(file.mf->data + hdr.reloff);
169: 
170:   // Read one Mach-O relocation at a time and create a Relocation for it
171:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.nreloc; i++) {
172:     i64 addend = 0;
173: 
174:     // Mach-O relocation doesn't contain an addend. UNSIGNED relocs have
175:     // addends in the input section they refer to. Users can also specify
176:     // an addend for other types of relocations by prepending an ADDEND
177:     // reloc.
178:     switch (rels[i].type) {
179:     case ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED: {
180:       i64 size = 1 << rels[i].p2size;
181:       ASSERT(size == 4 || size == 8);
182:       if (size == 4)
183:         addend = *(il32 *)(file.mf->data + hdr.offset + rels[i].offset);
184:       else
185:         addend = *(il64 *)(file.mf->data + hdr.offset + rels[i].offset);
186:       break;
187:     }
188:     case ARM64_RELOC_ADDEND:
189:       addend = sign_extend(rels[i].idx, 23);
190:       i++;
191:       break;
192:     case ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT:
193:       ASSERT(rels[i].is_pcrel);
194:       break;
195:     }
196: 
197:     MachRel &r = rels[i];
198: 
199:     vec.push_back(Relocation<E>{
200:       .offset = r.offset,
201:       .type = (u8)r.type,
202:       .size = (u8)(1 << r.p2size),
203:       .is_subtracted = (i > 0 && rels[i - 1].type == ARM64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR),
204:     });
205: 
206:     Relocation<E> &rel = vec.back();
207: 
208:     // A relocation refers to either a symbol or a section
209:     if (r.is_extern) {
210:       rel.target = file.syms[r.idx];
211:       rel.is_sym = true;
212:       rel.addend = addend;
213:     } else {
214:       u64 addr = r.is_pcrel ? (hdr.addr + r.offset + addend) : addend;
215:       Subsection<E> *target = file.find_subsection(ctx, addr);
216:       if (!target)
217:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": bad relocation: " << r.offset;
218: 
219:       rel.target = target;
220:       rel.is_sym = false;
221:       rel.addend = addr - target->input_addr;
222:     }
223:   }
224: 
225:   return vec;
226: }
227: 
228: template <>
229: void Subsection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
230:   for (Relocation<E> &r : get_rels()) {
231:     Symbol<E> *sym = r.sym();
232:     if (!sym || !sym->file)
233:       continue;
234: 
235:     if (sym->file->is_dylib)
236:       ((DylibFile<E> *)sym->file)->is_alive = true;
237: 
238:     bool is_tlv_reloc = (r.type == ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGE21 ||
239:                          r.type == ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGEOFF12);
240:     if (is_tlv_reloc != sym->is_tlv)
241:       Error(ctx) << "illegal thread local variable reference to regular symbol `"
242:                  << *sym << "`";
243: 
244:     switch (r.type) {
245:     case ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26:
246:       if (sym->is_imported)
247:         sym->flags |= NEEDS_STUB;
248:       break;
249:     case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGE21:
250:     case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGEOFF12:
251:     case ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT:
252:       sym->flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
253:       break;
254:     case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGE21:
255:     case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGEOFF12:
256:       sym->flags |= NEEDS_THREAD_PTR;
257:       break;
258:     }
259:   }
260: }
261: 
262: template <>
263: void Subsection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
264:   std::span<Relocation<E>> rels = get_rels();
265: 
266:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
267:     Relocation<E> &r = rels[i];
268: 
269:     if (r.sym() && !r.sym()->file) {
270:       Error(ctx) << "undefined symbol: " << isec->file << ": " << *r.sym();
271:       continue;
272:     }
273: 
274:     u8 *loc = buf + r.offset;
275:     u64 S = r.get_addr(ctx);
276:     u64 A = r.addend;
277:     u64 P = get_addr(ctx) + r.offset;
278:     u64 G = r.sym() ? r.sym()->got_idx * sizeof(Word<E>) : 0;
279:     u64 GOT = ctx.got.hdr.addr;
280: 
281:     switch (r.type) {
282:     case ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED:
283:       ASSERT(r.size == sizeof(Word<E>));
284: 
285:       // Do not write a value if we have a dynamic relocation for this reloc.
286:       if (r.sym() && r.sym()->is_imported)
287:         break;
288: 
289:       // __thread_vars contains TP-relative addresses to symbols in the
290:       // TLS initialization image (i.e. __thread_data and __thread_bss).
291:       if (r.refers_to_tls())
292:         *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A - ctx.tls_begin;
293:       else
294:         *(Word<E> *)loc = S + A;
295:       break;
296:     case ARM64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR:
297:       // A SUBTRACTOR relocation is always followed by an UNSIGNED relocation.
298:       // They work as a pair to materialize a relative address between two
299:       // locations.
300:       ASSERT(r.size == 4 || r.size == 8);
301:       i++;
302:       ASSERT(rels[i].type == ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED);
303:       if (r.size == 4)
304:         *(ul32 *)loc = rels[i].get_addr(ctx) + rels[i].addend - S;
305:       else
306:         *(ul64 *)loc = rels[i].get_addr(ctx) + rels[i].addend - S;
307:       break;
308:     case ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26: {
309:       i64 val = S + A - P;
310:       if (val < -(1 << 27) || (1 << 27) <= val)
311:         val = isec->osec.thunks[r.thunk_idx]->get_addr(r.thunk_sym_idx) - P;
312:       *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 27, 2);
313:       break;
314:     }
315:     case ARM64_RELOC_PAGE21:
316:       *(ul32 *)loc |= page_offset(S + A, P);
317:       break;
318:     case ARM64_RELOC_PAGEOFF12:
319:       write_add_ldst(loc, S + A);
320:       break;
321:     case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGE21:
322:       *(ul32 *)loc |= page_offset(G + GOT + A, P);
323:       break;
324:     case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGEOFF12:
325:       write_add_ldst(loc, G + GOT + A);
326:       break;
327:     case ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT:
328:       ASSERT(r.size == 4);
329:       *(ul32 *)loc = G + GOT + A - P;
330:       break;
331:     case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGE21:
332:       *(ul32 *)loc |= page_offset(r.sym()->get_tlv_addr(ctx) + A, P);
333:       break;
334:     case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGEOFF12:
335:       write_add_ldst(loc, r.sym()->get_tlv_addr(ctx) + A);
336:       break;
337:     default:
338:       Fatal(ctx) << *isec << ": unknown reloc: " << (int)r.type;
339:     }
340:   }
341: }
342: 
343: template <>
344: void RangeExtensionThunk<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
345:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + output_section.hdr.offset + offset;
346: 
347:   static const ul32 data[] = {
348:     0x9000'0010, // adrp x16, 0   # R_AARCH64_ADR_PREL_PG_HI21
349:     0x9100'0210, // add  x16, x16 # R_AARCH64_ADD_ABS_LO12_NC
350:     0xd61f'0200, // br   x16
351:   };
352: 
353:   static_assert(ENTRY_SIZE == sizeof(data));
354: 
355:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
356:     u64 addr = symbols[i]->get_addr(ctx);
357:     u64 pc = output_section.hdr.addr + offset + i * ENTRY_SIZE;
358: 
359:     u8 *loc = buf + i * ENTRY_SIZE;
360:     memcpy(loc , data, sizeof(data));
361:     *(ul32 *)loc |= page_offset(addr, pc);
362:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 4) |= bits(addr, 11, 0) << 10;
363:   }
364: }
365: 
366: } // namespace mold::macho
367: 
368: #endif
