================
File: AGENTS.md
================
  1: # AGENTS.md
  2: 
  3: ## Repository Identity
  4: 
  5: This checkout is a local `sold` worktree: a Mach-O-capable revival of mold.
  6: The build system, binary name, version string, and many docs still use the
  7: name `mold`, but when `LICENSE.md` exists in the repository root CMake sets
  8: `MOLD_IS_SOLD=ON` and includes the Mach-O linker codepath in addition to the
  9: ELF linker.
 10: 
 11: The top-level executable entrypoint is `common/main.cc`:
 12: 
 13: - the built executable is still named `mold`
 14: - if `argv[0]` is `ld64` or starts with `ld64.`, control goes to
 15:   `mold::macho::main`
 16: - all other invocations go to `mold::elf::main`
 17: 
 18: Do not treat the top-level `README.md` as authoritative for build, test, or
 19: architecture details. It is intentionally sparse and macOS-oriented. The real
 20: sources of truth are `CMakeLists.txt`, the code under `common/`, `elf/`, and
 21: `macho/`, the tests under `test/`, the benchmark scripts and docs under
 22: `benchmarks/`, and the docs in `docs/`.
 23: 
 24: ## Current Local Status
 25: 
 26: These statements are true in this worktree as of the current milestone and are
 27: important context for future agents:
 28: 
 29: - The Rust-first macOS benchmark harness under `benchmarks/` exists and is
 30:   usable.
 31: - The active seeded corpus in `benchmarks/corpus.toml` currently contains two
 32:   real local executable targets:
 33:   - `forge` from `/Users/dcazares/Documents/Code/forge-fresh`
 34:   - `tools-mcp` from `/Users/dcazares/Documents/Code/tools-mcp`
 35: - The real Mach-O linker path now replays both seeded Rust targets
 36:   successfully when invoked through a real `ld64` entrypoint.
 37: - A replay-only validation run was written to
 38:   `benchmarks/out-direct-replay/results.json` with single measured runs and no
 39:   warmups. The current baseline there is:
 40:   - `forge`: Apple `0.31s`, sold `0.60s`
 41:   - `tools-mcp`: Apple `0.22s`, sold `0.32s`
 42: - Those numbers are useful as a current baseline, not as a statistically
 43:   stable performance claim.
 44: - Those replay-only outputs passed `file`, `lipo`, `otool`, and target smoke
 45:   checks.
 46: - The shared `benchmarks/out` tree is mutable during Cargo capture/replay and
 47:   can become stale or inconsistent if another benchmark run is touching the
 48:   same target directory at the same time. If you need isolated results, use a
 49:   separate `--out-dir`.
 50: 
 51: Recent code-level bring-up details that should not be regressed:
 52: 
 53: - The key Rust/Mach-O replay fix is in `macho/input-files.cc` inside
 54:   `ObjectFile<E>::resolve_symbols`. For `N_SECT` symbols, if
 55:   `sym_to_subsec[i]` is null, the code must fall back to
 56:   `find_subsection(ctx, msym.value)`. This matters for always-split sections
 57:   such as `__cstring`.
 58: - Do not reintroduce the idea that defined `private extern` symbols should be
 59:   treated as fully file-local. Rust archive replay depends on those symbols
 60:   remaining globally resolvable within the current link even though they are
 61:   module-private for export visibility.
 62: 
 63: ## Working Rules For Agents
 64: 
 65: - Treat the core linker as a C++20 codebase. The benchmark harness is
 66:   Python/TOML with Rust/Cargo integration. On non-MSVC builds, the C++ linker
 67:   code compiles with `-fno-exceptions`, `-fno-unwind-tables`, and
 68:   `-fno-asynchronous-unwind-tables`. Do not introduce exception-based control
 69:   flow into the linker.
 70: - Keep builds out of source.
 71: - Do not edit generated per-target wrapper `.cc` files in the build tree.
 72:   Edit the real sources under `common/`, `elf/`, or `macho/`.
 73: - If you change user-visible CLI behavior, help text, version output, or
 74:   installed names, review `docs/mold.1`, `docs/mold.md`, and the shell tests.
 75: - If you change build options or dependency handling, review both
 76:   `CMakeLists.txt` and `install-build-deps.sh`.
 77: - If you change benchmark harness behavior, update `benchmarks/README.md` and
 78:   keep `benchmarks/corpus.toml` assumptions honest.
 79: - If you change Mach-O symbol resolution, subsection handling, archive
 80:   extraction, or Rust-relevant linker behavior, do not stop at unit reasoning.
 81:   Re-run at least the focused Mach-O tests and preferably one captured Rust
 82:   replay target.
 83: - If you change sold-only behavior, remember that this checkout is a sold
 84:   build because `LICENSE.md` is present.
 85: 
 86: ## Configure, Build, Install, Test
 87: 
 88: Use out-of-source builds. From the repository root, these commands are
 89: correct:
 90: 
 91: ```bash
 92: # Configure
 93: cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
 94: 
 95: # Build
 96: cmake --build build
 97: 
 98: # Run all configured tests
 99: ctest --test-dir build --output-on-failure
100: 
101: # Run a single test by regex
102: ctest --test-dir build -R '<test-regex>' --output-on-failure
103: 
104: # Install
105: cmake --install build
106: ```
107: 
108: Important build and test facts:
109: 
110: - `include(CTest)` enables `BUILD_TESTING` unless it is explicitly turned off.
111: - The main binary is `build/mold`.
112: - When `BUILD_TESTING=ON` and `WIN32` is false, the build adds post-build
113:   symlinks in the build directory:
114:   - `build/ld`
115:   - `build/ld64` only when `MOLD_IS_SOLD=ON`
116: - On Apple hosts, CTest adds `test/macho` only when `MOLD_IS_SOLD=ON`.
117: - On non-Apple UNIX hosts, CTest adds `test/elf`.
118: - Mach-O test names are prefixed with `CMAKE_HOST_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR`, and on
119:   `arm64` hosts CMake also adds an `x86_64-...` copy of each Mach-O test.
120: - ELF test names are machine-prefixed by the triple setup in
121:   `test/elf/CMakeLists.txt`, for example `x86_64-common`.
122: - ELF test discovery is filename-driven: underscore-free `*.sh` are treated as
123:   common tests, while `${MACHINE}_*.sh` are machine-specific tests for that
124:   architecture.
125: - In ELF test setup, host normalization maps `arm.*` to `arm` and `ppc64` to
126:   `powerpc64` before native-triple matching.
127: - Native ELF tests get `SKIP_REGULAR_EXPRESSION "skipped"`. Non-native ELF
128:   tests are only added when `MOLD_ENABLE_QEMU_TESTS=ON` and receive
129:   `TRIPLE=<triple>` in their environment.
130: 
131: Current useful configure options:
132: 
133: - `-DMOLD_USE_ASAN=ON`: AddressSanitizer and UBSan
134: - `-DMOLD_USE_TSAN=ON`: ThreadSanitizer
135: - `-DMOLD_LTO=ON`: link-time optimization for the linker binary itself
136: - `-DMOLD_USE_MOLD=ON`: build `mold` with `-fuse-ld=mold`
137: - `-DMOLD_USE_MIMALLOC=OFF`: disable mimalloc
138: - `-DMOLD_USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC=ON`: use a system mimalloc instead of vendored
139: - `-DMOLD_USE_SYSTEM_TBB=ON`: use system TBB instead of vendored
140: - `-DMOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC=ON`: Linux packaging-style build with static
141:   libstdc++ and libcrypto
142: - `-DBUILD_TESTING=OFF`: disable tests and the build-tree `ld`/`ld64`
143:   symlink setup
144: - `-DMOLD_ENABLE_QEMU_TESTS=ON`: enable non-native ELF tests
145: - `-DMOLD_ENABLE_QEMU_TESTS_RV32=ON`: add RV32 ELF tests
146: - `-DMOLD_ENABLE_QEMU_TESTS_POWER10=ON`: add Power10 ELF tests
147: 
148: CMake 4 compatibility note:
149: 
150: - `CMakeLists.txt` already sets `CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5` when
151:   `CMAKE_VERSION >= 4.0` so vendored oneTBB can still configure without
152:   requiring a manual policy override.
153: 
154: Install behavior:
155: 
156: - `cmake --install` installs the `mold` executable into
157:   `${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}`.
158: - It installs `docs/mold.1` into `${CMAKE_INSTALL_MANDIR}/man1/`.
159: - If a root-level `LICENSE` file exists, it is installed into
160:   `${CMAKE_INSTALL_DOCDIR}`. The sold-enabling file in this checkout is
161:   `LICENSE.md`, and that path is not installed by the current CMake logic.
162: - It installs a relative symlink at `${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBEXECDIR}/mold/ld`
163:   pointing back to the `mold` executable.
164: - It installs `${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld.mold` and a matching manpage symlink
165:   `ld.mold.1`.
166: - In sold builds it also installs:
167:   - `${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld64.mold`
168:   - `${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld.sold`
169:   - `${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld64.sold`
170: 
171: ## Build Dependencies And Linkage
172: 
173: The build prefers some system libraries when available and otherwise falls
174: back to vendored copies under `third-party/`.
175: 
176: - TBB is mandatory. By default the vendored copy is built; use
177:   `-DMOLD_USE_SYSTEM_TBB=ON` for a system TBB.
178: - zlib is used from the system if found, otherwise `third-party/zlib` is
179:   built.
180: - zstd is linked from the system if `zstd.h` is available, otherwise the
181:   vendored `third-party/zstd` build is used.
182: - mimalloc is enabled by default only on non-Apple, non-Android, non-32-bit
183:   builds. On Apple it is off by default.
184: - On non-Apple, non-Windows, non-`MOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC` builds, OpenSSL Crypto
185:   is required.
186: - `libm` and `-latomic` are added conditionally where needed.
187: - On Windows, plain MSVC is rejected; the build requires Clang.
188: 
189: Platform-specific extras:
190: 
191: - On non-Apple and non-Windows builds, CMake also builds `mold-wrapper.so`
192:   from `elf/mold-wrapper.c` and installs it under `${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/mold`.
193: - `elf/subprocess.cc` looks for `mold-wrapper.so` beside the executable, under
194:   `${MOLD_LIBDIR}/mold/`, and under `../lib/mold/`.
195: - `mold-wrapper.so` is not built on Apple.
196: 
197: ## Repository Map
198: 
199: ### `common/`
200: 
201: Shared infrastructure used by both ELF and Mach-O:
202: 
203: - `common/main.cc`: version string composition, signal handling, cleanup,
204:   default thread count, and ELF/Mach-O dispatch
205: - `common/common.h`: core types and helpers such as `Atomic<T>`,
206:   `ConcurrentMap`, `Counter`, `Timer`, `MappedFile`, `OutputFile`,
207:   `SyncOut`, `Warn`, `Error`, and `Fatal`
208: - `common/output-file-unix.h` and `common/output-file-win32.h`: output file
209:   implementations
210: - `common/filetype.h`: file-type detection, including LTO object detection
211: - additional utilities: compression, demangling, globbing, paths,
212:   HyperLogLog, UUIDs, tar support, and performance counters
213: 
214: ### `elf/`
215: 
216: ELF linker implementation:
217: 
218: - `elf/main.cc`: top-level ELF driver
219: - `elf/cmdline.cc`: GNU `ld`-style argument parsing and help/version output
220: - `elf/passes.cc`: major ELF pass pipeline
221: - `elf/input-files.cc`: object/shared-library parsing and symbol ingestion
222: - `elf/input-sections.cc`: input-section handling and relocation-side data
223: - `elf/output-chunks.cc`: output sections, GOT/PLT, dynamic tables, symbol
224:   tables, build IDs, debug compression, and final writers
225: - `elf/linker-script.cc`: linker scripts, version scripts, and dynamic lists
226: - `elf/gc-sections.cc`: section garbage collection
227: - `elf/icf.cc`: identical code/data folding
228: - `elf/relocatable.cc`: relocatable-output path
229: - `elf/mapfile.cc`: mapfile generation
230: - `elf/thunks.cc` and `elf/tls.cc`: thunks and TLS support
231: - `elf/lto*.cc`: LTO integration
232: - `elf/subprocess.cc` and `elf/mold-wrapper.c`: subprocess wrapper support
233: - `elf/arch-*.cc`: backend-specific relocation and thunk logic
234: - `elf/mold.h`: main ELF context and most ELF-side data structures
235: - `elf/elf.h`: ELF type definitions and target descriptors
236: 
237: ### `macho/`
238: 
239: Mach-O linker implementation, built only when sold support is enabled:
240: 
241: - `macho/main.cc`: top-level Mach-O driver and major link pipeline
242: - `macho/cmdline.cc`: `ld64`-style argument parsing
243: - `macho/input-files.cc`: Mach-O objects, archives, dylibs, universal
244:   binaries, text-based stubs, and symbol/subsection handling
245: - `macho/input-sections.cc`: subsection and relocation-facing input logic
246: - `macho/output-chunks.cc`: segment and section layout, bind/rebase/export
247:   data, fixup chains, code signatures, unwind info, stubs, GOT, TLS-related
248:   chunks, and final output writing
249: - `macho/dead-strip.cc`: dead stripping
250: - `macho/mapfile.cc`: map output
251: - `macho/thunks.cc`: branch thunk logic
252: - `macho/lto*.cc`: Mach-O LTO integration
253: - `macho/tapi.cc` and `macho/yaml.cc`: `.tbd` and YAML parsing
254: - `macho/mold.h`: main Mach-O context and most Mach-O data structures
255: - `macho/macho.h`: Mach-O constants and architecture type definitions
256: - `macho/README.md`: good background on Mach-O concepts and how this linker
257:   models them
258: 
259: ### `test/`
260: 
261: Shell-based CTest suites:
262: 
263: - `test/elf/`: ELF tests generated per architecture/triple
264: - `test/macho/`: Mach-O tests generated for the host architecture and, on
265:   `arm64` hosts, an additional `x86_64` run
266: - `test/elf/common.inc` and `test/macho/common.inc`: shared harness logic
267: 
268: Focused Mach-O tests that mattered for recent Rust replay bring-up:
269: 
270: - `x86_64-basic`
271: - `x86_64-hello`
272: - `x86_64-private-extern`
273: 
274: ### `benchmarks/`
275: 
276: Rust-first macOS benchmark harness:
277: 
278: - `benchmarks/README.md`: harness overview
279: - `benchmarks/corpus.toml`: current benchmark corpus manifest
280: - `benchmarks/capture_linker.py`: Cargo linker wrapper and capture tool
281: - `benchmarks/run.py`: orchestrates capture, replay, validation, smoke tests,
282:   timing, and result summaries
283: - `benchmarks/out/`: ignored generated output
284: 
285: ### Other Important Paths
286: 
287: - `docs/coding-guidelines.md`: canonical local style guidance
288: - `docs/design.md`: architecture and performance background
289: - `docs/mold.1`: installable manpage
290: - `docs/mold.md`: CLI documentation
291: - `install-build-deps.sh`: distro-oriented dependency bootstrap script
292: - `dist.sh`: packaging-oriented build script
293: - `third-party/`: vendored dependencies
294: 
295: ## Benchmark Harness Details
296: 
297: The benchmark harness is intentionally Rust-first and macOS-native. It is not
298: a generic linker benchmark suite, and it is not wired into CMake or CTest. Run
299: it manually with `python3 benchmarks/run.py ...`.
300: 
301: Capture flow:
302: 
303: - `benchmarks/run.py` sets `CARGO_TARGET_X86_64_APPLE_DARWIN_LINKER` to
304:   `benchmarks/capture_linker.py`
305: - it also sets:
306:   - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` to a benchmark-owned directory under
307:     `benchmarks/out/targets/<name>/cargo-target`
308:   - `SOLD_CAPTURE_DIR` to the target capture directory
309:   - `SOLD_REAL_LINKER` to the real linker driver, defaulting to `clang`
310: - `capture_linker.py` runs the real linker driver, records the argv, derives
311:   the concrete final `ld` command from `clang -###`, and copies replay inputs
312:   that would otherwise disappear
313: 
314: Replay-input preservation details:
315: 
316: - the capture tool copies absolute `.o` files
317: - it also copies inputs whose path components include `rustc...`, which is how
318:   many temporary Rust linker inputs are preserved for replay
319: - the capture record stores a mapping from original input paths to copied
320:   inputs and rewrites derived replay argv to point at those copies
321: 
322: Replay and validation behavior in `benchmarks/run.py`:
323: 
324: - only supports `Darwin` hosts
325: - currently enforces an x86_64-only MVP
326: - requires a Python with stdlib `tomllib`, plus external tools including
327:   `cargo`, the real linker driver (`clang` by default), `xcrun`, `/usr/bin/time`,
328:   `file`, `lipo`, and `otool`
329: - rejects captured links that use LTO
330: - can take `--sold-bin` as either a true `ld64` entrypoint or a built `mold`
331:   executable; if the latter is used, the runner creates an `ld64.sold`
332:   symlink under the chosen output directory so the Mach-O entrypoint is used
333: - rewrites side-effect outputs under the benchmark output tree for:
334:   - `-o`
335:   - `-dependency_info`
336:   - `-map`
337:   - `-object_path_lto`
338: - validates produced binaries with:
339:   - `file`
340:   - `lipo -archs`
341:   - `otool -l`
342: - runs a per-target smoke command after each replayed link
343: - writes machine-readable summaries to `results.json`
344: - defaults to `1` warmup run, `10` measured runs, `--cargo-clean`, and a
345:   required sold win threshold of `10%`
346: - returns exit status `1` if either linker fails, if smoke validation fails,
347:   or if sold misses the configured win threshold
348: 
349: Current corpus details:
350: 
351: - `forge`
352:   - project root:
353:     `/Users/dcazares/Documents/Code/forge-fresh`
354:   - cargo args:
355:     `build --release -p forge --bin forge`
356:   - binary relpath:
357:     `target/release/forge`
358:   - smoke:
359:     `/bin/test -x {binary}`
360:   - reason for weak smoke: the Forge CLI currently appears to require a PTY
361:     for simple startup paths, so `--help` and `--version` are not dependable
362:     non-interactive smokes
363: - `tools-mcp`
364:   - project root:
365:     `/Users/dcazares/Documents/Code/tools-mcp`
366:   - cargo args:
367:     `build --release --bin tools-mcp`
368:   - binary relpath:
369:     `target/release/tools-mcp`
370:   - smoke:
371:     `{binary} --help`
372: 
373: Practical benchmark caveats:
374: 
375: - `benchmarks/out` is ignored and generated, not source
376: - a shared output tree can become inconsistent if another benchmark run is
377:   still building or cleaning the same Cargo target directory
378: - if you need reliable bring-up or A/B comparison data, use a dedicated
379:   `--out-dir`
380: - only the default `benchmarks/out` path is ignored by `.gitignore`; custom
381:   output directories are not ignored automatically
382: - `benchmarks/out-direct-replay/results.json` is currently the cleanest
383:   verified replay-only baseline for real sold-vs-Apple results in this
384:   worktree, but it is still a local generated artifact rather than a tracked
385:   source file
386: 
387: Representative benchmark commands:
388: 
389: ```bash
390: python3 benchmarks/run.py \
391:   --corpus benchmarks/corpus.toml \
392:   --target forge \
393:   --sold-bin ./build/ld64
394: 
395: python3 benchmarks/run.py \
396:   --corpus benchmarks/corpus.toml \
397:   --target tools-mcp \
398:   --sold-bin ./build/mold
399: ```
400: 
401: Useful flags:
402: 
403: - `--target <name>`: run only selected corpus targets
404: - `--out-dir <dir>`: keep results isolated from another run
405: - `--no-cargo-clean`: reuse an existing benchmark-owned Cargo target dir
406: - `--warmups <n>` and `--runs <n>`: control benchmark length
407: - `--min-win-pct <pct>`: required sold win threshold
408: 
409: ## Template Instantiation Strategy
410: 
411: This codebase relies heavily on target-templated source files. To avoid
412: compiling one giant all-target translation unit, `CMakeLists.txt` generates
413: per-target wrapper `.cc` files in the build directory. Each generated file:
414: 
415: - defines `MOLD_<TARGET>`
416: - defines `MOLD_TARGET <TARGET>`
417: - includes the real source file from the source tree
418: 
419: Never edit those generated files directly.
420: 
421: Current instantiated target lists:
422: 
423: - ELF targets:
424:   `X86_64`, `I386`, `ARM64`, `ARM32`, `RV32LE`, `RV32BE`, `RV64LE`,
425:   `RV64BE`, `PPC32`, `PPC64V1`, `PPC64V2`, `S390X`, `SPARC64`, `M68K`,
426:   `SH4`, `ALPHA`
427: - Mach-O targets:
428:   `X86_64`, `ARM64`, `ARM64_32`
429: 
430: Current instantiated template counts from `CMakeLists.txt`:
431: 
432: - 16 ELF template source files x 16 ELF targets = 256 generated ELF
433:   translation units
434: - 11 Mach-O template source files x 3 Mach-O targets = 33 generated Mach-O
435:   translation units when sold is enabled
436: 
437: Non-template source details that matter:
438: 
439: - `macho/arch-x86-64.cc` is added as a non-template source
440: - `macho/yaml.cc` is added as a non-template source
441: 
442: If you add a new templated source file or target, you almost certainly need
443: to update these lists in `CMakeLists.txt`.
444: 
445: ## Execution Model, Memory, And Performance
446: 
447: Concurrency model:
448: 
449: - the codebase uses Intel oneTBB extensively, especially `parallel_for` and
450:   `parallel_for_each`
451: - high-level linker stages are orchestrated serially
452: - the heavy work inside most stages is parallelized over files, sections,
453:   relocations, and synthesized records
454: - shared state uses a mix of relaxed-order `Atomic<T>`, TBB concurrent
455:   containers, and selective locking
456: - `common/main.cc` caps the default thread count at
457:   `min(tbb max allowed parallelism, 32)`
458: - ELF exposes thread-count control through `--thread-count`
459: - Mach-O exposes thread-count control through `-thread_count`
460: - performance instrumentation uses `Timer` and `Counter`
461: 
462: I/O behavior:
463: 
464: - input files are memory-mapped via `MappedFile`
465: - regular Unix output files normally use `MemoryMappedOutputFile`
466: - special outputs such as stdout or non-regular files use a malloc-backed
467:   output path instead
468: - Windows output uses the Win32-specific output implementation
469: - mapfiles are separate outputs and are not part of the main mmap-backed image
470: - because output may be memory-mapped, `common/main.cc` installs signal
471:   handling to turn disk-full write faults into a clearer fatal message
472: 
473: ## Local Coding Conventions
474: 
475: The canonical style document is `docs/coding-guidelines.md`. Follow it unless
476: surrounding code has a stronger local pattern.
477: 
478: Key rules and caveats:
479: 
480: - prefer `i64` for integers unless object size matters enough to justify a
481:   smaller type
482: - avoid `auto` unless the actual type is obvious in immediate context
483: - keep inheritance shallow
484: - use `ASSERT` for always-on checks; `assert` still exists for debug-only
485:   assumptions
486: - prefer existing diagnostics helpers (`SyncOut`, `Warn`, `Error`, `Fatal`)
487:   over ad hoc output code
488: - prefer existing mapped-input and mapped-output helpers over introducing new
489:   file I/O patterns
490: - preserve the current data-oriented style: explicit loops, explicit types,
491:   and direct manipulation of linker state are normal here
492: 
493: ## Agent-Specific Gotchas
494: 
495: - `README.md` is not enough. Always verify behavior in code and CMake.
496: - Because `LICENSE.md` exists in this checkout, Mach-O code and sold install
497:   aliases are active here even though upstream mold can be ELF-only.
498: - If you change a templated source file, expect a wide rebuild because of
499:   per-target instantiation.
500: - If you add a new user-facing linker option, you usually need matching test
501:   coverage and possibly help-text or manpage updates.
502: - If you touch subprocess, wrapper, or Linux packaging logic, review
503:   `elf/subprocess.cc`, `elf/mold-wrapper.c`, `mold-wrapper.so` handling, and
504:   `dist.sh` together.
505: - If you touch Mach-O symbol resolution, always keep Rust replay in mind.
506:   Recent failures were not obvious from small synthetic tests alone.
507: - The worktree currently contains local helper files such as `CLAUDE.md`,
508:   `justfile`, `repomix.config.json`, and `sold.txt`. Do not assume they are
509:   upstream project files, but do not delete or overwrite them casually
510:   either.

================
File: CLAUDE.md
================
 1: # CLAUDE.md
 2: 
 3: This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
 4: 
 5: ## Project Overview
 6: 
 7: sold is the commercial version of mold, a high-performance linker. It supports both ELF (Linux) and Mach-O (macOS) targets. The project is written in C++20 with `-fno-exceptions`. The open-source mold repo only includes the ELF linker; this repo (sold) adds the Mach-O linker on top. The build system detects this via the presence of `LICENSE.md`.
 8: 
 9: ## Build Commands
10: 
11: ```bash
12: # Configure (out-of-source build required)
13: mkdir -p build && cd build
14: cmake -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release ..
15: 
16: # Build (uses all cores by default with template instantiation strategy)
17: cmake --build build
18: 
19: # Run tests (on macOS runs Mach-O tests, on Linux runs ELF tests)
20: cd build && ctest
21: 
22: # Run a single test by name
23: cd build && ctest -R <test-name>
24: 
25: # Build with sanitizers
26: cmake -DMOLD_USE_ASAN=ON ..   # AddressSanitizer
27: cmake -DMOLD_USE_TSAN=ON ..   # ThreadSanitizer
28: ```
29: 
30: The binary is at `build/mold`. Symlinks `build/ld` and `build/ld64` are created automatically for testing.
31: 
32: ## Architecture
33: 
34: ### Source Layout
35: 
36: - **`common/`** - Shared utilities: entry point (`main.cc`), compression, demangling, file I/O, concurrent data structures, timers. `common.h` defines core types (`i64`, custom `Atomic<T>` with relaxed ordering, `ConcurrentMap`, `MappedFile`, etc.).
37: - **`elf/`** - ELF linker. 16 architecture backends (X86_64, I386, ARM64, ARM32, RV32/64 LE/BE, PPC32, PPC64V1/V2, S390X, SPARC64, M68K, SH4, ALPHA). Key files: `main.cc` (entry), `cmdline.cc` (arg parsing), `passes.cc` (linker passes), `input-files.cc`, `output-chunks.cc`.
38: - **`macho/`** - Mach-O linker (sold-only). 3 architecture backends (X86_64, ARM64, ARM64_32). `mold.h` contains all Mach-O context and data structures.
39: - **`test/elf/`** and **`test/macho/`** - Shell-script-based tests integrated with CTest.
40: 
41: ### Template Instantiation Strategy
42: 
43: Almost all functions are templated on target architecture type (e.g., `X86_64`, `ARM64`). To parallelize compilation, CMakeLists.txt generates a separate `.cc` file per (source, target) pair that `#define`s the target and `#include`s the real source. This means there are ~256 ELF compilation units and ~33 Mach-O units from template files alone.
44: 
45: ### Concurrency Model
46: 
47: mold uses data parallelism via Intel TBB's `parallel_for_each`. Linker passes run serially, but each pass parallelizes internally over input data (files, sections, relocations). Shared state uses atomic variables (custom `Atomic<T>` with relaxed ordering by default) and TBB concurrent hashmaps. No channels, futures, or high-level synchronization primitives.
48: 
49: ### Key Dependencies (bundled in `third-party/`)
50: 
51: TBB (threading), mimalloc (allocator, non-Apple 64-bit only), zlib/zstd (compression), xxhash (hashing), rust-demangle.
52: 
53: ## Coding Conventions
54: 
55: - Use `i64` for integers by default (alias for `int64_t`); use smaller types only when allocating millions of objects.
56: - Do not use `auto` unless the type is obvious from immediate context. Currently `auto` is used only for lambdas.
57: - Keep class hierarchies shallow (max depth of 2).
58: - `ASSERT` is always-enabled; `assert` is debug-only.
59: - All file I/O is mmap-based (`MappedFile`).

================
File: dist.sh
================
 1: #!/bin/bash
 2: # This script creates a mold binary distribution. The output is
 3: # written in this directory as `mold-$version-$arch-linux.tar.gz`
 4: # (e.g. `mold-1.0.3-x86_64-linux.tar.gz`).
 5: #
 6: # The mold executable created by this script is statically-linked to
 7: # libstdc++ and libcrypto but dynamically-linked to libc, libm, libz
 8: # and librt, as they almost always exist on any Linux systems.
 9: 
10: case $# in
11: 0)
12:   arch=$(uname -m)
13:   [[ $arch = arm* ]] && arch=arm
14:   ;;
15: 1)
16:   arch="$1"
17:   ;;
18: *)
19:   echo "Usage: $0 [ x86_64 | aarch64 | arm | ppc64le | s390x ]"
20:   exit 1
21: esac
22: 
23: echo "$arch" | grep -Eq '^(x86_64|aarch64|arm|ppc64le|s390x)$' || \
24:   { echo "Error: no docker image for $arch"; exit 1; }
25: 
26: version=$(sed -n 's/^project(mold VERSION \(.*\))/\1/p' $(dirname $0)/CMakeLists.txt)
27: dest=mold-$version-$arch-linux
28: set -e -x
29: 
30: docker run --platform linux/$arch -i --rm -v "$(pwd):/mold" \
31:   -e "OWNER=$(id -u):$(id -g)" rui314/mold-builder:latest \
32:   bash -c "mkdir /tmp/build &&
33: cd /tmp/build &&
34: cmake -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=gcc-10 -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=g++-10 -DMOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC=On -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release /mold &&
35: cmake --build . -j\$(nproc) &&
36: [ $arch = arm ] || ctest -j\$(nproc) &&
37: cmake --install . --prefix $dest --strip &&
38: tar czf /mold/$dest.tar.gz $dest &&
39: cp mold mold-wrapper.so /mold &&
40: chown \$OWNER /mold/mold /mold/mold-wrapper.so /mold/$dest.tar.gz"

================
File: elf/arch-x86-64.cc
================
  1: // Supporting x86-64 is straightforward. Unlike its predecessor, i386,
  2: // x86-64 supports PC-relative addressing for position-independent code.
  3: // Being CISC, its instructions are variable in size. Branch instructions
  4: // take 4 bytes offsets, so we don't need range extension thunks.
  5: //
  6: // The psABI specifies %r11 as neither caller- nor callee-saved. It's
  7: // intentionally left out so that we can use it as a scratch register in
  8: // PLT.
  9: //
 10: // Thread Pointer (TP) is stored not to a general-purpose register but to
 11: // FS segment register. Segment register is a 64-bits register which can
 12: // be used as a base address for memory access. Each thread has a unique
 13: // FS value, and they access their thread-local variables relative to FS
 14: // as %fs:offset_from_tp.
 15: //
 16: // The value of a segment register itself is not generally readable from
 17: // the user space. As a workaround, libc initializes %fs:0 (the first word
 18: // referenced by FS) to the value of %fs itself. So we can obtain TP just
 19: // by `mov %fs:0, %rax` if we need it.
 20: //
 21: // For historical reasons, TP points past the end of the TLS block on x86.
 22: // This is contrary to other psABIs which usually use the beginning of the
 23: // TLS block as TP (with some addend). As a result, offsets from TP to
 24: // thread-local variables (TLVs) in the main executable are all negative.
 25: //
 26: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/x86-64.pdf
 27: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/i386.pdf
 28: 
 29: #include "mold.h"
 30: 
 31: namespace mold::elf {
 32: 
 33: using E = X86_64;
 34: 
 35: // This is a security-enhanced version of the regular PLT. The PLT
 36: // header and each PLT entry starts with endbr64 for the Intel's
 37: // control-flow enforcement security mechanism.
 38: //
 39: // Note that our IBT-enabled PLT instruction sequence is different
 40: // from the one used in GNU ld. GNU's IBTPLT implementation uses two
 41: // separate sections (.plt and .plt.sec) in which one PLT entry takes
 42: // 32 bytes in total. Our IBTPLT consists of just .plt and each entry
 43: // is 16 bytes long.
 44: //
 45: // Our PLT entry clobbers %r11, but that's fine because the resolver
 46: // function (_dl_runtime_resolve) clobbers %r11 anyway.
 47: template <>
 48: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 49:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 50:     0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfa, // endbr64
 51:     0x41, 0x53,             // push %r11
 52:     0xff, 0x35, 0, 0, 0, 0, // push GOTPLT+8(%rip)
 53:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *GOTPLT+16(%rip)
 54:     0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
 55:     0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
 56:     0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
 57:     0xcc, 0xcc,             // (padding)
 58:   };
 59: 
 60:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 61:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 8) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 4;
 62:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 14) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 2;
 63: }
 64: 
 65: template <>
 66: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 67:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 68:     0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfa, // endbr64
 69:     0x41, 0xbb, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov $index_in_relplt, %r11d
 70:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *foo@GOTPLT
 71:   };
 72: 
 73:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 74:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = sym.get_plt_idx(ctx);
 75:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 12) = sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 16;
 76: }
 77: 
 78: template <>
 79: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 80:   static const u8 insn[] = {
 81:     0xf3, 0x0f, 0x1e, 0xfa, // endbr64
 82:     0xff, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // jmp *foo@GOT
 83:     0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, 0xcc, // (padding)
 84:     0xcc, 0xcc,             // (padding)
 85:   };
 86: 
 87:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
 88:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 6) = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx) - 10;
 89: }
 90: 
 91: template <>
 92: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
 93:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
 94:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
 95: 
 96:   switch (rel.r_type) {
 97:   case R_NONE:
 98:     break;
 99:   case R_X86_64_32:
100:     *(ul32 *)loc = val;
101:     break;
102:   case R_X86_64_64:
103:     *(ul64 *)loc = val;
104:     break;
105:   case R_X86_64_PC32:
106:     *(ul32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
107:     break;
108:   case R_X86_64_PC64:
109:     *(ul64 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
110:     break;
111:   default:
112:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
113:   }
114: }
115: 
116: static u32 relax_gotpcrelx(u8 *loc) {
117:   switch ((loc[0] << 8) | loc[1]) {
118:   case 0xff15: return 0x90e8; // call *0(%rip) -> call 0
119:   case 0xff25: return 0x90e9; // jmp  *0(%rip) -> jmp  0
120:   }
121:   return 0;
122: }
123: 
124: static u32 relax_rex_gotpcrelx(u8 *loc) {
125:   switch ((loc[0] << 16) | (loc[1] << 8) | loc[2]) {
126:   case 0x488b05: return 0x488d05; // mov 0(%rip), %rax -> lea 0(%rip), %rax
127:   case 0x488b0d: return 0x488d0d; // mov 0(%rip), %rcx -> lea 0(%rip), %rcx
128:   case 0x488b15: return 0x488d15; // mov 0(%rip), %rdx -> lea 0(%rip), %rdx
129:   case 0x488b1d: return 0x488d1d; // mov 0(%rip), %rbx -> lea 0(%rip), %rbx
130:   case 0x488b25: return 0x488d25; // mov 0(%rip), %rsp -> lea 0(%rip), %rsp
131:   case 0x488b2d: return 0x488d2d; // mov 0(%rip), %rbp -> lea 0(%rip), %rbp
132:   case 0x488b35: return 0x488d35; // mov 0(%rip), %rsi -> lea 0(%rip), %rsi
133:   case 0x488b3d: return 0x488d3d; // mov 0(%rip), %rdi -> lea 0(%rip), %rdi
134:   case 0x4c8b05: return 0x4c8d05; // mov 0(%rip), %r8  -> lea 0(%rip), %r8
135:   case 0x4c8b0d: return 0x4c8d0d; // mov 0(%rip), %r9  -> lea 0(%rip), %r9
136:   case 0x4c8b15: return 0x4c8d15; // mov 0(%rip), %r10 -> lea 0(%rip), %r10
137:   case 0x4c8b1d: return 0x4c8d1d; // mov 0(%rip), %r11 -> lea 0(%rip), %r11
138:   case 0x4c8b25: return 0x4c8d25; // mov 0(%rip), %r12 -> lea 0(%rip), %r12
139:   case 0x4c8b2d: return 0x4c8d2d; // mov 0(%rip), %r13 -> lea 0(%rip), %r13
140:   case 0x4c8b35: return 0x4c8d35; // mov 0(%rip), %r14 -> lea 0(%rip), %r14
141:   case 0x4c8b3d: return 0x4c8d3d; // mov 0(%rip), %r15 -> lea 0(%rip), %r15
142:   }
143:   return 0;
144: }
145: 
146: static u32 relax_gottpoff(u8 *loc) {
147:   switch ((loc[0] << 16) | (loc[1] << 8) | loc[2]) {
148:   case 0x488b05: return 0x48c7c0; // mov 0(%rip), %rax -> mov $0, %rax
149:   case 0x488b0d: return 0x48c7c1; // mov 0(%rip), %rcx -> mov $0, %rcx
150:   case 0x488b15: return 0x48c7c2; // mov 0(%rip), %rdx -> mov $0, %rdx
151:   case 0x488b1d: return 0x48c7c3; // mov 0(%rip), %rbx -> mov $0, %rbx
152:   case 0x488b25: return 0x48c7c4; // mov 0(%rip), %rsp -> mov $0, %rsp
153:   case 0x488b2d: return 0x48c7c5; // mov 0(%rip), %rbp -> mov $0, %rbp
154:   case 0x488b35: return 0x48c7c6; // mov 0(%rip), %rsi -> mov $0, %rsi
155:   case 0x488b3d: return 0x48c7c7; // mov 0(%rip), %rdi -> mov $0, %rdi
156:   case 0x4c8b05: return 0x49c7c0; // mov 0(%rip), %r8  -> mov $0, %r8
157:   case 0x4c8b0d: return 0x49c7c1; // mov 0(%rip), %r9  -> mov $0, %r9
158:   case 0x4c8b15: return 0x49c7c2; // mov 0(%rip), %r10 -> mov $0, %r10
159:   case 0x4c8b1d: return 0x49c7c3; // mov 0(%rip), %r11 -> mov $0, %r11
160:   case 0x4c8b25: return 0x49c7c4; // mov 0(%rip), %r12 -> mov $0, %r12
161:   case 0x4c8b2d: return 0x49c7c5; // mov 0(%rip), %r13 -> mov $0, %r13
162:   case 0x4c8b35: return 0x49c7c6; // mov 0(%rip), %r14 -> mov $0, %r14
163:   case 0x4c8b3d: return 0x49c7c7; // mov 0(%rip), %r15 -> mov $0, %r15
164:   }
165:   return 0;
166: }
167: 
168: static u32 relax_gotpc32_tlsdesc(u8 *loc) {
169:   switch ((loc[0] << 16) | (loc[1] << 8) | loc[2]) {
170:   case 0x488d05: return 0x48c7c0; // lea 0(%rip), %rax -> mov $0, %rax
171:   case 0x488d0d: return 0x48c7c1; // lea 0(%rip), %rcx -> mov $0, %rcx
172:   case 0x488d15: return 0x48c7c2; // lea 0(%rip), %rdx -> mov $0, %rdx
173:   case 0x488d1d: return 0x48c7c3; // lea 0(%rip), %rbx -> mov $0, %rbx
174:   case 0x488d25: return 0x48c7c4; // lea 0(%rip), %rsp -> mov $0, %rsp
175:   case 0x488d2d: return 0x48c7c5; // lea 0(%rip), %rbp -> mov $0, %rbp
176:   case 0x488d35: return 0x48c7c6; // lea 0(%rip), %rsi -> mov $0, %rsi
177:   case 0x488d3d: return 0x48c7c7; // lea 0(%rip), %rdi -> mov $0, %rdi
178:   case 0x4c8d05: return 0x49c7c0; // lea 0(%rip), %r8  -> mov $0, %r8
179:   case 0x4c8d0d: return 0x49c7c1; // lea 0(%rip), %r9  -> mov $0, %r9
180:   case 0x4c8d15: return 0x49c7c2; // lea 0(%rip), %r10 -> mov $0, %r10
181:   case 0x4c8d1d: return 0x49c7c3; // lea 0(%rip), %r11 -> mov $0, %r11
182:   case 0x4c8d25: return 0x49c7c4; // lea 0(%rip), %r12 -> mov $0, %r12
183:   case 0x4c8d2d: return 0x49c7c5; // lea 0(%rip), %r13 -> mov $0, %r13
184:   case 0x4c8d35: return 0x49c7c6; // lea 0(%rip), %r14 -> mov $0, %r14
185:   case 0x4c8d3d: return 0x49c7c7; // lea 0(%rip), %r15 -> mov $0, %r15
186:   }
187:   return 0;
188: }
189: 
190: // Rewrite a function call to __tls_get_addr to a cheaper instruction
191: // sequence. We can do this when we know the thread-local variable's TP-
192: // relative address at link-time.
193: static void relax_gd_to_le(u8 *loc, ElfRel<E> rel, u64 val) {
194:   switch (rel.r_type) {
195:   case R_X86_64_PLT32:
196:   case R_X86_64_PC32:
197:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL:
198:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX: {
199:     // The original instructions are the following:
200:     //
201:     //  66 48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00    lea  foo@tlsgd(%rip), %rdi
202:     //  66 66 48 e8 00 00 00 00    call __tls_get_addr
203:     //
204:     // or
205:     //
206:     //  66 48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00    lea foo@tlsgd(%rip), %rdi
207:     //  66 48 ff 15 00 00 00 00    call *__tls_get_addr@GOT(%rip)
208:     static const u8 insn[] = {
209:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x04, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %fs:0, %rax
210:       0x48, 0x81, 0xc0, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // add $tp_offset, %rax
211:     };
212:     memcpy(loc - 4, insn, sizeof(insn));
213:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 8) = val;
214:     break;
215:   }
216:   case R_X86_64_PLTOFF64: {
217:     // The original instructions are the following:
218:     //
219:     //  48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00           lea    foo@tlsgd(%rip), %rdi
220:     //  48 b8 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00  movabs __tls_get_addr, %rax
221:     //  48 01 d8                       add    %rbx, %rax
222:     //  ff d0                          call   *%rax
223:     static const u8 insn[] = {
224:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x04, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %fs:0, %rax
225:       0x48, 0x81, 0xc0, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // add $tp_offset, %rax
226:       0x66, 0x0f, 0x1f, 0x44, 0x00, 0x00,       // nop
227:     };
228:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
229:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 9) = val;
230:     break;
231:   }
232:   default:
233:     unreachable();
234:   }
235: }
236: 
237: static void relax_gd_to_ie(u8 *loc, ElfRel<E> rel, u64 val) {
238:   switch (rel.r_type) {
239:   case R_X86_64_PLT32:
240:   case R_X86_64_PC32:
241:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL:
242:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX: {
243:     static const u8 insn[] = {
244:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x04, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %fs:0, %rax
245:       0x48, 0x03, 0x05, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // add foo@gottpoff(%rip), %rax
246:     };
247:     memcpy(loc - 4, insn, sizeof(insn));
248:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 8) = val - 12;
249:     break;
250:   }
251:   case R_X86_64_PLTOFF64: {
252:     static const u8 insn[] = {
253:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x04, 0x25, 0, 0, 0, 0, // mov %fs:0, %rax
254:       0x48, 0x03, 0x05, 0, 0, 0, 0,             // add foo@gottpoff(%rip), %rax
255:       0x66, 0x0f, 0x1f, 0x44, 0x00, 0x00,       // nop
256:     };
257:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
258:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 9) = val - 13;
259:     break;
260:   }
261:   default:
262:     unreachable();
263:   }
264: }
265: 
266: // Rewrite a function call to __tls_get_addr to a cheaper instruction
267: // sequence. The difference from relax_gd_to_le is that we are
268: // materializing a Dynamic Thread Pointer for the current ELF module
269: // instead of an address for a particular thread-local variable.
270: static void relax_ld_to_le(u8 *loc, ElfRel<E> rel, u64 val) {
271:   switch (rel.r_type) {
272:   case R_X86_64_PLT32:
273:   case R_X86_64_PC32: {
274:     // The original instructions are the following:
275:     //
276:     //  48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00    lea    foo@tlsld(%rip), %rdi
277:     //  e8 00 00 00 00          call   __tls_get_addr
278:     static const u8 insn[] = {
279:       0x31, 0xc0,                   // xor %eax, %eax
280:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x00,       // mov %fs:(%rax), %rax
281:       0x48, 0x2d, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // sub $tls_size, %rax
282:     };
283:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
284:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
285:     break;
286:   }
287:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL:
288:   case R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX: {
289:     // The original instructions are the following:
290:     //
291:     //  48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00    lea    foo@tlsld(%rip), %rdi
292:     //  ff 15 00 00 00 00       call   *__tls_get_addr@GOT(%rip)
293:     static const u8 insn[] = {
294:       0x31, 0xc0,                   // xor %eax, %eax
295:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x00,       // mov %fs:(%rax), %rax
296:       0x48, 0x2d, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // sub $tls_size, %rax
297:       0x90,                         // nop
298:     };
299:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
300:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
301:     break;
302:   }
303:   case R_X86_64_PLTOFF64: {
304:     // The original instructions are the following:
305:     //
306:     //  48 8d 3d 00 00 00 00           lea    foo@tlsld(%rip), %rdi
307:     //  48 b8 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00  movabs __tls_get_addr@GOTOFF, %rax
308:     //  48 01 d8                       add    %rbx, %rax
309:     //  ff d0                          call   *%rax
310:     static const u8 insn[] = {
311:       0x31, 0xc0,                   // xor %eax, %eax
312:       0x64, 0x48, 0x8b, 0x00,       // mov %fs:(%rax), %rax
313:       0x48, 0x2d, 0, 0, 0, 0,       // sub $tls_size, %rax
314:       0x0f, 0x1f, 0x44, 0x00, 0x00, // nop
315:       0x0f, 0x1f, 0x44, 0x00, 0x00, // nop
316:     };
317:     memcpy(loc - 3, insn, sizeof(insn));
318:     *(ul32 *)(loc + 5) = val;
319:     break;
320:   }
321:   default:
322:     unreachable();
323:   }
324: }
325: 
326: // Apply relocations to SHF_ALLOC sections (i.e. sections that are
327: // mapped to memory at runtime) based on the result of
328: // scan_relocations().
329: template <>
330: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
331:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
332: 
333:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
334:   if (ctx.reldyn)
335:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
336:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
337: 
338:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
339:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
340:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
341:       continue;
342: 
343:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
344:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
345: 
346:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
347:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
348:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
349:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
350:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
351:     };
352: 
353:     auto write32 = [&](u64 val) {
354:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
355:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
356:     };
357: 
358:     auto write32s = [&](u64 val) {
359:       check(val, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
360:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
361:     };
362: 
363:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
364:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
365:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
366:     u64 G = sym.get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr;
367:     u64 GOTPLT = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr;
368: 
369:     switch (rel.r_type) {
370:     case R_X86_64_8:
371:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
372:       *loc = S + A;
373:       break;
374:     case R_X86_64_16:
375:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
376:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A;
377:       break;
378:     case R_X86_64_32:
379:       write32(S + A);
380:       break;
381:     case R_X86_64_32S:
382:       write32s(S + A);
383:       break;
384:     case R_X86_64_64:
385:       apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
386:       break;
387:     case R_X86_64_PC8:
388:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 7), 1 << 7);
389:       *loc = S + A - P;
390:       break;
391:     case R_X86_64_PC16:
392:       check(S + A - P, -(1 << 15), 1 << 15);
393:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A - P;
394:       break;
395:     case R_X86_64_PC32:
396:     case R_X86_64_PLT32:
397:       write32s(S + A - P);
398:       break;
399:     case R_X86_64_PC64:
400:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - P;
401:       break;
402:     case R_X86_64_GOT32:
403:       write32s(G + A);
404:       break;
405:     case R_X86_64_GOT64:
406:       *(ul64 *)loc = G + A;
407:       break;
408:     case R_X86_64_GOTOFF64:
409:     case R_X86_64_PLTOFF64:
410:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - GOTPLT;
411:       break;
412:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC32:
413:       write32s(GOTPLT + A - P);
414:       break;
415:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC64:
416:       *(ul64 *)loc = GOTPLT + A - P;
417:       break;
418:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL:
419:       write32s(G + GOTPLT + A - P);
420:       break;
421:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL64:
422:       *(ul64 *)loc = G + GOTPLT + A - P;
423:       break;
424:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX:
425:       // We always want to relax GOTPCRELX relocs even if --no-relax
426:       // was given because some static PIE runtime code depends on these
427:       // relaxations.
428:       if (!sym.is_imported && !sym.is_ifunc() && sym.is_relative()) {
429:         u32 insn = relax_gotpcrelx(loc - 2);
430:         i64 val = S + A - P;
431:         if (insn && (i32)val == val) {
432:           loc[-2] = insn >> 8;
433:           loc[-1] = insn;
434:           *(ul32 *)loc = val;
435:           break;
436:         }
437:       }
438:       write32s(G + GOTPLT + A - P);
439:       break;
440:     case R_X86_64_REX_GOTPCRELX:
441:       if (!sym.is_imported && !sym.is_ifunc() && sym.is_relative()) {
442:         u32 insn = relax_rex_gotpcrelx(loc - 3);
443:         i64 val = S + A - P;
444:         if (insn && (i32)val == val) {
445:           loc[-3] = insn >> 16;
446:           loc[-2] = insn >> 8;
447:           loc[-1] = insn;
448:           *(ul32 *)loc = val;
449:           break;
450:         }
451:       }
452:       write32s(G + GOTPLT + A - P);
453:       break;
454:     case R_X86_64_TLSGD:
455:       if (sym.has_tlsgd(ctx)) {
456:         write32s(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) + A - P);
457:       } else if (sym.has_gottp(ctx)) {
458:         relax_gd_to_ie(loc, rels[i + 1], sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - P);
459:         i++;
460:       } else {
461:         relax_gd_to_le(loc, rels[i + 1], S - ctx.tp_addr);
462:         i++;
463:       }
464:       break;
465:     case R_X86_64_TLSLD:
466:       if (ctx.got->has_tlsld(ctx)) {
467:         write32s(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) + A - P);
468:       } else {
469:         relax_ld_to_le(loc, rels[i + 1], ctx.tp_addr - ctx.tls_begin);
470:         i++;
471:       }
472:       break;
473:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF32:
474:       write32s(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
475:       break;
476:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF64:
477:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
478:       break;
479:     case R_X86_64_TPOFF32:
480:       write32s(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
481:       break;
482:     case R_X86_64_TPOFF64:
483:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.tp_addr;
484:       break;
485:     case R_X86_64_GOTTPOFF:
486:       if (sym.has_gottp(ctx)) {
487:         write32s(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) + A - P);
488:       } else {
489:         u32 insn = relax_gottpoff(loc - 3);
490:         loc[-3] = insn >> 16;
491:         loc[-2] = insn >> 8;
492:         loc[-1] = insn;
493:         write32s(S - ctx.tp_addr);
494:         assert(A == -4);
495:       }
496:       break;
497:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC32_TLSDESC:
498:       if (sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
499:         write32s(sym.get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx) + A - P);
500:       } else {
501:         u32 insn = relax_gotpc32_tlsdesc(loc - 3);
502:         loc[-3] = insn >> 16;
503:         loc[-2] = insn >> 8;
504:         loc[-1] = insn;
505:         write32s(S - ctx.tp_addr);
506:         assert(A == -4);
507:       }
508:       break;
509:     case R_X86_64_SIZE32:
510:       write32(sym.esym().st_size + A);
511:       break;
512:     case R_X86_64_SIZE64:
513:       *(ul64 *)loc = sym.esym().st_size + A;
514:       break;
515:     case R_X86_64_TLSDESC_CALL:
516:       if (!sym.has_tlsdesc(ctx)) {
517:         // call *(%rax) -> nop
518:         loc[0] = 0x66;
519:         loc[1] = 0x90;
520:       }
521:       break;
522:     default:
523:       unreachable();
524:     }
525:   }
526: }
527: 
528: // This function is responsible for applying relocations against
529: // non-SHF_ALLOC sections (i.e. sections that are not mapped to memory
530: // at runtime).
531: //
532: // Relocations against non-SHF_ALLOC sections are much easier to
533: // handle than that against SHF_ALLOC sections. It is because, since
534: // they are not mapped to memory, they don't contain any variable or
535: // function and never need PLT or GOT. Non-SHF_ALLOC sections are
536: // mostly debug info sections.
537: //
538: // Relocations against non-SHF_ALLOC sections are not scanned by
539: // scan_relocations.
540: template <>
541: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
542:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
543: 
544:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
545:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
546:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
547:       continue;
548: 
549:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
550:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
551: 
552:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
553:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
554:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
555:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
556:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
557:     };
558: 
559:     auto write32 = [&](u64 val) {
560:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
561:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
562:     };
563: 
564:     auto write32s = [&](u64 val) {
565:       check(val, -(1LL << 31), 1LL << 31);
566:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
567:     };
568: 
569:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
570:     i64 frag_addend;
571:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
572: 
573:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
574:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
575: 
576:     switch (rel.r_type) {
577:     case R_X86_64_8:
578:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 8);
579:       *loc = S + A;
580:       break;
581:     case R_X86_64_16:
582:       check(S + A, 0, 1 << 16);
583:       *(ul16 *)loc = S + A;
584:       break;
585:     case R_X86_64_32:
586:       write32(S + A);
587:       break;
588:     case R_X86_64_32S:
589:       write32s(S + A);
590:       break;
591:     case R_X86_64_64:
592:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
593:         *(ul64 *)loc = *val;
594:       else
595:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A;
596:       break;
597:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF32:
598:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
599:         *(ul32 *)loc = *val;
600:       else
601:         write32s(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
602:       break;
603:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF64:
604:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
605:         *(ul64 *)loc = *val;
606:       else
607:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
608:       break;
609:     case R_X86_64_GOTOFF64:
610:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr;
611:       break;
612:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC64:
613:       // PC-relative relocation doesn't make sense for non-memory-allocated
614:       // section, but GCC 6.3.0 seems to create this reloc for
615:       // _GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_.
616:       *(ul64 *)loc = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr + A;
617:       break;
618:     case R_X86_64_SIZE32:
619:       write32(sym.esym().st_size + A);
620:       break;
621:     case R_X86_64_SIZE64:
622:       *(ul64 *)loc = sym.esym().st_size + A;
623:       break;
624:     default:
625:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
626:                  << rel;
627:       break;
628:     }
629:   }
630: }
631: 
632: // Linker has to create data structures in an output file to apply
633: // some type of relocations. For example, if a relocation refers a GOT
634: // or a PLT entry of a symbol, linker has to create an entry in .got
635: // or in .plt for that symbol. In order to fix the file layout, we
636: // need to scan relocations.
637: template <>
638: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
639:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
640: 
641:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
642:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
643: 
644:   // Scan relocations
645:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
646:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
647:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
648:       continue;
649: 
650:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
651:     u8 *loc = (u8 *)(contents.data() + rel.r_offset);
652: 
653:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
654:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
655: 
656:     switch (rel.r_type) {
657:     case R_X86_64_8:
658:     case R_X86_64_16:
659:     case R_X86_64_32:
660:     case R_X86_64_32S:
661:       scan_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
662:       break;
663:     case R_X86_64_64:
664:       scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
665:       break;
666:     case R_X86_64_PC8:
667:     case R_X86_64_PC16:
668:     case R_X86_64_PC32:
669:     case R_X86_64_PC64:
670:       scan_pcrel(ctx, sym, rel);
671:       break;
672:     case R_X86_64_GOT32:
673:     case R_X86_64_GOT64:
674:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC32:
675:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC64:
676:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL:
677:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCREL64:
678:     case R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX:
679:     case R_X86_64_REX_GOTPCRELX:
680:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
681:       break;
682:     case R_X86_64_PLT32:
683:     case R_X86_64_PLTOFF64:
684:       if (sym.is_imported)
685:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
686:       break;
687:     case R_X86_64_TLSGD:
688:       if (rel.r_addend != -4)
689:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad r_addend for R_X86_64_TLSGD";
690: 
691:       if (i + 1 == rels.size())
692:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLSGD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOTPCREL";
693: 
694:       if (u32 ty = rels[i + 1].r_type;
695:           ty != R_X86_64_PLT32 && ty != R_X86_64_PC32 &&
696:           ty != R_X86_64_PLTOFF64 && ty != R_X86_64_GOTPCREL &&
697:           ty != R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX)
698:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLSGD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOTPCREL";
699: 
700:       if (ctx.arg.is_static ||
701:           (ctx.arg.relax && !sym.is_imported && !ctx.arg.shared)) {
702:         // We always relax if -static because libc.a doesn't contain
703:         // __tls_get_addr().
704:         i++;
705:       } else if (ctx.arg.relax && !sym.is_imported && ctx.arg.shared &&
706:                  !ctx.arg.z_dlopen) {
707:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
708:         i++;
709:       } else {
710:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
711:       }
712:       break;
713:     case R_X86_64_TLSLD:
714:       if (rel.r_addend != -4)
715:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad r_addend for R_X86_64_TLSLD";
716: 
717:       if (i + 1 == rels.size())
718:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLSLD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOTPCREL";
719: 
720:       if (u32 ty = rels[i + 1].r_type;
721:           ty != R_X86_64_PLT32 && ty != R_X86_64_PC32 &&
722:           ty != R_X86_64_PLTOFF64 && ty != R_X86_64_GOTPCREL &&
723:           ty != R_X86_64_GOTPCRELX)
724:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": TLSLD reloc must be followed by PLT or GOTPCREL";
725: 
726:       // We always relax if -static because libc.a doesn't contain
727:       // __tls_get_addr().
728:       if (ctx.arg.is_static || (ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared))
729:         i++;
730:       else
731:         ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
732:       break;
733:     case R_X86_64_GOTTPOFF: {
734:       if (rel.r_addend != -4)
735:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad r_addend for R_X86_64_GOTTPOFF";
736: 
737:       bool do_relax = ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared &&
738:                       !sym.is_imported && relax_gottpoff(loc - 3);
739:       if (!do_relax)
740:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
741:       break;
742:     }
743:     case R_X86_64_GOTPC32_TLSDESC: {
744:       if (rel.r_addend != -4)
745:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad r_addend for R_X86_64_GOTPC32_TLSDESC";
746: 
747:       if (relax_gotpc32_tlsdesc(loc - 3) == 0)
748:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": GOTPC32_TLSDESC relocation is used"
749:                    << " against an invalid code sequence";
750: 
751:       if (!relax_tlsdesc(ctx, sym))
752:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSDESC;
753:       break;
754:     }
755:     case R_X86_64_TPOFF32:
756:     case R_X86_64_TPOFF64:
757:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
758:       break;
759:     case R_X86_64_GOTOFF64:
760:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF32:
761:     case R_X86_64_DTPOFF64:
762:     case R_X86_64_SIZE32:
763:     case R_X86_64_SIZE64:
764:     case R_X86_64_TLSDESC_CALL:
765:       break;
766:     default:
767:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
768:     }
769:   }
770: }
771: 
772: } // namespace mold::elf

 122: template <typename E>
 123: inline std::string stt_to_string(u32 st_type) {
 124:   switch (st_type) {
 125:   case STT_NOTYPE: return "STT_NOTYPE";
 126:   case STT_OBJECT: return "STT_OBJECT";
 127:   case STT_FUNC: return "STT_FUNC";
 128:   case STT_SECTION: return "STT_SECTION";
 129:   case STT_FILE: return "STT_FILE";
 130:   case STT_COMMON: return "STT_COMMON";
 131:   case STT_TLS: return "STT_TLS";
 132:   case STT_GNU_IFUNC: return "STT_GNU_IFUNC";
 133:   }
 134: 
 135:   if constexpr (std::is_same_v<E, SPARC64>)
 136:     if (st_type == STT_SPARC_REGISTER)
 137:       return "STT_SPARC_REGISTER";
 138: 
 139:   return "unknown st_type (" + std::to_string(st_type) + ")";
 140: }

1324: 
1325: //
1326: // ELF types
1327: //
1328: 
1329: template <typename E> using I16 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, il16, ib16>;
1330: template <typename E> using I32 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, il32, ib32>;
1331: template <typename E> using I64 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, il64, ib64>;
1332: template <typename E> using U16 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, ul16, ub16>;
1333: template <typename E> using U24 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, ul24, ub24>;
1334: template <typename E> using U32 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, ul32, ub32>;
1335: template <typename E> using U64 = std::conditional_t<E::is_le, ul64, ub64>;
1336: 
1337: template <typename E> using Word = std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U64<E>, U32<E>>;
1338: template <typename E> using IWord = std::conditional_t<E::is_64, I64<E>, I32<E>>;
1339: 
1340: template <typename E> requires E::is_64
1341: struct ElfSym<E> {
1342:   bool is_undef() const { return st_shndx == SHN_UNDEF; }
1343:   bool is_abs() const { return st_shndx == SHN_ABS; }
1344:   bool is_common() const { return st_shndx == SHN_COMMON; }
1345:   bool is_weak() const { return st_bind == STB_WEAK; }
1346:   bool is_undef_weak() const { return is_undef() && is_weak(); }
1347: 
1348:   U32<E> st_name;
1349: 
1350: #ifdef __LITTLE_ENDIAN__
1351:   u8 st_type : 4;
1352:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1353:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1354:   u8 : 6;
1355: #else
1356:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1357:   u8 st_type : 4;
1358:   u8 : 6;
1359:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1360: #endif
1361: 
1362:   U16<E> st_shndx;
1363:   U64<E> st_value;
1364:   U64<E> st_size;
1365: };
1366: 
1367: template <typename E> requires (!E::is_64)
1368: struct ElfSym<E> {
1369:   bool is_undef() const { return st_shndx == SHN_UNDEF; }
1370:   bool is_abs() const { return st_shndx == SHN_ABS; }
1371:   bool is_common() const { return st_shndx == SHN_COMMON; }
1372:   bool is_weak() const { return st_bind == STB_WEAK; }
1373:   bool is_undef_weak() const { return is_undef() && is_weak(); }
1374: 
1375:   U32<E> st_name;
1376:   U32<E> st_value;
1377:   U32<E> st_size;
1378: 
1379: #ifdef __LITTLE_ENDIAN__
1380:   u8 st_type : 4;
1381:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1382:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1383:   u8 : 6;
1384: #else
1385:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1386:   u8 st_type : 4;
1387:   u8 : 6;
1388:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1389: #endif
1390: 
1391:   U16<E> st_shndx;
1392: };
1393: 
1394: template <typename E>
1395: struct ElfShdr {
1396:   U32<E> sh_name;
1397:   U32<E> sh_type;
1398:   Word<E> sh_flags;
1399:   Word<E> sh_addr;
1400:   Word<E> sh_offset;
1401:   Word<E> sh_size;
1402:   U32<E> sh_link;
1403:   U32<E> sh_info;
1404:   Word<E> sh_addralign;
1405:   Word<E> sh_entsize;
1406: };
1407: 
1408: template <typename E>
1409: struct ElfEhdr {
1410:   u8 e_ident[16];
1411:   U16<E> e_type;
1412:   U16<E> e_machine;
1413:   U32<E> e_version;
1414:   Word<E> e_entry;
1415:   Word<E> e_phoff;
1416:   Word<E> e_shoff;
1417:   U32<E> e_flags;
1418:   U16<E> e_ehsize;
1419:   U16<E> e_phentsize;
1420:   U16<E> e_phnum;
1421:   U16<E> e_shentsize;
1422:   U16<E> e_shnum;
1423:   U16<E> e_shstrndx;
1424: };
1425: 
1426: template <typename E> requires E::is_64
1427: struct ElfPhdr<E> {
1428:   U32<E> p_type;
1429:   U32<E> p_flags;
1430:   U64<E> p_offset;
1431:   U64<E> p_vaddr;
1432:   U64<E> p_paddr;
1433:   U64<E> p_filesz;
1434:   U64<E> p_memsz;
1435:   U64<E> p_align;
1436: };
1437: 
1438: template <typename E> requires (!E::is_64)
1439: struct ElfPhdr<E> {
1440:   U32<E> p_type;
1441:   U32<E> p_offset;
1442:   U32<E> p_vaddr;
1443:   U32<E> p_paddr;
1444:   U32<E> p_filesz;
1445:   U32<E> p_memsz;
1446:   U32<E> p_flags;
1447:   U32<E> p_align;
1448: };
1449: 
1450: // Depending on the target, ElfRel may or may not contain r_addend member.
1451: // The relocation record containing r_addend is called RELA, and that
1452: // without r_addend is called REL.
1453: //
1454: // If REL, relocation addends are stored as parts of section contents.
1455: // That means we add a computed value to an existing value when writing a
1456: // relocated value if REL. If RELA, we just overwrite an existing value
1457: // with a newly computed value.
1458: //
1459: // We don't want to have too many `if (REL)`s and `if (RELA)`s in our
1460: // codebase, so ElfRel always takes r_addend as a constructor argument.
1461: // If it's REL, the argument will simply be ignored.
1462: template <typename E> requires E::is_le && E::is_rela
1463: struct ElfRel<E> {
1464:   ElfRel() = default;
1465:   ElfRel(u64 offset, u32 type, u32 sym, i64 addend)
1466:     : r_offset(offset), r_type(type), r_sym(sym), r_addend(addend) {}
1467: 
1468:   Word<E> r_offset;
1469:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, u8> r_type;
1470:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, U24<E>> r_sym;
1471:   IWord<E> r_addend;
1472: };
1473: 
1474: template <typename E> requires (!E::is_le) && E::is_rela
1475: struct ElfRel<E> {
1476:   ElfRel() = default;
1477:   ElfRel(u64 offset, u32 type, u32 sym, i64 addend)
1478:     : r_offset(offset), r_sym(sym), r_type(type), r_addend(addend) {}
1479: 
1480:   Word<E> r_offset;
1481:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, U24<E>> r_sym;
1482:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, u8> r_type;
1483:   IWord<E> r_addend;
1484: };
1485: 
1486: template <typename E> requires E::is_le && (!E::is_rela)
1487: struct ElfRel<E> {
1488:   ElfRel() = default;
1489:   ElfRel(u64 offset, u32 type, u32 sym, i64 addend = 0)
1490:     : r_offset(offset), r_type(type), r_sym(sym) {}
1491: 
1492:   Word<E> r_offset;
1493:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, u8> r_type;
1494:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, U24<E>> r_sym;
1495: };
1496: 
1497: template <typename E> requires (!E::is_le) && (!E::is_rela)
1498: struct ElfRel<E> {
1499:   ElfRel() = default;
1500:   ElfRel(u64 offset, u32 type, u32 sym, i64 addend = 0)
1501:     : r_offset(offset), r_sym(sym), r_type(type) {}
1502: 
1503:   Word<E> r_offset;
1504:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, U24<E>> r_sym;
1505:   std::conditional_t<E::is_64, U32<E>, u8> r_type;
1506: };
1507: 
1508: template <typename E>
1509: struct ElfDyn {
1510:   Word<E> d_tag;
1511:   Word<E> d_val;
1512: };

1575: template <>
1576: struct ElfSym<PPC64V2> {
1577:   bool is_undef() const { return st_shndx == SHN_UNDEF; }
1578:   bool is_abs() const { return st_shndx == SHN_ABS; }
1579:   bool is_common() const { return st_shndx == SHN_COMMON; }
1580:   bool is_weak() const { return st_bind == STB_WEAK; }
1581:   bool is_undef_weak() const { return is_undef() && is_weak(); }
1582: 
1583:   bool preserves_r2() const { return ppc_local_entry != 1; }
1584:   bool uses_toc() const { return ppc_local_entry > 1; }
1585: 
1586:   ul32 st_name;
1587: 
1588: #ifdef __LITTLE_ENDIAN__
1589:   u8 st_type : 4;
1590:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1591:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1592:   u8 : 3;
1593:   u8 ppc_local_entry : 3; // This is PPC64V2-specific
1594: #else
1595:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1596:   u8 st_type : 4;
1597:   u8 ppc_local_entry : 3;
1598:   u8 : 3;
1599:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1600: #endif
1601: 
1602:   ul16 st_shndx;
1603:   ul64 st_value;
1604:   ul64 st_size;
1605: };
1606: 
1607: template <>
1608: struct ElfSym<ALPHA> {
1609:   bool is_undef() const { return st_shndx == SHN_UNDEF; }
1610:   bool is_abs() const { return st_shndx == SHN_ABS; }
1611:   bool is_common() const { return st_shndx == SHN_COMMON; }
1612:   bool is_weak() const { return st_bind == STB_WEAK; }
1613:   bool is_undef_weak() const { return is_undef() && is_weak(); }
1614: 
1615:   ul32 st_name;
1616: 
1617: #ifdef __LITTLE_ENDIAN__
1618:   u8 st_type : 4;
1619:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1620:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1621:   u8 alpha_st_other : 6; // contains STO_ALPHA_NOPV, STO_ALPHA_STD_GPLOAD or 0
1622: #else
1623:   u8 st_bind : 4;
1624:   u8 st_type : 4;
1625:   u8 alpha_st_other : 6;
1626:   u8 st_visibility : 2;
1627: #endif
1628: 
1629:   ul16 st_shndx;
1630:   ul64 st_value;
1631:   ul64 st_size;
1632: };
1633: 
1634: template <>
1635: struct ElfRel<SPARC64> {
1636:   ElfRel() = default;
1637:   ElfRel(u64 offset, u32 type, u32 sym, i64 addend)
1638:     : r_offset(offset), r_sym(sym), r_type_data(0), r_type(type),
1639:       r_addend(addend) {}
1640: 
1641:   ub64 r_offset;
1642:   ub32 r_sym;
1643:   ub24 r_type_data; // SPARC-specific: used for R_SPARC_OLO10
1644:   u8 r_type;
1645:   ib64 r_addend;
1646: };

1648: //
1649: // Machine descriptions
1650: //
1651: 
1652: template <typename E>
1653: static constexpr bool supports_ifunc = requires { E::R_IRELATIVE; };
1654: 
1655: template <typename E>
1656: static constexpr bool supports_tlsdesc = requires { E::R_TLSDESC; };
1657: 
1658: template <typename E>
1659: static constexpr bool needs_thunk = requires { E::thunk_size; };
1660: 
1661: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_x86_64 = std::is_same_v<E, X86_64>;
1662: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_i386 = std::is_same_v<E, I386>;
1663: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm64 = std::is_same_v<E, ARM64>;
1664: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm32 = std::is_same_v<E, ARM32>;
1665: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv64le = std::is_same_v<E, RV64LE>;
1666: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv64be = std::is_same_v<E, RV64BE>;
1667: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv32le = std::is_same_v<E, RV32LE>;
1668: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv32be = std::is_same_v<E, RV32BE>;
1669: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_ppc32 = std::is_same_v<E, PPC32>;
1670: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_ppc64v1 = std::is_same_v<E, PPC64V1>;
1671: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_ppc64v2 = std::is_same_v<E, PPC64V2>;
1672: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_s390x = std::is_same_v<E, S390X>;
1673: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_sparc64 = std::is_same_v<E, SPARC64>;
1674: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_m68k = std::is_same_v<E, M68K>;
1675: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_sh4 = std::is_same_v<E, SH4>;
1676: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_alpha = std::is_same_v<E, ALPHA>;
1677: 
1678: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_x86 = is_x86_64<E> || is_i386<E>;
1679: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm = is_arm64<E> || is_arm32<E>;
1680: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv64 = is_rv64le<E> || is_rv64be<E>;
1681: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_rv32 = is_rv32le<E> || is_rv32be<E>;
1682: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_riscv = is_rv64<E> || is_rv32<E>;
1683: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_ppc64 = is_ppc64v1<E> || is_ppc64v2<E>;
1684: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_ppc = is_ppc64<E> || is_ppc32<E>;
1685: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_sparc = is_sparc64<E>;
1686: 
1687: struct X86_64 {
1688:   static constexpr std::string_view target_name = "x86_64";
1689:   static constexpr bool is_64 = true;
1690:   static constexpr bool is_le = true;
1691:   static constexpr bool is_rela = true;
1692:   static constexpr u32 page_size = 4096;
1693:   static constexpr u32 e_machine = EM_X86_64;
1694:   static constexpr u32 plt_hdr_size = 32;
1695:   static constexpr u32 plt_size = 16;
1696:   static constexpr u32 pltgot_size = 16;
1697: 
1698:   static constexpr u32 R_COPY = R_X86_64_COPY;
1699:   static constexpr u32 R_GLOB_DAT = R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT;
1700:   static constexpr u32 R_JUMP_SLOT = R_X86_64_JUMP_SLOT;
1701:   static constexpr u32 R_ABS = R_X86_64_64;
1702:   static constexpr u32 R_RELATIVE = R_X86_64_RELATIVE;
1703:   static constexpr u32 R_IRELATIVE = R_X86_64_IRELATIVE;
1704:   static constexpr u32 R_DTPOFF = R_X86_64_DTPOFF64;
1705:   static constexpr u32 R_TPOFF = R_X86_64_TPOFF64;
1706:   static constexpr u32 R_DTPMOD = R_X86_64_DTPMOD64;
1707:   static constexpr u32 R_TLSDESC = R_X86_64_TLSDESC;
1708: };

================
File: elf/output-chunks.cc
================
   1: #include "mold.h"
   2: #include "../common/sha.h"
   3: 
   4: #include <cctype>
   5: #include <set>
   6: #include <shared_mutex>
   7: #include <span>
   8: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
   9: #include <tbb/parallel_scan.h>
  10: #include <tbb/parallel_sort.h>
  11: 
  12: #ifndef _WIN32
  13: # include <sys/mman.h>
  14: #endif
  15: 
  16: namespace mold::elf {
  17: 
  18: // The hash function for .hash.
  19: static u32 elf_hash(std::string_view name) {
  20:   u32 h = 0;
  21:   for (u8 c : name) {
  22:     h = (h << 4) + c;
  23:     u32 g = h & 0xf0000000;
  24:     if (g != 0)
  25:       h ^= g >> 24;
  26:     h &= ~g;
  27:   }
  28:   return h;
  29: }
  30: 
  31: // The hash function for .gnu.hash.
  32: static u32 djb_hash(std::string_view name) {
  33:   u32 h = 5381;
  34:   for (u8 c : name)
  35:     h = (h << 5) + h + c;
  36:   return h;
  37: }
  38: 
  39: template <typename E>
  40: u64 get_eflags(Context<E> &ctx) {
  41:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>)
  42:     return EF_ARM_EABI_VER5;
  43: 
  44:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>) {
  45:     std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> objs = ctx.objs;
  46:     std::erase(objs, ctx.internal_obj);
  47: 
  48:     if (objs.empty())
  49:       return 0;
  50: 
  51:     u32 ret = objs[0]->get_ehdr().e_flags;
  52:     for (i64 i = 1; i < objs.size(); i++) {
  53:       u32 flags = objs[i]->get_ehdr().e_flags;
  54:       if (flags & EF_RISCV_RVC)
  55:         ret |= EF_RISCV_RVC;
  56: 
  57:       if ((flags & EF_RISCV_FLOAT_ABI) != (ret & EF_RISCV_FLOAT_ABI))
  58:         Error(ctx) << *objs[i]
  59:                    << ": cannot link object files with different floating-point ABI from "
  60:                    << *objs[0];
  61: 
  62:       if ((flags & EF_RISCV_RVE) != (ret & EF_RISCV_RVE))
  63:         Error(ctx) << *objs[i]
  64:                    << ": cannot link object files with different EF_RISCV_RVE from "
  65:                    << *objs[0];
  66: 
  67:     }
  68:     return ret;
  69:   }
  70: 
  71:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v2<E>)
  72:     return 2;
  73:   return 0;
  74: }
  75: 
  76: template <typename E>
  77: void OutputEhdr<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
  78:   ElfEhdr<E> &hdr = *(ElfEhdr<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
  79:   memset(&hdr, 0, sizeof(hdr));
  80: 
  81:   auto get_entry_addr = [&]() -> u64 {
  82:     if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
  83:       return 0;
  84: 
  85:     if (!ctx.arg.entry.empty())
  86:       if (Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, ctx.arg.entry);
  87:           sym->file && !sym->file->is_dso)
  88:         return sym->get_addr(ctx);
  89: 
  90:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = find_section(ctx, ".text"))
  91:       return osec->shdr.sh_addr;
  92:     return 0;
  93:   };
  94: 
  95:   memcpy(&hdr.e_ident, "\177ELF", 4);
  96:   hdr.e_ident[EI_CLASS] = E::is_64 ? ELFCLASS64 : ELFCLASS32;
  97:   hdr.e_ident[EI_DATA] = E::is_le ? ELFDATA2LSB : ELFDATA2MSB;
  98:   hdr.e_ident[EI_VERSION] = EV_CURRENT;
  99:   hdr.e_machine = E::e_machine;
 100:   hdr.e_version = EV_CURRENT;
 101:   hdr.e_entry = get_entry_addr();
 102:   hdr.e_flags = get_eflags(ctx);
 103:   hdr.e_ehsize = sizeof(ElfEhdr<E>);
 104: 
 105:   // If e_shstrndx is too large, a dummy value is set to e_shstrndx.
 106:   // The real value is stored to the zero'th section's sh_link field.
 107:   if (ctx.shstrtab->shndx < SHN_LORESERVE)
 108:     hdr.e_shstrndx = ctx.shstrtab->shndx;
 109:   else
 110:     hdr.e_shstrndx = SHN_XINDEX;
 111: 
 112:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
 113:     hdr.e_type = ET_REL;
 114:   else if (ctx.arg.pic)
 115:     hdr.e_type = ET_DYN;
 116:   else
 117:     hdr.e_type = ET_EXEC;
 118: 
 119:   if (ctx.phdr) {
 120:     hdr.e_phoff = ctx.phdr->shdr.sh_offset;
 121:     hdr.e_phentsize = sizeof(ElfPhdr<E>);
 122:     hdr.e_phnum = ctx.phdr->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(ElfPhdr<E>);
 123:   }
 124: 
 125:   if (ctx.shdr) {
 126:     hdr.e_shoff = ctx.shdr->shdr.sh_offset;
 127:     hdr.e_shentsize = sizeof(ElfShdr<E>);
 128: 
 129:     // Since e_shnum is a 16-bit integer field, we can't store a very
 130:     // large value there. If it is >65535, the real value is stored to
 131:     // the zero'th section's sh_size field.
 132:     i64 shnum = ctx.shdr->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(ElfShdr<E>);
 133:     hdr.e_shnum = (shnum <= UINT16_MAX) ? shnum : 0;
 134:   }
 135: }
 136: 
 137: template <typename E>
 138: void OutputShdr<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 139:   ElfShdr<E> *hdr = (ElfShdr<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
 140:   memset(hdr, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
 141: 
 142:   i64 shnum = ctx.shdr->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(ElfShdr<E>);
 143:   if (UINT16_MAX < shnum)
 144:     hdr->sh_size = shnum;
 145: 
 146:   if (SHN_LORESERVE <= ctx.shstrtab->shndx)
 147:     hdr->sh_link = ctx.shstrtab->shndx;
 148: 
 149:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 150:     if (chunk->shndx)
 151:       hdr[chunk->shndx] = chunk->shdr;
 152: }
 153: 
 154: template <typename E>
 155: i64 to_phdr_flags(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 156:   // All sections are put into a single RWX segment if --omagic
 157:   if (ctx.arg.omagic)
 158:     return PF_R | PF_W | PF_X;
 159: 
 160:   bool write = (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_WRITE);
 161:   bool exec = (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR);
 162: 
 163:   // .text is not readable if --execute-only
 164:   if (exec && ctx.arg.execute_only) {
 165:     if (write)
 166:       Error(ctx) << "--execute-only is not compatible with writable section: "
 167:                  << chunk->name;
 168:     return PF_X;
 169:   }
 170: 
 171:   // .rodata is merged with .text if --no-rosegment
 172:   if (!write && !ctx.arg.rosegment)
 173:     exec = true;
 174: 
 175:   return PF_R | (write ? PF_W : PF_NONE) | (exec ? PF_X : PF_NONE);
 176: }
 177: 
 178: // PT_GNU_RELRO segment is a security mechanism to make more pages
 179: // read-only than we could have done without it.
 180: //
 181: // Traditionally, sections are either read-only or read-write.  If a
 182: // section contains dynamic relocations, it must have been put into a
 183: // read-write segment so that the program loader can mutate its
 184: // contents in memory, even if no one will write to it at runtime.
 185: //
 186: // RELRO segment allows us to make such pages writable only when a
 187: // program is being loaded. After that, the page becomes read-only.
 188: //
 189: // Some sections, such as .init, .fini, .got, .dynamic, contain
 190: // dynamic relocations but doesn't have to be writable at runtime,
 191: // so they are put into a RELRO segment.
 192: template <typename E>
 193: bool is_relro(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 194:   u64 flags = chunk->shdr.sh_flags;
 195:   u64 type = chunk->shdr.sh_type;
 196: 
 197:   if (flags & SHF_WRITE)
 198:     return (flags & SHF_TLS) || type == SHT_INIT_ARRAY ||
 199:            type == SHT_FINI_ARRAY || type == SHT_PREINIT_ARRAY ||
 200:            chunk == ctx.got || chunk == ctx.dynamic ||
 201:            chunk == ctx.relro_padding ||
 202:            (ctx.arg.z_now && ctx.gotplt && chunk == ctx.gotplt) ||
 203:            chunk->name == ".alpha_got" || chunk->name == ".toc" ||
 204:            chunk->name.ends_with(".rel.ro");
 205:   return false;
 206: }
 207: 
 208: template <typename E>
 209: static std::vector<ElfPhdr<E>> create_phdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 210:   std::vector<ElfPhdr<E>> vec;
 211: 
 212:   auto define = [&](u64 type, u64 flags, i64 min_align, Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 213:     vec.push_back({});
 214:     ElfPhdr<E> &phdr = vec.back();
 215:     phdr.p_type = type;
 216:     phdr.p_flags = flags;
 217:     phdr.p_align = std::max<u64>(min_align, chunk->shdr.sh_addralign);
 218:     phdr.p_offset = chunk->shdr.sh_offset;
 219:     phdr.p_filesz =
 220:       (chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS) ? 0 : (u64)chunk->shdr.sh_size;
 221:     phdr.p_vaddr = chunk->shdr.sh_addr;
 222:     phdr.p_paddr = chunk->shdr.sh_addr;
 223:     phdr.p_memsz = chunk->shdr.sh_size;
 224:   };
 225: 
 226:   auto append = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 227:     ElfPhdr<E> &phdr = vec.back();
 228:     phdr.p_align = std::max<u64>(phdr.p_align, chunk->shdr.sh_addralign);
 229:     if (!(chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS))
 230:       phdr.p_filesz = chunk->shdr.sh_addr + chunk->shdr.sh_size - phdr.p_vaddr;
 231:     phdr.p_memsz = chunk->shdr.sh_addr + chunk->shdr.sh_size - phdr.p_vaddr;
