 232:   };
 233: 
 234:   auto is_bss = [](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 235:     return chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS &&
 236:            !(chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS);
 237:   };
 238: 
 239:   auto is_tbss = [](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 240:     return chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS &&
 241:            (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS);
 242:   };
 243: 
 244:   auto is_note = [](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 245:     ElfShdr<E> &shdr = chunk->shdr;
 246:     return (shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOTE) && (shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
 247:   };
 248: 
 249:   // Create a PT_PHDR for the program header itself.
 250:   if (ctx.phdr && (ctx.phdr->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 251:     define(PT_PHDR, PF_R, sizeof(Word<E>), ctx.phdr);
 252: 
 253:   // Create a PT_INTERP.
 254:   if (ctx.interp)
 255:     define(PT_INTERP, PF_R, 1, ctx.interp);
 256: 
 257:   // Create a PT_NOTE for SHF_NOTE sections.
 258:   for (i64 i = 0, end = ctx.chunks.size(); i < end;) {
 259:     Chunk<E> *first = ctx.chunks[i++];
 260:     if (!is_note(first))
 261:       continue;
 262: 
 263:     i64 flags = to_phdr_flags(ctx, first);
 264:     i64 alignment = first->shdr.sh_addralign;
 265:     define(PT_NOTE, flags, alignment, first);
 266: 
 267:     while (i < end && is_note(ctx.chunks[i]) &&
 268:            to_phdr_flags(ctx, ctx.chunks[i]) == flags)
 269:       append(ctx.chunks[i++]);
 270:   }
 271: 
 272:   // Create PT_LOAD segments.
 273:   {
 274:     i64 idx = vec.size();
 275:     std::vector<Chunk<E> *> chunks = ctx.chunks;
 276:     std::erase_if(chunks, is_tbss);
 277: 
 278:     for (i64 i = 0, end = chunks.size(); i < end;) {
 279:       Chunk<E> *first = chunks[i++];
 280:       if (!(first->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 281:         continue;
 282: 
 283:       i64 flags = to_phdr_flags(ctx, first);
 284:       define(PT_LOAD, flags, ctx.page_size, first);
 285: 
 286:       // Add contiguous ALLOC sections as long as they have the same
 287:       // section flags and there's no on-disk gap in between.
 288:       if (!is_bss(first))
 289:         while (i < end && !is_bss(chunks[i]) &&
 290:                to_phdr_flags(ctx, chunks[i]) == flags &&
 291:                chunks[i]->shdr.sh_offset - first->shdr.sh_offset ==
 292:                chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr - first->shdr.sh_addr)
 293:           append(chunks[i++]);
 294: 
 295:       while (i < end && is_bss(chunks[i]) &&
 296:              to_phdr_flags(ctx, chunks[i]) == flags)
 297:         append(chunks[i++]);
 298:     }
 299: 
 300:     // The ELF spec says that "loadable segment entries in the program
 301:     // header table appear in ascending order, sorted on the p_vaddr
 302:     // member".
 303:     std::stable_sort(vec.begin() + idx, vec.end(),
 304:                      [](const ElfPhdr<E> &a, const ElfPhdr<E> &b) {
 305:       return a.p_vaddr < b.p_vaddr;
 306:     });
 307:   }
 308: 
 309:   // Create a PT_TLS.
 310:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.chunks.size(); i++) {
 311:     if (ctx.chunks[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS) {
 312:       define(PT_TLS, PF_R, 1, ctx.chunks[i++]);
 313:       while (i < ctx.chunks.size() && (ctx.chunks[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS))
 314:         append(ctx.chunks[i++]);
 315:     }
 316:   }
 317: 
 318:   // Add PT_DYNAMIC
 319:   if (ctx.dynamic && ctx.dynamic->shdr.sh_size)
 320:     define(PT_DYNAMIC, PF_R | PF_W, 1, ctx.dynamic);
 321: 
 322:   // Add PT_GNU_EH_FRAME
 323:   if (ctx.eh_frame_hdr)
 324:     define(PT_GNU_EH_FRAME, PF_R, 1, ctx.eh_frame_hdr);
 325: 
 326:   // Add PT_GNU_STACK, which is a marker segment that doesn't really
 327:   // contain any segments. It controls executable bit of stack area.
 328:   ElfPhdr<E> phdr = {};
 329:   phdr.p_type = PT_GNU_STACK,
 330:   phdr.p_flags = ctx.arg.z_execstack ? (PF_R | PF_W | PF_X) : (PF_R | PF_W),
 331:   phdr.p_align = 1;
 332:   vec.push_back(phdr);
 333: 
 334:   // Create a PT_GNU_RELRO.
 335:   if (ctx.arg.z_relro) {
 336:     for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.chunks.size(); i++) {
 337:       if (!is_relro(ctx, ctx.chunks[i]))
 338:         continue;
 339: 
 340:       define(PT_GNU_RELRO, PF_R, 1, ctx.chunks[i++]);
 341:       while (i < ctx.chunks.size() && is_relro(ctx, ctx.chunks[i]))
 342:         append(ctx.chunks[i++]);
 343:       vec.back().p_align = 1;
 344:     }
 345:   }
 346: 
 347:   // Add PT_ARM_EDXIDX
 348:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>)
 349:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = find_section(ctx, SHT_ARM_EXIDX))
 350:       define(PT_ARM_EXIDX, PF_R, 4, osec);
 351: 
 352:   // Set p_paddr if --physical-image-base was given. --physical-image-base
 353:   // is typically used in embedded programming to specify the base address
 354:   // of a memory-mapped ROM area. In that environment, paddr refers to a
 355:   // segment's initial location in ROM and vaddr refers the its run-time
 356:   // address.
 357:   //
 358:   // When a device is turned on, it start executing code at a fixed
 359:   // location in the ROM area. At that location is a startup routine that
 360:   // copies data or code from ROM to RAM before using them.
 361:   //
 362:   // .data must have different paddr and vaddr because ROM is not writable.
 363:   // paddr of .rodata and .text may or may be equal to vaddr. They can be
 364:   // directly read or executed from ROM, but oftentimes they are copied
 365:   // from ROM to RAM because Flash or EEPROM are usually much slower than
 366:   // DRAM.
 367:   //
 368:   // We want to keep vaddr == pvaddr for as many segments as possible so
 369:   // that they can be directly read/executed from ROM. If a gap between
 370:   // two segments is two page size or larger, we give up and pack segments
 371:   // tightly so that we don't waste too much ROM area.
 372:   if (ctx.arg.physical_image_base) {
 373:     for (i64 i = 0; i < vec.size(); i++) {
 374:       if (vec[i].p_type != PT_LOAD)
 375:         continue;
 376: 
 377:       u64 addr = *ctx.arg.physical_image_base;
 378:       bool in_sync = (vec[i].p_vaddr == addr);
 379: 
 380:       vec[i].p_paddr = addr;
 381:       addr += vec[i].p_memsz;
 382: 
 383:       for (i++; i < vec.size() && vec[i].p_type == PT_LOAD; i++) {
 384:         ElfPhdr<E> &p = vec[i];
 385:         if (in_sync && addr <= p.p_vaddr && p.p_vaddr < addr + ctx.page_size * 2) {
 386:           p.p_paddr = p.p_vaddr;
 387:           addr = p.p_vaddr + p.p_memsz;
 388:         } else {
 389:           in_sync = false;
 390:           p.p_paddr = addr;
 391:           addr += p.p_memsz;
 392:         }
 393:       }
 394:       break;
 395:     }
 396:   }
 397: 
 398:   return vec;
 399: }
 400: 
 401: template <typename E>
 402: void OutputPhdr<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 403:   phdrs = create_phdr(ctx);
 404:   this->shdr.sh_size = phdrs.size() * sizeof(ElfPhdr<E>);
 405: 
 406:   ctx.tls_begin = get_tls_begin(ctx);
 407:   ctx.tp_addr = get_tp_addr(ctx);
 408:   ctx.dtp_addr = get_dtp_addr(ctx);
 409: }
 410: 
 411: template <typename E>
 412: void OutputPhdr<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 413:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, phdrs);
 414: }
 415: 
 416: template <typename E>
 417: void InterpSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 418:   this->shdr.sh_size = ctx.arg.dynamic_linker.size() + 1;
 419: }
 420: 
 421: template <typename E>
 422: void InterpSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 423:   write_string(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, ctx.arg.dynamic_linker);
 424: }
 425: 
 426: template <typename E>
 427: void RelDynSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 428:   i64 offset = 0;
 429: 
 430:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 431:     chunk->reldyn_offset = offset;
 432:     offset += chunk->get_reldyn_size(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
 433:   }
 434: 
 435:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 436:     file->reldyn_offset = offset;
 437:     offset += file->num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
 438:   }
 439: 
 440:   this->shdr.sh_size = offset;
 441:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
 442: }
 443: 
 444: template <typename E>
 445: void RelDynSection<E>::sort(Context<E> &ctx) {
 446:   Timer t(ctx, "sort_dynamic_relocs");
 447: 
 448:   ElfRel<E> *begin = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
 449:   ElfRel<E> *end = (ElfRel<E> *)((u8 *)begin + this->shdr.sh_size);
 450: 
 451:   auto get_rank = [](u32 r_type) {
 452:     if (r_type == E::R_RELATIVE)
 453:       return 0;
 454:     if constexpr (supports_ifunc<E>)
 455:       if (r_type == E::R_IRELATIVE)
 456:         return 2;
 457:     return 1;
 458:   };
 459: 
 460:   // This is the reason why we sort dynamic relocations. Quote from
 461:   // https://www.airs.com/blog/archives/186:
 462:   //
 463:   //   The dynamic linker in glibc uses a one element cache when processing
 464:   //   relocs: if a relocation refers to the same symbol as the previous
 465:   //   relocation, then the dynamic linker reuses the value rather than
 466:   //   looking up the symbol again. Thus the dynamic linker gets the best
 467:   //   results if the dynamic relocations are sorted so that all dynamic
 468:   //   relocations for a given dynamic symbol are adjacent.
 469:   //
 470:   //   Other than that, the linker sorts together all relative relocations,
 471:   //   which don't have symbols. Two relative relocations, or two relocations
 472:   //   against the same symbol, are sorted by the address in the output
 473:   //   file. This tends to optimize paging and caching when there are two
 474:   //   references from the same page.
 475:   //
 476:   // We group IFUNC relocations at the end of .rel.dyn because we want to
 477:   // apply all the other relocations before running user-supplied ifunc
 478:   // resolver functions.
 479:   tbb::parallel_sort(begin, end, [&](const ElfRel<E> &a, const ElfRel<E> &b) {
 480:     return std::tuple(get_rank(a.r_type), a.r_sym, a.r_offset) <
 481:            std::tuple(get_rank(b.r_type), b.r_sym, b.r_offset);
 482:   });
 483: }
 484: 
 485: template <typename E>
 486: void RelrDynSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 487:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
 488: 
 489:   i64 n = ctx.got->relr.size();
 490:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 491:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
 492:       n += osec->relr.size();
 493:   this->shdr.sh_size = n * sizeof(Word<E>);
 494: }
 495: 
 496: template <typename E>
 497: void RelrDynSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 498:   Word<E> *buf = (Word<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
 499: 
 500:   for (u64 val : ctx.got->relr)
 501:     *buf++ = (val & 1) ? val : (ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + val);
 502: 
 503:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 504:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
 505:       for (u64 val : osec->relr)
 506:         *buf++ = (val & 1) ? val : (osec->shdr.sh_addr + val);
 507: }
 508: 
 509: template <typename E>
 510: void StrtabSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 511:   i64 offset = 1;
 512: 
 513:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 514:     chunk->strtab_offset = offset;
 515:     offset += chunk->strtab_size;
 516:   }
 517: 
 518:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 519:     file->strtab_offset = offset;
 520:     offset += file->strtab_size;
 521:   }
 522: 
 523:   for (SharedFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos) {
 524:     file->strtab_offset = offset;
 525:     offset += file->strtab_size;
 526:   }
 527: 
 528:   this->shdr.sh_size = (offset == 1) ? 0 : offset;
 529: }
 530: 
 531: template <typename E>
 532: void ShstrtabSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 533:   std::unordered_map<std::string_view, i64> map;
 534:   i64 offset = 1;
 535: 
 536:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 537:     if (chunk->kind() != ChunkKind::HEADER && !chunk->name.empty()) {
 538:       auto [it, inserted] = map.insert({chunk->name, offset});
 539:       chunk->shdr.sh_name = it->second;
 540:       if (inserted)
 541:         offset += chunk->name.size() + 1;
 542:     }
 543:   }
 544: 
 545:   this->shdr.sh_size = offset;
 546: }
 547: 
 548: template <typename E>
 549: void ShstrtabSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 550:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
 551:   base[0] = '\0';
 552: 
 553:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 554:     if (chunk->kind() != ChunkKind::HEADER && !chunk->name.empty())
 555:       write_string(base + chunk->shdr.sh_name, chunk->name);
 556: }
 557: 
 558: template <typename E>
 559: i64 DynstrSection<E>::add_string(std::string_view str) {
 560:   if (this->shdr.sh_size == 0)
 561:     this->shdr.sh_size = 1;
 562: 
 563:   if (str.empty())
 564:     return 0;
 565: 
 566:   auto [it, inserted] = strings.insert({str, this->shdr.sh_size});
 567:   if (inserted)
 568:     this->shdr.sh_size += str.size() + 1;
 569:   return it->second;
 570: }
 571: 
 572: template <typename E>
 573: i64 DynstrSection<E>::find_string(std::string_view str) {
 574:   if (str.empty())
 575:     return 0;
 576: 
 577:   auto it = strings.find(str);
 578:   assert(it != strings.end());
 579:   return it->second;
 580: }
 581: 
 582: template <typename E>
 583: void DynstrSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 584:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
 585:   base[0] = '\0';
 586: 
 587:   for (std::pair<std::string_view, i64> pair : strings)
 588:     write_string(base + pair.second, pair.first);
 589: 
 590:   if (!ctx.dynsym->symbols.empty()) {
 591:     i64 offset = dynsym_offset;
 592:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : std::span<Symbol<E> *>(ctx.dynsym->symbols).subspan(1))
 593:       offset += write_string(base + offset, sym->name());
 594:   }
 595: }
 596: 
 597: template <typename E>
 598: void SymtabSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 599:   i64 nsyms = 1;
 600: 
 601:   // Section symbols
 602:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 603:     if (chunk->shndx)
 604:       nsyms++;
 605: 
 606:   // Linker-synthesized symbols
 607:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 608:     chunk->local_symtab_idx = nsyms;
 609:     nsyms += chunk->num_local_symtab;
 610:   }
 611: 
 612:   // File local symbols
 613:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 614:     file->local_symtab_idx = nsyms;
 615:     nsyms += file->num_local_symtab;
 616:   }
 617: 
 618:   // File global symbols
 619:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 620:     file->global_symtab_idx = nsyms;
 621:     nsyms += file->num_global_symtab;
 622:   }
 623: 
 624:   for (SharedFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos) {
 625:     file->global_symtab_idx = nsyms;
 626:     nsyms += file->num_global_symtab;
 627:   }
 628: 
 629:   this->shdr.sh_info = ctx.objs[0]->global_symtab_idx;
 630:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.strtab->shndx;
 631:   this->shdr.sh_size = (nsyms == 1) ? 0 : nsyms * sizeof(ElfSym<E>);
 632: }
 633: 
 634: template <typename E>
 635: void SymtabSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 636:   ElfSym<E> *buf = (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
 637:   memset(buf, 0, sizeof(ElfSym<E>));
 638: 
 639:   // Write the initial NUL byte to .strtab.
 640:   ctx.buf[ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset] = '\0';
 641: 
 642:   // Create section symbols
 643:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 644:     if (chunk->shndx) {
 645:       ElfSym<E> &sym = buf[chunk->shndx];
 646:       memset(&sym, 0, sizeof(sym));
 647:       sym.st_type = STT_SECTION;
 648:       sym.st_value = chunk->shdr.sh_addr;
 649:       sym.st_shndx = chunk->shndx;
 650:     }
 651:   }
 652: 
 653:   // Populate linker-synthesized symbols
 654:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 655:     chunk->populate_symtab(ctx);
 656:   });
 657: 
 658:   // Copy symbols from input files
 659:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 660:     file->populate_symtab(ctx);
 661:   });
 662: 
 663:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, [&](SharedFile<E> *file) {
 664:     file->populate_symtab(ctx);
 665:   });
 666: }
 667: 
 668: template <typename E>
 669: static std::vector<Word<E>> create_dynamic_section(Context<E> &ctx) {
 670:   std::vector<Word<E>> vec;
 671: 
 672:   auto define = [&](u64 tag, u64 val) {
 673:     vec.push_back(tag);
 674:     vec.push_back(val);
 675:   };
 676: 
 677:   for (SharedFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos)
 678:     define(DT_NEEDED, ctx.dynstr->find_string(file->soname));
 679: 
 680:   if (!ctx.arg.rpaths.empty())
 681:     define(ctx.arg.enable_new_dtags ? DT_RUNPATH : DT_RPATH,
 682:            ctx.dynstr->find_string(ctx.arg.rpaths));
 683: 
 684:   if (!ctx.arg.soname.empty())
 685:     define(DT_SONAME, ctx.dynstr->find_string(ctx.arg.soname));
 686: 
 687:   for (std::string_view str : ctx.arg.auxiliary)
 688:     define(DT_AUXILIARY, ctx.dynstr->find_string(str));
 689: 
 690:   for (std::string_view str : ctx.arg.filter)
 691:     define(DT_FILTER, ctx.dynstr->find_string(str));
 692: 
 693:   if (ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_size) {
 694:     define(E::is_rela ? DT_RELA : DT_REL, ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_addr);
 695:     define(E::is_rela ? DT_RELASZ : DT_RELSZ, ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_size);
 696:     define(E::is_rela ? DT_RELAENT : DT_RELENT, sizeof(ElfRel<E>));
 697:   }
 698: 
 699:   if (ctx.relrdyn) {
 700:     define(DT_RELR, ctx.relrdyn->shdr.sh_addr);
 701:     define(DT_RELRSZ, ctx.relrdyn->shdr.sh_size);
 702:     define(DT_RELRENT, ctx.relrdyn->shdr.sh_entsize);
 703:   }
 704: 
 705:   if (ctx.relplt->shdr.sh_size) {
 706:     define(DT_JMPREL, ctx.relplt->shdr.sh_addr);
 707:     define(DT_PLTRELSZ, ctx.relplt->shdr.sh_size);
 708:     define(DT_PLTREL, E::is_rela ? DT_RELA : DT_REL);
 709:   }
 710: 
 711:   if constexpr (is_sparc<E>) {
 712:     if (ctx.plt->shdr.sh_size)
 713:       define(DT_PLTGOT, ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr);
 714:   } else if constexpr (is_ppc32<E>) {
 715:     if (ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_size)
 716:       define(DT_PLTGOT, ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr + GotPltSection<E>::HDR_SIZE);
 717:   } else {
 718:     if (ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_size)
 719:       define(DT_PLTGOT, ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr);
 720:   }
 721: 
 722:   if (ctx.dynsym->shdr.sh_size) {
 723:     define(DT_SYMTAB, ctx.dynsym->shdr.sh_addr);
 724:     define(DT_SYMENT, sizeof(ElfSym<E>));
 725:   }
 726: 
 727:   if (ctx.dynstr->shdr.sh_size) {
 728:     define(DT_STRTAB, ctx.dynstr->shdr.sh_addr);
 729:     define(DT_STRSZ, ctx.dynstr->shdr.sh_size);
 730:   }
 731: 
 732:   if (find_section(ctx, SHT_INIT_ARRAY)) {
 733:     define(DT_INIT_ARRAY, ctx.__init_array_start->value);
 734:     define(DT_INIT_ARRAYSZ,
 735:            ctx.__init_array_end->value - ctx.__init_array_start->value);
 736:   }
 737: 
 738:   if (find_section(ctx, SHT_PREINIT_ARRAY)) {
 739:     define(DT_PREINIT_ARRAY, ctx.__preinit_array_start->value);
 740:     define(DT_PREINIT_ARRAYSZ,
 741:            ctx.__preinit_array_end->value - ctx.__preinit_array_start->value);
 742:   }
 743: 
 744:   if (find_section(ctx, SHT_FINI_ARRAY)) {
 745:     define(DT_FINI_ARRAY, ctx.__fini_array_start->value);
 746:     define(DT_FINI_ARRAYSZ,
 747:            ctx.__fini_array_end->value - ctx.__fini_array_start->value);
 748:   }
 749: 
 750:   if (ctx.versym->shdr.sh_size)
 751:     define(DT_VERSYM, ctx.versym->shdr.sh_addr);
 752: 
 753:   if (ctx.verneed->shdr.sh_size) {
 754:     define(DT_VERNEED, ctx.verneed->shdr.sh_addr);
 755:     define(DT_VERNEEDNUM, ctx.verneed->shdr.sh_info);
 756:   }
 757: 
 758:   if (ctx.verdef) {
 759:     define(DT_VERDEF, ctx.verdef->shdr.sh_addr);
 760:     define(DT_VERDEFNUM, ctx.verdef->shdr.sh_info);
 761:   }
 762: 
 763:   if (Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, ctx.arg.init);
 764:       sym->file && !sym->file->is_dso)
 765:     define(DT_INIT, sym->get_addr(ctx));
 766: 
 767:   if (Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, ctx.arg.fini);
 768:       sym->file && !sym->file->is_dso)
 769:     define(DT_FINI, sym->get_addr(ctx));
 770: 
 771:   if (ctx.hash)
 772:     define(DT_HASH, ctx.hash->shdr.sh_addr);
 773:   if (ctx.gnu_hash)
 774:     define(DT_GNU_HASH, ctx.gnu_hash->shdr.sh_addr);
 775:   if (ctx.has_textrel)
 776:     define(DT_TEXTREL, 0);
 777: 
 778:   i64 flags = 0;
 779:   i64 flags1 = 0;
 780: 
 781:   if (ctx.arg.pie)
 782:     flags1 |= DF_1_PIE;
 783: 
 784:   if (ctx.arg.z_now) {
 785:     flags |= DF_BIND_NOW;
 786:     flags1 |= DF_1_NOW;
 787:   }
 788: 
 789:   if (ctx.arg.z_origin) {
 790:     flags |= DF_ORIGIN;
 791:     flags1 |= DF_1_ORIGIN;
 792:   }
 793: 
 794:   if (!ctx.arg.z_dlopen)
 795:     flags1 |= DF_1_NOOPEN;
 796:   if (ctx.arg.z_nodefaultlib)
 797:     flags1 |= DF_1_NODEFLIB;
 798:   if (!ctx.arg.z_delete)
 799:     flags1 |= DF_1_NODELETE;
 800:   if (!ctx.arg.z_dump)
 801:     flags1 |= DF_1_NODUMP;
 802:   if (ctx.arg.z_initfirst)
 803:     flags1 |= DF_1_INITFIRST;
 804:   if (ctx.arg.z_interpose)
 805:     flags1 |= DF_1_INTERPOSE;
 806: 
 807:   if (!ctx.got->gottp_syms.empty())
 808:     flags |= DF_STATIC_TLS;
 809:   if (ctx.has_textrel)
 810:     flags |= DF_TEXTREL;
 811: 
 812:   if (flags)
 813:     define(DT_FLAGS, flags);
 814:   if (flags1)
 815:     define(DT_FLAGS_1, flags1);
 816: 
 817:   if constexpr (is_ppc32<E>)
 818:     define(DT_PPC_GOT, ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr);
 819: 
 820:   if constexpr (is_ppc64<E>) {
 821:     // PPC64_GLINK is defined by the psABI to refer 32 bytes before
 822:     // the first PLT entry. I don't know why it's 32 bytes off, but
 823:     // it's what it is.
 824:     define(DT_PPC64_GLINK, ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr + E::plt_hdr_size - 32);
 825:   }
 826: 
 827:   // GDB needs a DT_DEBUG entry in an executable to store a word-size
 828:   // data for its own purpose. Its content is not important.
 829:   if (!ctx.arg.shared)
 830:     define(DT_DEBUG, 0);
 831: 
 832:   define(DT_NULL, 0);
 833: 
 834:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.spare_dynamic_tags; i++)
 835:     define(DT_NULL, 0);
 836: 
 837:   return vec;
 838: }
 839: 
 840: template <typename E>
 841: void DynamicSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 842:   if (ctx.arg.is_static && !ctx.arg.pie)
 843:     return;
 844: 
 845:   this->shdr.sh_size = create_dynamic_section(ctx).size() * sizeof(Word<E>);
 846:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynstr->shndx;
 847: }
 848: 
 849: template <typename E>
 850: void DynamicSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 851:   std::vector<Word<E>> contents = create_dynamic_section(ctx);
 852:   assert(this->shdr.sh_size == contents.size() * sizeof(contents[0]));
 853:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, contents);
 854: }
 855: 
 856: template <typename E>
 857: void OutputSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 858:   if (this->shdr.sh_type != SHT_NOBITS)
 859:     write_to(ctx, ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
 860: }
 861: 
 862: template <typename E>
 863: void OutputSection<E>::write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
 864:   auto clear = [&](u8 *loc, i64 size) {
 865:     // As a special case, .init and .fini are filled with NOPs for s390x
 866:     // because the runtime executes the sections as if they were a single
 867:     // function. .init and .fini are superceded by .init_array and
 868:     // .fini_array but being actively used only on s390x.
 869:     if constexpr (is_s390x<E>) {
 870:       if (this->name == ".init" || this->name == ".fini") {
 871:         for (i64 i = 0; i < size; i += 2)
 872:           *(ub16 *)(loc + i) = 0x0700; // nop
 873:         return;
 874:       }
 875:     }
 876:     memset(loc, 0, size);
 877:   };
 878: 
 879:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)members.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 880:     // Copy section contents to an output file
 881:     InputSection<E> &isec = *members[i];
 882:     isec.write_to(ctx, buf + isec.offset);
 883: 
 884:     // Clear trailing padding
 885:     u64 this_end = isec.offset + isec.sh_size;
 886:     u64 next_start = (i == members.size() - 1) ?
 887:       (u64)this->shdr.sh_size : members[i + 1]->offset;
 888:     clear(buf + this_end, next_start - this_end);
 889:   });
 890: 
 891:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
 892:     tbb::parallel_for_each(thunks,
 893:                            [&](std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>> &thunk) {
 894:       thunk->copy_buf(ctx);
 895:     });
 896:   }
 897: }
 898: 
 899: // .relr.dyn contains base relocations encoded in a space-efficient form.
 900: // The contents of the section is essentially just a list of addresses
 901: // that have to be fixed up at runtime.
 902: //
 903: // Here is the encoding scheme (we assume 64-bit ELF in this description
 904: // for the sake of simplicity): .relr.dyn contains zero or more address
 905: // groups. Each address group consists of a 64-bit start address followed
 906: // by zero or more 63-bit bitmaps. Let A be the address of a start
 907: // address. Then, the loader fixes address A. If Nth bit in the following
 908: // bitmap is on, the loader also fixes address A + N * 8. In this scheme,
 909: // one address and one bitmap can represent up to 64 base relocations in a
 910: // 512 bytes range.
 911: //
 912: // A start address and a bitmap is distinguished by the lowest significant
 913: // bit. An address must be even and thus its LSB is 0 (odd address is not
 914: // representable in this encoding and such relocation must be stored to
 915: // the .rel.dyn section). A bitmap has LSB 1.
 916: static std::vector<u64> encode_relr(std::span<u64> pos, i64 word_size) {
 917:   std::vector<u64> vec;
 918:   u64 num_bits = word_size * 8 - 1;
 919:   u64 max_delta = num_bits * word_size;
 920: 
 921:   for (i64 i = 0; i < pos.size();) {
 922:     assert(i == 0 || pos[i - 1] <= pos[i]);
 923:     assert(pos[i] % word_size == 0);
 924: 
 925:     vec.push_back(pos[i]);
 926:     u64 base = pos[i] + word_size;
 927:     i++;
 928: 
 929:     for (;;) {
 930:       u64 bits = 0;
 931:       for (; i < pos.size() && pos[i] - base < max_delta; i++)
 932:         bits |= 1LL << ((pos[i] - base) / word_size);
 933: 
 934:       if (!bits)
 935:         break;
 936: 
 937:       vec.push_back((bits << 1) | 1);
 938:       base += max_delta;
 939:     }
 940:   }
 941:   return vec;
 942: }
 943: 
 944: template <typename E>
 945: void OutputSection<E>::construct_relr(Context<E> &ctx) {
 946:   if (!ctx.arg.pic)
 947:     return;
 948:   if (!(this->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 949:     return;
 950:   if (this->shdr.sh_addralign % sizeof(Word<E>))
 951:     return;
 952: 
 953:   // Skip it if it is a text section because .text doesn't usually
 954:   // contain any dynamic relocations.
 955:   if (this->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR)
 956:     return;
 957: 
 958:   // Collect base relocations
 959:   std::vector<std::vector<u64>> shards(members.size());
 960: 
 961:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)members.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 962:     InputSection<E> &isec = *members[i];
 963:     if ((1 << isec.p2align) < sizeof(Word<E>))
 964:       return;
 965: 
 966:     for (const ElfRel<E> &r : isec.get_rels(ctx))
 967:       if (r.r_type == E::R_ABS && (r.r_offset % sizeof(Word<E>)) == 0)
 968:         if (Symbol<E> &sym = *isec.file.symbols[r.r_sym];
 969:             !sym.is_absolute() && !sym.is_imported)
 970:           shards[i].push_back(isec.offset + r.r_offset);
 971:   });
 972: 
 973:   // Compress them
 974:   std::vector<u64> pos = flatten(shards);
 975:   relr = encode_relr(pos, sizeof(Word<E>));
 976: }
 977: 
 978: // Compute spaces needed for thunk symbols
 979: template <typename E>
 980: void OutputSection<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 981:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all || ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file || ctx.arg.relocatable)
 982:     return;
 983: 
 984:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
 985:     this->strtab_size = 0;
 986:     this->num_local_symtab = 0;
 987: 
 988:     if constexpr (is_arm32<E>)
 989:       this->strtab_size = 9; // for "$t", "$a" and "$d" symbols
 990: 
 991:     for (std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>> &thunk : thunks) {
 992:       // For ARM32, we emit additional symbol "$t", "$a" and "$d" for
 993:       // each thunk to mark the beginning of ARM code.
 994:       if constexpr (is_arm32<E>)
 995:         this->num_local_symtab += thunk->symbols.size() * 4;
 996:       else
 997:         this->num_local_symtab += thunk->symbols.size();
 998: 
 999:       for (Symbol<E> *sym : thunk->symbols)
1000:         this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$thunk");
1001:     }
1002:   }
1003: }
1004: 
1005: // If we create range extension thunks, we also synthesize symbols to mark
1006: // the locations of thunks. Creating such symbols is optional, but it helps
1007: // disassembling and/or debugging our output.
1008: template <typename E>
1009: void OutputSection<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1010:   if (this->strtab_size == 0)
1011:     return;
1012: 
1013:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
1014:     ElfSym<E> *esym =
1015:       (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset) + this->local_symtab_idx;
1016: 
1017:     u8 *strtab_base = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1018:     u8 *strtab = strtab_base + this->strtab_offset;
1019: 
1020:     if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
1021:       // ARM uses these symbols to mark the begining of Thumb code, ARM
1022:       // code and data, respectively. Our thunk contains all of them.
1023:       strtab += write_string(strtab, "$t");
1024:       strtab += write_string(strtab, "$a");
1025:       strtab += write_string(strtab, "$d");
1026:     }
1027: 
1028:     for (std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>> &thunk : thunks) {
1029:       for (i64 i = 0; i < thunk->symbols.size(); i++) {
1030:         Symbol<E> &sym = *thunk->symbols[i];
1031: 
1032:         auto write_esym = [&](i64 st_name, i64 off) {
1033:           memset(esym, 0, sizeof(*esym));
1034:           esym->st_name = st_name;
1035:           esym->st_type = STT_FUNC;
1036:           esym->st_shndx = this->shndx;
1037:           esym->st_value = thunk->get_addr(i) + off;
1038:           esym++;
1039:         };
1040: 
1041:         write_esym(strtab - strtab_base, 0);
1042: 
1043:         strtab += write_string(strtab, sym.name()) - 1;
1044:         strtab += write_string(strtab, "$thunk");
1045: 
1046:         // Emit "$t", "$a" and "$d" if ARM32.
1047:         if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
1048:           write_esym(this->strtab_offset, 0);
1049:           write_esym(this->strtab_offset + 3, 4);
1050:           write_esym(this->strtab_offset + 6, 16);
1051:         }
1052:       }
1053:     }
1054:   }
1055: }
1056: 
1057: template <typename E>
1058: void GotSection<E>::add_got_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1059:   sym->set_got_idx(ctx, this->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>));
1060:   this->shdr.sh_size += sizeof(Word<E>);
1061:   got_syms.push_back(sym);
1062: }
1063: 
1064: template <typename E>
1065: void GotSection<E>::add_gottp_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1066:   sym->set_gottp_idx(ctx, this->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>));
1067:   this->shdr.sh_size += sizeof(Word<E>);
1068:   gottp_syms.push_back(sym);
1069: }
1070: 
1071: template <typename E>
1072: void GotSection<E>::add_tlsgd_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1073:   sym->set_tlsgd_idx(ctx, this->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>));
1074:   this->shdr.sh_size += sizeof(Word<E>) * 2;
1075:   tlsgd_syms.push_back(sym);
1076: }
1077: 
1078: template <typename E>
1079: void GotSection<E>::add_tlsdesc_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1080:   assert(supports_tlsdesc<E>);
1081:   sym->set_tlsdesc_idx(ctx, this->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>));
1082:   this->shdr.sh_size += sizeof(Word<E>) * 2;
1083:   tlsdesc_syms.push_back(sym);
1084: 
1085:   if (sym != ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_)
1086:     ctx.dynsym->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1087: }
1088: 
1089: template <typename E>
1090: void GotSection<E>::add_tlsld(Context<E> &ctx) {
1091:   if (tlsld_idx != -1)
1092:     return;
1093:   tlsld_idx = this->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(Word<E>);
1094:   this->shdr.sh_size += sizeof(Word<E>) * 2;
1095: }
1096: 
1097: template <typename E>
1098: u64 GotSection<E>::get_tlsld_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
1099:   assert(tlsld_idx != -1);
1100:   return this->shdr.sh_addr + tlsld_idx * sizeof(Word<E>);
1101: }
1102: 
1103: template <typename E>
1104: struct GotEntry {
1105:   bool is_relr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
1106:     return r_type == E::R_RELATIVE && ctx.arg.pack_dyn_relocs_relr;
1107:   }
1108: 
1109:   i64 idx = 0;
1110:   u64 val = 0;
1111:   i64 r_type = R_NONE;
1112:   Symbol<E> *sym = nullptr;
1113: };
1114: 
1115: // Get .got and .rel.dyn contents.
1116: //
1117: // .got is a linker-synthesized constant pool whose entry is of pointer
1118: // size. If we know a correct value for an entry, we'll just set that value
1119: // to the entry. Otherwise, we'll create a dynamic relocation and let the
1120: // dynamic linker to fill the entry at load-time.
1121: //
1122: // Most GOT entries contain addresses of global variable. If a global
1123: // variable is an imported symbol, we don't know its address until runtime.
1124: // GOT contains the addresses of such variables at runtime so that we can
1125: // access imported global variables via GOT.
1126: //
1127: // Thread-local variables (TLVs) also use GOT entries. We need them because
1128: // TLVs are accessed in a different way than the ordinary global variables.
1129: // Their addresses are not unique; each thread has its own copy of TLVs.
1130: template <typename E>
1131: static std::vector<GotEntry<E>> get_got_entries(Context<E> &ctx) {
1132:   std::vector<GotEntry<E>> entries;
1133: 
1134:   // Create GOT entries for ordinary symbols
1135:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got->got_syms) {
1136:     i64 idx = sym->get_got_idx(ctx);
1137: 
1138:     // If a symbol is imported, let the dynamic linker to resolve it.
1139:     if (sym->is_imported) {
1140:       entries.push_back({idx, 0, E::R_GLOB_DAT, sym});
1141:       continue;
1142:     }
1143: 
1144:     // IFUNC always needs to be fixed up by the dynamic linker.
1145:     if constexpr (supports_ifunc<E>) {
1146:       if (sym->is_ifunc()) {
1147:         entries.push_back({idx, sym->get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT), E::R_IRELATIVE});
1148:         continue;
1149:       }
1150:     }
1151: 
1152:     // If we know an address at link-time, fill that GOT entry now.
1153:     // It may need a base relocation, though.
1154:     if (ctx.arg.pic && sym->is_relative())
1155:       entries.push_back({idx, sym->get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT), E::R_RELATIVE});
1156:     else
1157:       entries.push_back({idx, sym->get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT)});
1158:   }
1159: 
1160:   // Create GOT entries for TLVs.
1161:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got->tlsgd_syms) {
1162:     i64 idx = sym->get_tlsgd_idx(ctx);
1163: 
1164:     // If a symbol is imported, let the dynamic linker to resolve it.
1165:     if (sym->is_imported) {
1166:       entries.push_back({idx, 0, E::R_DTPMOD, sym});
1167:       entries.push_back({idx + 1, 0, E::R_DTPOFF, sym});
1168:       continue;
1169:     }
1170: 
1171:     // If we are creating a shared library, we know the TLV's offset
1172:     // within the current TLS block. We don't know the module ID though.
1173:     if (ctx.arg.shared) {
1174:       entries.push_back({idx, 0, E::R_DTPMOD});
1175:       entries.push_back({idx + 1, sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.dtp_addr});
1176:       continue;
1177:     }
1178: 
1179:     // If we are creating an executable, we know both the module ID and the
1180:     // offset. Module ID 1 indicates the main executable.
1181:     entries.push_back({idx, 1});
1182:     entries.push_back({idx + 1, sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.dtp_addr});
1183:   }
1184: 
1185:   if constexpr (supports_tlsdesc<E>) {
1186:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got->tlsdesc_syms) {
1187:       // _TLS_MODULE_BASE_ is a linker-synthesized virtual symbol that
1188:       // refers the begining of the TLS block.
1189:       if (sym == ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_)
1190:         entries.push_back({sym->get_tlsdesc_idx(ctx), 0, E::R_TLSDESC});
1191:       else
1192:         entries.push_back({sym->get_tlsdesc_idx(ctx), 0, E::R_TLSDESC, sym});
1193:     }
1194:   }
1195: 
1196:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got->gottp_syms) {
1197:     i64 idx = sym->get_gottp_idx(ctx);
1198: 
1199:     // If we know nothing about the symbol, let the dynamic linker
1200:     // to fill the GOT entry.
1201:     if (sym->is_imported) {
1202:       entries.push_back({idx, 0, E::R_TPOFF, sym});
1203:       continue;
1204:     }
1205: 
1206:     // If we know the offset within the current thread vector,
1207:     // let the dynamic linker to adjust it.
1208:     if (ctx.arg.shared) {
1209:       entries.push_back({idx, sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.tls_begin, E::R_TPOFF});
1210:       continue;
1211:     }
1212: 
1213:     // Otherwise, we know the offset from the thread pointer (TP) at
1214:     // link-time, so we can fill the GOT entry directly.
1215:     entries.push_back({idx, sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.tp_addr});
1216:   }
1217: 
1218:   if (ctx.got->tlsld_idx != -1) {
1219:     if (ctx.arg.shared)
1220:       entries.push_back({ctx.got->tlsld_idx, 0, E::R_DTPMOD});
1221:     else
1222:       entries.push_back({ctx.got->tlsld_idx, 1}); // 1 means the main executable
1223:   }
1224: 
1225:   return entries;
1226: }
1227: 
1228: template <typename E>
1229: i64 GotSection<E>::get_reldyn_size(Context<E> &ctx) const {
1230:   i64 n = 0;
1231:   for (GotEntry<E> &ent : get_got_entries(ctx))
1232:     if (!ent.is_relr(ctx) && ent.r_type != R_NONE)
1233:       n++;
1234:   return n;
1235: }
1236: 
1237: // Fill .got and .rel.dyn.
1238: template <typename E>
1239: void GotSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1240:   Word<E> *buf = (Word<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
1241:   memset(buf, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
1242: 
1243:   // s390x psABI requires GOT[0] to be set to the link-time value of _DYNAMIC.
1244:   if constexpr (is_s390x<E>)
1245:     if (ctx.dynamic)
1246:       buf[0] = ctx.dynamic->shdr.sh_addr;
1247: 
1248:   // arm64 psABI doesn't say anything about GOT[0], but glibc/arm64's code
1249:   // path for -static-pie wrongly assumed that GOT[0] refers _DYNAMIC.
1250:   //
1251:   // https://sourceware.org/git/?p=glibc.git;a=commitdiff;h=43d06ed218fc8be5
1252:   if constexpr (is_arm64<E>)
1253:     if (ctx.dynamic && ctx.arg.is_static && ctx.arg.pie)
1254:       buf[0] = ctx.dynamic->shdr.sh_addr;
1255: 
1256:   ElfRel<E> *rel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
1257:                                  this->reldyn_offset);
1258: 
1259:   for (GotEntry<E> &ent : get_got_entries(ctx)) {
1260:     if (ent.is_relr(ctx) || ent.r_type == R_NONE) {
1261:       buf[ent.idx] = ent.val;
1262:     } else {
1263:       *rel++ = ElfRel<E>(this->shdr.sh_addr + ent.idx * sizeof(Word<E>),
1264:                          ent.r_type,
1265:                          ent.sym ? ent.sym->get_dynsym_idx(ctx) : 0,
1266:                          ent.val);
1267: 
1268:       if (ctx.arg.apply_dynamic_relocs)
1269:         buf[ent.idx] = ent.val;
1270:     }
1271:   }
1272: }
1273: 
1274: template <typename E>
1275: void GotSection<E>::construct_relr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1276:   assert(ctx.arg.pack_dyn_relocs_relr);
1277: 
1278:   std::vector<u64> pos;
1279:   for (GotEntry<E> &ent : get_got_entries(ctx))
1280:     if (ent.is_relr(ctx))
1281:       pos.push_back(ent.idx * sizeof(Word<E>));
1282: 
1283:   relr = encode_relr(pos, sizeof(Word<E>));
1284: }
1285: 
1286: template <typename E>
1287: void GotSection<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1288:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all || ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file)
1289:     return;
1290: 
1291:   this->strtab_size = 0;
1292:   this->num_local_symtab = 0;
1293: 
1294:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : got_syms) {
1295:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$got");
1296:     this->num_local_symtab++;
1297:   }
1298: 
1299:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : gottp_syms) {
1300:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$gottp");
1301:     this->num_local_symtab++;
1302:   }
1303: 
1304:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : tlsgd_syms) {
1305:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$tlsgd");
1306:     this->num_local_symtab++;
1307:   }
1308: 
1309:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : tlsdesc_syms) {
1310:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$tlsdesc");
1311:     this->num_local_symtab++;
1312:   }
1313: 
1314:   if (tlsld_idx != -1) {
1315:     this->strtab_size += sizeof("$tlsld");
1316:     this->num_local_symtab++;
1317:   }
1318: }
1319: 
1320: template <typename E>
1321: void GotSection<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1322:   if (this->strtab_size == 0)
1323:     return;
1324: 
1325:   ElfSym<E> *esym =
1326:     (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset) + this->local_symtab_idx;
1327: 
1328:   u8 *strtab_base = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1329:   u8 *strtab = strtab_base + this->strtab_offset;
1330: 
1331:   auto write = [&](std::string_view name, std::string_view suffix, i64 value) {
1332:     memset(esym, 0, sizeof(*esym));
1333:     esym->st_name = strtab - strtab_base;
1334:     esym->st_type = STT_OBJECT;
1335:     esym->st_shndx = this->shndx;
1336:     esym->st_value = value;
1337:     esym++;
1338: 
1339:     strtab += write_string(strtab, name) - 1;
1340:     strtab += write_string(strtab, suffix);
1341:   };
1342: 
1343:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : got_syms)
1344:     write(sym->name(), "$got", sym->get_got_addr(ctx));
1345: 
1346:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : gottp_syms)
1347:     write(sym->name(), "$gottp", sym->get_gottp_addr(ctx));
1348: 
1349:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : tlsgd_syms)
1350:     write(sym->name(), "$tlsgd", sym->get_tlsgd_addr(ctx));
1351: 
1352:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : tlsdesc_syms)
1353:     write(sym->name(), "$tlsdesc", sym->get_tlsdesc_addr(ctx));
1354: 
1355:   if (tlsld_idx != -1)
1356:     write("", "$tlsld", get_tlsld_addr(ctx));
1357: }
1358: 
1359: template <typename E>
1360: void GotPltSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1361:   this->shdr.sh_size = HDR_SIZE + ctx.plt->symbols.size() * ENTRY_SIZE;
1362: }
1363: 
1364: template <typename E>
1365: void GotPltSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1366:   // On PPC64, it's dynamic loader responsibility to fill the .got.plt
1367:   // section. Dynamic loader finds the address of the first PLT entry by
1368:   // DT_PPC64_GLINK and assumes that each PLT entry is 4 bytes long.
1369:   if constexpr (!is_ppc64<E>) {
1370:     Word<E> *buf = (Word<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
1371: 
1372:     // The first slot of .got.plt points to _DYNAMIC, as requested by
1373:     // the psABI. The second and the third slots are reserved by the psABI.
1374:     static_assert(HDR_SIZE / sizeof(Word<E>) == 3);
1375: 
1376:     buf[0] = ctx.dynamic ? (u64)ctx.dynamic->shdr.sh_addr : 0;
1377:     buf[1] = 0;
1378:     buf[2] = 0;
1379: 
1380:     for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.plt->symbols.size(); i++)
1381:       buf[i + 3] = ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr;
1382:   }
1383: }
1384: 
1385: template <typename E>
1386: void PltSection<E>::add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1387:   assert(!sym->has_plt(ctx));
1388: 
1389:   sym->set_plt_idx(ctx, symbols.size());
1390:   symbols.push_back(sym);
1391:   ctx.dynsym->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1392: }
1393: 
1394: template <typename E>
1395: void PltSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1396:   if (symbols.empty())
1397:     this->shdr.sh_size = 0;
1398:   else
1399:     this->shdr.sh_size = to_plt_offset<E>(symbols.size());
1400: }
1401: 
1402: template <typename E>
1403: void PltSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1404:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + ctx.plt->shdr.sh_offset;
1405:   write_plt_header(ctx, buf);
1406: 
1407:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++)
1408:     write_plt_entry(ctx, buf + to_plt_offset<E>(i), *symbols[i]);
1409: }
1410: 
1411: template <typename E>
1412: void PltSection<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1413:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all || ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file)
1414:     return;
1415: 
1416:   this->num_local_symtab = symbols.size();
1417:   this->strtab_size = 0;
1418: 
1419:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols)
1420:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$plt");
1421: }
1422: 
1423: template <typename E>
1424: void PltSection<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1425:   if (this->strtab_size == 0)
1426:     return;
1427: 
1428:   ElfSym<E> *esym =
1429:     (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset) + this->local_symtab_idx;
1430: 
1431:   u8 *strtab_base = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1432:   u8 *strtab = strtab_base + this->strtab_offset;
1433: 
1434:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols) {
1435:     memset(esym, 0, sizeof(*esym));
1436:     esym->st_name = strtab - strtab_base;
1437:     esym->st_type = STT_FUNC;
1438:     esym->st_shndx = this->shndx;
1439:     esym->st_value = sym->get_plt_addr(ctx);
1440:     esym++;
1441: 
1442:     strtab += write_string(strtab, sym->name()) - 1;
1443:     strtab += write_string(strtab, "$plt");
1444:   }
1445: }
1446: 
1447: template <typename E>
1448: void PltGotSection<E>::add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1449:   assert(!sym->has_plt(ctx));
1450:   assert(sym->has_got(ctx));
1451: 
1452:   sym->set_pltgot_idx(ctx, symbols.size());
1453:   symbols.push_back(sym);
1454:   this->shdr.sh_size = symbols.size() * E::pltgot_size;
1455: }
1456: 
1457: template <typename E>
1458: void PltGotSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1459:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + ctx.pltgot->shdr.sh_offset;
1460:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++)
1461:     write_pltgot_entry(ctx, buf + i * E::pltgot_size, *symbols[i]);
1462: }
1463: 
1464: template <typename E>
1465: void PltGotSection<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1466:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all || ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file)
1467:     return;
1468: 
1469:   this->num_local_symtab = symbols.size();
1470:   this->strtab_size = 0;
1471: 
1472:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols)
1473:     this->strtab_size += sym->name().size() + sizeof("$pltgot");
1474: }
1475: 
1476: template <typename E>
1477: void PltGotSection<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1478:   if (this->strtab_size == 0)
1479:     return;
1480: 
1481:   ElfSym<E> *esym =
1482:     (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset) + this->local_symtab_idx;
1483: 
1484:   u8 *strtab_base = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1485:   u8 *strtab = strtab_base + this->strtab_offset;
1486: 
1487:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols) {
1488:     memset(esym, 0, sizeof(*esym));
1489:     esym->st_name = strtab - strtab_base;
1490:     esym->st_type = STT_FUNC;
1491:     esym->st_shndx = this->shndx;
1492:     esym->st_value = sym->get_plt_addr(ctx);
1493:     esym++;
1494: 
1495:     strtab += write_string(strtab, sym->name()) - 1;
1496:     strtab += write_string(strtab, "$pltgot");
1497:   }
1498: }
1499: 
1500: template <typename E>
1501: void RelPltSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1502:   this->shdr.sh_size = ctx.plt->symbols.size() * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
1503:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
1504: 
1505:   if (!is_sparc<E>)
1506:     this->shdr.sh_info = ctx.gotplt->shndx;
1507: }
1508: 
1509: template <typename E>
1510: void RelPltSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1511:   ElfRel<E> *buf = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
1512: 
1513:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.plt->symbols) {
1514:     // SPARC doesn't have a .got.plt because its role is merged to .plt.
1515:     // On SPARC, .plt is writable (!) and the dynamic linker directly
1516:     // modifies .plt's machine instructions as it resolves dynamic symbols.
1517:     // Therefore, it doesn't need a separate section to store the symbol
1518:     // resolution results. That is of course horrible from the security
1519:     // point of view, though.
1520:     u64 addr = is_sparc<E> ? sym->get_plt_addr(ctx) : sym->get_gotplt_addr(ctx);
1521:     *buf++ = ElfRel<E>(addr, E::R_JUMP_SLOT, sym->get_dynsym_idx(ctx), 0);
1522:   }
1523: }
1524: 
1525: template<typename E>
1526: ElfSym<E> to_output_esym(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, u32 st_name,
1527:                          U32<E> *shn_xindex) {
1528:   ElfSym<E> esym;
1529:   memset(&esym, 0, sizeof(esym));
1530: 
1531:   esym.st_name = st_name;
1532:   esym.st_type = sym.esym().st_type;
1533:   esym.st_size = sym.esym().st_size;
1534: 
1535:   if (sym.is_local(ctx))
1536:     esym.st_bind = STB_LOCAL;
1537:   else if (sym.is_weak)
1538:     esym.st_bind = STB_WEAK;
1539:   else if (sym.file->is_dso)
1540:     esym.st_bind = STB_GLOBAL;
1541:   else
1542:     esym.st_bind = sym.esym().st_bind;
1543: 
1544:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v2<E>)
1545:     esym.ppc_local_entry = sym.esym().ppc_local_entry;
1546: 
1547:   if constexpr (is_alpha<E>)
1548:     esym.alpha_st_other = sym.esym().alpha_st_other;
1549: 
1550:   auto get_st_shndx = [&](Symbol<E> &sym) -> u32 {
1551:     if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag())
1552:       if (frag->is_alive)
1553:         return frag->output_section.shndx;
1554: 
1555:     if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
1556:       if (sym.has_opd(ctx))
1557:         return ctx.extra.opd->shndx;
1558: 
1559:     if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section()) {
1560:       if (isec->is_alive)
1561:         return isec->output_section->shndx;
1562:       else if (isec->is_killed_by_icf())
1563:         return isec->leader->output_section->shndx;
1564:     }
1565: 
1566:     return SHN_UNDEF;
1567:   };
1568: 
1569:   u32 shndx = 0;
1570:   if (sym.has_copyrel) {
1571:     shndx = sym.is_copyrel_readonly ? ctx.copyrel_relro->shndx : ctx.copyrel->shndx;
1572:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx);
1573:   } else if (sym.file->is_dso || sym.esym().is_undef()) {
1574:     shndx = SHN_UNDEF;
1575:     if (sym.is_canonical)
1576:       esym.st_value = sym.get_plt_addr(ctx);
1577:   } else if (Chunk<E> *osec = sym.get_output_section()) {
1578:     // Linker-synthesized symbols
1579:     shndx = osec->shndx;
1580:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx);
1581:   } else if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag()) {
1582:     // Section fragment
1583:     shndx = frag->output_section.shndx;
1584:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx);
1585:   } else if (!sym.get_input_section()) {
1586:     // Absolute symbol
1587:     shndx = SHN_ABS;
1588:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx);
1589:   } else if (sym.get_type() == STT_TLS) {
1590:     shndx = get_st_shndx(sym);
1591:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx) - ctx.tls_begin;
1592:   } else {
1593:     shndx = get_st_shndx(sym);
1594:     esym.st_visibility = sym.visibility;
1595:     esym.st_value = sym.get_addr(ctx, NO_PLT);
1596:   }
1597: 
1598:   // Symbol's st_shndx is only 16 bits wide, so we can't store a large
1599:   // section index there. If the total number of sections is equal to
1600:   // or greater than SHN_LORESERVE (= 65280), the real index is stored
1601:   // to a SHT_SYMTAB_SHNDX section which contains a parallel array of
1602:   // the symbol table.
1603:   if (shn_xindex) {
1604:     *shn_xindex = shndx;
1605:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_XINDEX;
1606:   } else {
1607:     if (shndx >= SHN_LORESERVE && shndx != SHN_ABS && shndx != SHN_COMMON)
1608:       Fatal(ctx) << sym << ": internal error: output symbol index too large: "
1609:                  << shndx;
1610:     esym.st_shndx = shndx;
1611:   }
1612: 
1613:   return esym;
1614: }
1615: 
1616: template <typename E>
1617: void DynsymSection<E>::add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1618:   if (symbols.empty())
1619:     symbols.resize(1);
1620: 
1621:   if (sym->get_dynsym_idx(ctx) == -1) {
1622:     sym->set_dynsym_idx(ctx, -2);
1623:     symbols.push_back(sym);
1624:   }
1625: }
1626: 
1627: template <typename E>
1628: void DynsymSection<E>::finalize(Context<E> &ctx) {
1629:   Timer t(ctx, "DynsymSection::finalize");
1630:   if (symbols.empty())
1631:     return;
1632: 
1633:   // Sort symbols. In any symtab, local symbols must precede global symbols.
1634:   auto first_global = std::stable_partition(symbols.begin() + 1, symbols.end(),
1635:                                             [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
1636:     return sym->is_local(ctx);
1637:   });
1638: 
1639:   // We also place undefined symbols before defined symbols for .gnu.hash.
1640:   // Defined symbols are sorted by their hashes for .gnu.hash.
1641:   if (ctx.gnu_hash) {
1642:     // Count the number of exported symbols to compute the size of .gnu.hash.
1643:     i64 num_exported = 0;
1644:     for (i64 i = 1; i < symbols.size(); i++)
1645:       if (symbols[i]->is_exported)
1646:         num_exported++;
1647: 
1648:     u32 num_buckets = num_exported / ctx.gnu_hash->LOAD_FACTOR + 1;
1649:     ctx.gnu_hash->num_buckets = num_buckets;
1650: 
1651:     tbb::parallel_for((i64)(first_global - symbols.begin()), (i64)symbols.size(),
1652:                       [&](i64 i) {
1653:       Symbol<E> &sym = *symbols[i];
1654:       sym.set_dynsym_idx(ctx, i);
1655:       sym.set_djb_hash(ctx, djb_hash(sym.name()));
1656:     });
1657: 
1658:     tbb::parallel_sort(first_global, symbols.end(),
1659:                        [&](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) {
1660:       if (a->is_exported != b->is_exported)
1661:         return b->is_exported;
1662: 
1663:       u32 h1 = a->get_djb_hash(ctx) % num_buckets;
1664:       u32 h2 = b->get_djb_hash(ctx) % num_buckets;
1665:       return std::tuple(h1, a->get_dynsym_idx(ctx)) <
1666:              std::tuple(h2, b->get_dynsym_idx(ctx));
1667:     });
1668:   }
1669: 
1670:   // Compute .dynstr size
1671:   ctx.dynstr->dynsym_offset = ctx.dynstr->shdr.sh_size;
1672: 
1673:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> size;
1674:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)1, (i64)symbols.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1675:     symbols[i]->set_dynsym_idx(ctx, i);
1676:     size.local() += symbols[i]->name().size() + 1;
1677:   });
1678: 
1679:   ctx.dynstr->shdr.sh_size += size.combine(std::plus());
1680: 
1681:   // ELF's symbol table sh_info holds the offset of the first global symbol.
1682:   this->shdr.sh_info = first_global - symbols.begin();
1683: }
1684: 
1685: template <typename E>
1686: void DynsymSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1687:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynstr->shndx;
1688:   this->shdr.sh_size = sizeof(ElfSym<E>) * symbols.size();
1689: }
1690: 
1691: template <typename E>
1692: void DynsymSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1693:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
1694:   memset(base, 0, sizeof(ElfSym<E>));
1695:   i64 name_offset = ctx.dynstr->dynsym_offset;
1696: 
1697:   for (i64 i = 1; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
1698:     Symbol<E> &sym = *symbols[i];
1699:     ElfSym<E> &esym =
1700:       *(ElfSym<E> *)(base + sym.get_dynsym_idx(ctx) * sizeof(ElfSym<E>));
1701: 
1702:     esym = to_output_esym(ctx, sym, name_offset, nullptr);
1703:     name_offset += sym.name().size() + 1;
1704:     assert(esym.st_bind != STB_LOCAL || i < this->shdr.sh_info);
1705:   }
1706: }
1707: 
1708: template <typename E>
1709: void HashSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1710:   if (ctx.dynsym->symbols.empty())
1711:     return;
1712: 
1713:   i64 header_size = 8;
1714:   i64 num_slots = ctx.dynsym->symbols.size();
1715:   this->shdr.sh_size = header_size + num_slots * 8;
1716:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
1717: }
1718: 
1719: template <typename E>
1720: void HashSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1721:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
1722:   memset(base, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
1723: 
1724:   std::span<Symbol<E> *> syms = ctx.dynsym->symbols;
1725:   U32<E> *hdr = (U32<E> *)base;
1726:   U32<E> *buckets = (U32<E> *)(base + 8);
1727:   U32<E> *chains = buckets + syms.size();
1728: 
1729:   hdr[0] = hdr[1] = syms.size();
1730: 
1731:   std::vector<u32> hashes(syms.size());
1732:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)1, (i64)syms.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1733:     hashes[i] = elf_hash(syms[i]->name()) % syms.size();
1734:   });
1735: 
1736:   for (i64 i = 1; i < syms.size(); i++) {
1737:     i64 h = hashes[i];
1738:     chains[syms[i]->get_dynsym_idx(ctx)] = buckets[h];
1739:     buckets[h] = syms[i]->get_dynsym_idx(ctx);
1740:   }
1741: }
1742: 
1743: template <typename E>
1744: std::span<Symbol<E> *>
1745: GnuHashSection<E>::get_exported_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1746:   std::span<Symbol<E> *> syms = ctx.dynsym->symbols;
1747:   auto it = std::partition_point(syms.begin() + 1, syms.end(), [](Symbol<E> *sym) {
1748:     return !sym->is_exported;
1749:   });
1750:   return syms.subspan(it - syms.begin());
1751: }
1752: 
1753: template <typename E>
1754: void GnuHashSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1755:   if (ctx.dynsym->symbols.empty())
1756:     return;
1757: 
1758:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
1759: 
1760:   i64 num_exported = get_exported_symbols(ctx).size();
1761:   if (num_exported) {
1762:     // We allocate 12 bits for each symbol in the bloom filter.
1763:     i64 num_bits = num_exported * 12;
1764:     num_bloom = bit_ceil(num_bits / (sizeof(Word<E>) * 8));
1765:   }
1766: 
1767:   this->shdr.sh_size = HEADER_SIZE;                  // Header
1768:   this->shdr.sh_size += num_bloom * sizeof(Word<E>); // Bloom filter
1769:   this->shdr.sh_size += num_buckets * 4;             // Hash buckets
1770:   this->shdr.sh_size += num_exported * 4;            // Hash values
1771: }
1772: 
1773: template <typename E>
1774: void GnuHashSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1775:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
1776:   memset(base, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
1777: 
1778:   std::span<Symbol<E> *> syms = get_exported_symbols(ctx);
1779:   std::vector<u32> indices(syms.size());
1780:   i64 exported_offset = ctx.dynsym->symbols.size() - syms.size();
1781: 
1782:   *(U32<E> *)base = num_buckets;
1783:   *(U32<E> *)(base + 4) = exported_offset;
1784:   *(U32<E> *)(base + 8) = num_bloom;
1785:   *(U32<E> *)(base + 12) = BLOOM_SHIFT;
1786: 
1787:   // Write a bloom filter
1788:   Word<E> *bloom = (Word<E> *)(base + HEADER_SIZE);
1789: 
1790:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++) {
1791:     constexpr i64 word_bits = sizeof(Word<E>) * 8;
1792: 
1793:     i64 h = syms[i]->get_djb_hash(ctx);
1794:     indices[i] = h % num_buckets;
1795: 
1796:     i64 idx = (h / word_bits) % num_bloom;
1797:     bloom[idx] |= 1LL << (h % word_bits);
1798:     bloom[idx] |= 1LL << ((h >> BLOOM_SHIFT) % word_bits);
1799:   }
1800: 
1801:   // Write hash bucket indices
1802:   U32<E> *buckets = (U32<E> *)(bloom + num_bloom);
1803: 
1804:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++)
1805:     if (!buckets[indices[i]])
1806:       buckets[indices[i]] = i + exported_offset;
1807: 
1808:   // Write a hash table
1809:   U32<E> *table = buckets + num_buckets;
1810: 
1811:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++) {
1812:     // The last entry in a chain must be terminated with an entry with
1813:     // least-significant bit 1.
1814:     u32 h = syms[i]->get_djb_hash(ctx);
1815:     if (i == syms.size() - 1 || indices[i] != indices[i + 1])
1816:       table[i] = h | 1;
1817:     else
1818:       table[i] = h & ~1;
1819:   }
1820: }
1821: 
1822: template <typename E>
1823: std::string_view
1824: get_merged_output_name(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name, u64 flags) {
1825:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable && !ctx.arg.relocatable_merge_sections)
1826:     return name;
1827:   if (ctx.arg.unique && ctx.arg.unique->match(name))
1828:     return name;
1829:   if (name == ".rodata" || name.starts_with(".rodata."))
1830:     return (flags & SHF_STRINGS) ? ".rodata.str" : ".rodata.cst";
1831:   return name;
1832: }
1833: 
1834: template <typename E>
1835: MergedSection<E>::MergedSection(std::string_view name, u64 flags, u32 type) {
1836:   this->name = name;
1837:   this->shdr.sh_flags = flags;
1838:   this->shdr.sh_type = type;
1839: }
1840: 
1841: template <typename E>
1842: MergedSection<E> *
1843: MergedSection<E>::get_instance(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name,
1844:                                u64 type, u64 flags) {
1845:   name = get_merged_output_name(ctx, name, flags);
1846:   flags = flags & ~(u64)SHF_GROUP & ~(u64)SHF_COMPRESSED;
1847: 
1848:   auto find = [&]() -> MergedSection * {
1849:     for (std::unique_ptr<MergedSection<E>> &osec : ctx.merged_sections)
1850:       if (std::tuple(name, flags, type) ==
1851:           std::tuple(osec->name, osec->shdr.sh_flags, osec->shdr.sh_type))
1852:         return osec.get();
1853:     return nullptr;
1854:   };
1855: 
1856:   // Search for an exiting output section.
1857:   static std::shared_mutex mu;
1858:   {
1859:     std::shared_lock lock(mu);
1860:     if (MergedSection *osec = find())
1861:       return osec;
1862:   }
1863: 
1864:   // Create a new output section.
1865:   std::unique_lock lock(mu);
1866:   if (MergedSection *osec = find())
1867:     return osec;
1868: 
1869:   MergedSection *osec = new MergedSection(name, flags, type);
1870:   ctx.merged_sections.emplace_back(osec);
1871:   return osec;
1872: }
1873: 
1874: template <typename E>
1875: SectionFragment<E> *
1876: MergedSection<E>::insert(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view data, u64 hash,
1877:                          i64 p2align) {
1878:   std::call_once(once_flag, [&] {
1879:     // We aim 2/3 occupation ratio
1880:     map.resize(estimator.get_cardinality() * 3 / 2);
1881:   });
1882: 
1883:   // Even if GC is enabled, we garbage-collect only memory-mapped strings.
1884:   // Non-memory-allocated strings are typically identifiers used by debug info.
1885:   // To remove such strings, use the `strip` command.
1886:   bool is_alive = !ctx.arg.gc_sections || !(this->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
1887: 
1888:   SectionFragment<E> *frag;
1889:   bool inserted;
1890:   std::tie(frag, inserted) =
1891:     map.insert(data, hash, SectionFragment(this, is_alive));
1892:   update_maximum(frag->p2align, p2align);
1893:   return frag;
1894: }
1895: 
1896: template <typename E>
1897: void MergedSection<E>::assign_offsets(Context<E> &ctx) {
1898:   std::vector<i64> sizes(map.NUM_SHARDS);
1899:   std::vector<i64> max_p2aligns(map.NUM_SHARDS);
1900:   shard_offsets.resize(map.NUM_SHARDS + 1);
1901: 
1902:   i64 shard_size = map.nbuckets / map.NUM_SHARDS;
1903: 
1904:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, map.NUM_SHARDS, [&](i64 i) {
1905:     struct KeyVal {
1906:       std::string_view key;
1907:       SectionFragment<E> *val;
1908:     };
1909: 
1910:     std::vector<KeyVal> fragments;
1911:     fragments.reserve(shard_size);
1912: 
1913:     for (i64 j = shard_size * i; j < shard_size * (i + 1); j++)
1914:       if (const char *key = map.get_key(j))
1915:         if (SectionFragment<E> &frag = map.values[j]; frag.is_alive)
1916:           fragments.push_back({{key, map.key_sizes[j]}, &frag});
1917: 
1918:     // Sort fragments to make output deterministic.
1919:     tbb::parallel_sort(fragments.begin(), fragments.end(),
1920:                        [](const KeyVal &a, const KeyVal &b) {
1921:       return std::tuple{(u32)a.val->p2align, a.key.size(), a.key} <
1922:              std::tuple{(u32)b.val->p2align, b.key.size(), b.key};
1923:     });
1924: 
1925:     // Assign offsets.
1926:     i64 offset = 0;
1927:     i64 p2align = 0;
1928: 
1929:     for (KeyVal &kv : fragments) {
1930:       SectionFragment<E> &frag = *kv.val;
1931:       offset = align_to(offset, 1 << frag.p2align);
1932:       frag.offset = offset;
1933:       offset += kv.key.size();
1934:       p2align = std::max<i64>(p2align, frag.p2align);
1935:     }
1936: 
1937:     sizes[i] = offset;
1938:     max_p2aligns[i] = p2align;
1939: 
1940:     static Counter merged_strings("merged_strings");
1941:     merged_strings += fragments.size();
1942:   });
1943: 
1944:   i64 p2align = 0;
1945:   for (i64 x : max_p2aligns)
1946:     p2align = std::max(p2align, x);
1947: 
1948:   for (i64 i = 1; i < map.NUM_SHARDS + 1; i++)
1949:     shard_offsets[i] =
1950:       align_to(shard_offsets[i - 1] + sizes[i - 1], 1 << p2align);
1951: 
1952:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)1, map.NUM_SHARDS, [&](i64 i) {
1953:     for (i64 j = shard_size * i; j < shard_size * (i + 1); j++)
1954:       if (SectionFragment<E> &frag = map.values[j]; frag.is_alive)
1955:         frag.offset += shard_offsets[i];
1956:   });
1957: 
1958:   this->shdr.sh_size = shard_offsets[map.NUM_SHARDS];
1959:   this->shdr.sh_addralign = 1 << p2align;
1960: }
1961: 
1962: template <typename E>
1963: void MergedSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1964:   write_to(ctx, ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
1965: }
1966: 
1967: template <typename E>
1968: void MergedSection<E>::write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
1969:   i64 shard_size = map.nbuckets / map.NUM_SHARDS;
1970: 
1971:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, map.NUM_SHARDS, [&](i64 i) {
1972:     memset(buf + shard_offsets[i], 0, shard_offsets[i + 1] - shard_offsets[i]);
1973: 
1974:     for (i64 j = shard_size * i; j < shard_size * (i + 1); j++)
1975:       if (const char *key = map.get_key(j))
1976:         if (SectionFragment<E> &frag = map.values[j]; frag.is_alive)
1977:           memcpy(buf + frag.offset, key, map.key_sizes[j]);
1978:   });
1979: }
1980: 
1981: template <typename E>
1982: void MergedSection<E>::print_stats(Context<E> &ctx) {
1983:   i64 used = 0;
1984:   for (i64 i = 0; i < map.nbuckets; i++)
1985:     if (map.get_key(i))
1986:       used++;
1987: 
1988:   SyncOut(ctx) << this->name
1989:                << " estimation=" << estimator.get_cardinality()
1990:                << " actual=" << used;
1991: }
1992: 
1993: template <typename E>
1994: void EhFrameSection<E>::construct(Context<E> &ctx) {
1995:   Timer t(ctx, "eh_frame");
1996: 
1997:   // If .eh_frame is missing in all input files, we don't want to
1998:   // create an output .eh_frame section.
1999:   if (std::all_of(ctx.objs.begin(), ctx.objs.end(),
2000:                   [](ObjectFile<E> *file) { return file->cies.empty(); })) {
2001:     this->shdr.sh_size = 0;
2002:     return;
2003:   }
2004: 
2005:   // Remove dead FDEs and assign them offsets within their corresponding
2006:   // CIE group.
2007:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2008:     std::erase_if(file->fdes, [](FdeRecord<E> &fde) { return !fde.is_alive; });
2009: 
2010:     i64 offset = 0;
2011:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes) {
2012:       fde.output_offset = offset;
2013:       offset += fde.size(*file);
2014:     }
2015:     file->fde_size = offset;
2016:   });
2017: 
2018:   // Uniquify CIEs and assign offsets to them.
2019:   std::vector<CieRecord<E> *> leaders;
2020:   auto find_leader = [&](CieRecord<E> &cie) -> CieRecord<E> * {
2021:     for (CieRecord<E> *leader : leaders)
2022:       if (cie.equals(*leader))
2023:         return leader;
2024:     return nullptr;
2025:   };
2026: 
2027:   i64 offset = 0;
2028:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
2029:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies) {
2030:       if (CieRecord<E> *leader = find_leader(cie)) {
2031:         cie.output_offset = leader->output_offset;
2032:       } else {
2033:         cie.output_offset = offset;
2034:         cie.is_leader = true;
2035:         offset += cie.size();
2036:         leaders.push_back(&cie);
2037:       }
2038:     }
2039:   }
2040: 
2041:   // Assign FDE offsets to files.
2042:   i64 idx = 0;
2043:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
2044:     file->fde_idx = idx;
2045:     idx += file->fdes.size();
2046: 
2047:     file->fde_offset = offset;
2048:     offset += file->fde_size;
2049:   }
2050: 
2051:   // .eh_frame must end with a null word.
2052:   this->shdr.sh_size = offset + 4;
2053: }
2054: 
2055: // Write to .eh_frame and .eh_frame_hdr.
2056: template <typename E>
2057: void EhFrameSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2058:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
2059: 
2060:   struct HdrEntry {
2061:     I32<E> init_addr;
2062:     I32<E> fde_addr;
2063:   };
2064: 
2065:   HdrEntry *eh_hdr_begin = nullptr;
2066:   if (ctx.eh_frame_hdr)
2067:     eh_hdr_begin = (HdrEntry *)(ctx.buf + ctx.eh_frame_hdr->shdr.sh_offset +
2068:                    EhFrameHdrSection<E>::HEADER_SIZE);
2069: 
2070:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2071:     // Copy CIEs.
2072:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies) {
2073:       if (!cie.is_leader)
2074:         continue;
2075: 
2076:       std::string_view contents = cie.get_contents();
2077:       memcpy(base + cie.output_offset, contents.data(), contents.size());
2078: 
2079:       if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
2080:         continue;
2081: 
2082:       for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : cie.get_rels()) {
2083:         assert(rel.r_offset - cie.input_offset < contents.size());
2084: 
2085:         Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[rel.r_sym];
2086:         u64 loc = cie.output_offset + rel.r_offset - cie.input_offset;
2087:         u64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx) + get_addend(cie.input_section, rel);
2088:         apply_reloc(ctx, rel, loc, val);
2089:       }
2090:     }
2091: 
2092:     // Copy FDEs.
2093:     for (i64 i = 0; i < file->fdes.size(); i++) {
2094:       FdeRecord<E> &fde = file->fdes[i];
2095:       i64 offset = file->fde_offset + fde.output_offset;
2096: 
2097:       std::string_view contents = fde.get_contents(*file);
2098:       memcpy(base + offset, contents.data(), contents.size());
2099: 
2100:       CieRecord<E> &cie = file->cies[fde.cie_idx];
2101:       *(U32<E> *)(base + offset + 4) = offset + 4 - cie.output_offset;
2102: 
2103:       if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
2104:         continue;
2105: 
2106:       bool is_first = true;
2107:       for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : fde.get_rels(*file)) {
2108:         assert(rel.r_offset - fde.input_offset < contents.size());
2109: 
2110:         Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[rel.r_sym];
2111:         u64 loc = offset + rel.r_offset - fde.input_offset;
2112:         u64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx) + get_addend(cie.input_section, rel);
2113:         apply_reloc(ctx, rel, loc, val);
2114: 
2115:         if (eh_hdr_begin && is_first) {
2116:           // Write to .eh_frame_hdr
2117:           HdrEntry &ent = eh_hdr_begin[file->fde_idx + i];
2118:           u64 sh_addr = ctx.eh_frame_hdr->shdr.sh_addr;
2119:           ent.init_addr = val - sh_addr;
2120:           ent.fde_addr = this->shdr.sh_addr + offset - sh_addr;
2121:           is_first = false;
2122:         }
2123:       }
2124:     }
2125:   });
2126: 
2127:   // Write a terminator.
2128:   *(U32<E> *)(base + this->shdr.sh_size - 4) = 0;
2129: 
2130:   // Sort .eh_frame_hdr contents.
2131:   if (eh_hdr_begin) {
2132:     tbb::parallel_sort(eh_hdr_begin, eh_hdr_begin + ctx.eh_frame_hdr->num_fdes,
2133:                       [](const HdrEntry &a, const HdrEntry &b) {
2134:       return a.init_addr < b.init_addr;
2135:     });
2136:   }
2137: }
2138: 
2139: template <typename E>
2140: void EhFrameHdrSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2141:   num_fdes = 0;
2142:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
2143:     num_fdes += file->fdes.size();
2144:   this->shdr.sh_size = HEADER_SIZE + num_fdes * 8;
2145: }
2146: 
2147: template <typename E>
2148: void EhFrameHdrSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2149:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
2150: 
2151:   // Write a header. The actual table is written by EhFrameHdr<E>::copy_buf.
2152:   base[0] = 1;
2153:   base[1] = DW_EH_PE_pcrel | DW_EH_PE_sdata4;
2154:   base[2] = DW_EH_PE_udata4;
2155:   base[3] = DW_EH_PE_datarel | DW_EH_PE_sdata4;
2156: 
2157:   *(U32<E> *)(base + 4) = ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_addr - this->shdr.sh_addr - 4;
2158:   *(U32<E> *)(base + 8) = num_fdes;
2159: }
2160: 
2161: template <typename E>
2162: void EhFrameRelocSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2163:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> count;
2164: 
2165:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2166:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies)
2167:       if (cie.is_leader)
2168:         count.local() += cie.get_rels().size();
2169: 
2170:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes)
2171:       count.local() += fde.get_rels(*file).size();
2172:   });
2173: 
2174:   this->shdr.sh_size = count.combine(std::plus()) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
2175:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.symtab->shndx;
2176:   this->shdr.sh_info = ctx.eh_frame->shndx;
2177: }
2178: 
2179: template <typename E>
2180: void EhFrameRelocSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2181:   ElfRel<E> *buf = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
2182: 
2183:   auto copy = [&](ObjectFile<E> &file, InputSection<E> &isec,
2184:                   const ElfRel<E> &r, u64 offset) {
2185:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[r.r_sym];
2186:     memset(buf, 0, sizeof(*buf));
2187: 
2188:     if (sym.esym().st_type == STT_SECTION) {
2189:       // We discard section symbols in input files and re-create new
2190:       // ones for each output section. So we need to adjust relocations'
2191:       // addends if they refer a section symbol.
2192:       InputSection<E> *target = sym.get_input_section();
2193:       buf->r_sym = target->output_section->shndx;
2194: 
2195:       if constexpr (E::is_rela)
2196:         buf->r_addend = get_addend(isec, r) + target->offset;
2197:       else if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
2198:         write_addend(ctx.buf + ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_offset + offset,
2199:                      get_addend(isec, r) + target->offset, r);
2200:     } else {
2201:       buf->r_sym = sym.get_output_sym_idx(ctx);
2202:       if constexpr (E::is_rela)
2203:         buf->r_addend = get_addend(isec, r);
2204:     }
2205: 
2206:     buf->r_offset = ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_addr + offset;
2207:     buf->r_type = r.r_type;
2208:     buf++;
2209:   };
2210: 
2211:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
2212:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : file->cies)
2213:       if (cie.is_leader)
2214:         for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : cie.get_rels())
2215:           copy(*file, cie.input_section, rel,
2216:                cie.output_offset + rel.r_offset - cie.input_offset);
2217: 
2218:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes) {
2219:       i64 offset = file->fde_offset + fde.output_offset;
2220:       for (const ElfRel<E> &rel : fde.get_rels(*file))
2221:         copy(*file, file->cies[fde.cie_idx].input_section, rel,
2222:              offset + rel.r_offset - fde.input_offset);
2223:     }
2224:   }
2225: }
2226: 
2227: template <typename E>
2228: void CopyrelSection<E>::add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
2229:   if (sym->has_copyrel)
2230:     return;
2231: 
2232:   assert(!ctx.arg.shared);
2233:   assert(sym->file->is_dso);
2234: 
2235:   symbols.push_back(sym);
2236: 
2237:   SharedFile<E> &file = *(SharedFile<E> *)sym->file;
2238:   i64 alignment = file.get_alignment(sym);
2239:   u64 offset = align_to(this->shdr.sh_size, alignment);
2240: 
2241:   this->shdr.sh_size = offset + sym->esym().st_size;
2242:   this->shdr.sh_addralign = std::max<i64>(alignment, this->shdr.sh_addralign);
2243: 
2244:   // We need to create dynamic symbols not only for this particular symbol
2245:   // but also for its aliases (i.e. other symbols at the same address)
2246:   // becasue otherwise the aliases are broken apart at runtime.
2247:   // For example, `environ`, `_environ` and `__environ` in libc.so are
2248:   // aliases. If one of the symbols is copied by a copy relocation, other
2249:   // symbols have to refer to the copied place as well.
2250:   for (Symbol<E> *sym2 : file.find_aliases(sym)) {
2251:     sym2->add_aux(ctx);
2252:     sym2->is_imported = true;
2253:     sym2->is_exported = true;
2254:     sym2->has_copyrel = true;
2255:     sym2->is_copyrel_readonly = is_relro;
2256:     sym2->value = offset;
2257:     ctx.dynsym->add_symbol(ctx, sym2);
2258:   }
2259: }
2260: 
2261: template <typename E>
2262: void CopyrelSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2263:   // SHT_NOBITS sections (i.e. BSS sections) have to be at the end of
2264:   // a segment, so a .copyrel.rel.ro usually requires one extra
2265:   // segment for it. We turn a .copyrel.rel.ro into a regular section
2266:   // if it is very small to avoid the cost of the extra segment.
2267:   constexpr i64 threshold = 4096;
2268:   if (is_relro && ctx.arg.z_relro && this->shdr.sh_size < threshold)
2269:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
2270: }
2271: 
2272: template <typename E>
2273: void CopyrelSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2274:   if (this->shdr.sh_type == SHT_PROGBITS)
2275:     memset(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
2276: 
2277:   ElfRel<E> *rel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
2278:                                  this->reldyn_offset);
2279: 
2280:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : symbols)
2281:     *rel++ = ElfRel<E>(sym->get_addr(ctx), E::R_COPY, sym->get_dynsym_idx(ctx),
2282:                        0);
2283: }
2284: 
2285: template <typename E>
2286: void VersymSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2287:   this->shdr.sh_size = contents.size() * sizeof(contents[0]);
2288:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynsym->shndx;
2289: }
2290: 
2291: template <typename E>
2292: void VersymSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2293:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, contents);
2294: }
2295: 
2296: template <typename E>
2297: void VerneedSection<E>::construct(Context<E> &ctx) {
2298:   Timer t(ctx, "fill_verneed");
2299: 
2300:   if (ctx.dynsym->symbols.empty())
2301:     return;
2302: 
2303:   // Create a list of versioned symbols and sort by file and version.
2304:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> syms(ctx.dynsym->symbols.begin() + 1,
2305:                                 ctx.dynsym->symbols.end());
2306: 
2307:   std::erase_if(syms, [](Symbol<E> *sym) {
2308:     return !sym->file->is_dso || sym->ver_idx <= VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED;
2309:   });
2310: 
2311:   if (syms.empty())
2312:     return;
2313: 
2314:   sort(syms, [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) {
2315:     return std::tuple(((SharedFile<E> *)a->file)->soname, a->ver_idx) <
2316:            std::tuple(((SharedFile<E> *)b->file)->soname, b->ver_idx);
2317:   });
2318: 
2319:   // Resize of .gnu.version
2320:   ctx.versym->contents.resize(ctx.dynsym->symbols.size(), 1);
2321:   ctx.versym->contents[0] = 0;
2322: 
2323:   // Allocate a large enough buffer for .gnu.version_r.
2324:   contents.resize((sizeof(ElfVerneed<E>) + sizeof(ElfVernaux<E>)) * syms.size());
2325: 
2326:   // Fill .gnu.version_r.
2327:   u8 *buf = (u8 *)&contents[0];
2328:   u8 *ptr = buf;
2329:   ElfVerneed<E> *verneed = nullptr;
2330:   ElfVernaux<E> *aux = nullptr;
2331: 
2332:   u16 veridx = VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED + ctx.arg.version_definitions.size();
2333: 
2334:   auto start_group = [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
2335:     this->shdr.sh_info++;
2336:     if (verneed)
2337:       verneed->vn_next = ptr - (u8 *)verneed;
2338: 
2339:     verneed = (ElfVerneed<E> *)ptr;
2340:     ptr += sizeof(*verneed);
2341:     verneed->vn_version = 1;
2342:     verneed->vn_file = ctx.dynstr->find_string(((SharedFile<E> *)file)->soname);
2343:     verneed->vn_aux = sizeof(ElfVerneed<E>);
2344:     aux = nullptr;
2345:   };
2346: 
2347:   auto add_entry = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
2348:     verneed->vn_cnt++;
2349: 
2350:     if (aux)
2351:       aux->vna_next = sizeof(ElfVernaux<E>);
2352:     aux = (ElfVernaux<E> *)ptr;
2353:     ptr += sizeof(*aux);
2354: 
2355:     std::string_view verstr = sym->get_version();
2356:     aux->vna_hash = elf_hash(verstr);
2357:     aux->vna_other = ++veridx;
2358:     aux->vna_name = ctx.dynstr->add_string(verstr);
2359:   };
2360: 
2361:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++) {
2362:     if (i == 0 || syms[i - 1]->file != syms[i]->file) {
2363:       start_group(syms[i]->file);
2364:       add_entry(syms[i]);
2365:     } else if (syms[i - 1]->ver_idx != syms[i]->ver_idx) {
2366:       add_entry(syms[i]);
2367:     }
2368: 
2369:     ctx.versym->contents[syms[i]->get_dynsym_idx(ctx)] = veridx;
2370:   }
2371: 
2372:   // Resize .gnu.version_r to fit to its contents.
2373:   contents.resize(ptr - buf);
2374: }
2375: 
2376: template <typename E>
2377: void VerneedSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2378:   this->shdr.sh_size = contents.size();
