2379:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynstr->shndx;
2380: }
2381: 
2382: template <typename E>
2383: void VerneedSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2384:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, contents);
2385: }
2386: 
2387: template <typename E>
2388: void VerdefSection<E>::construct(Context<E> &ctx) {
2389:   Timer t(ctx, "fill_verdef");
2390: 
2391:   if (ctx.arg.version_definitions.empty())
2392:     return;
2393: 
2394:   // Resize .gnu.version
2395:   ctx.versym->contents.resize(ctx.dynsym->symbols.size(), 1);
2396:   ctx.versym->contents[0] = 0;
2397: 
2398:   // Allocate a buffer for .gnu.version_d.
2399:   contents.resize((sizeof(ElfVerdef<E>) + sizeof(ElfVerdaux<E>)) *
2400:                   (ctx.arg.version_definitions.size() + 1));
2401: 
2402:   u8 *buf = (u8 *)&contents[0];
2403:   u8 *ptr = buf;
2404:   ElfVerdef<E> *verdef = nullptr;
2405: 
2406:   auto write = [&](std::string_view verstr, i64 idx, i64 flags) {
2407:     this->shdr.sh_info++;
2408:     if (verdef)
2409:       verdef->vd_next = ptr - (u8 *)verdef;
2410: 
2411:     verdef = (ElfVerdef<E> *)ptr;
2412:     ptr += sizeof(ElfVerdef<E>);
2413: 
2414:     verdef->vd_version = 1;
2415:     verdef->vd_flags = flags;
2416:     verdef->vd_ndx = idx;
2417:     verdef->vd_cnt = 1;
2418:     verdef->vd_hash = elf_hash(verstr);
2419:     verdef->vd_aux = sizeof(ElfVerdef<E>);
2420: 
2421:     ElfVerdaux<E> *aux = (ElfVerdaux<E> *)ptr;
2422:     ptr += sizeof(ElfVerdaux<E>);
2423:     aux->vda_name = ctx.dynstr->add_string(verstr);
2424:   };
2425: 
2426:   std::string_view basename = ctx.arg.soname.empty() ?
2427:     ctx.arg.output : ctx.arg.soname;
2428:   write(basename, 1, VER_FLG_BASE);
2429: 
2430:   i64 idx = 2;
2431:   for (std::string_view verstr : ctx.arg.version_definitions)
2432:     write(verstr, idx++, 0);
2433: 
2434:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : std::span<Symbol<E> *>(ctx.dynsym->symbols).subspan(1))
2435:     ctx.versym->contents[sym->get_dynsym_idx(ctx)] = sym->ver_idx;
2436: }
2437: 
2438: template <typename E>
2439: void VerdefSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2440:   this->shdr.sh_size = contents.size();
2441:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.dynstr->shndx;
2442: }
2443: 
2444: template <typename E>
2445: void VerdefSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2446:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset, contents);
2447: }
2448: 
2449: inline i64 BuildId::size() const {
2450:   switch (kind) {
2451:   case HEX:
2452:     return value.size();
2453:   case HASH:
2454:     return hash_size;
2455:   case UUID:
2456:     return 16;
2457:   default:
2458:     unreachable();
2459:   }
2460: }
2461: 
2462: template <typename E>
2463: void BuildIdSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2464:   this->shdr.sh_size = HEADER_SIZE + ctx.arg.build_id.size();
2465: }
2466: 
2467: template <typename E>
2468: void BuildIdSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2469:   U32<E> *base = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
2470:   memset(base, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
2471:   base[0] = 4;                          // Name size
2472:   base[1] = ctx.arg.build_id.size();    // Hash size
2473:   base[2] = NT_GNU_BUILD_ID;            // Type
2474:   memcpy(base + 3, "GNU", 4);           // Name string
2475: }
2476: 
2477: template <typename E>
2478: static void compute_sha256(Context<E> &ctx, i64 offset) {
2479:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf;
2480:   i64 filesize = ctx.output_file->filesize;
2481: 
2482:   i64 shard_size = 4096 * 1024;
2483:   i64 num_shards = align_to(filesize, shard_size) / shard_size;
2484:   std::vector<u8> shards(num_shards * SHA256_SIZE);
2485: 
2486:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, num_shards, [&](i64 i) {
2487:     u8 *begin = buf + shard_size * i;
2488:     u8 *end = (i == num_shards - 1) ? buf + filesize : begin + shard_size;
2489:     sha256_hash(begin, end - begin, shards.data() + i * SHA256_SIZE);
2490: 
2491: #ifndef _WIN32
2492:     // We call munmap early for each chunk so that the last munmap
2493:     // gets cheaper. We assume that the .note.build-id section is
2494:     // at the beginning of an output file. This is an ugly performance
2495:     // hack, but we can save about 30 ms for a 2 GiB output.
2496:     if (i > 0 && ctx.output_file->is_mmapped)
2497:       munmap(begin, end - begin);
2498: #endif
2499:    });
2500: 
2501:   assert(ctx.arg.build_id.size() <= SHA256_SIZE);
2502: 
2503:   u8 digest[SHA256_SIZE];
2504:   sha256_hash(shards.data(), shards.size(), digest);
2505:   memcpy(buf + offset, digest, ctx.arg.build_id.size());
2506: 
2507: #ifndef _WIN32
2508:   if (ctx.output_file->is_mmapped) {
2509:     munmap(buf, std::min(filesize, shard_size));
2510:     ctx.output_file->is_unmapped = true;
2511:   }
2512: #endif
2513: }
2514: 
2515: template <typename E>
2516: void BuildIdSection<E>::write_buildid(Context<E> &ctx) {
2517:   Timer t(ctx, "build_id");
2518: 
2519:   switch (ctx.arg.build_id.kind) {
2520:   case BuildId::HEX:
2521:     write_vector(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + HEADER_SIZE,
2522:                  ctx.arg.build_id.value);
2523:     return;
2524:   case BuildId::HASH:
2525:     // Modern x86 processors have purpose-built instructions to accelerate
2526:     // SHA256 computation, and SHA256 outperforms MD5 on such computers.
2527:     // So, we always compute SHA256 and truncate it if smaller digest was
2528:     // requested.
2529:     compute_sha256(ctx, this->shdr.sh_offset + HEADER_SIZE);
2530:     return;
2531:   case BuildId::UUID: {
2532:     std::array<u8, 16> uuid = get_uuid_v4();
2533:     memcpy(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + HEADER_SIZE, uuid.data(), 16);
2534:     return;
2535:   }
2536:   default:
2537:     unreachable();
2538:   }
2539: }
2540: 
2541: template <typename E>
2542: void NotePackageSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2543:   if (!ctx.arg.package_metadata.empty()) {
2544:     // +17 is for the header and the NUL terminator
2545:     this->shdr.sh_size = align_to(ctx.arg.package_metadata.size() + 17, 4);
2546:   }
2547: }
2548: 
2549: template <typename E>
2550: void NotePackageSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2551:   U32<E> *buf = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
2552:   memset(buf, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
2553: 
2554:   buf[0] = 4;                                      // Name size
2555:   buf[1] = this->shdr.sh_size - 16;                // Content size
2556:   buf[2] = NT_FDO_PACKAGING_METADATA;              // Type
2557:   memcpy(buf + 3, "FDO", 4);                       // Name
2558:   write_string(buf + 4, ctx.arg.package_metadata); // Content
2559: }
2560: 
2561: // Merges input files' .note.gnu.property values.
2562: template <typename E>
2563: void NotePropertySection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
2564:   // The rules we support are only specified for x86 psABI
2565:   if (!is_x86<E>)
2566:     return;
2567: 
2568:   // Reset to the initial state so that this function is idempotent
2569:   properties.clear();
2570: 
2571:   // Obtain the list of keys
2572:   std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> files = ctx.objs;
2573:   std::erase(files, ctx.internal_obj);
2574:   std::set<u32> keys;
2575: 
2576:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : files)
2577:     for (std::pair<u32, u32> kv : file->gnu_properties)
2578:       keys.insert(kv.first);
2579: 
2580:   auto get_value = [](ObjectFile<E> *file, u32 key) -> u32 {
2581:     auto it = file->gnu_properties.find(key);
2582:     if (it != file->gnu_properties.end())
2583:       return it->second;
2584:     return 0;
2585:   };
2586: 
2587:   // Merge values for each key
2588:   for (u32 key : keys) {
2589:     auto has_key = [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2590:       return file->gnu_properties.contains(key);
2591:     };
2592: 
2593:     if (GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_AND_LO <= key &&
2594:         key <= GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_AND_HI) {
2595:       // An AND feature is set if all input objects have the property and
2596:       // the feature.
2597:       if (std::all_of(files.begin(), files.end(), has_key)) {
2598:         properties[key] = 0xffff'ffff;
2599:         for (ObjectFile<E> *file : files)
2600:           properties[key] &= get_value(file, key);
2601:       }
2602:     } else if (GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_OR_LO <= key &&
2603:                key <= GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_OR_HI) {
2604:       // An OR feature is set if some input object has the feature.
2605:       for (ObjectFile<E> *file : files)
2606:         properties[key] |= get_value(file, key);
2607:     } else if (GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_OR_AND_LO <= key &&
2608:                key <= GNU_PROPERTY_X86_UINT32_OR_AND_HI) {
2609:       // An OR-AND feature is set if all input object files have the property
2610:       // and some of them has the feature.
2611:       if (std::all_of(files.begin(), files.end(), has_key))
2612:         for (ObjectFile<E> *file : files)
2613:           properties[key] |= get_value(file, key);
2614:     }
2615:   }
2616: 
2617:   if (ctx.arg.z_ibt)
2618:     properties[GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_AND] |= GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_IBT;
2619:   if (ctx.arg.z_shstk)
2620:     properties[GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_AND] |= GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_SHSTK;
2621: 
2622:   std::erase_if(properties, [](std::pair<u32, u32> kv) {
2623:     return kv.second == 0;
2624:   });
2625: 
2626:   if (properties.empty())
2627:     this->shdr.sh_size = 0;
2628:   else
2629:     this->shdr.sh_size = 16 + ENTRY_SIZE * properties.size();
2630: }
2631: 
2632: template <typename E>
2633: void NotePropertySection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2634:   U32<E> *buf = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
2635:   memset(buf, 0, this->shdr.sh_size);
2636: 
2637:   buf[0] = 4;                              // Name size
2638:   buf[1] = ENTRY_SIZE * properties.size(); // Content size
2639:   buf[2] = NT_GNU_PROPERTY_TYPE_0;         // Type
2640:   memcpy(buf + 3, "GNU", 4);               // Name
2641: 
2642:   i64 idx = 4;
2643:   for (std::pair<u32, u32> kv : properties) {
2644:     buf[idx] = kv.first;                   // Feature type
2645:     buf[idx + 1] = 4;                      // Feature size
2646:     buf[idx + 2] = kv.second;              // Feature flags
2647:     idx += ENTRY_SIZE / sizeof(U32<E>);
2648:   }
2649: }
2650: 
2651: // This page explains the format of .gdb_index:
2652: // https://sourceware.org/gdb/onlinedocs/gdb/Index-Section-Format.html
2653: template <typename E>
2654: void GdbIndexSection<E>::construct(Context<E> &ctx) {
2655:   Timer t(ctx, "GdbIndexSection::construct");
2656: 
2657:   std::atomic_bool has_debug_info = false;
2658: 
2659:   // Read debug sections
2660:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2661:     if (file->debug_info) {
2662:       // Read compilation units from .debug_info.
2663:       file->compunits = read_compunits(ctx, *file);
2664: 
2665:       // Count the number of address areas contained in this file.
2666:       file->num_areas = estimate_address_areas(ctx, *file);
2667:       has_debug_info = true;
2668:     }
2669:   });
2670: 
2671:   if (!has_debug_info)
2672:     return;
2673: 
2674:   // Initialize `area_offset` and `compunits_idx`.
2675:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size() - 1; i++) {
2676:     ctx.objs[i + 1]->area_offset =
2677:       ctx.objs[i]->area_offset + ctx.objs[i]->num_areas * 20;
2678:     ctx.objs[i + 1]->compunits_idx =
2679:       ctx.objs[i]->compunits_idx + ctx.objs[i]->compunits.size();
2680:   }
2681: 
2682:   // Read .debug_gnu_pubnames and .debug_gnu_pubtypes.
2683:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2684:     file->gdb_names = read_pubnames(ctx, *file);
2685:   });
2686: 
2687:   // Estimate the unique number of pubnames.
2688:   HyperLogLog estimator;
2689:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2690:     HyperLogLog e;
2691:     for (GdbIndexName &name : file->gdb_names)
2692:       e.insert(name.hash);
2693:     estimator.merge(e);
2694:   });
2695: 
2696:   // Uniquify pubnames by inserting all name strings into a concurrent
2697:   // hashmap.
2698:   map.resize(estimator.get_cardinality() * 2);
2699:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> num_names;
2700: 
2701:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2702:     for (GdbIndexName &name : file->gdb_names) {
2703:       MapEntry *ent;
2704:       bool inserted;
2705:       std::tie(ent, inserted) = map.insert(name.name, name.hash, {file, name.hash});
2706:       if (inserted)
2707:         num_names.local()++;
2708: 
2709:       ObjectFile<E> *old_val = ent->owner;
2710:       while (file->priority < old_val->priority &&
2711:              !ent->owner.compare_exchange_weak(old_val, file));
2712: 
2713:       ent->num_attrs++;
2714:       name.entry_idx = ent - map.values;
2715:     }
2716:   });
2717: 
2718:   // Assign offsets for names and attributes within each file.
2719:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2720:     for (GdbIndexName &name : file->gdb_names) {
2721:       MapEntry &ent = map.values[name.entry_idx];
2722:       if (ent.owner == file) {
2723:         ent.attr_offset = file->attrs_size;
2724:         file->attrs_size += (ent.num_attrs + 1) * 4;
2725:         ent.name_offset = file->names_size;
2726:         file->names_size += name.name.size() + 1;
2727:       }
2728:     }
2729:   });
2730: 
2731:   // Compute per-file name and attributes offsets.
2732:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size() - 1; i++)
2733:     ctx.objs[i + 1]->attrs_offset =
2734:       ctx.objs[i]->attrs_offset + ctx.objs[i]->attrs_size;
2735: 
2736:   ctx.objs[0]->names_offset =
2737:     ctx.objs.back()->attrs_offset + ctx.objs.back()->attrs_size;
2738: 
2739:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size() - 1; i++)
2740:     ctx.objs[i + 1]->names_offset =
2741:       ctx.objs[i]->names_offset + ctx.objs[i]->names_size;
2742: 
2743:   // .gdb_index contains an on-disk hash table for pubnames and
2744:   // pubtypes. We aim 75% utilization. As per the format specification,
2745:   // It must be a power of two.
2746:   i64 num_symtab_entries =
2747:     std::max<i64>(bit_ceil(num_names.combine(std::plus()) * 4 / 3), 16);
2748: 
2749:   // Now that we can compute the size of this section.
2750:   ObjectFile<E> &last = *ctx.objs.back();
2751:   i64 compunits_size = (last.compunits_idx + last.compunits.size()) * 16;
2752:   i64 areas_size = last.area_offset + last.num_areas * 20;
2753:   i64 offset = sizeof(header);
2754: 
2755:   header.cu_list_offset = offset;
2756:   offset += compunits_size;
2757: 
2758:   header.cu_types_offset = offset;
2759:   header.areas_offset = offset;
2760:   offset += areas_size;
2761: 
2762:   header.symtab_offset = offset;
2763:   offset += num_symtab_entries * 8;
2764: 
2765:   header.const_pool_offset = offset;
2766:   offset += last.names_offset + last.names_size;
2767: 
2768:   this->shdr.sh_size = offset;
2769: }
2770: 
2771: template <typename E>
2772: void GdbIndexSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2773:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
2774: 
2775:   // Write section header.
2776:   memcpy(buf, &header, sizeof(header));
2777:   buf += sizeof(header);
2778: 
2779:   // Write compilation unit list.
2780:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
2781:     if (file->debug_info) {
2782:       u64 offset = file->debug_info->offset;
2783:       for (std::string_view cu : file->compunits) {
2784:         *(ul64 *)buf = offset;
2785:         *(ul64 *)(buf + 8) = cu.size();
2786:         buf += 16;
2787:         offset += cu.size();
2788:       }
2789:     }
2790:   }
2791: 
2792:   // Skip address areas. It'll be filled by write_address_areas.
2793:   buf += header.symtab_offset - header.areas_offset;
2794: 
2795:   // Write an on-disk hash table for names.
2796:   u32 symtab_size = header.const_pool_offset - header.symtab_offset;
2797:   memset(buf, 0, symtab_size);
2798: 
2799:   assert(has_single_bit(symtab_size / 8));
2800:   u32 mask = symtab_size / 8 - 1;
2801: 
2802:   for (i64 i = 0; i < map.nbuckets; i++) {
2803:     if (map.get_key(i)) {
2804:       u32 hash = map.values[i].hash;
2805:       u32 step = (hash & mask) | 1;
2806:       u32 j = hash & mask;
2807: 
2808:       while (*(U32<E> *)(buf + j * 8))
2809:         j = (j + step) & mask;
2810: 
2811:       ObjectFile<E> &file = *map.values[i].owner;
2812:       *(ul32 *)(buf + j * 8) = file.names_offset + map.values[i].name_offset;
2813:       *(ul32 *)(buf + j * 8 + 4) = file.attrs_offset + map.values[i].attr_offset;
2814:     }
2815:   }
2816: 
2817:   buf += symtab_size;
2818: 
2819:   // Write CU vector
2820:   memset(buf, 0, ctx.objs[0]->names_offset);
2821: 
2822:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2823:     std::atomic_uint32_t *attrs = (std::atomic_uint32_t *)buf;
2824: 
2825:     for (GdbIndexName &name : file->gdb_names) {
2826:       MapEntry &ent = map.values[name.entry_idx];
2827:       u32 idx = (ent.owner.load()->attrs_offset + ent.attr_offset) / 4;
2828:       attrs[idx + ++attrs[idx]] = name.attr;
2829:     }
2830:   });
2831: 
2832:   // Sort CU vector for build reproducibility
2833:   const i64 shard_size = map.nbuckets / map.NUM_SHARDS;
2834: 
2835:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)map.NUM_SHARDS, [&](i64 i) {
2836:     u32 *attrs = (u32 *)buf;
2837: 
2838:     for (i64 j = shard_size * i; j < shard_size * (i + 1); j++) {
2839:       if (map.get_key(j)) {
2840:         MapEntry &ent = map.values[j];
2841:         u32 idx = (ent.owner.load()->attrs_offset + ent.attr_offset) / 4;
2842:         u32 *start = attrs + idx + 1;
2843:         std::sort(start, start + attrs[idx]);
2844:       }
2845:     }
2846:   });
2847: 
2848:   // .gdb_index contents are little-endian, so swap bytes if big-endian.
2849:   if constexpr (std::endian::native == std::endian::big)
2850:     for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs[0]->names_offset; i += 4)
2851:       *(u32 *)(buf + i) = bswap(*(u32 *)(buf + i));
2852: 
2853:   // Write pubnames and pubtypes.
2854:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)map.NUM_SHARDS, [&](i64 i) {
2855:     for (i64 j = shard_size * i; j < shard_size * (i + 1); j++) {
2856:       if (const char *key = map.get_key(j)) {
2857:         ObjectFile<E> &file = *map.values[j].owner;
2858:         std::string_view name{key, map.key_sizes[j]};
2859:         write_string(buf + file.names_offset + map.values[j].name_offset, name);
2860:       }
2861:     }
2862:   });
2863: }
2864: 
2865: template <typename E>
2866: void GdbIndexSection<E>::write_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx) {
2867:   Timer t(ctx, "GdbIndexSection::write_address_areas");
2868: 
2869:   if (this->shdr.sh_size == 0)
2870:     return;
2871: 
2872:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
2873: 
2874:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
2875:     std::string_view name = chunk->name;
2876:     if (name == ".debug_info")
2877:       ctx.debug_info = chunk;
2878:     if (name == ".debug_abbrev")
2879:       ctx.debug_abbrev = chunk;
2880:     if (name == ".debug_ranges")
2881:       ctx.debug_ranges = chunk;
2882:     if (name == ".debug_addr")
2883:       ctx.debug_addr = chunk;
2884:     if (name == ".debug_rnglists")
2885:       ctx.debug_rnglists = chunk;
2886:   }
2887: 
2888:   assert(ctx.debug_info);
2889:   assert(ctx.debug_abbrev);
2890: 
2891:   struct Entry {
2892:     ul64 start;
2893:     ul64 end;
2894:     ul32 attr;
2895:   };
2896: 
2897:   // Read address ranges from debug sections and copy them to .gdb_index.
2898:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2899:     if (!file->debug_info)
2900:       return;
2901: 
2902:     Entry *begin = (Entry *)(base + header.areas_offset + file->area_offset);
2903:     Entry *e = begin;
2904:     u64 offset = file->debug_info->offset;
2905: 
2906:     for (i64 i = 0; i < file->compunits.size(); i++) {
2907:       std::vector<u64> addrs = read_address_areas(ctx, *file, offset);
2908: 
2909:       for (i64 j = 0; j < addrs.size(); j += 2) {
2910:         // Skip an empty range
2911:         if (addrs[j] == addrs[j + 1])
2912:           continue;
2913: 
2914:         // Gdb crashes if there are entries with address 0.
2915:         if (addrs[j] == 0)
2916:           continue;
2917: 
2918:         assert(e < begin + file->num_areas);
2919:         e->start = addrs[j];
2920:         e->end = addrs[j + 1];
2921:         e->attr = file->compunits_idx + i;
2922:         e++;
2923:       }
2924:       offset += file->compunits[i].size();
2925:     }
2926: 
2927:     // Fill trailing null entries with dummy values because gdb
2928:     // crashes if there are entries with address 0.
2929:     u64 filler;
2930:     if (e == begin)
2931:       filler = ctx.etext->get_addr(ctx) - 1;
2932:     else
2933:       filler = e[-1].start;
2934: 
2935:     for (; e < begin + file->num_areas; e++) {
2936:       e->start = filler;
2937:       e->end = filler;
2938:       e->attr = file->compunits_idx;
2939:     }
2940:   });
2941: }
2942: 
2943: template <typename E>
2944: CompressedSection<E>::CompressedSection(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> &chunk) {
2945:   assert(chunk.name.starts_with(".debug"));
2946:   this->name = chunk.name;
2947: 
2948:   uncompressed.reset(new u8[chunk.shdr.sh_size]);
2949:   chunk.write_to(ctx, uncompressed.get());
2950: 
2951:   switch (ctx.arg.compress_debug_sections) {
2952:   case COMPRESS_ZLIB:
2953:     chdr.ch_type = ELFCOMPRESS_ZLIB;
2954:     compressed.reset(new ZlibCompressor(uncompressed.get(), chunk.shdr.sh_size));
2955:     break;
2956:   case COMPRESS_ZSTD:
2957:     chdr.ch_type = ELFCOMPRESS_ZSTD;
2958:     compressed.reset(new ZstdCompressor(uncompressed.get(), chunk.shdr.sh_size));
2959:     break;
2960:   default:
2961:     unreachable();
2962:   }
2963: 
2964:   chdr.ch_size = chunk.shdr.sh_size;
2965:   chdr.ch_addralign = chunk.shdr.sh_addralign;
2966: 
2967:   this->shdr = chunk.shdr;
2968:   this->shdr.sh_flags |= SHF_COMPRESSED;
2969:   this->shdr.sh_addralign = 1;
2970:   this->shdr.sh_size = sizeof(chdr) + compressed->compressed_size;
2971:   this->shndx = chunk.shndx;
2972: 
2973:   // We don't need to keep the original data unless --gdb-index is given.
2974:   if (!ctx.arg.gdb_index)
2975:     uncompressed.reset(nullptr);
2976: }
2977: 
2978: template <typename E>
2979: void CompressedSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2980:   u8 *base = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset;
2981:   memcpy(base, &chdr, sizeof(chdr));
2982:   compressed->write_to(base + sizeof(chdr));
2983: }
2984: 
2985: template <typename E>
2986: RelocSection<E>::RelocSection(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec)
2987:   : output_section(osec) {
2988:   if constexpr (E::is_rela) {
2989:     this->name = save_string(ctx, ".rela" + std::string(osec.name));
2990:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_RELA;
2991:   } else {
2992:     this->name = save_string(ctx, ".rel" + std::string(osec.name));
2993:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_REL;
2994:   }
2995: 
2996:   this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_INFO_LINK;
2997:   this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
2998:   this->shdr.sh_entsize = sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
2999: 
3000:   // Compute an offset for each input section
3001:   offsets.resize(osec.members.size());
3002: 
3003:   auto scan = [&](const tbb::blocked_range<i64> &r, i64 sum, bool is_final) {
3004:     for (i64 i = r.begin(); i < r.end(); i++) {
3005:       InputSection<E> &isec = *osec.members[i];
3006:       if (is_final)
3007:         offsets[i] = sum;
3008:       sum += isec.get_rels(ctx).size();
3009:     }
3010:     return sum;
3011:   };
3012: 
3013:   i64 num_entries = tbb::parallel_scan(
3014:     tbb::blocked_range<i64>(0, osec.members.size()), 0, scan, std::plus());
3015: 
3016:   this->shdr.sh_size = num_entries * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
3017: }
3018: 
3019: template <typename E>
3020: void RelocSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
3021:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.symtab->shndx;
3022:   this->shdr.sh_info = output_section.shndx;
3023: }
3024: 
3025: template <typename E>
3026: void RelocSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
3027:   auto write = [&](ElfRel<E> &out, InputSection<E> &isec, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
3028:     i64 symidx = 0;
3029:     i64 addend = 0;
3030: 
3031:     Symbol<E> &sym = *isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
3032: 
3033:     if (sym.esym().st_type == STT_SECTION) {
3034:       if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag()) {
3035:         symidx = frag->output_section.shndx;
3036:         addend = frag->offset + sym.value + get_addend(isec, rel);
3037:       } else {
3038:         InputSection<E> *target = sym.get_input_section();
3039: 
3040:         if (OutputSection<E> *osec = target->output_section) {
3041:           symidx = osec->shndx;
3042:           addend = get_addend(isec, rel) + target->offset;
3043:         } else if (isec.name() == ".eh_frame") {
3044:           symidx = ctx.eh_frame->shndx;
3045:           addend = get_addend(isec, rel);
3046:         } else {
3047:           // This is usually a dead debug section referring a
3048:           // COMDAT-eliminated section.
3049:         }
3050:       }
3051:     } else {
3052:       if (sym.sym_idx)
3053:         symidx = sym.get_output_sym_idx(ctx);
3054:       addend = get_addend(isec, rel);
3055:     }
3056: 
3057:     if constexpr (is_alpha<E>)
3058:       if (rel.r_type == R_ALPHA_GPDISP || rel.r_type == R_ALPHA_LITUSE)
3059:         addend = rel.r_addend;
3060: 
3061:     i64 r_offset = isec.output_section->shdr.sh_addr + isec.offset + rel.r_offset;
3062:     out = ElfRel<E>(r_offset, rel.r_type, symidx, addend);
3063: 
3064:     if (ctx.arg.relocatable) {
3065:       u8 *base = ctx.buf + isec.output_section->shdr.sh_offset + isec.offset;
3066:       write_addend(base + rel.r_offset, addend, rel);
3067:     }
3068:   };
3069: 
3070:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)output_section.members.size(), [&](i64 i) {
3071:     ElfRel<E> *buf = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset) + offsets[i];
3072:     InputSection<E> &isec = *output_section.members[i];
3073:     std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = isec.get_rels(ctx);
3074: 
3075:     for (i64 j = 0; j < rels.size(); j++)
3076:       write(buf[j], isec, rels[j]);
3077:   });
3078: }
3079: 
3080: template <typename E>
3081: void ComdatGroupSection<E>::update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {
3082:   assert(ctx.arg.relocatable);
3083:   this->shdr.sh_link = ctx.symtab->shndx;
3084:   this->shdr.sh_info = sym.get_output_sym_idx(ctx);
3085: }
3086: 
3087: template <typename E>
3088: void ComdatGroupSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
3089:   U32<E> *buf = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset);
3090:   *buf++ = GRP_COMDAT;
3091:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : members)
3092:     *buf++ = chunk->shndx;
3093: }
3094: 
3095: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
