================
File: elf/input-files.cc
================
   1: #include "mold.h"
   2: 
   3: #include <bit>
   4: #include <cstring>
   5: #include <tbb/parallel_sort.h>
   6: 
   7: #ifndef _WIN32
   8: # include <unistd.h>
   9: #endif
  10: 
  11: namespace mold::elf {
  12: 
  13: template <typename E>
  14: InputFile<E>::InputFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf)
  15:   : mf(mf), filename(mf->name) {
  16:   if (mf->size < sizeof(ElfEhdr<E>))
  17:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": file too small";
  18:   if (memcmp(mf->data, "\177ELF", 4))
  19:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": not an ELF file";
  20: 
  21:   ElfEhdr<E> &ehdr = *(ElfEhdr<E> *)mf->data;
  22:   is_dso = (ehdr.e_type == ET_DYN);
  23: 
  24:   ElfShdr<E> *sh_begin = (ElfShdr<E> *)(mf->data + ehdr.e_shoff);
  25: 
  26:   // e_shnum contains the total number of sections in an object file.
  27:   // Since it is a 16-bit integer field, it's not large enough to
  28:   // represent >65535 sections. If an object file contains more than 65535
  29:   // sections, the actual number is stored to sh_size field.
  30:   i64 num_sections = (ehdr.e_shnum == 0) ? sh_begin->sh_size : ehdr.e_shnum;
  31: 
  32:   if (mf->data + mf->size < (u8 *)(sh_begin + num_sections))
  33:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": e_shoff or e_shnum corrupted: "
  34:                << mf->size << " " << num_sections;
  35:   elf_sections = {sh_begin, sh_begin + num_sections};
  36: 
  37:   // e_shstrndx is a 16-bit field. If .shstrtab's section index is
  38:   // too large, the actual number is stored to sh_link field.
  39:   i64 shstrtab_idx = (ehdr.e_shstrndx == SHN_XINDEX)
  40:     ? sh_begin->sh_link : ehdr.e_shstrndx;
  41: 
  42:   shstrtab = this->get_string(ctx, shstrtab_idx);
  43: }
  44: 
  45: template <typename E>
  46: std::span<ElfPhdr<E>> InputFile<E>::get_phdrs() {
  47:   ElfEhdr<E> &ehdr = get_ehdr();
  48:   return {(ElfPhdr<E> *)(mf->data + ehdr.e_phoff), ehdr.e_phnum};
  49: }
  50: 
  51: template <typename E>
  52: ElfShdr<E> *InputFile<E>::find_section(i64 type) {
  53:   for (ElfShdr<E> &sec : elf_sections)
  54:     if (sec.sh_type == type)
  55:       return &sec;
  56:   return nullptr;
  57: }
  58: 
  59: template <typename E>
  60: void InputFile<E>::clear_symbols() {
  61:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : get_global_syms()) {
  62:     if (__atomic_load_n(&sym->file, __ATOMIC_ACQUIRE) == this) {
  63:       sym->origin = 0;
  64:       sym->value = -1;
  65:       sym->sym_idx = -1;
  66:       sym->ver_idx = 0;
  67:       sym->is_weak = false;
  68:       sym->is_imported = false;
  69:       sym->is_exported = false;
  70:       __atomic_store_n(&sym->file, nullptr, __ATOMIC_RELEASE);
  71:     }
  72:   }
  73: }
  74: 
  75: // Find the source filename. It should be listed in symtab as STT_FILE.
  76: template <typename E>
  77: std::string_view InputFile<E>::get_source_name() const {
  78:   for (i64 i = 0; i < first_global; i++)
  79:     if (Symbol<E> *sym = symbols[i]; sym->get_type() == STT_FILE)
  80:       return sym->name();
  81:   return "";
  82: }
  83: 
  84: template <typename E>
  85: ObjectFile<E>::ObjectFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
  86:                           std::string archive_name, bool is_in_lib)
  87:   : InputFile<E>(ctx, mf), archive_name(archive_name), is_in_lib(is_in_lib) {
  88:   this->is_alive = !is_in_lib;
  89: }
  90: 
  91: template <typename E>
  92: ObjectFile<E> *
  93: ObjectFile<E>::create(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
  94:                       std::string archive_name, bool is_in_lib) {
  95:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = new ObjectFile<E>(ctx, mf, archive_name, is_in_lib);
  96:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(obj);
  97:   return obj;
  98: }
  99: 
 100: template <typename E>
 101: static bool is_debug_section(const ElfShdr<E> &shdr, std::string_view name) {
 102:   return !(shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) && name.starts_with(".debug");
 103: }
 104: 
 105: template <typename E>
 106: void
 107: ObjectFile<E>::read_note_gnu_property(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfShdr<E> &shdr) {
 108:   std::string_view data = this->get_string(ctx, shdr);
 109: 
 110:   while (!data.empty()) {
 111:     ElfNhdr<E> &hdr = *(ElfNhdr<E> *)data.data();
 112:     data = data.substr(sizeof(hdr));
 113: 
 114:     std::string_view name = data.substr(0, hdr.n_namesz - 1);
 115:     data = data.substr(align_to(hdr.n_namesz, 4));
 116: 
 117:     std::string_view desc = data.substr(0, hdr.n_descsz);
 118:     data = data.substr(align_to(hdr.n_descsz, sizeof(Word<E>)));
 119: 
 120:     if (hdr.n_type != NT_GNU_PROPERTY_TYPE_0 || name != "GNU")
 121:       continue;
 122: 
 123:     while (!desc.empty()) {
 124:       u32 type = *(U32<E> *)desc.data();
 125:       u32 size = *(U32<E> *)(desc.data() + 4);
 126:       desc = desc.substr(8);
 127: 
 128:       // The majority of currently defined .note.gnu.property
 129:       // use 32-bit values.
 130:       // We don't know how to handle anything else, so if we encounter
 131:       // one, skip it.
 132:       //
 133:       // The following properties have a different size:
 134:       // - GNU_PROPERTY_STACK_SIZE
 135:       // - GNU_PROPERTY_NO_COPY_ON_PROTECTED
 136:       if (size == 4)
 137:         gnu_properties[type] |= *(U32<E> *)desc.data();
 138:       desc = desc.substr(align_to(size, sizeof(Word<E>)));
 139:     }
 140:   }
 141: }
 142: 
 143: template <typename E>
 144: void ObjectFile<E>::initialize_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 145:   // Read sections
 146:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->elf_sections.size(); i++) {
 147:     const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = this->elf_sections[i];
 148: 
 149:     if ((shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXCLUDE) && !(shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) &&
 150:         shdr.sh_type != SHT_LLVM_ADDRSIG && !ctx.arg.relocatable)
 151:       continue;
 152: 
 153:     switch (shdr.sh_type) {
 154:     case SHT_GROUP: {
 155:       // Get the signature of this section group.
 156:       if (shdr.sh_info >= this->elf_syms.size())
 157:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid symbol index";
 158:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[shdr.sh_info];
 159: 
 160:       std::string_view signature;
 161:       if (esym.st_type == STT_SECTION) {
 162:         signature = this->shstrtab.data() +
 163:                     this->elf_sections[esym.st_shndx].sh_name;
 164:       } else {
 165:         signature = this->symbol_strtab.data() + esym.st_name;
 166:       }
 167: 
 168:       // Ignore a broken comdat group GCC emits for .debug_macros.
 169:       // https://github.com/rui314/mold/issues/438
 170:       if (signature.starts_with("wm4."))
 171:         continue;
 172: 
 173:       // Get comdat group members.
 174:       std::span<U32<E>> entries = this->template get_data<U32<E>>(ctx, shdr);
 175: 
 176:       if (entries.empty())
 177:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": empty SHT_GROUP";
 178:       if (entries[0] == 0)
 179:         continue;
 180:       if (entries[0] != GRP_COMDAT)
 181:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": unsupported SHT_GROUP format";
 182: 
 183:       typename decltype(ctx.comdat_groups)::const_accessor acc;
 184:       ctx.comdat_groups.insert(acc, {signature, ComdatGroup()});
 185:       ComdatGroup *group = const_cast<ComdatGroup *>(&acc->second);
 186:       comdat_groups.push_back({group, (u32)i, entries.subspan(1)});
 187:       break;
 188:     }
 189:     case SHT_SYMTAB_SHNDX:
 190:       symtab_shndx_sec = this->template get_data<U32<E>>(ctx, shdr);
 191:       break;
 192:     case SHT_SYMTAB:
 193:     case SHT_STRTAB:
 194:     case SHT_REL:
 195:     case SHT_RELA:
 196:     case SHT_NULL:
 197:     case SHT_ARM_ATTRIBUTES:
 198:       break;
 199:     default: {
 200:       std::string_view name = this->shstrtab.data() + shdr.sh_name;
 201: 
 202:       // .note.GNU-stack section controls executable-ness of the stack
 203:       // area in GNU linkers. We ignore that section because silently
 204:       // making the stack area executable is too dangerous. Tell our
 205:       // users about the difference if that matters.
 206:       if (name == ".note.GNU-stack" && !ctx.arg.relocatable) {
 207:         if (shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) {
 208:           if (!ctx.arg.z_execstack && !ctx.arg.z_execstack_if_needed)
 209:             Warn(ctx) << *this << ": this file may cause a segmentation"
 210:               " fault because it requires an executable stack. See"
 211:               " https://github.com/rui314/mold/tree/main/docs/execstack.md"
 212:               " for more info.";
 213:           needs_executable_stack = true;
 214:         }
 215:         continue;
 216:       }
 217: 
 218:       if (name == ".note.gnu.property") {
 219:         read_note_gnu_property(ctx, shdr);
 220:         continue;
 221:       }
 222: 
 223:       // Ignore a build-id section in an input file. This doesn't normally
 224:       // happen, but you can create such object file with
 225:       // `ld.bfd -r --build-id`.
 226:       if (name == ".note.gnu.build-id")
 227:         continue;
 228: 
 229:       // Ignore these sections for compatibility with old glibc i386 CRT files.
 230:       if (name == ".gnu.linkonce.t.__x86.get_pc_thunk.bx" ||
 231:           name == ".gnu.linkonce.t.__i686.get_pc_thunk.bx")
 232:         continue;
 233: 
 234:       // Also ignore this for compatibility with ICC
 235:       if (name == ".gnu.linkonce.d.DW.ref.__gxx_personality_v0")
 236:         continue;
 237: 
 238:       // Ignore debug sections if --strip-all or --strip-debug is given.
 239:       if ((ctx.arg.strip_all || ctx.arg.strip_debug) &&
 240:           is_debug_section(shdr, name))
 241:         continue;
 242: 
 243:       // Save .llvm_addrsig for --icf=safe.
 244:       if (shdr.sh_type == SHT_LLVM_ADDRSIG && !ctx.arg.relocatable) {
 245:         llvm_addrsig = std::make_unique<InputSection<E>>(ctx, *this, name, i);
 246:         continue;
 247:       }
 248: 
 249:       // If an output file doesn't have a section header (i.e.
 250:       // --oformat=binary is given), we discard all non-memory-allocated
 251:       // sections. This is because without a section header, we can't find
 252:       // their places in an output file in the first place.
 253:       if (ctx.arg.oformat_binary && !(shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 254:         continue;
 255: 
 256:       this->sections[i] = std::make_unique<InputSection<E>>(ctx, *this, name, i);
 257: 
 258:       if constexpr (is_ppc32<E>)
 259:         if (name == ".got2")
 260:           ppc32_got2 = this->sections[i].get();
 261: 
 262:       // Save debug sections for --gdb-index.
 263:       if (ctx.arg.gdb_index) {
 264:         InputSection<E> *isec = this->sections[i].get();
 265: 
 266:         if (name == ".debug_info")
 267:           debug_info = isec;
 268:         if (name == ".debug_ranges")
 269:           debug_ranges = isec;
 270:         if (name == ".debug_rnglists")
 271:           debug_rnglists = isec;
 272: 
 273:         // If --gdb-index is given, contents of .debug_gnu_pubnames and
 274:         // .debug_gnu_pubtypes are copied to .gdb_index, so keeping them
 275:         // in an output file is just a waste of space.
 276:         if (name == ".debug_gnu_pubnames") {
 277:           debug_pubnames = isec;
 278:           isec->is_alive = false;
 279:         }
 280: 
 281:         if (name == ".debug_gnu_pubtypes") {
 282:           debug_pubtypes = isec;
 283:           isec->is_alive = false;
 284:         }
 285: 
 286:         // .debug_types is similar to .debug_info but contains type info
 287:         // only. It exists only in DWARF 4, has been removed in DWARF 5 and
 288:         // neither GCC nor Clang generate it by default
 289:         // (-fdebug-types-section is needed). As such there is probably
 290:         // little need to support it.
 291:         if (name == ".debug_types")
 292:           Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": mold's --gdb-index is not compatible"
 293:             " with .debug_types; to fix this error, remove"
 294:             " -fdebug-types-section and recompile";
 295:       }
 296: 
 297:       static Counter counter("regular_sections");
 298:       counter++;
 299:       break;
 300:     }
 301:     }
 302:   }
 303: 
 304:   // Attach relocation sections to their target sections.
 305:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->elf_sections.size(); i++) {
 306:     const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = this->elf_sections[i];
 307:     if (shdr.sh_type != (E::is_rela ? SHT_RELA : SHT_REL))
 308:       continue;
 309: 
 310:     if (shdr.sh_info >= sections.size())
 311:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocated section index: "
 312:                  << (u32)shdr.sh_info;
 313: 
 314:     if (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &target = sections[shdr.sh_info]) {
 315:       assert(target->relsec_idx == -1);
 316:       target->relsec_idx = i;
 317:     }
 318:   }
 319: }
 320: 
 321: template <typename E>
 322: void ObjectFile<E>::initialize_ehframe_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 323:   for (i64 i = 0; i < sections.size(); i++) {
 324:     std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec = sections[i];
 325:     if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->name() == ".eh_frame") {
 326:       read_ehframe(ctx, *isec);
 327:     }
 328:   }
 329: }
 330: 
 331: // .eh_frame contains data records explaining how to handle exceptions.
 332: // When an exception is thrown, the runtime searches a record from
 333: // .eh_frame with the current program counter as a key. A record that
 334: // covers the current PC explains how to find a handler and how to
 335: // transfer the control ot it.
 336: //
 337: // Unlike the most other sections, linker has to parse .eh_frame contents
 338: // because of the following reasons:
 339: //
 340: // - There's usually only one .eh_frame section for each object file,
 341: //   which explains how to handle exceptions for all functions in the same
 342: //   object. If we just copy them, the resulting .eh_frame section will
 343: //   contain lots of records for dead sections (i.e. de-duplicated inline
 344: //   functions). We want to copy only records for live functions.
 345: //
 346: // - .eh_frame contains two types of records: CIE and FDE. There's usually
 347: //   only one CIE at beginning of .eh_frame section followed by FDEs.
 348: //   Compiler usually emits the identical CIE record for all object files.
 349: //   We want to merge identical CIEs in an output .eh_frame section to
 350: //   reduce the section size.
 351: //
 352: // - Scanning a .eh_frame section to find a record is an O(n) operation
 353: //   where n is the number of records in the section. To reduce it to
 354: //   O(log n), linker creates a .eh_frame_hdr section. The section
 355: //   contains a sorted list of [an address in .text, an FDE address whose
 356: //   coverage starts at the .text address] to make binary search doable.
 357: //   In order to create .eh_frame_hdr, linker has to read .eh_frame.
 358: //
 359: // This function parses an input .eh_frame section.
 360: template <typename E>
 361: void ObjectFile<E>::read_ehframe(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec) {
 362:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels = isec.get_rels(ctx);
 363:   i64 cies_begin = cies.size();
 364:   i64 fdes_begin = fdes.size();
 365: 
 366:   // Read CIEs and FDEs until empty.
 367:   std::string_view contents = this->get_string(ctx, isec.shdr());
 368:   i64 rel_idx = 0;
 369: 
 370:   for (std::string_view data = contents; !data.empty();) {
 371:     i64 size = *(U32<E> *)data.data();
 372:     if (size == 0)
 373:       break;
 374: 
 375:     i64 begin_offset = data.data() - contents.data();
 376:     i64 end_offset = begin_offset + size + 4;
 377:     i64 id = *(U32<E> *)(data.data() + 4);
 378:     data = data.substr(size + 4);
 379: 
 380:     i64 rel_begin = rel_idx;
 381:     while (rel_idx < rels.size() && rels[rel_idx].r_offset < end_offset)
 382:       rel_idx++;
 383:     assert(rel_idx == rels.size() || begin_offset <= rels[rel_begin].r_offset);
 384: 
 385:     if (id == 0) {
 386:       // This is CIE.
 387:       cies.emplace_back(ctx, *this, isec, begin_offset, rels, rel_begin);
 388:     } else {
 389:       // This is FDE.
 390:       if (rel_begin == rel_idx || rels[rel_begin].r_sym == 0) {
 391:         // FDE has no valid relocation, which means FDE is dead from
 392:         // the beginning. Compilers usually don't create such FDE, but
 393:         // `ld -r` tend to generate such dead FDEs.
 394:         continue;
 395:       }
 396: 
 397:       if (rels[rel_begin].r_offset - begin_offset != 8)
 398:         Fatal(ctx) << isec << ": FDE's first relocation should have offset 8";
 399: 
 400:       fdes.emplace_back(begin_offset, rel_begin);
 401:     }
 402:   }
 403: 
 404:   // Associate CIEs to FDEs.
 405:   auto find_cie = [&](i64 offset) {
 406:     for (i64 i = cies_begin; i < cies.size(); i++)
 407:       if (cies[i].input_offset == offset)
 408:         return i;
 409:     Fatal(ctx) << isec << ": bad FDE pointer";
 410:   };
 411: 
 412:   for (i64 i = fdes_begin; i < fdes.size(); i++) {
 413:     i64 cie_offset = *(I32<E> *)(contents.data() + fdes[i].input_offset + 4);
 414:     fdes[i].cie_idx = find_cie(fdes[i].input_offset + 4 - cie_offset);
 415:   }
 416: 
 417:   auto get_isec = [&](const FdeRecord<E> &fde) -> InputSection<E> * {
 418:     return get_section(this->elf_syms[rels[fde.rel_idx].r_sym]);
 419:   };
 420: 
 421:   // We assume that FDEs for the same input sections are contiguous
 422:   // in `fdes` vector.
 423:   std::stable_sort(fdes.begin() + fdes_begin, fdes.end(),
 424:                    [&](const FdeRecord<E> &a, const FdeRecord<E> &b) {
 425:     return get_isec(a)->get_priority() < get_isec(b)->get_priority();
 426:   });
 427: 
 428:   // Associate FDEs to input sections.
 429:   for (i64 i = fdes_begin; i < fdes.size();) {
 430:     InputSection<E> *isec = get_isec(fdes[i]);
 431:     assert(isec->fde_begin == -1);
 432:     isec->fde_begin = i++;
 433: 
 434:     while (i < fdes.size() && isec == get_isec(fdes[i]))
 435:       i++;
 436:     isec->fde_end = i;
 437:   }
 438: }
 439: 
 440: // Returns a symbol object for a given key. This function handles
 441: // the -wrap option.
 442: template <typename E>
 443: static Symbol<E> *insert_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfSym<E> &esym,
 444:                                 std::string_view key, std::string_view name) {
 445:   if (esym.is_undef() && name.starts_with("__real_") &&
 446:       ctx.arg.wrap.contains(name.substr(7))) {
 447:     return get_symbol(ctx, key.substr(7), name.substr(7));
 448:   }
 449: 
 450:   Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, key, name);
 451: 
 452:   if (esym.is_undef() && sym->is_wrapped) {
 453:     key = save_string(ctx, "__wrap_" + std::string(key));
 454:     name = save_string(ctx, "__wrap_" + std::string(name));
 455:     return get_symbol(ctx, key, name);
 456:   }
 457:   return sym;
 458: }
 459: 
 460: template <typename E>
 461: void ObjectFile<E>::initialize_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 462:   if (!symtab_sec)
 463:     return;
 464: 
 465:   static Counter counter("all_syms");
 466:   counter += this->elf_syms.size();
 467: 
 468:   // Initialize local symbols
 469:   this->local_syms.resize(this->first_global);
 470:   this->local_syms[0].file = this;
 471:   this->local_syms[0].sym_idx = 0;
 472: 
 473:   for (i64 i = 1; i < this->first_global; i++) {
 474:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
 475:     if (esym.is_common())
 476:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": common local symbol?";
 477: 
 478:     std::string_view name;
 479:     if (esym.st_type == STT_SECTION)
 480:       name = this->shstrtab.data() + this->elf_sections[get_shndx(esym)].sh_name;
 481:     else
 482:       name = this->symbol_strtab.data() + esym.st_name;
 483: 
 484:     Symbol<E> &sym = this->local_syms[i];
 485:     sym.set_name(name);
 486:     sym.file = this;
 487:     sym.value = esym.st_value;
 488:     sym.sym_idx = i;
 489: 
 490:     if (!esym.is_abs())
 491:       sym.set_input_section(sections[get_shndx(esym)].get());
 492:   }
 493: 
 494:   this->symbols.resize(this->elf_syms.size());
 495: 
 496:   i64 num_globals = this->elf_syms.size() - this->first_global;
 497:   has_symver.resize(num_globals);
 498: 
 499:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->first_global; i++)
 500:     this->symbols[i] = &this->local_syms[i];
 501: 
 502:   // Initialize global symbols
 503:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 504:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
 505: 
 506:     // Get a symbol name
 507:     std::string_view key = this->symbol_strtab.data() + esym.st_name;
 508:     std::string_view name = key;
 509: 
 510:     // Parse symbol version after atsign
 511:     if (i64 pos = name.find('@'); pos != name.npos) {
 512:       std::string_view ver = name.substr(pos);
 513:       name = name.substr(0, pos);
 514: 
 515:       if (ver != "@" && ver != "@@") {
 516:         if (ver.starts_with("@@"))
 517:           key = name;
 518:         has_symver.set(i - this->first_global);
 519:       }
 520:     }
 521: 
 522:     this->symbols[i] = insert_symbol(ctx, esym, key, name);
 523:     if (esym.is_common())
 524:       has_common_symbol = true;
 525:   }
 526: }
 527: 
 528: // Relocations are usually sorted by r_offset in relocation tables,
 529: // but for some reason only RISC-V does not follow that convention.
 530: // We expect them to be sorted, so sort them if necessary.
 531: template <typename E>
 532: void ObjectFile<E>::sort_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
 533:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>) {
 534:     auto less = [&](const ElfRel<E> &a, const ElfRel<E> &b) {
 535:       return a.r_offset < b.r_offset;
 536:     };
 537: 
 538:     for (i64 i = 1; i < sections.size(); i++) {
 539:       std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec = sections[i];
 540:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive || !(isec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 541:         continue;
 542: 
 543:       std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels = isec->get_rels(ctx);
 544:       if (!std::is_sorted(rels.begin(), rels.end(), less))
 545:         sort(rels, less);
 546:     }
 547:   }
 548: }
 549: 
 550: static size_t find_null(std::string_view data, u64 entsize) {
 551:   if (entsize == 1)
 552:     return data.find('\0');
 553: 
 554:   for (i64 i = 0; i <= data.size() - entsize; i += entsize)
 555:     if (data.substr(i, entsize).find_first_not_of('\0') == data.npos)
 556:       return i;
 557: 
 558:   return data.npos;
 559: }
 560: 
 561: // Mergeable sections (sections with SHF_MERGE bit) typically contain
 562: // string literals. Linker is expected to split the section contents
 563: // into null-terminated strings, merge them with mergeable strings
 564: // from other object files, and emit uniquified strings to an output
 565: // file.
 566: //
 567: // This mechanism reduces the size of an output file. If two source
 568: // files happen to contain the same string literal, the output will
 569: // contain only a single copy of it.
 570: //
 571: // It is less common than string literals, but mergeable sections can
 572: // contain fixed-sized read-only records too.
 573: //
 574: // This function splits the section contents into small pieces that we
 575: // call "section fragments". Section fragment is a unit of merging.
 576: //
 577: // We do not support mergeable sections that have relocations.
 578: template <typename E>
 579: static std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>>
 580: split_section(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &sec) {
 581:   if (!sec.is_alive || sec.relsec_idx != -1)
 582:     return nullptr;
 583: 
 584:   const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = sec.shdr();
 585:   if (!(shdr.sh_flags & SHF_MERGE))
 586:     return nullptr;
 587: 
 588:   std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> rec(new MergeableSection<E>);
 589:   rec->parent = MergedSection<E>::get_instance(ctx, sec.name(), shdr.sh_type,
 590:                                                shdr.sh_flags);
 591:   rec->p2align = sec.p2align;
 592: 
 593:   if (sec.sh_size == 0)
 594:     return rec;
 595: 
 596:   // If thes section contents are compressed, uncompress them.
 597:   sec.uncompress(ctx);
 598: 
 599:   std::string_view data = sec.contents;
 600:   const char *begin = data.data();
 601:   u64 entsize = shdr.sh_entsize;
 602:   HyperLogLog estimator;
 603: 
 604:   // Split sections
 605:   if (shdr.sh_flags & SHF_STRINGS) {
 606:     if (entsize == 0) {
 607:       // GHC (Glasgow Haskell Compiler) sometimes creates a mergeable
 608:       // string section with entsize of 0 instead of 1, though such
 609:       // entsize is technically wrong. This is a workaround for the issue.
 610:       entsize = 1;
 611:     }
 612: 
 613:     while (!data.empty()) {
 614:       size_t end = find_null(data, entsize);
 615:       if (end == data.npos)
 616:         Fatal(ctx) << sec << ": string is not null terminated";
 617: 
 618:       std::string_view substr = data.substr(0, end + entsize);
 619:       data = data.substr(end + entsize);
 620: 
 621:       rec->strings.push_back(substr);
 622:       rec->frag_offsets.push_back(substr.data() - begin);
 623: 
 624:       u64 hash = hash_string(substr);
 625:       rec->hashes.push_back(hash);
 626:       estimator.insert(hash);
 627:     }
 628:   } else {
 629:     // OCaml compiler seems to create a mergeable non-string section with
 630:     // entisze of 0. Such section is malformed. We do not split such section.
 631:     if (entsize == 0)
 632:       return nullptr;
 633: 
 634:     if (data.size() % entsize)
 635:       Fatal(ctx) << sec << ": section size is not multiple of sh_entsize";
 636: 
 637:     while (!data.empty()) {
 638:       std::string_view substr = data.substr(0, entsize);
 639:       data = data.substr(entsize);
 640: 
 641:       rec->strings.push_back(substr);
 642:       rec->frag_offsets.push_back(substr.data() - begin);
 643: 
 644:       u64 hash = hash_string(substr);
 645:       rec->hashes.push_back(hash);
 646:       estimator.insert(hash);
 647:     }
 648:   }
 649: 
 650:   rec->parent->estimator.merge(estimator);
 651: 
 652:   static Counter counter("string_fragments");
 653:   counter += rec->fragments.size();
 654:   return rec;
 655: }
 656: 
 657: // Usually a section is an atomic unit of inclusion or exclusion.
 658: // Linker doesn't care about its contents. However, if a section is a
 659: // mergeable section (a section with SHF_MERGE bit set), the linker is
 660: // expected to split it into smaller pieces and merge each piece with
 661: // other pieces from different object files. In mold, we call the
 662: // atomic unit of mergeable section "section pieces".
 663: //
 664: // This feature is typically used for string literals. String literals
 665: // are usually put into a mergeable section by a compiler. If the same
 666: // string literal happen to occur in two different translation units,
 667: // a linker merges them into a single instance of a string, so that
 668: // a linker's output doesn't contain duplicate string literals.
 669: //
 670: // Handling symbols in mergeable sections is a bit tricky. Assume that
 671: // we have a mergeable section with the following contents and symbols:
 672: //
 673: //   Hello world\0foo bar\0
 674: //   ^            ^
 675: //   .rodata      .L.str1
 676: //   .L.str0
 677: //
 678: // '\0' represents a NUL byte. This mergeable section contains two
 679: // section pieces, "Hello world" and "foo bar". The first string is
 680: // referred by two symbols, .rodata and .L.str0, and the second by
 681: // .L.str1. .rodata is a section symbol and therefore a local symbol
 682: // and refers the begining of the section.
 683: //
 684: // In this example, there are actually two different ways to point to
 685: // string "foo bar", because .rodata+12 and .L.str1+0 refer the same
 686: // place in the section. This kind of "out-of-bound" reference occurs
 687: // only when a symbol is a section symbol. In other words, compiler
 688: // may use an offset from the beginning of a section to refer any
 689: // section piece in a section, but it doesn't do for any other types
 690: // of symbols.
 691: //
 692: // In mold, we attach section pieces symbols. If a relocation refers a
 693: // section symbol whose section is a mergeable section, we create a
 694: // new dummy symbol with a section piece and redirect the relocation
 695: // to the symbol. If a non-section symbol refers a section piece, the
 696: // section piece is attached to the symbol.
 697: template <typename E>
 698: void ObjectFile<E>::initialize_mergeable_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 699:   mergeable_sections.resize(sections.size());
 700: 
 701:   for (i64 i = 0; i < sections.size(); i++) {
 702:     if (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec = sections[i]) {
 703:       if (std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> m = split_section(ctx, *isec)) {
 704:         mergeable_sections[i] = std::move(m);
 705:         isec->is_alive = false;
 706:       }
 707:     }
 708:   }
 709: }
 710: 
 711: template <typename E>
 712: void ObjectFile<E>::resolve_section_pieces(Context<E> &ctx) {
 713:   for (std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> &m : mergeable_sections) {
 714:     if (m) {
 715:       m->fragments.reserve(m->strings.size());
 716:       for (i64 i = 0; i < m->strings.size(); i++)
 717:         m->fragments.push_back(m->parent->insert(ctx, m->strings[i], m->hashes[i],
 718:                                                  m->p2align));
 719: 
 720:       // Shrink vectors that we will never use again to reclaim memory.
 721:       m->strings.clear();
 722:       m->hashes.clear();
 723:     }
 724:   }
 725: 
 726:   // Attach section pieces to symbols.
 727:   for (i64 i = 1; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 728:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
 729:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
 730: 
 731:     if (esym.is_abs() || esym.is_common() || esym.is_undef())
 732:       continue;
 733: 
 734:     std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> &m = mergeable_sections[get_shndx(esym)];
 735:     if (!m || m->fragments.empty())
 736:       continue;
 737: 
 738:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
 739:     i64 frag_offset;
 740:     std::tie(frag, frag_offset) = m->get_fragment(esym.st_value);
 741: 
 742:     if (!frag)
 743:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad symbol value: " << esym.st_value;
 744: 
 745:     sym.set_frag(frag);
 746:     sym.value = frag_offset;
 747:   }
 748: 
 749:   // Compute the size of frag_syms.
 750:   i64 nfrag_syms = 0;
 751:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections)
 752:     if (isec && isec->is_alive && (isec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 753:       for (ElfRel<E> &r : isec->get_rels(ctx))
 754:         if (const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[r.r_sym];
 755:             esym.st_type == STT_SECTION && mergeable_sections[get_shndx(esym)])
 756:           nfrag_syms++;
 757: 
 758:   this->frag_syms.resize(nfrag_syms);
 759: 
 760:   // For each relocation referring a mergeable section symbol, we create
 761:   // a new dummy non-section symbol and redirect the relocation to the
 762:   // newly-created symbol.
 763:   i64 idx = 0;
 764:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections) {
 765:     if (!isec || !isec->is_alive || !(isec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 766:       continue;
 767: 
 768:     for (ElfRel<E> &r : isec->get_rels(ctx)) {
 769:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[r.r_sym];
 770:       if (esym.st_type != STT_SECTION)
 771:         continue;
 772: 
 773:       std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> &m = mergeable_sections[get_shndx(esym)];
 774:       if (!m)
 775:         continue;
 776: 
 777:       i64 r_addend = get_addend(*isec, r);
 778: 
 779:       SectionFragment<E> *frag;
 780:       i64 in_frag_offset;
 781:       std::tie(frag, in_frag_offset) = m->get_fragment(esym.st_value + r_addend);
 782: 
 783:       if (!frag)
 784:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": bad relocation at " << r.r_sym;
 785: 
 786:       Symbol<E> &sym = this->frag_syms[idx];
 787:       sym.file = this;
 788:       sym.set_name("<fragment>");
 789:       sym.sym_idx = r.r_sym;
 790:       sym.visibility = STV_HIDDEN;
 791:       sym.set_frag(frag);
 792:       sym.value = in_frag_offset - r_addend;
 793:       r.r_sym = this->elf_syms.size() + idx;
 794:       idx++;
 795:     }
 796:   }
 797: 
 798:   assert(idx == this->frag_syms.size());
 799: 
 800:   for (Symbol<E> &sym : this->frag_syms)
 801:     this->symbols.push_back(&sym);
 802: }
 803: 
 804: template <typename E>
 805: void ObjectFile<E>::mark_addrsig(Context<E> &ctx) {
 806:   // Parse a .llvm_addrsig section.
 807:   if (llvm_addrsig) {
 808:     u8 *cur = (u8 *)llvm_addrsig->contents.data();
 809:     u8 *end = cur + llvm_addrsig->contents.size();
 810: 
 811:     while (cur != end) {
 812:       Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[read_uleb(cur)];
 813:       if (sym.file == this)
 814:         if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
 815:           isec->address_significant = true;
 816:     }
 817:   }
 818: 
 819:   // We treat a symbol's address as significant if
 820:   //
 821:   // 1. we have no address significance information for the symbol, or
 822:   // 2. the symbol can be referenced from the outside in an address-
 823:   //    significant manner.
 824:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : this->symbols)
 825:     if (sym->file == this)
 826:       if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym->get_input_section())
 827:         if (!llvm_addrsig || sym->is_exported)
 828:           isec->address_significant = true;
 829: }
 830: 
 831: template <typename E>
 832: void ObjectFile<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
 833:   sections.resize(this->elf_sections.size());
 834:   symtab_sec = this->find_section(SHT_SYMTAB);
 835: 
 836:   if (symtab_sec) {
 837:     // In ELF, all local symbols precede global symbols in the symbol table.
 838:     // sh_info has an index of the first global symbol.
 839:     this->first_global = symtab_sec->sh_info;
 840:     this->elf_syms = this->template get_data<ElfSym<E>>(ctx, *symtab_sec);
 841:     this->symbol_strtab = this->get_string(ctx, symtab_sec->sh_link);
 842:   }
 843: 
 844:   initialize_sections(ctx);
 845:   initialize_symbols(ctx);
 846:   sort_relocations(ctx);
 847:   initialize_ehframe_sections(ctx);
 848: }
 849: 
 850: // Symbols with higher priorities overwrites symbols with lower priorities.
 851: // Here is the list of priorities, from the highest to the lowest.
 852: //
 853: //  1. Strong defined symbol
 854: //  2. Weak defined symbol
 855: //  3. Strong defined symbol in a DSO/archive
 856: //  4. Weak Defined symbol in a DSO/archive
 857: //  5. Common symbol
 858: //  6. Common symbol in an archive
 859: //  7. Unclaimed (nonexistent) symbol
 860: //
 861: // Ties are broken by file priority.
 862: template <typename E>
 863: static u64 get_rank(InputFile<E> *file, const ElfSym<E> &esym, bool is_in_archive) {
 864:   auto get_sym_rank = [&] {
 865:     if (esym.is_common()) {
 866:       assert(!file->is_dso);
 867:       return is_in_archive ? 6 : 5;
 868:     }
 869: 
 870:     if (file->is_dso || is_in_archive)
 871:       return (esym.st_bind == STB_WEAK) ? 4 : 3;
 872: 
 873:     if (esym.st_bind == STB_WEAK)
 874:       return 2;
 875:     return 1;
 876:   };
 877: 
 878:   return (get_sym_rank() << 24) + file->priority;
 879: }
 880: 
 881: template <typename E>
 882: static u64 get_rank(const Symbol<E> &sym) {
 883:   if (!sym.file)
 884:     return 7 << 24;
 885:   return get_rank(sym.file, sym.esym(), !sym.file->is_alive);
 886: }
 887: 
 888: // Symbol's visibility is set to the most restrictive one. For example,
 889: // if one input file has a defined symbol `foo` with the default
 890: // visibility and the other input file has an undefined symbol `foo`
 891: // with the hidden visibility, the resulting symbol is a hidden defined
 892: // symbol.
 893: template <typename E>
 894: void ObjectFile<E>::merge_visibility(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
 895:                                      u8 visibility) {
 896:   // Canonicalize visibility
 897:   if (visibility == STV_INTERNAL)
 898:     visibility = STV_HIDDEN;
 899: 
 900:   auto priority = [&](u8 visibility) {
 901:     switch (visibility) {
 902:     case STV_HIDDEN:
 903:       return 1;
 904:     case STV_PROTECTED:
 905:       return 2;
 906:     case STV_DEFAULT:
 907:       return 3;
 908:     }
 909:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": unknown symbol visibility: " << sym;
 910:   };
 911: 
 912:   update_minimum(sym.visibility, visibility, [&](u8 a, u8 b) {
 913:     return priority(a) < priority(b);
 914:   });
 915: }
 916: 
 917: template <typename E>
 918: static void print_trace_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, InputFile<E> &file,
 919:                                const ElfSym<E> &esym, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 920:   if (!esym.is_undef())
 921:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << file << ": definition of " << sym;
 922:   else if (esym.is_weak())
 923:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << file << ": weak reference to " << sym;
 924:   else
 925:     SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << file << ": reference to " << sym;
 926: }
 927: 
 928: template <typename E>
 929: void ObjectFile<E>::resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 930:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 931:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
 932:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
 933: 
 934:     if (esym.is_undef())
 935:       continue;
 936: 
 937:     InputSection<E> *isec = nullptr;
 938:     if (!esym.is_abs() && !esym.is_common()) {
 939:       isec = get_section(esym);
 940:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive)
 941:         continue;
 942:     }
 943: 
 944:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
 945:     if (get_rank(this, esym, !this->is_alive) < get_rank(sym)) {
 946:       sym.file = this;
 947:       sym.set_input_section(isec);
 948:       sym.value = esym.st_value;
 949:       sym.sym_idx = i;
 950:       sym.ver_idx = ctx.default_version;
 951:       sym.is_weak = esym.is_weak();
 952:     }
 953:   }
 954: }
 955: 
 956: template <typename E>
 957: void
 958: ObjectFile<E>::mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx,
 959:                                  std::function<void(InputFile<E> *)> feeder) {
 960:   assert(this->is_alive);
 961: 
 962:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 963:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
 964:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
 965: 
 966:     if (!esym.is_undef() && exclude_libs)
 967:       merge_visibility(ctx, sym, STV_HIDDEN);
 968:     else
 969:       merge_visibility(ctx, sym, esym.st_visibility);
 970: 
 971:     if (sym.is_traced)
 972:       print_trace_symbol(ctx, *this, esym, sym);
 973: 
 974:     if (esym.is_weak())
 975:       continue;
 976: 
 977:     if (!sym.file)
 978:       continue;
 979: 
 980:     bool keep = esym.is_undef() || (esym.is_common() && !sym.esym().is_common());
 981:     if (keep && !sym.file->is_alive.test_and_set()) {
 982:       feeder(sym.file);
 983: 
 984:       if (sym.is_traced)
 985:         SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << *this << " keeps " << *sym.file
 986:                      << " for " << sym;
 987:     }
 988:   }
 989: }
 990: 
 991: template <typename E>
 992: void ObjectFile<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
 993:   // Scan relocations against seciton contents
 994:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections)
 995:     if (isec && isec->is_alive && (isec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
 996:       isec->scan_relocations(ctx);
 997: 
 998:   // Scan relocations against exception frames
 999:   for (CieRecord<E> &cie : cies) {
1000:     for (ElfRel<E> &rel : cie.get_rels()) {
1001:       Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[rel.r_sym];
1002: 
1003:       if (sym.is_imported) {
1004:         if (sym.get_type() != STT_FUNC)
1005:           Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": " << sym
1006:                      << ": .eh_frame CIE record with an external data reference"
1007:                      << " is not supported";
1008:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
1009:       }
1010:     }
1011:   }
1012: }
1013: 
1014: // Common symbols are used by C's tantative definitions. Tentative
1015: // definition is an obscure C feature which allows users to omit `extern`
1016: // from global variable declarations in a header file. For example, if you
1017: // have a tentative definition `int foo;` in a header which is included
1018: // into multiple translation units, `foo` will be included into multiple
1019: // object files, but it won't cause the duplicate symbol error. Instead,
1020: // the linker will merge them into a single instance of `foo`.
1021: //
1022: // If a header file contains a tentative definition `int foo;` and one of
1023: // a C file contains a definition with initial value such as `int foo = 5;`,
1024: // then the "real" definition wins. The symbol for the tentative definition
1025: // will be resolved to the real definition. If there is no "real"
1026: // definition, the tentative definition gets the default initial value 0.
1027: //
1028: // Tentative definitions are represented as "common symbols" in an object
1029: // file. In this function, we allocate spaces in .common or .tls_common
1030: // for remaining common symbols that were not resolved to usual defined
1031: // symbols in previous passes.
1032: template <typename E>
1033: void ObjectFile<E>::convert_common_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1034:   if (!has_common_symbol)
1035:     return;
1036: 
1037:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1038:     if (!this->elf_syms[i].is_common())
1039:       continue;
1040: 
1041:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1042:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
1043: 
1044:     if (sym.file != this) {
1045:       if (ctx.arg.warn_common)
1046:         Warn(ctx) << *this << ": multiple common symbols: " << sym;
1047:       continue;
1048:     }
1049: 
1050:     elf_sections2.push_back({});
1051:     ElfShdr<E> &shdr = elf_sections2.back();
1052:     memset(&shdr, 0, sizeof(shdr));
1053: 
1054:     std::string_view name;
1055: 
1056:     if (sym.get_type() == STT_TLS) {
1057:       name = ".tls_common";
1058:       shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE | SHF_TLS;
1059:     } else {
1060:       name = ".common";
1061:       shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE;
1062:     }
1063: 
1064:     shdr.sh_type = SHT_NOBITS;
1065:     shdr.sh_size = this->elf_syms[i].st_size;
1066:     shdr.sh_addralign = this->elf_syms[i].st_value;
1067: 
1068:     i64 idx = this->elf_sections.size() + elf_sections2.size() - 1;
1069:     std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> isec =
1070:       std::make_unique<InputSection<E>>(ctx, *this, name, idx);
1071: 
1072:     sym.file = this;
1073:     sym.set_input_section(isec.get());
1074:     sym.value = 0;
1075:     sym.sym_idx = i;
1076:     sym.ver_idx = ctx.default_version;
1077:     sym.is_weak = false;
1078: 
1079:     sections.push_back(std::move(isec));
1080:   }
1081: }
1082: 
1083: template <typename E>
1084: static bool should_write_to_local_symtab(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
1085:   if (sym.get_type() == STT_SECTION)
1086:     return false;
1087: 
1088:   // Local symbols are discarded if --discard-local is given or they
1089:   // are in a mergeable section. I *believe* we exclude symbols in
1090:   // mergeable sections because (1) there are too many and (2) they are
1091:   // merged, so their origins shouldn't matter, but I don't really
1092:   // know the rationale. Anyway, this is the behavior of the
1093:   // traditional linkers.
1094:   if (sym.name().starts_with(".L")) {
1095:     if (ctx.arg.discard_locals)
1096:       return false;
1097: 
1098:     if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
1099:       if (isec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_MERGE)
1100:         return false;
1101:   }
1102: 
1103:   return true;
1104: }
1105: 
1106: template <typename E>
1107: void ObjectFile<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1108:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all)
1109:     return;
1110: 
1111:   this->output_sym_indices.resize(this->elf_syms.size(), -1);
1112: 
1113:   auto is_alive = [&](Symbol<E> &sym) -> bool {
1114:     if (!ctx.arg.gc_sections)
1115:       return true;
1116: 
1117:     if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = sym.get_frag())
1118:       return frag->is_alive;
1119:     if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
1120:       return isec->is_alive;
1121:     return true;
1122:   };
1123: 
1124:   // Compute the size of local symbols
1125:   if (!ctx.arg.discard_all && !ctx.arg.strip_all && !ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file) {
1126:     for (i64 i = 1; i < this->first_global; i++) {
1127:       Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1128: 
1129:       if (is_alive(sym) && should_write_to_local_symtab(ctx, sym)) {
1130:         this->strtab_size += sym.name().size() + 1;
1131:         this->output_sym_indices[i] = this->num_local_symtab++;
1132:         sym.write_to_symtab = true;
1133:       }
1134:     }
1135:   }
1136: 
1137:   // Compute the size of global symbols.
1138:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1139:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1140: 
1141:     if (sym.file == this && is_alive(sym) &&
1142:         (!ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file || sym.write_to_symtab)) {
1143:       this->strtab_size += sym.name().size() + 1;
1144:       // Global symbols can be demoted to local symbols based on visibility,
1145:       // version scripts etc.
1146:       if (sym.is_local(ctx))
1147:         this->output_sym_indices[i] = this->num_local_symtab++;
1148:       else
1149:         this->output_sym_indices[i] = this->num_global_symtab++;
1150:       sym.write_to_symtab = true;
1151:     }
1152:   }
1153: }
1154: 
1155: template <typename E>
1156: void ObjectFile<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1157:   ElfSym<E> *symtab_base = (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset);
1158: 
1159:   u8 *strtab_base = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1160:   i64 strtab_off = this->strtab_offset;
1161: 
1162:   auto write_sym = [&](Symbol<E> &sym, i64 &symtab_idx) {
1163:     U32<E> *xindex = nullptr;
1164:     if (ctx.symtab_shndx)
1165:       xindex = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab_shndx->shdr.sh_offset +
1166:                           symtab_idx * 4);
1167: 
1168:     symtab_base[symtab_idx++] = to_output_esym(ctx, sym, strtab_off, xindex);
1169:     strtab_off += write_string(strtab_base + strtab_off, sym.name());
1170:   };
1171: 
1172:   i64 local_symtab_idx = this->local_symtab_idx;
1173:   i64 global_symtab_idx = this->global_symtab_idx;
1174:   for (i64 i = 1; i < this->first_global; i++) {
1175:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1176:     if (sym.write_to_symtab)
1177:       write_sym(sym, local_symtab_idx);
1178:   }
1179: 
1180:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1181:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1182:     if (sym.file == this && sym.write_to_symtab) {
1183:       if (sym.is_local(ctx))
1184:         write_sym(sym, local_symtab_idx);
1185:       else
1186:         write_sym(sym, global_symtab_idx);
1187:     }
1188:   }
1189: }
1190: 
1191: template <typename E>
1192: std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, const InputFile<E> &file) {
1193:   if (file.is_dso) {
1194:     out << path_clean(file.filename);
1195:     return out;
1196:   }
1197: 
1198:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = (ObjectFile<E> *)&file;
1199:   if (obj->archive_name == "")
1200:     out << path_clean(obj->filename);
1201:   else
1202:     out << path_clean(obj->archive_name) << "(" << obj->filename + ")";
1203:   return out;
1204: }
1205: 
1206: template <typename E>
1207: SharedFile<E> *
1208: SharedFile<E>::create(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
1209:   SharedFile<E> *obj = new SharedFile(ctx, mf);
1210:   ctx.dso_pool.emplace_back(obj);
1211:   return obj;
1212: }
1213: 
1214: template <typename E>
1215: SharedFile<E>::SharedFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf)
1216:   : InputFile<E>(ctx, mf) {
1217:   this->is_needed = ctx.as_needed;
1218:   this->is_alive = !ctx.as_needed;
1219: }
1220: 
1221: template <typename E>
1222: std::string SharedFile<E>::get_soname(Context<E> &ctx) {
1223:   if (ElfShdr<E> *sec = this->find_section(SHT_DYNAMIC))
1224:     for (ElfDyn<E> &dyn : this->template get_data<ElfDyn<E>>(ctx, *sec))
1225:       if (dyn.d_tag == DT_SONAME)
1226:         return this->symbol_strtab.data() + dyn.d_val;
1227: 
1228:   if (this->mf->given_fullpath)
1229:     return this->filename;
1230: 
1231:   return filepath(this->filename).filename().string();
1232: }
1233: 
1234: template <typename E>
1235: void SharedFile<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
1236:   symtab_sec = this->find_section(SHT_DYNSYM);
1237:   if (!symtab_sec)
1238:     return;
1239: 
1240:   this->symbol_strtab = this->get_string(ctx, symtab_sec->sh_link);
1241:   soname = get_soname(ctx);
1242:   version_strings = read_verdef(ctx);
1243: 
1244:   // Read a symbol table.
1245:   std::span<ElfSym<E>> esyms = this->template get_data<ElfSym<E>>(ctx, *symtab_sec);
1246: 
1247:   std::span<U16<E>> vers;
1248:   if (ElfShdr<E> *sec = this->find_section(SHT_GNU_VERSYM))
1249:     vers = this->template get_data<U16<E>>(ctx, *sec);
1250: 
1251:   for (i64 i = symtab_sec->sh_info; i < esyms.size(); i++) {
1252:     u16 ver;
1253:     if (vers.empty() || esyms[i].is_undef())
1254:       ver = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
1255:     else
1256:       ver = (vers[i] & ~VERSYM_HIDDEN);
1257: 
1258:     if (ver == VER_NDX_LOCAL)
1259:       continue;
1260: 
1261:     std::string_view name = this->symbol_strtab.data() + esyms[i].st_name;
1262:     bool is_hidden = (!vers.empty() && (vers[i] & VERSYM_HIDDEN));
1263: 
1264:     this->elf_syms2.push_back(esyms[i]);
1265:     this->versyms.push_back(ver);
1266: 
1267:     if (is_hidden) {
1268:       std::string_view mangled_name = save_string(
1269:         ctx, std::string(name) + "@" + std::string(version_strings[ver]));
1270:       this->symbols.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, mangled_name, name));
1271:     } else {
1272:       this->symbols.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, name));
1273:     }
1274:   }
1275: 
1276:   this->elf_syms = elf_syms2;
1277:   this->first_global = 0;
1278: 
1279:   static Counter counter("dso_syms");
1280:   counter += this->elf_syms.size();
1281: }
1282: 
1283: // Symbol versioning is a GNU extension to the ELF file format. I don't
1284: // particularly like the feature as it complicates the semantics of
1285: // dynamic linking, but we need to support it anyway because it is
1286: // mandatory on glibc-based systems such as most Linux distros.
1287: //
1288: // Let me explain what symbol versioning is. Symbol versioning is a
1289: // mechanism to allow multiple symbols of the same name but of different
1290: // versions live together in a shared object file. It's convenient if you
1291: // want to make an API-breaking change to some function but want to keep
1292: // old programs working with the newer libraries.
1293: //
1294: // With symbol versioning, dynamic symbols are resolved by (name, version)
1295: // tuple instead of just by name. For example, glibc 2.35 defines two
1296: // different versions of `posix_spawn`, `posix_spawn` of version
1297: // "GLIBC_2.15" and that of version "GLIBC_2.2.5". Any executable that
1298: // uses `posix_spawn` is linked either to that of "GLIBC_2.15" or that of
1299: // "GLIBC_2.2.5"
1300: //
1301: // Versions are just strings, and no ordering is defined between them.
1302: // For example, "GLIBC_2.15" is not considered a newer version of
1303: // "GLIBC_2.2.5" or vice versa. They are considered just different.
1304: //
1305: // If a shared object file has versioned symbols, it contains a parallel
1306: // array for the symbol table. Version strings can be found in that
1307: // parallel table.
1308: //
1309: // One version is considered the "default" version for each shared object.
1310: // If an undefiend symbol `foo` is resolved to a symbol defined by the
1311: // shared object, it's marked so that it'll be resolved to (`foo`, the
1312: // default version of the library) at load-time.
1313: template <typename E>
1314: std::vector<std::string_view> SharedFile<E>::read_verdef(Context<E> &ctx) {
1315:   std::vector<std::string_view> ret(VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED + 1);
1316: 
1317:   ElfShdr<E> *verdef_sec = this->find_section(SHT_GNU_VERDEF);
1318:   if (!verdef_sec)
1319:     return ret;
1320: 
1321:   std::string_view verdef = this->get_string(ctx, *verdef_sec);
1322:   std::string_view strtab = this->get_string(ctx, verdef_sec->sh_link);
1323: 
1324:   ElfVerdef<E> *ver = (ElfVerdef<E> *)verdef.data();
1325: 
1326:   for (;;) {
1327:     if (ret.size() <= ver->vd_ndx)
1328:       ret.resize(ver->vd_ndx + 1);
1329: 
1330:     ElfVerdaux<E> *aux = (ElfVerdaux<E> *)((u8 *)ver + ver->vd_aux);
1331:     ret[ver->vd_ndx] = strtab.data() + aux->vda_name;
1332:     if (!ver->vd_next)
1333:       break;
1334: 
1335:     ver = (ElfVerdef<E> *)((u8 *)ver + ver->vd_next);
1336:   }
1337:   return ret;
1338: }
1339: 
1340: template <typename E>
1341: void SharedFile<E>::resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1342:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->symbols.size(); i++) {
1343:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1344:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
1345:     if (esym.is_undef())
1346:       continue;
1347: 
1348:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
1349: 
1350:     if (get_rank(this, esym, false) < get_rank(sym)) {
1351:       sym.file = this;
1352:       sym.origin = 0;
1353:       sym.value = esym.st_value;
1354:       sym.sym_idx = i;
1355:       sym.ver_idx = versyms[i];
1356:       sym.is_weak = false;
1357:     }
1358:   }
1359: }
1360: 
1361: template <typename E>
1362: void
1363: SharedFile<E>::mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx,
1364:                                  std::function<void(InputFile<E> *)> feeder) {
1365:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1366:     const ElfSym<E> &esym = this->elf_syms[i];
1367:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1368: 
1369:     if (sym.is_traced)
1370:       print_trace_symbol(ctx, *this, esym, sym);
1371: 
1372:     if (esym.is_undef() && sym.file && !sym.file->is_alive.test_and_set()) {
1373:       feeder(sym.file);
1374: 
1375:       if (sym.is_traced)
1376:         SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << *this << " keeps " << *sym.file
1377:                      << " for " << sym;
1378:     }
1379:   }
1380: }
1381: 
1382: template <typename E>
1383: std::span<Symbol<E> *> SharedFile<E>::find_aliases(Symbol<E> *sym) {
1384:   assert(sym->file == this);
1385: 
1386:   std::call_once(init_aliases, [&] {
1387:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : this->symbols)
1388:       if (sym->file == this)
1389:         aliases.push_back(sym);
1390: 
1391:     tbb::parallel_sort(aliases.begin(), aliases.end(),
1392:                        [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) {
1393:       const ElfSym<E> &x = a->esym();
1394:       const ElfSym<E> &y = b->esym();
1395:       return std::tuple{x.st_value, &x} < std::tuple{y.st_value, &y};
1396:     });
1397:   });
1398: 
1399:   auto [begin, end] = std::equal_range(aliases.begin(), aliases.end(), sym,
1400:                                        [&](Symbol<E> *x, Symbol<E> *y) {
1401:     return x->esym().st_value < y->esym().st_value;
1402:   });
1403: 
1404:   return {&*begin, &*end};
1405: }
1406: 
1407: // Infer an alignment of a DSO symbol. An alignment of a symbol in other
1408: // .so is not something we usually care about, but when we create a copy
1409: // relocation for a symbol, we need to preserve its alignment requirement.
1410: //
1411: // Symbol alignment is not explicitly represented in an ELF file. In this
1412: // function, we conservatively infer it from a symbol address and a
1413: // section alignment requirement.
1414: template <typename E>
1415: i64 SharedFile<E>::get_alignment(Symbol<E> *sym) {
1416:   ElfShdr<E> &shdr = this->elf_sections[sym->esym().st_shndx];
1417:   i64 align = std::max<i64>(1, shdr.sh_addralign);
1418:   if (sym->value)
1419:     align = std::min<i64>(align, 1LL << std::countr_zero(sym->value));
1420:   return align;
1421: }
1422: 
1423: template <typename E>
1424: bool SharedFile<E>::is_readonly(Symbol<E> *sym) {
1425:   u64 val = sym->esym().st_value;
1426: 
1427:   for (ElfPhdr<E> &phdr : this->get_phdrs())
1428:     if (phdr.p_type == PT_LOAD && !(phdr.p_flags & PF_W) &&
1429:         phdr.p_vaddr <= val && val < phdr.p_vaddr + phdr.p_memsz)
1430:       return true;
1431:   return false;
1432: }
1433: 
1434: template <typename E>
1435: void SharedFile<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1436:   if (ctx.arg.strip_all)
1437:     return;
1438: 
1439:   this->output_sym_indices.resize(this->elf_syms.size(), -1);
1440: 
1441:   // Compute the size of global symbols.
1442:   for (i64 i = this->first_global; i < this->symbols.size(); i++) {
1443:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[i];
1444: 
1445:     if (sym.file == this && (sym.is_imported || sym.is_exported) &&
1446:         (!ctx.arg.retain_symbols_file || sym.write_to_symtab)) {
1447:       this->strtab_size += sym.name().size() + 1;
1448:       this->output_sym_indices[i] = this->num_global_symtab++;
1449:       sym.write_to_symtab = true;
1450:     }
1451:   }
1452: }
1453: 
1454: template <typename E>
1455: void SharedFile<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1456:   ElfSym<E> *symtab =
1457:     (ElfSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_offset) + this->global_symtab_idx;
1458: 
1459:   u8 *strtab = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab->shdr.sh_offset;
1460:   i64 strtab_off = this->strtab_offset;
1461: 
1462:   for (i64 i = 0, j = this->first_global; j < this->elf_syms.size(); i++, j++) {
1463:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->symbols[j];
1464:     if (sym.file != this || !sym.write_to_symtab)
1465:       continue;
1466: 
1467:     U32<E> *xindex = nullptr;
1468:     if (ctx.symtab_shndx)
1469:       xindex = (U32<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab_shndx->shdr.sh_offset +
1470:                           (this->global_symtab_idx + i) * 4);
1471: 
1472:     *symtab++ = to_output_esym(ctx, sym, strtab_off, xindex);
1473:     strtab_off += write_string(strtab + strtab_off, sym.name());
1474:   }
1475: }
1476: 
1477: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
1478: 
1479: template class InputFile<E>;
1480: template class ObjectFile<E>;
1481: template class SharedFile<E>;
1482: template std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &, const InputFile<E> &);
1483: 
1484: } // namespace mold::elf

  17: template <typename E>
  18: void apply_exclude_libs(Context<E> &ctx) {
  19:   Timer t(ctx, "apply_exclude_libs");
  20: 
  21:   if (ctx.arg.exclude_libs.empty())
  22:     return;
  23: 
  24:   std::unordered_set<std::string_view> set(ctx.arg.exclude_libs.begin(),
  25:                                            ctx.arg.exclude_libs.end());
  26: 
  27:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
  28:     if (!file->archive_name.empty())
  29:       if (set.contains("ALL") ||
  30:           set.contains(filepath(file->archive_name).filename().string()))
  31:         file->exclude_libs = true;
  32:   }
  33: }
  34: 
  35: template <typename E>
  36: void create_synthetic_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
  37:   auto push = [&]<typename T>(T *x) {
  38:     ctx.chunks.push_back(x);
  39:     ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(x);
  40:     return x;
  41:   };
  42: 
  43:   if (!ctx.arg.oformat_binary) {
  44:     auto find = [&](std::string_view name) {
  45:       for (SectionOrder &ord : ctx.arg.section_order)
  46:         if (ord.type == SectionOrder::SECTION && ord.name == name)
  47:           return true;
  48:       return false;
  49:     };
  50: 
  51:     if (ctx.arg.section_order.empty() || find("EHDR"))
  52:       ctx.ehdr = push(new OutputEhdr<E>(SHF_ALLOC));
  53:     else
  54:       ctx.ehdr = push(new OutputEhdr<E>(0));
  55: 
  56:     if (ctx.arg.section_order.empty() || find("PHDR"))
  57:       ctx.phdr = push(new OutputPhdr<E>(SHF_ALLOC));
  58:     else
  59:       ctx.phdr = push(new OutputPhdr<E>(0));
  60: 
  61:     ctx.shdr = push(new OutputShdr<E>);
  62:   }
  63: 
  64:   ctx.got = push(new GotSection<E>);
  65: 
  66:   if constexpr (!is_sparc<E>)
  67:     ctx.gotplt = push(new GotPltSection<E>);
  68: 
  69:   ctx.reldyn = push(new RelDynSection<E>);
  70:   ctx.relplt = push(new RelPltSection<E>);
  71: 
  72:   if (ctx.arg.pack_dyn_relocs_relr)
  73:     ctx.relrdyn = push(new RelrDynSection<E>);
  74: 
  75:   ctx.strtab = push(new StrtabSection<E>);
  76:   ctx.plt = push(new PltSection<E>);
  77:   ctx.pltgot = push(new PltGotSection<E>);
  78:   ctx.symtab = push(new SymtabSection<E>);
  79:   ctx.dynsym = push(new DynsymSection<E>);
  80:   ctx.dynstr = push(new DynstrSection<E>);
  81:   ctx.eh_frame = push(new EhFrameSection<E>);
  82:   ctx.copyrel = push(new CopyrelSection<E>(false));
  83:   ctx.copyrel_relro = push(new CopyrelSection<E>(true));
  84: 
  85:   if (!ctx.arg.oformat_binary)
  86:     ctx.shstrtab = push(new ShstrtabSection<E>);
  87: 
  88:   if (!ctx.arg.dynamic_linker.empty())
  89:     ctx.interp = push(new InterpSection<E>);
  90:   if (ctx.arg.build_id.kind != BuildId::NONE)
  91:     ctx.buildid = push(new BuildIdSection<E>);
  92:   if (ctx.arg.eh_frame_hdr)
  93:     ctx.eh_frame_hdr = push(new EhFrameHdrSection<E>);
  94:   if (ctx.arg.gdb_index)
  95:     ctx.gdb_index = push(new GdbIndexSection<E>);
  96:   if (ctx.arg.z_relro && ctx.arg.section_order.empty() &&
  97:       ctx.arg.z_separate_code != SEPARATE_LOADABLE_SEGMENTS)
  98:     ctx.relro_padding = push(new RelroPaddingSection<E>);
  99:   if (ctx.arg.hash_style_sysv)
 100:     ctx.hash = push(new HashSection<E>);
 101:   if (ctx.arg.hash_style_gnu)
 102:     ctx.gnu_hash = push(new GnuHashSection<E>);
 103:   if (!ctx.arg.version_definitions.empty())
 104:     ctx.verdef = push(new VerdefSection<E>);
 105:   if (ctx.arg.emit_relocs)
 106:     ctx.eh_frame_reloc = push(new EhFrameRelocSection<E>);
 107: 
 108:   if (ctx.arg.shared || !ctx.dsos.empty() || ctx.arg.pie)
 109:     ctx.dynamic = push(new DynamicSection<E>);
 110: 
 111:   ctx.versym = push(new VersymSection<E>);
 112:   ctx.verneed = push(new VerneedSection<E>);
 113:   ctx.note_package = push(new NotePackageSection<E>);
 114:   ctx.note_property = push(new NotePropertySection<E>);
 115: 
 116: 
 117:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
 118:     ctx.extra.opd = push(new PPC64OpdSection);
 119: 
 120:   if constexpr (is_sparc<E>) {
 121:     if (ctx.arg.is_static)
 122:       ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sec = push(new SparcTlsGetAddrSection);
 123:     ctx.extra.tls_get_addr_sym = get_symbol(ctx, "__tls_get_addr");
 124:   }
 125: 
 126:   if constexpr (is_alpha<E>)
 127:     ctx.extra.got = push(new AlphaGotSection);
 128: 
 129:   // If .dynamic exists, .dynsym and .dynstr must exist as well
 130:   // since .dynamic refers them.
 131:   if (ctx.dynamic) {
 132:     ctx.dynstr->keep();
 133:     ctx.dynsym->keep();
 134:   }
 135: }
 136: 
 137: template <typename E>
 138: static void mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx) {
 139:   auto mark_symbol = [&](std::string_view name) {
 140:     if (InputFile<E> *file = get_symbol(ctx, name)->file)
 141:       file->is_alive = true;
 142:   };
 143: 
 144:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.undefined)
 145:     mark_symbol(name);
 146:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.require_defined)
 147:     mark_symbol(name);
 148: 
 149:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> roots;
 150: 
 151:   for (InputFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
 152:     if (file->is_alive)
 153:       roots.push_back(file);
 154: 
 155:   for (InputFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos)
 156:     if (file->is_alive)
 157:       roots.push_back(file);
 158: 
 159:   tbb::parallel_for_each(roots, [&](InputFile<E> *file,
 160:                                     tbb::feeder<InputFile<E> *> &feeder) {
 161:     if (file->is_alive)
 162:       file->mark_live_objects(ctx, [&](InputFile<E> *obj) { feeder.add(obj); });
 163:   });
 164: }
 165: 
 166: template <typename E>
 167: void do_resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 168:   auto for_each_file = [&](std::function<void(InputFile<E> *)> fn) {
 169:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, fn);
 170:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, fn);
 171:   };
 172: 
 173:   // Due to legacy reasons, archive members will only get included in the final
 174:   // binary if they satisfy one of the undefined symbols in a non-archive object
 175:   // file. This is called archive extraction. In finalize_archive_extraction,
 176:   // this is processed as follows:
 177:   //
 178:   // 1. Do preliminary symbol resolution assuming all archive members
 179:   //    are included. This matches the undefined symbols with ones to be
 180:   //    extracted from archives.
 181:   //
 182:   // 2. Do a mark & sweep pass to eliminate unneeded archive members.
 183:   //
 184:   // Note that the symbol resolution inside finalize_archive_extraction uses a
 185:   // different rule. In order to prevent extracting archive members that can be
 186:   // satisfied by either non-archive object files or DSOs, the archive members
 187:   // are given a lower priority. This is not correct for the general case, where
 188:   // *extracted* object files have precedence over DSOs and even non-archive
 189:   // files that are passed earlier in the command line. Hence, the symbol
 190:   // resolution is thrown away once we determine which archive members to
 191:   // extract, and redone later with the formal rule.
 192:   {
 193:     Timer t(ctx, "extract_archive_members");
 194: 
 195:     // Register symbols
 196:     for_each_file([&](InputFile<E> *file) { file->resolve_symbols(ctx); });
 197: 
 198:     // Mark reachable objects to decide which files to include into an output.
 199:     // This also merges symbol visibility.
 200:     mark_live_objects(ctx);
 201: 
 202:     // Cleanup. The rule used for archive extraction isn't accurate for the
 203:     // general case of symbol extraction, so reset the resolution to be redone
 204:     // later.
 205:     for_each_file([](InputFile<E> *file) { file->clear_symbols(); });
 206: 
 207:     // Now that the symbol references are gone, remove the eliminated files from
 208:     // the file list.
 209:     std::erase_if(ctx.objs, [](InputFile<E> *file) { return !file->is_alive; });
 210:     std::erase_if(ctx.dsos, [](InputFile<E> *file) { return !file->is_alive; });
 211:   }
 212: 
 213:   // COMDAT elimination needs to happen exactly here.
 214:   //
 215:   // It needs to be after archive extraction, otherwise we might assign COMDAT
 216:   // leader to an archive member that is not supposed to be extracted.
 217:   //
 218:   // It needs to happen before symbol resolution, otherwise we could eliminate
 219:   // a symbol that is already resolved to and cause dangling references.
 220:   {
 221:     Timer t(ctx, "eliminate_comdats");
 222: 
 223:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 224:       for (ComdatGroupRef<E> &ref : file->comdat_groups)
 225:         update_minimum(ref.group->owner, file->priority);
 226:     });
 227: 
 228:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 229:       for (ComdatGroupRef<E> &ref : file->comdat_groups)
 230:         if (ref.group->owner != file->priority)
 231:           for (u32 i : ref.members)
 232:             if (file->sections[i])
 233:               file->sections[i]->kill();
 234:     });
 235:   }
 236: 
 237:   // Since we have turned on object files live bits, their symbols
 238:   // may now have higher priority than before. So run the symbol
 239:   // resolution pass again to get the final resolution result.
 240:   for_each_file([&](InputFile<E> *file) { file->resolve_symbols(ctx); });
 241: }
 242: 
 243: template <typename E>
 244: void resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 245:   Timer t(ctx, "resolve_symbols");
 246: 
 247:   std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> objs = ctx.objs;
 248:   std::vector<SharedFile<E> *> dsos = ctx.dsos;
 249: 
 250:   do_resolve_symbols(ctx);
 251: 
 252:   if (ctx.has_lto_object) {
 253:     // Do link-time optimization. We pass all IR object files to the
 254:     // compiler backend to compile them into a few ELF object files.
 255:     //
 256:     // The compiler backend needs to know how symbols are resolved,
 257:     // so compute symbol visibility, import/export bits, etc early.
 258:     mark_live_objects(ctx);
 259:     apply_version_script(ctx);
 260:     parse_symbol_version(ctx);
 261:     compute_import_export(ctx);
 262: 
 263:     // Do LTO. It compiles IR object files into a few big ELF files.
 264:     std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> lto_objs = do_lto(ctx);
 265: 
 266:     // do_resolve_symbols() have removed unreferenced files. Restore the
 267:     // original files here because some of them may have to be resurrected
 268:     // because they are referenced by the ELF files returned from do_lto().
 269:     ctx.objs = objs;
 270:     ctx.dsos = dsos;
 271: 
 272:     append(ctx.objs, lto_objs);
 273: 
 274:     // Redo name resolution from scratch.
 275:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 276:       file->clear_symbols();
 277:     });
 278: 
 279:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, [&](SharedFile<E> *file) {
 280:       file->clear_symbols();
 281:     });
 282: 
 283:     // Remove IR object files.
 284:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
 285:       if (file->is_lto_obj)
 286:         file->is_alive = false;
 287: 
 288:     std::erase_if(ctx.objs, [](ObjectFile<E> *file) { return file->is_lto_obj; });
 289: 
 290:     do_resolve_symbols(ctx);
 291:   }
 292: }
 293: 
 294: // .eh_frame sections are parsed and regenerated by the linker for the purpose
 295: // of deduplication and garbage collection. As such, the input sections should
 296: // not be copied over.
 297: // However, in very rare cases (e.g. GCC CRT compiled with LTO) we might need
 298: // to resolve cross-object .eh_frame section references (they only point to
 299: // begin or end and don't depend on the actual section contents).
 300: // Therefore, the sections are "killed" after symbol resolution as a separate
 301: // pass.
 302: template <typename E>
 303: void kill_eh_frame_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 304:   Timer t(ctx, "kill_eh_frame_sections");
 305: 
 306:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 307:     for (i64 i = 0; i < file->sections.size(); i++) {
 308:       if (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec = file->sections[i]) {
 309:         if (isec && isec->is_alive && isec->name() == ".eh_frame") {
 310:           isec->is_alive = false;
 311:         }
 312:       }
 313:     }
 314:   });
 315: }
 316: 
 317: template <typename E>
 318: void resolve_section_pieces(Context<E> &ctx) {
 319:   Timer t(ctx, "resolve_section_pieces");
 320: 
 321:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 322:     file->initialize_mergeable_sections(ctx);
 323:   });
 324: 
 325:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 326:     file->resolve_section_pieces(ctx);
 327:   });
 328: }
 329: 
 330: template <typename E>
 331: void convert_common_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 332:   Timer t(ctx, "convert_common_symbols");
 333: 
 334:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 335:     file->convert_common_symbols(ctx);
 336:   });
 337: }
 338: 
 339: template <typename E>
 340: static std::string get_cmdline_args(Context<E> &ctx) {
 341:   std::stringstream ss;
 342:   ss << ctx.cmdline_args[1];
 343:   for (i64 i = 2; i < ctx.cmdline_args.size(); i++)
 344:     ss << " " << ctx.cmdline_args[i];
 345:   return ss.str();
 346: }
 347: 
 348: template <typename E>
 349: void add_comment_string(Context<E> &ctx, std::string str) {
 350:   MergedSection<E> *sec =
 351:     MergedSection<E>::get_instance(ctx, ".comment", SHT_PROGBITS,
 352:                                    SHF_MERGE | SHF_STRINGS);
 353: 
 354:   std::string_view buf = save_string(ctx, str);
 355:   std::string_view data(buf.data(), buf.size() + 1);
 356:   sec->insert(ctx, data, hash_string(data), 0);
 357: }
 358: 
 359: template <typename E>
 360: void compute_merged_section_sizes(Context<E> &ctx) {
 361:   Timer t(ctx, "compute_merged_section_sizes");
 362: 
 363:   // Add an identification string to .comment.
 364:   if (!ctx.arg.oformat_binary)
 365:     add_comment_string(ctx, mold_version);
 366: 
 367:   // Embed command line arguments for debugging.
 368:   if (char *env = getenv("MOLD_DEBUG"); env && env[0])
 369:     add_comment_string(ctx, "mold command line: " + get_cmdline_args(ctx));
 370: 
 371:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.merged_sections,
 372:                          [&](std::unique_ptr<MergedSection<E>> &sec) {
 373:     sec->assign_offsets(ctx);
 374:   });
 375: }
 376: 
 377: template <typename T>
 378: static std::vector<std::span<T>> split(std::vector<T> &input, i64 unit) {
 379:   std::span<T> span(input);
 380:   std::vector<std::span<T>> vec;
 381: 
 382:   while (span.size() >= unit) {
 383:     vec.push_back(span.subspan(0, unit));
 384:     span = span.subspan(unit);
 385:   }
 386:   if (!span.empty())
 387:     vec.push_back(span);
 388:   return vec;
 389: }
 390: 
 391: template <typename E>
 392: static u64 canonicalize_type(std::string_view name, u64 type) {
 393:   if (type == SHT_PROGBITS) {
 394:     if (name == ".init_array" || name.starts_with(".init_array."))
 395:       return SHT_INIT_ARRAY;
 396:     if (name == ".fini_array" || name.starts_with(".fini_array."))
 397:       return SHT_FINI_ARRAY;
 398:   }
 399: 
 400:   if constexpr (is_x86_64<E>)
 401:     if (type == SHT_X86_64_UNWIND)
 402:       return SHT_PROGBITS;
 403:   return type;
 404: }
 405: 
 406: struct OutputSectionKey {
 407:   std::string_view name;
 408:   u64 type;
 409:   u64 flags;
 410: 
 411:   bool operator==(const OutputSectionKey &other) const {
 412:     return name == other.name && type == other.type && flags == other.flags;
 413:   }
 414: };
 415: 
 416: template <typename E>
 417: std::string_view
 418: get_output_name(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name, u64 flags) {
 419:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable && !ctx.arg.relocatable_merge_sections)
 420:     return name;
 421:   if (ctx.arg.unique && ctx.arg.unique->match(name))
 422:     return name;
 423:   if (flags & SHF_MERGE)
 424:     return name;
 425: 
 426:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
 427:     if (name.starts_with(".ARM.exidx"))
 428:       return ".ARM.exidx";
 429:     if (name.starts_with(".ARM.extab"))
 430:       return ".ARM.extab";
 431:   }
 432: 
 433:   if constexpr (is_alpha<E>) {
 434:     if (name.starts_with(".sdata."))
 435:       return ".sdata";
 436:     if (name.starts_with(".sbss."))
 437:       return ".sbss";
 438:   }
 439: 
 440:   if (ctx.arg.z_keep_text_section_prefix) {
 441:     static std::string_view prefixes[] = {
 442:       ".text.hot.", ".text.unknown.", ".text.unlikely.", ".text.startup.",
 443:       ".text.exit."
 444:     };
 445: 
 446:     for (std::string_view prefix : prefixes) {
 447:       std::string_view stem = prefix.substr(0, prefix.size() - 1);
 448:       if (name == stem || name.starts_with(prefix))
 449:         return stem;
 450:     }
 451:   }
 452: 
 453:   static std::string_view prefixes[] = {
 454:     ".text.", ".data.rel.ro.", ".data.", ".rodata.", ".bss.rel.ro.", ".bss.",
 455:     ".init_array.", ".fini_array.", ".tbss.", ".tdata.", ".gcc_except_table.",
 456:     ".ctors.", ".dtors.", ".gnu.warning.",
 457:   };
 458: 
 459:   for (std::string_view prefix : prefixes) {
 460:     std::string_view stem = prefix.substr(0, prefix.size() - 1);
 461:     if (name == stem || name.starts_with(prefix))
 462:       return stem;
 463:   }
 464: 
 465:   return name;
 466: }
 467: 
 468: template <typename E>
 469: static OutputSectionKey
 470: get_output_section_key(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec) {
 471:   const ElfShdr<E> &shdr = isec.shdr();
 472:   std::string_view name = get_output_name(ctx, isec.name(), shdr.sh_flags);
 473:   u64 type = canonicalize_type<E>(name, shdr.sh_type);
 474:   u64 flags = shdr.sh_flags & ~(u64)SHF_COMPRESSED;
 475: 
 476:   if (!ctx.arg.relocatable)
 477:     flags &= ~(u64)SHF_GROUP & ~(u64)SHF_GNU_RETAIN;
 478: 
 479:   // .init_array is usually writable. We don't want to create multiple
 480:   // .init_array output sections, so make it always writable.
 481:   // So is .fini_array.
 482:   if (type == SHT_INIT_ARRAY || type == SHT_FINI_ARRAY)
 483:     flags |= SHF_WRITE;
 484:   return {name, type, flags};
 485: }
 486: 
 487: // Create output sections for input sections.
 488: template <typename E>
 489: void create_output_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 490:   Timer t(ctx, "create_output_sections");
 491: 
 492:   struct Hash {
 493:     size_t operator()(const OutputSectionKey &k) const {
 494:       u64 h = hash_string(k.name);
 495:       h = combine_hash(h, std::hash<u64>{}(k.type));
 496:       h = combine_hash(h, std::hash<u64>{}(k.flags));
 497:       return h;
 498:     }
 499:   };
 500: 
 501:   std::unordered_map<OutputSectionKey, OutputSection<E> *, Hash> map;
 502:   std::shared_mutex mu;
 503: 
 504:   // Instantiate output sections
 505:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 506:     // Make a per-thread cache of the main map to avoid lock contention.
 507:     // It makes a noticeable difference if we have millions of input sections.
 508:     decltype(map) cache;
 509:     {
 510:       std::shared_lock lock(mu);
 511:       cache = map;
 512:     }
 513: 
 514:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
 515:       if (!isec || !isec->is_alive)
 516:         continue;
 517: 
 518:       OutputSectionKey key = get_output_section_key(ctx, *isec);
 519: 
 520:       if (auto it = cache.find(key); it != cache.end()) {
 521:         isec->output_section = it->second;
 522:         continue;
 523:       }
 524: 
 525:       auto get_or_insert = [&] {
 526:         {
 527:           std::shared_lock lock(mu);
 528:           if (auto it = map.find(key); it != map.end())
 529:             return it->second;
 530:         }
 531: 
 532:         std::unique_ptr<OutputSection<E>> osec =
 533:           std::make_unique<OutputSection<E>>(key.name, key.type, key.flags);
 534: 
 535:         std::unique_lock lock(mu);
 536:         auto [it, inserted] = map.insert({key, osec.get()});
 537:         OutputSection<E> *ret = it->second;
 538:         lock.unlock();
 539: 
 540:         if (inserted)
 541:           ctx.osec_pool.emplace_back(std::move(osec));
 542:         return ret;
 543:       };
 544: 
 545:       OutputSection<E> *osec = get_or_insert();
 546:       isec->output_section = osec;
 547:       cache.insert({key, osec});
 548:     }
 549:   });
 550: 
 551:   // Add input sections to output sections
 552:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
 553:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections)
 554:       if (isec && isec->is_alive)
 555:         isec->output_section->members.push_back(isec.get());
 556: 
 557:   // Add output sections and mergeable sections to ctx.chunks
 558:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> vec;
 559:   for (std::pair<const OutputSectionKey, OutputSection<E> *> &kv : map)
 560:     vec.push_back(kv.second);
 561: 
 562:   for (std::unique_ptr<MergedSection<E>> &osec : ctx.merged_sections)
 563:     if (osec->shdr.sh_size)
 564:       vec.push_back(osec.get());
 565: 
 566:   // Sections are added to the section lists in an arbitrary order
 567:   // because they are created in parallel. Sort them to to make the
 568:   // output deterministic.
