 569:   tbb::parallel_sort(vec.begin(), vec.end(), [](Chunk<E> *x, Chunk<E> *y) {
 570:     return std::tuple(x->name, x->shdr.sh_type, x->shdr.sh_flags) <
 571:            std::tuple(y->name, y->shdr.sh_type, y->shdr.sh_flags);
 572:   });
 573: 
 574:   append(ctx.chunks, vec);
 575: }
 576: 
 577: // Create a dummy object file containing linker-synthesized
 578: // symbols.
 579: template <typename E>
 580: void create_internal_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 581:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = new ObjectFile<E>;
 582:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(obj);
 583:   ctx.internal_obj = obj;
 584:   ctx.objs.push_back(obj);
 585: 
 586:   // Create linker-synthesized symbols.
 587:   ctx.internal_esyms.resize(1);
 588: 
 589:   obj->symbols.push_back(new Symbol<E>);
 590:   obj->first_global = 1;
 591:   obj->is_alive = true;
 592:   obj->priority = 1;
 593: 
 594:   auto add = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 595:     obj->symbols.push_back(sym);
 596: 
 597:     // An actual value will be set to a linker-synthesized symbol by
 598:     // fix_synthetic_symbols(). Until then, `value` doesn't have a valid
 599:     // value. 0xdeadbeef is a unique dummy value to make debugging easier
 600:     // if the field is accidentally used before it gets a valid one.
 601:     sym->value = 0xdeadbeef;
 602: 
 603:     ElfSym<E> esym;
 604:     memset(&esym, 0, sizeof(esym));
 605:     esym.st_type = STT_NOTYPE;
 606:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_ABS;
 607:     esym.st_bind = STB_GLOBAL;
 608:     esym.st_visibility = STV_DEFAULT;
 609:     ctx.internal_esyms.push_back(esym);
 610:   };
 611: 
 612:   auto add_undef = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 613:     obj->symbols.push_back(sym);
 614:     sym->value = 0xdeadbeef;
 615: 
 616:     ElfSym<E> esym;
 617:     memset(&esym, 0, sizeof(esym));
 618:     esym.st_type = STT_NOTYPE;
 619:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF;
 620:     esym.st_bind = STB_GLOBAL;
 621:     esym.st_visibility = STV_DEFAULT;
 622:     ctx.internal_esyms.push_back(esym);
 623:   };
 624: 
 625:   // Add --defsym symbols
 626:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.defsyms.size(); i++) {
 627:     Symbol<E> *sym = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].first;
 628:     std::variant<Symbol<E> *, u64> val = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].second;
 629:     add(sym);
 630: 
 631:     if (Symbol<E> **ref = std::get_if<Symbol<E> *>(&val)) {
 632:       // Add an undefined symbol to keep a reference to the defsym target.
 633:       // This prevents elimination by e.g. LTO or gc-sections.
 634:       // The undefined symbol will never make to the final object file; we
 635:       // double-check that the defsym target is not undefined in
 636:       // fix_synthetic_symbols.
 637:       add_undef(*ref);
 638:     }
 639:   }
 640: 
 641:   // Add --section-order symbols
 642:   for (SectionOrder &ord : ctx.arg.section_order)
 643:     if (ord.type == SectionOrder::SYMBOL)
 644:       add(get_symbol(ctx, ord.name));
 645: 
 646:   obj->elf_syms = ctx.internal_esyms;
 647:   obj->has_symver.resize(ctx.internal_esyms.size() - 1);
 648: }
 649: 
 650: template <typename E>
 651: static std::optional<std::string>
 652: get_start_stop_name(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> &chunk) {
 653:   if ((chunk.shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) && !chunk.name.empty()) {
 654:     if (is_c_identifier(chunk.name))
 655:       return std::string(chunk.name);
 656: 
 657:     if (ctx.arg.start_stop) {
 658:       auto isalnum = [](char c) {
 659:         return ('a' <= c && c <= 'z') || ('A' <= c && c <= 'Z') ||
 660:                ('0' <= c && c <= '9');
 661:       };
 662: 
 663:       std::string s{chunk.name};
 664:       if (s.starts_with('.'))
 665:         s = s.substr(1);
 666: 
 667:       for (i64 i = 0; i < s.size(); i++)
 668:         if (!isalnum(s[i]))
 669:           s[i] = '_';
 670:       return s;
 671:     }
 672:   }
 673: 
 674:   return {};
 675: }
 676: 
 677: template <typename E>
 678: void add_synthetic_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 679:   ObjectFile<E> &obj = *ctx.internal_obj;
 680: 
 681:   auto add = [&](std::string_view name) {
 682:     ElfSym<E> esym;
 683:     memset(&esym, 0, sizeof(esym));
 684:     esym.st_type = STT_NOTYPE;
 685:     esym.st_shndx = SHN_ABS;
 686:     esym.st_bind = STB_GLOBAL;
 687:     esym.st_visibility = STV_HIDDEN;
 688:     ctx.internal_esyms.push_back(esym);
 689: 
 690:     Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, name);
 691:     sym->value = 0xdeadbeef; // unique dummy value
 692:     obj.symbols.push_back(sym);
 693:     return sym;
 694:   };
 695: 
 696:   ctx.__ehdr_start = add("__ehdr_start");
 697:   ctx.__init_array_start = add("__init_array_start");
 698:   ctx.__init_array_end = add("__init_array_end");
 699:   ctx.__fini_array_start = add("__fini_array_start");
 700:   ctx.__fini_array_end = add("__fini_array_end");
 701:   ctx.__preinit_array_start = add("__preinit_array_start");
 702:   ctx.__preinit_array_end = add("__preinit_array_end");
 703:   ctx._DYNAMIC = add("_DYNAMIC");
 704:   ctx._GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_ = add("_GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_");
 705:   ctx._PROCEDURE_LINKAGE_TABLE_ = add("_PROCEDURE_LINKAGE_TABLE_");
 706:   ctx.__bss_start = add("__bss_start");
 707:   ctx._end = add("_end");
 708:   ctx._etext = add("_etext");
 709:   ctx._edata = add("_edata");
 710:   ctx.__executable_start = add("__executable_start");
 711: 
 712:   ctx.__rel_iplt_start =
 713:     add(E::is_rela ? "__rela_iplt_start" : "__rel_iplt_start");
 714:   ctx.__rel_iplt_end =
 715:     add(E::is_rela ? "__rela_iplt_end" : "__rel_iplt_end");
 716: 
 717:   if (ctx.arg.eh_frame_hdr)
 718:     ctx.__GNU_EH_FRAME_HDR = add("__GNU_EH_FRAME_HDR");
 719: 
 720:   if (!get_symbol(ctx, "end")->file)
 721:     ctx.end = add("end");
 722:   if (!get_symbol(ctx, "etext")->file)
 723:     ctx.etext = add("etext");
 724:   if (!get_symbol(ctx, "edata")->file)
 725:     ctx.edata = add("edata");
 726:   if (!get_symbol(ctx, "__dso_handle")->file)
 727:     ctx.__dso_handle = add("__dso_handle");
 728: 
 729:   if constexpr (supports_tlsdesc<E>)
 730:     ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_ = add("_TLS_MODULE_BASE_");
 731: 
 732:   if constexpr (is_riscv<E>)
 733:     if (!ctx.arg.shared)
 734:       ctx.__global_pointer = add("__global_pointer$");
 735: 
 736:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
 737:     ctx.__exidx_start = add("__exidx_start");
 738:     ctx.__exidx_end = add("__exidx_end");
 739:   }
 740: 
 741:   if constexpr (is_ppc64<E>)
 742:     ctx.extra.TOC = add(".TOC.");
 743: 
 744:   if constexpr (is_ppc32<E>)
 745:     ctx.extra._SDA_BASE_ = add("_SDA_BASE_");
 746: 
 747:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 748:     if (std::optional<std::string> name = get_start_stop_name(ctx, *chunk)) {
 749:       add(save_string(ctx, "__start_" + *name));
 750:       add(save_string(ctx, "__stop_" + *name));
 751: 
 752:       if (ctx.arg.physical_image_base) {
 753:         add(save_string(ctx, "__phys_start_" + *name));
 754:         add(save_string(ctx, "__phys_stop_" + *name));
 755:       }
 756:     }
 757:   }
 758: 
 759:   obj.elf_syms = ctx.internal_esyms;
 760:   obj.has_symver.resize(ctx.internal_esyms.size() - 1);
 761: 
 762:   obj.resolve_symbols(ctx);
 763: 
 764:   // Make all synthetic symbols relative ones by associating them to
 765:   // a dummy output section.
 766:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : obj.symbols)
 767:     if (sym->file == &obj)
 768:       sym->set_output_section(ctx.symtab);
 769: 
 770:   // Handle --defsym symbols.
 771:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.defsyms.size(); i++) {
 772:     Symbol<E> *sym = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].first;
 773:     std::variant<Symbol<E> *, u64> val = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].second;
 774: 
 775:     Symbol<E> *target = nullptr;
 776:     if (Symbol<E> **ref = std::get_if<Symbol<E> *>(&val))
 777:       target = *ref;
 778: 
 779:     // If the alias refers another symobl, copy ELF symbol attributes.
 780:     if (target) {
 781:       ElfSym<E> &esym = obj.elf_syms[i + 1];
 782:       esym.st_type = target->esym().st_type;
 783:       if constexpr (is_ppc64v2<E>)
 784:         esym.ppc_local_entry = target->esym().ppc_local_entry;
 785:     }
 786: 
 787:     // Make the target absolute if necessary.
 788:     if (!target || target->is_absolute())
 789:       sym->origin = 0;
 790:   }
 791: 
 792: }
 793: 
 794: template <typename E>
 795: void check_cet_errors(Context<E> &ctx) {
 796:   bool warning = (ctx.arg.z_cet_report == CET_REPORT_WARNING);
 797:   assert(warning || ctx.arg.z_cet_report == CET_REPORT_ERROR);
 798: 
 799:   auto has_feature = [](ObjectFile<E> *file, u32 feature) {
 800:     return std::any_of(file->gnu_properties.begin(), file->gnu_properties.end(),
 801:                        [&](std::pair<u32, u32> kv) {
 802:       return kv.first == GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_AND &&
 803:              (kv.second & feature);
 804:     });
 805:   };
 806: 
 807:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 808:     if (file == ctx.internal_obj)
 809:       continue;
 810: 
 811:     if (!has_feature(file, GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_IBT)) {
 812:       if (warning)
 813:         Warn(ctx) << *file << ": -cet-report=warning: "
 814:                   << "missing GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_IBT";
 815:       else
 816:         Error(ctx) << *file << ": -cet-report=error: "
 817:                    << "missing GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_IBT";
 818:     }
 819: 
 820:     if (!has_feature(file, GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_SHSTK)) {
 821:       if (warning)
 822:         Warn(ctx) << *file << ": -cet-report=warning: "
 823:                   << "missing GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_SHSTK";
 824:       else
 825:         Error(ctx) << *file << ": -cet-report=error: "
 826:                    << "missing GNU_PROPERTY_X86_FEATURE_1_SHSTK";
 827:     }
 828:   }
 829: }
 830: 
 831: template <typename E>
 832: void print_dependencies(Context<E> &ctx) {
 833:   SyncOut(ctx) <<
 834: R"(# This is an output of the mold linker's --print-dependencies option.
 835: #
 836: # Each line consists of 4 fields, <section1>, <section2>, <symbol-type> and
 837: # <symbol>, separated by tab characters. It indicates that <section1> depends
 838: # on <section2> to use <symbol>. <symbol-type> is either "u" or "w" for
 839: # regular undefined or weak undefined, respectively.
 840: #
 841: # If you want to obtain dependency information per function granularity,
 842: # compile source files with the -ffunction-sections compiler flag.
 843: )";
 844: 
 845:   auto println = [&](auto &src, Symbol<E> &sym, ElfSym<E> &esym) {
 846:     if (InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section())
 847:       SyncOut(ctx) << src << "\t" << *isec
 848:                    << "\t" << (esym.is_weak() ? 'w' : 'u')
 849:                    << "\t" << sym;
 850:     else
 851:       SyncOut(ctx) << src << "\t" << *sym.file
 852:                    << "\t" << (esym.is_weak() ? 'w' : 'u')
 853:                    << "\t" << sym;
 854:   };
 855: 
 856:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
 857:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
 858:       if (!isec)
 859:         continue;
 860: 
 861:       std::unordered_set<void *> visited;
 862: 
 863:       for (const ElfRel<E> &r : isec->get_rels(ctx)) {
 864:         if (r.r_type == R_NONE)
 865:           continue;
 866: 
 867:         ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[r.r_sym];
 868:         Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[r.r_sym];
 869: 
 870:         if (esym.is_undef() && sym.file && sym.file != file &&
 871:             visited.insert((void *)&sym).second)
 872:           println(*isec, sym, esym);
 873:       }
 874:     }
 875:   }
 876: 
 877:   for (SharedFile<E> *file : ctx.dsos) {
 878:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->symbols.size(); i++) {
 879:       ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[i];
 880:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
 881:       if (esym.is_undef() && sym.file && sym.file != file)
 882:         println(*file, sym, esym);
 883:     }
 884:   }
 885: }
 886: 
 887: template <typename E>
 888: static std::string create_response_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 889:   std::string buf;
 890:   std::stringstream out;
 891: 
 892:   std::string cwd = std::filesystem::current_path().string();
 893:   out << "-C " << cwd.substr(1) << "\n";
 894: 
 895:   if (cwd != "/") {
 896:     out << "--chroot ..";
 897:     i64 depth = std::count(cwd.begin(), cwd.end(), '/');
 898:     for (i64 i = 1; i < depth; i++)
 899:       out << "/..";
 900:     out << "\n";
 901:   }
 902: 
 903:   for (i64 i = 1; i < ctx.cmdline_args.size(); i++) {
 904:     std::string_view arg = ctx.cmdline_args[i];
 905:     if (arg != "-repro" && arg != "--repro")
 906:       out << arg << "\n";
 907:   }
 908:   return out.str();
 909: }
 910: 
 911: template <typename E>
 912: void write_repro_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 913:   std::string path = ctx.arg.output + ".repro.tar";
 914: 
 915:   std::unique_ptr<TarWriter> tar =
 916:     TarWriter::open(path, filepath(ctx.arg.output).filename().string() + ".repro");
 917:   if (!tar)
 918:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << path << ": " << errno_string();
 919: 
 920:   tar->append("response.txt", save_string(ctx, create_response_file(ctx)));
 921:   tar->append("version.txt", save_string(ctx, mold_version + "\n"));
 922: 
 923:   std::unordered_set<std::string> seen;
 924:   for (std::unique_ptr<MappedFile<Context<E>>> &mf : ctx.mf_pool) {
 925:     if (!mf->parent) {
 926:       std::string path = to_abs_path(mf->name).string();
 927:       if (seen.insert(path).second) {
 928:         // We reopen a file because we may have modified the contents of mf
 929:         // in memory, which is mapped with PROT_WRITE and MAP_PRIVATE.
 930:         MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf2 = MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, path);
 931:         tar->append(path, mf2->get_contents());
 932:         mf2->unmap();
 933:       }
 934:     }
 935:   }
 936: }
 937: 
 938: template <typename E>
 939: void check_duplicate_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 940:   Timer t(ctx, "check_duplicate_symbols");
 941: 
 942:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 943:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 944:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[i];
 945:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
 946: 
 947:       // Skip if our symbol is undef or weak
 948:       if (sym.file == file || sym.file == ctx.internal_obj ||
 949:           esym.is_undef() || esym.is_common() || (esym.st_bind == STB_WEAK))
 950:         continue;
 951: 
 952:       // Skip if our symbol is in a dead section. In most cases, the
 953:       // section has been eliminated due to comdat deduplication.
 954:       if (!esym.is_abs()) {
 955:         InputSection<E> *isec = file->get_section(esym);
 956:         if (!isec || !isec->is_alive)
 957:           continue;
 958:       }
 959: 
 960:       Error(ctx) << "duplicate symbol: " << *file << ": " << *sym.file
 961:                  << ": " << sym;
 962:     }
 963:   });
 964: 
 965:   ctx.checkpoint();
 966: }
 967: 
 968: template <typename E>
 969: void check_symbol_types(Context<E> &ctx) {
 970:   Timer t(ctx, "check_symbol_types");
 971: 
 972:   auto check = [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
 973:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
 974:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[i];
 975:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
 976: 
 977:       if (!sym.file)
 978:         continue;
 979: 
 980:       u32 x = sym.esym().st_type;
 981:       if (x == STT_GNU_IFUNC)
 982:         x = STT_FUNC;
 983: 
 984:       u32 y = esym.st_type;
 985:       if (y == STT_GNU_IFUNC)
 986:         y = STT_FUNC;
 987: 
 988:       if (x != STT_NOTYPE && y != STT_NOTYPE && x != y)
 989:         Warn(ctx) << "symbol type mismatch: " << sym << '\n'
 990:                   << ">>> defined in " << *sym.file << " as "
 991:                   << stt_to_string<E>(sym.esym().st_type) << '\n'
 992:                   << ">>> defined in " << *file << " as "
 993:                   << stt_to_string<E>(esym.st_type);
 994:     }
 995:   };
 996: 
 997:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, check);
 998:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, check);
 999: }
1000: 
1001: template <typename E>
1002: void sort_init_fini(Context<E> &ctx) {
1003:   Timer t(ctx, "sort_init_fini");
1004: 
1005:   auto get_priority = [](InputSection<E> *isec) {
1006:     static std::regex re(R"(\.(\d+)$)", std::regex_constants::optimize);
1007:     std::string_view name = isec->name();
1008:     std::cmatch m;
1009:     if (std::regex_search(name.data(), name.data() + name.size(), m, re))
1010:       return std::stoi(m[1]);
1011:     return 65536;
1012:   };
1013: 
1014:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
1015:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec()) {
1016:       if (osec->name == ".init_array" || osec->name == ".preinit_array" ||
1017:           osec->name == ".fini_array") {
1018:         if (ctx.arg.shuffle_sections == SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_REVERSE)
1019:           std::reverse(osec->members.begin(), osec->members.end());
1020: 
1021:         std::unordered_map<InputSection<E> *, i64> map;
1022:         for (InputSection<E> *isec : osec->members)
1023:           map.insert({isec, get_priority(isec)});
1024: 
1025:         sort(osec->members, [&](InputSection<E> *a, InputSection<E> *b) {
1026:           return map[a] < map[b];
1027:         });
1028:       }
1029:     }
1030:   }
1031: }
1032: 
1033: template <typename E>
1034: void sort_ctor_dtor(Context<E> &ctx) {
1035:   Timer t(ctx, "sort_ctor_dtor");
1036: 
1037:   auto get_priority = [](InputSection<E> *isec) {
1038:     auto opts = std::regex_constants::optimize | std::regex_constants::ECMAScript;
1039:     static std::regex re1(R"((?:clang_rt\.)?crtbegin)", opts);
1040:     static std::regex re2(R"((?:clang_rt\.)?crtend)", opts);
1041:     static std::regex re3(R"(\.(\d+)$)", opts);
1042: 
1043:     // crtbegin.o and crtend.o contain marker symbols such as
1044:     // __CTOR_LIST__ or __DTOR_LIST__. So they have to be at the
1045:     // beginning or end of the section.
1046:     std::smatch m;
1047:     if (std::regex_search(isec->file.filename, m, re1))
1048:       return -2;
1049:     if (std::regex_search(isec->file.filename, m, re2))
1050:       return 65536;
1051: 
1052:     std::string name(isec->name());
1053:     if (std::regex_search(name, m, re3))
1054:       return std::stoi(m[1]);
1055:     return -1;
1056:   };
1057: 
1058:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
1059:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec()) {
1060:       if (osec->name == ".ctors" || osec->name == ".dtors") {
1061:         if (ctx.arg.shuffle_sections != SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_REVERSE)
1062:           std::reverse(osec->members.begin(), osec->members.end());
1063: 
1064:         std::unordered_map<InputSection<E> *, i64> map;
1065:         for (InputSection<E> *isec : osec->members)
1066:           map.insert({isec, get_priority(isec)});
1067: 
1068:         sort(osec->members, [&](InputSection<E> *a, InputSection<E> *b) {
1069:           return map[a] < map[b];
1070:         });
1071:       }
1072:     }
1073:   }
1074: }
1075: 
1076: template <typename T>
1077: static void shuffle(std::vector<T> &vec, u64 seed) {
1078:   if (vec.empty())
1079:     return;
1080: 
1081:   // Xorshift random number generator. We use this RNG because it is
1082:   // measurably faster than MT19937.
1083:   auto rand = [&] {
1084:     seed ^= seed << 13;
1085:     seed ^= seed >> 7;
1086:     seed ^= seed << 17;
1087:     return seed;
1088:   };
1089: 
1090:   // The Fisher-Yates shuffling algorithm.
1091:   //
1092:   // We don't want to use std::shuffle for build reproducibility. That is,
1093:   // std::shuffle's implementation is not guaranteed to be the same across
1094:   // platform, so even though the result is guaranteed to be randomly
1095:   // shuffled, the exact order may be different across implementations.
1096:   //
1097:   // We are not using std::uniform_int_distribution for the same reason.
1098:   for (i64 i = 0; i < vec.size() - 1; i++)
1099:     std::swap(vec[i], vec[i + rand() % (vec.size() - i)]);
1100: }
1101: 
1102: template <typename E>
1103: void shuffle_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
1104:   Timer t(ctx, "shuffle_sections");
1105: 
1106:   auto is_eligible = [](OutputSection<E> &osec) {
1107:     return osec.name != ".init" && osec.name != ".fini" &&
1108:            osec.name != ".ctors" && osec.name != ".dtors" &&
1109:            osec.name != ".init_array" && osec.name != ".preinit_array" &&
1110:            osec.name != ".fini_array";
1111:   };
1112: 
1113:   switch (ctx.arg.shuffle_sections) {
1114:   case SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_NONE:
1115:     unreachable();
1116:   case SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_SHUFFLE: {
1117:     u64 seed;
1118:     if (ctx.arg.shuffle_sections_seed)
1119:       seed = *ctx.arg.shuffle_sections_seed;
1120:     else
1121:       seed = ((u64)std::random_device()() << 32) | std::random_device()();
1122: 
1123:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1124:       if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
1125:         if (is_eligible(*osec))
1126:           shuffle(osec->members, seed + hash_string(osec->name));
1127:     });
1128:     break;
1129:   }
1130:   case SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_REVERSE:
1131:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1132:       if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
1133:         if (is_eligible(*osec))
1134:           std::reverse(osec->members.begin(), osec->members.end());
1135:     });
1136:     break;
1137:   }
1138: }
1139: 
1140: template <typename E>
1141: void compute_section_sizes(Context<E> &ctx) {
1142:   Timer t(ctx, "compute_section_sizes");
1143: 
1144:   struct Group {
1145:     i64 size = 0;
1146:     i64 p2align = 0;
1147:     i64 offset = 0;
1148:     std::span<InputSection<E> *> members;
1149:   };
1150: 
1151:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1152:     OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec();
1153:     if (!osec)
1154:       return;
1155: 
1156:     // This pattern will be processed in the next loop.
1157:     if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>)
1158:       if ((osec->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) && !ctx.arg.relocatable)
1159:         return;
1160: 
1161:     // Since one output section may contain millions of input sections,
1162:     // we first split input sections into groups and assign offsets to
1163:     // groups.
1164:     std::vector<Group> groups;
1165:     constexpr i64 group_size = 10000;
1166: 
1167:     for (std::span<InputSection<E> *> span : split(osec->members, group_size))
1168:       groups.push_back(Group{.members = span});
1169: 
1170:     tbb::parallel_for_each(groups, [](Group &group) {
1171:       for (InputSection<E> *isec : group.members) {
1172:         group.size = align_to(group.size, 1 << isec->p2align) + isec->sh_size;
1173:         group.p2align = std::max<i64>(group.p2align, isec->p2align);
1174:       }
1175:     });
1176: 
1177:     i64 offset = 0;
1178:     i64 p2align = 0;
1179: 
1180:     for (i64 i = 0; i < groups.size(); i++) {
1181:       offset = align_to(offset, 1 << groups[i].p2align);
1182:       groups[i].offset = offset;
1183:       offset += groups[i].size;
1184:       p2align = std::max(p2align, groups[i].p2align);
1185:     }
1186: 
1187:     osec->shdr.sh_size = offset;
1188:     osec->shdr.sh_addralign = 1 << p2align;
1189: 
1190:     // Assign offsets to input sections.
1191:     tbb::parallel_for_each(groups, [](Group &group) {
1192:       i64 offset = group.offset;
1193:       for (InputSection<E> *isec : group.members) {
1194:         offset = align_to(offset, 1 << isec->p2align);
1195:         isec->offset = offset;
1196:         offset += isec->sh_size;
1197:       }
1198:     });
1199:   });
1200: 
1201:   // On ARM32 or ARM64, we may need to create so-called "range extension
1202:   // thunks" to extend branch instructions reach, as they can jump only
1203:   // to ±16 MiB or ±128 MiB, respecitvely.
1204:   //
1205:   // In the following loop, We compute the sizes of sections while
1206:   // inserting thunks. This pass cannot be parallelized. That is,
1207:   // create_range_extension_thunks is parallelized internally, but the
1208:   // function itself is not thread-safe.
1209:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
1210:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
1211:       OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec();
1212:       if (osec && (osec->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) && !ctx.arg.relocatable) {
1213:         create_range_extension_thunks(ctx, *osec);
1214: 
1215:         for (InputSection<E> *isec : osec->members)
1216:           osec->shdr.sh_addralign =
1217:             std::max<u32>(osec->shdr.sh_addralign, 1 << isec->p2align);
1218:       }
1219:     }
1220:   }
1221: 
1222:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
1223:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
1224:       if (u32 align = ctx.arg.section_align[osec->name])
1225:         osec->shdr.sh_addralign = std::max<u32>(osec->shdr.sh_addralign, align);
1226: }
1227: 
1228: // Find all unresolved symbols and attach them to the most appropriate files.
1229: // Note that even a symbol that will be reported as an undefined symbol will
1230: // get an owner file in this function. Such symbol will be reported by
1231: // ObjectFile<E>::scan_relocations().
1232: template <typename E>
1233: void claim_unresolved_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1234:   Timer t(ctx, "claim_unresolved_symbols");
1235: 
1236:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1237:     if (!file->is_alive)
1238:       return;
1239: 
1240:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1241:       const ElfSym<E> &esym = file->elf_syms[i];
1242:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->symbols[i];
1243:       if (!esym.is_undef())
1244:         continue;
1245: 
1246:       std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
1247: 
1248:       if (sym.file)
1249:         if (!sym.esym().is_undef() || sym.file->priority <= file->priority)
1250:           continue;
1251: 
1252:       // If a symbol name is in the form of "foo@version", search for
1253:       // symbol "foo" and check if the symbol has version "version".
1254:       if (file->has_symver.get(i - file->first_global)) {
1255:         std::string_view str = file->symbol_strtab.data() + esym.st_name;
1256:         i64 pos = str.find('@');
1257:         assert(pos != str.npos);
1258: 
1259:         std::string_view name = str.substr(0, pos);
1260:         std::string_view ver = str.substr(pos + 1);
1261: 
1262:         Symbol<E> *sym2 = get_symbol(ctx, name);
1263:         if (sym2->file && sym2->file->is_dso && sym2->get_version() == ver) {
1264:           file->symbols[i] = sym2;
1265:           continue;
1266:         }
1267:       }
1268: 
1269:       auto claim = [&](bool is_imported) {
1270:         if (sym.is_traced)
1271:           SyncOut(ctx) << "trace-symbol: " << *file << ": unresolved"
1272:                        << (esym.is_weak() ? " weak" : "")
1273:                        << " symbol " << sym;
1274: 
1275:         sym.file = file;
1276:         sym.origin = 0;
1277:         sym.value = 0;
1278:         sym.sym_idx = i;
1279:         sym.is_weak = false;
1280:         sym.is_imported = is_imported;
1281:         sym.is_exported = false;
1282:         sym.ver_idx = is_imported ? 0 : ctx.default_version;
1283:       };
1284: 
1285:       if (esym.is_undef_weak()) {
1286:         if (ctx.arg.shared && sym.visibility != STV_HIDDEN &&
1287:             ctx.arg.z_dynamic_undefined_weak) {
1288:           // Global weak undefined symbols are promoted to dynamic symbols
1289:           // when linking a DSO unless `-z nodynamic_undefined_weak` was given.
1290:           claim(true);
1291:         } else {
1292:           // Otherwise, weak undefs are converted to absolute symbols with value 0.
1293:           claim(false);
1294:         }
1295:         continue;
1296:       }
1297: 
1298:       // Traditionally, remaining undefined symbols cause a link failure
1299:       // only when we are creating an executable. Undefined symbols in
1300:       // shared objects are promoted to dynamic symbols, so that they'll
1301:       // get another chance to be resolved at run-time. You can change the
1302:       // behavior by passing `-z defs` to the linker.
1303:       //
1304:       // Even if `-z defs` is given, weak undefined symbols are still
1305:       // promoted to dynamic symbols for compatibility with other linkers.
1306:       // Some major programs, notably Firefox, depend on the behavior
1307:       // (they use this loophole to export symbols from libxul.so).
1308:       if (ctx.arg.shared && sym.visibility != STV_HIDDEN && !ctx.arg.z_defs) {
1309:         claim(true);
1310:         continue;
1311:       }
1312: 
1313:       // Convert remaining undefined symbols to absolute symbols with value 0.
1314:       claim(false);
1315:     }
1316:   });
1317: }
1318: 
1319: template <typename E>
1320: void scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
1321:   Timer t(ctx, "scan_relocations");
1322: 
1323:   // Scan relocations to find dynamic symbols.
1324:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1325:     file->scan_relocations(ctx);
1326:   });
1327: 
1328:   // Exit if there was a relocation that refers an undefined symbol.
1329:   ctx.checkpoint();
1330: 
1331:   // Aggregate dynamic symbols to a single vector.
1332:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> files;
1333:   append(files, ctx.objs);
1334:   append(files, ctx.dsos);
1335: 
1336:   std::vector<std::vector<Symbol<E> *>> vec(files.size());
1337: 
1338:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)files.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1339:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : files[i]->symbols)
1340:       if (sym->file == files[i])
1341:         if (sym->flags || sym->is_imported || sym->is_exported)
1342:           vec[i].push_back(sym);
1343:   });
1344: 
1345:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> syms = flatten(vec);
1346:   ctx.symbol_aux.reserve(syms.size());
1347: 
1348:   // Assign offsets in additional tables for each dynamic symbol.
1349:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : syms) {
1350:     sym->add_aux(ctx);
1351: 
1352:     if (sym->is_imported || sym->is_exported)
1353:       ctx.dynsym->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1354: 
1355:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_GOT)
1356:       ctx.got->add_got_symbol(ctx, sym);
1357: 
1358:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_CPLT) {
1359:       sym->is_canonical = true;
1360: 
1361:       // A canonical PLT needs to be visible from DSOs.
1362:       sym->is_exported = true;
1363: 
1364:       // We can't use .plt.got for a canonical PLT because otherwise
1365:       // .plt.got and .got would refer to each other, resulting in an
1366:       // infinite loop at runtime.
1367:       ctx.plt->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1368:     } else if (sym->flags & NEEDS_PLT) {
1369:       if (sym->flags & NEEDS_GOT)
1370:         ctx.pltgot->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1371:       else
1372:         ctx.plt->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1373:     }
1374: 
1375:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_GOTTP)
1376:       ctx.got->add_gottp_symbol(ctx, sym);
1377: 
1378:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_TLSGD)
1379:       ctx.got->add_tlsgd_symbol(ctx, sym);
1380: 
1381:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_TLSDESC)
1382:       ctx.got->add_tlsdesc_symbol(ctx, sym);
1383: 
1384:     if (sym->flags & NEEDS_COPYREL) {
1385:       if (((SharedFile<E> *)sym->file)->is_readonly(sym))
1386:         ctx.copyrel_relro->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1387:       else
1388:         ctx.copyrel->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1389:     }
1390: 
1391:     if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
1392:       if (sym->flags & NEEDS_PPC_OPD)
1393:         ctx.extra.opd->add_symbol(ctx, sym);
1394: 
1395:     sym->flags = 0;
1396:   }
1397: 
1398:   if (ctx.needs_tlsld)
1399:     ctx.got->add_tlsld(ctx);
1400: 
1401:   if constexpr (is_alpha<E>)
1402:     ctx.extra.got->finalize();
1403: 
1404:   if (ctx.has_textrel && ctx.arg.warn_textrel)
1405:     Warn(ctx) << "creating a DT_TEXTREL in an output file";
1406: }
1407: 
1408: // Report all undefined symbols, grouped by symbol.
1409: template <typename E>
1410: void report_undef_errors(Context<E> &ctx) {
1411:   constexpr i64 max_errors = 3;
1412: 
1413:   for (auto &pair : ctx.undef_errors) {
1414:     std::string_view sym_name = pair.first;
1415:     std::span<std::string> errors = pair.second;
1416: 
1417:     if (ctx.arg.demangle)
1418:       sym_name = demangle(sym_name);
1419: 
1420:     std::stringstream ss;
1421:     ss << "undefined symbol: " << sym_name << "\n";
1422: 
1423:     for (i64 i = 0; i < errors.size() && i < max_errors; i++)
1424:       ss << errors[i];
1425: 
1426:     if (errors.size() > max_errors)
1427:       ss << ">>> referenced " << (errors.size() - max_errors) << " more times\n";
1428: 
1429:     if (ctx.arg.unresolved_symbols == UNRESOLVED_ERROR)
1430:       Error(ctx) << ss.str();
1431:     else if (ctx.arg.unresolved_symbols == UNRESOLVED_WARN)
1432:       Warn(ctx) << ss.str();
1433:   }
1434: 
1435:   ctx.checkpoint();
1436: }
1437: 
1438: template <typename E>
1439: void create_reloc_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
1440:   Timer t(ctx, "create_reloc_sections");
1441: 
1442:   // Create .rela.* sections
1443:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.chunks.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1444:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = ctx.chunks[i]->to_osec())
1445:       osec->reloc_sec.reset(new RelocSection<E>(ctx, *osec));
1446:   });
1447: 
1448:   for (i64 i = 0, end = ctx.chunks.size(); i < end; i++)
1449:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = ctx.chunks[i]->to_osec())
1450:       if (RelocSection<E> *x = osec->reloc_sec.get())
1451:         ctx.chunks.push_back(x);
1452: }
1453: 
1454: // Copy chunks to an output file
1455: template <typename E>
1456: void copy_chunks(Context<E> &ctx) {
1457:   Timer t(ctx, "copy_chunks");
1458: 
1459:   auto copy = [&](Chunk<E> &chunk) {
1460:     std::string name = chunk.name.empty() ? "(header)" : std::string(chunk.name);
1461:     Timer t2(ctx, name, &t);
1462:     chunk.copy_buf(ctx);
1463:   };
1464: 
1465:   // For --relocatable and --emit-relocs, we want to copy non-relocation
1466:   // sections first. This is because REL-type relocation sections (as
1467:   // opposed to RELA-type) stores relocation addends to target sections.
1468:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1469:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_type != (E::is_rela ? SHT_RELA : SHT_REL))
1470:       copy(*chunk);
1471:   });
1472: 
1473:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1474:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_type == (E::is_rela ? SHT_RELA : SHT_REL))
1475:       copy(*chunk);
1476:   });
1477: 
1478:   // Undefined symbols in SHF_ALLOC sections are found by scan_relocations(),
1479:   // but those in non-SHF_ALLOC sections cannot be found until we copy section
1480:   // contents. So we need to call this function again to report possible
1481:   // undefined errors.
1482:   report_undef_errors(ctx);
1483: 
1484:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>)
1485:     fixup_arm_exidx_section(ctx);
1486: }
1487: 
1488: template <typename E>
1489: void construct_relr(Context<E> &ctx) {
1490:   Timer t(ctx, "construct_relr");
1491: 
1492:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1493:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
1494:       osec->construct_relr(ctx);
1495:   });
1496: 
1497:   ctx.got->construct_relr(ctx);
1498: }
1499: 
1500: template <typename E>
1501: void create_output_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1502:   Timer t(ctx, "compute_symtab_size");
1503: 
1504:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1505:     chunk->compute_symtab_size(ctx);
1506:   });
1507: 
1508:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1509:     file->compute_symtab_size(ctx);
1510:   });
1511: 
1512:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, [&](SharedFile<E> *file) {
1513:     file->compute_symtab_size(ctx);
1514:   });
1515: }
1516: 
1517: template <typename E>
1518: void apply_version_script(Context<E> &ctx) {
1519:   Timer t(ctx, "apply_version_script");
1520: 
1521:   // If all patterns are simple (i.e. not containing any meta-
1522:   // characters and is not a C++ name), we can simply look up
1523:   // symbols.
1524:   auto is_simple = [&] {
1525:     for (VersionPattern &v : ctx.version_patterns)
1526:       if (v.is_cpp || v.pattern.find_first_of("*?[") != v.pattern.npos)
1527:         return false;
1528:     return true;
1529:   };
1530: 
1531:   if (is_simple()) {
1532:     for (VersionPattern &v : ctx.version_patterns) {
1533:       Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, v.pattern);
1534: 
1535:       if (!sym->file && !ctx.arg.undefined_version)
1536:         Warn(ctx) << v.source << ": cannot assign version `" << v.ver_str
1537:                   << "` to symbol `" << *sym << "`: symbol not found";
1538: 
1539:       if (sym->file && !sym->file->is_dso)
1540:         sym->ver_idx = v.ver_idx;
1541:     }
1542:     return;
1543:   }
1544: 
1545:   // Otherwise, use glob pattern matchers.
1546:   MultiGlob matcher;
1547:   MultiGlob cpp_matcher;
1548: 
1549:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.version_patterns.size(); i++) {
1550:     VersionPattern &v = ctx.version_patterns[i];
1551:     if (v.is_cpp) {
1552:       if (!cpp_matcher.add(v.pattern, i))
1553:         Fatal(ctx) << "invalid version pattern: " << v.pattern;
1554:     } else {
1555:       if (!matcher.add(v.pattern, i))
1556:         Fatal(ctx) << "invalid version pattern: " << v.pattern;
1557:     }
1558:   }
1559: 
1560:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1561:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->get_global_syms()) {
1562:       if (sym->file != file)
1563:         continue;
1564: 
1565:       std::string_view name = sym->name();
1566:       i64 match = INT64_MAX;
1567: 
1568:       if (std::optional<u32> idx = matcher.find(name))
1569:         match = std::min<i64>(match, *idx);
1570: 
1571:       // Match non-mangled symbols against the C++ pattern as well.
1572:       // Weird, but required to match other linkers' behavior.
1573:       if (!cpp_matcher.empty()) {
1574:         if (std::optional<std::string_view> s = cpp_demangle(name))
1575:           name = *s;
1576:         if (std::optional<u32> idx = cpp_matcher.find(name))
1577:           match = std::min<i64>(match, *idx);
1578:       }
1579: 
1580:       if (match != INT64_MAX)
1581:         sym->ver_idx = ctx.version_patterns[match].ver_idx;
1582:     }
1583:   });
1584: }
1585: 
1586: template <typename E>
1587: void parse_symbol_version(Context<E> &ctx) {
1588:   if (!ctx.arg.shared)
1589:     return;
1590: 
1591:   Timer t(ctx, "parse_symbol_version");
1592: 
1593:   std::unordered_map<std::string_view, u16> verdefs;
1594:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.version_definitions.size(); i++)
1595:     verdefs[ctx.arg.version_definitions[i]] = i + VER_NDX_LAST_RESERVED + 1;
1596: 
1597:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1598:     for (i64 i = file->first_global; i < file->elf_syms.size(); i++) {
1599:       // Match VERSION part of symbol foo@VERSION with version definitions.
1600:       if (!file->has_symver.get(i - file->first_global))
1601:         continue;
1602: 
1603:       Symbol<E> *sym = file->symbols[i];
1604:       if (sym->file != file)
1605:         continue;
1606: 
1607:       const char *name = file->symbol_strtab.data() + file->elf_syms[i].st_name;
1608:       std::string_view ver = strchr(name, '@') + 1;
1609: 
1610:       bool is_default = false;
1611:       if (ver.starts_with('@')) {
1612:         is_default = true;
1613:         ver = ver.substr(1);
1614:       }
1615: 
1616:       auto it = verdefs.find(ver);
1617:       if (it == verdefs.end()) {
1618:         Error(ctx) << *file << ": symbol " << *sym <<  " has undefined version "
1619:                    << ver;
1620:         continue;
1621:       }
1622: 
1623:       sym->ver_idx = it->second;
1624:       if (!is_default)
1625:         sym->ver_idx |= VERSYM_HIDDEN;
1626: 
1627:       // If both symbol `foo` and `foo@VERSION` are defined, `foo@VERSION`
1628:       // hides `foo` so that all references to `foo` are resolved to a
1629:       // versioned symbol. Likewise, if `foo@VERSION` and `foo@@VERSION` are
1630:       // defined, the default one takes precedence.
1631:       Symbol<E> *sym2 = get_symbol(ctx, sym->name());
1632:       if (sym2->file == file &&
1633:           !file->has_symver.get(sym2->sym_idx - file->first_global))
1634:         if (sym2->ver_idx == ctx.default_version ||
1635:             (sym2->ver_idx & ~VERSYM_HIDDEN) == (sym->ver_idx & ~VERSYM_HIDDEN))
1636:           sym2->ver_idx = VER_NDX_LOCAL;
1637:     }
1638:   });
1639: }
1640: 
1641: template <typename E>
1642: void compute_import_export(Context<E> &ctx) {
1643:   Timer t(ctx, "compute_import_export");
1644: 
1645:   // If we are creating an executable, we want to export symbols referenced
1646:   // by DSOs unless they are explicitly marked as local by a version script.
1647:   if (!ctx.arg.shared) {
1648:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dsos, [&](SharedFile<E> *file) {
1649:       for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->symbols) {
1650:         if (sym->file && !sym->file->is_dso && sym->visibility != STV_HIDDEN) {
1651:           if (sym->ver_idx != VER_NDX_LOCAL ||
1652:               !ctx.default_version_from_version_script) {
1653:             std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
1654:             sym->is_exported = true;
1655:           }
1656:         }
1657:       }
1658:     });
1659:   }
1660: 
1661:   // Export symbols that are not hidden or marked as local.
1662:   // We also want to mark imported symbols as such.
1663:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1664:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->get_global_syms()) {
1665:       if (!sym->file || sym->visibility == STV_HIDDEN ||
1666:           sym->ver_idx == VER_NDX_LOCAL)
1667:         continue;
1668: 
1669:       // If we are using a symbol in a DSO, we need to import it at runtime.
1670:       if (sym->file != file && sym->file->is_dso && !sym->is_absolute()) {
1671:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
1672:         sym->is_imported = true;
1673:         continue;
1674:       }
1675: 
1676:       // If we are creating a DSO, all global symbols are exported by default.
1677:       if (sym->file == file) {
1678:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
1679:         sym->is_exported = true;
1680: 
1681:         if (ctx.arg.shared && sym->visibility != STV_PROTECTED &&
1682:             !ctx.arg.Bsymbolic &&
1683:             !(ctx.arg.Bsymbolic_functions && sym->get_type() == STT_FUNC))
1684:           sym->is_imported = true;
1685:       }
1686:     }
1687:   });
1688: }
1689: 
1690: template <typename E>
1691: void mark_addrsig(Context<E> &ctx) {
1692:   Timer t(ctx, "mark_addrsig");
1693: 
1694:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
1695:     file->mark_addrsig(ctx);
1696:   });
1697: }
1698: 
1699: template <typename E>
1700: void clear_padding(Context<E> &ctx) {
1701:   Timer t(ctx, "clear_padding");
1702: 
1703:   auto zero = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk, i64 next_start) {
1704:     i64 pos = chunk->shdr.sh_offset + chunk->shdr.sh_size;
1705:     memset(ctx.buf + pos, 0, next_start - pos);
1706:   };
1707: 
1708:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> chunks = ctx.chunks;
1709: 
1710:   std::erase_if(chunks, [](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1711:     return chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS;
1712:   });
1713: 
1714:   for (i64 i = 1; i < chunks.size(); i++)
1715:     zero(chunks[i - 1], chunks[i]->shdr.sh_offset);
1716:   zero(chunks.back(), ctx.output_file->filesize);
1717: }
1718: 
1719: // We want to sort output chunks in the following order.
1720: //
1721: //   <ELF header>
1722: //   <program header>
1723: //   .interp
1724: //   .note
1725: //   .hash
1726: //   .gnu.hash
1727: //   .dynsym
1728: //   .dynstr
1729: //   .gnu.version
1730: //   .gnu.version_r
1731: //   .rela.dyn
1732: //   .rela.plt
1733: //   <readonly data>
1734: //   <readonly code>
1735: //   <writable tdata>
1736: //   <writable tbss>
1737: //   <writable RELRO data>
1738: //   .got
1739: //   .toc
1740: //   .alpha_got
1741: //   <writable RELRO bss>
1742: //   .relro_padding
1743: //   <writable non-RELRO data>
1744: //   <writable non-RELRO bss>
1745: //   <non-memory-allocated sections>
1746: //   <section header>
1747: //
1748: // .interp and some other linker-synthesized sections are placed at the
1749: // beginning of a file because they are needed by loader. Especially on
1750: // a hard drive with spinning disks, it is important to read these
1751: // sections in a single seek.
1752: //
1753: // .note sections are also placed at the beginning so that they are
1754: // included in a core crash dump even if it's truncated by ulimit. In
1755: // particular, if .note.gnu.build-id is in a truncated core file, you
1756: // can at least identify which executable has crashed.
1757: //
1758: // A PT_NOTE segment will contain multiple .note sections if exists,
1759: // but there's no way to represent a gap between .note sections.
1760: // Therefore, we sort .note sections by decreasing alignment
1761: // requirement. I believe each .note section size is a multiple of its
1762: // alignment, so by sorting them by alignment, we should be able to
1763: // avoid a gap between .note sections.
1764: //
1765: // .toc is placed right after .got for PPC64. PPC-specific .toc section
1766: // contains data that may be accessed with a 16-bit offset relative to
1767: // %r2. %r2 is set to .got + 32 KiB. Therefore, .toc needs to be within
1768: // [.got, .got + 64 KiB).
1769: //
1770: // Other file layouts are possible, but this layout is chosen to keep
1771: // the number of segments as few as possible.
1772: template <typename E>
1773: void sort_output_sections_regular(Context<E> &ctx) {
1774:   auto get_rank1 = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1775:     u64 type = chunk->shdr.sh_type;
1776:     u64 flags = chunk->shdr.sh_flags;
1777: 
1778:     if (chunk == ctx.ehdr)
1779:       return 0;
1780:     if (chunk == ctx.phdr)
1781:       return 1;
1782:     if (chunk == ctx.interp)
1783:       return 2;
1784:     if (type == SHT_NOTE && (flags & SHF_ALLOC))
1785:       return 3;
1786:     if (chunk == ctx.hash)
1787:       return 4;
1788:     if (chunk == ctx.gnu_hash)
1789:       return 5;
1790:     if (chunk == ctx.dynsym)
1791:       return 6;
1792:     if (chunk == ctx.dynstr)
1793:       return 7;
1794:     if (chunk == ctx.versym)
1795:       return 8;
1796:     if (chunk == ctx.verneed)
1797:       return 9;
1798:     if (chunk == ctx.reldyn)
1799:       return 10;
1800:     if (chunk == ctx.relplt)
1801:       return 11;
1802:     if (chunk == ctx.shdr)
1803:       return INT32_MAX;
1804: 
1805:     bool alloc = (flags & SHF_ALLOC);
1806:     bool writable = (flags & SHF_WRITE);
1807:     bool exec = (flags & SHF_EXECINSTR);
1808:     bool tls = (flags & SHF_TLS);
1809:     bool relro = is_relro(ctx, chunk);
1810:     bool is_bss = (type == SHT_NOBITS);
1811: 
1812:     return (1 << 10) | (!alloc << 9) | (writable << 8) | (exec << 7) |
1813:            (!tls << 6) | (!relro << 5) | (is_bss << 4);
1814:   };
1815: 
1816:   auto get_rank2 = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) -> i64 {
1817:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOTE)
1818:       return -chunk->shdr.sh_addralign;
1819: 
1820:     if (chunk == ctx.got)
1821:       return 1;
1822:     if (chunk->name == ".toc")
1823:       return 2;
1824:     if (chunk->name == ".alpha_got")
1825:       return 3;
1826:     if (chunk == ctx.relro_padding)
1827:       return INT_MAX;
1828:     return 0;
1829:   };
1830: 
1831:   sort(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *a, Chunk<E> *b) {
1832:     // Sort sections by segments
1833:     i64 x = get_rank1(a);
1834:     i64 y = get_rank1(b);
1835:     if (x != y)
1836:       return x < y;
1837: 
1838:     // Ties are broken by additional rules
1839:     return get_rank2(a) < get_rank2(b);
1840:   });
1841: }
1842: 
1843: template <typename E>
1844: static std::string_view get_section_order_group(Chunk<E> &chunk) {
1845:   if (chunk.shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS)
1846:     return "BSS";
1847:   if (chunk.shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR)
1848:     return "TEXT";
1849:   if (chunk.shdr.sh_flags & SHF_WRITE)
1850:     return "DATA";
1851:   return "RODATA";
1852: };
1853: 
1854: // Sort sections according to a --section-order argument.
1855: template <typename E>
1856: void sort_output_sections_by_order(Context<E> &ctx) {
1857:   auto get_rank = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) -> i64 {
1858:     u64 flags = chunk->shdr.sh_flags;
1859: 
1860:     if (chunk == ctx.ehdr && !(chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
1861:       return -2;
1862:     if (chunk == ctx.phdr && !(chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
1863:       return -1;
1864: 
1865:     if (chunk == ctx.shdr)
1866:       return INT32_MAX;
1867:     if (!(flags & SHF_ALLOC))
1868:       return INT32_MAX - 1;
1869: 
1870:     for (i64 i = 0; const SectionOrder &arg : ctx.arg.section_order) {
1871:       if (arg.type == SectionOrder::SECTION && arg.name == chunk->name)
1872:         return i;
1873:       i++;
1874:     }
1875: 
1876:     std::string_view group = get_section_order_group(*chunk);
1877: 
1878:     for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.section_order.size(); i++) {
1879:       SectionOrder arg = ctx.arg.section_order[i];
1880:       if (arg.type == SectionOrder::GROUP && arg.name == group)
1881:         return i;
1882:     }
1883: 
1884:     Error(ctx) << "--section-order: missing section specification for "
1885:                << chunk->name;
1886:     return 0;
1887:   };
1888: 
1889:   // It is an error if a section order cannot be determined by a given
1890:   // section order list.
1891:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
1892:     chunk->sect_order = get_rank(chunk);
1893: 
1894:   // Sort output sections by --section-order
1895:   sort(ctx.chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *a, Chunk<E> *b) {
1896:     return a->sect_order < b->sect_order;
1897:   });
1898: }
1899: 
1900: template <typename E>
1901: void sort_output_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
1902:   if (ctx.arg.section_order.empty())
1903:     sort_output_sections_regular(ctx);
1904:   else
1905:     sort_output_sections_by_order(ctx);
1906: }
1907: 
1908: template <typename E>
1909: static bool is_tbss(Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1910:   return (chunk->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS) && (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS);
1911: }
1912: 
1913: // This function assigns virtual addresses to output sections. Assigning
1914: // addresses is a bit tricky because we want to pack sections as tightly
1915: // as possible while not violating the constraints imposed by the hardware
1916: // and the OS kernel. Specifically, we need to satisfy the following
1917: // constraints:
1918: //
1919: // - Memory protection (readable, writable and executable) works at page
1920: //   granularity. Therefore, if we want to set different memory attributes
1921: //   to two sections, we need to place them into separate pages.
1922: //
1923: // - The ELF spec requires that a section's file offset is congruent to
1924: //   its virtual address modulo the page size. For example, a section at
1925: //   virtual address 0x401234 on x86-64 (4 KiB, or 0x1000 byte page
1926: //   system) can be at file offset 0x3234 or 0x50234 but not at 0x1000.
1927: //
1928: // We need to insert paddings between sections if we can't satisfy the
1929: // above constraints without them.
1930: //
1931: // We don't want to waste too much memory and disk space for paddings.
1932: // There are a few tricks we can use to minimize paddings as below:
1933: //
1934: // - We want to place sections with the same memory attributes
1935: //   contiguous as possible.
1936: //
1937: // - We can map the same file region to memory more than once. For
1938: //   example, we can write code (with R and X bits) and read-only data
1939: //   (with R bit) adjacent on file and map it twice as the last page of
1940: //   the executable segment and the first page of the read-only data
1941: //   segment. This doesn't save memory but saves disk space.
1942: template <typename E>
1943: static void set_virtual_addresses_regular(Context<E> &ctx) {
1944:   constexpr i64 RELRO = 1LL << 32;
1945: 
1946:   auto get_flags = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1947:     i64 flags = to_phdr_flags(ctx, chunk);
1948:     if (is_relro(ctx, chunk))
1949:       return flags | RELRO;
1950:     return flags;
1951:   };
1952: 
1953:   // Assign virtual addresses
1954:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> &chunks = ctx.chunks;
1955:   u64 addr = ctx.arg.image_base;
1956: 
1957:   // TLS chunks alignments are special: in addition to having their virtual
1958:   // addresses aligned, they also have to be aligned when the region of
1959:   // tls_begin is copied to a new thread's storage area. In other words, their
1960:   // offset against tls_begin also has to be aligned.
1961:   //
1962:   // A good way to achieve this is to take the largest alignment requirement
1963:   // of all TLS sections and make tls_begin also aligned to that.
1964:   Chunk<E> *first_tls_chunk = nullptr;
1965:   u64 tls_alignment = 1;
1966:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : chunks) {
1967:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_TLS) {
1968:       if (!first_tls_chunk)
1969:         first_tls_chunk = chunk;
1970:       tls_alignment = std::max(tls_alignment, (u64)chunk->shdr.sh_addralign);
1971:     }
1972:   }
1973: 
1974:   auto alignment = [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1975:     return chunk == first_tls_chunk ? tls_alignment : (u64)chunk->shdr.sh_addralign;
1976:   };
1977: 
1978:   for (i64 i = 0; i < chunks.size(); i++) {
1979:     if (!(chunks[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
1980:       continue;
1981: 
1982:     // .relro_padding is a padding section to extend a PT_GNU_RELRO
1983:     // segment to cover an entire page. Technically, we don't need a
1984:     // .relro_padding section because we can leave a trailing part of a
1985:     // segment an unused space. However, the `strip` command would delete
1986:     // such an unused trailing part and make an executable invalid.
1987:     // So we add a dummy section.
1988:     if (chunks[i] == ctx.relro_padding) {
1989:       chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr = addr;
1990:       chunks[i]->shdr.sh_size = align_to(addr, ctx.page_size) - addr;
1991:       addr += ctx.page_size;
1992:       continue;
1993:     }
1994: 
1995:     // Handle --section-start first
1996:     if (auto it = ctx.arg.section_start.find(chunks[i]->name);
1997:         it != ctx.arg.section_start.end()) {
1998:       addr = it->second;
1999:       chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr = addr;
2000:       addr += chunks[i]->shdr.sh_size;
2001:       continue;
2002:     }
2003: 
2004:     // Memory protection works at page size granularity. We need to
2005:     // put sections with different memory attributes into different
2006:     // pages. We do it by inserting paddings here.
2007:     if (i > 0 && chunks[i - 1] != ctx.relro_padding) {
2008:       i64 flags1 = get_flags(chunks[i - 1]);
2009:       i64 flags2 = get_flags(chunks[i]);
2010: 
2011:       if (flags1 != flags2) {
2012:         switch (ctx.arg.z_separate_code) {
2013:         case SEPARATE_LOADABLE_SEGMENTS:
2014:           addr = align_to(addr, ctx.page_size);
2015:           break;
2016:         case SEPARATE_CODE:
2017:           if ((flags1 & PF_X) != (flags2 & PF_X)) {
2018:             addr = align_to(addr, ctx.page_size);
2019:             break;
2020:           }
2021:           [[fallthrough]];
2022:         case NOSEPARATE_CODE:
2023:           if (addr % ctx.page_size != 0)
2024:             addr += ctx.page_size;
2025:           break;
2026:         default:
2027:           unreachable();
2028:         }
2029:       }
2030:     }
2031: 
2032:     // TLS BSS sections are laid out so that they overlap with the
2033:     // subsequent non-tbss sections. Overlapping is fine because a STT_TLS
2034:     // segment contains an initialization image for newly-created threads,
2035:     // and no one except the runtime reads its contents. Even the runtime
2036:     // doesn't need a BSS part of a TLS initialization image; it just
2037:     // leaves zero-initialized bytes as-is instead of copying zeros.
2038:     // So no one really read tbss at runtime.
2039:     //
2040:     // We can instead allocate a dedicated virtual address space to tbss,
2041:     // but that would be just a waste of the address and disk space.
2042:     if (is_tbss(chunks[i])) {
2043:       u64 addr2 = addr;
2044:       for (;;) {
2045:         addr2 = align_to(addr2, alignment(chunks[i]));
2046:         chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr = addr2;
2047:         addr2 += chunks[i]->shdr.sh_size;
2048:         if (i + 2 == chunks.size() || !is_tbss(chunks[i + 1]))
2049:           break;
2050:         i++;
2051:       }
2052:       continue;
2053:     }
2054: 
2055:     addr = align_to(addr, alignment(chunks[i]));
2056:     chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr = addr;
2057:     addr += chunks[i]->shdr.sh_size;
2058:   }
2059: }
2060: 
2061: template <typename E>
2062: static void set_virtual_addresses_by_order(Context<E> &ctx) {
2063:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> &c = ctx.chunks;
2064:   u64 addr = ctx.arg.image_base;
2065:   i64 i = 0;
2066: 
2067:   while (i < c.size() && !(c[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
2068:     i++;
2069: 
2070:   auto assign_addr = [&] {
2071:     if (i != 0) {
2072:       i64 flags1 = to_phdr_flags(ctx, c[i - 1]);
2073:       i64 flags2 = to_phdr_flags(ctx, c[i]);
2074: 
2075:       // Memory protection works at page size granularity. We need to
2076:       // put sections with different memory attributes into different
2077:       // pages. We do it by inserting paddings here.
2078:       if (flags1 != flags2) {
2079:         switch (ctx.arg.z_separate_code) {
2080:         case SEPARATE_LOADABLE_SEGMENTS:
2081:           addr = align_to(addr, ctx.page_size);
2082:           break;
2083:         case SEPARATE_CODE:
2084:           if ((flags1 & PF_X) != (flags2 & PF_X))
2085:             addr = align_to(addr, ctx.page_size);
2086:           break;
2087:         default:
2088:           break;
2089:         }
2090:       }
2091:     }
2092: 
2093:     addr = align_to(addr, c[i]->shdr.sh_addralign);
2094:     c[i]->shdr.sh_addr = addr;
2095:     addr += c[i]->shdr.sh_size;
2096: 
2097:     do {
2098:       i++;
2099:     } while (i < c.size() && !(c[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC));
2100:   };
2101: 
2102:   for (i64 j = 0; j < ctx.arg.section_order.size(); j++) {
2103:     SectionOrder &ord = ctx.arg.section_order[j];
2104:     switch (ord.type) {
2105:     case SectionOrder::SECTION:
2106:       if (i < c.size() && j == c[i]->sect_order)
2107:         assign_addr();
2108:       break;
2109:     case SectionOrder::GROUP:
2110:       while (i < c.size() && j == c[i]->sect_order)
2111:         assign_addr();
2112:       break;
2113:     case SectionOrder::ADDR:
2114:       addr = ord.value;
2115:       break;
2116:     case SectionOrder::ALIGN:
2117:       addr = align_to(addr, ord.value);
2118:       break;
2119:     case SectionOrder::SYMBOL:
2120:       get_symbol(ctx, ord.name)->value = addr;
2121:       break;
2122:     default:
2123:       unreachable();
2124:     }
2125:   }
2126: }
2127: 
2128: // Returns the smallest integer N that satisfies N >= val and
2129: // N mod align == skew mod align.
2130: //
2131: // Section's file offset must be congruent to its virtual address modulo
2132: // the page size. We use this function to satisfy that requirement.
2133: static u64 align_with_skew(u64 val, u64 align, u64 skew) {
2134:   u64 x = align_down(val, align) + skew % align;
2135:   return (val <= x) ? x : x + align;
2136: }
2137: 
2138: // Assign file offsets to output sections.
2139: template <typename E>
2140: static i64 set_file_offsets(Context<E> &ctx) {
2141:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> &chunks = ctx.chunks;
2142:   u64 fileoff = 0;
2143:   i64 i = 0;
2144: 
2145:   while (i < chunks.size()) {
2146:     Chunk<E> &first = *chunks[i];
2147: 
2148:     if (!(first.shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)) {
2149:       fileoff = align_to(fileoff, first.shdr.sh_addralign);
2150:       first.shdr.sh_offset = fileoff;
2151:       fileoff += first.shdr.sh_size;
2152:       i++;
2153:       continue;
2154:     }
2155: 
2156:     if (first.shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS) {
2157:       i++;
2158:       continue;
2159:     }
2160: 
2161:     if (first.shdr.sh_addralign > ctx.page_size)
2162:       fileoff = align_to(fileoff, first.shdr.sh_addralign);
2163:     else
2164:       fileoff = align_with_skew(fileoff, ctx.page_size, first.shdr.sh_addr);
2165: 
2166:     // Assign ALLOC sections contiguous file offsets as long as they
2167:     // are contiguous in memory.
2168:     for (;;) {
2169:       chunks[i]->shdr.sh_offset =
2170:         fileoff + chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr - first.shdr.sh_addr;
2171:       i++;
2172: 
2173:       if (i >= chunks.size() ||
2174:           !(chunks[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) ||
2175:           chunks[i]->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS)
2176:         break;
2177: 
2178:       // If --start-section is given, addresses may not increase
2179:       // monotonically.
2180:       if (chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr < first.shdr.sh_addr)
2181:         break;
2182: 
2183:       i64 gap_size = chunks[i]->shdr.sh_addr - chunks[i - 1]->shdr.sh_addr -
2184:                      chunks[i - 1]->shdr.sh_size;
2185: 
2186:       // If --start-section is given, there may be a large gap between
2187:       // sections. We don't want to allocate a disk space for a gap if
2188:       // exists.
2189:       if (gap_size >= ctx.page_size)
2190:         break;
2191:     }
2192: 
2193:     fileoff = chunks[i - 1]->shdr.sh_offset + chunks[i - 1]->shdr.sh_size;
2194: 
2195:     while (i < chunks.size() &&
2196:            (chunks[i]->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) &&
2197:            chunks[i]->shdr.sh_type == SHT_NOBITS)
2198:       i++;
2199:   }
2200: 
2201:   return fileoff;
2202: }
2203: 
2204: template <typename E>
2205: void compute_section_headers(Context<E> &ctx) {
2206:   // Update sh_size for each chunk.
2207:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2208:     chunk->update_shdr(ctx);
2209: 
2210:   // Remove empty chunks.
2211:   std::erase_if(ctx.chunks, [](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
2212:     return chunk->kind() != OUTPUT_SECTION && chunk->shdr.sh_size == 0;
2213:   });
2214: 
2215:   // Set section indices.
2216:   i64 shndx = 1;
2217:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.chunks.size(); i++)
2218:     if (ctx.chunks[i]->kind() != HEADER)
2219:       ctx.chunks[i]->shndx = shndx++;
2220: 
2221:   if (ctx.symtab && SHN_LORESERVE <= shndx) {
2222:     SymtabShndxSection<E> *sec = new SymtabShndxSection<E>;
2223:     sec->shndx = shndx++;
2224:     sec->shdr.sh_link = ctx.symtab->shndx;
2225:     ctx.symtab_shndx = sec;
2226:     ctx.chunks.push_back(sec);
2227:     ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(sec);
2228:   }
2229: 
2230:   if (ctx.shdr)
2231:     ctx.shdr->shdr.sh_size = shndx * sizeof(ElfShdr<E>);
2232: 
2233:   // Some types of section header refer other section by index.
2234:   // Recompute the section header to fill such fields with correct values.
2235:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2236:     chunk->update_shdr(ctx);
2237: 
2238:   if (ctx.symtab_shndx) {
2239:     i64 symtab_size = ctx.symtab->shdr.sh_size / sizeof(ElfSym<E>);
2240:     ctx.symtab_shndx->shdr.sh_size = symtab_size * 4;
2241:   }
2242: }
2243: 
2244: // Assign virtual addresses and file offsets to output sections.
2245: template <typename E>
2246: i64 set_osec_offsets(Context<E> &ctx) {
2247:   Timer t(ctx, "set_osec_offsets");
2248: 
2249:   for (;;) {
2250:     if (ctx.arg.section_order.empty())
2251:       set_virtual_addresses_regular(ctx);
2252:     else
2253:       set_virtual_addresses_by_order(ctx);
2254: 
2255:     i64 fileoff = set_file_offsets(ctx);
2256: 
2257:     // Assigning new offsets may change the contents and the length
2258:     // of the program header, so repeat it until converge.
2259:     if (!ctx.phdr)
2260:       return fileoff;
2261: 
2262:     i64 sz = ctx.phdr->shdr.sh_size;
2263:     ctx.phdr->update_shdr(ctx);
2264:     if (sz == ctx.phdr->shdr.sh_size)
2265:       return fileoff;
2266:   }
2267: }
2268: 
2269: template <typename E>
2270: static i64 get_num_irelative_relocs(Context<E> &ctx) {
2271:   i64 n = std::count_if(ctx.got->got_syms.begin(), ctx.got->got_syms.end(),
2272:                         [](Symbol<E> *sym) { return sym->is_ifunc(); });
2273:   return n + ctx.num_ifunc_dynrels;
2274: }
2275: 
2276: template <typename E>
2277: static u64 to_paddr(Context<E> &ctx, u64 vaddr) {
2278:   for (ElfPhdr<E> &phdr : ctx.phdr->phdrs)
2279:     if (phdr.p_type == PT_LOAD)
2280:       if (phdr.p_vaddr <= vaddr && vaddr < phdr.p_vaddr + phdr.p_memsz)
2281:         return phdr.p_paddr + (vaddr - phdr.p_vaddr);
2282:   return 0;
2283: }
2284: 
2285: template <typename E>
2286: void fix_synthetic_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
2287:   auto start = [](Symbol<E> *sym, auto &chunk, i64 bias = 0) {
2288:     if (sym && chunk) {
2289:       sym->set_output_section(chunk);
2290:       sym->value = chunk->shdr.sh_addr + bias;
2291:     }
2292:   };
2293: 
2294:   auto stop = [](Symbol<E> *sym, auto &chunk) {
2295:     if (sym && chunk) {
2296:       sym->set_output_section(chunk);
2297:       sym->value = chunk->shdr.sh_addr + chunk->shdr.sh_size;
2298:     }
2299:   };
2300: 
2301:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> sections;
2302:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2303:     if (chunk->kind() != HEADER && (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC))
2304:       sections.push_back(chunk);
2305: 
2306:   auto find = [&](std::string name) -> Chunk<E> * {
2307:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : sections)
2308:       if (chunk->name == name)
2309:         return chunk;
2310:     return nullptr;
2311:   };
2312: 
2313:   // __bss_start
2314:   if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find(".bss"))
2315:     start(ctx.__bss_start, chunk);
2316: 
2317:   if (ctx.ehdr && (ctx.ehdr->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)) {
2318:     ctx.__ehdr_start->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2319:     ctx.__ehdr_start->value = ctx.ehdr->shdr.sh_addr;
2320:     ctx.__executable_start->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2321:     ctx.__executable_start->value = ctx.ehdr->shdr.sh_addr;
2322:   }
2323: 
2324:   if (ctx.__dso_handle) {
2325:     ctx.__dso_handle->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2326:     ctx.__dso_handle->value = sections[0]->shdr.sh_addr;
2327:   }
2328: 
2329:   // __rel_iplt_start and __rel_iplt_end. These symbols need to be
2330:   // defined in a statically-linked non-relocatable executable because
2331:   // such executable lacks the .dynamic section and thus there's no way
2332:   // to find ifunc relocations other than these symbols.
2333:   //
2334:   // We don't want to set values to these symbols if we are creating a
2335:   // static PIE due to a glibc bug. Static PIE has a dynamic section.
2336:   // If we set values to these symbols in a static PIE, glibc attempts
2337:   // to run ifunc initializers twice, with the second attempt with wrong
2338:   // function addresses, causing a segmentation fault.
2339:   if (ctx.reldyn && ctx.arg.is_static && !ctx.arg.pie) {
2340:     stop(ctx.__rel_iplt_start, ctx.reldyn);
2341:     stop(ctx.__rel_iplt_end, ctx.reldyn);
2342: 
2343:     ctx.__rel_iplt_start->value -=
2344:       get_num_irelative_relocs(ctx) * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
2345:   }
2346: 
2347:   // __{init,fini}_array_{start,end}
2348:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : sections) {
2349:     switch (chunk->shdr.sh_type) {
2350:     case SHT_INIT_ARRAY:
2351:       start(ctx.__init_array_start, chunk);
2352:       stop(ctx.__init_array_end, chunk);
2353:       break;
2354:     case SHT_PREINIT_ARRAY:
2355:       start(ctx.__preinit_array_start, chunk);
2356:       stop(ctx.__preinit_array_end, chunk);
2357:       break;
2358:     case SHT_FINI_ARRAY:
2359:       start(ctx.__fini_array_start, chunk);
2360:       stop(ctx.__fini_array_end, chunk);
2361:       break;
2362:     }
2363:   }
2364: 
2365:   // _end, _etext, _edata and the like
2366:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : sections) {
2367:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) {
2368:       stop(ctx._end, chunk);
2369:       stop(ctx.end, chunk);
2370:     }
2371: 
2372:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) {
2373:       stop(ctx._etext, chunk);
2374:       stop(ctx.etext, chunk);
2375:     }
2376: 
2377:     if (chunk->shdr.sh_type != SHT_NOBITS &&
2378:         (chunk->shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)) {
2379:       stop(ctx._edata, chunk);
2380:       stop(ctx.edata, chunk);
2381:     }
2382:   }
2383: 
2384:   // _DYNAMIC
2385:   start(ctx._DYNAMIC, ctx.dynamic);
2386: 
2387:   // _GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_. I don't know why, but for the sake of
2388:   // compatibility with existing code, it must be set to the beginning of
2389:   // .got.plt instead of .got only on i386 and x86-64.
2390:   if constexpr (is_x86<E>)
2391:     start(ctx._GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_, ctx.gotplt);
2392:   else
2393:     start(ctx._GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_, ctx.got);
2394: 
2395:   // _PROCEDURE_LINKAGE_TABLE_. We need this on SPARC.
2396:   start(ctx._PROCEDURE_LINKAGE_TABLE_, ctx.plt);
2397: 
2398:   // _TLS_MODULE_BASE_. This symbol is used to obtain the address of
2399:   // the TLS block in the TLSDESC model. I believe GCC and Clang don't
2400:   // create a reference to it, but Intel compiler seems to be using
2401:   // this symbol.
2402:   if (ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_) {
2403:     ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2404:     ctx._TLS_MODULE_BASE_->value = ctx.tls_begin;
2405:   }
2406: 
2407:   // __GNU_EH_FRAME_HDR
2408:   start(ctx.__GNU_EH_FRAME_HDR, ctx.eh_frame_hdr);
2409: 
2410:   // RISC-V's __global_pointer$
2411:   if (ctx.__global_pointer) {
2412:     if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find(".sdata")) {
2413:       start(ctx.__global_pointer, chunk, 0x800);
2414:     } else {
2415:       ctx.__global_pointer->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2416:       ctx.__global_pointer->value = 0;
2417:     }
2418:   }
2419: 
2420:   // ARM32's __exidx_{start,end}
2421:   if (ctx.__exidx_start) {
2422:     if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find(".ARM.exidx")) {
2423:       start(ctx.__exidx_start, chunk);
2424:       stop(ctx.__exidx_end, chunk);
2425:     }
2426:   }
2427: 
2428:   // PPC64's ".TOC." symbol.
2429:   if constexpr (is_ppc64<E>) {
2430:     if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find(".got")) {
2431:       start(ctx.extra.TOC, chunk, 0x8000);
2432:     } else if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find(".toc")) {
2433:       start(ctx.extra.TOC, chunk, 0x8000);
2434:     } else {
2435:       ctx.extra.TOC->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2436:       ctx.extra.TOC->value = 0;
2437:     }
2438:   }
2439: 
2440:   // __start_ and __stop_ symbols
2441:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : sections) {
2442:     if (std::optional<std::string> name = get_start_stop_name(ctx, *chunk)) {
2443:       start(get_symbol(ctx, save_string(ctx, "__start_" + *name)), chunk);
2444:       stop(get_symbol(ctx, save_string(ctx, "__stop_" + *name)), chunk);
2445: 
2446:       if (ctx.arg.physical_image_base) {
2447:         u64 paddr = to_paddr(ctx, chunk->shdr.sh_addr);
2448: 
2449:         Symbol<E> *x = get_symbol(ctx, save_string(ctx, "__phys_start_" + *name));
2450:         x->set_output_section(chunk);
2451:         x->value = paddr;
2452: 
2453:         Symbol<E> *y = get_symbol(ctx, save_string(ctx, "__phys_stop_" + *name));
2454:         y->set_output_section(chunk);
2455:         y->value = paddr + chunk->shdr.sh_size;
2456:       }
2457:     }
2458:   }
2459: 
2460:   // --defsym=sym=value symbols
2461:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.arg.defsyms.size(); i++) {
2462:     Symbol<E> *sym = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].first;
2463:     std::variant<Symbol<E> *, u64> val = ctx.arg.defsyms[i].second;
2464: 
2465:     if (u64 *addr = std::get_if<u64>(&val)) {
2466:       sym->origin = 0;
2467:       sym->value = *addr;
2468:       continue;
2469:     }
2470: 
2471:     Symbol<E> *sym2 = std::get<Symbol<E> *>(val);
2472:     if (!sym2->file) {
2473:       Error(ctx) << "--defsym: undefined symbol: " << *sym2;
2474:       continue;
2475:     }
2476: 
2477:     sym->value = sym2->value;
2478:     sym->origin = sym2->origin;
2479:     sym->visibility = sym2->visibility.load();
2480:   }
2481: 
2482: 
2483:   // --section-order symbols
2484:   for (SectionOrder &ord : ctx.arg.section_order)
2485:     if (ord.type == SectionOrder::SYMBOL)
2486:       get_symbol(ctx, ord.name)->set_output_section(sections[0]);
2487: }
2488: 
2489: template <typename E>
2490: i64 compress_debug_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
2491:   Timer t(ctx, "compress_debug_sections");
2492: 
2493:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.chunks.size(), [&](i64 i) {
2494:     Chunk<E> &chunk = *ctx.chunks[i];
2495: 
2496:     if ((chunk.shdr.sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC) || chunk.shdr.sh_size == 0 ||
2497:         !chunk.name.starts_with(".debug"))
2498:       return;
2499: 
2500:     Chunk<E> *comp = new CompressedSection<E>(ctx, chunk);
2501:     ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(comp);
2502:     ctx.chunks[i] = comp;
2503:   });
2504: 
2505:   ctx.shstrtab->update_shdr(ctx);
2506: 
2507:   if (ctx.ehdr)
2508:     ctx.ehdr->update_shdr(ctx);
2509:   if (ctx.shdr)
2510:     ctx.shdr->update_shdr(ctx);
2511: 
2512:   return set_osec_offsets(ctx);
2513: }
2514: 
2515: // Write Makefile-style dependency rules to a file specified by
2516: // --dependency-file. This is analogous to the compiler's -M flag.
2517: template <typename E>
2518: void write_dependency_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
2519:   std::vector<std::string> deps;
2520:   std::unordered_set<std::string> seen;
2521: 
2522:   for (std::unique_ptr<MappedFile<Context<E>>> &mf : ctx.mf_pool)
2523:     if (!mf->parent)
2524:       if (std::string path = path_clean(mf->name); seen.insert(path).second)
2525:         deps.push_back(path);
2526: 
2527:   std::ofstream out;
2528:   out.open(ctx.arg.dependency_file);
2529:   if (out.fail())
2530:     Fatal(ctx) << "--dependency-file: cannot open " << ctx.arg.dependency_file
2531:                << ": " << errno_string();
2532: 
2533:   out << ctx.arg.output << ":";
2534:   for (std::string &s : deps)
2535:     out << " " << s;
2536:   out << "\n";
2537: 
2538:   for (std::string &s : deps)
2539:     out << "\n" << s << ":\n";
2540:   out.close();
2541: }
2542: 
2543: template <typename E>
2544: void show_stats(Context<E> &ctx) {
2545:   for (ObjectFile<E> *obj : ctx.objs) {
2546:     static Counter defined("defined_syms");
2547:     defined += obj->first_global - 1;
2548: 
2549:     static Counter undefined("undefined_syms");
2550:     undefined += obj->symbols.size() - obj->first_global;
2551: 
2552:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &sec : obj->sections) {
2553:       if (!sec || !sec->is_alive)
2554:         continue;
2555: 
2556:       static Counter alloc("reloc_alloc");
2557:       static Counter nonalloc("reloc_nonalloc");
2558: 
2559:       if (sec->shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC)
2560:         alloc += sec->get_rels(ctx).size();
2561:       else
2562:         nonalloc += sec->get_rels(ctx).size();
2563:     }
2564: 
2565:     static Counter comdats("comdats");
2566:     comdats += obj->comdat_groups.size();
2567: 
2568:     static Counter removed_comdats("removed_comdat_mem");
2569:     for (ComdatGroupRef<E> &ref : obj->comdat_groups)
2570:       if (ref.group->owner != obj->priority)
2571:         removed_comdats += ref.members.size();
2572: 
2573:     static Counter num_cies("num_cies");
2574:     num_cies += obj->cies.size();
2575: 
2576:     static Counter num_unique_cies("num_unique_cies");
2577:     for (CieRecord<E> &cie : obj->cies)
2578:       if (cie.is_leader)
2579:         num_unique_cies++;
2580: 
2581:     static Counter num_fdes("num_fdes");
2582:     num_fdes +=  obj->fdes.size();
2583:   }
2584: 
2585:   static Counter num_bytes("total_input_bytes");
2586:   for (std::unique_ptr<MappedFile<Context<E>>> &mf : ctx.mf_pool)
2587:     num_bytes += mf->size;
2588: 
2589:   static Counter num_input_sections("input_sections");
2590:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
2591:     num_input_sections += file->sections.size();
2592: 
2593:   static Counter num_output_chunks("output_chunks", ctx.chunks.size());
2594:   static Counter num_objs("num_objs", ctx.objs.size());
2595:   static Counter num_dsos("num_dsos", ctx.dsos.size());
2596: 
2597:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
2598:     static Counter thunk_bytes("thunk_bytes");
2599:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2600:       if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
2601:         for (std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>> &thunk : osec->thunks)
2602:           thunk_bytes += thunk->size();
2603:   }
2604: 
2605:   Counter::print();
2606: 
2607:   for (std::unique_ptr<MergedSection<E>> &sec : ctx.merged_sections)
2608:     sec->print_stats(ctx);
2609: }
2610: 
2611: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
