  25: static std::pair<std::string_view, std::string_view>
  26: split_string(std::string_view str, char sep) {
  27:   size_t pos = str.find(sep);
  28:   if (pos == str.npos)
  29:     return {str, ""};
  30:   return {str.substr(0, pos), str.substr(pos + 1)};
  31: }
  32: 
  33: template <typename E>
  34: static bool has_lto_obj(Context<E> &ctx) {
  35:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
  36:     if (file->lto_module)
  37:       return true;
  38:   return false;
  39: }
  40: 
  41: template <typename E>
  42: static void resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
  43:   Timer t(ctx, "resolve_symbols");
  44: 
  45:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> files;
  46:   append(files, ctx.objs);
  47:   append(files, ctx.dylibs);
  48: 
  49:   tbb::parallel_for_each(files, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
  50:     file->resolve_symbols(ctx);
  51:   });
  52: 
  53:   if (has_lto_obj(ctx))
  54:     do_lto(ctx);
  55: 
  56:   // We want to keep symbols may be referenced indirectly.
  57:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.u)
  58:     if (InputFile<E> *file = get_symbol(ctx, name)->file)
  59:       file->is_alive = true;
  60: 
  61:   if (InputFile<E> *file = ctx.arg.entry->file)
  62:     file->is_alive = true;
  63: 
  64:   if (InputFile<E> *file = ctx._objc_msgSend->file; file && file->is_dylib)
  65:     file->is_alive = true;
  66: 
  67:   if (!ctx.arg.fixup_chains)
  68:     if (InputFile<E> *file = ctx.dyld_stub_binder->file; file && file->is_dylib)
  69:       file->is_alive = true;
  70: 
  71:   // Mark reachable object files
  72:   std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> live_objs;
  73:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
  74:     if (file->is_alive)
  75:       live_objs.push_back(file);
  76: 
  77:   for (i64 i = 0; i < live_objs.size(); i++) {
  78:     live_objs[i]->mark_live_objects(ctx, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
  79:       live_objs.push_back(file);
  80:     });
  81:   }
  82: 
  83:   // Remove symbols of eliminated files.
  84:   tbb::parallel_for_each(files, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
  85:     if (!file->is_alive)
  86:       file->clear_symbols();
  87:   });
  88: 
  89:   // Redo symbol resolution because extracting object files from archives
  90:   // may raise the priority of symbols defined by the object file.
  91:   tbb::parallel_for_each(files, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
  92:     if (file->is_alive)
  93:       file->resolve_symbols(ctx);
  94:   });
  95: 
  96:   std::erase_if(ctx.objs, [](InputFile<E> *file) { return !file->is_alive; });
  97:   std::erase_if(ctx.dylibs, [](InputFile<E> *file) { return !file->is_alive; });
  98: 
  99:   for (i64 i = 1; DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
 100:     if (file->dylib_idx != BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_MAIN_EXECUTABLE)
 101:       file->dylib_idx = i++;
 102: }
 103: 
 104: template <typename E>
 105: static void handle_exported_symbols_list(Context<E> &ctx) {
 106:   Timer t(ctx, "handle_exported_symbols_list");
 107:   if (ctx.arg.exported_symbols_list.empty())
 108:     return;
 109: 
 110:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 111:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
 112:       if (sym && sym->file == file)
 113:         if (sym->visibility != SCOPE_LOCAL)
 114:           sym->visibility = ctx.arg.exported_symbols_list.find(sym->name)
 115:             ? SCOPE_GLOBAL : SCOPE_MODULE;
 116:   });
 117: }
 118: 
 119: template <typename E>
 120: static void handle_unexported_symbols_list(Context<E> &ctx) {
 121:   Timer t(ctx, "handle_unexported_symbols_list");
 122:   if (ctx.arg.unexported_symbols_list.empty())
 123:     return;
 124: 
 125:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 126:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
 127:       if (sym && sym->file == file)
 128:         if (sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL &&
 129:             ctx.arg.unexported_symbols_list.find(sym->name))
 130:           sym->visibility = SCOPE_MODULE;
 131:   });
 132: }
 133: 
 134: template <typename E>
 135: static void compute_import_export(Context<E> &ctx) {
 136:   // Compute is_imported and is_exported values
 137:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 138:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms) {
 139:       if (!sym || sym->visibility != SCOPE_GLOBAL)
 140:         continue;
 141: 
 142:       // If we are creating a dylib, all global symbols are exported by default.
 143:       if (sym->file == file && ctx.output_type != MH_EXECUTE) {
 144:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
 145:         sym->is_exported = true;
 146:         if (ctx.arg.flat_namespace)
 147:           sym->is_imported = true;
 148:         continue;
 149:       }
 150: 
 151:       // If we are using a dylib symbol, we need to import it.
 152:       if (sym->file && sym->file->is_dylib) {
 153:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
 154:         sym->is_imported = true;
 155:       }
 156:     }
 157:   });
 158: 
 159:   // We want to export symbols referenced by dylibs.
 160:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.dylibs, [&](DylibFile<E> *file) {
 161:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms) {
 162:       if (sym && sym->file && !sym->file->is_dylib &&
 163:           sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL) {
 164:         std::scoped_lock lock(sym->mu);
 165:         sym->is_exported = true;
 166:       }
 167:     }
 168:   });
 169: 
 170:   // Some symbols are referenced only by linker-synthesized sections.
 171:   // We need to handle such symbols too.
 172:   auto import = [&](Symbol<E> &sym) {
 173:     if (!sym.file)
 174:       Error(ctx) << "undefined symbol: " << sym;
 175: 
 176:     if (sym.file->is_dylib) {
 177:       sym.is_imported = true;
 178:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
 179:     }
 180:   };
 181: 
 182:   if (ctx.objc_stubs)
 183:     import(*ctx._objc_msgSend);
 184:   if (ctx.stub_helper)
 185:     import(*ctx.dyld_stub_binder);
 186: }
 187: 
 188: template <typename E>
 189: static void create_internal_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 190:   Timer t(ctx, "create_internal_file");
 191: 
 192:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = new ObjectFile<E>;
 193:   obj->is_alive = true;
 194:   obj->mach_syms = obj->mach_syms2;
 195:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(obj);
 196:   ctx.objs.push_back(obj);
 197:   ctx.internal_obj = obj;
 198: 
 199:   auto add = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 200:     sym->file = obj;
 201:     obj->syms.push_back(sym);
 202:   };
 203: 
 204:   add(ctx.__dyld_private);
 205:   add(ctx.___dso_handle);
 206: 
 207:   switch (ctx.output_type) {
 208:   case MH_EXECUTE: {
 209:     add(ctx.__mh_execute_header);
 210:     ctx.__mh_execute_header->visibility = SCOPE_GLOBAL;
 211:     ctx.__mh_execute_header->is_exported = true;
 212:     ctx.__mh_execute_header->value = ctx.arg.pagezero_size;
 213:     break;
 214:   }
 215:   case MH_DYLIB:
 216:     add(ctx.__mh_dylib_header);
 217:     break;
 218:   case MH_BUNDLE:
 219:     add(ctx.__mh_bundle_header);
 220:     break;
 221:   default:
 222:     unreachable();
 223:   }
 224: 
 225:   // Add start stop symbols.
 226:   std::set<std::string_view> start_stop_symbols;
 227:   std::mutex mu;
 228: 
 229:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 230:     std::set<std::string_view> set;
 231:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
 232:       if (!sym->file)
 233:         if (sym->name.starts_with("segment$start$") ||
 234:             sym->name.starts_with("segment$end$") ||
 235:             sym->name.starts_with("section$start$") ||
 236:             sym->name.starts_with("section$end$"))
 237:           set.insert(sym->name);
 238: 
 239:     std::scoped_lock lock(mu);
 240:     start_stop_symbols.merge(set);
 241:   });
 242: 
 243:   for (std::string_view name : start_stop_symbols)
 244:     add(get_symbol(ctx, name));
 245: }
 246: 
 247: // Some pieces of code or data such as C++ inline functions or
 248: // instantiated templates can be defined by multiple input files.
 249: // We want to deduplicate them so that only one copy of them is
 250: // included into the output file.
 251: //
 252: // In Mach-O, such code or data are simply defined as weak symbols to
 253: // allow multiple definitions. In this function, we eliminate
 254: // subsections that are referenced by weak symbols that are overridden
 255: // by other files' instances.
 256: template <typename E>
 257: static void remove_unreferenced_subsections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 258:   Timer t(ctx, "remove_unreferenced_subsections");
 259: 
 260:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 261:     if (file == ctx.internal_obj)
 262:       return;
 263: 
 264:     if (u32 flags = ((MachHeader *)file->mf->data)->flags;
 265:         !(flags & MH_SUBSECTIONS_VIA_SYMBOLS))
 266:       return;
 267: 
 268:     for (i64 i = 0; i < file->mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 269:       MachSym<E> &msym = file->mach_syms[i];
 270:       Symbol<E> &sym = *file->syms[i];
 271:       if (sym.file != file && (msym.type == N_SECT) &&
 272:           (msym.desc & N_WEAK_DEF) && !(msym.desc & N_ALT_ENTRY))
 273:         file->sym_to_subsec[i]->is_alive = false;
 274:     }
 275: 
 276:     std::erase_if(file->subsections, [](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 277:       return !subsec->is_alive;
 278:     });
 279:   });
 280: }
 281: 
 282: template <typename E>
 283: static bool compare_segments(const std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &a,
 284:                              const std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &b) {
 285:   // We want to sort output segments in the following order:
 286:   // __TEXT, __DATA_CONST, __DATA, <other segments>, __LINKEDIT
 287:   auto get_rank = [](std::string_view name) {
 288:     if (name == "__TEXT")
 289:       return 0;
 290:     if (name == "__DATA_CONST")
 291:       return 1;
 292:     if (name == "__DATA")
 293:       return 2;
 294:     if (name == "__LINKEDIT")
 295:       return 4;
 296:     return 3;
 297:   };
 298: 
 299:   std::string_view x = a->cmd.get_segname();
 300:   std::string_view y = b->cmd.get_segname();
 301:   return std::tuple{get_rank(x), x} < std::tuple{get_rank(y), y};
 302: }
 303: 
 304: template <typename E>
 305: static bool compare_chunks(const Chunk<E> *a, const Chunk<E> *b) {
 306:   assert(a->hdr.get_segname() == b->hdr.get_segname());
 307: 
 308:   auto is_bss = [](const Chunk<E> *x) {
 309:     return x->hdr.type == S_ZEROFILL || x->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL;
 310:   };
 311: 
 312:   if (is_bss(a) != is_bss(b))
 313:     return !is_bss(a);
 314: 
 315:   static const std::string_view rank[] = {
 316:     // __TEXT
 317:     "__mach_header",
 318:     "__stubs",
 319:     "__text",
 320:     "__stub_helper",
 321:     "__gcc_except_tab",
 322:     "__cstring",
 323:     "__eh_frame",
 324:     "__unwind_info",
 325:     // __DATA_CONST
 326:     "__got",
 327:     "__const",
 328:     // __DATA
 329:     "__mod_init_func",
 330:     "__la_symbol_ptr",
 331:     "__thread_ptrs",
 332:     "__data",
 333:     "__objc_imageinfo",
 334:     "__thread_vars",
 335:     "__thread_ptr",
 336:     "__thread_data",
 337:     "__thread_bss",
 338:     "__common",
 339:     "__bss",
 340:     // __LINKEDIT
 341:     "__rebase",
 342:     "__binding",
 343:     "__weak_binding",
 344:     "__lazy_binding",
 345:     "__chainfixups",
 346:     "__export",
 347:     "__func_starts",
 348:     "__data_in_code",
 349:     "__symbol_table",
 350:     "__ind_sym_tab",
 351:     "__string_table",
 352:     "__code_signature",
 353:   };
 354: 
 355:   auto get_rank = [](std::string_view name) {
 356:     i64 i = 0;
 357:     for (; i < sizeof(rank) / sizeof(rank[0]); i++)
 358:       if (name == rank[i])
 359:         return i;
 360:     return i;
 361:   };
 362: 
 363:   std::string_view x = a->hdr.get_sectname();
 364:   std::string_view y = b->hdr.get_sectname();
 365:   return std::tuple{get_rank(x), x} < std::tuple{get_rank(y), y};
 366: }
 367: 
 368: template <typename E>
 369: static Chunk<E> *find_section(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view segname,
 370:                               std::string_view sectname) {
 371:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 372:     if (chunk->hdr.match(segname, sectname))
 373:       return chunk;
 374:   return nullptr;
 375: }
 376: 
 377: template <typename E>
 378: static void claim_unresolved_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 379:   Timer t(ctx, "claim_unresolved_symbols");
 380: 
 381:   // Handle -U
 382:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.U) {
 383:     Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, name);
 384:     if (!sym->file) {
 385:       sym->file = ctx.internal_obj;
 386:       sym->visibility = SCOPE_GLOBAL;
 387:       sym->is_imported = true;
 388:       sym->is_common = false;
 389:       sym->is_weak = true;
 390:       sym->is_tlv = false;
 391:       sym->subsec = nullptr;
 392:       sym->value = 0;
 393:       ctx.internal_obj->syms.push_back(sym);
 394:     }
 395:   }
 396: 
 397:   // Find all undefined symbols
 398:   std::vector<std::vector<Symbol<E> *>> msgsend_syms(ctx.objs.size());
 399:   std::vector<std::vector<Symbol<E> *>> other_syms(ctx.objs.size());
 400: 
 401:   auto is_null = [](InputFile<E> **ptr) {
 402:     return __atomic_load_n(ptr, __ATOMIC_RELAXED) == nullptr;
 403:   };
 404: 
 405:   auto compare_exchange = [](InputFile<E> **ptr, InputFile<E> *desired) {
 406:     InputFile<E> *expected = nullptr;
 407:     return __atomic_compare_exchange_n(ptr, &expected, desired, false,
 408:                                        __ATOMIC_RELAXED, __ATOMIC_RELAXED);
 409:   };
 410: 
 411:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 412:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.objs[i]->syms) {
 413:       InputFile<E> *expected = nullptr;
 414:       if (is_null(&sym->file) &&
 415:           (sym->name.starts_with("_objc_msgSend$") || !ctx.arg.undefined_error) &&
 416:           compare_exchange(&sym->file, ctx.internal_obj)) {
 417:         if (sym->name.starts_with("_objc_msgSend$")) {
 418:           msgsend_syms[i].push_back(sym);
 419:         } else {
 420:           other_syms[i].push_back(sym);
 421:           sym->is_imported = true;
 422:         }
 423:       }
 424:     }
 425:   });
 426: 
 427:   auto less = [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) { return a->name < b->name; };
 428: 
 429:   // _objc_msgSend is the underlying function of the Objective-C's dynamic
 430:   // message dispatching. The function takes an receiver object and an
 431:   // interned method name string as the first and the second arguments,
 432:   // respectively, along with the arguments for the method.
 433:   //
 434:   // Since Xcode 14, Apple's clang no longer directly generate machine
 435:   // code to call _objc_msgSend but instead generate machine code call to
 436:   // _objc_msgSend$foo where foo is the receiver's class name. It is now
 437:   // the linker's responsibility to detect a call to such symbol and
 438:   // generate stub machine code to call _objc_msgSend with appropriate
 439:   // arguments.
 440:   //
 441:   // The stub code is created in the `__objc_stubs` section.
 442:   //
 443:   // Apple did it as a size optimization. Since the code stub is now
 444:   // shared among functions that call the same class with the same
 445:   // message, it can reduce the output file size.
 446:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> msgsend_syms2 = flatten(msgsend_syms);
 447:   tbb::parallel_sort(msgsend_syms2.begin(), msgsend_syms2.end(), less);
 448: 
 449:   if (!msgsend_syms2.empty()) {
 450:     ctx.objc_stubs.reset(new ObjcStubsSection<E>(ctx));
 451:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : msgsend_syms2)
 452:       ctx.internal_obj->add_msgsend_symbol(ctx, *sym);
 453:   }
 454: 
 455:   // Promote remaining undefined symbols to dynamic symbols if
 456:   // -undefined [ warning | suppress | dynamic_lookup ] was given.
 457:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> other_syms2 = flatten(other_syms);
 458:   tbb::parallel_sort(other_syms2.begin(), other_syms2.end(), less);
 459:   append(ctx.internal_obj->syms, other_syms2);
 460: }
 461: 
 462: template <typename E>
 463: static void create_synthetic_chunks(Context<E> &ctx) {
 464:   Timer t(ctx, "create_synthetic_chunks");
 465: 
 466:   // Create symbol import tables.
 467:   if (ctx.arg.fixup_chains) {
 468:     ctx.chained_fixups.reset(new ChainedFixupsSection<E>(ctx));
 469:   } else {
 470:     ctx.rebase.reset(new RebaseSection<E>(ctx));
 471:     ctx.bind.reset(new BindSection<E>(ctx));
 472:     ctx.stub_helper.reset(new StubHelperSection<E>(ctx));
 473:     ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr.reset(new LazySymbolPtrSection<E>(ctx));
 474:     ctx.lazy_bind.reset(new LazyBindSection<E>(ctx));
 475:   }
 476: 
 477:   // Create a __DATA,__objc_imageinfo section.
 478:   ctx.image_info = ObjcImageInfoSection<E>::create(ctx);
 479: 
 480:   // Create a __LINKEDIT,__func_starts section.
 481:   if (ctx.arg.function_starts)
 482:     ctx.function_starts.reset(new FunctionStartsSection(ctx));
 483: 
 484:   // Create a __LINKEDIT,__data_in_code section.
 485:   if (ctx.arg.data_in_code_info)
 486:     ctx.data_in_code.reset(new DataInCodeSection(ctx));
 487: 
 488:   // Create a __TEXT,__init_offsets section.
 489:   if (ctx.arg.init_offsets)
 490:     ctx.init_offsets.reset(new InitOffsetsSection(ctx));
 491: 
 492:   // Handle -sectcreate
 493:   for (SectCreateOption arg : ctx.arg.sectcreate) {
 494:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
 495:       MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, std::string(arg.filename));
 496:     new SectCreateSection<E>(ctx, arg.segname, arg.sectname, mf->get_contents());
 497:   }
 498: 
 499:   // We add subsections specified by -order_file to output sections.
 500:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.order_file)
 501:     if (Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, name); sym->file)
 502:       if (Subsection<E> *subsec = sym->subsec)
 503:         if (!subsec->added_to_osec)
 504:           subsec->isec->osec.add_subsec(subsec);
 505: 
 506:   // Add remaining subsections to output sections.
 507:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
 508:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : file->subsections)
 509:       if (!subsec->added_to_osec)
 510:         subsec->isec->osec.add_subsec(subsec);
 511: 
 512:   // Add output sections to segments.
 513:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 514:     if (chunk != ctx.data)
 515:       if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
 516:         if (osec->members.empty())
 517:           continue;
 518: 
 519:     chunk->seg->chunks.push_back(chunk);
 520:   }
 521: 
 522:   // Handle -add_empty_section
 523:   for (AddEmptySectionOption &opt : ctx.arg.add_empty_section) {
 524:     if (!find_section(ctx, opt.segname, opt.sectname)) {
 525:       OutputSegment<E> *seg = OutputSegment<E>::get_instance(ctx, opt.segname);
 526:       Chunk<E> *sec = new SectCreateSection<E>(ctx, opt.segname, opt.sectname, {});
 527:       seg->chunks.push_back(sec);
 528:     }
 529:   }
 530: 
 531:   // Even though redundant, section headers have its containing segments name
 532:   // in Mach-O.
 533:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 534:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
 535:       if (!chunk->is_hidden)
 536:         chunk->hdr.set_segname(seg->cmd.segname);
 537: 
 538:   // Sort segments and output sections.
 539:   sort(ctx.segments, compare_segments<E>);
 540: 
 541:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 542:     sort(seg->chunks, compare_chunks<E>);
 543: }
 544: 
 545: // Merge S_CSTRING_LITERALS or S_{4,8,16}BYTE_LITERALS subsections by contents.
 546: template <typename E>
 547: static void uniquify_literals(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec) {
 548:   Timer t(ctx, "uniquify_literals " + std::string(osec.hdr.get_sectname()));
 549: 
 550:   struct Entry {
 551:     Entry(Subsection<E> *subsec) : owner(subsec) {}
 552:     Entry(const Entry &other) = default;
 553: 
 554:     Atomic<Subsection<E> *> owner = nullptr;
 555:     Atomic<u8> p2align = 0;
 556:   };
 557: 
 558:   struct SubsecRef {
 559:     Subsection<E> *subsec = nullptr;
 560:     u64 hash = 0;
 561:     Entry *ent = nullptr;
 562:   };
 563: 
 564:   std::vector<SubsecRef> vec(osec.members.size());
 565: 
 566:   // Estimate the number of unique strings.
 567:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<HyperLogLog> estimators;
 568: 
 569:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)osec.members.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 570:     Subsection<E> *subsec = osec.members[i];
 571:     u64 h = hash_string(subsec->get_contents());
 572:     vec[i].subsec = subsec;
 573:     vec[i].hash = h;
 574:     estimators.local().insert(h);
 575:   });
 576: 
 577:   HyperLogLog estimator;
 578:   for (HyperLogLog &e : estimators)
 579:     estimator.merge(e);
 580: 
 581:   // Create a hash map large enough to hold all strings.
 582:   ConcurrentMap<Entry> map(estimator.get_cardinality() * 3 / 2);
 583: 
 584:   // Insert all strings into the hash table.
 585:   tbb::parallel_for_each(vec, [&](SubsecRef &ref) {
 586:     if (!ref.subsec)
 587:       return;
 588: 
 589:     std::string_view key = ref.subsec->get_contents();
 590:     ref.ent = map.insert(key, ref.hash, {ref.subsec}).first;
 591: 
 592:     Subsection<E> *existing = ref.ent->owner;
 593:     while (existing->isec->file.priority < ref.subsec->isec->file.priority &&
 594:            !ref.ent->owner.compare_exchange_weak(existing, ref.subsec,
 595:                                                  std::memory_order_relaxed));
 596: 
 597:     update_maximum(ref.ent->p2align, ref.subsec->p2align.load());
 598:   });
 599: 
 600:   // Decide who will become the owner for each subsection.
 601:   tbb::parallel_for_each(vec, [&](SubsecRef &ref) {
 602:     if (ref.subsec && ref.subsec != ref.ent->owner) {
 603:       ref.subsec->replacer = ref.ent->owner;
 604:       ref.subsec->is_replaced = true;
 605:     }
 606:   });
 607: 
 608:   static Counter counter("num_merged_strings");
 609:   counter += std::erase_if(osec.members, [](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 610:     return subsec->is_replaced;
 611:   });
 612: }
 613: 
 614: // Merge S_LITERAL_POINTERS subsections such as __DATA,__objc_selrefs
 615: // by contents.
 616: //
 617: // Each S_LITERAL_POINTERS subsection is 8 bytes long and contains a
 618: // single relocation record. We merge two subsections if they contain
 619: // the same relcoation.
 620: template <typename E>
 621: static void uniquify_literal_pointers(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec) {
 622:   Timer t(ctx, "uniquify_literal_pointers");
 623: 
 624:   auto get_target = [](Relocation<E> &r) -> Subsection<E> * {
 625:     Subsection<E> *subsec = r.sym() ? r.sym()->subsec : r.subsec();
 626:     if (!subsec)
 627:       return nullptr;
 628:     return subsec->is_replaced ? subsec->replacer : subsec;
 629:   };
 630: 
 631:   std::unordered_map<Subsection<E> *, Subsection<E> *> map;
 632: 
 633:   for (Subsection<E> *subsec : osec.members) {
 634:     assert(subsec->input_size == sizeof(Word<E>));
 635:     std::span<Relocation<E>> rels = subsec->get_rels();
 636: 
 637:     if (rels.size() == 1) {
 638:       if (Subsection<E> *target = get_target(rels[0])) {
 639:         auto [it, inserted] = map.insert({target, subsec});
 640:         if (!inserted) {
 641:           subsec->replacer = it->second;
 642:           subsec->is_replaced = true;
 643:         }
 644:       }
 645:     }
 646:   }
 647: 
 648:   static Counter counter("num_merged_literal_pointers");
 649:   counter += std::erase_if(osec.members, [](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 650:     return subsec->is_replaced;
 651:   });
 652: }
 653: 
 654: template <typename E>
 655: static void merge_mergeable_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 656:   Timer t(ctx, "merge_mergeable_sections");
 657: 
 658:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 659:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec()) {
 660:       switch (chunk->hdr.type) {
 661:       case S_CSTRING_LITERALS:
 662:       case S_4BYTE_LITERALS:
 663:       case S_8BYTE_LITERALS:
 664:       case S_16BYTE_LITERALS:
 665:         uniquify_literals(ctx, *osec);
 666:         break;
 667:       }
 668:     }
 669:   }
 670: 
 671:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
 672:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
 673:       if (chunk->hdr.type == S_LITERAL_POINTERS)
 674:         uniquify_literal_pointers(ctx, *osec);
 675: 
 676:   // Rewrite relocations and symbols.
 677:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 678:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections)
 679:       if (isec)
 680:         for (Relocation<E> &r : isec->rels)
 681:           if (r.subsec() && r.subsec()->is_replaced)
 682:             r.target = r.subsec()->replacer;
 683:   });
 684: 
 685:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> files;
 686:   append(files, ctx.objs);
 687:   append(files, ctx.dylibs);
 688: 
 689:   tbb::parallel_for_each(files, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
 690:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
 691:       if (sym && sym->file == file && sym->subsec && sym->subsec->is_replaced)
 692:         sym->subsec = sym->subsec->replacer;
 693:   });
 694: 
 695:   // Remove deduplicated subsections from each file's subsection vector.
 696:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 697:     std::erase_if(file->subsections, [](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 698:       return subsec->is_replaced;
 699:     });
 700:   });
 701: }
 702: 
 703: template <typename E>
 704: static void scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
 705:   Timer t(ctx, "scan_relocations");
 706: 
 707:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 708:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : file->subsections) {
 709:       subsec->scan_relocations(ctx);
 710:       for (UnwindRecord<E> &rec : subsec->get_unwind_records())
 711:         if (rec.personality)
 712:           rec.personality->flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
 713:     }
 714: 
 715:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : file->cies)
 716:       if (cie->personality)
 717:         cie->personality->flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
 718:   });
 719: 
 720:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> files;
 721:   append(files, ctx.objs);
 722:   append(files, ctx.dylibs);
 723: 
 724:   std::vector<std::vector<Symbol<E> *>> vec(files.size());
 725: 
 726:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)files.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 727:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : files[i]->syms)
 728:       if (sym && sym->file == files[i] && sym->flags)
 729:         vec[i].push_back(sym);
 730:   });
 731: 
 732:   for (std::span<Symbol<E> *> syms : vec) {
 733:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : syms) {
 734:       if (sym->flags & NEEDS_GOT)
 735:         ctx.got.add(ctx, sym);
 736: 
 737:       if (sym->flags & NEEDS_STUB) {
 738:         if (ctx.arg.bind_at_load || ctx.arg.fixup_chains)
 739:           ctx.got.add(ctx, sym);
 740:         ctx.stubs.add(ctx, sym);
 741:       }
 742: 
 743:       if (sym->flags & NEEDS_THREAD_PTR)
 744:         ctx.thread_ptrs.add(ctx, sym);
 745: 
 746:       sym->flags = 0;
 747:     }
 748:   }
 749: }
 750: 
 751: template <typename E>
 752: static i64 assign_offsets(Context<E> &ctx) {
 753:   Timer t(ctx, "assign_offsets");
 754: 
 755:   i64 sect_idx = 1;
 756:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 757:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
 758:       if (!chunk->is_hidden)
 759:         chunk->sect_idx = sect_idx++;
 760: 
 761:   i64 fileoff = 0;
 762:   i64 vmaddr = ctx.arg.pagezero_size;
 763: 
 764:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments) {
 765:     seg->set_offset(ctx, fileoff, vmaddr);
 766:     fileoff += seg->cmd.filesize;
 767:     vmaddr += seg->cmd.vmsize;
 768:   }
 769:   return fileoff;
 770: }
 771: 
 772: // An address of a symbol of type S_THREAD_LOCAL_VARIABLES is computed
 773: // as a relative address to the beginning of the first thread-local
 774: // section. This function finds the beginning address.
 775: template <typename E>
 776: static u64 get_tls_begin(Context<E> &ctx) {
 777:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 778:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
 779:       if (chunk->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_REGULAR ||
 780:           chunk->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL)
 781:         return chunk->hdr.addr;
 782:   return 0;
 783: }
 784: 
 785: template <typename E>
 786: static void fix_synthetic_symbol_values(Context<E> &ctx) {
 787:   ctx.__dyld_private->value = ctx.data->hdr.addr;
 788:   ctx.__mh_dylib_header->value = ctx.data->hdr.addr;
 789:   ctx.__mh_bundle_header->value = ctx.data->hdr.addr;
 790:   ctx.___dso_handle->value = ctx.data->hdr.addr;
 791: 
 792:   auto find_segment = [&](std::string_view name) -> SegmentCommand<E> * {
 793:     for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 794:       if (seg->cmd.get_segname() == name)
 795:         return &seg->cmd;
 796:     return nullptr;
 797:   };
 798: 
 799:   auto find_section = [&](std::string_view name) -> MachSection<E> * {
 800:     size_t pos = name.find('$');
 801:     if (pos == name.npos)
 802:       return nullptr;
 803: 
 804:     std::string_view segname = name.substr(0, pos);
 805:     std::string_view sectname = name.substr(pos + 1);
 806: 
 807:     for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 808:       for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
 809:         if (chunk->hdr.match(segname, sectname))
 810:           return &chunk->hdr;
 811:     return nullptr;
 812:   };
 813: 
 814:   i64 objc_stubs_offset = 0;
 815: 
 816:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.internal_obj->syms) {
 817:     std::string_view name = sym->name;
 818: 
 819:     if (remove_prefix(name, "segment$start$")) {
 820:       sym->value = ctx.text->hdr.addr;
 821:       if (SegmentCommand<E> *cmd = find_segment(name))
 822:         sym->value = cmd->vmaddr;
 823:       continue;
 824:     }
 825: 
 826:     if (remove_prefix(name, "segment$end$")) {
 827:       sym->value = ctx.text->hdr.addr;
 828:       if (SegmentCommand<E> *cmd = find_segment(name))
 829:         sym->value = cmd->vmaddr + cmd->vmsize;
 830:       continue;
 831:     }
 832: 
 833:     if (remove_prefix(name, "section$start$")) {
 834:       sym->value = ctx.text->hdr.addr;
 835:       if (MachSection<E> *hdr = find_section(name))
 836:         sym->value = hdr->addr;
 837:       continue;
 838:     }
 839: 
 840:     if (remove_prefix(name, "section$end$")) {
 841:       sym->value = ctx.text->hdr.addr;
 842:       if (MachSection<E> *hdr = find_section(name))
 843:         sym->value = hdr->addr + hdr->size;
 844:       continue;
 845:     }
 846: 
 847:     if (name.starts_with("_objc_msgSend$")) {
 848:       sym->value = ctx.objc_stubs->hdr.addr + objc_stubs_offset;
 849:       objc_stubs_offset += ObjcStubsSection<E>::ENTRY_SIZE;
 850:       continue;
 851:     }
 852:   }
 853: }
 854: 
 855: template <typename E>
 856: static void copy_sections_to_output_file(Context<E> &ctx) {
 857:   Timer t(ctx, "copy_sections_to_output_file");
 858: 
 859:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.segments,
 860:                          [&](std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg) {
 861:     Timer t2(ctx, std::string(seg->cmd.get_segname()), &t);
 862: 
 863:     // Fill text segment paddings with single-byte NOP instructions so
 864:     // that otool wouldn't out-of-sync when disassembling an output file.
 865:     // Do this only for x86-64 because ARM64 instructions are always 4
 866:     // bytes long.
 867:     if constexpr (is_x86<E>)
 868:       if (seg->cmd.get_segname() == "__TEXT")
 869:         memset(ctx.buf + seg->cmd.fileoff, 0x90, seg->cmd.filesize);
 870: 
 871:     tbb::parallel_for_each(seg->chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *sec) {
 872:       if (sec->hdr.type != S_ZEROFILL) {
 873:         Timer t3(ctx, std::string(sec->hdr.get_sectname()), &t2);
 874:         sec->copy_buf(ctx);
 875:       }
 876:     });
 877:   });
 878: }
 879: 
 880: template <typename E>
 881: static void compute_uuid(Context<E> &ctx) {
 882:   Timer t(ctx, "copy_sections_to_output_file");
 883: 
 884:   // Compute a markle tree of height two.
 885:   i64 filesize = ctx.output_file->filesize;
 886:   i64 shard_size = 4096 * 1024;
 887:   i64 num_shards = align_to(filesize, shard_size) / shard_size;
 888:   std::vector<u8> shards(num_shards * SHA256_SIZE);
 889: 
 890:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, num_shards, [&](i64 i) {
 891:     u8 *begin = ctx.buf + shard_size * i;
 892:     u8 *end = (i == num_shards - 1) ? ctx.buf + filesize : begin + shard_size;
 893:     sha256_hash(begin, end - begin, shards.data() + i * SHA256_SIZE);
 894:   });
 895: 
 896:   u8 buf[SHA256_SIZE];
 897:   sha256_hash(shards.data(), shards.size(), buf);
 898:   memcpy(ctx.uuid, buf, 16);
 899:   ctx.mach_hdr.copy_buf(ctx);
 900: }
 901: 
 902: template <typename E>
 903: static MappedFile<Context<E>> *
 904: strip_universal_header(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
 905:   FatHeader &hdr = *(FatHeader *)mf->data;
 906:   assert(hdr.magic == FAT_MAGIC);
 907: 
 908:   FatArch *arch = (FatArch *)(mf->data + sizeof(hdr));
 909:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.nfat_arch; i++)
 910:     if (arch[i].cputype == E::cputype)
 911:       return mf->slice(ctx, mf->name, arch[i].offset, arch[i].size);
 912:   Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": fat file contains no matching file";
 913: }
 914: 
 915: template <typename E>
 916: static void read_file(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
 917:   if (get_file_type(ctx, mf) == FileType::MACH_UNIVERSAL)
 918:     mf = strip_universal_header(ctx, mf);
 919: 
 920:   switch (get_file_type(ctx, mf)) {
 921:   case FileType::TAPI:
 922:   case FileType::MACH_DYLIB:
 923:   case FileType::MACH_EXE: {
 924:     DylibFile<E> *file = DylibFile<E>::create(ctx, mf);
 925:     ctx.tg.run([file, &ctx] { file->parse(ctx); });
 926:     ctx.dylibs.push_back(file);
 927:     break;
 928:   }
 929:   case FileType::MACH_OBJ:
 930:   case FileType::LLVM_BITCODE: {
 931:     ObjectFile<E> *file = ObjectFile<E>::create(ctx, mf, "");
 932:     ctx.tg.run([file, &ctx] { file->parse(ctx); });
 933:     ctx.objs.push_back(file);
 934:     break;
 935:   }
 936:   case FileType::AR:
 937:     for (MappedFile<Context<E>> *child : read_archive_members(ctx, mf)) {
 938:       if (get_file_type(ctx, child) == FileType::MACH_OBJ) {
 939:         ObjectFile<E> *file = ObjectFile<E>::create(ctx, child, mf->name);
 940:         ctx.tg.run([file, &ctx] { file->parse(ctx); });
 941:         ctx.objs.push_back(file);
 942:       }
 943:     }
 944:     break;
 945:   default:
 946:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": unknown file type";
 947:     break;
 948:   }
 949: }
 950: 
 951: template <typename E>
 952: static std::vector<std::string>
 953: read_filelist(Context<E> &ctx, std::string arg) {
 954:   std::string path;
 955:   std::string dir;
 956: 
 957:   if (size_t pos = arg.find(','); pos != arg.npos) {
 958:     path = arg.substr(0, pos);
 959:     dir = arg.substr(pos + 1) + "/";
 960:   } else {
 961:     path = arg;
 962:   }
 963: 
 964:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path);
 965:   if (!mf)
 966:     Fatal(ctx) << "-filepath: cannot open " << path;
 967: 
 968:   std::vector<std::string> vec;
 969:   for (std::string_view str = mf->get_contents(); !str.empty();) {
 970:     std::string_view path;
 971:     std::tie(path, str) = split_string(str, '\n');
 972:     vec.push_back(path_clean(dir + std::string(path)));
 973:   }
 974:   return vec;
 975: }
 976: 
 977: template <typename E>
 978: static bool has_dylib(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view path) {
 979:   for (DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
 980:     if (file->install_name == path)
 981:       return true;
 982:   return false;
 983: }
 984: 
 985: template <typename E>
 986: static void read_input_files(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<std::string> args) {
 987:   Timer t(ctx, "read_input_files");
 988: 
 989:   std::unordered_set<std::string> libs;
 990:   std::unordered_set<std::string> frameworks;
 991:   std::unordered_map<std::string, DylibFile<E> *> lib_dylibs;
 992:   std::unordered_map<std::string, DylibFile<E> *> framework_dylibs;
 993: 
 994:   auto search = [&](std::vector<std::string> names) -> MappedFile<Context<E>> * {
 995:     for (std::string dir : ctx.arg.library_paths) {
 996:       for (std::string name : names) {
 997:         std::string path = dir + "/lib" + name;
 998:         if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path))
 999:           return mf;
1000:         ctx.missing_files.insert(path);
1001:       }
1002:     }
1003:     return nullptr;
1004:   };
1005: 
1006:   auto find_library = [&](std::string name) -> MappedFile<Context<E>> * {
1007:     // -search_paths_first
1008:     if (ctx.arg.search_paths_first)
1009:       return search({name + ".tbd", name + ".dylib", name + ".a"});
1010: 
1011:     // -search_dylibs_first
1012:     if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = search({name + ".tbd", name + ".dylib"}))
1013:       return mf;
1014:     return search({name + ".a"});
1015:   };
1016: 
1017:   auto read_library = [&](std::string name) {
1018:     if (!libs.insert(name).second) {
1019:       if (ctx.reader.needed)
1020:         if (auto it = lib_dylibs.find(name); it != lib_dylibs.end())
1021:           it->second->is_alive = true;
1022:       return;
1023:     }
1024: 
1025:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_library(name);
1026:     if (!mf)
1027:       Fatal(ctx) << "library not found: -l" << name;
1028: 
1029:     i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1030:     read_file(ctx, mf);
1031: 
1032:     if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1033:       lib_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1034:   };
1035: 
1036:   auto read_bundle = [&](std::string name) {
1037:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, name);
1038:     if (!mf)
1039:       Fatal(ctx) << "-bundle_loader: cannot open " << name;
1040:     read_file(ctx, mf);
1041:   };
1042: 
1043:   auto find_framework = [&](std::string name) -> MappedFile<Context<E>> * {
1044:     std::string suffix;
1045:     std::tie(name, suffix) = split_string(name, ',');
1046: 
1047:     for (std::string path : ctx.arg.framework_paths) {
1048:       path = get_realpath(path + "/" + name + ".framework/" + name);
1049: 
1050:       if (!suffix.empty())
1051:         if (auto *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path + suffix))
1052:           return mf;
1053: 
1054:       if (auto *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path + ".tbd"))
1055:         return mf;
1056: 
1057:       if (auto *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path))
1058:         return mf;
1059:     }
1060:     return nullptr;
1061:   };
1062: 
1063:   auto read_framework = [&](std::string name) {
1064:     if (!frameworks.insert(name).second) {
1065:       if (ctx.reader.needed)
1066:         if (auto it = framework_dylibs.find(name); it != framework_dylibs.end())
1067:           it->second->is_alive = true;
1068:       return;
1069:     }
1070: 
1071:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_framework(name);
1072:     if (!mf)
1073:       Fatal(ctx) << "-framework not found: " << name;
1074: 
1075:     i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1076:     read_file(ctx, mf);
1077: 
1078:     if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1079:       framework_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1080:   };
1081: 
1082:   while (!args.empty()) {
1083:     const std::string &opt = args[0];
1084:     args = args.subspan(1);
1085: 
1086:     if (!opt.starts_with('-')) {
1087:       read_file(ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, opt));
1088:       continue;
1089:     }
1090: 
1091:     if (opt == "-all_load") {
1092:       ctx.reader.all_load = true;
1093:       continue;
1094:     }
1095: 
1096:     if (opt == "-noall_load") {
1097:       ctx.reader.all_load = false;
1098:       continue;
1099:     }
1100: 
1101:     if (args.empty())
1102:       Fatal(ctx) << opt << ": missing argument";
1103: 
1104:     const std::string &arg = args[0];
1105:     args = args.subspan(1);
1106:     ReaderContext orig = ctx.reader;
1107: 
1108:     if (opt == "-filelist") {
1109:       for (std::string &path : read_filelist(ctx, arg)) {
1110:         MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path);
1111:         if (!mf)
1112:           Fatal(ctx) << "-filelist " << arg << ": cannot open file: " << path;
1113:         read_file(ctx, mf);
1114:       }
1115:     } else if (opt == "-force_load") {
1116:       ctx.reader.all_load = true;
1117:       read_file(ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, arg));
1118:     } else if (opt == "-framework") {
1119:       read_framework(arg);
1120:     } else if (opt == "-needed_framework" || opt == "-needed-framework") {
1121:       ctx.reader.needed = true;
1122:       read_framework(arg);
1123:     } else if (opt == "-weak_framework") {
1124:       ctx.reader.weak = true;
1125:       read_framework(arg);
1126:     } else if (opt == "-l") {
1127:       read_library(arg);
1128:     } else if (opt == "-needed-l") {
1129:       ctx.reader.needed = true;
1130:       read_library(arg);
1131:     } else if (opt == "-hidden-l") {
1132:       ctx.reader.hidden = true;
1133:       read_library(arg);
1134:     } else if (opt == "-weak-l") {
1135:       ctx.reader.weak = true;
1136:       read_library(arg);
1137:     } else if (opt == "-reexport-l") {
1138:       ctx.reader.reexport = true;
1139:       read_library(arg);
1140:     } else if (opt == "-reexport_library") {
1141:       ctx.reader.reexport = true;
1142:       read_file(ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, arg));
1143:     } else {
1144:       unreachable();
1145:     }
1146: 
1147:     ctx.reader = orig;
1148:   }
1149: 
1150:   // With -bundle_loader, we can import symbols from a main executable.
1151:   if (!ctx.arg.bundle_loader.empty())
1152:     read_bundle(ctx.arg.bundle_loader);
1153: 
1154:   // An object file can contain linker directives to load other object
1155:   // files or libraries, so process them if any. We accept the common
1156:   // autolink forms and warn for the rest instead of hard-failing.
1157:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.objs.size(); i++) {
1158:     ObjectFile<E> *file = ctx.objs[i];
1159:     std::vector<std::string> opts = file->get_linker_options(ctx);
1160: 
1161:     ReaderContext orig = ctx.reader;
1162: 
1163:     auto warn_bad_linker_option = [&](std::string_view opt) {
1164:       Warn(ctx) << *file << ": ignoring unsupported LC_LINKER_OPTION command: "
1165:                 << opt;
1166:     };
1167: 
1168:     auto warn_malformed_linker_option = [&](std::string_view opt) {
1169:       Warn(ctx) << *file << ": ignoring malformed LC_LINKER_OPTION command: "
1170:                 << opt;
1171:     };
1172: 
1173:     for (i64 j = 0; j < opts.size();) {
1174:       ctx.reader = orig;
1175:       ctx.reader.implicit = true;
1176: 
1177:       std::string_view opt = opts[j];
1178: 
1179:       if (opt == "-framework" || opt == "-needed_framework" ||
1180:           opt == "-needed-framework") {
1181:         if (j + 1 >= opts.size()) {
1182:           warn_malformed_linker_option(opt);
1183:           j++;
1184:           continue;
1185:         }
1186: 
1187:         if (opt != "-framework")
1188:           ctx.reader.needed = true;
1189: 
1190:         if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_framework(opts[j + 1])) {
1191:           std::string name = opts[j + 1];
1192: 
1193:           if (!frameworks.insert(name).second) {
1194:             if (ctx.reader.needed)
1195:               if (auto it = framework_dylibs.find(name); it != framework_dylibs.end())
1196:                 it->second->is_alive = true;
1197:           } else {
1198:             i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1199:             read_file(ctx, mf);
1200:             if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1201:               framework_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1202:           }
1203:         }
1204: 
1205:         j += 2;
1206:         continue;
1207:       }
1208: 
1209:       if (opt == "-needed-l" || opt == "-l") {
1210:         if (j + 1 >= opts.size()) {
1211:           warn_malformed_linker_option(opt);
1212:           j++;
1213:           continue;
1214:         }
1215: 
1216:         if (opt == "-needed-l")
1217:           ctx.reader.needed = true;
1218: 
1219:         if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_library(opts[j + 1])) {
1220:           std::string name = opts[j + 1];
1221: 
1222:           if (!libs.insert(name).second) {
1223:             if (ctx.reader.needed)
1224:               if (auto it = lib_dylibs.find(name); it != lib_dylibs.end())
1225:                 it->second->is_alive = true;
1226:           } else {
1227:             i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1228:             read_file(ctx, mf);
1229:             if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1230:               lib_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1231:           }
1232:         }
1233: 
1234:         j += 2;
1235:         continue;
1236:       }
1237: 
1238:       if (opt.starts_with("-needed-l")) {
1239:         if (opt.size() == std::string_view("-needed-l").size()) {
1240:           warn_malformed_linker_option(opt);
1241:           j++;
1242:           continue;
1243:         }
1244: 
1245:         ctx.reader.needed = true;
1246:         std::string name = std::string(opt.substr(std::string_view("-needed-l").size()));
1247: 
1248:         if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_library(name)) {
1249:           if (!libs.insert(name).second) {
1250:             if (auto it = lib_dylibs.find(name); it != lib_dylibs.end())
1251:               it->second->is_alive = true;
1252:           } else {
1253:             i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1254:             read_file(ctx, mf);
1255:             if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1256:               lib_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1257:           }
1258:         }
1259: 
1260:         j++;
1261:         continue;
1262:       }
1263: 
1264:       if (opt.starts_with("-l")) {
1265:         if (opt.size() == std::string_view("-l").size()) {
1266:           warn_malformed_linker_option(opt);
1267:           j++;
1268:           continue;
1269:         }
1270: 
1271:         std::string name = std::string(opt.substr(2));
1272: 
1273:         if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = find_library(name)) {
1274:           if (!libs.insert(name).second) {
1275:             if (ctx.reader.needed)
1276:               if (auto it = lib_dylibs.find(name); it != lib_dylibs.end())
1277:                 it->second->is_alive = true;
1278:           } else {
1279:             i64 num_dylibs = ctx.dylibs.size();
1280:             read_file(ctx, mf);
1281:             if (ctx.dylibs.size() != num_dylibs)
1282:               lib_dylibs.emplace(name, ctx.dylibs.back());
1283:           }
1284:         }
1285: 
1286:         j++;
1287:         continue;
1288:       }
1289: 
1290:       warn_bad_linker_option(opt);
1291:       j++;
1292:     }
1293: 
1294:     ctx.reader = orig;
1295:   }
1296: 
1297:   ctx.tg.wait();
1298: 
1299:   std::unordered_set<std::string_view> hoisted_libs;
1300:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.dylibs.size(); i++)
1301:     for (DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs[i]->hoisted_libs)
1302:       if (hoisted_libs.insert(file->install_name).second)
1303:         ctx.dylibs.push_back(file);
1304: 
1305:   if (ctx.objs.empty())
1306:     Fatal(ctx) << "no input files";
1307: 
1308:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1309:     file->priority = ctx.file_priority++;
1310:   for (DylibFile<E> *dylib : ctx.dylibs)
1311:     dylib->priority = ctx.file_priority++;
1312: 
1313:   for (i64 i = 1; DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
1314:     if (file->dylib_idx != BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_MAIN_EXECUTABLE)
1315:       file->dylib_idx = i++;
1316: }
1317: 
1318: template <typename E>
1319: void print_dependencies(Context<E> &ctx) {
1320:   SyncOut(ctx) <<
1321: R"(# This is an output of the sold linker's --print-dependencies option.
1322: #
1323: # Each line consists of 4 fields, <file1>, <file2>, <symbol-type> and
1324: # <symbol>, separated by tab characters. It indicates that <file1> depends
1325: # on <file2> to use <symbol>. <symbol-type> is either "u" or "w" for
1326: # regular undefined or weak undefined, respectively.
1327: )";
1328: 
1329:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
1330:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections) {
1331:       if (!isec)
1332:         continue;
1333: 
1334:       MachRel *mach_rels = (MachRel *)(file->mf->data + isec->hdr.reloff);
1335:       std::unordered_set<Symbol<E> *> visited;
1336: 
1337:       for (i64 i = 0; i < isec->hdr.nreloc; i++) {
1338:         MachRel &r = mach_rels[i];
1339:         if (!r.is_extern)
1340:           continue;
1341: 
1342:         MachSym<E> &msym = file->mach_syms[r.idx];
1343:         Symbol<E> &sym = *file->syms[r.idx];
1344: 
1345:         if (msym.is_undef() && sym.file && sym.file != file && visited.insert(&sym).second)
1346:           SyncOut(ctx) << *file << '\t' << *sym.file
1347:                        << '\t' << ((msym.desc & N_WEAK_DEF) ? 'w' : 'u')
1348:                        << '\t' << sym;
1349:       }
1350:     }
1351:   }
1352: }
1353: 
1354: template <typename E>
1355: static void write_dependency_info(Context<E> &ctx) {
1356:   static constexpr u8 LINKER_VERSION = 0;
1357:   static constexpr u8 INPUT_FILE = 0x10;
1358:   static constexpr u8 NOT_FOUND_FILE = 0x11;
1359:   static constexpr u8 OUTPUT_FILE = 0x40;
1360: 
1361:   std::ofstream out;
1362:   out.open(std::string(ctx.arg.dependency_info).c_str());
1363:   if (!out.is_open())
1364:     Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << ctx.arg.dependency_info
1365:                << ": " << errno_string();
1366: 
1367:   out << LINKER_VERSION << mold_version << '\0';
1368: 
1369:   std::set<std::string_view> input_files;
1370:   for (std::unique_ptr<MappedFile<Context<E>>> &mf : ctx.mf_pool)
1371:     if (!mf->parent)
1372:       input_files.insert(mf->name);
1373: 
1374:   for (std::string_view s : input_files)
1375:     out << INPUT_FILE << s << '\0';
1376: 
1377:   for (std::string_view s : ctx.missing_files)
1378:     out << NOT_FOUND_FILE << s << '\0';
1379: 
1380:   out << OUTPUT_FILE << ctx.arg.output << '\0';
1381:   out.close();
1382: }
1383: 
1384: template <typename E>
1385: static void print_stats(Context<E> &ctx) {
1386:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
1387:     static Counter subsections("num_subsections");
1388:     subsections += file->subsections.size();
1389: 
1390:     static Counter syms("num_syms");
1391:     syms += file->syms.size();
1392: 
1393:     static Counter rels("num_rels");
1394:     for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : file->sections)
1395:       if (isec)
1396:         rels += isec->rels.size();
1397:   }
1398: 
1399:   static Counter num_objs("num_objs", ctx.objs.size());
1400:   static Counter num_dylibs("num_dylibs", ctx.dylibs.size());
1401: 
1402:   Counter::print();
1403: }
1404: 
1405: template <typename E>
1406: static int redo_main(int argc, char **argv, i64 arch) {
1407:   switch (arch) {
1408:   case CPU_TYPE_ARM64_32:
1409:     return macho_main<ARM64_32>(argc, argv);
1410:   case CPU_TYPE_X86_64:
1411:     return macho_main<X86_64>(argc, argv);
1412:   }
1413:   unreachable();
1414: }
1415: 
1416: template <typename E>
1417: int macho_main(int argc, char **argv) {
1418:   Context<E> ctx;
1419: 
1420:   for (i64 i = 0; i < argc; i++)
1421:     ctx.cmdline_args.push_back(argv[i]);
1422: 
1423:   std::vector<std::string> file_args = parse_nonpositional_args(ctx);
1424: 
1425:   if constexpr (is_arm64<E>)
1426:     if (ctx.arg.arch != E::cputype)
1427:       return redo_main<E>(argc, argv, ctx.arg.arch);
1428: 
1429:   Timer t(ctx, "all");
1430: 
1431:   tbb::global_control tbb_cont(tbb::global_control::max_allowed_parallelism,
1432:                                ctx.arg.thread_count);
1433: 
1434:   if (ctx.arg.adhoc_codesign)
1435:     ctx.code_sig.reset(new CodeSignatureSection<E>(ctx));
1436: 
1437:   read_input_files(ctx, file_args);
1438: 
1439:   // `-ObjC` is an option to load all members of static archive
1440:   // libraries that implement an Objective-C class or category.
1441:   if (ctx.arg.ObjC)
1442:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1443:       if (!file->archive_name.empty() && file->is_objc_object(ctx))
1444:         file->is_alive = true;
1445: 
1446:   resolve_symbols(ctx);
1447: 
1448:   if (ctx.output_type == MH_EXECUTE && !ctx.arg.entry->file)
1449:     Error(ctx) << "undefined entry point symbol: " << *ctx.arg.entry;
1450: 
1451:   create_internal_file(ctx);
1452: 
1453:   // Handle -exported_symbol and -exported_symbols_list
1454:   handle_exported_symbols_list(ctx);
1455: 
1456:   // Handle -unexported_symbol and -unexported_symbols_list
1457:   handle_unexported_symbols_list(ctx);
1458: 
1459:   if (ctx.arg.trace) {
1460:     for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1461:       SyncOut(ctx) << *file;
1462:     for (DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
1463:       SyncOut(ctx) << *file;
1464:   }
1465: 
1466:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1467:     file->convert_common_symbols(ctx);
1468: 
1469:   claim_unresolved_symbols(ctx);
1470: 
1471:   if (ctx.arg.dead_strip)
1472:     dead_strip(ctx);
1473:   else
1474:     remove_unreferenced_subsections(ctx);
1475: 
1476:   if (ctx.arg.print_dependencies)
1477:     print_dependencies(ctx);
1478: 
1479:   create_synthetic_chunks(ctx);
1480:   merge_mergeable_sections(ctx);
1481: 
1482:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1483:     file->check_duplicate_symbols(ctx);
1484: 
1485:   for (SectAlignOption &opt : ctx.arg.sectalign)
1486:     if (Chunk<E> *chunk = find_section(ctx, opt.segname, opt.sectname))
1487:       chunk->hdr.p2align = opt.p2align;
1488: 
1489:   bool has_pagezero_seg = ctx.arg.pagezero_size;
1490:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.segments.size(); i++)
1491:     ctx.segments[i]->seg_idx = (has_pagezero_seg ? i + 1 : i);
1492: 
1493:   compute_import_export(ctx);
1494: 
1495:   scan_relocations(ctx);
1496: 
1497:   i64 output_size = assign_offsets(ctx);
1498:   ctx.tls_begin = get_tls_begin(ctx);
1499:   fix_synthetic_symbol_values(ctx);
1500: 
1501:   ctx.output_file =
1502:     OutputFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, ctx.arg.output, output_size, 0777);
1503:   ctx.buf = ctx.output_file->buf;
1504: 
1505:   copy_sections_to_output_file(ctx);
1506: 
1507:   if (ctx.chained_fixups)
1508:     ctx.chained_fixups->write_fixup_chains(ctx);
1509: 
1510:   if (ctx.code_sig)
1511:     ctx.code_sig->write_signature(ctx);
1512:   else if (ctx.arg.uuid == UUID_HASH)
1513:     compute_uuid(ctx);
1514: 
1515:   ctx.output_file->close(ctx);
1516: 
1517:   if (!ctx.arg.dependency_info.empty())
1518:     write_dependency_info(ctx);
1519: 
1520:   ctx.checkpoint();
1521:   t.stop();
1522: 
1523:   if (ctx.arg.perf)
1524:     print_timer_records(ctx.timer_records);
1525: 
1526:   if (ctx.arg.stats)
1527:     print_stats(ctx);
1528: 
1529:   if (!ctx.arg.map.empty())
1530:     print_map(ctx);
1531: 
1532:   if (ctx.arg.quick_exit) {
1533:     std::cout << std::flush;
1534:     std::cerr << std::flush;
1535:     _exit(0);
1536:   }
1537: 
1538:   return 0;
1539: }
1540: 
1541: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
1542: 
1543: #ifdef MOLD_ARM64
1544: 
1545: extern template int macho_main<ARM64_32>(int, char **);
1546: extern template int macho_main<X86_64>(int, char **);
1547: 
1548: int main(int argc, char **argv) {
1549:   return macho_main<ARM64>(argc, argv);
1550: }
1551: 
1552: #else
1553: 
1554: template int macho_main<E>(int, char **);
1555: 
1556: #endif
1557: }

 35: #include "../third-party/xxhash/xxhash.h"
 36: 
 37: #ifdef NDEBUG
 38: # define unreachable() __builtin_unreachable()
 39: #else
 40: # define unreachable() assert(0 && "unreachable")
 41: #endif
 42: 
 43: // __builtin_assume() is supported only by clang, and [[assume]] is
 44: // available only in C++23, so we use this macro when giving a hint to
 45: // the compiler's optimizer what's true.
 46: #define ASSUME(x) do { if (!(x)) __builtin_unreachable(); } while (0)
 47: 
 48: // This is an assert() that is enabled even in the release build.
 49: #define ASSERT(x)                                       \
 50:   do {                                                  \
 51:     if (!(x)) {                                         \
 52:       std::cerr << "Assertion failed: (" << #x          \
 53:                 << "), function " << __FUNCTION__       \
 54:                 << ", file " << __FILE__                \
 55:                 << ", line " << __LINE__ << ".\n";      \
 56:       std::abort();                                     \
 57:     }                                                   \
 58:   } while (0)
 59: 
 60: inline uint64_t hash_string(std::string_view str) {
 61:   return XXH3_64bits(str.data(), str.size());
 62: }

 71:     return k1 == k2;

106: template <typename Context>
107: class SyncOut {
108: public:
109:   SyncOut(Context &ctx, std::ostream *out = &std::cout) : out(out) {
110:     opt_demangle = ctx.arg.demangle;
111:   }
112: 
113:   ~SyncOut() {
114:     if (out) {
115:       std::scoped_lock lock(mu);
116:       *out << ss.str() << "\n";
117:     }
118:   }
119: 
120:   template <class T> SyncOut &operator<<(T &&val) {
121:     if (out)
122:       ss << std::forward<T>(val);
123:     return *this;
124:   }
125: 
126:   static inline std::mutex mu;
127: 
128: private:
129:   std::ostream *out;
130:   std::stringstream ss;
131: };

133: template <typename Context>
134: static std::string add_color(Context &ctx, std::string msg) {
135:   if (ctx.arg.color_diagnostics)
136:     return "mold: \033[0;1;31m" + msg + ":\033[0m ";
137:   return "mold: " + msg + ": ";
138: }

145:   }
146:

148:     out.~SyncOut();
149:     cleanup();
150:     _exit(1);
151:   }

154:     out << std::forward<T>(val);
155:     return *this;
156:   }

159:   SyncOut<Context> out;

205: //
206: // Atomics
207: //
208: 
209: // This is the same as std::atomic except that the default memory
210: // order is relaxed instead of sequential consistency.
211: template <typename T>
212: struct Atomic : std::atomic<T> {
213:   static constexpr std::memory_order relaxed = std::memory_order_relaxed;
214: 
215:   using std::atomic<T>::atomic;
216: 
217:   Atomic(const Atomic<T> &other) { store(other.load()); }
218: 
219:   Atomic<T> &operator=(const Atomic<T> &other) {
220:     store(other.load());
221:     return *this;
222:   }
223: 
224:   void operator=(T val) { store(val); }
225:   operator T() const { return load(); }
226: 
227:   void store(T val) { std::atomic<T>::store(val, relaxed); }
228:   T load() const { return std::atomic<T>::load(relaxed); }
229:   T exchange(T val) { return std::atomic<T>::exchange(val, relaxed); }
230:   T operator|=(T val) { return std::atomic<T>::fetch_or(val, relaxed); }
231:   T operator++() { return std::atomic<T>::fetch_add(1, relaxed) + 1; }
232:   T operator--() { return std::atomic<T>::fetch_sub(1, relaxed) - 1; }
233:   T operator++(int) { return std::atomic<T>::fetch_add(1, relaxed); }
234:   T operator--(int) { return std::atomic<T>::fetch_sub(1, relaxed); }
235: 
236:   bool test_and_set() {
237:     // A relaxed load + branch (assuming miss) takes only around 20 cycles,
238:     // while an atomic RMW can easily take hundreds on x86. We note that it's
239:     // common that another thread beat us in marking, so doing an optimistic
240:     // early test tends to improve performance in the ~20% ballpark.
241:     return load() || exchange(true);
242:   }
243: };
244: 
245: //
246: // Bit vector
247: //
248: 
249: class BitVector {
250: public:
251:   BitVector() = default;
252:   BitVector(u32 size) : vec((size + 7) / 8) {}
253: 
254:   void resize(u32 size) { vec.resize((size + 7) / 8); }
255:   bool get(u32 idx) const { return vec[idx / 8] & (1 << (idx % 8)); }
256:   void set(u32 idx) { vec[idx / 8] |= 1 << (idx % 8); }
257: 
258: private:
259:   std::vector<u8> vec;
260: };
261: 
262: //
263: // Utility functions
264: //
265: 
266: // Some C++ libraries haven't implemented std::has_single_bit yet.
267: inline bool has_single_bit(u64 val) {
268:   return std::popcount(val) == 1;
269: }
270: 
271: // Some C++ libraries haven't implemented std::bit_ceil yet.
272: inline u64 bit_ceil(u64 val) {
273:   if (has_single_bit(val))
274:     return val;
275:   return 1LL << (64 - std::countl_zero(val));
276: }
277: 
278: inline u64 align_to(u64 val, u64 align) {
279:   if (align == 0)
280:     return val;
281:   assert(has_single_bit(align));
282:   return (val + align - 1) & ~(align - 1);
283: }
284: 
285: inline u64 align_down(u64 val, u64 align) {
286:   assert(has_single_bit(align));
287:   return val & ~(align - 1);
288: }
289: 
290: inline u64 bit(u64 val, i64 pos) {
291:   return (val >> pos) & 1;
292: };
293: 
294: // Returns [hi:lo] bits of val.
295: inline u64 bits(u64 val, u64 hi, u64 lo) {
296:   return (val >> lo) & ((1LL << (hi - lo + 1)) - 1);
297: }
298: 
299: inline i64 sign_extend(u64 val, i64 size) {
300:   return (i64)(val << (63 - size)) >> (63 - size);
301: };
302: 
303: template <typename T, typename Compare = std::less<T>>
304: void update_minimum(std::atomic<T> &atomic, u64 new_val, Compare cmp = {}) {
305:   T old_val = atomic.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
306:   while (cmp(new_val, old_val) &&
307:          !atomic.compare_exchange_weak(old_val, new_val,
308:                                        std::memory_order_relaxed));
309: }
310: 
311: template <typename T, typename Compare = std::less<T>>
312: void update_maximum(std::atomic<T> &atomic, u64 new_val, Compare cmp = {}) {
313:   T old_val = atomic.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
314:   while (cmp(old_val, new_val) &&
315:          !atomic.compare_exchange_weak(old_val, new_val,
316:                                        std::memory_order_relaxed));
317: }
318: 
319: template <typename T, typename U>
320: inline void append(std::vector<T> &vec1, std::vector<U> vec2) {
321:   vec1.insert(vec1.end(), vec2.begin(), vec2.end());
322: }
323: 
324: template <typename T>
325: inline std::vector<T> flatten(std::vector<std::vector<T>> &vec) {
326:   i64 size = 0;
327:   for (std::vector<T> &v : vec)
328:     size += v.size();
329: 
330:   std::vector<T> ret;
331:   ret.reserve(size);
332:   for (std::vector<T> &v : vec)
333:     append(ret, v);
334:   return ret;
335: }

337: template <typename T>
338: inline void sort(T &vec) {
339:   std::stable_sort(vec.begin(), vec.end());
340: }
341: 
342: template <typename T, typename U>
343: inline void sort(T &vec, U less) {
344:   std::stable_sort(vec.begin(), vec.end(), less);
345: }
346: 
347: template <typename T>
348: inline void remove_duplicates(std::vector<T> &vec) {
349:   vec.erase(std::unique(vec.begin(), vec.end()), vec.end());
350: }

352: inline i64 write_string(void *buf, std::string_view str) {
353:   memcpy(buf, str.data(), str.size());
354:   *((u8 *)buf + str.size()) = '\0';
355:   return str.size() + 1;
356: }
357: 
358: template <typename T>
359: inline i64 write_vector(void *buf, const std::vector<T> &vec) {
360:   i64 sz = vec.size() * sizeof(T);
361:   memcpy(buf, vec.data(), sz);
362:   return sz;
363: }
364: 
365: inline void encode_uleb(std::vector<u8> &vec, u64 val) {
366:   do {
367:     u8 byte = val & 0x7f;
368:     val >>= 7;
369:     vec.push_back(val ? (byte | 0x80) : byte);
370:   } while (val);
371: }
372: 
373: inline void encode_sleb(std::vector<u8> &vec, i64 val) {
374:   for (;;) {
375:     u8 byte = val & 0x7f;
376:     val >>= 7;
377: 
378:     bool neg = (byte & 0x40);
379:     if ((val == 0 && !neg) || (val == -1 && neg)) {
380:       vec.push_back(byte);
381:       break;
382:     }
383:     vec.push_back(byte | 0x80);
384:   }
385: }
386: 
387: inline i64 write_uleb(u8 *buf, u64 val) {
388:   i64 i = 0;
389:   do {
390:     u8 byte = val & 0x7f;
391:     val >>= 7;
392:     buf[i++] = val ? (byte | 0x80) : byte;
393:   } while (val);
394:   return i;
395: }
396: 
397: inline u64 read_uleb(u8 *&buf) {
398:   u64 val = 0;
399:   u8 shift = 0;
400:   u8 byte;
401:   do {
402:     byte = *buf++;
403:     val |= (byte & 0x7f) << shift;
404:     shift += 7;
405:   } while (byte & 0x80);
406:   return val;
407: }
408: 
409: inline u64 read_uleb(u8 const*&buf) {
410:   return read_uleb(const_cast<u8 *&>(buf));
411: }
412: 
413: inline u64 read_uleb(std::string_view &str) {
414:   u8 *start = (u8 *)&str[0];
415:   u8 *ptr = start;
416:   u64 val = read_uleb(ptr);
417:   str = str.substr(ptr - start);
418:   return val;
419: }
420: 
421: inline i64 uleb_size(u64 val) {
422: #if __GNUC__
423: #pragma GCC unroll 8
424: #endif
425:   for (int i = 1; i < 9; i++)
426:     if (val < (1LL << (7 * i)))
427:       return i;
428:   return 9;
429: }
430: 
431: template <typename Context>
432: std::string_view save_string(Context &ctx, const std::string &str) {
433:   u8 *buf = new u8[str.size() + 1];
434:   memcpy(buf, str.data(), str.size());
435:   buf[str.size()] = '\0';
436:   ctx.string_pool.push_back(std::unique_ptr<u8[]>(buf));
437:   return {(char *)buf, str.size()};
438: }
439: 
440: inline bool remove_prefix(std::string_view &s, std::string_view prefix) {
441:   if (s.starts_with(prefix)) {
442:     s = s.substr(prefix.size());
443:     return true;
444:   }
445:   return false;
446: }
447: 
448: //
449: // Concurrent Map
450: //
451: 
452: // This is an implementation of a fast concurrent hash map. Unlike
453: // ordinary hash tables, this impl just aborts if it becomes full.
454: // So you need to give a correct estimation of the final size before
455: // using it. We use this hash map to uniquify pieces of data in
456: // mergeable sections.
457: template <typename T>
458: class ConcurrentMap {
459: public:
460:   ConcurrentMap() {}
461: 
462:   ConcurrentMap(i64 nbuckets) {
463:     resize(nbuckets);
464:   }
465: 
466:   ~ConcurrentMap() {
467:     if (keys) {
468:       free((void *)keys);
469:       free((void *)key_sizes);
470:       free((void *)values);
471:     }
472:   }
473: 
474:   void resize(i64 nbuckets) {
475:     this->~ConcurrentMap();
476: 
477:     nbuckets = std::max<i64>(MIN_NBUCKETS, bit_ceil(nbuckets));
478: 
479:     this->nbuckets = nbuckets;
480:     keys = (std::atomic<const char *> *)calloc(nbuckets, sizeof(char *));
481:     key_sizes = (u32 *)malloc(nbuckets * sizeof(u32));
482:     values = (T *)malloc(nbuckets * sizeof(T));
483:   }
484: 
485:   std::pair<T *, bool> insert(std::string_view key, u64 hash, const T &val) {
486:     if (!keys)
487:       return {nullptr, false};
488: 
489:     assert(has_single_bit(nbuckets));
490:     i64 idx = hash & (nbuckets - 1);
491:     i64 retry = 0;
492: 
493:     while (retry < MAX_RETRY) {
494:       const char *ptr = keys[idx].load(std::memory_order_acquire);
495:       if (ptr == marker) {
496:         pause();
497:         continue;
498:       }
499: 
500:       if (ptr == nullptr) {
501:         if (!keys[idx].compare_exchange_weak(ptr, marker,
502:                                              std::memory_order_acquire))
503:           continue;
504:         new (values + idx) T(val);
505:         key_sizes[idx] = key.size();
506:         keys[idx].store(key.data(), std::memory_order_release);
507:         return {values + idx, true};
508:       }
509: 
510:       if (key.size() == key_sizes[idx] &&
511:           memcmp(ptr, key.data(), key_sizes[idx]) == 0)
512:         return {values + idx, false};
513: 
514:       u64 mask = nbuckets / NUM_SHARDS - 1;
515:       idx = (idx & ~mask) | ((idx + 1) & mask);
516:       retry++;
517:     }
518: 
519:     assert(false && "ConcurrentMap is full");
520:     return {nullptr, false};
521:   }
522: 
523:   const char *get_key(i64 idx) {
524:     return keys[idx].load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
525:   }
526: 
527:   static constexpr i64 MIN_NBUCKETS = 2048;
528:   static constexpr i64 NUM_SHARDS = 16;
529:   static constexpr i64 MAX_RETRY = 128;
530: 
531:   i64 nbuckets = 0;
532:   u32 *key_sizes = nullptr;
533:   T *values = nullptr;
534: 
535: private:
536:   static void pause() {
537: #if defined(__x86_64__)
538:     asm volatile("pause");
539: #elif defined(__aarch64__)
540:     asm volatile("yield");
541: #endif
542:   }
543: 
544: private:
545:   std::atomic<const char *> *keys = nullptr;
546:   static constexpr const char *marker = "marker";
547: };
548:

550: // output-file.h
551: //

553: template <typename Context>
554: class OutputFile {
555: public:
556:   static std::unique_ptr<OutputFile<Context>>
557:   open(Context &ctx, std::string path, i64 filesize, i64 perm);
558: 
559:   virtual void close(Context &ctx) = 0;
560:   virtual ~OutputFile() = default;
561: 
562:   u8 *buf = nullptr;
563:   std::string path;
564:   i64 filesize;
565:   bool is_mmapped;
566:   bool is_unmapped = false;
567: 
568: protected:
569:   OutputFile(std::string path, i64 filesize, bool is_mmapped)
570:     : path(path), filesize(filesize), is_mmapped(is_mmapped) {}
571: };

573: //
574: // hyperloglog.cc
575: //
576: 
577: class HyperLogLog {
578: public:
579:   HyperLogLog() : buckets(NBUCKETS) {}
580: 
581:   void insert(u32 hash) {
582:     update_maximum(buckets[hash & (NBUCKETS - 1)], std::countl_zero(hash) + 1);
583:   }
584: 
585:   i64 get_cardinality() const;
586: 
587:   void merge(const HyperLogLog &other) {
588:     for (i64 i = 0; i < NBUCKETS; i++)
589:       update_maximum(buckets[i], other.buckets[i]);
590:   }
591: 
592: private:
593:   static constexpr i64 NBUCKETS = 2048;
594:   static constexpr double ALPHA = 0.79402;
595: 
596:   std::vector<std::atomic_uint8_t> buckets;
597: };

599: //
600: // glob.cc
601: //
602: 
603: class Glob {
604:   typedef enum { STRING, STAR, QUESTION, BRACKET } Kind;
605: 
606:   struct Element {
607:     Element(Kind k) : kind(k) {}
608:     Kind kind;
609:     std::string str;
610:     std::bitset<256> bitset;
611:   };
612: 
613: public:
614:   static std::optional<Glob> compile(std::string_view pat);
615:   bool match(std::string_view str);
616: 
617: private:
618:   Glob(std::vector<Element> &&vec) : elements(vec) {}
619:   static bool do_match(std::string_view str, std::span<Element> elements);
620: 
621:   std::vector<Element> elements;
622: };

624: //
625: // multi-glob.cc
626: //
627: 
628: class MultiGlob {
629: public:
630:   bool add(std::string_view pat, u32 val);
631:   bool empty() const { return strings.empty(); }
632:   std::optional<u32> find(std::string_view str);
633: 
634: private:
635:   struct TrieNode {
636:     u32 value = -1;
637:     TrieNode *suffix_link = nullptr;
638:     std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> children[256];
639:   };
640: 
641:   void compile();
642:   void fix_suffix_links(TrieNode &node);
643:   void fix_values();
644: 
645:   std::vector<std::string> strings;
646:   std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> root;
647:   std::vector<std::pair<Glob, u32>> globs;
648:   std::once_flag once;
649:   bool is_compiled = false;
650: };

656: std::array<u8, 16> get_uuid_v4();
657: 
658: //
659: // filepath.cc
660: //
661: 
662: template <typename T>
663: std::filesystem::path filepath(const T &path) {
664:   return {path, std::filesystem::path::format::generic_format};
665: }
666: 
667: std::string get_realpath(std::string_view path);
668: std::string path_clean(std::string_view path);
669: std::filesystem::path to_abs_path(std::filesystem::path path);

684:   virtual void write_to(u8 *buf) = 0;
685:   virtual ~Compressor() {}
686:   i64 compressed_size = 0;
687: };

692:   void write_to(u8 *buf) override;

697: };

708: //
709: // perf.cc
710: //
711: 
712: // Counter is used to collect statistics numbers.
713: class Counter {
714: public:
715:   Counter(std::string_view name, i64 value = 0) : name(name), values(value) {
716:     static std::mutex mu;
717:     std::scoped_lock lock(mu);
718:     instances.push_back(this);
719:   }
720: 
721:   Counter &operator++(int) {
722:     if (enabled) [[unlikely]]
723:       values.local()++;
724:     return *this;
725:   }
726: 
727:   Counter &operator+=(int delta) {
728:     if (enabled) [[unlikely]]
729:       values.local() += delta;
730:     return *this;
731:   }
732: 
733:   static void print();
734: 
735:   static inline bool enabled = false;
736: 
737: private:
738:   i64 get_value();
739: 
740:   std::string_view name;
741:   tbb::enumerable_thread_specific<i64> values;
742: 
743:   static inline std::vector<Counter *> instances;
744: };
745: 
746: // Timer and TimeRecord records elapsed time (wall clock time)
747: // used by each pass of the linker.
748: struct TimerRecord {
749:   TimerRecord(std::string name, TimerRecord *parent = nullptr);
750:   void stop();
751: 
752:   std::string name;
753:   TimerRecord *parent;
754:   tbb::concurrent_vector<TimerRecord *> children;
755:   i64 start;
756:   i64 end;
757:   i64 user;
758:   i64 sys;
759:   bool stopped = false;
760: };
761: 
762: void
763: print_timer_records(tbb::concurrent_vector<std::unique_ptr<TimerRecord>> &);
764: 
765: template <typename Context>
766: class Timer {
767: public:
768:   Timer(Context &ctx, std::string name, Timer *parent = nullptr) {
769:     record = new TimerRecord(name, parent ? parent->record : nullptr);
770:     ctx.timer_records.push_back(std::unique_ptr<TimerRecord>(record));
771:   }
772: 
773:   Timer(const Timer &) = delete;
774: 
775:   ~Timer() {
776:     record->stop();
777:   }
778: 
779:   void stop() {
780:     record->stop();
781:   }
782: 
783: private:
784:   TimerRecord *record;
785: };
