787: //
788: // tar.cc
789: //
790: 
791: // TarFile is a class to create a tar file.
792: //
793: // If you pass `--repro` to mold, mold collects all input files and
794: // put them into `<output-file-path>.repro.tar`, so that it is easy to
795: // run the same command with the same command line arguments.
796: class TarWriter {
797: public:
798:   static std::unique_ptr<TarWriter>
799:   open(std::string output_path, std::string basedir);
800: 
801:   ~TarWriter();
802:   void append(std::string path, std::string_view data);
803: 
804: private:
805:   static constexpr i64 BLOCK_SIZE = 512;
806: 
807:   TarWriter(FILE *out, std::string basedir) : out(out), basedir(basedir) {}
808: 
809:   FILE *out = nullptr;
810:   std::string basedir;
811: };
812: 
813: //
814: // Memory-mapped file
815: //
816: 
817: // MappedFile represents an mmap'ed input file.
818: // mold uses mmap-IO only.
819: template <typename Context>
820: class MappedFile {
821: public:
822:   static MappedFile *open(Context &ctx, std::string path);
823:   static MappedFile *must_open(Context &ctx, std::string path);
824: 
825:   ~MappedFile() { unmap(); }
826:   void unmap();
827: 
828:   MappedFile *slice(Context &ctx, std::string name, u64 start, u64 size);
829: 
830:   std::string_view get_contents() {
831:     return std::string_view((char *)data, size);
832:   }
833: 
834:   i64 get_offset() const {
835:     return parent ? (data - parent->data + parent->get_offset()) : 0;
836:   }
837: 
838:   // Returns a string that uniquely identify a file that is possibly
839:   // in an archive.
840:   std::string get_identifier() const {
841:     if (parent) {
842:       // We use the file offset within an archive as an identifier
843:       // because archive members may have the same name.
844:       return parent->name + ":" + std::to_string(get_offset());
845:     }
846: 
847:     if (thin_parent) {
848:       // If this is a thin archive member, the filename part is
849:       // guaranteed to be unique.
850:       return thin_parent->name + ":" + name;
851:     }
852:     return name;
853:   }
854: 
855:   std::string name;
856:   u8 *data = nullptr;
857:   i64 size = 0;
858:   bool given_fullpath = true;
859:   MappedFile *parent = nullptr;
860:   MappedFile *thin_parent = nullptr;
861:   int fd = -1;
862: #ifdef _WIN32
863:   HANDLE file_handle = INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE;
864: #endif
865: };
866: 
867: template <typename Context>
868: MappedFile<Context> *MappedFile<Context>::open(Context &ctx, std::string path) {
869:   if (path.starts_with('/') && !ctx.arg.chroot.empty())
870:     path = ctx.arg.chroot + "/" + path_clean(path);
871: 
872:   i64 fd;
873: #ifdef _WIN32
874:     fd = ::_open(path.c_str(), O_RDONLY);
875: #else
876:     fd = ::open(path.c_str(), O_RDONLY);
877: #endif
878: 
879:   if (fd == -1)
880:     return nullptr;
881: 
882:   struct stat st;
883:   if (fstat(fd, &st) == -1)
884:     Fatal(ctx) << path << ": fstat failed: " << errno_string();
885: 
886:   MappedFile *mf = new MappedFile;
887:   ctx.mf_pool.push_back(std::unique_ptr<MappedFile>(mf));
888: 
889:   mf->name = path;
890:   mf->size = st.st_size;
891: 
892:   if (st.st_size > 0) {
893: #ifdef _WIN32
894:     HANDLE handle = CreateFileMapping((HANDLE)_get_osfhandle(fd),
895:                                       nullptr, PAGE_READWRITE, 0,
896:                                       st.st_size, nullptr);
897:     if (!handle)
898:       Fatal(ctx) << path << ": CreateFileMapping failed: " << GetLastError();
899:     mf->file_handle = handle;
900:     mf->data = (u8 *)MapViewOfFile(handle, FILE_MAP_ALL_ACCESS, 0, 0, st.st_size);
901:     if (!mf->data)
902:       Fatal(ctx) << path << ": MapViewOfFile failed: " << GetLastError();
903: #else
904:     mf->data = (u8 *)mmap(nullptr, st.st_size, PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE,
905:                           MAP_PRIVATE, fd, 0);
906:     if (mf->data == MAP_FAILED)
907:       Fatal(ctx) << path << ": mmap failed: " << errno_string();
908: #endif
909:     }
910: 
911:   close(fd);
912:   return mf;
913: }
914: 
915: template <typename Context>
916: MappedFile<Context> *
917: MappedFile<Context>::must_open(Context &ctx, std::string path) {
918:   if (MappedFile *mf = MappedFile::open(ctx, path))
919:     return mf;
920:   Fatal(ctx) << "cannot open " << path << ": " << errno_string();
921: }
922: 
923: template <typename Context>
924: MappedFile<Context> *
925: MappedFile<Context>::slice(Context &ctx, std::string name, u64 start, u64 size) {
926:   MappedFile *mf = new MappedFile;
927:   mf->name = name;
928:   mf->data = data + start;
929:   mf->size = size;
930:   mf->parent = this;
931: 
932:   ctx.mf_pool.push_back(std::unique_ptr<MappedFile>(mf));
933:   return mf;
934: }
935: 
936: template <typename Context>
937: void MappedFile<Context>::unmap() {
938:   if (size == 0 || parent || !data)
939:     return;
940: 
941: #ifdef _WIN32
942:   UnmapViewOfFile(data);
943:   if (file_handle != INVALID_HANDLE_VALUE)
944:     CloseHandle(file_handle);
945: #else
946:   munmap(data, size);
947: #endif
948: 
949:   data = nullptr;
950: }
951: 
952: } // namespace mold

================
File: elf/arch-ppc64v2.cc
================
  1: // This file implements the PowerPC ELFv2 ABI which was standardized in
  2: // 2014. Modern little-endian PowerPC systems are based on this ABI.
  3: // The ABI is often referred to as "ppc64le". This shouldn't be confused
  4: // with "ppc64" which refers to the original, big-endian PowerPC systems.
  5: //
  6: // PPC64 is a bit tricky to support because PC-relative load/store
  7: // instructions hadn't been available until Power10 which debuted in 2021.
  8: // Prior to Power10, it wasn't trivial for position-independent code (PIC)
  9: // to load a value from, for example, .got, as we can't do that with [PC +
 10: // the offset to the .got entry].
 11: //
 12: // In the following, I'll explain how PIC is supported on pre-Power10
 13: // systems first and then explain what has changed with Power10.
 14: //
 15: //
 16: // Position-independent code on Power9 or earlier:
 17: //
 18: // We can get the program counter on older PPC64 systems with the
 19: // following four instructions
 20: //
 21: //   mflr  r1  // save the current link register to r1
 22: //   bl    .+4 // branch to the next instruction as if it were a function
 23: //   mflr  r0  // copy the return address to r0
 24: //   mtlr  r1  // restore the original link register value
 25: //
 26: // , but it's too expensive to do if we do this for each load/store.
 27: //
 28: // As a workaround, most functions are compiled in such a way that r2 is
 29: // assumed to always contain the address of .got + 0x8000. With this, we
 30: // can for example load the first entry of .got with a single instruction
 31: // `lw r0, -0x8000(r2)`. r2 is called the TOC pointer.
 32: //
 33: // There's only one .got for each ELF module. Therefore, if a callee is in
 34: // the same ELF module, r2 doesn't have to be recomputed. Most function
 35: // calls are usually within the same ELF module, so this mechanism is
 36: // efficient.
 37: //
 38: // A function compiled for pre-Power10 usually has two entry points,
 39: // global and local. The global entry point usually 8 bytes precedes
 40: // the local entry point. In between is the following instructions:
 41: //
 42: //   addis r2, r12, .TOC.@ha
 43: //   addi  r2, r2,  .TOC.@lo + 4;
 44: //
 45: // The global entry point assumes that the address of itself is in r12,
 46: // and it computes its own TOC pointer from r12. It's easy to do so for
 47: // the callee because the offset between its .got + 0x8000 and the
 48: // function is known at link-time. The above code sequence then falls
 49: // through to the local entry point that assumes r2 is .got + 0x8000.
 50: //
 51: // So, if a callee's TOC pointer is different from the current one
 52: // (e.g. calling a function in another .so), we first load the callee's
 53: // address to r12 (e.g. from .got.plt with a r2-relative load) and branch
 54: // to that address. Then the callee computes its own TOC pointer using
 55: // r12.
 56: //
 57: //
 58: // Position-independent code on Power10:
 59: //
 60: // Power10 added 8-bytes-long instructions to the ISA. Some of them are
 61: // PC-relative load/store instructions that take 34 bits offsets.
 62: // Functions compiled with `-mcpu=power10` use these instructions for PIC.
 63: // r2 does not have a special meaning in such fucntions.
 64: //
 65: // When a fucntion compiled for Power10 calls a function that uses the TOC
 66: // pointer, we need to compute a correct value for TOC and set it to r2
 67: // before transferring the control to the callee. Thunks are responsible
 68: // for doing it.
 69: //
 70: // `_NOTOC` relocations such as `R_PPC64_REL24_NOTOC` indicate that the
 71: // callee does not use TOC (i.e. compiled with `-mcpu=power10`). If a
 72: // function using TOC is referenced via a `_NOTOC` relocation, that call
 73: // is made through a range extension thunk.
 74: //
 75: //
 76: // Note on section names: the PPC64 psABI uses a weird naming convention
 77: // which calls .got.plt .plt. We ignored that part because it's just
 78: // confusing. Since the runtime only cares about segments, we should be
 79: // able to name sections whatever we want.
 80: //
 81: // https://github.com/rui314/psabi/blob/main/ppc64v2.pdf
 82: 
 83: #include "mold.h"
 84: 
 85: namespace mold::elf {
 86: 
 87: using E = PPC64V2;
 88: 
 89: static u64 lo(u64 x)    { return x & 0xffff; }
 90: static u64 hi(u64 x)    { return x >> 16; }
 91: static u64 ha(u64 x)    { return (x + 0x8000) >> 16; }
 92: static u64 high(u64 x)  { return (x >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 93: static u64 higha(u64 x) { return ((x + 0x8000) >> 16) & 0xffff; }
 94: 
 95: static u64 prefix34(u64 x) {
 96:   return bits(x, 33, 16) | (bits(x, 15, 0) << 32);
 97: }
 98: 
 99: // .plt is used only for lazy symbol resolution on PPC64. All PLT
100: // calls are made via range extension thunks even if they are within
101: // reach. Thunks read addresses from .got.plt and jump there.
102: // Therefore, once PLT symbols are resolved and final addresses are
103: // written to .got.plt, thunks just skip .plt and directly jump to the
104: // resolved addresses.
105: template <>
106: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) {
107:   static const ul32 insn[] = {
108:     // Get PC
109:     0x7c08'02a6, // mflr    r0
110:     0x429f'0005, // bcl     20, 31, 4 // obtain PC
111:     0x7d68'02a6, // mflr    r11
112:     0x7c08'03a6, // mtlr    r0
113: 
114:     // Compute the PLT entry index
115:     0xe80b'002c, // ld      r0, 44(r11)
116:     0x7d8b'6050, // subf    r12, r11, r12
117:     0x7d60'5a14, // add     r11, r0, r11
118:     0x380c'ffcc, // addi    r0, r12, -52
119:     0x7800'f082, // rldicl  r0, r0, 62, 2
120: 
121:     // Load .got.plt[0] and .got.plt[1] and branch to .got.plt[0]
122:     0xe98b'0000, // ld      r12, 0(r11)
123:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr   r12
124:     0xe96b'0008, // ld      r11, 8(r11)
125:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
126: 
127:     // .quad .got.plt - .plt - 8
128:     0x0000'0000,
129:     0x0000'0000,
130:   };
131: 
132:   memcpy(buf, insn, sizeof(insn));
133:   *(ul64 *)(buf + 52) = ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr - ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - 8;
134: }
135: 
136: template <>
137: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {
138:   // When the control is transferred to a PLT entry, the PLT entry's
139:   // address is already set to %r12 by the caller.
140:   i64 offset = ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr - sym.get_plt_addr(ctx);
141:   *(ul32 *)buf = 0x4b00'0000 | (offset & 0x00ff'ffff);        // b plt0
142: }
143: 
144: // .plt.got is not necessary on PPC64 because range extension thunks
145: // directly read GOT entries and jump there.
146: template <>
147: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym) {}
148: 
149: template <>
150: void EhFrameSection<E>::apply_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
151:                                     u64 offset, u64 val) {
152:   u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->shdr.sh_offset + offset;
153: 
154:   switch (rel.r_type) {
155:   case R_NONE:
156:     break;
157:   case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
158:     *(ul64 *)loc = val;
159:     break;
160:   case R_PPC64_REL32:
161:     *(ul32 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
162:     break;
163:   case R_PPC64_REL64:
164:     *(ul64 *)loc = val - this->shdr.sh_addr - offset;
165:     break;
166:   default:
167:     Fatal(ctx) << "unsupported relocation in .eh_frame: " << rel;
168:   }
169: }
170: 
171: static u64 get_local_entry_offset(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
172:   i64 val = sym.esym().ppc_local_entry;
173:   assert(val <= 7);
174:   if (val == 7)
175:     Fatal(ctx) << sym << ": local entry offset 7 is reserved";
176: 
177:   if (val == 0 || val == 1)
178:     return 0;
179:   return 1 << val;
180: }
181: 
182: template <>
183: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
184:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
185: 
186:   ElfRel<E> *dynrel = nullptr;
187:   if (ctx.reldyn)
188:     dynrel = (ElfRel<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.reldyn->shdr.sh_offset +
189:                            file.reldyn_offset + this->reldyn_offset);
190: 
191:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
192:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
193:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE)
194:       continue;
195: 
196:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
197:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
198: 
199:     u64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx);
200:     u64 A = rel.r_addend;
201:     u64 P = get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
202:     u64 G = sym.get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
203:     u64 GOT = ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr;
204:     u64 TOC = ctx.extra.TOC->value;
205: 
206:     auto r2save_thunk_addr = [&] { return get_thunk_addr(i); };
207:     auto no_r2save_thunk_addr = [&] { return get_thunk_addr(i) + 4; };
208: 
209:     switch (rel.r_type) {
210:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
211:       if (name() == ".toc")
212:         apply_toc_rel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
213:       else
214:         apply_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel, loc, S, A, P, dynrel);
215:       break;
216:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_HA:
217:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(S + A - TOC);
218:       break;
219:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO:
220:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(S + A - TOC);
221:       break;
222:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_DS:
223:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO_DS:
224:       *(ul16 *)loc |= (S + A - TOC) & 0xfffc;
225:       break;
226:     case R_PPC64_REL24:
227:       if (sym.has_plt(ctx) || !sym.esym().preserves_r2()) {
228:         i64 val = r2save_thunk_addr() + A - P;
229:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
230: 
231:         // The thunk saves %r2 to the caller's r2 save slot. We need to
232:         // restore it after function return. To do so, there's usually a
233:         // NOP as a placeholder after a BL. 0x6000'0000 is a NOP.
234:         if (*(ul32 *)(loc + 4) == 0x6000'0000)
235:           *(ul32 *)(loc + 4) = 0xe841'0018; // ld r2, 24(r1)
236:       } else {
237:         i64 val = S + get_local_entry_offset(ctx, sym) + A - P;
238:         if (sign_extend(val, 25) != val)
239:           val = no_r2save_thunk_addr() + A - P;
240:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
241:       }
242:       break;
243:     case R_PPC64_REL24_NOTOC:
244:       if (sym.has_plt(ctx) || sym.esym().uses_toc()) {
245:         i64 val = no_r2save_thunk_addr() + A - P;
246:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
247:       } else {
248:         i64 val = S + A - P;
249:         if (sign_extend(val, 25) != val)
250:           val = no_r2save_thunk_addr() + A - P;
251:         *(ul32 *)loc |= bits(val, 25, 2) << 2;
252:       }
253:       break;
254:     case R_PPC64_REL64:
255:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - P;
256:       break;
257:     case R_PPC64_REL16_HA:
258:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(S + A - P);
259:       break;
260:     case R_PPC64_REL16_LO:
261:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(S + A - P);
262:       break;
263:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HA:
264:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(G + GOT - TOC);
265:       break;
266:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HI:
267:       *(ul16 *)loc = hi(G + GOT - TOC);
268:       break;
269:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO:
270:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(G + GOT - TOC);
271:       break;
272:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO_DS:
273:       *(ul16 *)loc |= (G + GOT - TOC) & 0xfffc;
274:       break;
275:     case R_PPC64_PLT_PCREL34:
276:     case R_PPC64_PLT_PCREL34_NOTOC:
277:     case R_PPC64_GOT_PCREL34:
278:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(G + GOT - P);
279:       break;
280:     case R_PPC64_PCREL34:
281:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(S + A - P);
282:       break;
283:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_HA:
284:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - TOC);
285:       break;
286:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_LO_DS:
287:       *(ul16 *)loc |= (sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - TOC) & 0xfffc;
288:       break;
289:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL_PCREL34:
290:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(sym.get_gottp_addr(ctx) - P);
291:       break;
292:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_HA:
293:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - TOC);
294:       break;
295:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_LO:
296:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - TOC);
297:       break;
298:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD_PCREL34:
299:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(sym.get_tlsgd_addr(ctx) - P);
300:       break;
301:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_HA:
302:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - TOC);
303:       break;
304:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_LO:
305:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - TOC);
306:       break;
307:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD_PCREL34:
308:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(ctx.got->get_tlsld_addr(ctx) - P);
309:       break;
310:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_HA:
311:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
312:       break;
313:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_LO:
314:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
315:       break;
316:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL34:
317:       *(ul64 *)loc |= prefix34(S + A - ctx.dtp_addr);
318:       break;
319:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_HA:
320:       *(ul16 *)loc = ha(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
321:       break;
322:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_LO:
323:       *(ul16 *)loc = lo(S + A - ctx.tp_addr);
324:       break;
325:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ:
326:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ_NOTOC:
327:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL:
328:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL_NOTOC:
329:     case R_PPC64_TLS:
330:     case R_PPC64_TLSGD:
331:     case R_PPC64_TLSLD:
332:       break;
333:     default:
334:       unreachable();
335:     }
336:   }
337: }
338: 
339: template <>
340: void InputSection<E>::apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base) {
341:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
342: 
343:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
344:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
345:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
346:       continue;
347: 
348:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
349:     u8 *loc = base + rel.r_offset;
350: 
351:     auto check = [&](i64 val, i64 lo, i64 hi) {
352:       if (val < lo || hi <= val)
353:         Error(ctx) << *this << ": relocation " << rel << " against "
354:                    << sym << " out of range: " << val << " is not in ["
355:                    << lo << ", " << hi << ")";
356:     };
357: 
358:     SectionFragment<E> *frag;
359:     i64 frag_addend;
360:     std::tie(frag, frag_addend) = get_fragment(ctx, rel);
361: 
362:     u64 S = frag ? frag->get_addr(ctx) : sym.get_addr(ctx);
363:     u64 A = frag ? frag_addend : (i64)rel.r_addend;
364: 
365:     switch (rel.r_type) {
366:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
367:       if (std::optional<u64> val = get_tombstone(sym, frag))
368:         *(ul64 *)loc = *val;
369:       else
370:         *(ul64 *)loc = S + A;
371:       break;
372:     case R_PPC64_ADDR32: {
373:       i64 val = S + A;
374:       check(val, 0, 1LL << 32);
375:       *(ul32 *)loc = val;
376:       break;
377:     }
378:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL64:
379:       *(ul64 *)loc = S + A - ctx.dtp_addr;
380:       break;
381:     default:
382:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid relocation for non-allocated sections: "
383:                  << rel;
384:     }
385:   }
386: }
387: 
388: template <>
389: void InputSection<E>::scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx) {
390:   assert(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC);
391: 
392:   this->reldyn_offset = file.num_dynrel * sizeof(ElfRel<E>);
393:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = get_rels(ctx);
394: 
395:   // Scan relocations
396:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
397:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
398:     if (rel.r_type == R_NONE || record_undef_error(ctx, rel))
399:       continue;
400: 
401:     Symbol<E> &sym = *file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
402: 
403:     if (sym.is_ifunc())
404:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT | NEEDS_PLT;
405: 
406:     switch (rel.r_type) {
407:     case R_PPC64_ADDR64:
408:       if (name() == ".toc")
409:         scan_toc_rel(ctx, sym, rel);
410:       else
411:         scan_dyn_absrel(ctx, sym, rel);
412:       break;
413:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_HA:
414:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL_PCREL34:
415:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOTTP;
416:       break;
417:     case R_PPC64_REL24:
418:       if (sym.is_imported)
419:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
420:       break;
421:     case R_PPC64_REL24_NOTOC:
422:       if (sym.is_imported)
423:         sym.flags |= NEEDS_PLT;
424:       ctx.extra.is_power10 = true;
425:       break;
426:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HA:
427:     case R_PPC64_PLT_PCREL34:
428:     case R_PPC64_PLT_PCREL34_NOTOC:
429:     case R_PPC64_GOT_PCREL34:
430:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_GOT;
431:       break;
432:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_HA:
433:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD_PCREL34:
434:       sym.flags |= NEEDS_TLSGD;
435:       break;
436:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_HA:
437:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD_PCREL34:
438:       ctx.needs_tlsld = true;
439:       break;
440:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_HA:
441:     case R_PPC64_TPREL16_LO:
442:       check_tlsle(ctx, sym, rel);
443:       break;
444:     case R_PPC64_REL64:
445:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_HA:
446:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO:
447:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_LO_DS:
448:     case R_PPC64_TOC16_DS:
449:     case R_PPC64_REL16_HA:
450:     case R_PPC64_REL16_LO:
451:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_HI:
452:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO:
453:     case R_PPC64_PLT16_LO_DS:
454:     case R_PPC64_PCREL34:
455:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ:
456:     case R_PPC64_PLTSEQ_NOTOC:
457:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL:
458:     case R_PPC64_PLTCALL_NOTOC:
459:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TPREL16_LO_DS:
460:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSGD16_LO:
461:     case R_PPC64_GOT_TLSLD16_LO:
462:     case R_PPC64_TLS:
463:     case R_PPC64_TLSGD:
464:     case R_PPC64_TLSLD:
465:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_HA:
466:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL16_LO:
467:     case R_PPC64_DTPREL34:
468:       break;
469:     default:
470:       Error(ctx) << *this << ": unknown relocation: " << rel;
471:     }
472:   }
473: }
474: 
475: template <>
476: void RangeExtensionThunk<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
477:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + output_section.shdr.sh_offset + offset;
478: 
479:   // If the destination is PLT, we read an address from .got.plt or .got
480:   // and jump there.
481:   static const ul32 plt_thunk[] = {
482:     0xf841'0018, // std   r2, 24(r1)
483:     0x3d82'0000, // addis r12, r2, foo@gotplt@toc@ha
484:     0xe98c'0000, // ld    r12, foo@gotplt@toc@lo(r12)
485:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr r12
486:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
487:   };
488: 
489:   static const ul32 plt_thunk_power10[] = {
490:     0xf841'0018, // std   r2, 24(r1)
491:     0x0410'0000, // pld   r12, foo@gotplt@pcrel
492:     0xe580'0000,
493:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr r12
494:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
495:   };
496: 
497:   // If the destination is a non-imported function, we directly jump
498:   // to its local entry point.
499:   static const ul32 local_thunk[] = {
500:     0xf841'0018, // std   r2, 24(r1)
501:     0x3d82'0000, // addis r12, r2,  foo@toc@ha
502:     0x398c'0000, // addi  r12, r12, foo@toc@lo
503:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr r12
504:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
505:   };
506: 
507:   static const ul32 local_thunk_power10[] = {
508:     0xf841'0018, // std   r2, 24(r1)
509:     0x0610'0000, // pla   r12, foo@pcrel
510:     0x3980'0000,
511:     0x7d89'03a6, // mtctr r12
512:     0x4e80'0420, // bctr
513:   };
514: 
515:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(plt_thunk));
516:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(plt_thunk_power10));
517:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(local_thunk));
518:   static_assert(E::thunk_size == sizeof(local_thunk_power10));
519: 
520:   for (i64 i = 0; i < symbols.size(); i++) {
521:     Symbol<E> &sym = *symbols[i];
522:     ul32 *loc = (ul32 *)(buf + i * E::thunk_size);
523: 
524:     if (sym.has_plt(ctx)) {
525:       u64 got = sym.has_got(ctx) ? sym.get_got_addr(ctx) : sym.get_gotplt_addr(ctx);
526: 
527:       if (ctx.extra.is_power10) {
528:         memcpy(loc, plt_thunk_power10, E::thunk_size);
529:         *(ul64 *)(loc + 1) |= prefix34(got - get_addr(i) - 4);
530:       } else {
531:         i64 val = got - ctx.extra.TOC->value;
532:         memcpy(loc, plt_thunk, E::thunk_size);
533:         loc[1] |= higha(val);
534:         loc[2] |= lo(val);
535:       }
536:     } else {
537:       if (ctx.extra.is_power10) {
538:         memcpy(loc, local_thunk_power10, E::thunk_size);
539:         *(ul64 *)(loc + 1) |= prefix34(sym.get_addr(ctx) - get_addr(i) - 4);
540:       } else {
541:         i64 val = sym.get_addr(ctx) - ctx.extra.TOC->value;
542:         memcpy(loc, local_thunk, E::thunk_size);
543:         loc[1] |= higha(val);
544:         loc[2] |= lo(val);
545:       }
546:     }
547:   }
548: }
549: 
550: } // namespace mold::elf

  58: // Mergeable section fragments

  64:     : output_section(*sec), is_alive(is_alive) {}

  74: // Additional class members for dynamic symbols. Because most symbols
  75: // don't need them and we allocate tens of millions of symbol objects
  76: // for large programs, we separate them from `Symbol` class to save
  77: // memory.
  78: template <typename E>
  79: struct SymbolAux {
  80:   i32 got_idx = -1;
  81:   i32 gottp_idx = -1;
  82:   i32 tlsgd_idx = -1;
  83:   i32 tlsdesc_idx = -1;
  84:   i32 plt_idx = -1;
  85:   i32 pltgot_idx = -1;
  86:   i32 dynsym_idx = -1;
  87:   u32 djb_hash = 0;

 100: class RangeExtensionThunk {};

 102: template <typename E> requires needs_thunk<E>
 103: class RangeExtensionThunk<E> {
 104: public:
 105:   RangeExtensionThunk(OutputSection<E> &osec, i64 thunk_idx, i64 offset)
 106:     : output_section(osec), thunk_idx(thunk_idx), offset(offset) {}
 107: 
 108:   i64 size() const { return E::thunk_hdr_size + symbols.size() * E::thunk_size; }
 109:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx);
 110: 
 111:   u64 get_addr(i64 idx) const {
 112:     return output_section.shdr.sh_addr + offset + E::thunk_hdr_size +
 113:            idx * E::thunk_size;
 114:   }
 115: 
 116:   static constexpr i64 alignment = 4;
 117: 
 118:   OutputSection<E> &output_section;
 119:   i64 thunk_idx;
 120:   i64 offset;
 121:   std::mutex mu;
 122:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> symbols;
 123: };

 125: struct RangeExtensionRef {
 126:   i16 thunk_idx = -1;
 127:   i16 sym_idx = -1;
 128: };
 129: 
 130: template <typename E>
 131: void create_range_extension_thunks(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec);
 132: 
 133: //
 134: // input-sections.cc
 135: //
 136: 
 137: // .eh_frame section contains CIE and FDE records to teach the runtime
 138: // how to handle exceptions. Usually, a .eh_frame contains one CIE
 139: // followed by as many FDEs as the number of functions defined by the
 140: // file. CIE contains common information for FDEs (it is actually
 141: // short for Common Information Entry). FDE contains the start address
 142: // of a function and its length as well as how to handle exceptions
 143: // for that function.
 144: //
 145: // Unlike other sections, the linker has to parse .eh_frame for optimal
 146: // output for the following reasons:
 147: //
 148: //  - Compilers tend to emit the same CIE as long as the programming
 149: //    language is the same, so CIEs in input object files are almost
 150: //    always identical. We want to merge them to make a resulting
 151: //    .eh_frame smaller.
 152: //
 153: //  - If we eliminate a function (e.g. when we see two object files
 154: //    containing the duplicate definition of an inlined function), we
 155: //    want to also eliminate a corresponding FDE so that a resulting
 156: //    .eh_frame doesn't contain a dead FDE entry.
 157: //
 158: //  - If we need to compare two function definitions for equality for
 159: //    ICF, we need to compare not only the function body but also its
 160: //    exception handlers.
 161: //
 162: // Note that we assume that the first relocation entry for an FDE
 163: // always points to the function that the FDE is associated to.
 164: template <typename E>
 165: struct CieRecord {
 166:   CieRecord(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, InputSection<E> &isec,
 167:             u32 input_offset, std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels, u32 rel_idx)
 168:     : file(file), input_section(isec), input_offset(input_offset),
 169:       rel_idx(rel_idx), rels(rels), contents(file.get_string(ctx, isec.shdr())) {}
 170: 
 171:   i64 size() const {
 172:     return *(U32<E> *)(contents.data() + input_offset) + 4;
 173:   }
 174: 
 175:   std::string_view get_contents() const {
 176:     return contents.substr(input_offset, size());
 177:   }
 178: 
 179:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> get_rels() const {
 180:     i64 end = rel_idx;
 181:     while (end < rels.size() && rels[end].r_offset < input_offset + size())
 182:       end++;
 183:     return rels.subspan(rel_idx, end - rel_idx);
 184:   }
 185: 
 186:   bool equals(const CieRecord &other) const;
 187: 
 188:   ObjectFile<E> &file;
 189:   InputSection<E> &input_section;
 190:   u32 input_offset = -1;
 191:   u32 output_offset = -1;
 192:   u32 rel_idx = -1;
 193:   u32 icf_idx = -1;
 194:   bool is_leader = false;
 195:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels;
 196:   std::string_view contents;
 197: };
 198: 
 199: template <typename E>
 200: struct FdeRecord {
 201:   FdeRecord(u32 input_offset, u32 rel_idx)
 202:     : input_offset(input_offset), rel_idx(rel_idx) {}
 203: 
 204:   i64 size(ObjectFile<E> &file) const;
 205:   std::string_view get_contents(ObjectFile<E> &file) const;
 206:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> get_rels(ObjectFile<E> &file) const;
 207: 
 208:   u32 input_offset = -1;
 209:   u32 output_offset = -1;
 210:   u32 rel_idx = -1;
 211:   u16 cie_idx = -1;
 212:   Atomic<bool> is_alive = true;
 213: };
 214: 
 215: // A struct to hold target-dependent input section members.
 216: template <typename E>
 217: struct InputSectionExtras {};
 218: 
 219: template <typename E> requires needs_thunk<E>
 220: struct InputSectionExtras<E> {
 221:   std::vector<RangeExtensionRef> range_extn;
 222: };
 223: 
 224: template <typename E> requires is_riscv<E>
 225: struct InputSectionExtras<E> {
 226:   std::vector<i32> r_deltas;
 227: };
 228: 
 229: // InputSection represents a section in an input object file.
 230: template <typename E>
 231: class InputSection {
 232: public:
 233:   InputSection(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, std::string_view name,
 234:                i64 shndx);
 235: 
 236:   void uncompress(Context<E> &ctx);
 237:   void uncompress_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf);
 238:   void scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx);
 239:   void write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf);
 240:   void apply_reloc_alloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base);
 241:   void apply_reloc_nonalloc(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *base);
 242:   void kill();
 243: 
 244:   std::string_view name() const;
 245:   i64 get_priority() const;
 246:   u64 get_addr() const;
 247:   const ElfShdr<E> &shdr() const;
 248:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> get_rels(Context<E> &ctx) const;
 249:   std::span<FdeRecord<E>> get_fdes() const;
 250:   std::string_view get_func_name(Context<E> &ctx, i64 offset) const;
 251:   bool is_relr_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel) const;
 252:   bool is_killed_by_icf() const;
 253: 
 254:   bool record_undef_error(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 255: 
 256:   ObjectFile<E> &file;
 257:   OutputSection<E> *output_section = nullptr;
 258:   u64 sh_size = -1;
 259: 
 260:   std::string_view contents;
 261: 
 262:   [[no_unique_address]] InputSectionExtras<E> extra;
 263: 
 264:   i32 fde_begin = -1;
 265:   i32 fde_end = -1;
 266: 
 267:   u32 offset = -1;
 268:   u32 shndx = -1;
 269:   u32 relsec_idx = -1;
 270:   u32 reldyn_offset = 0;
 271: 
 272:   // For COMDAT de-duplication and garbage collection
 273:   std::atomic_bool is_alive = true;
 274:   u8 p2align = 0;
 275: 
 276:   bool address_significant : 1 = false;
 277:   bool uncompressed : 1 = false;
 278: 
 279:   // For garbage collection
 280:   Atomic<bool> is_visited = false;
 281: 
 282:   // For ICF
 283:   //
 284:   // `leader` is the section that this section has been merged with.
 285:   // Three kind of values are possible:
 286:   // - `leader == nullptr`: This section was not eligible for ICF.
 287:   // - `leader == this`: This section was retained.
 288:   // - `leader != this`: This section was merged with another identical section.
 289:   InputSection<E> *leader = nullptr;
 290:   u32 icf_idx = -1;
 291:   bool icf_eligible = false;
 292:   bool icf_leaf = false;
 293: 
 294: private:
 295:   void scan_pcrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 296:   void scan_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 297:   void scan_dyn_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 298:   void scan_toc_rel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 299: 
 300:   void check_tlsle(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 301: 
 302:   void apply_dyn_absrel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
 303:                         u8 *loc, u64 S, i64 A, u64 P, ElfRel<E> *&dynrel);
 304: 
 305:   void apply_toc_rel(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel,
 306:                      u8 *loc, u64 S, i64 A, u64 P, ElfRel<E> *&dynrel);
 307: 
 308:   void copy_contents_riscv(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf);
 309: 
 310:   std::pair<SectionFragment<E> *, i64>
 311:   get_fragment(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
 312: 
 313:   u64 get_thunk_addr(i64 idx);
 314: 
 315:   std::optional<u64> get_tombstone(Symbol<E> &sym, SectionFragment<E> *frag);
 316: };
 317: 
 318: //
 319: // tls.cc
 320: //
 321: 
 322: template<typename E> u64 get_tls_begin(Context<E> &);
 323: template<typename E> u64 get_tp_addr(Context<E> &);
 324: template<typename E> u64 get_dtp_addr(Context<E> &);
 325: 
 326: //
 327: // output-chunks.cc
 328: //
 329: 
 330: template <typename E>
 331: u64 get_eflags(Context<E> &ctx);
 332: 
 333: template <typename E>
 334: i64 to_phdr_flags(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> *chunk);
 335: 
 336: template <typename E>
 337: bool is_relro(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> *chunk);
 338: 
 339: template <typename E>
 340: std::string_view get_output_name(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name, u64 flags);
 341: 
 342: template <typename E>
 343: void write_plt_header(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf);
 344: 
 345: template <typename E>
 346: void write_plt_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym);
 347: 
 348: template <typename E>
 349: void write_pltgot_entry(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf, Symbol<E> &sym);
 350: 
 351: typedef enum { HEADER, OUTPUT_SECTION, SYNTHETIC } ChunkKind;
 352: 
 353: // Chunk represents a contiguous region in an output file.
 354: template <typename E>
 355: class Chunk {
 356: public:
 357:   virtual ~Chunk() = default;
 358:   virtual ChunkKind kind() { return SYNTHETIC; }
 359:   virtual OutputSection<E> *to_osec() { return nullptr; }
 360:   virtual i64 get_reldyn_size(Context<E> &ctx) const { return 0; }
 361:   virtual void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {}
 362:   virtual void write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) { unreachable(); }
 363:   virtual void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) {}
 364: 
 365:   // For --gdb-index
 366:   virtual u8 *get_uncompressed_data() { return nullptr; }
 367: 
 368:   std::string_view name;
 369:   ElfShdr<E> shdr = { .sh_addralign = 1 };
 370:   i64 shndx = 0;
 371: 
 372:   // Some synethetic sections add local symbols to the output.
 373:   // For example, range extension thunks adds function_name@thunk
 374:   // symbol for each thunk entry. The following members are used
 375:   // for such synthesizing symbols.
 376:   virtual void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {};
 377:   virtual void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {};
 378: 
 379:   i64 local_symtab_idx = 0;
 380:   i64 num_local_symtab = 0;
 381:   i64 strtab_size = 0;
 382:   i64 strtab_offset = 0;
 383: 
 384:   // Offset in .rel.dyn
 385:   i64 reldyn_offset = 0;
 386: 
 387:   // For --section-order
 388:   i64 sect_order = 0;
 389: };
 390: 
 391: // ELF header
 392: template <typename E>
 393: class OutputEhdr : public Chunk<E> {
 394: public:
 395:   OutputEhdr(u32 sh_flags) {
 396:     this->name = "EHDR";
 397:     this->shdr.sh_flags = sh_flags;
 398:     this->shdr.sh_size = sizeof(ElfEhdr<E>);
 399:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 400:   }
 401: 
 402:   ChunkKind kind() override { return HEADER; }
 403:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 404: };
 405: 
 406: // Section header
 407: template <typename E>
 408: class OutputShdr : public Chunk<E> {
 409: public:
 410:   OutputShdr() {
 411:     this->name = "SHDR";
 412:     this->shdr.sh_size = 1;
 413:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 414:   }
 415: 
 416:   ChunkKind kind() override { return HEADER; }
 417:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 418: };
 419: 
 420: // Program header
 421: template <typename E>
 422: class OutputPhdr : public Chunk<E> {
 423: public:
 424:   OutputPhdr(u32 sh_flags) {
 425:     this->name = "PHDR";
 426:     this->shdr.sh_flags = sh_flags;
 427:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 428:   }
 429: 
 430:   ChunkKind kind() override { return HEADER; }
 431:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 432:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 433: 
 434:   std::vector<ElfPhdr<E>> phdrs;
 435: };
 436: 
 437: template <typename E>
 438: class InterpSection : public Chunk<E> {
 439: public:
 440:   InterpSection() {
 441:     this->name = ".interp";
 442:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
 443:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;
 444:   }
 445: 
 446:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 447:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 448: };
 449: 
 450: // Sections
 451: template <typename E>
 452: class OutputSection : public Chunk<E> {
 453: public:
 454:   OutputSection(std::string_view name, u32 type, u64 flags) {
 455:     this->name = name;
 456:     this->shdr.sh_type = type;
 457:     this->shdr.sh_flags = flags;
 458:   }
 459: 
 460:   ChunkKind kind() override { return OUTPUT_SECTION; }
 461:   OutputSection<E> *to_osec() override { return this; }
 462:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 463:   void write_to(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *buf) override;
 464: 
 465:   void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 466:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 467: 
 468:   std::vector<InputSection<E> *> members;
 469: 
 470:   void construct_relr(Context<E> &ctx);
 471:   std::vector<u64> relr;
 472: 
 473:   std::vector<std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>>> thunks;
 474:   std::unique_ptr<RelocSection<E>> reloc_sec;
 475: };
 476: 
 477: template <typename E>
 478: class GotSection : public Chunk<E> {
 479: public:
 480:   GotSection() {
 481:     this->name = ".got";
 482:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
 483:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE;
 484:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 485: 
 486:     // We always create a .got so that _GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_ has
 487:     // something to point to. s390x psABI defines GOT[1] as a
 488:     // reserved slot, so we allocate one more on s390x.
 489:     this->shdr.sh_size = (is_s390x<E> ? 2 : 1) * sizeof(Word<E>);
 490:   }
 491: 
 492:   void add_got_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 493:   void add_gottp_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 494:   void add_tlsgd_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 495:   void add_tlsdesc_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 496:   void add_tlsld(Context<E> &ctx);
 497: 
 498:   u64 get_tlsld_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
 499:   bool has_tlsld(Context<E> &ctx) const { return tlsld_idx != -1; }
 500:   i64 get_reldyn_size(Context<E> &ctx) const override;
 501:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 502: 
 503:   void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 504:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 505: 
 506:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> got_syms;
 507:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> gottp_syms;
 508:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> tlsgd_syms;
 509:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> tlsdesc_syms;
 510:   u32 tlsld_idx = -1;
 511: 
 512:   void construct_relr(Context<E> &ctx);
 513:   std::vector<u64> relr;
 514: };
 515: 
 516: template <typename E>
 517: class GotPltSection : public Chunk<E> {
 518: public:
 519:   GotPltSection() {
 520:     this->name = ".got.plt";
 521:     this->shdr.sh_type = is_ppc64<E> ? SHT_NOBITS : SHT_PROGBITS;
 522:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE;
 523:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 524:     this->shdr.sh_size = HDR_SIZE;
 525:   }
 526: 
 527:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 528:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 529: 
 530:   static constexpr i64 HDR_SIZE = (is_ppc64v2<E> ? 2 : 3) * sizeof(Word<E>);
 531:   static constexpr i64 ENTRY_SIZE = (is_ppc64v1<E> ? 3 : 1) * sizeof(Word<E>);
 532: };
 533: 
 534: template <typename E>
 535: class PltSection : public Chunk<E> {
 536: public:
 537:   PltSection() {
 538:     this->name = ".plt";
 539:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
 540: 
 541:     if constexpr (is_sparc<E>) {
 542:       this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR | SHF_WRITE;
 543:       this->shdr.sh_addralign = 256;
 544:     } else {
 545:       this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR;
 546:       this->shdr.sh_addralign = 16;
 547:     }
 548:   }
 549: 
 550:   void add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 551:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 552:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 553: 
 554:   void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 555:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 556: 
 557:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> symbols;
 558: };
 559: 
 560: template <typename E>
 561: class PltGotSection : public Chunk<E> {
 562: public:
 563:   PltGotSection() {
 564:     this->name = ".plt.got";
 565:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
 566:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR;
 567:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 16;
 568:   }
 569: 
 570:   void add_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym);
 571:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 572: 
 573:   void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 574:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 575: 
 576:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> symbols;
 577: };

 581: public:
 582:   RelPltSection() {

 585:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;

 587:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 588:   }

 592: };

 893: template <typename E>
 894: class VerdefSection : public Chunk<E> {
 895: public:
 896:   VerdefSection() {
 897:     this->name = ".gnu.version_d";
 898:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_GNU_VERDEF;
 899:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;
 900:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 8;
 901:   }
 902: 
 903:   void construct(Context<E> &ctx);
 904:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 905:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 906: 
 907:   std::vector<u8> contents;
 908: };
 909: 
 910: template <typename E>
 911: class BuildIdSection : public Chunk<E> {
 912: public:
 913:   BuildIdSection() {
 914:     this->name = ".note.gnu.build-id";
 915:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_NOTE;
 916:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;
 917:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 4;
 918:     this->shdr.sh_size = 1;
 919:   }
 920: 
 921:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 922:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 923:   void write_buildid(Context<E> &ctx);
 924: 
 925:   static constexpr i64 HEADER_SIZE = 16;
 926: };

 929: class NotePackageSection : public Chunk<E> {
 930: public:
 931:   NotePackageSection() {
 932:     this->name = ".note.package";
 933:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_NOTE;
 934:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;
 935:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 4;
 936:   }

 942: template <typename E>
 943: class NotePropertySection : public Chunk<E> {
 944: public:
 945:   NotePropertySection() {
 946:     this->name = ".note.gnu.property";
 947:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_NOTE;
 948:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC;
 949:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = sizeof(Word<E>);
 950:   }
 951: 
 952:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 953:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 954: 
 955: private:
 956:   static constexpr i64 ENTRY_SIZE = E::is_64 ? 16 : 12;
 957: 
 958:   std::map<u32, u32> properties;
 959: };

 961: struct GdbIndexName {
 962:   std::string_view name;
 963:   u32 hash = 0;
 964:   u32 attr = 0;
 965:   u32 entry_idx = 0;
 966: };
 967: 
 968: template <typename E>
 969: class GdbIndexSection : public Chunk<E> {
 970: public:
 971:   GdbIndexSection() {
 972:     this->name = ".gdb_index";
 973:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
 974:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 4;
 975:   }
 976: 
 977:   void construct(Context<E> &ctx);
 978:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
 979:   void write_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx);
 980: 
 981: private:
 982:   struct SectionHeader {
 983:     ul32 version = 7;
 984:     ul32 cu_list_offset = 0;
 985:     ul32 cu_types_offset = 0;
 986:     ul32 areas_offset = 0;
 987:     ul32 symtab_offset = 0;
 988:     ul32 const_pool_offset = 0;
 989:   };
 990: 
 991:   struct MapEntry {
 992:     MapEntry(ObjectFile<E> *owner, u32 hash) : owner(owner), hash(hash) {}
 993: 
 994:     MapEntry(const MapEntry &other)
 995:       : owner(other.owner.load()), num_attrs(other.num_attrs.load()),
 996:         hash(other.hash), name_offset(other.name_offset),
 997:         attr_offset(other.attr_offset) {}
 998: 
 999:     std::atomic<ObjectFile<E> *> owner;
1000:     std::atomic_uint32_t num_attrs = 0;
1001:     u32 hash = 0;
1002:     u32 name_offset = -1;
1003:     u32 attr_offset = -1;
1004:   };
1005: 
1006:   SectionHeader header;
1007:   ConcurrentMap<MapEntry> map;
1008: };
1009: 
1010: template <typename E>
1011: class CompressedSection : public Chunk<E> {
1012: public:
1013:   CompressedSection(Context<E> &ctx, Chunk<E> &chunk);
1014:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1015:   u8 *get_uncompressed_data() override { return uncompressed.get(); }
1016: 
1017: private:
1018:   ElfChdr<E> chdr = {};
1019:   std::unique_ptr<Compressor> compressed;
1020:   std::unique_ptr<u8[]> uncompressed;
1021: };
1022: 
1023: template <typename E>
1024: class RelocSection : public Chunk<E> {
1025: public:
1026:   RelocSection(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec);
1027:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1028:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1029: 
1030: private:
1031:   OutputSection<E> &output_section;
1032:   std::vector<i64> offsets;
1033: };
1034: 
1035: // PT_GNU_RELRO works on page granularity. We want to align its end to
1036: // a page boundary. We append this section at end of a segment so that
1037: // the segment always ends at a page boundary.
1038: template <typename E>
1039: class RelroPaddingSection : public Chunk<E> {
1040: public:
1041:   RelroPaddingSection() {
1042:     this->name = ".relro_padding";
1043:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_NOBITS;
1044:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE;
1045:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 1;
1046:     this->shdr.sh_size = 1;
1047:   }
1048: };
1049: 
1050: template <typename E>
1051: class ComdatGroupSection : public Chunk<E> {
1052: public:
1053:   ComdatGroupSection(Symbol<E> &sym, std::vector<Chunk<E> *> members)
1054:     : sym(sym), members(std::move(members)) {
1055:     this->name = ".group";
1056:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_GROUP;
1057:     this->shdr.sh_entsize = 4;
1058:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 4;
1059:     this->shdr.sh_size = this->members.size() * 4 + 4;
1060:   }
1061: 
1062:   void update_shdr(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1063:   void copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1064: 
1065: private:
1066:   Symbol<E> &sym;
1067:   std::vector<Chunk<E> *> members;
1068: };

1071: // dwarf.cc
1072: //
1073: 
1074: template <typename E>
1075: std::vector<std::string_view>
1076: read_compunits(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file);
1077: 
1078: template <typename E>
1079: std::vector<GdbIndexName> read_pubnames(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file);
1080: 
1081: template <typename E>
1082: i64 estimate_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file);

1084: template <typename E>
1085: std::vector<u64>
1086: read_address_areas(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file, i64 offset);
1087: 
1088: //
1089: // input-files.cc
1090: //
1091: 
1092: // A comdat section typically represents an inline function,
1093: // which are de-duplicated by the linker.
1094: //
1095: // For each inline function, there's one comdat section, which
1096: // contains section indices of the function code and its data such as
1097: // string literals, if any.
1098: //
1099: // Comdat sections are identified by its signature. If two comdat
1100: // sections have the same signature, the linker picks up one and
1101: // discards the other by eliminating all sections that the other
1102: // comdat section refers to.
1103: struct ComdatGroup {
1104:   ComdatGroup() = default;
1105:   ComdatGroup(const ComdatGroup &other) : owner(other.owner.load()) {}
1106: 
1107:   // The file priority of the owner file of this comdat section.
1108:   std::atomic_uint32_t owner = -1;
1109: };
1110: 
1111: template <typename E>
1112: struct ComdatGroupRef {
1113:   ComdatGroup *group;
1114:   u32 sect_idx;
1115:   std::span<U32<E>> members;
1116: };
1117: 
1118: template <typename E>
1119: struct MergeableSection {
1120:   std::pair<SectionFragment<E> *, i64> get_fragment(i64 offset);
1121: 
1122:   MergedSection<E> *parent;
1123:   u8 p2align = 0;
1124:   std::vector<std::string_view> strings;
1125:   std::vector<u64> hashes;
1126:   std::vector<u32> frag_offsets;
1127:   std::vector<SectionFragment<E> *> fragments;
1128: };
1129: 
1130: // InputFile is the base class of ObjectFile and SharedFile.
1131: template <typename E>
1132: class InputFile {
1133: public:
1134:   InputFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf);
1135:   InputFile() : filename("<internal>") {}
1136: 
1137:   virtual ~InputFile() = default;
1138: 
1139:   template<typename T> std::span<T>
1140:   get_data(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfShdr<E> &shdr);
1141: 
1142:   template<typename T> std::span<T>
1143:   get_data(Context<E> &ctx, i64 idx);
1144: 
1145:   std::string_view get_string(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfShdr<E> &shdr);
1146:   std::string_view get_string(Context<E> &ctx, i64 idx);
1147: 
1148:   ElfEhdr<E> &get_ehdr() { return *(ElfEhdr<E> *)mf->data; }
1149:   std::span<ElfPhdr<E>> get_phdrs();
1150: 
1151:   ElfShdr<E> *find_section(i64 type);
1152: 
1153:   virtual void resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) = 0;
1154:   void clear_symbols();
1155: 
1156:   virtual void
1157:   mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx,
1158:                     std::function<void(InputFile<E> *)> feeder) = 0;
1159: 
1160:   std::span<Symbol<E> *> get_global_syms();
1161:   std::string_view get_source_name() const;
1162: 
1163:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf = nullptr;
1164:   std::span<ElfShdr<E>> elf_sections;
1165:   std::span<ElfSym<E>> elf_syms;
1166:   std::vector<Symbol<E> *> symbols;
1167:   i64 first_global = 0;
1168: 
1169:   std::string filename;
1170:   bool is_dso = false;
1171:   u32 priority;
1172:   Atomic<bool> is_alive = false;
1173:   std::string_view shstrtab;
1174:   std::string_view symbol_strtab;
1175: 
1176:   // To create an output .symtab
1177:   u64 local_symtab_idx = 0;
1178:   u64 global_symtab_idx = 0;
1179:   u64 num_local_symtab = 0;
1180:   u64 num_global_symtab = 0;
1181:   u64 strtab_offset = 0;
1182:   u64 strtab_size = 0;
1183: 
1184:   // For --emit-relocs
1185:   std::vector<i32> output_sym_indices;
1186: 
1187: protected:
1188:   std::vector<Symbol<E>> local_syms;
1189:   std::vector<Symbol<E>> frag_syms;
1190: };
1191: 
1192: // ObjectFile represents an input .o file.
1193: template <typename E>
1194: class ObjectFile : public InputFile<E> {
1195: public:
1196:   ObjectFile() = default;
1197: 
1198:   static ObjectFile<E> *create(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
1199:                                std::string archive_name, bool is_in_lib);
1200: 
1201:   void parse(Context<E> &ctx);
1202:   void initialize_mergeable_sections(Context<E> &ctx);
1203:   void resolve_section_pieces(Context<E> &ctx);
1204:   void resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) override;
1205:   void mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx,
1206:                          std::function<void(InputFile<E> *)> feeder) override;
1207:   void convert_undefined_weak_symbols(Context<E> &ctx);
1208:   void mark_addrsig(Context<E> &ctx);
1209:   void scan_relocations(Context<E> &ctx);
1210:   void convert_common_symbols(Context<E> &ctx);
1211:   void compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx);
1212:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx);
1213: 
1214:   i64 get_shndx(const ElfSym<E> &esym);
1215:   InputSection<E> *get_section(const ElfSym<E> &esym);
1216: 
1217:   std::string archive_name;
1218:   std::vector<std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>>> sections;
1219:   std::vector<std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>>> mergeable_sections;
1220:   bool is_in_lib = false;
1221:   std::vector<ElfShdr<E>> elf_sections2;
1222:   std::vector<CieRecord<E>> cies;
1223:   std::vector<FdeRecord<E>> fdes;
1224:   BitVector has_symver;
1225:   std::vector<ComdatGroupRef<E>> comdat_groups;
1226:   bool exclude_libs = false;
1227:   std::map<u32, u32> gnu_properties;
1228:   bool is_lto_obj = false;
1229:   bool needs_executable_stack = false;
1230: 
1231:   u64 num_dynrel = 0;
1232:   u64 reldyn_offset = 0;
1233: 
1234:   u64 fde_idx = 0;
1235:   u64 fde_offset = 0;
1236:   u64 fde_size = 0;
1237: 
1238:   // For ICF
1239:   std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> llvm_addrsig;
1240: 
1241:   // For .gdb_index
1242:   InputSection<E> *debug_info = nullptr;
1243:   InputSection<E> *debug_ranges = nullptr;
1244:   InputSection<E> *debug_rnglists = nullptr;
1245:   InputSection<E> *debug_pubnames = nullptr;
1246:   InputSection<E> *debug_pubtypes = nullptr;
1247:   std::vector<std::string_view> compunits;
1248:   std::vector<GdbIndexName> gdb_names;
1249:   i64 compunits_idx = 0;
1250:   i64 attrs_size = 0;
1251:   i64 attrs_offset = 0;
1252:   i64 names_size = 0;
1253:   i64 names_offset = 0;
1254:   i64 num_areas = 0;
1255:   i64 area_offset = 0;
1256: 
1257:   // For PPC32
1258:   InputSection<E> *ppc32_got2 = nullptr;
1259: 
1260: private:
1261:   ObjectFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
1262:              std::string archive_name, bool is_in_lib);
1263: 
1264:   void initialize_sections(Context<E> &ctx);
1265:   void initialize_symbols(Context<E> &ctx);
1266:   void sort_relocations(Context<E> &ctx);
1267:   void initialize_ehframe_sections(Context<E> &ctx);
1268:   void read_note_gnu_property(Context <E> &ctx, const ElfShdr <E> &shdr);
1269:   void read_ehframe(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec);
1270:   void override_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym,
1271:                        const ElfSym<E> &esym, i64 symidx);
1272:   void merge_visibility(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, u8 visibility);
1273: 
1274:   bool has_common_symbol = false;
1275: 
1276:   const ElfShdr<E> *symtab_sec;
1277:   std::span<U32<E>> symtab_shndx_sec;
1278: };
1279:

1296:   void populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx);
1297: 
1298:   bool is_needed = false;

1466:   void add_symbol(Context<PPC64V1> &ctx, Symbol<PPC64V1> *sym);
1467:   i64 get_reldyn_size(Context<PPC64V1> &ctx) const override;
1468:   void copy_buf(Context<PPC64V1> &ctx) override;
1469: 
1470:   static constexpr i64 ENTRY_SIZE = sizeof(Word<PPC64V1>) * 3;
1471: 
1472:   std::vector<Symbol<PPC64V1> *> symbols;
1473: };
1474: 
1475: //
1476: // arch-sparc.cc
1477: //
1478: 
1479: class SparcTlsGetAddrSection : public Chunk<SPARC64> {
1480: public:
1481:   SparcTlsGetAddrSection() {
1482:     this->name = ".tls_get_addr";
1483:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
1484:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR;
1485:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 4;
1486:     this->shdr.sh_size = 24;
1487:   }
1488: 
1489:   void copy_buf(Context<SPARC64> &ctx) override;
1490: };

1494: //

1496: class AlphaGotSection : public Chunk<ALPHA> {
1497: public:
1498:   AlphaGotSection() {
1499:     this->name = ".alpha_got";
1500:     this->shdr.sh_type = SHT_PROGBITS;
1501:     this->shdr.sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_WRITE;
1502:     this->shdr.sh_addralign = 8;
1503:   }
1504: 
1505:   void add_symbol(Symbol<ALPHA> &sym, i64 addend);
1506:   void finalize();
1507:   u64 get_addr(Symbol<ALPHA> &sym, i64 addend);
1508:   i64 get_reldyn_size(Context<ALPHA> &ctx) const override;
1509:   void copy_buf(Context<ALPHA> &ctx) override;
1510: 
1511:   struct Entry {
1512:     bool operator==(const Entry &) const = default;
1513:     Symbol<ALPHA> *sym;
1514:     i64 addend;
1515:   };
1516: 
1517: private:
1518:   std::vector<Entry> entries;
1519:   std::mutex mu;
1520: };

1600: // Context represents a context object for each invocation of the linker.
1601: // It contains command line flags, pointers to singleton objects
1602: // (such as linker-synthesized output sections), unique_ptrs for
1603: // resource management, and other miscellaneous objects.
1604: template <typename E>
1605: struct Context {
1606:   Context() = default;
1607:   Context(const Context<E> &) = delete;
1608: 
1609:   void checkpoint() {
1610:     if (has_error) {
1611:       cleanup();
1612:       _exit(1);
1613:     }
1614:   }
1615: 
1616:   // Command-line arguments
1617:   struct {
1618:     BuildId build_id;
1619:     CetReportKind z_cet_report = CET_REPORT_NONE;
1620:     CompressKind compress_debug_sections = COMPRESS_NONE;
1621:     SeparateCodeKind z_separate_code = NOSEPARATE_CODE;
1622:     ShuffleSectionsKind shuffle_sections = SHUFFLE_SECTIONS_NONE;
1623:     UnresolvedKind unresolved_symbols = UNRESOLVED_ERROR;
1624:     bool Bsymbolic = false;
1625:     bool Bsymbolic_functions = false;
1626:     bool allow_multiple_definition = false;
1627:     bool apply_dynamic_relocs = true;
1628:     bool color_diagnostics = false;
1629:     bool default_symver = false;
1630:     bool demangle = true;
1631:     bool discard_all = false;
1632:     bool discard_locals = false;
1633:     bool eh_frame_hdr = true;
1634:     bool emit_relocs = false;
1635:     bool enable_new_dtags = true;
1636:     bool execute_only = false;
1637:     bool export_dynamic = false;
1638:     bool fatal_warnings = false;
1639:     bool fork = true;
1640:     bool gc_sections = false;
1641:     bool gdb_index = false;
1642:     bool hash_style_gnu = true;
1643:     bool hash_style_sysv = true;
1644:     bool icf = false;
1645:     bool icf_all = false;
1646:     bool ignore_data_address_equality = false;
1647:     bool is_static = false;
1648:     bool lto_pass2 = false;
1649:     bool noinhibit_exec = false;
1650:     bool oformat_binary = false;
1651:     bool omagic = false;
1652:     bool pack_dyn_relocs_relr = false;
1653:     bool perf = false;
1654:     bool pic = false;
1655:     bool pie = false;
1656:     bool print_dependencies = false;
1657:     bool print_gc_sections = false;
1658:     bool print_icf_sections = false;
1659:     bool print_map = false;
1660:     bool quick_exit = true;
1661:     bool relax = true;
1662:     bool relocatable = false;
1663:     bool relocatable_merge_sections = false;
1664:     bool repro = false;
1665:     bool rosegment = true;
1666:     bool shared = false;
1667:     bool start_stop = false;
1668:     bool stats = false;
1669:     bool strip_all = false;
1670:     bool strip_debug = false;
1671:     bool suppress_warnings = false;
1672:     bool trace = false;
1673:     bool undefined_version = false;
1674:     bool warn_common = false;
1675:     bool warn_once = false;
1676:     bool warn_textrel = false;
1677:     bool z_copyreloc = true;
1678:     bool z_defs = false;
1679:     bool z_delete = true;
1680:     bool z_dlopen = true;
1681:     bool z_dump = true;
1682:     bool z_dynamic_undefined_weak = true;
1683:     bool z_execstack = false;
1684:     bool z_execstack_if_needed = false;
1685:     bool z_ibt = false;
1686:     bool z_initfirst = false;
1687:     bool z_interpose = false;
1688:     bool z_keep_text_section_prefix = false;
1689:     bool z_nodefaultlib = false;
1690:     bool z_now = false;
1691:     bool z_origin = false;
1692:     bool z_relro = true;
1693:     bool z_shstk = false;
1694:     bool z_text = false;
1695:     i64 filler = -1;
1696:     i64 spare_dynamic_tags = 5;
1697:     i64 thread_count = 0;
1698:     std::string_view emulation;
1699:     std::optional<Glob> unique;
1700:     std::optional<u64> physical_image_base;
1701:     std::optional<u64> shuffle_sections_seed;
1702:     std::string Map;
1703:     std::string chroot;

1705:     std::string directory;
1706:     std::string dynamic_linker;
1707:     std::string entry = "_start";
1708:     std::string fini = "_fini";
1709:     std::string init = "_init";
1710:     std::string output = "a.out";
1711:     std::string package_metadata;
1712:     std::string plugin;
1713:     std::string rpaths;
1714:     std::string soname;
1715:     std::string sysroot;

1723:     std::vector<std::string> library_paths;
1724:     std::vector<std::string> plugin_opt;
1725:     std::vector<std::string> version_definitions;

1727:     std::vector<std::string_view> exclude_libs;
1728:     std::vector<std::string_view> filter;
1729:     std::vector<std::string_view> require_defined;
1730:     std::vector<std::string_view> trace_symbol;
1731:     std::vector<std::string_view> undefined;
1732:     u64 image_base = 0x200000;
1733:   } arg;
1734: 
1735:   std::vector<VersionPattern> version_patterns;
1736:   u16 default_version = VER_NDX_GLOBAL;
1737:   bool default_version_from_version_script = false; // true if default_version is set by a wildcard in version script.
1738:   i64 page_size = -1;
1739: 
1740:   // Reader context
1741:   bool as_needed = false;
1742:   bool whole_archive = false;
1743:   bool is_static;
1744:   bool in_lib = false;
1745:   i64 file_priority = 10000;
1746:   std::unordered_set<std::string_view> visited;
1747:   tbb::task_group tg;
1748: 
1749:   bool has_error = false;
1750:   bool has_lto_object = false;
1751: 
1752:   // Symbol table
1753:   tbb::concurrent_hash_map<std::string_view, Symbol<E>, HashCmp> symbol_map;
1754:   tbb::concurrent_hash_map<std::string_view, ComdatGroup, HashCmp> comdat_groups;
1755:   tbb::concurrent_vector<std::unique_ptr<MergedSection<E>>> merged_sections;
1756:

1764:   tbb::concurrent_vector<std::unique_ptr<Chunk<E>>> chunk_pool;
1765:   tbb::concurrent_vector<std::unique_ptr<OutputSection<E>>> osec_pool;
1766:

1768:   std::vector<SymbolAux<E>> symbol_aux;

1771:   std::vector<std::string_view> cmdline_args;
1772:

1774:   std::vector<ObjectFile<E> *> objs;
1775:   std::vector<SharedFile<E> *> dsos;

1786:   std::atomic_bool needs_tlsld = false;
1787:   std::atomic_bool has_textrel = false;
1788:   std::atomic_uint32_t num_ifunc_dynrels = 0;
1789: 
1790:   tbb::concurrent_hash_map<std::string_view, std::vector<std::string>> undef_errors;
1791: 
1792:   // Output chunks
1793:   OutputEhdr<E> *ehdr = nullptr;
1794:   OutputShdr<E> *shdr = nullptr;
1795:   OutputPhdr<E> *phdr = nullptr;
1796:   InterpSection<E> *interp = nullptr;
1797:   GotSection<E> *got = nullptr;
1798:   GotPltSection<E> *gotplt = nullptr;
1799:   RelPltSection<E> *relplt = nullptr;
1800:   RelDynSection<E> *reldyn = nullptr;
1801:   RelrDynSection<E> *relrdyn = nullptr;
1802:   DynamicSection<E> *dynamic = nullptr;
1803:   StrtabSection<E> *strtab = nullptr;
1804:   DynstrSection<E> *dynstr = nullptr;
1805:   HashSection<E> *hash = nullptr;
1806:   GnuHashSection<E> *gnu_hash = nullptr;
1807:   ShstrtabSection<E> *shstrtab = nullptr;
1808:   PltSection<E> *plt = nullptr;
1809:   PltGotSection<E> *pltgot = nullptr;
1810:   SymtabSection<E> *symtab = nullptr;
1811:   SymtabShndxSection<E> *symtab_shndx = nullptr;
1812:   DynsymSection<E> *dynsym = nullptr;
1813:   EhFrameSection<E> *eh_frame = nullptr;
1814:   EhFrameHdrSection<E> *eh_frame_hdr = nullptr;

1817:   CopyrelSection<E> *copyrel_relro = nullptr;
