1823:   NotePropertySection<E> *note_property = nullptr;
1824:   GdbIndexSection<E> *gdb_index = nullptr;
1825:   RelroPaddingSection<E> *relro_padding = nullptr;
1826: 
1827:   [[no_unique_address]] ContextExtras<E> extra;
1828: 
1829:   // For --gdb-index
1830:   Chunk<E> *debug_info = nullptr;
1831:   Chunk<E> *debug_abbrev = nullptr;
1832:   Chunk<E> *debug_ranges = nullptr;
1833:   Chunk<E> *debug_addr = nullptr;
1834:   Chunk<E> *debug_rnglists = nullptr;
1835: 
1836:   // For thread-local variables
1837:   u64 tls_begin = 0;
1838:   u64 tp_addr = 0;
1839:   u64 dtp_addr = 0;
1840: 
1841:   // Linker-synthesized symbols
1842:   Symbol<E> *_DYNAMIC = nullptr;
1843:   Symbol<E> *_GLOBAL_OFFSET_TABLE_ = nullptr;
1844:   Symbol<E> *_PROCEDURE_LINKAGE_TABLE_ = nullptr;
1845:   Symbol<E> *_TLS_MODULE_BASE_ = nullptr;
1846:   Symbol<E> *__GNU_EH_FRAME_HDR = nullptr;
1847:   Symbol<E> *__bss_start = nullptr;
1848:   Symbol<E> *__dso_handle = nullptr;
1849:   Symbol<E> *__ehdr_start = nullptr;
1850:   Symbol<E> *__executable_start = nullptr;
1851:   Symbol<E> *__exidx_end = nullptr;
1852:   Symbol<E> *__exidx_start = nullptr;
1853:   Symbol<E> *__fini_array_end = nullptr;
1854:   Symbol<E> *__fini_array_start = nullptr;
1855:   Symbol<E> *__global_pointer = nullptr;
1856:   Symbol<E> *__init_array_end = nullptr;
1857:   Symbol<E> *__init_array_start = nullptr;
1858:   Symbol<E> *__preinit_array_end = nullptr;
1859:   Symbol<E> *__preinit_array_start = nullptr;
1860:   Symbol<E> *__rel_iplt_end = nullptr;
1861:   Symbol<E> *__rel_iplt_start = nullptr;
1862:   Symbol<E> *_edata = nullptr;
1863:   Symbol<E> *_end = nullptr;
1864:   Symbol<E> *_etext = nullptr;
1865:   Symbol<E> *edata = nullptr;
1866:   Symbol<E> *end = nullptr;
1867:   Symbol<E> *etext = nullptr;
1868: };
1869: 
1870: template <typename E>
1871: std::string_view get_machine_type(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf);
1872: 
1873: template <typename E>
1874: MappedFile<Context<E>> *open_library(Context<E> &ctx, std::string path);
1875: 
1876: template <typename E>
1877: MappedFile<Context<E>> *find_library(Context<E> &ctx, std::string path);
1878: 
1879: template <typename E>
1880: void read_file(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf);
1881: 
1882: template <typename E>
1883: int elf_main(int argc, char **argv);
1884: 
1885: int main(int argc, char **argv);
1886: 
1887: template <typename E>
1888: std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, const InputFile<E> &file);
1889: 
1890: //
1891: // Symbol
1892: //
1893: 
1894: enum {
1895:   NEEDS_GOT       = 1 << 0,
1896:   NEEDS_PLT       = 1 << 1,
1897:   NEEDS_CPLT      = 1 << 2,
1898:   NEEDS_GOTTP     = 1 << 3,
1899:   NEEDS_TLSGD     = 1 << 4,
1900:   NEEDS_COPYREL   = 1 << 5,
1901:   NEEDS_TLSDESC   = 1 << 6,
1902:   NEEDS_PPC_OPD   = 1 << 7, // for PPCv1
1903: };
1904: 
1905: // A struct to hold target-dependent symbol members.
1906: template <typename E>
1907: struct SymbolExtras {};
1908: 
1909: template <typename E> requires needs_thunk<E>
1910: struct SymbolExtras<E> {
1911:   // For range extension thunks
1912:   i16 thunk_idx = -1;
1913:   i16 thunk_sym_idx = -1;
1914: };
1915: 
1916: // Flags for Symbol<E>::get_addr()
1917: enum {
1918:   NO_PLT = 1 << 0, // Request an address other than .plt
1919:   NO_OPD = 1 << 1, // Request an address other than .opd (PPC64V1 only)
1920: };
1921: 
1922: // Symbol class represents a defined symbol.
1923: //
1924: // A symbol has not only one but several different addresses if it
1925: // has PLT or GOT entries. This class provides various functions to
1926: // compute different addresses.
1927: template <typename E>
1928: class Symbol {
1929: public:
1930:   Symbol() = default;
1931:   Symbol(std::string_view name) : nameptr(name.data()), namelen(name.size()) {}
1932:   Symbol(const Symbol<E> &other) : Symbol(other.name()) {}
1933: 
1934:   u64 get_addr(Context<E> &ctx, i64 flags = 0) const;
1935:   u64 get_got_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1936:   u64 get_gotplt_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1937:   u64 get_gottp_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1938:   u64 get_tlsgd_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1939:   u64 get_tlsdesc_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1940:   u64 get_plt_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1941:   u64 get_opd_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1942: 
1943:   void set_got_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1944:   void set_gottp_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1945:   void set_tlsgd_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1946:   void set_tlsdesc_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1947:   void set_plt_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1948:   void set_pltgot_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1949:   void set_opd_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1950:   void set_dynsym_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx);
1951: 
1952:   i32 get_got_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1953:   i32 get_gottp_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1954:   i32 get_tlsgd_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1955:   i32 get_tlsdesc_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1956:   i32 get_plt_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1957:   i32 get_pltgot_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1958:   i32 get_opd_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1959:   i32 get_dynsym_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1960: 
1961:   bool has_plt(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1962:   bool has_got(Context<E> &ctx) const { return get_got_idx(ctx) != -1; }
1963:   bool has_gottp(Context<E> &ctx) const { return get_gottp_idx(ctx) != -1; }
1964:   bool has_tlsgd(Context<E> &ctx) const { return get_tlsgd_idx(ctx) != -1; }
1965:   bool has_tlsdesc(Context<E> &ctx) const { return get_tlsdesc_idx(ctx) != -1; }
1966:   bool has_opd(Context<E> &ctx) const { return get_opd_idx(ctx) != -1; }
1967: 
1968:   u32 get_djb_hash(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1969:   void set_djb_hash(Context<E> &ctx, u32 hash);
1970: 
1971:   bool is_absolute() const;
1972:   bool is_relative() const { return !is_absolute(); }
1973:   bool is_local(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1974:   bool is_ifunc() const { return get_type() == STT_GNU_IFUNC; }
1975:   bool is_remaining_undef_weak() const;
1976: 
1977:   InputSection<E> *get_input_section() const;
1978:   Chunk<E> *get_output_section() const;
1979:   SectionFragment<E> *get_frag() const;
1980: 
1981:   void set_input_section(InputSection<E> *);
1982:   void set_output_section(Chunk<E> *);
1983:   void set_frag(SectionFragment<E> *);
1984: 
1985:   void set_name(std::string_view);
1986:   std::string_view name() const;
1987: 
1988:   u32 get_type() const;
1989:   std::string_view get_version() const;
1990:   i64 get_output_sym_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const;
1991:   const ElfSym<E> &esym() const;
1992:   void add_aux(Context<E> &ctx);
1993: 
1994:   // A symbol is owned by a file. If two or more files define the
1995:   // same symbol, the one with the strongest definition owns the symbol.
1996:   // If `file` is null, the symbol is equivalent to nonexistent.
1997:   InputFile<E> *file = nullptr;
1998: 
1999:   // A symbol usually belongs to an input section, but it can belong
2000:   // to a section fragment, an output section or nothing
2001:   // (i.e. absolute symbol). `origin` holds one of them. We use the
2002:   // least significant two bits to distinguish type.
2003:   enum : uintptr_t {
2004:     TAG_ABS  = 0b00,
2005:     TAG_ISEC = 0b01,
2006:     TAG_OSEC = 0b10,
2007:     TAG_FRAG = 0b11,
2008:     TAG_MASK = 0b11,
2009:   };
2010: 
2011:   uintptr_t origin = 0;
2012: 
2013:   // `value` contains symbol value. If it's an absolute symbol, it is
2014:   // equivalent to its address. If it belongs to an input section or a
2015:   // section fragment, value is added to the base of the input section
2016:   // to yield an address.
2017:   u64 value = 0;
2018: 
2019:   const char *nameptr = nullptr;
2020:   i32 namelen = 0;
2021: 
2022:   // Index into the symbol table of the owner file.
2023:   i32 sym_idx = -1;
2024: 
2025:   i32 aux_idx = -1;
2026:   u16 ver_idx = 0;
2027: 
2028:   // `flags` has NEEDS_ flags.
2029:   Atomic<u8> flags = 0;
2030: 
2031:   tbb::spin_mutex mu;
2032:   Atomic<u8> visibility = STV_DEFAULT;
2033: 
2034:   bool is_weak : 1 = false;
2035:   bool write_to_symtab : 1 = false; // for --strip-all and the like
2036:   bool is_traced : 1 = false;       // for --trace-symbol
2037:   bool is_wrapped : 1 = false;      // for --wrap
2038: 
2039:   // If a symbol can be resolved to a symbol in a different ELF file at
2040:   // runtime, `is_imported` is true. If a symbol is a dynamic symbol and
2041:   // can be used by other ELF file at runtime, `is_exported` is true.
2042:   //
2043:   // Note that both can be true at the same time. Such symbol represents
2044:   // a function or data exported from this ELF file which can be
2045:   // imported by other definition at runtime. That is actually a usual
2046:   // exported symbol when creating a DSO. In other words, a dynamic
2047:   // symbol exported by a DSO is usually imported by itself.
2048:   //
2049:   // If is_imported is true and is_exported is false, it is a dynamic
2050:   // symbol just imported from other DSO.
2051:   //
2052:   // If is_imported is false and is_exported is true, there are two
2053:   // possible cases. If we are creating an executable, we know that
2054:   // exported symbols cannot be intercepted by any DSO (because the
2055:   // dynamic loader searches a dynamic symbol from an executable before
2056:   // examining any DSOs), so any exported symbol is export-only in an
2057:   // executable. If we are creating a DSO, export-only symbols
2058:   // represent a protected symbol (i.e. a symbol whose visibility is
2059:   // STV_PROTECTED).
2060:   bool is_imported : 1 = false;
2061:   bool is_exported : 1 = false;
2062: 
2063:   // `is_canonical` is true if this symbol represents a "canonical" PLT.
2064:   // Here is the explanation as to what the canonical PLT is.
2065:   //
2066:   // In C/C++, the process-wide function pointer equality is guaranteed.
2067:   // That is, if you take an address of a function `foo`, it's always
2068:   // evaluated to the same address wherever you do that.
2069:   //
2070:   // For the sake of explanation, assume that `libx.so` exports a
2071:   // function symbol `foo`, and there's a program that uses `libx.so`.
2072:   // Both `libx.so` and the main executable take the address of `foo`,
2073:   // which must be evaluated to the same address because of the above
2074:   // guarantee.
2075:   //
2076:   // If the main executable is position-independent code (PIC), `foo` is
2077:   // evaluated to the beginning of the function code, as you would have
2078:   // expected. The address of `foo` is stored to GOTs, and the machine
2079:   // code that takes the address of `foo` reads the GOT entries at
2080:   // runtime.
2081:   //
2082:   // However, if it's not PIC, the main executable's code was compiled
2083:   // to not use GOT (note that shared objects are always PIC, only
2084:   // executables can be non-PIC). It instead assumes that `foo` (and any
2085:   // other global variables/functions) has an address that is fixed at
2086:   // link-time. This assumption is correct if `foo` is in the same
2087:   // position-dependent executable, but it's not if `foo` is imported
2088:   // from some other DSO at runtime.
2089:   //
2090:   // In this case, we use the address of the `foo`'s PLT entry in the
2091:   // main executable (whose address is fixed at link-time) as its
2092:   // address. In order to guarantee pointer equality, we also need to
2093:   // fill foo's GOT entries in DSOs with the addres of the foo's PLT
2094:   // entry instead of `foo`'s real address. We can do that by setting a
2095:   // symbol value to `foo`'s dynamic symbol. If a symbol value is set,
2096:   // the dynamic loader initialize `foo`'s GOT entries with that value
2097:   // instead of the symbol's real address.
2098:   //
2099:   // We call such PLT entry in the main executable as "canonical".
2100:   // If `foo` has a canonical PLT, its address is evaluated to its
2101:   // canonical PLT's address. Otherwise, it's evaluated to `foo`'s
2102:   // address.
2103:   //
2104:   // Only non-PIC main executables may have canonical PLTs. PIC
2105:   // executables and shared objects never have a canonical PLT.
2106:   //
2107:   // This bit manages if we need to make this symbol's PLT canonical.
2108:   // This bit is meaningful only when the symbol has a PLT entry.
2109:   bool is_canonical : 1 = false;
2110: 
2111:   // If an input object file is not compiled with -fPIC (or with
2112:   // -fno-PIC), the file not position independent. That means the
2113:   // machine code included in the object file does not use GOT to access
2114:   // global variables. Instead, it assumes that addresses of global
2115:   // variables are known at link-time.
2116:   //
2117:   // Let's say `libx.so` exports a global variable `foo`, and a main
2118:   // executable uses the variable. If the executable is not compiled
2119:   // with -fPIC, we can't simply apply a relocation that refers `foo`
2120:   // because `foo`'s address is not known at link-time.
2121:   //
2122:   // In this case, we could print out the "recompile with -fPIC" error
2123:   // message, but there's a way to workaround.
2124:   //
2125:   // The loader supports a feature so-called "copy relocations".
2126:   // A copy relocation instructs the loader to copy data from a DSO to a
2127:   // specified location in the main executable. By using this feature,
2128:   // we can copy `foo`'s data to a BSS region at runtime. With that,
2129:   // we can apply relocations agianst `foo` as if `foo` existed in the
2130:   // main executable's BSS area, whose address is known at link-time.
2131:   //
2132:   // Copy relocations are used only by position-dependent executables.
2133:   // Position-independent executables and DSOs don't need them because
2134:   // they use GOT to access global variables.
2135:   //
2136:   // `has_copyrel` is true if we need to emit a copy relocation for this
2137:   // symbol. If the original symbol in a DSO is in a read-only memory
2138:   // region, `is_copyrel_readonly` is set to true so that the copied data
2139:   // will become read-only at run-time.
2140:   bool has_copyrel : 1 = false;
2141:   bool is_copyrel_readonly : 1 = false;
2142: 
2143:   // For LTO. True if the symbol is referenced by a regular object (as
2144:   // opposed to IR object).
2145:   bool referenced_by_regular_obj : 1 = false;
2146: 
2147:   // Target-dependent extra members.
2148:   [[no_unique_address]] SymbolExtras<E> extra;
2149: };
2150: 
2151: // If we haven't seen the same `key` before, create a new instance
2152: // of Symbol and returns it. Otherwise, returns the previously-
2153: // instantiated object. `key` is usually the same as `name`.
2154: template <typename E>
2155: Symbol<E> *get_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view key,
2156:                       std::string_view name) {
2157:   typename decltype(ctx.symbol_map)::const_accessor acc;
2158:   ctx.symbol_map.insert(acc, {key, Symbol<E>(name)});
2159:   return const_cast<Symbol<E> *>(&acc->second);
2160: }
2161: 
2162: template <typename E>
2163: Symbol<E> *get_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name) {
2164:   return get_symbol(ctx, name, name);
2165: }
2166: 
2167: template <typename E>
2168: std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, const Symbol<E> &sym) {
2169:   if (opt_demangle)
2170:     out << demangle(sym.name());
2171:   else
2172:     out << sym.name();
2173:   return out;
2174: }
2175: 
2176: //
2177: // Inline objects and functions
2178: //
2179: 
2180: template <typename E>
2181: inline i64 FdeRecord<E>::size(ObjectFile<E> &file) const {
2182:   return *(U32<E> *)(file.cies[cie_idx].contents.data() + input_offset) + 4;
2183: }
2184: 
2185: template <typename E>
2186: inline std::string_view FdeRecord<E>::get_contents(ObjectFile<E> &file) const {
2187:   return file.cies[cie_idx].contents.substr(input_offset, size(file));
2188: }
2189: 
2190: template <typename E>
2191: inline std::span<ElfRel<E>>
2192: FdeRecord<E>::get_rels(ObjectFile<E> &file) const {
2193:   std::span<ElfRel<E>> rels = file.cies[cie_idx].rels;
2194:   i64 end = rel_idx;
2195:   while (end < rels.size() && rels[end].r_offset < input_offset + size(file))
2196:     end++;
2197:   return rels.subspan(rel_idx, end - rel_idx);
2198: }
2199: 
2200: template <typename E>
2201: inline std::ostream &
2202: operator<<(std::ostream &out, const InputSection<E> &isec) {
2203:   out << isec.file << ":(" << isec.name() << ")";
2204:   return out;
2205: }
2206: 
2207: template <typename E>
2208: inline u64 SectionFragment<E>::get_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2209:   return output_section.shdr.sh_addr + offset;
2210: }
2211: 
2212: template <typename E>
2213: inline void InputSection<E>::kill() {
2214:   if (is_alive.exchange(false))
2215:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : get_fdes())
2216:       fde.is_alive = false;
2217: }
2218: 
2219: template <typename E>
2220: inline u64 InputSection<E>::get_addr() const {
2221:   return output_section->shdr.sh_addr + offset;
2222: }
2223: 
2224: template <typename E>
2225: inline std::string_view InputSection<E>::name() const {
2226:   if (file.elf_sections.size() <= shndx)
2227:     return ".common";
2228:   return file.shstrtab.data() + file.elf_sections[shndx].sh_name;
2229: }
2230: 
2231: template <typename E>
2232: inline i64 InputSection<E>::get_priority() const {
2233:   return ((i64)file.priority << 32) | shndx;
2234: }
2235: 
2236: template <typename E>
2237: i64 get_addend(u8 *loc, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
2238: 
2239: template <typename E> requires E::is_rela && (!is_sh4<E>)
2240: inline i64 get_addend(u8 *loc, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
2241:   return rel.r_addend;
2242: }
2243: 
2244: template <typename E>
2245: i64 get_addend(InputSection<E> &isec, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
2246:   return get_addend((u8 *)isec.contents.data() + rel.r_offset, rel);
2247: }
2248: 
2249: template <typename E>
2250: void write_addend(u8 *loc, i64 val, const ElfRel<E> &rel);
2251: 
2252: template <typename E> requires E::is_rela
2253: void write_addend(u8 *loc, i64 val, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {}
2254: 
2255: template <typename E>
2256: inline const ElfShdr<E> &InputSection<E>::shdr() const {
2257:   if (shndx < file.elf_sections.size())
2258:     return file.elf_sections[shndx];
2259:   return file.elf_sections2[shndx - file.elf_sections.size()];
2260: }
2261: 
2262: template <typename E>
2263: inline std::span<ElfRel<E>> InputSection<E>::get_rels(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2264:   if (relsec_idx == -1)
2265:     return {};
2266:   return file.template get_data<ElfRel<E>>(ctx, file.elf_sections[relsec_idx]);
2267: }
2268: 
2269: template <typename E>
2270: inline std::span<FdeRecord<E>> InputSection<E>::get_fdes() const {
2271:   if (fde_begin == -1)
2272:     return {};
2273:   std::span<FdeRecord<E>> span(file.fdes);
2274:   return span.subspan(fde_begin, fde_end - fde_begin);
2275: }
2276: 
2277: template <typename E>
2278: std::pair<SectionFragment<E> *, i64>
2279: InputSection<E>::get_fragment(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
2280:   assert(!(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_ALLOC));
2281: 
2282:   const ElfSym<E> &esym = file.elf_syms[rel.r_sym];
2283:   if (esym.st_type == STT_SECTION)
2284:     if (std::unique_ptr<MergeableSection<E>> &m =
2285:         file.mergeable_sections[file.get_shndx(esym)])
2286:       return m->get_fragment(esym.st_value + get_addend(*this, rel));
2287: 
2288:   return {nullptr, 0};
2289: }
2290: 
2291: template <typename E>
2292: u64 InputSection<E>::get_thunk_addr(i64 idx) {
2293:   if constexpr (needs_thunk<E>) {
2294:     RangeExtensionRef ref = extra.range_extn[idx];
2295:     assert(ref.thunk_idx != -1);
2296:     return output_section->thunks[ref.thunk_idx]->get_addr(ref.sym_idx);
2297:   }
2298:   unreachable();
2299: }
2300: 
2301: // Input object files may contain duplicate code for inline functions
2302: // and such. Linkers de-duplicate them at link-time. However, linkers
2303: // generaly don't remove debug info for de-duplicated functions because
2304: // doing that requires parsing the entire debug section.
2305: //
2306: // Instead, linkers write "tombstone" values to dead debug info records
2307: // instead of bogus values so that debuggers can skip them.
2308: //
2309: // This function returns a tombstone value for the symbol if the symbol
2310: // refers a dead debug info section.
2311: template <typename E>
2312: inline std::optional<u64>
2313: InputSection<E>::get_tombstone(Symbol<E> &sym, SectionFragment<E> *frag) {
2314:   if (frag)
2315:     return {};
2316: 
2317:   InputSection<E> *isec = sym.get_input_section();
2318: 
2319:   // Setting a tombstone is a special feature for a dead debug section.
2320:   if (!isec || isec->is_alive)
2321:     return {};
2322: 
2323:   std::string_view s = name();
2324:   if (!s.starts_with(".debug"))
2325:     return {};
2326: 
2327:   // If the section was dead due to ICF, we don't want to emit debug
2328:   // info for that section but want to set real values to .debug_line so
2329:   // that users can set a breakpoint inside a merged section.
2330:   if (isec->is_killed_by_icf() && s == ".debug_line")
2331:     return {};
2332: 
2333:   // 0 is an invalid value in most debug info sections, so we use it
2334:   // as a tombstone value. .debug_loc and .debug_ranges reserve 0 as
2335:   // the terminator marker, so we use 1 if that's the case.
2336:   return (s == ".debug_loc" || s == ".debug_ranges") ? 1 : 0;
2337: }
2338: 
2339: template <typename E>
2340: inline bool
2341: InputSection<E>::is_relr_reloc(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfRel<E> &rel) const {
2342:   return ctx.arg.pack_dyn_relocs_relr &&
2343:          !(shdr().sh_flags & SHF_EXECINSTR) &&
2344:          (shdr().sh_addralign % sizeof(Word<E>)) == 0 &&
2345:          (rel.r_offset % sizeof(Word<E>)) == 0;
2346: }
2347: 
2348: template<typename E>
2349: inline bool InputSection<E>::is_killed_by_icf() const {
2350:   return this->leader && this->leader != this;
2351: }
2352: 
2353: template <typename E>
2354: std::pair<SectionFragment<E> *, i64>
2355: MergeableSection<E>::get_fragment(i64 offset) {
2356:   std::vector<u32> &vec = frag_offsets;
2357:   auto it = std::upper_bound(vec.begin(), vec.end(), offset);
2358:   i64 idx = it - 1 - vec.begin();
2359:   return {fragments[idx], offset - vec[idx]};
2360: }
2361: 
2362: template <typename E>
2363: template <typename T>
2364: inline std::span<T>
2365: InputFile<E>::get_data(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfShdr<E> &shdr) {
2366:   std::string_view view = this->get_string(ctx, shdr);
2367:   if (view.size() % sizeof(T))
2368:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": corrupted section";
2369:   return {(T *)view.data(), view.size() / sizeof(T)};
2370: }
2371: 
2372: template <typename E>
2373: template <typename T>
2374: inline std::span<T> InputFile<E>::get_data(Context<E> &ctx, i64 idx) {
2375:   if (elf_sections.size() <= idx)
2376:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid section index";
2377:   return this->template get_data<T>(elf_sections[idx]);
2378: }
2379: 
2380: template <typename E>
2381: inline std::string_view
2382: InputFile<E>::get_string(Context<E> &ctx, const ElfShdr<E> &shdr) {
2383:   u8 *begin = mf->data + shdr.sh_offset;
2384:   u8 *end = begin + shdr.sh_size;
2385:   if (mf->data + mf->size < end)
2386:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": section header is out of range: " << shdr.sh_offset;
2387:   return {(char *)begin, (size_t)(end - begin)};
2388: }
2389: 
2390: template <typename E>
2391: inline std::string_view InputFile<E>::get_string(Context<E> &ctx, i64 idx) {
2392:   assert(idx < elf_sections.size());
2393: 
2394:   if (elf_sections.size() <= idx)
2395:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid section index: " << idx;
2396:   return this->get_string(ctx, elf_sections[idx]);
2397: }
2398: 
2399: template <typename E>
2400: inline std::span<Symbol<E> *> InputFile<E>::get_global_syms() {
2401:   return std::span<Symbol<E> *>(this->symbols).subspan(this->first_global);
2402: }
2403: 
2404: template <typename E>
2405: inline i64 ObjectFile<E>::get_shndx(const ElfSym<E> &esym) {
2406:   assert(&this->elf_syms[0] <= &esym);
2407:   assert(&esym <= &this->elf_syms[this->elf_syms.size() - 1]);
2408: 
2409:   if (esym.st_shndx == SHN_XINDEX)
2410:     return symtab_shndx_sec[&esym - &this->elf_syms[0]];
2411:   return esym.st_shndx;
2412: }
2413: 
2414: template <typename E>
2415: inline InputSection<E> *ObjectFile<E>::get_section(const ElfSym<E> &esym) {
2416:   return sections[get_shndx(esym)].get();
2417: }
2418: 
2419: template <typename E>
2420: OutputSection<E> *find_section(Context<E> &ctx, u32 sh_type) {
2421:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2422:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
2423:       if (osec->shdr.sh_type == sh_type)
2424:         return osec;
2425:   return nullptr;
2426: }
2427: 
2428: template <typename E>
2429: OutputSection<E> *find_section(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name) {
2430:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks)
2431:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
2432:       if (osec->name == name)
2433:         return osec;
2434:   return nullptr;
2435: }
2436: 
2437: template <typename E>
2438: u64 Symbol<E>::get_addr(Context<E> &ctx, i64 flags) const {
2439:   if (SectionFragment<E> *frag = get_frag()) {
2440:     if (!frag->is_alive) {
2441:       // This condition is met if a non-alloc section refers an
2442:       // alloc section and if the referenced piece of data is
2443:       // garbage-collected. Typically, this condition occurs if a
2444:       // debug info section refers a string constant in .rodata.
2445:       return 0;
2446:     }
2447: 
2448:     return frag->get_addr(ctx) + value;
2449:   }
2450: 
2451:   if (has_copyrel) {
2452:     return is_copyrel_readonly
2453:       ? ctx.copyrel_relro->shdr.sh_addr + value
2454:       : ctx.copyrel->shdr.sh_addr + value;
2455:   }
2456: 
2457:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>)
2458:     if (!(flags & NO_OPD) && has_opd(ctx))
2459:       return get_opd_addr(ctx);
2460: 
2461:   if (!(flags & NO_PLT) && has_plt(ctx)) {
2462:     assert(is_imported || is_ifunc());
2463:     return get_plt_addr(ctx);
2464:   }
2465: 
2466:   InputSection<E> *isec = get_input_section();
2467:   if (!isec)
2468:     return value; // absolute symbol
2469: 
2470:   if (!isec->is_alive) {
2471:     if (isec->is_killed_by_icf())
2472:       return isec->leader->get_addr() + value;
2473: 
2474:     if (isec->name() == ".eh_frame") {
2475:       auto test = [this](const char *pattern) {
2476:       if (name() == pattern)
2477:         return true;
2478: 
2479:       // If LTO was enabled when building GCC and its CRT itself, then GCC
2480:       // will be able to move around the .eh_frame marker symbols. A suffix
2481:       // seems to be attached in such cases; we detect it here.
2482:       //
2483:       // This tests for `<pattern>.lto_priv.*`. It's a little convoluted
2484:       // but this is the best we can do without compile-time string
2485:       // concatenation.
2486:       if (name().starts_with(pattern) && name().substr(strlen(pattern)).starts_with(".lto_priv."))
2487:         return true;
2488: 
2489:       return false;
2490:     };
2491: 
2492:     // .eh_frame contents are parsed and reconstructed by the linker,
2493:     // so pointing to a specific location in a source .eh_frame
2494:     // section doesn't make much sense. However, CRT files contain
2495:     // symbols pointing to the very beginning and ending of the section.
2496:     if (test("__EH_FRAME_BEGIN__") || test("__EH_FRAME_LIST__") ||
2497:           test(".eh_frame_seg") || esym().st_type == STT_SECTION)
2498:     return ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_addr;
2499: 
2500:       if (test("__FRAME_END__") || test("__EH_FRAME_LIST_END__"))
2501:         return ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_addr + ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_size;
2502: 
2503:       // ARM object files contain "$d" local symbol at the beginning
2504:       // of data sections. Their values are not significant for .eh_frame,
2505:       // so we just treat them as offset 0.
2506:       if (name() == "$d" || name().starts_with("$d."))
2507:         return ctx.eh_frame->shdr.sh_addr;
2508: 
2509:       Fatal(ctx) << "symbol referring .eh_frame is not supported: "
2510:                  << *this << " " << *file;
2511:     }
2512: 
2513:     // The control can reach here if there's a relocation that refers
2514:     // a local symbol belonging to a comdat group section. This is a
2515:     // violation of the spec, as all relocations should use only global
2516:     // symbols of comdat members. However, .eh_frame tends to have such
2517:     // relocations.
2518:     return 0;
2519:   }
2520: 
2521:   return isec->get_addr() + value;
2522: }
2523: 
2524: template <typename E>
2525: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_got_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2526:   return ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + get_got_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
2527: }
2528: 
2529: template <typename E>
2530: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_gotplt_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2531:   assert(get_plt_idx(ctx) != -1);
2532:   return ctx.gotplt->shdr.sh_addr + GotPltSection<E>::HDR_SIZE +
2533:          get_plt_idx(ctx) * GotPltSection<E>::ENTRY_SIZE;
2534: }
2535: 
2536: template <typename E>
2537: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_gottp_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2538:   assert(get_gottp_idx(ctx) != -1);
2539:   return ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + get_gottp_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
2540: }
2541: 
2542: template <typename E>
2543: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_tlsgd_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2544:   assert(get_tlsgd_idx(ctx) != -1);
2545:   return ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + get_tlsgd_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
2546: }
2547: 
2548: template <typename E>
2549: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_tlsdesc_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2550:   assert(get_tlsdesc_idx(ctx) != -1);
2551:   return ctx.got->shdr.sh_addr + get_tlsdesc_idx(ctx) * sizeof(Word<E>);
2552: }
2553: 
2554: template <typename E>
2555: inline u64 to_plt_offset(i32 pltidx) {
2556:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v1<E>) {
2557:     // The PPC64 ELFv1 ABI requires PLT entries to vary in size
2558:     // depending on their indices. For entries whose PLT index is
2559:     // less than 32768, the entry size is 8 bytes. Other entries are
2560:     // 12 bytes long.
2561:     if (pltidx < 0x8000)
2562:       return E::plt_hdr_size + pltidx * 8;
2563:     return E::plt_hdr_size + 0x8000 * 8 + (pltidx - 0x8000) * 12;
2564:   } else {
2565:     return E::plt_hdr_size + pltidx * E::plt_size;
2566:   }
2567: }
2568: 
2569: template <typename E>
2570: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_plt_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2571:   if (i32 idx = get_plt_idx(ctx); idx != -1)
2572:     return ctx.plt->shdr.sh_addr + to_plt_offset<E>(idx);
2573:   return ctx.pltgot->shdr.sh_addr + get_pltgot_idx(ctx) * E::pltgot_size;
2574: }
2575: 
2576: template <typename E>
2577: inline u64 Symbol<E>::get_opd_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2578:   assert(get_opd_idx(ctx) != -1);
2579:   return ctx.extra.opd->shdr.sh_addr +
2580:          get_opd_idx(ctx) * PPC64OpdSection::ENTRY_SIZE;
2581: }
2582: 
2583: template <typename E>
2584: inline void Symbol<E>::set_got_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx) {
2585:   assert(aux_idx != -1);
2586:   assert(ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].got_idx < 0);
2587:   ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].got_idx = idx;
2588: }
2589: 
2590: template <typename E>
2591: inline void Symbol<E>::set_gottp_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx) {
2592:   assert(aux_idx != -1);
2593:   assert(ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].gottp_idx < 0);
2594:   ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].gottp_idx = idx;
2595: }
2596: 
2597: template <typename E>
2598: inline void Symbol<E>::set_tlsgd_idx(Context<E> &ctx, i32 idx) {
2599:   assert(aux_idx != -1);
2600:   assert(ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].tlsgd_idx < 0);
2601:   ctx.symbol_aux[aux_idx].tlsgd_idx = idx;
2602: }
2603:

2624:

2692:   return get_plt_idx(ctx) != -1 || get_pltgot_idx(ctx) != -1;
2693: }
