2695: template <typename E>
2696: inline bool Symbol<E>::is_absolute() const {
2697:   if (file && file->is_dso)
2698:     return esym().is_abs();
2699: 
2700:   return !is_imported && !get_frag() && !get_input_section() &&
2701:          !get_output_section();
2702: }
2703: 
2704: template <typename E>
2705: inline bool Symbol<E>::is_local(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2706:   if (ctx.arg.relocatable)
2707:     return esym().st_bind == STB_LOCAL;
2708:   return !is_imported && !is_exported;
2709: }
2710: 
2711: // A remaining weak undefined symbol is promoted to a dynamic symbol
2712: // in DSO and resolved to 0 in an executable. This function returns
2713: // true if it's latter.
2714: template<typename E>
2715: inline bool Symbol<E>::is_remaining_undef_weak() const {
2716:   return !is_imported && esym().is_undef_weak();
2717: }
2718: 
2719: template <typename E>
2720: inline InputSection<E> *Symbol<E>::get_input_section() const {
2721:   if ((origin & TAG_MASK) == TAG_ISEC)
2722:     return (InputSection<E> *)(origin & ~TAG_MASK);
2723:   return nullptr;
2724: }
2725: 
2726: template <typename E>
2727: inline Chunk<E> *Symbol<E>::get_output_section() const {
2728:   if ((origin & TAG_MASK) == TAG_OSEC)
2729:     return (Chunk<E> *)(origin & ~TAG_MASK);
2730:   return nullptr;
2731: }
2732: 
2733: template <typename E>
2734: inline SectionFragment<E> *Symbol<E>::get_frag() const {
2735:   if ((origin & TAG_MASK) == TAG_FRAG)
2736:     return (SectionFragment<E> *)(origin & ~TAG_MASK);
2737:   return nullptr;
2738: }
2739: 
2740: template <typename E>
2741: inline void Symbol<E>::set_input_section(InputSection<E> *isec) {
2742:   uintptr_t addr = (uintptr_t)isec;
2743:   assert((addr & TAG_MASK) == 0);
2744:   origin = addr | TAG_ISEC;
2745: }
2746: 
2747: template <typename E>
2748: inline void Symbol<E>::set_output_section(Chunk<E> *osec) {
2749:   uintptr_t addr = (uintptr_t)osec;
2750:   assert((addr & TAG_MASK) == 0);
2751:   origin = addr | TAG_OSEC;
2752: }
2753: 
2754: template <typename E>
2755: inline void Symbol<E>::set_frag(SectionFragment<E> *frag) {
2756:   uintptr_t addr = (uintptr_t)frag;
2757:   assert((addr & TAG_MASK) == 0);
2758:   origin = addr | TAG_FRAG;
2759: }
2760: 
2761: template <typename E>
2762: inline u32 Symbol<E>::get_type() const {
2763:   if (esym().st_type == STT_GNU_IFUNC && file->is_dso)
2764:     return STT_FUNC;
2765:   return esym().st_type;
2766: }
2767: 
2768: template <typename E>
2769: inline std::string_view Symbol<E>::get_version() const {
2770:   if (file->is_dso)
2771:     return ((SharedFile<E> *)file)->version_strings[ver_idx];
2772:   return "";
2773: }
2774: 
2775: template <typename E>
2776: inline i64 Symbol<E>::get_output_sym_idx(Context<E> &ctx) const {
2777:   i64 i = file->output_sym_indices[sym_idx];
2778:   assert(i != -1);
2779:   if (is_local(ctx))
2780:     return file->local_symtab_idx + i;
2781:   return file->global_symtab_idx + i;
2782: }
2783: 
2784: template <typename E>
2785: inline const ElfSym<E> &Symbol<E>::esym() const {
2786:   return file->elf_syms[sym_idx];
2787: }
2788: 
2789: template <typename E>
2790: inline void Symbol<E>::set_name(std::string_view name) {
2791:   nameptr = name.data();
2792:   namelen = name.size();
2793: }
2794: 
2795: template <typename E>
2796: inline std::string_view Symbol<E>::name() const {
2797:   return {nameptr, (size_t)namelen};
2798: }
2799: 
2800: template <typename E>
2801: inline void Symbol<E>::add_aux(Context<E> &ctx) {
2802:   if (aux_idx == -1) {
2803:     i64 sz = ctx.symbol_aux.size();
2804:     aux_idx = sz;
2805:     ctx.symbol_aux.resize(sz + 1);
2806:   }
2807: }
2808: 
2809: inline bool is_c_identifier(std::string_view s) {
2810:   if (s.empty())
2811:     return false;
2812: 
2813:   auto is_alpha = [](char c) {
2814:     return c == '_' || ('a' <= c && c <= 'z') || ('A' <= c && c <= 'Z');
2815:   };
2816: 
2817:   auto is_alnum = [&](char c) {
2818:     return is_alpha(c) || ('0' <= c && c <= '9');
2819:   };
2820: 
2821:   if (!is_alpha(s[0]))
2822:     return false;
2823:   for (i64 i = 1; i < s.size(); i++)
2824:     if (!is_alnum(s[i]))
2825:       return false;
2826:   return true;
2827: }
2828: 
2829: template <typename E>
2830: inline bool relax_tlsdesc(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
2831:   // TLSDESC relocs must be always relaxed for statically-linked
2832:   // executables even if -no-relax is given. It is because a
2833:   // statically-linked executable doesn't contain a tranpoline
2834:   // function needed for TLSDESC.
2835:   if (ctx.arg.is_static)
2836:     return true;
2837:   return ctx.arg.relax && !ctx.arg.shared && !sym.is_imported;
2838: }
2839: 
2840: } // namespace mold::elf

================
File: elf/thunks.cc
================
  1: #if MOLD_ARM32 || MOLD_ARM64 || MOLD_PPC32 || MOLD_PPC64V1 || MOLD_PPC64V2
  2: 
  3: // RISC instructions are usually up to 4 bytes long, so the immediates of
  4: // their branch instructions are naturally smaller than 32 bits.  This is
  5: // contrary to x86-64 on which branch instructions take 4 bytes immediates
  6: // and can jump to anywhere within PC ± 2 GiB.
  7: //
  8: // In fact, ARM32's branch instructions can jump only within ±16 MiB and
  9: // ARM64's ±128 MiB, for example. If a branch target is further than that,
 10: // we need to let it branch to a linker-synthesized code sequence that
 11: // construct a full 32 bit address in a register and jump there. That
 12: // linker-synthesized code is called "thunk".
 13: //
 14: // The function in this file creates thunks.
 15: //
 16: // Note that although thunks play an important role in an executable, they
 17: // don't take up too much space in it. For example, among the clang-16's
 18: // text segment whose size is ~300 MiB on ARM64, thunks in total occupy
 19: // only ~30 KiB or 0.01%. Of course the number depends on an ISA; we would
 20: // need more thunks on ARM32 whose branch range is shorter than ARM64.
 21: // That said, the total size of thunks still isn't that much. Therefore,
 22: // we don't need to try too hard to reduce thunk size to the absolute
 23: // minimum.
 24: 
 25: #include "mold.h"
 26: 
 27: #include <tbb/parallel_for.h>
 28: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
 29: 
 30: namespace mold::elf {
 31: 
 32: // Returns a branch reach in bytes for a given target.
 33: template <typename E>
 34: static consteval i64 max_distance() {
 35:   // ARM64's branch has 26 bits immediate. The immediate is padded with
 36:   // implicit two-bit zeros because all instructions are 4 bytes aligned
 37:   // and therefore the least two bits are always zero. So the branch
 38:   // operand is effectively 28 bits long. That means the branch range is
 39:   // [-2^27, 2^27) or PC ± 128 MiB.
 40:   if (is_arm64<E>)
 41:     return 1 << 27;
 42: 
 43:   // ARM32's Thumb branch has 24 bits immediate, and the instructions are
 44:   // aligned to 2, so it's effectively 25 bits. It's [-2^24, 2^24) or PC ±
 45:   // 16 MiB.
 46:   //
 47:   // ARM32's non-Thumb branches have twice longer range than its Thumb
 48:   // counterparts, but we conservatively use the Thumb's limitation.
 49:   if (is_arm32<E>)
 50:     return 1 << 24;
 51: 
 52:   // PPC's branch has 24 bits immediate, and the instructions are aligned
 53:   // to 4, therefore the reach is [-2^25, 2^25) or PC ± 32 MiB.
 54:   assert(is_ppc<E>);
 55:   return 1 << 25;
 56: }
 57: 
 58: // We create thunks for each 12.8/1.6/3.2 MiB code block for
 59: // ARM64/ARM32/PPC, respectively.
 60: template <typename E>
 61: static constexpr i64 batch_size = max_distance<E>() / 10;
 62: 
 63: // We assume that a single thunk group is smaller than 100 KiB.
 64: static constexpr i64 max_thunk_size = 102400;
 65: 
 66: // Returns true if a given relocation is of type used for function calls.
 67: template <typename E>
 68: static bool needs_thunk_rel(const ElfRel<E> &r) {
 69:   u32 ty = r.r_type;
 70: 
 71:   if constexpr (is_arm64<E>) {
 72:     return ty == R_AARCH64_JUMP26 || ty == R_AARCH64_CALL26;
 73:   } else if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
 74:     return ty == R_ARM_JUMP24 || ty == R_ARM_THM_JUMP24 ||
 75:            ty == R_ARM_CALL   || ty == R_ARM_THM_CALL   ||
 76:            ty == R_ARM_PLT32;
 77:   } else if constexpr (is_ppc32<E>) {
 78:     return ty == R_PPC_REL24  || ty == R_PPC_PLTREL24 || ty == R_PPC_LOCAL24PC;
 79:   } else {
 80:     static_assert(is_ppc64<E>);
 81:     return ty == R_PPC64_REL24 || ty == R_PPC64_REL24_NOTOC;
 82:   }
 83: }
 84: 
 85: template <typename E>
 86: static bool is_reachable(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec,
 87:                          Symbol<E> &sym, const ElfRel<E> &rel) {
 88:   // We create thunks with a pessimistic assumption that all
 89:   // out-of-section relocations would be out-of-range.
 90:   InputSection<E> *isec2 = sym.get_input_section();
 91:   if (!isec2 || isec.output_section != isec2->output_section)
 92:     return false;
 93: 
 94:   // Even if the target is the same section, we branch to its PLT
 95:   // if it has one. So a symbol with a PLT is also considered an
 96:   // out-of-section reference.
 97:   if (sym.has_plt(ctx))
 98:     return false;
 99: 
100:   // If the target section is in the same output section but
101:   // hasn't got any address yet, that's unreacahble.
102:   if (isec2->offset == -1)
103:     return false;
104: 
105:   // Thumb and ARM B instructions cannot be converted to BX, so we
106:   // always have to make them jump to a thunk to switch processor mode
107:   // even if their destinations are within their ranges.
108:   if constexpr (is_arm32<E>) {
109:     bool is_thumb = sym.get_addr(ctx) & 1;
110:     if ((rel.r_type == R_ARM_THM_JUMP24 && !is_thumb) ||
111:         (rel.r_type == R_ARM_JUMP24 && is_thumb) ||
112:         (rel.r_type == R_ARM_PLT32 && is_thumb))
113:       return false;
114:   }
115: 
116:   // PowerPC before Power9 lacks PC-relative load/store instructions.
117:   // Functions compiled for Power9 or earlier assume that r2 points to
118:   // GOT+0x8000, while those for Power10 uses r2 as a scratch register.
119:   // We need to a thunk to reconstruct r2 for interworking.
120:   if constexpr (is_ppc64v2<E>) {
121:     if (rel.r_type == R_PPC64_REL24 && !sym.esym().preserves_r2())
122:       return false;
123:     if (rel.r_type == R_PPC64_REL24_NOTOC && sym.esym().uses_toc())
124:       return false;
125:   }
126: 
127:   // Compute a distance between the relocated place and the symbol
128:   // and check if they are within reach.
129:   i64 S = sym.get_addr(ctx, NO_OPD);
130:   i64 A = get_addend(isec, rel);
131:   i64 P = isec.get_addr() + rel.r_offset;
132:   i64 val = S + A - P;
133:   return -max_distance<E>() <= val && val < max_distance<E>();
134: }
135: 
136: template <typename E>
137: static void reset_thunk(RangeExtensionThunk<E> &thunk) {
138:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : thunk.symbols) {
139:     sym->extra.thunk_idx = -1;
140:     sym->extra.thunk_sym_idx = -1;
141:     sym->flags = 0;
142:   }
143: }
144: 
145: // Scan relocations to collect symbols that need thunks.
146: template <typename E>
147: static void scan_rels(Context<E> &ctx, InputSection<E> &isec,
148:                       RangeExtensionThunk<E> &thunk) {
149:   std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = isec.get_rels(ctx);
150:   std::vector<RangeExtensionRef> &range_extn = isec.extra.range_extn;
151:   range_extn.resize(rels.size());
152: 
153:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
154:     const ElfRel<E> &rel = rels[i];
155:     if (!needs_thunk_rel(rel))
156:       continue;
157: 
158:     // Skip if the symbol is undefined. apply_reloc() will report an error.
159:     Symbol<E> &sym = *isec.file.symbols[rel.r_sym];
160:     if (!sym.file)
161:       continue;
162: 
163:     // Skip if the destination is within reach.
164:     if (is_reachable(ctx, isec, sym, rel))
165:       continue;
166: 
167:     // This relocation needs a thunk. If the symbol is already in a
168:     // previous thunk, reuse it.
169:     if (sym.extra.thunk_idx != -1) {
170:       range_extn[i].thunk_idx = sym.extra.thunk_idx;
171:       range_extn[i].sym_idx = sym.extra.thunk_sym_idx;
172:       continue;
173:     }
174: 
175:     // Otherwise, add the symbol to the current thunk if it's not
176:     // added already.
177:     range_extn[i].thunk_idx = thunk.thunk_idx;
178:     range_extn[i].sym_idx = -1;
179: 
180:     if (sym.flags.exchange(-1) == 0) {
181:       std::scoped_lock lock(thunk.mu);
182:       thunk.symbols.push_back(&sym);
183:     }
184:   }
185: }
186: 
187: template <typename E>
188: void create_range_extension_thunks(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSection<E> &osec) {
189:   std::span<InputSection<E> *> m = osec.members;
190:   if (m.empty())
191:     return;
192: 
193:   m[0]->offset = 0;
194: 
195:   // Initialize input sections with a dummy offset so that we can
196:   // distinguish sections that have got an address with the one who
197:   // haven't.
198:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)1, (i64)m.size(), [&](i64 i) {
199:     m[i]->offset = -1;
200:   });
201: 
202:   // We create thunks from the beginning of the section to the end.
203:   // We manage progress using four offsets which increase monotonically.
204:   // The locations they point to are always A <= B <= C <= D.
205:   //
206:   // A is the input section with the smallest address than can reach
207:   // anywhere from the current batch.
208:   //
209:   // D is the input section with the largest address such that the thunk
210:   // is reachable from the current batch if it's inserted right before D
211:   // but at least partially unreacahble if it's inserted after D.
212:   //
213:   //  ................................ <input sections> ............
214:   //     A    B    C    D
215:   //                    ^ We insert a thunk for the current batch just before D
216:   //          <--->       The current batch, which is smaller than batch_size
217:   //     <-------->       Smaller than max_distance
218:   //          <-------->  Smaller than max_distance
219:   //     <------------->  Reachable from the current batch
220:   i64 a = 0;
221:   i64 b = 0;
222:   i64 c = 0;
223:   i64 d = 0;
224:   i64 offset = 0;
225:   i64 thunk_idx = 0;
226: 
227:   while (b < m.size()) {
228:     // Move D foward as far as we can jump from B to anywhere in a thunk at D.
229:     while (d < m.size() &&
230:            align_to(offset, 1 << m[d]->p2align) + m[d]->sh_size + max_thunk_size <
231:            m[b]->offset + max_distance<E>()) {
232:       offset = align_to(offset, 1 << m[d]->p2align);
233:       m[d]->offset = offset;
234:       offset += m[d]->sh_size;
235:       d++;
236:     }
237: 
238:     // Move C forward so that C is apart from B by BATCH_SIZE. We want
239:     // to make sure that there's at least one section between B and C
240:     // to ensure progress.
241:     c = b + 1;
242:     while (c < m.size() &&
243:            m[c]->offset + m[c]->sh_size < m[b]->offset + batch_size<E>)
244:       c++;
245: 
246:     // Move A forward so that A is reachable from C.
247:     i64 c_offset = (c == m.size()) ? offset : m[c]->offset;
248:     while (a < m.size() && m[a]->offset + max_distance<E>() < c_offset)
249:       a++;
250: 
251:     // Erase references to out-of-range thunks.
252:     while (thunk_idx < osec.thunks.size() &&
253:            osec.thunks[thunk_idx]->offset < m[a]->offset)
254:       reset_thunk(*osec.thunks[thunk_idx++]);
255: 
256:     // Create a thunk for input sections between B and C and place it at D.
257:     offset = align_to(offset, RangeExtensionThunk<E>::alignment);
258:     RangeExtensionThunk<E> *thunk =
259:       new RangeExtensionThunk<E>(osec, osec.thunks.size(), offset);
260:     osec.thunks.emplace_back(thunk);
261: 
262:     // Scan relocations between B and C to collect symbols that need thunks.
263:     tbb::parallel_for_each(m.begin() + b, m.begin() + c,
264:                            [&](InputSection<E> *isec) {
265:       scan_rels(ctx, *isec, *thunk);
266:     });
267: 
268:     // Now that we know the number of symbols in the thunk, we can compute
269:     // its size.
270:     assert(thunk->size() < max_thunk_size);
271:     offset += thunk->size();
272: 
273:     // Sort symbols added to the thunk to make the output deterministic.
274:     sort(thunk->symbols, [](Symbol<E> *a, Symbol<E> *b) {
275:       return std::tuple{a->file->priority, a->sym_idx} <
276:              std::tuple{b->file->priority, b->sym_idx};
277:     });
278: 
279:     // Assign offsets within the thunk to the symbols.
280:     for (i64 i = 0; i < thunk->symbols.size(); i++) {
281:       Symbol<E> &sym = *thunk->symbols[i];
282:       sym.extra.thunk_idx = thunk->thunk_idx;
283:       sym.extra.thunk_sym_idx = i;
284:     }
285: 
286:     // Scan relocations again to fix symbol offsets in the last thunk.
287:     tbb::parallel_for_each(m.begin() + b, m.begin() + c,
288:                            [&](InputSection<E> *isec) {
289:       std::span<Symbol<E> *> syms = isec->file.symbols;
290:       std::span<const ElfRel<E>> rels = isec->get_rels(ctx);
291:       std::span<RangeExtensionRef> range_extn = isec->extra.range_extn;
292: 
293:       for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++)
294:         if (range_extn[i].thunk_idx == thunk->thunk_idx)
295:           range_extn[i].sym_idx = syms[rels[i].r_sym]->extra.thunk_sym_idx;
296:     });
297: 
298:     // Move B forward to point to the begining of the next batch.
299:     b = c;
300:   }
301: 
302:   while (thunk_idx < osec.thunks.size())
303:     reset_thunk(*osec.thunks[thunk_idx++]);
304: 
305:   osec.shdr.sh_size = offset;
306: }
307: 
308: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
309: 
310: static_assert(max_thunk_size / E::thunk_size < INT16_MAX);
311: 
312: template void create_range_extension_thunks(Context<E> &, OutputSection<E> &);
313: 
314: } // namespace mold::elf
315: 
316: #endif

================
File: macho/dead-strip.cc
================
  1: #include "mold.h"
  2: 
  3: #include <tbb/concurrent_vector.h>
  4: #include <tbb/parallel_for_each.h>
  5: 
  6: namespace mold::macho {
  7: 
  8: template <typename E>
  9: static void collect_root_set(Context<E> &ctx,
 10:                              tbb::concurrent_vector<Subsection<E> *> &rootset) {
 11:   Timer t(ctx, "collect_root_set");
 12: 
 13:   auto add = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 14:     if (sym && sym->subsec)
 15:       rootset.push_back(sym->subsec);
 16:   };
 17: 
 18:   auto keep = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) {
 19:     if (sym->no_dead_strip)
 20:       return true;
 21:     if (ctx.output_type == MH_DYLIB || ctx.output_type == MH_BUNDLE)
 22:       if (sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL)
 23:         return true;
 24:     return false;
 25:   };
 26: 
 27:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
 28:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
 29:       if (sym->file == file && keep(sym))
 30:         add(sym);
 31: 
 32:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->init_functions)
 33:       add(sym);
 34: 
 35:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : file->subsections)
 36:       if (const MachSection<E> &hdr = subsec->isec->hdr;
 37:           (hdr.attr & S_ATTR_NO_DEAD_STRIP) ||
 38:           hdr.type == S_MOD_INIT_FUNC_POINTERS ||
 39:           hdr.type == S_MOD_TERM_FUNC_POINTERS)
 40:         rootset.push_back(subsec);
 41: 
 42:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : file->cies)
 43:       add(cie->personality);
 44:   });
 45: 
 46:   for (std::string_view name : ctx.arg.u)
 47:     if (Symbol<E> *sym = get_symbol(ctx, name); sym->file)
 48:       add(sym);
 49: 
 50:   add(ctx.arg.entry);
 51: 
 52:   if (ctx.stub_helper)
 53:     add(get_symbol(ctx, "dyld_stub_binder"));
 54: }
 55: 
 56: template <typename E>
 57: static void visit(Context<E> &ctx, Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 58:   if (!subsec)
 59:     return;
 60: 
 61:   if (subsec->is_alive.test_and_set())
 62:     return;
 63: 
 64:   for (Relocation<E> &rel : subsec->get_rels()) {
 65:     if (rel.sym())
 66:       visit(ctx, rel.sym()->subsec);
 67:     else
 68:       visit(ctx, rel.subsec());
 69:   }
 70: 
 71:   for (UnwindRecord<E> &rec : subsec->get_unwind_records()) {
 72:     visit(ctx, rec.subsec);
 73:     visit(ctx, rec.lsda);
 74: 
 75:     if (rec.personality)
 76:       visit(ctx, rec.personality->subsec);
 77: 
 78:     if (rec.fde) {
 79:       visit(ctx, rec.fde->subsec);
 80:       visit(ctx, rec.fde->lsda);
 81:     }
 82:   }
 83: }
 84: 
 85: template <typename E>
 86: static bool refers_to_live_subsection(Subsection<E> &subsec) {
 87:   for (Relocation<E> &rel : subsec.get_rels()) {
 88:     if (rel.sym()) {
 89:       if (rel.sym()->subsec && rel.sym()->subsec->is_alive)
 90:         return true;
 91:     } else {
 92:       if (rel.subsec()->is_alive)
 93:         return true;
 94:     }
 95:   }
 96:   return false;
 97: }
 98: 
 99: template <typename E>
100: static void mark(Context<E> &ctx,
101:                  tbb::concurrent_vector<Subsection<E> *> &rootset) {
102:   Timer t(ctx, "mark");
103: 
104:   tbb::parallel_for_each(rootset, [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
105:     visit(ctx, subsec);
106:   });
107: 
108:   Atomic<bool> repeat;
109:   do {
110:     repeat = false;
111:     tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
112:       for (Subsection<E> *subsec : file->subsections) {
113:         if ((subsec->isec->hdr.attr & S_ATTR_LIVE_SUPPORT) &&
114:             !subsec->is_alive &&
115:             refers_to_live_subsection(*subsec)) {
116:           visit(ctx, subsec);
117:           repeat = true;
118:         }
119:       }
120:     });
121:   } while (repeat);
122: }
123: 
124: template <typename E>
125: static void sweep(Context<E> &ctx) {
126:   Timer t(ctx, "sweep");
127: 
128:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
129:     for (Symbol<E> *&sym : file->syms)
130:       if (sym->file == file && sym->subsec && !sym->subsec->is_alive)
131:         sym = nullptr;
132:   });
133: 
134:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
135:     std::erase_if(file->subsections, [](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
136:       return !subsec->is_alive;
137:     });
138:   });
139: }
140: 
141: template <typename E>
142: void dead_strip(Context<E> &ctx) {
143:   Timer t(ctx, "dead_strip");
144: 
145:   tbb::concurrent_vector<Subsection<E> *> rootset;
146:   collect_root_set(ctx, rootset);
147:   mark(ctx, rootset);
148:   sweep(ctx);
149: }
150: 
151: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
152: 
153: template void dead_strip(Context<E> &);
154: 
155: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: macho/tapi.cc
================
  1: // On macOS, you can pass a test file describing a dylib instead of an
  2: // actual dylib file to link against a dynamic library. Such text file
  3: // should be in the YAML format and contains dylib's exported symbols
  4: // as well as the file's various attributes. The extension of the text
  5: // file is `.tbd`.
  6: //
  7: // .tbd files allows users to link against a library without
  8: // distributing the binary of the library file itself.
  9: //
 10: // This file contains functions to parse the .tbd file.
 11: 
 12: #include "mold.h"
 13: 
 14: #include <optional>
 15: #include <regex>
 16: #include <unordered_set>
 17: 
 18: namespace mold::macho {
 19: 
 20: template <typename T, typename U>
 21: static void merge(std::set<T> &a, U &&b) {
 22:   a.insert(b.begin(), b.end());
 23: }
 24: 
 25: static std::vector<YamlNode>
 26: get_vector(YamlNode &node, std::string_view key) {
 27:   if (auto *map = std::get_if<std::map<std::string_view, YamlNode>>(&node.data))
 28:     if (auto it = map->find(key); it != map->end())
 29:       if (auto *vec = std::get_if<std::vector<YamlNode>>(&it->second.data))
 30:         return *vec;
 31:   return {};
 32: }
 33: 
 34: static std::vector<std::string_view>
 35: get_string_vector(YamlNode &node, std::string_view key) {
 36:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
 37:   for (YamlNode &mem : get_vector(node, key))
 38:     if (auto val = std::get_if<std::string_view>(&mem.data))
 39:       vec.push_back(*val);
 40:   return vec;
 41: }
 42: 
 43: static std::optional<std::string_view>
 44: get_string(YamlNode &node, std::string_view key) {
 45:   if (auto *map = std::get_if<std::map<std::string_view, YamlNode>>(&node.data))
 46:     if (auto it = map->find(key); it != map->end())
 47:       if (auto *str = std::get_if<std::string_view>(&it->second.data))
 48:         return *str;
 49:   return {};
 50: }
 51: 
 52: static bool contains(const std::vector<YamlNode> &vec, std::string_view key) {
 53:   for (const YamlNode &mem : vec)
 54:     if (auto val = std::get_if<std::string_view>(&mem.data))
 55:       if (*val == key)
 56:         return true;
 57:   return false;
 58: }
 59: 
 60: template <typename E>
 61: static bool match_arch(Context<E> &ctx, const std::vector<YamlNode> &vec) {
 62:   static_assert(is_arm<E> || is_x86<E>);
 63:   std::string arch = is_arm<E> ? "arm64" : "x86_64";
 64: 
 65:   for (const YamlNode &mem : vec)
 66:     if (auto val = std::get_if<std::string_view>(&mem.data))
 67:       if (*val == arch || val->starts_with(arch + "-"))
 68:         return true;
 69:   return false;
 70: }
 71: 
 72: template <typename E>
 73: static std::optional<TextDylib>
 74: to_tbd(Context<E> &ctx, YamlNode &node, std::string_view filename) {
 75:   if (!match_arch(ctx, get_vector(node, "targets")))
 76:     return {};
 77: 
 78:   if (ctx.arg.application_extension &&
 79:       contains(get_vector(node, "flags"), "not_app_extension_safe"))
 80:     Warn(ctx) << "linking against a dylib which is not safe for use in "
 81:               << "application extensions: " << filename;
 82: 
 83:   TextDylib tbd;
 84: 
 85:   if (auto val = get_string(node, "install-name"))
 86:     tbd.install_name = *val;
 87: 
 88:   for (YamlNode &mem : get_vector(node, "reexported-libraries"))
 89:     if (match_arch(ctx, get_vector(mem, "targets")))
 90:       append(tbd.reexported_libs, get_string_vector(mem, "libraries"));
 91: 
 92:   auto concat = [&](const std::string &x, std::string_view y) {
 93:     return save_string(ctx, x + std::string(y));
 94:   };
 95: 
 96:   for (std::string_view key : {"exports", "reexports"}) {
 97:     for (YamlNode &mem : get_vector(node, key)) {
 98:       if (match_arch(ctx, get_vector(mem, "targets"))) {
 99:         merge(tbd.exports, get_string_vector(mem, "symbols"));
100:         merge(tbd.weak_exports, get_string_vector(mem, "weak-symbols"));
101: 
102:         for (std::string_view s : get_string_vector(mem, "objc-classes")) {
103:           tbd.exports.insert(concat("_OBJC_CLASS_$_", s));
104:           tbd.exports.insert(concat("_OBJC_METACLASS_$_", s));
105:         }
106: 
107:         for (std::string_view s : get_string_vector(mem, "objc-eh-types"))
108:           tbd.exports.insert(concat("_OBJC_EHTYPE_$_", s));
109: 
110:         for (std::string_view s : get_string_vector(mem, "objc-ivars"))
111:           tbd.exports.insert(concat("_OBJC_IVAR_$_", s));
112:       }
113:     }
114:   }
115: 
116:   return tbd;
117: }
118: 
119: static std::vector<std::string_view> split_string(std::string_view str, i64 max) {
120:   std::vector<std::string_view> vec;
121:   while (vec.size() < max) {
122:     size_t pos = str.find('$');
123:     if (pos == str.npos) {
124:       vec.push_back(str);
125:       return vec;
126:     }
127: 
128:     vec.push_back(str.substr(0, pos));
129:     str = str.substr(pos + 1);
130:   }
131:   return vec;
132: }
133: 
134: // Dylib can contain special symbols whose name starts with "$ld$".
135: // Such symbols aren't actually symbols but linker directives.
136: // We interpret such symbols in this function.
137: template <typename E>
138: static void
139: interpret_ld_symbols(Context<E> &ctx, TextDylib &tbd, std::string_view filename) {
140:   std::set<std::string_view> syms;
141:   std::unordered_set<std::string_view> hidden_syms;
142: 
143:   for (std::string_view str : tbd.exports) {
144:     // $ld$previous$ symbol replaces the default install name with a
145:     // specified one if the platform OS version is in a specified range.
146:     if (remove_prefix(str, "$ld$previous$")) {
147:       std::vector<std::string_view> args = split_string(str, 6);
148:       if (args.size() != 6)
149:         Fatal(ctx) << filename << ": malformed symbol: $ld$previous$" << str;
150: 
151:       std::string_view install_name = args[0];
152:       i64 platform = std::stoi(std::string(args[2]));
153:       VersionTriple min_version = parse_version(ctx, args[3]);
154:       VersionTriple max_version = parse_version(ctx, args[4]);
155:       std::string_view symbol_name = args[5];
156: 
157:       if (!symbol_name.empty()) {
158:         // ld64 source seems to have implemented a feature to give an
159:         // alternative install name for a matching symbol, but it didn't
160:         // work in practice (or I may be using the feature in a wrong way.)
161:         // Ignore such symbol for now.
162:         continue;
163:       }
164: 
165:       if (platform == ctx.arg.platform &&
166:           min_version <= ctx.arg.platform_min_version &&
167:           ctx.arg.platform_min_version < max_version) {
168:         tbd.install_name = install_name;
169:       }
170:       continue;
171:     }
172: 
173:     // $ld$add$os_version$symbol adds a symbol if the given OS version
174:     // matches.
175:     if (remove_prefix(str, "$ld$add$os")) {
176:       std::vector<std::string_view> args = split_string(str, 2);
177:       if (args.size() != 2)
178:         Fatal(ctx) << filename << ": malformed symbol: $ld$add$os" << str;
179: 
180:       VersionTriple version = parse_version(ctx, args[0]);
181:       if (ctx.arg.platform_min_version == version)
182:         syms.insert(args[1]);
183:       continue;
184:     }
185: 
186:     // $ld$hide$os_version$symbol hides a symbol if the given OS version
187:     // matches.
188:     if (remove_prefix(str, "$ld$hide$os")) {
189:       std::vector<std::string_view> args = split_string(str, 2);
190:       if (args.size() != 2)
191:         Fatal(ctx) << filename << ": malformed symbol: $ld$hide$os" << str;
192: 
193:       VersionTriple version = parse_version(ctx, args[0]);
194:       if (ctx.arg.platform_min_version == version)
195:         hidden_syms.insert(args[1]);
196:       continue;
197:     }
198: 
199:     // $ld$install_name$os_version$name changes the install name to a
200:     // given name.
201:     if (remove_prefix(str, "$ld$install_name$os")) {
202:       std::vector<std::string_view> args = split_string(str, 2);
203:       if (args.size() != 2)
204:         Fatal(ctx) << filename << ": malformed symbol: $ld$install_name$os" << str;
205: 
206:       VersionTriple version = parse_version(ctx, args[0]);
207:       if (ctx.arg.platform_min_version == version)
208:         tbd.install_name = args[1];
209:       continue;
210:     }
211:   }
212: 
213:   for (std::string_view str : tbd.exports)
214:     if (!str.starts_with("$ld$") && !hidden_syms.contains(str))
215:       syms.insert(str);
216: 
217:   tbd.exports = std::move(syms);
218: }
219: 
220: // A single YAML file may contain multiple text dylibs. The first text
221: // dylib is the main file followed by optional other text dylibs for
222: // re-exported libraries.
223: //
224: // This fucntion squashes multiple text dylibs into a single text dylib
225: // by copying symbols of re-exported text dylibs to the main text dylib.
226: template <typename E>
227: static TextDylib squash(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<TextDylib> tbds) {
228:   std::unordered_map<std::string_view, TextDylib> map;
229:   std::vector<std::string_view> remainings;
230: 
231:   TextDylib main = std::move(tbds[0]);
232:   for (TextDylib &tbd : tbds.subspan(1))
233:     map[tbd.install_name] = std::move(tbd);
234: 
235:   std::function<void(TextDylib &)> visit = [&](TextDylib &tbd) {
236:     for (std::string_view lib : tbd.reexported_libs) {
237:       auto it = map.find(lib);
238: 
239:       if (it != map.end()) {
240:         TextDylib &child = it->second;
241:         merge(main.exports, child.exports);
242:         merge(main.weak_exports, child.weak_exports);
243:         visit(child);
244:       } else {
245:         remainings.push_back(lib);
246:       }
247:     }
248:   };
249: 
250:   visit(main);
251:   main.reexported_libs = remainings;
252:   return main;
253: }
254: 
255: template <typename E>
256: static std::string_view replace_crlf(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view str) {
257:   if (str.find('\r') == str.npos)
258:     return str;
259: 
260:   std::string buf;
261:   for (char c : str)
262:     if (c != '\r')
263:       buf += c;
264:   return save_string(ctx, buf);
265: }
266: 
267: template <typename E>
268: TextDylib parse_tbd(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
269:   std::string_view contents = mf->get_contents();
270:   contents = replace_crlf(ctx, contents);
271: 
272:   std::variant<std::vector<YamlNode>, YamlError> res = parse_yaml(contents);
273: 
274:   if (YamlError *err = std::get_if<YamlError>(&res)) {
275:     i64 lineno = std::count(contents.begin(), contents.begin() + err->pos, '\n');
276:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ":" << (lineno + 1)
277:                << ": YAML parse error: " << err->msg;
278:   }
279: 
280:   std::vector<YamlNode> &nodes = std::get<std::vector<YamlNode>>(res);
281:   if (nodes.empty())
282:     Fatal(ctx) << mf->name << ": malformed TBD file";
283: 
284:   std::vector<TextDylib> vec;
285:   for (YamlNode &node : nodes)
286:     if (std::optional<TextDylib> dylib = to_tbd(ctx, node, mf->name))
287:       vec.push_back(*dylib);
288: 
289:   for (TextDylib &tbd : vec)
290:     interpret_ld_symbols(ctx, tbd, mf->name);
291: 
292:   return squash(ctx, vec);
293: }
294: 
295: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
296: 
297: template TextDylib parse_tbd(Context<E> &, MappedFile<Context<E>> *);
298: 
299: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: CMakeLists.txt
================
  1: # You can customize a build by specifying CMake options. An option may be
  2: # given in the -Dvariable=value form. For a boolean variable, `ON` or `1`
  3: # means true while `OFF` or `0` means false.
  4: #
  5: # Here are a couple of common cmake options:
  6: #
  7: #  -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=<command-name>
  8: #
  9: #   Specifies a C compiler name to use. The default value is `cc`.
 10: #
 11: #  -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=<command-name>
 12: #
 13: #   Specifies a C++ compiler name to use. The default value is `c++`.
 14: #
 15: #  -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=<directory>
 16: #
 17: #   Specifies an install target directory. The default value is `/usr/local`.
 18: #
 19: #  -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=[Debug | Release | RelWithDebInfo | MinSizeRel]
 20: #
 21: #   Specifies a build type. The default is `Release` which is the right
 22: #   option unless you are debugging mold.
 23: #
 24: # An example of a cmake command line is shown below:
 25: #
 26: #   $ cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/usr -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=g++-12 ..
 27: #
 28: # where `..` refers this directory.
 29: #
 30: # With cmake, you may run `cmake --install .` instead of `make install` to
 31: # install build artifacts to system directories. If you want to install
 32: # artifacts to a temporary target directory, run `cmake --install . --prefix
 33: # <dir>`.
 34: #
 35: # You can see the current cmake variables and their values by running
 36: # `cmake -N -L .` in a build directory.
 37: #
 38: # Note that in this file, we provide various dials and knobs to configure
 39: # how to build mold. However, as a policy, we do not provide a way to
 40: # enable/disable any individual mold's feature. In other words, we do not
 41: # provide options like `--enable-foo` or `--disable-foo`. The motivation
 42: # behind it is build reproducibility. We want to guarantees that all builds
 43: # of the mold linker of the same version will have the exactly same set of
 44: # features and behave exactly the same.
 45: 
 46: cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.13)
 47: project(mold VERSION 1.11.0)
 48: 
 49: include(CMakeDependentOption)
 50: include(GNUInstallDirs)
 51: 
 52: # CMake 4 removed compatibility with policy versions below 3.5, but our
 53: # vendored oneTBB still declares 3.1. Set a floor before add_subdirectory()
 54: # so configure works without requiring manual -D flags.
 55: if(CMAKE_VERSION VERSION_GREATER_EQUAL 4.0)
 56:   set(CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM "3.5" CACHE STRING
 57:     "Minimum policy version for vendored subprojects" FORCE)
 58: endif()
 59: 
 60: # Build mold itself using mold if -DMOLD_USE_MOLD=ON
 61: option(MOLD_USE_MOLD "Use mold to build mold" OFF)
 62: if(MOLD_USE_MOLD)
 63:   add_link_options(-fuse-ld=mold)
 64: endif()
 65: 
 66: add_executable(mold)
 67: target_compile_features(mold PRIVATE cxx_std_20)
 68: target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE ${CMAKE_DL_LIBS})
 69: 
 70: if(NOT "${CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_FRONTEND_VARIANT}" STREQUAL "MSVC")
 71:   target_compile_options(mold PRIVATE
 72:     -fno-exceptions
 73:     -fno-unwind-tables
 74:     -fno-asynchronous-unwind-tables
 75:     -Wno-sign-compare
 76:     -Wno-unused-function
 77:     -Wno-bitfield-constant-conversion
 78:     -ggnu-pubnames)
 79: endif()
 80: 
 81: if(CMAKE_BUILD_TYPE STREQUAL "Debug")
 82:   target_compile_options(mold PRIVATE -D_GLIBCXX_ASSERTIONS)
 83: 
 84:   if(NOT APPLE AND NOT WIN32)
 85:     add_link_options(-Wl,--gdb-index)
 86:   endif()
 87: endif()
 88: 
 89: # Build mold with -flto if -DMOLD_LTO=ON
 90: option(MOLD_LTO "Build mold with link-time optimization enabled")
 91: if(MOLD_LTO)
 92:   set_property(TARGET mold PROPERTY INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION ON)
 93: endif()
 94: 
 95: # Enable AddressSanitizer if -DMOLD_USE_ASAN=ON
 96: option(MOLD_USE_ASAN "Build mold with AddressSanitizer" OFF)
 97: if(MOLD_USE_ASAN)
 98:   target_compile_options(mold PRIVATE -fsanitize=address -fsanitize=undefined)
 99:   target_link_options(mold PRIVATE -fsanitize=address -fsanitize=undefined)
100: endif()
101: 
102: # Enabled ThreadSanitizer if -DMOLD_USE_TSAN=ON
103: option(MOLD_USE_TSAN "Build mold with ThreadSanitizer" OFF)
104: if(MOLD_USE_TSAN)
105:   target_compile_options(mold PRIVATE -fsanitize=thread)
106:   target_link_options(mold PRIVATE -fsanitize=thread)
107: endif()
108: 
109: # Statically-link libstdc++ and libcrypto if -DMOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC=ON.
110: #
111: # This option is intended to be used by `./dist.sh` script to create a
112: # mold binary that works on various Linux distros. You probably don't
113: # need nor want to set this to ON.
114: option(MOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC "Statically link libstdc++ and libcrypto" OFF)
115: if(MOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC)
116:   target_link_options(mold PRIVATE -static-libstdc++)
117:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE libcrypto.a)
118: endif()
119: 
120: # Find zlib. If libz.so is not found, we compile a bundled one and
121: # statically-link it to mold.
122: find_package(ZLIB QUIET)
123: if(ZLIB_FOUND)
124:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE ZLIB::ZLIB)
125: else()
126:   add_subdirectory(third-party/zlib EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
127:   target_include_directories(zlibstatic INTERFACE third-party/zlib
128:     $<TARGET_PROPERTY:zlibstatic,BINARY_DIR>)
129:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE zlibstatic)
130: endif()
131: 
132: # Find zstd compression library. Just like zlib, if libzstd.so is not
133: # found, we compile a bundled one and statically-link it to mold.
134: include(CheckIncludeFile)
135: check_include_file(zstd.h HAVE_ZSTD_H)
136: 
137: if(HAVE_ZSTD_H)
138:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE zstd)
139: else()
140:   add_subdirectory(third-party/zstd/build/cmake EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
141:   target_compile_definitions(libzstd_static PRIVATE
142:     ZSTD_BUILD_STATIC=1
143:     ZSTD_BUILD_SHARED=0
144:     ZSTD_BUILD_PROGRAMS=0
145:     ZSTD_MULTITHREAD_SUPPORT=0
146:     ZSTD_BUILD_TESTS=0)
147:   target_include_directories(mold PUBLIC third-party/zstd/lib)
148:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE libzstd_static)
149: endif()
150: 
151: # Find mimalloc. mimalloc is an alternative malloc implementation
152: # optimized for multi-threaded applications.
153: #
154: # If you want to use the usual libc's malloc, pass -DMOLD_USE_MIMALLOC=OFF.
155: #
156: # We enable mimalloc by default for 64-bit targets. It doesn't seem to
157: # be stable on 32-bit targets.
158: include(CheckCXXSourceCompiles)
159: check_cxx_source_compiles(
160:   "static_assert(sizeof(void *) == 4); int main() {}" P32)
161: 
162: cmake_dependent_option(MOLD_USE_MIMALLOC "Use mimalloc" ON
163:   "NOT APPLE; NOT ANDROID; NOT P32" OFF)
164: cmake_dependent_option(
165:   MOLD_USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC "Use system or vendored mimalloc" OFF
166:   MOLD_USE_MIMALLOC OFF)
167: 
168: # By default, we build a bundled mimalloc and statically-link it to
169: # mold. If you want to dynamically link to the system's
170: # libmimalloc.so, pass -DMOLD_USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC=ON.
171: if(MOLD_USE_MIMALLOC)
172:   if(MOLD_USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC)
173:     find_package(mimalloc REQUIRED)
174:     target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE mimalloc)
175:     target_compile_definitions(mold PRIVATE USE_SYSTEM_MIMALLOC)
176:   else()
177:     function(mold_add_mimalloc)
178:       set(MI_BUILD_STATIC ON CACHE INTERNAL "")
179:       set(MI_BUILD_TESTS OFF CACHE INTERNAL "")
180:       add_subdirectory(third-party/mimalloc EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
181:       target_compile_definitions(mimalloc-static PRIVATE MI_USE_ENVIRON=0)
182:       target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE mimalloc-static)
183:     endfunction()
184: 
185:     mold_add_mimalloc()
186:   endif()
187: endif()
188: 
189: # Find TBB. TBB (OneTBB or Intel TBB) is a high-level threading library.
190: # Use of this library is mandatory.
191: #
192: # By default, we build a bundled one and statically-link the library
193: # to mold. If you want to link to the system's libtbb2.so, pass
194: # -DMOLD_USE_SYSTEM_TBB=ON.
195: option(MOLD_USE_SYSTEM_TBB "Use system or vendored TBB" OFF)
196: if(MOLD_USE_SYSTEM_TBB)
197:   find_package(TBB REQUIRED)
198:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE TBB::tbb)
199: else()
200:   function(mold_add_tbb)
201:     set(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS OFF)
202:     set(TBB_TEST OFF CACHE INTERNAL "")
203:     set(TBB_STRICT OFF CACHE INTERNAL "")
204:     add_subdirectory(third-party/tbb EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
205:     target_compile_definitions(tbb PRIVATE __TBB_DYNAMIC_LOAD_ENABLED=0)
206:     target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE TBB::tbb)
207:   endfunction()
208: 
209:   mold_add_tbb()
210: endif()
211: 
212: # Check if this is a commercial version of mold (a.k.a. "sold")
213: if(EXISTS "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/LICENSE.md")
214:   set(MOLD_IS_SOLD ON)
215: endif()
216: 
217: # We always use Clang to build mold on Windows. MSVC can't compile mold.
218: if(WIN32)
219:   if(MSVC AND NOT CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "Clang")
220:     message(FATAL_ERROR
221:       "Your compiler is not supported; install Clang from Visual Studio Installer and re-run cmake with '-T clangcl'")
222:   endif()
223: 
224:   target_compile_definitions(mold PRIVATE NOGDI NOMINMAX)
225:   if(MINGW)
226:     target_compile_definitions(mold PRIVATE _WIN32_WINNT=0xA00)
227:     target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE bcrypt)
228:   endif()
229: else()
230:   include(CheckLibraryExists)
231:   check_library_exists(m pow "" LIBM_FOUND)
232:   if(LIBM_FOUND)
233:     target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE m)
234:   endif()
235: endif()
236: 
237: # Build mold-wrapper.so
238: if(NOT APPLE AND NOT WIN32)
239:   add_library(mold-wrapper SHARED)
240:   install(TARGETS mold-wrapper DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR}/mold)
241: 
242:   # Remove the default `lib` prefix
243:   set_target_properties(mold-wrapper PROPERTIES PREFIX "")
244:   target_link_libraries(mold-wrapper PRIVATE ${CMAKE_DL_LIBS})
245:   target_sources(mold-wrapper PRIVATE elf/mold-wrapper.c)
246: endif()
247: 
248: if(NOT APPLE AND NOT WIN32 AND NOT MOLD_MOSTLY_STATIC)
249:   find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED COMPONENTS Crypto)
250:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE OpenSSL::Crypto)
251: endif()
252: 
253: # If atomics doesn't work by default, add -latomic.
254: # We need the flag on riscv, armv6 and m68k.
255: include(CheckCXXSourceCompiles)
256: check_cxx_source_compiles("#include <atomic>
257: int main() {
258:   std::atomic_uint8_t  a;
259:   std::atomic_uint16_t b;
260:   std::atomic_uint32_t c;
261:   std::atomic_uint64_t d;
262:   return ++a + ++b + ++c + ++d;
263: }" HAVE_FULL_ATOMIC_SUPPORT)
264: 
265: if(NOT HAVE_FULL_ATOMIC_SUPPORT)
266:   target_link_libraries(mold PRIVATE atomic)
267: endif()
268: 
269: # Add -pthread
270: if(NOT APPLE AND NOT MSVC)
271:   target_compile_options(mold PRIVATE -pthread)
272:   target_link_options(mold PRIVATE -pthread)
273: endif()
274: 
275: # Create a .cc file containing the current git hash for `mold --version`.
276: add_custom_target(git_hash
277:   COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND}
278:     -DSOURCE_DIR=${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
279:     -DOUTPUT_FILE=${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/git-hash.cc
280:     -P ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/common/update-git-hash.cmake
281:   DEPENDS common/update-git-hash.cmake
282:   BYPRODUCTS git-hash.cc
283:   VERBATIM)
284: 
285: add_dependencies(mold git_hash)
286: 
287: # Create config.h file
288: configure_file(common/config.h.in config.h)
289: include_directories(${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
290: 
291: # Almost all functions are template in mold which take a target type
292: # (e.g. X86_64) as its type parameter. Since we suport more than 10
293: # targets, compiling a single source file for all the targets is very
294: # slow.
295: #
296: # As a workaround, we create a .cc file for each target and spawn many
297: # compiler instances. This is hacky but greatly reduces compile time
298: # on a multicore machine.
299: list(APPEND MOLD_ELF_TARGETS
300:   X86_64 I386 ARM64 ARM32 RV32LE RV32BE RV64LE RV64BE
301:   PPC32 PPC64V1 PPC64V2 S390X SPARC64 M68K SH4 ALPHA)
302: 
303: list(APPEND MOLD_ELF_TEMPLATE_FILES
304:   elf/cmdline.cc
305:   elf/dwarf.cc
306:   elf/gc-sections.cc
307:   elf/icf.cc
308:   elf/input-files.cc
309:   elf/input-sections.cc
310:   elf/linker-script.cc
311:   elf/lto.cc
312:   elf/main.cc
313:   elf/mapfile.cc
314:   elf/output-chunks.cc
315:   elf/passes.cc
316:   elf/relocatable.cc
317:   elf/subprocess.cc
318:   elf/thunks.cc
319:   elf/tls.cc
320:   )
321: 
322: list(APPEND MOLD_MACHO_TARGETS X86_64 ARM64 ARM64_32)
323: 
324: list(APPEND MOLD_MACHO_TEMPLATE_FILES
325:   macho/arch-arm64.cc
326:   macho/cmdline.cc
327:   macho/dead-strip.cc
328:   macho/input-files.cc
329:   macho/input-sections.cc
330:   macho/lto.cc
331:   macho/main.cc
332:   macho/mapfile.cc
333:   macho/output-chunks.cc
334:   macho/tapi.cc
335:   macho/thunks.cc
336:   )
337: 
338: function(mold_instantiate_templates SOURCE TARGET)
339:   set(PATH ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/${SOURCE}.${TARGET}.cc)
340:   file(WRITE ${PATH} "#define MOLD_${TARGET} 1
341: #define MOLD_TARGET ${TARGET}
342: #include \"${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/${SOURCE}\"
343: ")
344:   target_sources(mold PRIVATE ${PATH})
345: endfunction()
346: 
347: foreach (SOURCE IN LISTS MOLD_ELF_TEMPLATE_FILES)
348:   foreach(TARGET IN LISTS MOLD_ELF_TARGETS)
349:     mold_instantiate_templates(${SOURCE} ${TARGET})
350:   endforeach()
351: endforeach()
352: 
353: if(MOLD_IS_SOLD)
354:   foreach (SOURCE IN LISTS MOLD_MACHO_TEMPLATE_FILES)
355:     foreach(TARGET IN LISTS MOLD_MACHO_TARGETS)
356:       mold_instantiate_templates(${SOURCE} ${TARGET})
357:     endforeach()
358:   endforeach()
359: endif()
360: 
361: # Add other non-template source files.
362: target_sources(mold PRIVATE
363:   common/compress.cc
364:   common/demangle.cc
365:   common/filepath.cc
366:   common/glob.cc
367:   common/hyperloglog.cc
368:   common/main.cc
369:   common/multi-glob.cc
370:   common/perf.cc
371:   common/tar.cc
372:   common/uuid.cc
373:   elf/arch-alpha.cc
374:   elf/arch-arm32.cc
375:   elf/arch-arm64.cc
376:   elf/arch-i386.cc
377:   elf/arch-m68k.cc
378:   elf/arch-ppc32.cc
379:   elf/arch-ppc64v1.cc
380:   elf/arch-ppc64v2.cc
381:   elf/arch-riscv.cc
382:   elf/arch-s390x.cc
383:   elf/arch-sh4.cc
384:   elf/arch-sparc64.cc
385:   elf/arch-x86-64.cc
386:   elf/elf.cc
387:   git-hash.cc
388:   third-party/rust-demangle/rust-demangle.c
389:   )
390: 
391: if(MOLD_IS_SOLD)
392:   target_sources(mold PRIVATE
393:     macho/arch-x86-64.cc
394:     macho/yaml.cc
395:     )
396: endif()
397: 
398: include(CTest)
399: 
400: if(BUILD_TESTING)
401:   # Create the ld and ld64 symlinks required for testing
402:   if(NOT WIN32)
403:     add_custom_command(
404:       TARGET mold POST_BUILD
405:       COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E create_symlink mold ld
406:       BYPRODUCTS ld
407:       WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}
408:       VERBATIM)
409: 
410:     if(MOLD_IS_SOLD)
411:       add_custom_command(
412:         TARGET mold POST_BUILD
413:         COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND} -E create_symlink mold ld64
414:         BYPRODUCTS ld64
415:         WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}
416:         VERBATIM)
417:     endif()
418:   endif()
419: 
420:   if(${APPLE})
421:     if(MOLD_IS_SOLD)
422:       add_subdirectory(test/macho)
423:     endif()
424:   elseif(${UNIX})
425:     add_subdirectory(test/elf)
426:   endif()
427: endif()
428: 
429: if(NOT CMAKE_SKIP_INSTALL_RULES)
430:   install(TARGETS mold RUNTIME DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR})
431:   install(FILES docs/mold.1 DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_MANDIR}/man1/)
432: 
433:   if(EXISTS "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/LICENSE")
434:     install(FILES LICENSE DESTINATION ${CMAKE_INSTALL_DOCDIR})
435:   endif()
436: 
437:   function(mold_install_relative_symlink OLD NEW)
438:     install(CODE "
439:       get_filename_component(PREFIX_ABS \${CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX}/ ABSOLUTE)
440:       get_filename_component(OLD_ABS ${OLD} ABSOLUTE BASE_DIR \${PREFIX_ABS})
441:       get_filename_component(NEW_ABS ${NEW} ABSOLUTE BASE_DIR \${PREFIX_ABS})
442:       get_filename_component(NEW_DIR \${NEW_ABS} DIRECTORY)
443:       file(RELATIVE_PATH OLD_REL \${NEW_DIR} \${OLD_ABS})
444:       message(STATUS \"Installing symlink: \$ENV{DESTDIR}\${NEW_ABS} -> \${OLD_REL}\")
445:       file(MAKE_DIRECTORY \$ENV{DESTDIR}\${NEW_DIR})
446:       file(CREATE_LINK \${OLD_REL} \$ENV{DESTDIR}\${NEW_ABS} SYMBOLIC)")
447:   endfunction()
448: 
449:   mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/mold${CMAKE_EXECUTABLE_SUFFIX}
450:     ${CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBEXECDIR}/mold/ld${CMAKE_EXECUTABLE_SUFFIX})
451:   mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/mold${CMAKE_EXECUTABLE_SUFFIX}
452:     ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld.mold${CMAKE_EXECUTABLE_SUFFIX})
453:   mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_MANDIR}/man1/mold.1
454:     ${CMAKE_INSTALL_MANDIR}/man1/ld.mold.1)
455: 
456:   if(MOLD_IS_SOLD)
457:     mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/mold
458:       ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld64.mold)
459:     mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/mold
460:       ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld.sold)
461:     mold_install_relative_symlink(${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/mold
462:       ${CMAKE_INSTALL_BINDIR}/ld64.sold)
463:   endif()
464: endif()

================
File: README.md
================
1: # sold linker
2: 
3: _sold_ is a high-performance linker for macOS. I built it because Apple's ld
4: was too slow at the time, and I wasn't aware that they were developing a new
5: linker. Xcode 15 or later are shipped with Apple's new faster linker.
6: I recommend using that instead.

================
File: macho/macho.h
================
   1: #pragma once
   2: 
   3: #include "../common/integers.h"
   4: 
   5: #include <cassert>
   6: #include <cstdint>
   7: #include <cstring>
   8: #include <string>
   9: #include <string_view>
  10: 
  11: namespace mold::macho {
  12: 
  13: struct ARM64;
  14: struct ARM64_32;
  15: struct X86_64;
  16: 
  17: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm64 = std::is_same_v<E, ARM64>;
  18: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm64_32 = std::is_same_v<E, ARM64_32>;
  19: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_arm = is_arm64<E> || is_arm64_32<E>;
  20: template <typename E> static constexpr bool is_x86 = std::is_same_v<E, X86_64>;
  21: 
  22: template <typename E> using Word = std::conditional_t<is_arm64_32<E>, ul32, ul64>;
  23: template <typename E> using IWord = std::conditional_t<is_arm64_32<E>, il32, il64>;
  24: 
  25: template <typename E>
  26: std::string rel_to_string(u8 r_type);
  27: 
  28: enum : u32 {
  29:   FAT_MAGIC = 0xcafebabe,
  30: };
  31: 
  32: enum : u32 {
  33:   MH_OBJECT = 0x1,
  34:   MH_EXECUTE = 0x2,
  35:   MH_FVMLIB = 0x3,
  36:   MH_CORE = 0x4,
  37:   MH_PRELOAD = 0x5,
  38:   MH_DYLIB = 0x6,
  39:   MH_DYLINKER = 0x7,
  40:   MH_BUNDLE = 0x8,
  41:   MH_DYLIB_STUB = 0x9,
  42:   MH_DSYM = 0xa,
  43:   MH_KEXT_BUNDLE = 0xb,
  44: };

  46: enum : u32 {

  50:   MH_BINDATLOAD = 0x8,
  51:   MH_PREBOUND = 0x10,
  52:   MH_SPLIT_SEGS = 0x20,
  53:   MH_LAZY_INIT = 0x40,
  54:   MH_TWOLEVEL = 0x80,
  55:   MH_FORCE_FLAT = 0x100,
  56:   MH_NOMULTIDEFS = 0x200,
  57:   MH_NOFIXPREBINDING = 0x400,
  58:   MH_PREBINDABLE = 0x800,
  59:   MH_ALLMODSBOUND = 0x1000,
  60:   MH_SUBSECTIONS_VIA_SYMBOLS = 0x2000,
  61:   MH_CANONICAL = 0x4000,
  62:   MH_WEAK_DEFINES = 0x8000,

  64:   MH_ALLOW_STACK_EXECUTION = 0x20000,
  65:   MH_ROOT_SAFE = 0x40000,

  77: enum : u32 {

  79:   VM_PROT_WRITE = 0x2,
  80:   VM_PROT_EXECUTE = 0x4,
  81:   VM_PROT_NO_CHANGE = 0x8,

  83:   VM_PROT_WANTS_COPY = 0x10,

  85: 
  86: enum : u32 {
  87:   LC_REQ_DYLD = 0x80000000,
  88: };
  89: 
  90: enum : u32 {
  91:   LC_SEGMENT = 0x1,
  92:   LC_SYMTAB = 0x2,
  93:   LC_SYMSEG = 0x3,
  94:   LC_THREAD = 0x4,
  95:   LC_UNIXTHREAD = 0x5,

 518: //
 519: // Mach-O types
 520: //
 521: 
 522: template <>
 523: inline std::string rel_to_string<ARM64>(u8 type) {
 524:   switch (type) {
 525:   case ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED: return "ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED";
 526:   case ARM64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR: return "ARM64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR";
 527:   case ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26: return "ARM64_RELOC_BRANCH26";
 528:   case ARM64_RELOC_PAGE21: return "ARM64_RELOC_PAGE21";
 529:   case ARM64_RELOC_PAGEOFF12: return "ARM64_RELOC_PAGEOFF12";
 530:   case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGE21: return "ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGE21";
 531:   case ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGEOFF12: return "ARM64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD_PAGEOFF12";
 532:   case ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT: return "ARM64_RELOC_POINTER_TO_GOT";
 533:   case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGE21: return "ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGE21";
 534:   case ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGEOFF12: return "ARM64_RELOC_TLVP_LOAD_PAGEOFF12";
 535:   case ARM64_RELOC_ADDEND: return "ARM64_RELOC_ADDEND";
 536:   }
 537:   return "unknown (" + std::to_string(type) + ")";
 538: }
 539: 
 540: template <>
 541: inline std::string rel_to_string<X86_64>(u8 type) {
 542:   switch (type) {
 543:   case X86_64_RELOC_UNSIGNED: return "X86_64_RELOC_UNSIGNED";
 544:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED: return "X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED";
 545:   case X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH: return "X86_64_RELOC_BRANCH";
 546:   case X86_64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD: return "X86_64_RELOC_GOT_LOAD";
 547:   case X86_64_RELOC_GOT: return "X86_64_RELOC_GOT";
 548:   case X86_64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR: return "X86_64_RELOC_SUBTRACTOR";
 549:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_1: return "X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_1";
 550:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_2: return "X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_2";
 551:   case X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_4: return "X86_64_RELOC_SIGNED_4";
 552:   case X86_64_RELOC_TLV: return "X86_64_RELOC_TLV";
 553:   }
 554:   return "unknown (" + std::to_string(type) + ")";
 555: }
 556: 
 557: struct FatHeader {
 558:   ub32 magic;
 559:   ub32 nfat_arch;
 560: };
 561: 
 562: struct FatArch {
 563:   ub32 cputype;
 564:   ub32 cpusubtype;
 565:   ub32 offset;
 566:   ub32 size;
 567:   ub32 align;
 568: };
 569: 
 570: struct MachHeader {
 571:   ul32 magic;
 572:   ul32 cputype;
 573:   ul32 cpusubtype;
 574:   ul32 filetype;
 575:   ul32 ncmds;
 576:   ul32 sizeofcmds;
 577:   ul32 flags;
 578:   ul32 reserved;
 579: };
 580: 
 581: struct LoadCommand {
 582:   ul32 cmd;
 583:   ul32 cmdsize;
 584: };
 585: 
 586: template <typename E>
 587: struct SegmentCommand {
 588:   std::string_view get_segname() const {
 589:     return {segname, strnlen(segname, sizeof(segname))};
 590:   }
 591: 
 592:   ul32 cmd;
 593:   ul32 cmdsize;
 594:   char segname[16];
 595:   Word<E> vmaddr;
 596:   Word<E> vmsize;
 597:   Word<E> fileoff;
 598:   Word<E> filesize;
 599:   ul32 maxprot;
 600:   ul32 initprot;
 601:   ul32 nsects;
 602:   ul32 flags;
 603: };
 604: 
 605: template <typename E>
 606: struct MachSection {
 607:   void set_segname(std::string_view name) {
 608:     assert(name.size() <= sizeof(segname));
 609:     memcpy(segname, name.data(), name.size());
 610:   }
 611: 
 612:   std::string_view get_segname() const {
 613:     return {segname, strnlen(segname, sizeof(segname))};
 614:   }
 615: 
 616:   void set_sectname(std::string_view name) {
 617:     assert(name.size() <= sizeof(sectname));
 618:     memcpy(sectname, name.data(), name.size());
 619:   }
 620: 
 621:   std::string_view get_sectname() const {
 622:     return {sectname, strnlen(sectname, sizeof(sectname))};
 623:   }
 624: 
 625:   bool match(std::string_view segname, std::string_view sectname) const {
 626:     return get_segname() == segname && get_sectname() == sectname;
 627:   }
 628: 
 629:   bool is_text() const {
 630:     return (attr & S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS) || (attr & S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS);
 631:   }
 632: 
 633:   char sectname[16];
 634:   char segname[16];
 635:   Word<E> addr;
 636:   Word<E> size;
 637:   ul32 offset;
 638:   ul32 p2align;
 639:   ul32 reloff;
 640:   ul32 nreloc;
 641:   u8 type;
 642:   ul24 attr;
 643:   ul32 reserved1;
 644:   ul32 reserved2;
 645:   ul32 reserved3;
 646: };
 647: 
 648: struct DylibCommand {
 649:   ul32 cmd;

 651:   ul32 nameoff;

 781: // This struct is named `n_list` on BSD and macOS.
 782: template <typename E>
 783: struct MachSym {
 784:   bool is_undef() const {
 785:     return type == N_UNDF && !is_common();
 786:   }
 787: 
 788:   bool is_common() const {
 789:     return type == N_UNDF && is_extern && value;
 790:   }
 791: 
 792:   ul32 stroff;
 793: 
 794:   union {
 795:     u8 n_type;
 796:     struct {
 797:       u8 is_extern : 1;
 798:       u8 type : 3;
 799:       u8 is_private_extern : 1;
 800:       u8 stab : 3;
 801:     };
 802:   };
 803: 
 804:   u8 sect;
 805: 
 806:   union {
 807:     ul16 desc;
 808:     struct {
 809:       u8 padding;
 810:       u8 common_p2align : 4;
 811:     };
 812:   };
 813: 
 814:   Word<E> value;
 815: };
 816:

 819:   ul32 offset;
 820:   ul24 idx;
 821:   u8 is_pcrel : 1;
 822:   u8 p2size : 2;
 823:   u8 is_extern : 1;
 824:   u8 type : 4;
 825: };

 17: static const char helpmsg[] = R"(
 18: Sold-specific options:
 19:   --print-dependencies        Print input file dependency information
 20: 
 21: lld-compatible options:
 22:   --strict-auto-link          Ignored
 23: 
 24: Options:
 25:   -F<PATH>                    Add DIR to framework search path
 26:   -L<PATH>                    Add DIR to library search path
 27:   -ObjC                       Load all static archive members that implement
 28:                               an Objective-C class or category
 29:   -S                          Do not put debug symbols
 30:   -U <SYMBOL>                 Allow a symbol to be undefined
 31:   -Z                          Do not search the standard directories when
 32:                               searching for libraries and frameworks
 33:   -add_ast_path <FILE>        Add a N_AST symbol with the given filename
 34:   -add_empty_section <SEGNAME> <SECTNAME>
 35:                               Add an empty section
 36:   -adhoc_codesign             Add ad-hoc code signature to the output file
 37:     -no_adhoc_codesign
 38:   -all_load                   Include all objects from static archives
 39:     -noall_load
 40:   -application_extension      Verify that all dylibs are extension-safe
 41:     -no_application_extension
 42:   -arch <ARCH_NAME>           Specify target architecture
 43:   -bind_at_load               Resolve all dynamic symbols on process startup
 44:   -bundle                     Produce a mach-o bundle
 45:   -bundle_loader <EXECUTABLE> Resolve undefined symbols using the given executable
 46:   -compatibility_version <VERSION>
 47:                               Specifies the compatibility version number of the library
 48:   -current_version <VERSION>  Specifies the current version number of the library.
 49:   -data_in_code_info          Emit data-in-code information
 50:     -no_data_in_code_info
 51:   -dead_strip                 Remove unreachable functions and data
 52:   -dead_strip_dylibs          Remove unreachable dylibs from dependencies
 53:   -debug_variant              Ignored
 54:   -demangle                   Demangle C++ symbols in log messages (default)
 55:   -dependency_info <FILE>     Ignored
 56:   -dylib                      Produce a dynamic library
 57:   -dylib_compatibility_version <VERSION>
 58:                               Alias for -compatibility_version
 59:   -dylib_current_version <VERSION>

 75:   -framework <NAME>,[,<SUFFIX>]
 76:                               Search for a given framework
 77:   -headerpad <SIZE>           Allocate the size of padding after load commands
 78:   -headerpad_max_install_names
 79:                               Allocate MAXPATHLEN byte padding after load commands
 80:   -help                       Report usage information
 81:   -hidden-l<LIB>
 82:   -ignore_optimization_hints  Do not rewrite instructions as optimization (default)
 83:     -enable_optimization_hints

 85:     -no_init_offsets

 94:                               Search for a given framework
 95:   -no_deduplicate             Ignored
 96:   -no_function_starts         Do not generate an LC_FUNCTION_STARTS load command
 97:   -no_implicit_dylibs         Do not optimize references to system libraries
 98:   -no_uuid                    Do not generate an LC_UUID load command
 99:   -o <FILE>                   Set output filename
100:   -objc_abi_version <VERSION> Ignored
101:   -object_path_lto <FILE>     Write a LTO temporary file to a given path
102:   -order_file <FILE>          Layout functions and data according to specification in a given file
103:   -oso_prefix <PREFIX>        Remove a given prefix from OSO symbols
104:   -pagezero_size <SIZE>       Specify the size of the __PAGEZERO segment
105:   -platform_version <PLATFORM> <MIN_VERSION> <SDK_VERSION>
106:                               Set platform, platform version and SDK version
107:   -random_uuid                Generate a random LC_UUID load command
108:   -reexport-l<LIB>            Search for a given library and reexport it
109:   -reexport_library <FILE>    Reexport a given library
110:   -reproducible               Generate a reproducible output (default)
111:   -rpath <PATH>               Add PATH to the runpath search path list

120:   -t                          Print out each file the linker loads

122:   -u <SYMBOL>                 Force load a given symbol from archive if necessary
123:   -undefined [ error | warning | suppress | dynamic_lookup ]
124:                               Specify how to handle undefined symbols

128:                               Read a list of unexported symbols from a given file
129:   -v                          Report version information
130:   -w                          Suppress all warning messages

132:                               Search for a given framework
133:   -weak-l<LIB>                Search for a given library
134:   -x                          Strip local symbols)";

136: template <typename E>
137: static i64 parse_platform(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view arg) {
138:   static std::regex re(R"(\d+)", std::regex_constants::ECMAScript);
139:   if (std::regex_match(arg.begin(), arg.end(), re))
140:     return stoi(std::string(arg));
141: 
142:   if (arg == "macos")
143:     return PLATFORM_MACOS;
144:   if (arg == "ios")
145:     return PLATFORM_IOS;
146:   if (arg == "tvos")
147:     return PLATFORM_TVOS;
148:   if (arg == "watchos")
149:     return PLATFORM_WATCHOS;
150:   if (arg == "bridgeos")
151:     return PLATFORM_BRIDGEOS;
152:   if (arg == "mac-catalyst")
153:     return PLATFORM_MACCATALYST;
154:   if (arg == "ios-simulator")
155:     return PLATFORM_IOSSIMULATOR;
156:   if (arg == "tvos-simulator")
157:     return PLATFORM_TVOSSIMULATOR;
158:   if (arg == "watchos-simulator")
159:     return PLATFORM_WATCHOSSIMULATOR;
160:   if (arg == "driverkit")
161:     return PLATFORM_DRIVERKIT;
162:   Fatal(ctx) << "unknown -platform_version name: " << arg;
163: }
164: 
165: template <typename E>
166: VersionTriple parse_version(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view arg) {
167:   auto flags = std::regex_constants::ECMAScript | std::regex_constants::optimize;
168:   static std::regex re(R"((\d+)(?:\.(\d+))?(?:\.(\d+))?)", flags);
169: 
170:   std::cmatch m;
171:   if (!std::regex_match(arg.data(), arg.data() + arg.size(), m, re))
172:     Fatal(ctx) << "malformed version number: " << arg;
173: 
174:   VersionTriple v;
175:   v.major = (m[1].length() == 0) ? 0 : stoi(m[1]);
176:   v.minor = (m[2].length() == 0) ? 0 : stoi(m[2]);
177:   v.patch = (m[3].length() == 0) ? 0 : stoi(m[3]);
178:   return v;
179: }
180: 
181: template <typename E>
182: i64 parse_hex(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view arg) {
183:   auto flags = std::regex_constants::ECMAScript | std::regex_constants::icase;
184:   static std::regex re(R"((?:0x)?[0-9a-f]+)", flags);
185: 
186:   std::cmatch m;
187:   if (!std::regex_match(arg.begin(), arg.end(), re))
188:     Fatal(ctx) << "malformed hexadecimal number: " << arg;
189: 
190:   if (arg.starts_with("0x") || arg.starts_with("0X"))
191:     return std::stoll(std::string(arg.substr(2)), nullptr, 16);
192:   return std::stoll(std::string(arg), nullptr, 16);
193: }
194: 
195: static bool is_directory(std::filesystem::path path) {
196:   std::error_code ec;
197:   return std::filesystem::is_directory(path, ec) && !ec;
198: }
199: 
200: template <typename E>
201: static std::vector<std::string>
202: read_lines(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view path) {
203:   MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
204:     MappedFile<Context<E>>::must_open(ctx, std::string(path));
205:   std::string_view data((char *)mf->data, mf->size);
206: 
207:   std::vector<std::string> vec;
208: 
209:   while (!data.empty()) {
210:     size_t pos = data.find('\n');
211:     std::string_view line;
212: 
213:     if (pos == data.npos) {
214:       line = data;
215:       data = "";
216:     } else {
217:       line = data.substr(0, pos);
218:       data = data.substr(pos + 1);
219:     }
220: 
221:     line = string_trim(line);
222:     if (!line.empty() && !line.starts_with('#'))
223:       vec.push_back(std::string(line));
224:   }
225:   return vec;
226: }
227: 
228: template <typename E>
229: static bool should_enable_fixup_chains(Context<E> &ctx) {
230:   // Chained fixups are supported since macOS 11 or iOS 13.4.
231:   switch (ctx.arg.platform) {
232:   case PLATFORM_MACOS:
233:     return ctx.arg.platform_min_version >= VersionTriple{11, 0, 0};
234:   case PLATFORM_IOS:
235:     return ctx.arg.platform_min_version >= VersionTriple{13, 4, 0};
236:   }
237:   return false;
238: }
239: 
240: template <typename E>
241: std::vector<std::string> parse_nonpositional_args(Context<E> &ctx) {
242:   std::vector<std::string_view> &args = ctx.cmdline_args;
243:   std::vector<std::string> remaining;
244:   i64 i = 1;
245: 
246:   std::vector<std::string> framework_paths;
247:   std::vector<std::string> library_paths;
248:   bool nostdlib = false;
249:   bool version_shown = false;
250:   std::optional<i64> pagezero_size;
251:   std::optional<bool> fixup_chains;
252:   std::optional<bool> init_offsets;
253: 
254:   while (i < args.size()) {
255:     std::string_view arg;
256:     std::string_view arg2;
257:     std::string_view arg3;
258:     u64 hex_arg;
259: 
260:     auto read_arg = [&](std::string name) {
261:       if (args[i] == name) {
262:         if (args.size() <= i + 1)
263:           Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << name << ": argument missing";
264:         arg = args[i + 1];
265:         i += 2;
266:         return true;
267:       }
268:       return false;
269:     };
270: 
271:     auto read_arg2 = [&](std::string name) {
272:       if (args[i] == name) {
273:         if (args.size() <= i + 2)
274:           Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << name << ": argument missing";
275:         arg = args[i + 1];
276:         arg2 = args[i + 2];
277:         i += 3;
278:         return true;
279:       }
280:       return false;
281:     };
282: 
283:     auto read_arg3 = [&](std::string name) {
284:       if (args[i] == name) {
285:         if (args.size() <= i + 3)
286:           Fatal(ctx) << "option -" << name << ": argument missing";
287:         arg = args[i + 1];
288:         arg2 = args[i + 2];
289:         arg3 = args[i + 3];
290:         i += 4;
291:         return true;
292:       }
293:       return false;
294:     };
295: 
296:     auto read_joined = [&](std::string name) {
297:       if (read_arg(name))
298:         return true;
299:       if (args[i].starts_with(name)) {
300:         arg = args[i].substr(name.size());
301:         i++;
302:         return true;
303:       }
304:       return false;
305:     };
306: 
307:     auto read_flag = [&](std::string name) {
308:       if (args[i] == name) {
309:         i++;
310:         return true;
311:       }
312:       return false;
313:     };
314: 
315:     auto read_hex = [&](std::string name) {
316:       if (!read_arg(name))
317:         return false;
318: 
319:       size_t pos;
320:       hex_arg = std::stol(std::string(arg), &pos, 16);
321:       if (pos != arg.size())
322:         Fatal(ctx) << "malformed " << name << ": " << arg;
323:       return true;
324:     };
325: 
326:     if (args[i].starts_with('@')) {
327:       std::vector<std::string_view> vec =
328:         read_response_file(ctx, args[i].substr(1));
329:       args.erase(args.begin() + i);
330:       args.insert(args.begin() + i, vec.begin(), vec.end());
331:       continue;
332:     }
333: 
334:     if (read_flag("-help") || read_flag("--help")) {
335:       SyncOut(ctx) << "Usage: " << ctx.cmdline_args[0]
336:                    << " [options] file...\n" << helpmsg;
337:       exit(0);
338:     }
339: 
340:     if (read_flag("--print-dependencies")) {
341:       ctx.arg.print_dependencies = true;
342:     } else if (read_flag("--strict-auto-link")) {
343:     } else if (read_joined("-F")) {
344:       framework_paths.push_back(std::string(arg));
345:     } else if (read_joined("-L")) {
346:       library_paths.push_back(std::string(arg));
347:     } else if (read_flag("-Z")) {
348:       nostdlib = true;
349:     } else if (read_flag("-ObjC")) {
350:       ctx.arg.ObjC = true;
351:     } else if (read_flag("-S")) {
352:       ctx.arg.S = true;
353:     } else if (read_arg("-U")) {
354:       ctx.arg.U.push_back(std::string(arg));
355:     } else if (read_arg("-add_ast_path")) {
356:       ctx.arg.add_ast_path.push_back(std::string(arg));
357:     } else if (read_arg2("-add_empty_section")) {
358:       ctx.arg.add_empty_section.push_back({arg, arg2});
359:     } else if (read_flag("-adhoc_codesign")) {
360:       ctx.arg.adhoc_codesign = true;
361:     } else if (read_flag("-no_adhoc_codesign")) {
362:       ctx.arg.adhoc_codesign = false;
363:     } else if (read_flag("-all_load")) {
364:       remaining.push_back("-all_load");
365:     } else if (read_flag("-noall_load")) {
366:       remaining.push_back("-noall_load");
367:     } else if (read_flag("-application_extension")) {
368:       ctx.arg.application_extension = true;
369:     } else if (read_flag("-no_application_extension")) {
370:       ctx.arg.application_extension = false;
371:     } else if (read_arg("-arch")) {
372:       if (arg == "x86_64")
373:         ctx.arg.arch = CPU_TYPE_X86_64;
374:       else if (arg == "arm64")
375:         ctx.arg.arch = CPU_TYPE_ARM64;
376:       else
377:         Fatal(ctx) << "unknown -arch: " << arg;
378:     } else if (read_flag("-bind_at_load")) {
379:       ctx.arg.bind_at_load = true;
380:     } else if (read_flag("-bundle")) {
381:       ctx.output_type = MH_BUNDLE;
382:     } else if (read_arg("-bundle_loader")) {
383:       ctx.arg.bundle_loader = arg;
384:     } else if (read_arg("-compatibility_version") ||
385:                read_arg("-dylib_compatibility_version")) {
386:       ctx.arg.compatibility_version = parse_version(ctx, arg);
387:     } else if (read_arg("-current_version") ||
388:                read_arg("-dylib_current_version")) {
389:       ctx.arg.current_version = parse_version(ctx, arg);
390:     } else if (read_flag("-color-diagnostics") ||
391:                read_flag("--color-diagnostics")) {
392:       ctx.arg.color_diagnostics = true;
393:     } else if (read_flag("-data_in_code_info")) {
394:       ctx.arg.data_in_code_info = true;
395:     } else if (read_flag("-no_data_in_code_info")) {
396:       ctx.arg.data_in_code_info = false;
397:     } else if (read_flag("-dead_strip")) {
398:       ctx.arg.dead_strip = true;
399:     } else if (read_flag("-dead_strip_dylibs")) {
400:       ctx.arg.dead_strip_dylibs = true;
401:     } else if (read_flag("-debug_variant")) {
402:     } else if (read_flag("-demangle")) {
403:       ctx.arg.demangle = true;
404:     } else if (read_flag("-no_demangle")) {
405:       ctx.arg.demangle = false;
406:     } else if (read_arg("-dependency_info")) {
407:       ctx.arg.dependency_info = arg;
408:     } else if (read_flag("-dylib")) {
409:       ctx.output_type = MH_DYLIB;
410:     } else if (read_arg("-executable_path")) {
411:       ctx.arg.executable_path = arg;
412:     } else if (read_hex("-headerpad")) {
413:       ctx.arg.headerpad = hex_arg;
414:     } else if (read_flag("-headerpad_max_install_names")) {
415:       ctx.arg.headerpad = 1024;
416:     } else if (read_flag("-dynamic")) {
417:       ctx.arg.dynamic = true;
418:     } else if (read_arg("-e")) {
419:       ctx.arg.entry = get_symbol(ctx, arg);
420:     } else if (read_flag("-execute")) {
421:       ctx.output_type = MH_EXECUTE;
422:     } else if (read_flag("-export_dynamic")) {
423:       ctx.arg.export_dynamic = true;
424:     } else if (read_arg("-exported_symbol")) {
425:       if (!ctx.arg.exported_symbols_list.add(arg, 1))
426:         Fatal(ctx) << "-exported_symbol: invalid glob pattern: " << arg;
427:     } else if (read_arg("-exported_symbols_list")) {
428:       for (std::string_view pat : read_lines(ctx, arg))
429:         if (!ctx.arg.exported_symbols_list.add(pat, 1))
430:           Fatal(ctx) << "-exported_symbols_list: " << arg
431:                      << ": invalid glob pattern: " << pat;
432:     } else if (read_flag("-fatal_warnings")) {
433:       ctx.arg.fatal_warnings = true;
434:     } else if (read_arg("-filelist")) {
435:       remaining.push_back("-filelist");
436:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
437:     } else if (read_flag("-flat_namespace")) {
438:       ctx.arg.flat_namespace = true;
439:     } else if (read_arg("-final_output")) {
440:       ctx.arg.final_output = arg;
441:     } else if (read_flag("-fixup_chains")) {
442:       fixup_chains = true;
443:     } else if (read_flag("-no_fixup_chains")) {
444:       fixup_chains = false;
445:     } else if (read_arg("-force_load")) {
446:       remaining.push_back("-force_load");
447:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
448:     } else if (read_arg("-framework")) {
449:       remaining.push_back("-framework");
450:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
451:     } else if (read_arg("-lto_library")) {
452:       ctx.arg.lto_library = arg;
453:     } else if (read_arg("-macos_version_min") ||
454:                read_arg("-macosx_version_min")) {
455:       ctx.arg.platform = PLATFORM_MACOS;
456:       ctx.arg.platform_min_version = parse_version(ctx, arg);
457:     } else if (read_joined("-hidden-l")) {
458:       remaining.push_back("-hidden-l");
459:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
460:     } else if (read_flag("-ignore_optimization_hints")) {
461:     } else if (read_flag("-enable_optimization_hints")) {
462:     } else if (read_flag("-init_offsets")) {
463:       init_offsets = true;
464:     } else if (read_flag("-no_init_offsets")) {
465:       init_offsets = false;
466:     } else if (read_arg("-install_name") || read_arg("-dylib_install_name")) {
467:       ctx.arg.install_name = arg;
468:     } else if (read_joined("-l")) {
469:       remaining.push_back("-l");
470:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
471:     } else if (read_arg("-map")) {
472:       ctx.arg.map = arg;
473:     } else if (read_flag("-mark_dead_strippable_dylib")) {
474:       ctx.arg.mark_dead_strippable_dylib = true;
475:     } else if (read_arg("-mllvm")) {
476:       ctx.arg.mllvm.push_back(std::string(arg));
477:     } else if (read_joined("-needed-l")) {
478:       remaining.push_back("-needed-l");
479:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
480:     } else if (read_arg("-needed_framework") ||
481:                read_arg("-needed-framework")) {
482:       remaining.push_back("-needed_framework");
483:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
484:     } else if (read_flag("-no_deduplicate")) {
485:     } else if (read_flag("-no_function_starts")) {
486:       ctx.arg.function_starts = false;
487:     } else if (read_flag("-no_implicit_dylibs")) {
488:       ctx.arg.implicit_dylibs = false;
489:     } else if (read_flag("-no_uuid")) {
490:       ctx.arg.uuid = UUID_NONE;
491:     } else if (read_arg("-o")) {
492:       ctx.arg.output = arg;
493:     } else if (read_arg("-objc_abi_version")) {
494:     } else if (read_arg("-object_path_lto")) {
495:       ctx.arg.object_path_lto = arg;
496:     } else if (read_arg("-order_file")) {
497:       ctx.arg.order_file = read_lines(ctx, arg);
498:     } else if (read_arg("-oso_prefix")) {
499:       ctx.arg.oso_prefix = (arg == ".") ? ctx.cwd + "/" : arg;
500:     } else if (read_hex("-pagezero_size")) {
501:       pagezero_size = hex_arg;
502:     } else if (read_flag("-perf")) {
503:       ctx.arg.perf = true;
504:     } else if (read_arg3("-platform_version")) {
505:       ctx.arg.platform = parse_platform(ctx, arg);
506:       ctx.arg.platform_min_version = parse_version(ctx, arg2);
507:       ctx.arg.platform_sdk_version = parse_version(ctx, arg3);
508:     } else if (read_flag("-quick_exit")) {
509:       ctx.arg.quick_exit = true;
510:     } else if (read_flag("-no_quick_exit")) {
511:       ctx.arg.quick_exit = false;
512:     } else if (read_flag("-random_uuid")) {
513:       ctx.arg.uuid = UUID_RANDOM;
514:     } else if (read_joined("-reexport-l")) {
515:       remaining.push_back("-reexport-l");
516:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
517:     } else if (read_arg("-reexport_library")) {
518:       remaining.push_back("-reexport_library");
519:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
520:     } else if (read_flag("-reproducible")) {
521:     } else if (read_arg("-rpath")) {
522:       ctx.arg.rpaths.push_back(std::string(arg));
523:     } else if (read_flag("-search_paths_first")) {
524:       ctx.arg.search_paths_first = true;
525:     } else if (read_flag("-search_dylibs_first")) {
526:       ctx.arg.search_paths_first = false;
527:     } else if (read_arg3("-sectalign")) {
528:       u64 val = parse_hex(ctx, arg3);
529:       std::string key = std::string(arg) + "," + std::string(arg2);
530:       if (!has_single_bit(val))
531:         Fatal(ctx) << "-sectalign: invalid alignment value: " << arg3;
