532:       ctx.arg.sectalign.push_back({arg, arg2, (u8)std::countl_zero(val)});
533:     } else if (read_arg3("-sectcreate")) {
534:       ctx.arg.sectcreate.push_back({arg, arg2, arg3});
535:     } else if (read_hex("-stack_size")) {
536:       ctx.arg.stack_size = hex_arg;
537:     } else if (read_flag("-stats")) {
538:       ctx.arg.stats = true;
539:       Counter::enabled = true;
540:     } else if (read_arg("-syslibroot")) {
541:       ctx.arg.syslibroot.push_back(std::string(arg));
542:     } else if (read_flag("-t")) {
543:       ctx.arg.trace = true;
544:     } else if (read_arg("-thread_count")) {
545:       ctx.arg.thread_count = std::stoi(std::string(arg));
546:     } else if (read_arg("-u")) {
547:       ctx.arg.u.push_back(std::string(arg));
548:     } else if (read_arg("-undefined")) {
549:       if (arg == "error")
550:         ctx.arg.undefined_error = true;
551:       else if (arg == "warning" || arg == "suppress" || arg == "dynamic_lookup")
552:         ctx.arg.undefined_error = false;
553:       else
554:         Fatal(ctx) << "unknown -undefined argument: " << arg;
555:     } else if (read_arg("-umbrella")) {
556:       ctx.arg.umbrella = arg;
557:     } else if (read_arg("-unexported_symbol")) {
558:       if (!ctx.arg.unexported_symbols_list.add(arg, 1))
559:         Fatal(ctx) << "-unexported_symbol: invalid glob pattern: " << arg;
560:     } else if (read_arg("-unexported_symbols_list")) {
561:       for (std::string_view pat : read_lines(ctx, arg))
562:         if (!ctx.arg.unexported_symbols_list.add(pat, 1))
563:           Fatal(ctx) << "-unexported_symbols_list: " << arg
564:                      << ": invalid glob pattern: " << pat;
565:     } else if (read_flag("-v")) {
566:       SyncOut(ctx) << mold_version;
567:       version_shown = true;
568:     } else if (read_flag("-w")) {
569:       ctx.arg.suppress_warnings = true;
570:     } else if (read_arg("-weak_framework")) {
571:       remaining.push_back("-weak_framework");
572:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
573:     } else if (read_joined("-weak-l")) {
574:       remaining.push_back("-weak-l");
575:       remaining.push_back(std::string(arg));
576:     } else if (read_flag("-x")) {
577:       ctx.arg.x = true;
578:     } else if (read_flag("--no-call-graph-profile-sort")) {
579:     } else if (read_flag("--icf=none")) {
580:     } else {
581:       if (args[i][0] == '-')
582:         Fatal(ctx) << "unknown command line option: " << args[i];
583:       remaining.push_back(std::string(args[i]));
584:       i++;
585:     }
586:   }
587: 
588:   if (!ctx.arg.entry)
589:     ctx.arg.entry = get_symbol(ctx, "_main");
590: 
591:   if (ctx.arg.thread_count == 0)
592:     ctx.arg.thread_count = get_default_thread_count();
593: 
594:   if (!ctx.arg.bundle_loader.empty() && ctx.output_type != MH_BUNDLE)
595:     Fatal(ctx) << "-bundle_loader cannot be specified without -bundle";
596: 
597:   auto add_search_path = [&](std::vector<std::string> &vec, std::string path) {
598:     if (!path.starts_with('/') || ctx.arg.syslibroot.empty()) {
599:       if (is_directory(path))
600:         vec.push_back(path);
601:       return;
602:     }
603: 
604:     bool found = false;
605:     for (std::string &dir : ctx.arg.syslibroot) {
606:       std::string str = path_clean(dir + "/" + path);
607:       if (is_directory(str)) {
608:         vec.push_back(str);
609:         found = true;
610:       }
611:     }
612:     if (!found && is_directory(path))
613:       vec.push_back(path);
614:   };
615: 
616:   for (std::string &path : library_paths)
617:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.library_paths, path);
618: 
619:   if (!nostdlib) {
620:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.library_paths, "/usr/lib");
621:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.library_paths, "/usr/local/lib");
622:   }
623: 
624:   for (std::string &path : framework_paths)
625:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.framework_paths, path);
626: 
627:   if (!nostdlib) {
628:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.framework_paths, "/Library/Frameworks");
629:     add_search_path(ctx.arg.framework_paths, "/System/Library/Frameworks");
630:   }
631: 
632:   if (pagezero_size.has_value()) {
633:     if (ctx.output_type != MH_EXECUTE)
634:       Fatal(ctx) << "-pagezero_size option can only be used when"
635:                  << " linking a main executable";
636:     ctx.arg.pagezero_size = *pagezero_size;
637:   } else {
638:     ctx.arg.pagezero_size = (ctx.output_type == MH_EXECUTE) ? 0x100000000 : 0;
639:   }
640: 
641:   if (fixup_chains.has_value())
642:     ctx.arg.fixup_chains = *fixup_chains;
643:   else
644:     ctx.arg.fixup_chains = should_enable_fixup_chains(ctx);
645: 
646:   // -fixup_chain implies -init_offsets. They are not directly related,
647:   // but all runtimes that support chained fixups also support __init_offsets,
648:   // so we want to enable both if possible. This is what ld64 does too.
649:   if (init_offsets.has_value())
650:     ctx.arg.init_offsets = *init_offsets;
651:   else
652:     ctx.arg.init_offsets = ctx.arg.fixup_chains;
653: 
654:   if (ctx.arg.final_output.empty()) {
655:     if (!ctx.arg.install_name.empty())
656:       ctx.arg.final_output = ctx.arg.install_name;
657:     else
658:       ctx.arg.final_output = ctx.arg.output;
659:   }
660: 
661:   if (ctx.arg.executable_path.empty() && ctx.output_type == MH_EXECUTE)
662:     ctx.arg.executable_path = ctx.arg.output;
663: 
664:   if (ctx.arg.uuid == UUID_RANDOM)
665:     memcpy(ctx.uuid, get_uuid_v4().data(), 16);
666: 
667:   if (version_shown && remaining.empty())
668:     exit(0);
669:   return remaining;
670: }
671: 
672: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
673: 
674: template VersionTriple parse_version(Context<E> &, std::string_view);
675: template std::vector<std::string> parse_nonpositional_args(Context<E> &);
676: 
677: } // namespace mold::macho

================
File: macho/input-files.cc
================
   1: #include "mold.h"
   2: #include "../common/archive-file.h"
   3: 
   4: #include <regex>
   5: 
   6: namespace mold::macho {
   7: 
   8: template <typename E>
   9: std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, const InputFile<E> &file) {
  10:   if (file.archive_name.empty())
  11:     out << path_clean(file.filename);
  12:   else
  13:     out << path_clean(file.archive_name) << "(" << path_clean(file.filename) + ")";
  14:   return out;
  15: }
  16: 
  17: template <typename E>
  18: void InputFile<E>::clear_symbols() {
  19:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : syms) {
  20:     if (__atomic_load_n(&sym->file, __ATOMIC_ACQUIRE) == this) {
  21:       sym->visibility = SCOPE_LOCAL;
  22:       sym->is_imported = false;
  23:       sym->is_exported = false;
  24:       sym->is_common = false;
  25:       sym->is_weak = false;
  26:       sym->is_abs = false;
  27:       sym->is_tlv = false;
  28:       sym->no_dead_strip = false;
  29:       sym->subsec = nullptr;
  30:       sym->value = 0;
  31:       __atomic_store_n(&sym->file, nullptr, __ATOMIC_RELEASE);
  32:     }
  33:   }
  34: }
  35: 
  36: template <typename E>
  37: ObjectFile<E> *
  38: ObjectFile<E>::create(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf,
  39:                       std::string archive_name) {
  40:   ObjectFile<E> *obj = new ObjectFile<E>(mf);
  41:   obj->archive_name = archive_name;
  42:   obj->is_alive = archive_name.empty() || ctx.reader.all_load;
  43:   obj->is_hidden = ctx.reader.hidden;
  44:   ctx.obj_pool.emplace_back(obj);
  45:   return obj;
  46: };
  47: 
  48: template <typename E>
  49: void ObjectFile<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
  50:   if (get_file_type(ctx, this->mf) == FileType::LLVM_BITCODE) {
  51:     // Open an compiler IR file
  52:     load_lto_plugin(ctx);
  53: 
  54:     // It looks like module_create_from_memory is not thread-safe,
  55:     // so protect it with a lock.
  56:     {
  57:       static std::mutex mu;
  58:       std::scoped_lock lock(mu);
  59: 
  60:       this->lto_module =
  61:         ctx.lto.module_create_from_memory(this->mf->data, this->mf->size);
  62:       if (!this->lto_module)
  63:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": lto_module_create_from_memory failed";
  64:     }
  65: 
  66:     // Read a symbol table
  67:     parse_lto_symbols(ctx);
  68:     return;
  69:   }
  70: 
  71:   if (SymtabCommand *cmd = (SymtabCommand *)find_load_command(ctx, LC_SYMTAB))
  72:     mach_syms = {(MachSym<E> *)(this->mf->data + cmd->symoff), cmd->nsyms};
  73: 
  74:   parse_sections(ctx);
  75: 
  76:   if (MachHeader &hdr = *(MachHeader *)this->mf->data;
  77:       hdr.flags & MH_SUBSECTIONS_VIA_SYMBOLS) {
  78:     split_subsections_via_symbols(ctx);
  79:   } else {
  80:     init_subsections(ctx);
  81:   }
  82: 
  83:   split_cstring_literals(ctx);
  84:   split_fixed_size_literals(ctx);
  85:   split_literal_pointers(ctx);
  86: 
  87:   sort(subsections, [](Subsection<E> *a, Subsection<E> *b) {
  88:     return a->input_addr < b->input_addr;
  89:   });
  90: 
  91:   parse_symbols(ctx);
  92: 
  93:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections)
  94:     if (isec)
  95:       isec->parse_relocations(ctx);
  96: 
  97:   if (unwind_sec)
  98:     parse_compact_unwind(ctx);
  99: 
 100:   if (eh_frame_sec)
 101:     parse_eh_frame(ctx);
 102: 
 103:   associate_compact_unwind(ctx);
 104: 
 105:   if (mod_init_func)
 106:     parse_mod_init_func(ctx);
 107: }
 108: 
 109: template <typename E>
 110: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_sections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 111:   SegmentCommand<E> *cmd =
 112:     (SegmentCommand<E> *)find_load_command(ctx, LC_SEGMENT_64);
 113:   if (!cmd)
 114:     return;
 115: 
 116:   MachSection<E> *mach_sec = (MachSection<E> *)((u8 *)cmd + sizeof(*cmd));
 117:   sections.resize(cmd->nsects);
 118: 
 119:   for (i64 i = 0; i < cmd->nsects; i++) {
 120:     MachSection<E> &msec = mach_sec[i];
 121: 
 122:     if (msec.match("__LD", "__compact_unwind")) {
 123:       unwind_sec = &msec;
 124:       continue;
 125:     }
 126: 
 127: 
 128:     if (msec.match("__TEXT", "__eh_frame")) {
 129:       eh_frame_sec = &msec;
 130:       continue;
 131:     }
 132: 
 133:     if (msec.match("__DATA", "__objc_imageinfo") ||
 134:         msec.match("__DATA_CONST", "__objc_imageinfo")) {
 135:       if (msec.size != sizeof(ObjcImageInfo))
 136:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __objc_imageinfo: invalid size";
 137: 
 138:       objc_image_info =
 139:         (ObjcImageInfo *)(this->mf->get_contents().data() + msec.offset);
 140: 
 141:       if (objc_image_info->version != 0)
 142:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __objc_imageinfo: unknown version: "
 143:                    << (u32)objc_image_info->version;
 144:       continue;
 145:     }
 146: 
 147:     if (ctx.arg.init_offsets && msec.type == S_MOD_INIT_FUNC_POINTERS) {
 148:       mod_init_func = &msec;
 149:       continue;
 150:     }
 151: 
 152:     if (msec.match("__DWARF", "__debug_info"))
 153:       has_debug_info = true;
 154: 
 155:     if (msec.get_segname() == "__LLVM" || (msec.attr & S_ATTR_DEBUG))
 156:       continue;
 157: 
 158:     sections[i].reset(new InputSection<E>(ctx, *this, msec, i));
 159:   }
 160: }
 161: 
 162: template <typename E>
 163: static bool always_split(InputSection<E> &isec) {
 164:   if (isec.hdr.match("__TEXT", "__eh_frame"))
 165:     return true;
 166: 
 167:   u32 ty = isec.hdr.type;
 168:   return ty == S_4BYTE_LITERALS  || ty == S_8BYTE_LITERALS   ||
 169:          ty == S_16BYTE_LITERALS || ty == S_LITERAL_POINTERS ||
 170:          ty == S_CSTRING_LITERALS;
 171: }
 172: 
 173: template <typename E>
 174: void ObjectFile<E>::split_subsections_via_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 175:   struct MachSymOff {
 176:     MachSym<E> *msym;
 177:     i64 symidx;
 178:   };
 179: 
 180:   std::vector<MachSymOff> msyms;
 181: 
 182:   // Find all symbols whose type is N_SECT.
 183:   for (i64 i = 0; i < mach_syms.size(); i++)
 184:     if (MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 185:         !msym.stab && msym.type == N_SECT && sections[msym.sect - 1])
 186:       msyms.push_back({&msym, i});
 187: 
 188:   // Sort by address
 189:   sort(msyms, [](const MachSymOff &a, const MachSymOff &b) {
 190:     return std::tuple(a.msym->sect, a.msym->value) <
 191:            std::tuple(b.msym->sect, b.msym->value);
 192:   });
 193: 
 194:   sym_to_subsec.resize(mach_syms.size());
 195: 
 196:   // Split each input section
 197:   for (i64 i = 0; i < sections.size(); i++) {
 198:     InputSection<E> *isec = sections[i].get();
 199:     if (!isec || always_split(*isec))
 200:       continue;
 201: 
 202:     // We start with one big subsection and split it as we process symbols
 203:     auto add_subsec = [&](u32 addr) {
 204:       Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 205:         .isec = isec,
 206:         .input_addr = addr,
 207:         .input_size = (u32)(isec->hdr.addr + isec->hdr.size - addr),
 208:         .p2align = (u8)isec->hdr.p2align,
 209:         .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 210:       };
 211:       subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
 212:       subsections.push_back(subsec);
 213:     };
 214: 
 215:     add_subsec(isec->hdr.addr);
 216: 
 217:     // Find the symbols in the given section
 218:     struct Less {
 219:       bool operator()(MachSymOff &m, i64 idx) { return m.msym->sect < idx; }
 220:       bool operator()(i64 idx, MachSymOff &m) { return idx < m.msym->sect; }
 221:     };
 222: 
 223:     auto [it, end] = std::equal_range(msyms.begin(), msyms.end(), i + 1, Less{});
 224: 
 225:     for (; it != end; it++) {
 226:       // Split the last subsection into two with a symbol without N_ALT_ENTRY
 227:       // as a boundary. We don't want to create an empty subsection if there
 228:       // are two symbols at the same address.
 229:       MachSymOff &m = *it;
 230: 
 231:       if (!(m.msym->desc & N_ALT_ENTRY)) {
 232:         Subsection<E> &last = *subsections.back();
 233:         i64 size1 = (i64)m.msym->value - (i64)last.input_addr;
 234:         i64 size2 = (i64)isec->hdr.addr + (i64)isec->hdr.size - (i64)m.msym->value;
 235:         if (size1 > 0 && size2 > 0) {
 236:           last.input_size = size1;
 237:           add_subsec(m.msym->value);
 238:         }
 239:       }
 240:       sym_to_subsec[m.symidx] = subsections.back();
 241:     }
 242:   }
 243: }
 244: 
 245: // If a section is not splittable (i.e. doesn't have the
 246: // MH_SUBSECTIONS_VIA_SYMBOLS bit), we create one subsection for it
 247: // and let it cover the entire section.
 248: template <typename E>
 249: void ObjectFile<E>::init_subsections(Context<E> &ctx) {
 250:   subsections.resize(sections.size());
 251: 
 252:   for (i64 i = 0; i < sections.size(); i++) {
 253:     InputSection<E> *isec = sections[i].get();
 254:     if (!isec || always_split(*isec))
 255:       continue;
 256: 
 257:     Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 258:       .isec = isec,
 259:       .input_addr = (u32)isec->hdr.addr,
 260:       .input_size = (u32)isec->hdr.size,
 261:       .p2align = (u8)isec->hdr.p2align,
 262:       .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 263:     };
 264:     subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
 265:     subsections[i] = subsec;
 266:   }
 267: 
 268:   sym_to_subsec.resize(mach_syms.size());
 269: 
 270:   for (i64 i = 0; i < mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 271:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 272:     if (!msym.stab && msym.type == N_SECT)
 273:       sym_to_subsec[i] = subsections[msym.sect - 1];
 274:   }
 275: 
 276:   std::erase(subsections, nullptr);
 277: }
 278: 
 279: // Split __cstring section.
 280: template <typename E>
 281: void ObjectFile<E>::split_cstring_literals(Context<E> &ctx) {
 282:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections) {
 283:     if (!isec || isec->hdr.type != S_CSTRING_LITERALS)
 284:       continue;
 285: 
 286:     std::string_view str = isec->contents;
 287:     size_t pos = 0;
 288: 
 289:     while (pos < str.size()) {
 290:       size_t end = str.find('\0', pos);
 291:       if (end == str.npos)
 292:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << " corruupted cstring section: " << *isec;
 293: 
 294:       end = str.find_first_not_of('\0', end);
 295:       if (end == str.npos)
 296:         end = str.size();
 297: 
 298:       // A constant string in __cstring has no alignment info, so we
 299:       // need to infer it.
 300:       Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 301:         .isec = &*isec,
 302:         .input_addr = (u32)(isec->hdr.addr + pos),
 303:         .input_size = (u32)(end - pos),
 304:         .p2align = std::min<u8>(isec->hdr.p2align, std::countr_zero(pos)),
 305:         .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 306:       };
 307: 
 308:       subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
 309:       subsections.push_back(subsec);
 310:       pos = end;
 311:     }
 312:   }
 313: }
 314: 
 315: // Split S_{4,8,16}BYTE_LITERALS sections
 316: template <typename E>
 317: void ObjectFile<E>::split_fixed_size_literals(Context<E> &ctx) {
 318:   auto split = [&](InputSection<E> &isec, u32 size) {
 319:     if (isec.contents.size() % size)
 320:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid literals section";
 321: 
 322:     for (i64 pos = 0; pos < isec.contents.size(); pos += size) {
 323:       Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 324:         .isec = &isec,
 325:         .input_addr = (u32)(isec.hdr.addr + pos),
 326:         .input_size = size,
 327:         .p2align = (u8)std::countr_zero(size),
 328:         .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 329:       };
 330: 
 331:       subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
 332:       subsections.push_back(subsec);
 333:     }
 334:   };
 335: 
 336:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections) {
 337:     if (!isec)
 338:       continue;
 339: 
 340:     switch (isec->hdr.type) {
 341:     case S_4BYTE_LITERALS:
 342:       split(*isec, 4);
 343:       break;
 344:     case S_8BYTE_LITERALS:
 345:       split(*isec, 8);
 346:       break;
 347:     case S_16BYTE_LITERALS:
 348:       split(*isec, 16);
 349:       break;
 350:     }
 351:   }
 352: }
 353: 
 354: // Split S_LITERAL_POINTERS sections such as __DATA,__objc_selrefs.
 355: template <typename E>
 356: void ObjectFile<E>::split_literal_pointers(Context<E> &ctx) {
 357:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections) {
 358:     if (!isec || isec->hdr.type != S_LITERAL_POINTERS)
 359:       continue;
 360: 
 361:     std::string_view str = isec->contents;
 362:     assert(str.size() % sizeof(Word<E>) == 0);
 363: 
 364:     for (i64 pos = 0; pos < str.size(); pos += sizeof(Word<E>)) {
 365:       Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 366:         .isec = &*isec,
 367:         .input_addr = (u32)(isec->hdr.addr + pos),
 368:         .input_size = sizeof(Word<E>),
 369:         .p2align = (u8)std::countr_zero(sizeof(Word<E>)),
 370:         .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 371:       };
 372: 
 373:       subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
 374:       subsections.push_back(subsec);
 375:     }
 376:   }
 377: }
 378: 
 379: template <typename E>
 380: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 381:   SymtabCommand *cmd = (SymtabCommand *)find_load_command(ctx, LC_SYMTAB);
 382:   this->syms.reserve(mach_syms.size());
 383: 
 384:   i64 nlocal = 0;
 385:   for (MachSym<E> &msym : mach_syms)
 386:     if (!msym.is_extern)
 387:       nlocal++;
 388:   local_syms.reserve(nlocal);
 389: 
 390:   for (i64 i = 0; i < mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 391:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 392:     std::string_view name = (char *)(this->mf->data + cmd->stroff + msym.stroff);
 393: 
 394:     // Global symbol
 395:     if (msym.is_extern) {
 396:       this->syms.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, name));
 397:       continue;
 398:     }
 399: 
 400:     // Local symbol
 401:     local_syms.emplace_back(name);
 402:     Symbol<E> &sym = local_syms.back();
 403:     this->syms.push_back(&sym);
 404: 
 405:     sym.file = this;
 406:     sym.visibility = SCOPE_LOCAL;
 407:     sym.no_dead_strip = (msym.desc & N_NO_DEAD_STRIP);
 408: 
 409:     if (msym.type == N_ABS) {
 410:       sym.value = msym.value;
 411:       sym.is_abs = true;
 412:     } else if (!msym.stab && msym.type == N_SECT) {
 413:       sym.subsec = sym_to_subsec[i];
 414:       if (!sym.subsec)
 415:         sym.subsec = find_subsection(ctx, msym.value);
 416: 
 417:       // Subsec is null if a symbol is in a __compact_unwind.
 418:       if (sym.subsec) {
 419:         sym.value = msym.value - sym.subsec->input_addr;
 420:         sym.is_tlv = (sym.subsec->isec->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_VARIABLES);
 421:       } else {
 422:         sym.value = msym.value;
 423:       }
 424:     }
 425:   }
 426: }
 427: 
 428: // A Mach-O object file may contain command line option-like directives
 429: // such as "-lfoo" in its LC_LINKER_OPTION command. This function returns
 430: // such directives.
 431: template <typename E>
 432: std::vector<std::string> ObjectFile<E>::get_linker_options(Context<E> &ctx) {
 433:   if (get_file_type(ctx, this->mf) == FileType::LLVM_BITCODE)
 434:     return {};
 435: 
 436:   MachHeader &hdr = *(MachHeader *)this->mf->data;
 437:   u8 *p = this->mf->data + sizeof(hdr);
 438:   std::vector<std::string> vec;
 439: 
 440:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.ncmds; i++) {
 441:     LoadCommand &lc = *(LoadCommand *)p;
 442:     p += lc.cmdsize;
 443: 
 444:     if (lc.cmd == LC_LINKER_OPTION) {
 445:       LinkerOptionCommand *cmd = (LinkerOptionCommand *)&lc;
 446:       char *buf = (char *)cmd + sizeof(*cmd);
 447:       for (i64 i = 0; i < cmd->count; i++) {
 448:         vec.push_back(buf);
 449:         buf += vec.back().size() + 1;
 450:       }
 451:     }
 452:   }
 453:   return vec;
 454: }
 455: 
 456: template <typename E>
 457: LoadCommand *ObjectFile<E>::find_load_command(Context<E> &ctx, u32 type) {
 458:   if (!this->mf)
 459:     return nullptr;
 460: 
 461:   MachHeader &hdr = *(MachHeader *)this->mf->data;
 462:   u8 *p = this->mf->data + sizeof(hdr);
 463: 
 464:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.ncmds; i++) {
 465:     LoadCommand &lc = *(LoadCommand *)p;
 466:     if (lc.cmd == type)
 467:       return &lc;
 468:     p += lc.cmdsize;
 469:   }
 470:   return nullptr;
 471: }
 472: 
 473: template <typename E>
 474: Subsection<E> *
 475: ObjectFile<E>::find_subsection(Context<E> &ctx, u32 addr) {
 476:   assert(subsections.size() > 0);
 477: 
 478:   auto it = std::partition_point(subsections.begin(), subsections.end(),
 479:                                  [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 480:     return subsec->input_addr <= addr;
 481:   });
 482: 
 483:   if (it == subsections.begin())
 484:     return nullptr;
 485:   return *(it - 1);
 486: }
 487: 
 488: // __compact_unwind consitss of fixed-sized records so-called compact
 489: // unwinding records. There is usually a compact unwinding record for each
 490: // function, and the record explains how to handle exceptions for that
 491: // function.
 492: //
 493: // Output file's __compact_unwind contains not only unwinding records but
 494: // also contains a two-level lookup table to quickly find out an unwinding
 495: // record for a given function address. When an exception is thrown at
 496: // runtime, the runtime looks up the table with the current program
 497: // counter as a key to find out an unwinding record to know how to handle
 498: // the exception.
 499: //
 500: // In order to construct the lookup table, we need to parse input files'
 501: // unwinding records. The following function does that.
 502: template <typename E>
 503: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_compact_unwind(Context<E> &ctx) {
 504:   MachSection<E> &hdr = *unwind_sec;
 505: 
 506:   if (hdr.size % sizeof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>))
 507:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": invalid __compact_unwind section size";
 508: 
 509:   i64 num_entries = hdr.size / sizeof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>);
 510:   unwind_records.reserve(num_entries);
 511: 
 512:   CompactUnwindEntry<E> *src =
 513:     (CompactUnwindEntry<E> *)(this->mf->data + hdr.offset);
 514: 
 515:   // Read compact unwind entries
 516:   for (i64 i = 0; i < num_entries; i++) {
 517:     unwind_records.push_back(UnwindRecord<E>{
 518:       .code_len = src[i].code_len,
 519:       .encoding = src[i].encoding,
 520:     });
 521:   }
 522: 
 523:   auto find_symbol = [&](u32 addr) -> Symbol<E> * {
 524:     for (i64 i = 0; i < mach_syms.size(); i++)
 525:       if (MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i]; msym.is_extern && msym.value == addr)
 526:         return this->syms[i];
 527:     return nullptr;
 528:   };
 529: 
 530:   // Read relocations
 531:   MachRel *mach_rels = (MachRel *)(this->mf->data + hdr.reloff);
 532:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.nreloc; i++) {
 533:     MachRel &r = mach_rels[i];
 534:     if (r.offset >= hdr.size)
 535:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": relocation offset too large: " << i;
 536: 
 537:     i64 idx = r.offset / sizeof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>);
 538:     UnwindRecord<E> &dst = unwind_records[idx];
 539: 
 540:     auto error = [&] {
 541:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __compact_unwind: unsupported relocation: " << i
 542:                  << " " << *this->syms[r.idx];
 543:     };
 544: 
 545:     if ((1 << r.p2size) != sizeof(Word<E>) || r.type != E::abs_rel)
 546:       error();
 547: 
 548:     switch (r.offset % sizeof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>)) {
 549:     case offsetof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>, code_start): {
 550:       Subsection<E> *target;
 551:       if (r.is_extern) {
 552:         dst.subsec = sym_to_subsec[r.idx];
 553:         dst.input_offset = src[idx].code_start;
 554:       } else {
 555:         dst.subsec = find_subsection(ctx, src[idx].code_start);
 556:         dst.input_offset = dst.subsec->input_addr - src[idx].code_start;
 557:       }
 558: 
 559:       if (!dst.subsec)
 560:         error();
 561:       break;
 562:     }
 563:     case offsetof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>, personality):
 564:       if (r.is_extern) {
 565:         dst.personality = this->syms[r.idx];
 566:       } else {
 567:         u32 addr = *(ul32 *)(this->mf->data + hdr.offset + r.offset);
 568:         dst.personality = find_symbol(addr);
 569:       }
 570: 
 571:       if (!dst.personality)
 572:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __compact_unwind: unsupported "
 573:                    << "personality reference: " << i;
 574:       break;
 575:     case offsetof(CompactUnwindEntry<E>, lsda): {
 576:       u32 addr = *(ul32 *)((u8 *)this->mf->data + hdr.offset + r.offset);
 577: 
 578:       if (r.is_extern) {
 579:         dst.lsda = sym_to_subsec[r.idx];
 580:         dst.lsda_offset = addr;
 581:       } else {
 582:         dst.lsda = find_subsection(ctx, addr);
 583:         if (!dst.lsda)
 584:           error();
 585:         dst.lsda_offset = addr - dst.lsda->input_addr;
 586:       }
 587:       break;
 588:     }
 589:     default:
 590:       error();
 591:     }
 592:   }
 593: 
 594:   // We want to ignore compact unwind records that point to DWARF unwind
 595:   // info because we synthesize them ourselves. Object files usually don't
 596:   // contain such records, but `ld -r` often produces them.
 597:   std::erase_if(unwind_records, [](UnwindRecord<E> &rec) {
 598:     return (rec.encoding & UNWIND_MODE_MASK) == E::unwind_mode_dwarf;
 599:   });
 600: 
 601:   for (UnwindRecord<E> &rec : unwind_records) {
 602:     if (!rec.subsec)
 603:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __compact_unwind: missing relocation at offset 0x"
 604:                  << std::hex << rec.input_offset;
 605:     rec.subsec->has_compact_unwind = true;
 606:   }
 607: }
 608: 
 609: // __eh_frame contains variable-sized records called CIE and FDE.
 610: // In an __eh_frame section, there's usually one CIE record followed
 611: // by as many FDE records as the number of functions defined by the
 612: // same input file.
 613: //
 614: // A CIE usually contains one PC-relative GOT-referencing relocation.
 615: // FDE usually contains no relocations. However, object files created
 616: // by `ld -r` contains many relocations for __eh_frame.
 617: //
 618: // This function applies relocations against __eh_frame input sections
 619: // so that all __eh_frame contains only one relocation for an CIE and
 620: // no relocation for FDEs.
 621: template <typename E>
 622: static void apply_eh_frame_relocs(Context<E> &ctx, ObjectFile<E> &file) {
 623:   MachSection<E> &msec = *file.eh_frame_sec;
 624:   u8 *buf = (u8 *)file.mf->get_contents().data() + msec.offset;
 625:   MachRel *mach_rels = (MachRel *)(file.mf->data + msec.reloff);
 626: 
 627:   for (i64 i = 0; i < msec.nreloc; i++) {
 628:     MachRel &r1 = mach_rels[i];
 629: 
 630:     switch (r1.type) {
 631:     case E::subtractor_rel: {
 632:       if (i + 1 == msec.nreloc)
 633:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": __eh_frame: invalid subtractor reloc";
 634: 
 635:       MachRel &r2 = mach_rels[++i];
 636:       if (r2.type != E::abs_rel)
 637:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": __eh_frame: invalid subtractor reloc pair";
 638: 
 639:       u32 target1 = r1.is_extern ? file.mach_syms[r1.idx].value : r1.idx;
 640:       u32 target2 = r2.is_extern ? file.mach_syms[r2.idx].value : r2.idx;
 641: 
 642:       if (r1.p2size == 2)
 643:         *(ul32 *)(buf + r1.offset) += target2 - target1;
 644:       else if (r1.p2size == 3)
 645:         *(ul64 *)(buf + r1.offset) += (i32)(target2 - target1);
 646:       else
 647:         Fatal(ctx) << file << ": __eh_frame: invalid p2size";
 648:       break;
 649:     }
 650:     case E::gotpc_rel:
 651:       break;
 652:     default:
 653:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": unknown relocation type";
 654:     }
 655:   }
 656: }
 657: 
 658: template <typename E>
 659: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_eh_frame(Context<E> &ctx) {
 660:   apply_eh_frame_relocs(ctx, *this);
 661: 
 662:   const char *start = this->mf->get_contents().data() + eh_frame_sec->offset;
 663:   std::string_view data(start, eh_frame_sec->size);
 664: 
 665:   // Split section contents into CIE and FDE records
 666:   while (!data.empty()) {
 667:     u32 len = *(ul32 *)data.data();
 668:     if (len == 0xffff'ffff)
 669:       Fatal(ctx) << *this
 670:                  << ": __eh_frame record with an extended length is not supported";
 671: 
 672:     u32 offset = data.data() - start;
 673: 
 674:     u32 id = *(ul32 *)(data.data() + 4);
 675:     if (id == 0) {
 676:       cies.emplace_back(new CieRecord<E>{
 677:         .file = this,
 678:         .input_addr = (u32)(eh_frame_sec->addr + offset),
 679:       });
 680:     } else {
 681:       u64 addr = *(il64 *)(data.data() + 8) + eh_frame_sec->addr + offset + 8;
 682:       Subsection<E> *subsec = find_subsection(ctx, addr);
 683:       if (!subsec)
 684:         Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __unwind_info: FDE with invalid function"
 685:                    << " reference at 0x" << std::hex << offset;
 686: 
 687:       if (!subsec->has_compact_unwind) {
 688:         fdes.push_back(FdeRecord<E>{
 689:           .subsec = subsec,
 690:           .input_addr = (u32)(eh_frame_sec->addr + offset),
 691:        });
 692:       }
 693:     }
 694: 
 695:     data = data.substr(len + 4);
 696:   }
 697: 
 698:   sort(fdes, [](const FdeRecord<E> &a, const FdeRecord<E> &b) {
 699:     return a.subsec->input_addr < b.subsec->input_addr;
 700:   });
 701: 
 702:   for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : cies)
 703:     cie->parse(ctx);
 704:   for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : fdes)
 705:     fde.parse(ctx);
 706: 
 707:   MachRel *mach_rels = (MachRel *)(this->mf->data + eh_frame_sec->reloff);
 708: 
 709:   auto find_cie = [&](u32 input_addr) -> CieRecord<E> * {
 710:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : cies)
 711:       if (cie->input_addr <= input_addr &&
 712:           input_addr < cie->input_addr + cie->size())
 713:         return &*cie;
 714:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __eh_frame: unexpected relocation offset";
 715:   };
 716: 
 717:   for (i64 i = 0; i < eh_frame_sec->nreloc; i++) {
 718:     MachRel &r = mach_rels[i];
 719:     if (r.type != E::gotpc_rel)
 720:       continue;
 721: 
 722:     if (r.p2size != 2)
 723:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __eh_frame: unexpected p2size";
 724:     if (!r.is_extern)
 725:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __eh_frame: unexpected is_extern value";
 726: 
 727:     CieRecord<E> *cie = find_cie(eh_frame_sec->addr + r.offset);
 728:     cie->personality = this->syms[r.idx];
 729:     cie->personality_offset = eh_frame_sec->addr + r.offset - cie->input_addr;
 730:   }
 731: }
 732: 
 733: template <typename E>
 734: void ObjectFile<E>::associate_compact_unwind(Context<E> &ctx) {
 735:   // If a subsection has a DWARF unwind info, we need to create a compact
 736:   // unwind record that points to it.
 737:   for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : fdes) {
 738:     unwind_records.push_back(UnwindRecord<E>{
 739:       .subsec = fde.subsec,
 740:       .fde = &fde,
 741:       .input_offset = 0,
 742:       .code_len = fde.subsec->input_size,
 743:     });
 744:   }
 745: 
 746:   // Sort unwind entries by offset
 747:   sort(unwind_records, [](const UnwindRecord<E> &a, const UnwindRecord<E> &b) {
 748:     return std::tuple(a.subsec->input_addr, a.input_offset) <
 749:            std::tuple(b.subsec->input_addr, b.input_offset);
 750:   });
 751: 
 752:   // Associate unwind entries to subsections
 753:   for (i64 i = 0, end = unwind_records.size(); i < end;) {
 754:     Subsection<E> &subsec = *unwind_records[i].subsec;
 755:     subsec.unwind_offset = i;
 756: 
 757:     i64 j = i + 1;
 758:     while (j < end && unwind_records[j].subsec == &subsec)
 759:       j++;
 760:     subsec.nunwind = j - i;
 761:     i = j;
 762:   }
 763: }
 764: 
 765: // __mod_init_func section contains pointers to glolbal initializers, e.g.
 766: // functions that have to run before main().
 767: //
 768: // We can just copy input __mod_init_func sections into an output
 769: // __mod_init_func. In this case, the output consists of absolute
 770: // addresses of functions, which needs base relocation for PIE.
 771: //
 772: // If -init_offset is given, we translate __mod_init_func to __init_offset,
 773: // which contains 32-bit offsets from the image base to initializer functions.
 774: // __init_offset and __mod_init_func are functionally the same, but the
 775: // former doesn't need to be base-relocated and thus a bit more efficient.
 776: template <typename E>
 777: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_mod_init_func(Context<E> &ctx) {
 778:   MachSection<E> &hdr = *mod_init_func;
 779: 
 780:   if (hdr.size % sizeof(Word<E>))
 781:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __mod_init_func: unexpected section size";
 782: 
 783:   MachRel *begin = (MachRel *)(this->mf->data + hdr.reloff);
 784:   std::vector<MachRel> rels(begin, begin + hdr.nreloc);
 785: 
 786:   sort(rels, [](const MachRel &a, const MachRel &b) {
 787:     return a.offset < b.offset;
 788:   });
 789: 
 790:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rels.size(); i++) {
 791:     MachRel r = rels[i];
 792: 
 793:     if (r.type != E::abs_rel)
 794:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __mod_init_func: unexpected relocation type";
 795:     if (r.offset != i * sizeof(Word<E>))
 796:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __mod_init_func: unexpected relocation offset";
 797:     if (!r.is_extern)
 798:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __mod_init_func: unexpected is_extern value";
 799: 
 800:     Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[r.idx];
 801: 
 802:     if (sym->visibility != SCOPE_LOCAL)
 803:       Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": __mod_init_func: non-local initializer function";
 804: 
 805:     init_functions.push_back(sym);
 806:   }
 807: }
 808: 
 809: // Symbols with higher priorities overwrites symbols with lower priorities.
 810: // Here is the list of priorities, from the highest to the lowest.
 811: //
 812: //  1. Strong defined symbol
 813: //  2. Weak defined symbol
 814: //  3. Strong defined symbol in a DSO/archive
 815: //  4. Weak Defined symbol in a DSO/archive
 816: //  5. Common symbol
 817: //  6. Common symbol in an archive
 818: //  7. Unclaimed (nonexistent) symbol
 819: //
 820: // Ties are broken by file priority.
 821: template <typename E>
 822: static u64 get_rank(InputFile<E> *file, bool is_common, bool is_weak) {
 823:   bool is_in_archive = !file->is_alive;
 824: 
 825:   auto get_sym_rank = [&] {
 826:     if (is_common) {
 827:       assert(!file->is_dylib);
 828:       return is_in_archive ? 6 : 5;
 829:     }
 830: 
 831:     if (file->is_dylib || is_in_archive)
 832:       return is_weak ? 4 : 3;
 833:     return is_weak ? 2 : 1;
 834:   };
 835: 
 836:   return (get_sym_rank() << 24) + file->priority;
 837: }
 838: 
 839: template <typename E>
 840: static u64 get_rank(Symbol<E> &sym) {
 841:   if (!sym.file)
 842:     return 7 << 24;
 843:   return get_rank(sym.file, sym.is_common, sym.is_weak);
 844: }
 845: 
 846: template <typename E>
 847: void ObjectFile<E>::resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 848:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
 849:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 850: 
 851:     if (!msym.is_extern || msym.is_undef())
 852:       continue;
 853: 
 854:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->syms[i];
 855:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
 856:     bool is_weak = (msym.desc & N_WEAK_DEF);
 857: 
 858:     if (get_rank(this, msym.is_common(), is_weak) < get_rank(sym)) {
 859:       sym.file = this;
 860:       sym.visibility = SCOPE_MODULE;
 861:       sym.is_weak = is_weak;
 862:       sym.no_dead_strip = (msym.desc & N_NO_DEAD_STRIP);
 863: 
 864:       switch (msym.type) {
 865:       case N_UNDF:
 866:         assert(msym.is_common());
 867:         sym.subsec = nullptr;
 868:         sym.value = msym.value;
 869:         sym.is_common = true;
 870:         sym.is_abs = false;
 871:         sym.is_tlv = false;
 872:         break;
 873:       case N_ABS:
 874:         sym.subsec = nullptr;
 875:         sym.value = msym.value;
 876:         sym.is_common = false;
 877:         sym.is_abs = true;
 878:         sym.is_tlv = false;
 879:         break;
 880:       case N_SECT:
 881:         // Some symbols (notably in always-split sections such as __cstring)
 882:         // don't have a direct sym_to_subsec entry, so fall back to address lookup.
 883:         sym.subsec = sym_to_subsec[i];
 884:         if (!sym.subsec)
 885:           sym.subsec = find_subsection(ctx, msym.value);
 886:         if (!sym.subsec)
 887:           continue;
 888:         sym.value = msym.value - sym.subsec->input_addr;
 889:         sym.is_common = false;
 890:         sym.is_abs = false;
 891:         sym.is_tlv = (sym.subsec->isec->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_VARIABLES);
 892:         break;
 893:       default:
 894:         Fatal(ctx) << sym << ": unknown symbol type: " << (u64)msym.type;
 895:       }
 896:     }
 897:   }
 898: }
 899: 
 900: template <typename E>
 901: bool ObjectFile<E>::is_objc_object(Context<E> &ctx) {
 902:   for (std::unique_ptr<InputSection<E>> &isec : sections)
 903:     if (isec)
 904:       if (isec->hdr.match("__DATA", "__objc_catlist") ||
 905:           (isec->hdr.get_segname() == "__TEXT" &&
 906:            isec->hdr.get_sectname().starts_with("__swift")))
 907:         return true;
 908: 
 909:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++)
 910:     if (!mach_syms[i].is_undef() && mach_syms[i].is_extern &&
 911:         this->syms[i]->name.starts_with("_OBJC_CLASS_$_"))
 912:       return true;
 913: 
 914:   return false;
 915: }
 916: 
 917: template <typename E>
 918: void
 919: ObjectFile<E>::mark_live_objects(Context<E> &ctx,
 920:                                  std::function<void(ObjectFile<E> *)> feeder) {
 921:   assert(this->is_alive);
 922: 
 923:   auto is_module_local = [&](MachSym<E> &msym) {
 924:     return this->is_hidden || msym.is_private_extern ||
 925:            ((msym.desc & N_WEAK_REF) && (msym.desc & N_WEAK_DEF));
 926:   };
 927: 
 928:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
 929:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 930:     if (!msym.is_extern)
 931:       continue;
 932: 
 933:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->syms[i];
 934:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
 935: 
 936:     // If at least one symbol defines it as an GLOBAL symbol, the result
 937:     // is an GLOBAL symbol instead of MODULE, so that the symbol is exported.
 938:     if (!msym.is_undef() && !is_module_local(msym))
 939:       sym.visibility = SCOPE_GLOBAL;
 940: 
 941:     if (InputFile<E> *file = sym.file)
 942:       if (msym.is_undef() || (msym.is_common() && !sym.is_common))
 943:         if (!file->is_alive.test_and_set() && !file->is_dylib)
 944:           feeder((ObjectFile<E> *)file);
 945:   }
 946: 
 947:   for (Subsection<E> *subsec : subsections)
 948:     for (UnwindRecord<E> &rec : subsec->get_unwind_records())
 949:       if (Symbol<E> *sym = rec.personality)
 950:         if (InputFile<E> *file = sym->file)
 951:           if (!file->is_alive.test_and_set() && !file->is_dylib)
 952:             feeder((ObjectFile<E> *)file);
 953: }
 954: 
 955: template <typename E>
 956: void ObjectFile<E>::convert_common_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 957:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 958:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->syms[i];
 959:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 960: 
 961:     if (sym.file == this && sym.is_common) {
 962:       InputSection<E> *isec = get_common_sec(ctx);
 963:       Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
 964:         .isec = isec,
 965:         .input_size = (u32)msym.value,
 966:         .p2align = (u8)msym.common_p2align,
 967:         .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
 968:       };
 969: 
 970:       subsections.emplace_back(subsec);
 971: 
 972:       sym.is_weak = false;
 973:       sym.no_dead_strip = (msym.desc & N_NO_DEAD_STRIP);
 974:       sym.subsec = subsec;
 975:       sym.value = 0;
 976:       sym.is_common = false;
 977:       sym.is_abs = false;
 978:       sym.is_tlv = false;
 979:     }
 980:   }
 981: }
 982: 
 983: template <typename E>
 984: void ObjectFile<E>::check_duplicate_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
 985:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->mach_syms.size(); i++) {
 986:     Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[i];
 987:     MachSym<E> &msym = mach_syms[i];
 988:     if (sym && sym->file && sym->file != this && !msym.is_undef() &&
 989:         !msym.is_common() && !(msym.desc & N_WEAK_DEF))
 990:       Error(ctx) << "duplicate symbol: " << *this << ": " << *sym->file
 991:                  << ": " << *sym;
 992:   }
 993: }
 994: 
 995: template <typename E>
 996: InputSection<E> *ObjectFile<E>::get_common_sec(Context<E> &ctx) {
 997:   if (!common_sec) {
 998:     MachSection<E> *hdr = new MachSection<E>;
 999:     common_hdr.reset(hdr);
1000: 
1001:     memset(hdr, 0, sizeof(*hdr));
1002:     hdr->set_segname("__DATA");
1003:     hdr->set_sectname("__common");
1004:     hdr->type = S_ZEROFILL;
1005: 
1006:     common_sec = new InputSection<E>(ctx, *this, *hdr, sections.size());
1007:     sections.emplace_back(common_sec);
1008:   }
1009:   return common_sec;
1010: }
1011: 
1012: template <typename E>
1013: void ObjectFile<E>::parse_lto_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1014:   i64 nsyms = ctx.lto.module_get_num_symbols(this->lto_module);
1015:   this->syms.reserve(nsyms);
1016:   this->mach_syms2.reserve(nsyms);
1017: 
1018:   for (i64 i = 0; i < nsyms; i++) {
1019:     std::string_view name = ctx.lto.module_get_symbol_name(this->lto_module, i);
1020:     this->syms.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, name));
1021: 
1022:     u32 attr = ctx.lto.module_get_symbol_attribute(this->lto_module, i);
1023:     MachSym<E> msym = {};
1024: 
1025:     switch (attr & LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_MASK) {
1026:     case LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_REGULAR:
1027:     case LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_TENTATIVE:
1028:     case LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_WEAK:
1029:       msym.type = N_ABS;
1030:       break;
1031:     case LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_UNDEFINED:
1032:     case LTO_SYMBOL_DEFINITION_WEAKUNDEF:
1033:       msym.type = N_UNDF;
1034:       break;
1035:     default:
1036:       unreachable();
1037:     }
1038: 
1039:     switch (attr & LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_MASK) {
1040:     case 0:
1041:     case LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_INTERNAL:
1042:     case LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_HIDDEN:
1043:       break;
1044:     case LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_DEFAULT:
1045:     case LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_PROTECTED:
1046:     case LTO_SYMBOL_SCOPE_DEFAULT_CAN_BE_HIDDEN:
1047:       msym.is_extern = true;
1048:       break;
1049:     default:
1050:       unreachable();
1051:     }
1052: 
1053:     mach_syms2.push_back(msym);
1054:   }
1055: 
1056:   mach_syms = mach_syms2;
1057: }
1058: 
1059: template <typename E>
1060: std::string_view ObjectFile<E>::get_linker_optimization_hints(Context<E> &ctx) {
1061:   LinkEditDataCommand *cmd =
1062:     (LinkEditDataCommand *)find_load_command(ctx, LC_LINKER_OPTIMIZATION_HINT);
1063: 
1064:   if (cmd)
1065:     return {(char *)this->mf->data + cmd->dataoff, cmd->datasize};
1066:   return {};
1067: }
1068: 
1069: // As a space optimization, Xcode 14 or later emits code to just call
1070: // `_objc_msgSend$foo` to call `_objc_msgSend` function with a selector
1071: // `foo`.
1072: //
1073: // It is now the linker's responsibility to synthesize code and data
1074: // for undefined symbol of the form `_objc_msgSend$<method_name>`.
1075: // To do so, we need to synthsize three subsections containing the following
1076: // pieces of code/data:
1077: //
1078: //  1. `__objc_stubs`:    containing machine code to call `_objc_msgSend`
1079: //  2. `__objc_methname`: containing a null-terminated method name string
1080: //  3. `__objc_selrefs`:  containing a pointer to the method name string
1081: template <typename E>
1082: void ObjectFile<E>::add_msgsend_symbol(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
1083:   assert(this == ctx.internal_obj);
1084: 
1085:   std::string_view prefix = "_objc_msgSend$";
1086:   assert(sym.name.starts_with(prefix));
1087: 
1088:   this->syms.push_back(&sym);
1089:   sym.file = this;
1090: 
1091:   Subsection<E> *subsec = add_methname_string(ctx, sym.name.substr(prefix.size()));
1092:   ctx.objc_stubs->methnames.push_back(subsec);
1093:   ctx.objc_stubs->selrefs.push_back(add_selrefs(ctx, *subsec));
1094:   ctx.objc_stubs->hdr.size += ObjcStubsSection<E>::ENTRY_SIZE;
1095: }
1096: 
1097: template <typename E>
1098: Subsection<E> *
1099: ObjectFile<E>::add_methname_string(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view contents) {
1100:   assert(this == ctx.internal_obj);
1101:   assert(contents[contents.size()] == '\0');
1102: 
1103:   u64 addr = 0;
1104:   if (!sections.empty()) {
1105:     const MachSection<E> &hdr = sections.back()->hdr;
1106:     addr = hdr.addr + hdr.size;
1107:   }
1108: 
1109:   // Create a dummy Mach-O section
1110:   MachSection<E> *msec = new MachSection<E>;
1111:   mach_sec_pool.emplace_back(msec);
1112: 
1113:   memset(msec, 0, sizeof(*msec));
1114:   msec->set_segname("__TEXT");
1115:   msec->set_sectname("__objc_methname");
1116:   msec->addr = addr;
1117:   msec->size = contents.size() + 1;
1118:   msec->type = S_CSTRING_LITERALS;
1119: 
1120:   // Create a dummy InputSection
1121:   InputSection<E> *isec = new InputSection<E>(ctx, *this, *msec, sections.size());
1122:   sections.emplace_back(isec);
1123:   isec->contents = contents;
1124: 
1125:   Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
1126:     .isec = isec,
1127:     .input_addr = (u32)addr,
1128:     .input_size = (u32)contents.size() + 1,
1129:     .p2align = 0,
1130:     .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
1131:   };
1132: 
1133:   subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
1134:   subsections.push_back(subsec);
1135:   return subsec;
1136: }
1137: 
1138: template <typename E>
1139: Subsection<E> *
1140: ObjectFile<E>::add_selrefs(Context<E> &ctx, Subsection<E> &methname) {
1141:   assert(this == ctx.internal_obj);
1142: 
1143:   // Create a dummy Mach-O section
1144:   MachSection<E> *msec = new MachSection<E>;
1145:   mach_sec_pool.emplace_back(msec);
1146: 
1147:   memset(msec, 0, sizeof(*msec));
1148:   msec->set_segname("__DATA");
1149:   msec->set_sectname("__objc_selrefs");
1150:   msec->addr = sections.back()->hdr.addr + sections.back()->hdr.size,
1151:   msec->size = sizeof(Word<E>);
1152:   msec->type = S_LITERAL_POINTERS;
1153:   msec->attr = S_ATTR_NO_DEAD_STRIP;
1154: 
1155:   // Create a dummy InputSection
1156:   InputSection<E> *isec = new InputSection<E>(ctx, *this, *msec, sections.size());
1157:   sections.emplace_back(isec);
1158:   isec->contents = "\0\0\0\0\0\0\0\0"sv;
1159: 
1160:   // Create a dummy relocation
1161:   isec->rels.push_back(Relocation<E>{
1162:     .target = &methname,
1163:     .offset = 0,
1164:     .type = E::abs_rel,
1165:     .size = (u8)sizeof(Word<E>),
1166:     .is_sym = false,
1167:   });
1168: 
1169:   // Create a dummy subsection
1170:   Subsection<E> *subsec = new Subsection<E>{
1171:     .isec = isec,
1172:     .input_addr = (u32)msec->addr,
1173:     .input_size = sizeof(Word<E>),
1174:     .rel_offset = 0,
1175:     .nrels = 1,
1176:     .p2align = (u8)std::countr_zero(sizeof(Word<E>)),
1177:     .is_alive = !ctx.arg.dead_strip,
1178:   };
1179: 
1180:   subsec_pool.emplace_back(subsec);
1181:   subsections.push_back(subsec);
1182:   return subsec;
1183: }
1184: 
1185: template <typename E>
1186: void ObjectFile<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1187:   auto get_oso_name = [&]() -> std::string {
1188:     if (!this->mf)
1189:       return "<internal>";
1190: 
1191:     std::string name = path_clean(this->mf->name);
1192:     if (!this->mf->parent) {
1193:       if (name.starts_with('/'))
1194:         return name;
1195:       return ctx.cwd + "/" + name;
1196:     }
1197: 
1198:     std::string parent = path_clean(this->mf->parent->name);
1199:     if (parent.starts_with('/'))
1200:       return parent + "(" + name + ")";
1201:     return ctx.cwd + "/" + parent + "(" + name + ")";
1202:   };
1203: 
1204:   // Debug symbols. Mach-O executables and dylibs generally don't directly
1205:   // contain debug info records. Instead, they have only symbols that
1206:   // specify function/global variable names and their addresses along with
1207:   // pathnames to object files. The debugger reads the symbols on startup
1208:   // and read debug info from object files.
1209:   //
1210:   // Debug symbols are called "stab" symbols.
1211:   bool emit_debug_syms = has_debug_info && !ctx.arg.S;
1212: 
1213:   if (emit_debug_syms) {
1214:     this->oso_name = get_oso_name();
1215:     if (!ctx.arg.oso_prefix.empty() &&
1216:         this->oso_name.starts_with(ctx.arg.oso_prefix))
1217:       this->oso_name = this->oso_name.substr(ctx.arg.oso_prefix.size());
1218: 
1219:     this->strtab_size += this->oso_name.size() + 1;
1220:     this->num_stabs = 3;
1221:   }
1222: 
1223:   // Symbols copied from an input symtab to the output symtab
1224:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : this->syms) {
1225:     if (!sym || sym->file != this || (sym->subsec && !sym->subsec->is_alive))
1226:       continue;
1227: 
1228:     // Symbols starting with l or L are compiler-generated private labels
1229:     // that should be stripped from the symbol table.
1230:     if (sym->name.starts_with('l') || sym->name.starts_with('L'))
1231:       continue;
1232: 
1233:     if (ctx.arg.x && sym->visibility == SCOPE_LOCAL)
1234:       continue;
1235: 
1236:     if (sym->is_imported)
1237:       this->num_undefs++;
1238:     else if (sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL)
1239:       this->num_globals++;
1240:     else
1241:       this->num_locals++;
1242: 
1243:     if (emit_debug_syms && sym->subsec)
1244:       this->num_stabs += sym->subsec->isec->hdr.is_text() ? 2 : 1;
1245: 
1246:     this->strtab_size += sym->name.size() + 1;
1247:     sym->output_symtab_idx = -2;
1248:   }
1249: }
1250: 
1251: template <typename E>
1252: void ObjectFile<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1253:   MachSym<E> *buf = (MachSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab.hdr.offset);
1254:   u8 *strtab = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab.hdr.offset;
1255:   i64 stroff = this->strtab_offset;
1256: 
1257:   // Write to the string table
1258:   std::vector<i32> pos(this->syms.size());
1259: 
1260:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
1261:     Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[i];
1262:     if (sym && sym->file == this && sym->output_symtab_idx != -1) {
1263:       pos[i] = stroff;
1264:       stroff += write_string(strtab + stroff, sym->name);
1265:     }
1266:   }
1267: 
1268:   // Write debug symbols. A stab symbol of type N_SO with a nonempty name
1269:   // marks a start of a new object file.
1270:   //
1271:   // The first N_SO symbol is intended to have a source filename (e.g.
1272:   // path/too/foo.cc), but it looks like lldb doesn't actually use that
1273:   // symbol name. Souce filename is in the debug record and thus naturally
1274:   // lldb can read it, so it doesn't make much sense to parse a debug
1275:   // record just to set a source filename which will be ignored. So, we
1276:   // always set a dummy name "-" as a filename.
1277:   //
1278:   // The following N_OSO symbol specifies a object file path, which is
1279:   // followed by N_FUN, N_STSYM or N_GSYM symbols for functions,
1280:   // file-scope global variables and global variables, respectively.
1281:   //
1282:   // N_FUN symbol is always emitted as a pair. The first N_FUN symbol
1283:   // specifies the start address of a function, and the second specifies
1284:   // the size.
1285:   //
1286:   // At the end of stab symbols, we have a N_SO symbol without symbol name
1287:   // as an end marker.
1288:   if (has_debug_info && !ctx.arg.S) {
1289:     MachSym<E> *stab = buf + this->stabs_offset;
1290:     i64 stab_idx = 2;
1291: 
1292:     stab[0].stroff = 2; // string "-"
1293:     stab[0].n_type = N_SO;
1294: 
1295:     stab[1].stroff = stroff;
1296:     stab[1].n_type = N_OSO;
1297:     stab[1].sect = E::cpusubtype;
1298:     stab[1].desc = 1;
1299:     stroff += write_string(strtab + stroff, this->oso_name);
1300: 
1301:     for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
1302:       Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[i];
1303:       if (!sym || sym->file != this || sym->output_symtab_idx == -1 || !sym->subsec)
1304:         continue;
1305: 
1306:       stab[stab_idx].stroff = pos[i];
1307:       stab[stab_idx].sect = sym->subsec->isec->osec.sect_idx;
1308:       stab[stab_idx].value = sym->get_addr(ctx);
1309: 
1310:       if (sym->subsec->isec->hdr.is_text()) {
1311:         stab[stab_idx].n_type = N_FUN;
1312:         stab[stab_idx + 1].stroff = 1; // empty string
1313:         stab[stab_idx + 1].n_type = N_FUN;
1314:         stab[stab_idx + 1].value = sym->subsec->input_size;
1315:         stab_idx += 2;
1316:       } else {
1317:         stab[stab_idx].n_type = (sym->visibility == SCOPE_LOCAL) ? N_STSYM : N_GSYM;
1318:         stab_idx++;
1319:       }
1320:     }
1321: 
1322:     assert(stab_idx == this->num_stabs - 1);
1323:     stab[stab_idx].stroff = 1; // empty string
1324:     stab[stab_idx].n_type = N_SO;
1325:     stab[stab_idx].sect = 1;
1326:   }
1327: 
1328:   // Copy symbols from input symtabs to the output sytmab
1329:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
1330:     Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[i];
1331:     if (!sym || sym->file != this || sym->output_symtab_idx == -1)
1332:       continue;
1333: 
1334:     MachSym<E> &msym = buf[sym->output_symtab_idx];
1335:     msym.stroff = pos[i];
1336:     msym.is_extern = (sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL);
1337: 
1338:     if (sym->subsec && sym->subsec->is_alive) {
1339:       msym.type = N_SECT;
1340:       msym.sect = sym->subsec->isec->osec.sect_idx;
1341:       msym.value = sym->get_addr(ctx);
1342:     } else if (sym == ctx.__dyld_private || sym == ctx.__mh_dylib_header ||
1343:                sym == ctx.__mh_bundle_header || sym == ctx.___dso_handle) {
1344:       msym.type = N_SECT;
1345:       msym.sect = ctx.data->sect_idx;
1346:       msym.value = 0;
1347:     } else if (sym == ctx.__mh_execute_header) {
1348:       msym.type = N_SECT;
1349:       msym.sect = ctx.text->sect_idx;
1350:       msym.value = 0;
1351:     } else if (sym->is_imported) {
1352:       msym.type = N_UNDF;
1353:       msym.sect = N_UNDF;
1354:     } else {
1355:       msym.type = N_ABS;
1356:       msym.sect = N_ABS;
1357:     }
1358:   }
1359: }
1360: 
1361: static bool is_system_dylib(std::string_view path) {
1362:   if (!path.starts_with("/usr/lib/") &&
1363:       !path.starts_with("/System/Library/Frameworks/"))
1364:     return false;
1365: 
1366:   static std::regex re(
1367:     R"(/usr/lib/.+\.dylib|/System/Library/Frameworks/([^/]+)\.framework/.+/\1)",
1368:     std::regex_constants::ECMAScript | std::regex_constants::optimize);
1369: 
1370:   return std::regex_match(path.begin(), path.end(), re);
1371: }
1372: 
1373: template <typename E>
1374: DylibFile<E>::DylibFile(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf)
1375:     : InputFile<E>(mf) {
1376:   this->is_dylib = true;
1377:   this->is_weak = ctx.reader.weak;
1378:   this->is_reexported = ctx.reader.reexport;
1379: 
1380:   if (ctx.reader.implicit) {
1381:     // Libraries implicitly specified by LC_LINKER_OPTION are dead-strippable
1382:     // unless the linker option explicitly marks them as needed.
1383:     this->is_alive = ctx.reader.needed;
1384:   } else {
1385:     // Even if -dead_strip was not given, a dylib with
1386:     // MH_DEAD_STRIPPABLE_DYLIB is dead-stripped if unreferenced.
1387:     bool is_dead_strippable_dylib =
1388:       get_file_type(ctx, mf) == FileType::MACH_DYLIB &&
1389:       (((MachHeader *)mf->data)->flags & MH_DEAD_STRIPPABLE_DYLIB);
1390: 
1391:     bool is_dead_strippable = ctx.arg.dead_strip_dylibs || is_dead_strippable_dylib;
1392:     this->is_alive = ctx.reader.needed || !is_dead_strippable;
1393:   }
1394: }
1395: 
1396: template <typename E>
1397: DylibFile<E> *DylibFile<E>::create(Context<E> &ctx, MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf) {
1398:   DylibFile<E> *file = new DylibFile<E>(ctx, mf);
1399:   ctx.dylib_pool.emplace_back(file);
1400:   return file;
1401: }
1402: 
1403: template <typename E>
1404: static MappedFile<Context<E>> *
1405: find_external_lib(Context<E> &ctx, DylibFile<E> &loader, std::string path) {
1406:   auto find = [&](std::string path) -> MappedFile<Context<E>> * {
1407:     if (!path.starts_with('/'))
1408:       return MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, path);
1409: 
1410:     for (const std::string &root : ctx.arg.syslibroot) {
1411:       if (path.ends_with(".tbd")) {
1412:         if (auto *file = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, root + path))
1413:           return file;
1414:         continue;
1415:       }
1416: 
1417:       if (path.ends_with(".dylib")) {
1418:         std::string stem(path.substr(0, path.size() - 6));
1419:         if (auto *file = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, root + stem + ".tbd"))
1420:           return file;
1421:         if (auto *file = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, root + path))
1422:           return file;
1423:       }
1424: 
1425:       if (auto *file = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, root + path + ".tbd"))
1426:         return file;
1427:       if (auto *file = MappedFile<Context<E>>::open(ctx, root + path + ".dylib"))
1428:         return file;
1429:     }
1430: 
1431:     return nullptr;
1432:   };
1433: 
1434:   if (path.starts_with("@executable_path/") && ctx.output_type == MH_EXECUTE) {
1435:     path = path_clean(ctx.arg.executable_path + "/../" + path.substr(17));
1436:     return find(path);
1437:   }
1438: 
1439:   if (path.starts_with("@loader_path/")) {
1440:     path = path_clean(std::string(loader.mf->name) + "/../" + path.substr(13));
1441:     return find(path);
1442:   }
1443: 
1444:   if (path.starts_with("@rpath/")) {
1445:     for (std::string_view rpath : loader.rpaths) {
1446:       std::string p = path_clean(std::string(rpath) + "/" + path.substr(6));
1447:       if (MappedFile<Context<E>> *ret = find(p))
1448:         return ret;
1449:     }
1450:     return nullptr;
1451:   }
1452: 
1453:   return find(path);
1454: }
1455: 
1456: template <typename E>
1457: void DylibFile<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
1458:   switch (get_file_type(ctx, this->mf)) {
1459:   case FileType::TAPI:
1460:     parse_tapi(ctx);
1461:     break;
1462:   case FileType::MACH_DYLIB:
1463:     parse_dylib(ctx);
1464:     break;
1465:   case FileType::MACH_EXE:
1466:     parse_dylib(ctx);
1467:     dylib_idx = BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_MAIN_EXECUTABLE;
1468:     break;
1469:   default:
1470:     Fatal(ctx) << *this << ": is not a dylib";
1471:   }
1472: 
1473:   // Read reexported libraries if any
1474:   for (std::string_view path : reexported_libs) {
1475:     MappedFile<Context<E>> *mf =
1476:       find_external_lib(ctx, *this, std::string(path));
1477:     if (!mf)
1478:       Fatal(ctx) << install_name << ": cannot open reexported library " << path;
1479: 
1480:     DylibFile<E> *child = DylibFile<E>::create(ctx, mf);
1481:     child->parse(ctx);
1482: 
1483:     // By default, symbols defined by re-exported libraries are handled as
1484:     // if they were defined by the umbrella library. At runtime, the dynamic
1485:     // linker tries to find re-exported symbols from re-exported libraries.
1486:     // That incurs some run-time cost because the runtime has to do linear
1487:     // search.
1488:     //
1489:     // As an exception, system libraries get different treatment. Their
1490:     // symbols are directly linked against their original library names
1491:     // even if they are re-exported to reduce the cost of runtime symbol
1492:     // lookup. This optimization can be disable by passing `-no_implicit_dylibs`.
1493:     if (ctx.arg.implicit_dylibs && is_system_dylib(child->install_name)) {
1494:       hoisted_libs.push_back(child);
1495:       child->is_alive = false;
1496:     } else {
1497:       for (auto [name, flags] : child->exports)
1498:         add_export(ctx, name, flags);
1499:       append(hoisted_libs, child->hoisted_libs);
1500:     }
1501:   }
1502: 
1503:   // Initialize syms
1504:   for (auto [name, flags] : exports)
1505:     this->syms.push_back(get_symbol(ctx, name));
1506: }
1507: 
1508: template <typename E>
1509: void DylibFile<E>::add_export(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name, u32 flags) {
1510:   auto mask = EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_KIND_MASK;
1511:   auto tls = EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_KIND_THREAD_LOCAL;
1512:   auto weak = EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION;
1513: 
1514:   u32 &existing = exports[name];
1515:   if (existing == 0) {
1516:     existing = flags;
1517:     return;
1518:   }
1519: 
1520:   if (((existing & mask) == tls) != ((flags & mask) == tls))
1521:     Error(ctx) << *this << ": inconsistent TLS type: " << name;
1522: 
1523:   if ((existing & weak) && !(flags & weak))
1524:     existing = flags;
1525: }
1526: 
1527: template <typename E>
1528: void DylibFile<E>::read_trie(Context<E> &ctx, u8 *start, i64 offset,
1529:                              const std::string &prefix) {
1530:   u8 *buf = start + offset;
1531: 
1532:   if (*buf) {
1533:     read_uleb(buf); // size
1534:     u32 flags = read_uleb(buf);
1535:     std::string_view name;
1536: 
1537:     if (flags & EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_REEXPORT) {
1538:       read_uleb(buf); // skip a library ordinal
1539:       std::string_view str((char *)buf);
1540:       buf += str.size() + 1;
1541:       name = !str.empty() ? str : save_string(ctx, prefix);
1542:     } else if (flags & EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_STUB_AND_RESOLVER) {
1543:       name = save_string(ctx, prefix);
1544:       read_uleb(buf); // stub offset
1545:       read_uleb(buf); // resolver offset
1546:     } else {
1547:       name = save_string(ctx, prefix);
1548:       read_uleb(buf); // addr
1549:     }
1550: 
1551:     add_export(ctx, name, flags);
1552:   } else {
1553:     buf++;
1554:   }
1555: 
1556:   i64 nchild = *buf++;
1557: 
1558:   for (i64 i = 0; i < nchild; i++) {
1559:     std::string suffix((char *)buf);
1560:     buf += suffix.size() + 1;
1561:     i64 off = read_uleb(buf);
1562:     assert(off != offset);
1563:     read_trie(ctx, start, off, prefix + suffix);
1564:   }
1565: }
1566: 
1567: template <typename E>
1568: void DylibFile<E>::parse_tapi(Context<E> &ctx) {
1569:   TextDylib tbd = parse_tbd(ctx, this->mf);
1570: 
1571:   install_name = tbd.install_name;
1572:   reexported_libs = std::move(tbd.reexported_libs);
1573: 
1574:   for (std::string_view name : tbd.exports)
1575:     add_export(ctx, name, 0);
1576: 
1577:   for (std::string_view name : tbd.weak_exports)
1578:     add_export(ctx, name, EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION);
1579: }
1580: 
1581: template <typename E>
1582: void DylibFile<E>::parse_dylib(Context<E> &ctx) {
1583:   MachHeader &hdr = *(MachHeader *)this->mf->data;
1584:   u8 *p = this->mf->data + sizeof(hdr);
1585: 
1586:   if (ctx.arg.application_extension && !(hdr.flags & MH_APP_EXTENSION_SAFE))
1587:     Warn(ctx) << "linking against a dylib which is not safe for use in "
1588:               << "application extensions: " << *this;
1589: 
1590:   for (i64 i = 0; i < hdr.ncmds; i++) {
1591:     LoadCommand &lc = *(LoadCommand *)p;
1592: 
1593:     switch (lc.cmd) {
1594:     case LC_ID_DYLIB: {
1595:       DylibCommand &cmd = *(DylibCommand *)p;
1596:       install_name = (char *)p + cmd.nameoff;
1597:       break;
1598:     }
1599:     case LC_DYLD_INFO_ONLY: {
1600:       DyldInfoCommand &cmd = *(DyldInfoCommand *)p;
1601:       if (cmd.export_off && cmd.export_size)
1602:         read_trie(ctx, this->mf->data + cmd.export_off, 0, "");
1603:       break;
1604:     }
1605:     case LC_DYLD_EXPORTS_TRIE: {
1606:       LinkEditDataCommand &cmd = *(LinkEditDataCommand *)p;
1607:       read_trie(ctx, this->mf->data + cmd.dataoff, 0, "");
1608:       break;
1609:     }
1610:     case LC_REEXPORT_DYLIB:
1611:       if (!(hdr.flags & MH_NO_REEXPORTED_DYLIBS)) {
1612:         DylibCommand &cmd = *(DylibCommand *)p;
1613:         reexported_libs.push_back((char *)p + cmd.nameoff);
1614:       }
1615:       break;
1616:     case LC_RPATH: {
1617:       RpathCommand &cmd = *(RpathCommand *)p;
1618:       std::string rpath = (char *)p + cmd.path_off;
1619:       if (rpath.starts_with("@loader_path/"))
1620:         rpath = std::string(this->mf->name) + "/../" + rpath.substr(13);
1621:       rpaths.push_back(rpath);
1622:       break;
1623:     }
1624:     }
1625:     p += lc.cmdsize;
1626:   }
1627: }
1628: 
1629: template <typename E>
1630: void DylibFile<E>::resolve_symbols(Context<E> &ctx) {
1631:   auto it = exports.begin();
1632: 
1633:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
1634:     Symbol<E> &sym = *this->syms[i];
1635:     u32 flags = (*it++).second;
1636:     u32 kind = (flags & EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_KIND_MASK);
1637: 
1638:     std::scoped_lock lock(sym.mu);
1639: 
1640:     if (get_rank(this, false, false) < get_rank(sym)) {
1641:       sym.file = this;
1642:       sym.visibility = SCOPE_GLOBAL;
1643:       sym.is_weak = this->is_weak || (flags & EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION);
1644:       sym.no_dead_strip = false;
1645:       sym.subsec = nullptr;
1646:       sym.value = 0;
1647:       sym.is_common = false;
1648:       sym.is_abs = false;
1649:       sym.is_tlv = (kind == EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_KIND_THREAD_LOCAL);
1650:     }
1651:   }
1652: 
1653:   assert(it == exports.end());
1654: }
1655: 
1656: template <typename E>
1657: void DylibFile<E>::compute_symtab_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1658:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : this->syms) {
1659:     if (sym && sym->file == this && (sym->stub_idx != -1 || sym->got_idx != -1)) {
1660:       this->num_undefs++;
1661:       this->strtab_size += sym->name.size() + 1;
1662:       sym->output_symtab_idx = -2;
1663:     }
1664:   }
1665: }
1666: 
1667: template <typename E>
1668: void DylibFile<E>::populate_symtab(Context<E> &ctx) {
1669:   MachSym<E> *buf = (MachSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + ctx.symtab.hdr.offset);
1670:   u8 *strtab = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab.hdr.offset;
1671:   i64 stroff = this->strtab_offset;
1672: 
1673:   // Copy symbols from input symtabs to the output sytmab
1674:   for (i64 i = 0; i < this->syms.size(); i++) {
1675:     Symbol<E> *sym = this->syms[i];
1676:     if (!sym || sym->file != this || sym->output_symtab_idx == -1)
1677:       continue;
1678: 
1679:     MachSym<E> &msym = buf[sym->output_symtab_idx];
1680:     msym.stroff = stroff;
1681:     msym.is_extern = true;
1682:     msym.type = N_UNDF;
1683:     msym.sect = N_UNDF;
1684:     msym.desc = dylib_idx << 8;
1685: 
1686:     stroff += write_string(strtab + stroff, sym->name);
1687:   }
1688: }
1689: 
1690: using E = MOLD_TARGET;
1691: 
1692: template class InputFile<E>;
1693: template class ObjectFile<E>;
1694: template class DylibFile<E>;
1695: template std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &, const InputFile<E> &);
1696: 
1697: } // namespace mold::macho

  32: //
  33: // input-files.cc
  34: //
  35: 
  36: // We read Mach-O-native relocations from input files and translate
  37: // them to mold's representation of relocations and then attach them
  38: // to subsections.
  39: template <typename E>
  40: struct Relocation {
  41:   u64 get_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
  42:     return sym() ? sym()->get_addr(ctx) : subsec()->get_addr(ctx);
  43:   }
  44: 
  45:   // A relocation refers to either a symbol or a subsection
  46:   Symbol<E> *sym() const {
  47:     return is_sym ? (Symbol<E> *)target : nullptr;
  48:   }
  49: 
  50:   Subsection<E> *subsec() const {
  51:     return is_sym ? nullptr : (Subsection<E> *)target;
  52:   }
  53: 
  54:   bool refers_to_tls() const {
  55:     if (sym() && sym()->subsec) {
  56:       u32 type = sym()->subsec->isec->hdr.type;
  57:       return type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_REGULAR || type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL;
  58:     }
  59:     return false;
  60:   }
  61: 
  62:   void *target = nullptr;
  63:   i64 addend = 0;
  64:   u32 offset = 0;
  65:   u8 type = -1;
  66:   u8 size = 0;
  67:   bool is_sym : 1 = false;
  68:   bool is_subtracted : 1 = false;
  69: 
  70:   // For range extension thunks
  71:   i16 thunk_idx = -1;
  72:   i16 thunk_sym_idx = -1;
  73: };
  74: 
  75: template <typename E>
  76: std::ostream &operator<<(std::ostream &out, const Relocation<E> &rel) {
  77:   out << rel_to_string<E>(rel.type);
  78:   return out;
  79: }
  80: 
  81: // UnwindRecord represent a record describing how to handle exceptions.
  82: // At runtime, the exception handler searches an unwinding record by
  83: // instruction pointer.
  84: template <typename E>
  85: struct UnwindRecord {
  86:   u64 get_func_addr(Context<E> &ctx) const {
  87:     return subsec->get_addr(ctx) + input_offset;
  88:   }
  89: 
  90:   Subsection<E> *subsec = nullptr;
  91:   Symbol<E> *personality = nullptr;
  92:   Subsection<E> *lsda = nullptr;
  93:   FdeRecord<E> *fde = nullptr;
  94:   u32 input_offset = 0;
  95:   u32 code_len = 0;
  96:   u32 encoding = 0;
  97:   u32 lsda_offset = 0;
  98: };
  99: 
 100: template <typename E>
 101: struct CieRecord {
 102:   void parse(Context<E> &ctx);
 103: 
 104:   std::string_view get_contents() const;
 105:   i64 size() const { return get_contents().size(); }
 106:   void copy_to(Context<E> &ctx);
 107: 
 108:   ObjectFile<E> *file;
 109:   Symbol<E> *personality = nullptr;
 110:   u32 input_addr = 0;
 111:   u32 personality_offset = 0;
 112:   u32 output_offset = (u32)-1;
 113:   u8 lsda_size = 0;
 114:   bool is_alive = false;
 115: };

 122:   void copy_to(Context<E> &ctx);

 125:   Subsection<E> *subsec = nullptr;
