 289: 
 290: template <typename E>
 291: static std::vector<std::vector<u8>> create_load_commands(Context<E> &ctx) {
 292:   std::vector<std::vector<u8>> vec;
 293: 
 294:   if (ctx.arg.pagezero_size)
 295:     vec.push_back(create_pagezero_cmd(ctx));
 296: 
 297:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 298:     vec.push_back(create_segment_cmd(ctx, *seg));
 299: 
 300:   if (ctx.chained_fixups && ctx.chained_fixups->hdr.size) {
 301:     vec.push_back(create_dyld_chained_fixups(ctx));
 302:     if (ctx.export_.hdr.size)
 303:       vec.push_back(create_dyld_exports_trie(ctx));
 304:   } else {
 305:     vec.push_back(create_dyld_info_only_cmd(ctx));
 306:   }
 307: 
 308:   vec.push_back(create_symtab_cmd(ctx));
 309:   vec.push_back(create_dysymtab_cmd(ctx));
 310: 
 311:   if (ctx.arg.uuid != UUID_NONE)
 312:     vec.push_back(create_uuid_cmd(ctx));
 313: 
 314:   vec.push_back(create_build_version_cmd(ctx));
 315:   vec.push_back(create_source_version_cmd(ctx));
 316: 
 317:   if (ctx.arg.function_starts)
 318:     vec.push_back(create_function_starts_cmd(ctx));
 319: 
 320:   for (DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
 321:     if (file->dylib_idx != BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_MAIN_EXECUTABLE)
 322:       vec.push_back(create_load_dylib_cmd(ctx, *file));
 323: 
 324:   for (std::string_view rpath : ctx.arg.rpaths)
 325:     vec.push_back(create_rpath_cmd(ctx, rpath));
 326: 
 327:   if (ctx.data_in_code)
 328:     vec.push_back(create_data_in_code_cmd(ctx));
 329: 
 330:   if (!ctx.arg.umbrella.empty())
 331:     vec.push_back(create_sub_framework_cmd(ctx));
 332: 
 333:   switch (ctx.output_type) {
 334:   case MH_EXECUTE:
 335:     vec.push_back(create_dylinker_cmd(ctx));
 336:     vec.push_back(create_main_cmd(ctx));
 337:     break;
 338:   case MH_DYLIB:
 339:     vec.push_back(create_id_dylib_cmd(ctx));
 340:     break;
 341:   case MH_BUNDLE:
 342:     break;
 343:   default:
 344:     unreachable();
 345:   }
 346: 
 347:   if (ctx.code_sig)
 348:     vec.push_back(create_code_signature_cmd(ctx));
 349:   return vec;
 350: }
 351: 
 352: template <typename E>
 353: void OutputMachHeader<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 354:   std::vector<std::vector<u8>> cmds = create_load_commands(ctx);
 355:   this->hdr.size = sizeof(MachHeader) + flatten(cmds).size() + ctx.arg.headerpad;
 356: }
 357: 
 358: template <typename E>
 359: static bool has_tlv(Context<E> &ctx) {
 360:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 361:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
 362:       if (chunk->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_VARIABLES)
 363:         return true;
 364:   return false;
 365: }
 366: 
 367: template <typename E>
 368: static bool has_reexported_lib(Context<E> &ctx) {
 369:   for (DylibFile<E> *file : ctx.dylibs)
 370:     if (file->is_reexported)
 371:       return true;
 372:   return false;
 373: }
 374: 
 375: template <typename E>
 376: void OutputMachHeader<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 377:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset;
 378: 
 379:   std::vector<std::vector<u8>> cmds = create_load_commands(ctx);
 380: 
 381:   MachHeader &mhdr = *(MachHeader *)buf;
 382:   mhdr.magic = 0xfeedfacf;
 383:   mhdr.cputype = E::cputype;
 384:   mhdr.cpusubtype = E::cpusubtype;
 385:   mhdr.filetype = ctx.output_type;
 386:   mhdr.ncmds = cmds.size();
 387:   mhdr.sizeofcmds = flatten(cmds).size();
 388:   mhdr.flags = MH_TWOLEVEL | MH_NOUNDEFS | MH_DYLDLINK | MH_PIE;
 389: 
 390:   if (has_tlv(ctx))
 391:     mhdr.flags |= MH_HAS_TLV_DESCRIPTORS;
 392: 
 393:   if (ctx.output_type == MH_DYLIB && !has_reexported_lib(ctx))
 394:     mhdr.flags |= MH_NO_REEXPORTED_DYLIBS;
 395: 
 396:   if (ctx.arg.mark_dead_strippable_dylib)
 397:     mhdr.flags |= MH_DEAD_STRIPPABLE_DYLIB;
 398: 
 399:   if (ctx.arg.application_extension)
 400:     mhdr.flags |= MH_APP_EXTENSION_SAFE;
 401: 
 402:   write_vector(buf + sizeof(mhdr), flatten(cmds));
 403: }
 404: 
 405: template <typename E>
 406: OutputSection<E> *
 407: OutputSection<E>::get_instance(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view segname,
 408:                                std::string_view sectname) {
 409:   static std::shared_mutex mu;
 410: 
 411:   auto find = [&]() -> OutputSection<E> * {
 412:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : ctx.chunks) {
 413:       if (chunk->hdr.match(segname, sectname)) {
 414:         if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
 415:           return osec;
 416:         Fatal(ctx) << "reserved name is used: " << segname << "," << sectname;
 417:       }
 418:     }
 419:     return nullptr;
 420:   };
 421: 
 422:   {
 423:     std::shared_lock lock(mu);
 424:     if (OutputSection<E> *osec = find())
 425:       return osec;
 426:   }
 427: 
 428:   std::unique_lock lock(mu);
 429:   if (OutputSection<E> *osec = find())
 430:     return osec;
 431: 
 432:   OutputSection<E> *osec = new OutputSection<E>(ctx, segname, sectname);
 433:   ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(osec);
 434:   return osec;
 435: }
 436: 
 437: template <typename E>
 438: void OutputSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 439:   if constexpr (is_arm<E>) {
 440:     if (this->hdr.attr & S_ATTR_SOME_INSTRUCTIONS ||
 441:         this->hdr.attr & S_ATTR_PURE_INSTRUCTIONS) {
 442:       create_range_extension_thunks(ctx, *this);
 443:       return;
 444:     }
 445:   }
 446: 
 447:   // As a special case, we need a word-size padding at the beginning
 448:   // of __data for dyld. It is located by __dyld_private symbol.
 449:   u64 offset = (this == ctx.data) ? sizeof(Word<E>) : 0;
 450: 
 451:   for (Subsection<E> *subsec : members) {
 452:     offset = align_to(offset, 1 << subsec->p2align);
 453:     subsec->output_offset = offset;
 454:     offset += subsec->input_size;
 455:   }
 456:   this->hdr.size = offset;
 457: }
 458: 
 459: template <typename E>
 460: void OutputSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 461:   assert(this->hdr.type != S_ZEROFILL);
 462: 
 463:   tbb::parallel_for_each(members, [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
 464:     std::string_view data = subsec->get_contents();
 465:     u8 *loc = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset + subsec->output_offset;
 466:     memcpy(loc, data.data(), data.size());
 467:     subsec->apply_reloc(ctx, loc);
 468:   });
 469: 
 470:   if constexpr (is_arm<E>) {
 471:     tbb::parallel_for_each(thunks,
 472:                            [&](std::unique_ptr<RangeExtensionThunk<E>> &thunk) {
 473:       thunk->copy_buf(ctx);
 474:     });
 475:   }
 476: }
 477: 
 478: template <typename E>
 479: OutputSegment<E> *
 480: OutputSegment<E>::get_instance(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view name) {
 481:   static std::shared_mutex mu;
 482: 
 483:   auto find = [&]() -> OutputSegment<E> *{
 484:     for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
 485:       if (seg->cmd.get_segname() == name)
 486:         return seg.get();
 487:     return nullptr;
 488:   };
 489: 
 490:   {
 491:     std::shared_lock lock(mu);
 492:     if (OutputSegment<E> *seg = find())
 493:       return seg;
 494:   }
 495: 
 496:   std::unique_lock lock(mu);
 497:   if (OutputSegment<E> *seg = find())
 498:     return seg;
 499: 
 500:   OutputSegment<E> *seg = new OutputSegment<E>(name);
 501:   ctx.segments.emplace_back(seg);
 502:   return seg;
 503: }
 504: 
 505: template <typename E>
 506: OutputSegment<E>::OutputSegment(std::string_view name) {
 507:   cmd.cmd = LC_SEGMENT_64;
 508:   memcpy(cmd.segname, name.data(), name.size());
 509: 
 510:   if (name == "__PAGEZERO")
 511:     cmd.initprot = 0;
 512:   else if (name == "__TEXT")
 513:     cmd.initprot = VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_EXECUTE;
 514:   else if (name == "__LINKEDIT")
 515:     cmd.initprot = VM_PROT_READ;
 516:   else
 517:     cmd.initprot = VM_PROT_READ | VM_PROT_WRITE;
 518: 
 519:   cmd.maxprot = cmd.initprot;
 520: 
 521:   if (name == "__DATA_CONST")
 522:     cmd.flags = SG_READ_ONLY;
 523: }
 524: 
 525: template <typename E>
 526: void OutputSegment<E>::set_offset(Context<E> &ctx, i64 fileoff, u64 vmaddr) {
 527:   cmd.fileoff = fileoff;
 528:   cmd.vmaddr = vmaddr;
 529: 
 530:   if (cmd.get_segname() == "__LINKEDIT")
 531:     set_offset_linkedit(ctx, fileoff, vmaddr);
 532:   else
 533:     set_offset_regular(ctx, fileoff, vmaddr);
 534: }
 535: 
 536: template <typename E>
 537: void OutputSegment<E>::set_offset_regular(Context<E> &ctx, i64 fileoff,
 538:                                           u64 vmaddr) {
 539:   Timer t(ctx, std::string(cmd.get_segname()));
 540:   i64 i = 0;
 541: 
 542:   auto is_bss = [](Chunk<E> &x) {
 543:     return x.hdr.type == S_ZEROFILL || x.hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL;
 544:   };
 545: 
 546:   auto get_tls_alignment = [&] {
 547:     i64 val = 1;
 548:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : chunks)
 549:       if (chunk->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_REGULAR ||
 550:           chunk->hdr.type == S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL)
 551:         val = std::max<i64>(val, 1 << chunk->hdr.p2align);
 552:     return val;
 553:   };
 554: 
 555:   auto get_alignment = [&](Chunk<E> &chunk) -> u32 {
 556:     switch (chunk.hdr.type) {
 557:     case S_THREAD_LOCAL_REGULAR:
 558:     case S_THREAD_LOCAL_ZEROFILL:
 559:       // A TLS initialization image is copied as a contiguous block, so
 560:       // the alignment of it is the largest of __thread_data and
 561:       // __thread_bss.  This function returns an alignment value of a TLS
 562:       // initialization image.
 563:       return get_tls_alignment();
 564:     case S_THREAD_LOCAL_VARIABLES:
 565:       // __thread_vars needs to be aligned to word size because it
 566:       // contains pointers. For some reason, Apple's clang creates it with
 567:       // an alignment of 1. So we need to override.
 568:       return sizeof(Word<E>);
 569:     default:
 570:       return 1 << chunk.hdr.p2align;
 571:     }
 572:   };
 573: 
 574:   // Assign offsets to non-BSS sections
 575:   while (i < chunks.size() && !is_bss(*chunks[i])) {
 576:     Timer t2(ctx, std::string(chunks[i]->hdr.get_sectname()), &t);
 577:     Chunk<E> &sec = *chunks[i++];
 578: 
 579:     fileoff = align_to(fileoff, get_alignment(sec));
 580:     vmaddr = align_to(vmaddr, get_alignment(sec));
 581: 
 582:     sec.hdr.offset = fileoff;
 583:     sec.hdr.addr = vmaddr;
 584: 
 585:     sec.compute_size(ctx);
 586:     fileoff += sec.hdr.size;
 587:     vmaddr += sec.hdr.size;
 588:   }
 589: 
 590:   // Assign offsets to BSS sections
 591:   while (i < chunks.size()) {
 592:     Chunk<E> &sec = *chunks[i++];
 593:     assert(is_bss(sec));
 594: 
 595:     vmaddr = align_to(vmaddr, get_alignment(sec));
 596:     sec.hdr.addr = vmaddr;
 597:     sec.compute_size(ctx);
 598:     vmaddr += sec.hdr.size;
 599:   }
 600: 
 601:   cmd.vmsize = align_to(vmaddr - cmd.vmaddr, E::page_size);
 602:   cmd.filesize = align_to(fileoff - cmd.fileoff, E::page_size);
 603: }
 604: 
 605: template <typename E>
 606: void OutputSegment<E>::set_offset_linkedit(Context<E> &ctx, i64 fileoff,
 607:                                            u64 vmaddr) {
 608:   Timer t(ctx, "__LINKEDIT");
 609: 
 610:   // Unlike regular segments, __LINKEDIT member sizes can be computed in
 611:   // parallel except __string_table and __code_signature sections.
 612:   auto skip = [&](Chunk<E> *c) {
 613:     return c == &ctx.strtab || c == ctx.code_sig.get();
 614:   };
 615: 
 616:   tbb::parallel_for_each(chunks, [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
 617:     if (!skip(chunk)) {
 618:       Timer t2(ctx, std::string(chunk->hdr.get_sectname()), &t);
 619:       chunk->compute_size(ctx);
 620:     }
 621:   });
 622: 
 623:   for (Chunk<E> *chunk : chunks) {
 624:     fileoff = align_to(fileoff, 1 << chunk->hdr.p2align);
 625:     vmaddr = align_to(vmaddr, 1 << chunk->hdr.p2align);
 626: 
 627:     chunk->hdr.offset = fileoff;
 628:     chunk->hdr.addr = vmaddr;
 629: 
 630:     if (skip(chunk)) {
 631:       Timer t2(ctx, std::string(chunk->hdr.get_sectname()), &t);
 632:       chunk->compute_size(ctx);
 633:     }
 634: 
 635:     fileoff += chunk->hdr.size;
 636:     vmaddr += chunk->hdr.size;
 637:   }
 638: 
 639:   cmd.vmsize = align_to(vmaddr - cmd.vmaddr, E::page_size);
 640:   cmd.filesize = fileoff - cmd.fileoff;
 641: }
 642: 
 643: struct RebaseEntry {
 644:   RebaseEntry(const RebaseEntry &) = default;
 645:   RebaseEntry(i32 seg_idx, i32 offset) : seg_idx(seg_idx), offset(offset) {}
 646:   auto operator<=>(const RebaseEntry &) const = default;
 647: 
 648:   i32 seg_idx;
 649:   i32 offset;
 650: };
 651: 
 652: static std::vector<u8> encode_rebase_entries(std::vector<RebaseEntry> &rebases) {
 653:   std::vector<u8> buf;
 654:   buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_SET_TYPE_IMM | REBASE_TYPE_POINTER);
 655: 
 656:   // Sort rebase entries to reduce the size of the output
 657:   tbb::parallel_sort(rebases);
 658: 
 659:   // Emit rebase records
 660:   for (i64 i = 0; i < rebases.size();) {
 661:     RebaseEntry &cur = rebases[i];
 662:     RebaseEntry *last = (i == 0) ? nullptr : &rebases[i - 1];
 663: 
 664:     // Write a segment index and an offset
 665:     if (!last || last->seg_idx != cur.seg_idx ||
 666:         cur.offset - last->offset - 8 < 0) {
 667:       buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_SET_SEGMENT_AND_OFFSET_ULEB | cur.seg_idx);
 668:       encode_uleb(buf, cur.offset);
 669:     } else {
 670:       i64 dist = cur.offset - last->offset - 8;
 671:       assert(dist >= 0);
 672: 
 673:       if (dist % 8 == 0 && dist < 128) {
 674:         buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_ADD_ADDR_IMM_SCALED | (dist >> 3));
 675:       } else {
 676:         buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_ADD_ADDR_ULEB);
 677:         encode_uleb(buf, dist);
 678:       }
 679:     }
 680: 
 681:     // Advance j so that j refers to past of the end of consecutive relocs
 682:     i64 j = i + 1;
 683:     while (j < rebases.size() &&
 684:            rebases[j - 1].seg_idx == rebases[j].seg_idx &&
 685:            rebases[j - 1].offset + 8 == rebases[j].offset)
 686:       j++;
 687: 
 688:     // Write the consecutive relocs
 689:     if (j - i < 16) {
 690:       buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_DO_REBASE_IMM_TIMES | (j - i));
 691:     } else {
 692:       buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_DO_REBASE_ULEB_TIMES);
 693:       encode_uleb(buf, j - i);
 694:     }
 695: 
 696:     i = j;
 697:   }
 698: 
 699:   buf.push_back(REBASE_OPCODE_DONE);
 700:   buf.resize(align_to(buf.size(), 8));
 701:   return buf;
 702: }
 703: 
 704: template <typename E>
 705: static bool needs_rebasing(const Relocation<E> &r) {
 706:   // Rebase only ARM64_RELOC_UNSIGNED or X86_64_RELOC_UNSIGNED relocs.
 707:   if (r.type != E::abs_rel)
 708:     return false;
 709: 
 710:   // If the reloc specifies the relative address between two relocations,
 711:   // we don't need a rebase reloc.
 712:   if (r.is_subtracted)
 713:     return false;
 714: 
 715:   // If we have a dynamic reloc, we don't need to rebase it.
 716:   if (r.sym() && r.sym()->is_imported)
 717:     return false;
 718: 
 719:   // If it refers a TLS block, it's already relative to the thread
 720:   // pointer, so it doesn't have to be adjusted to the loaded address.
 721:   if (r.refers_to_tls())
 722:     return false;
 723: 
 724:   return true;
 725: }
 726: 
 727: template <typename E>
 728: inline void RebaseSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 729:   std::vector<std::vector<RebaseEntry>> vec(ctx.objs.size());
 730: 
 731:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 732:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : ctx.objs[i]->subsections) {
 733:       if (!subsec->is_alive)
 734:         continue;
 735: 
 736:       std::span<Relocation<E>> rels = subsec->get_rels();
 737:       OutputSegment<E> &seg = *subsec->isec->osec.seg;
 738:       i64 base = subsec->get_addr(ctx) - seg.cmd.vmaddr;
 739: 
 740:       for (Relocation<E> &rel : rels)
 741:         if (needs_rebasing(rel))
 742:           vec[i].emplace_back(seg.seg_idx, base + rel.offset);
 743:     }
 744:   });
 745: 
 746:   std::vector<RebaseEntry> rebases = flatten(vec);
 747: 
 748:   for (i64 i = 0; Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.stubs.syms)
 749:     if (!sym->has_got())
 750:       rebases.emplace_back(ctx.data_seg->seg_idx,
 751:                            ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.addr + i++ * sizeof(Word<E>) -
 752:                            ctx.data_seg->cmd.vmaddr);
 753: 
 754:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got.syms)
 755:     if (!sym->is_imported)
 756:       rebases.emplace_back(ctx.data_const_seg->seg_idx,
 757:                            sym->get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.data_const_seg->cmd.vmaddr);
 758: 
 759:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.thread_ptrs.syms)
 760:     if (!sym->is_imported)
 761:       rebases.emplace_back(ctx.data_seg->seg_idx,
 762:                            sym->get_tlv_addr(ctx) - ctx.data_seg->cmd.vmaddr);
 763: 
 764:   contents = encode_rebase_entries(rebases);
 765:   this->hdr.size = contents.size();
 766: }
 767: 
 768: template <typename E>
 769: inline void RebaseSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 770:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
 771: }
 772: 
 773: template <typename E>
 774: struct BindEntry {
 775:   BindEntry(const BindEntry &) = default;
 776:   BindEntry(Symbol<E> *sym, i32 seg_idx, i32 offset, i64 addend)
 777:     : sym(sym), seg_idx(seg_idx), offset(offset), addend(addend) {}
 778: 
 779:   Symbol<E> *sym;
 780:   i32 seg_idx;
 781:   i32 offset;
 782:   i64 addend;
 783: };
 784: 
 785: template <typename E>
 786: static i32 get_dylib_idx(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym) {
 787:   assert(sym.is_imported);
 788: 
 789:   if (ctx.arg.flat_namespace)
 790:     return BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_FLAT_LOOKUP;
 791: 
 792:   if (sym.file->is_dylib)
 793:     return ((DylibFile<E> *)sym.file)->dylib_idx;
 794: 
 795:   assert(!ctx.arg.U.empty());
 796:   return BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_FLAT_LOOKUP;
 797: }
 798: 
 799: template <typename E>
 800: std::vector<u8>
 801: encode_bind_entries(Context<E> &ctx, std::vector<BindEntry<E>> &bindings) {
 802:   std::vector<u8> buf;
 803:   buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_TYPE_IMM | BIND_TYPE_POINTER);
 804: 
 805:   // Sort the vector to minimize the encoded binding info size.
 806:   sort(bindings, [](const BindEntry<E> &a, const BindEntry<E> &b) {
 807:     return std::tuple(a.sym->name, a.seg_idx, a.offset, a.addend) <
 808:            std::tuple(b.sym->name, b.seg_idx, b.offset, b.addend);
 809:   });
 810: 
 811:   // Encode bindings
 812:   for (i64 i = 0; i < bindings.size(); i++) {
 813:     BindEntry<E> &b = bindings[i];
 814:     BindEntry<E> *last = (i == 0) ? nullptr : &bindings[i - 1];
 815: 
 816:     if (!last || b.sym->file != last->sym->file) {
 817:       i64 idx = get_dylib_idx(ctx, *b.sym);
 818:       if (idx < 0) {
 819:         buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_SPECIAL_IMM |
 820:                       (idx & BIND_IMMEDIATE_MASK));
 821:       } else if (idx < 16) {
 822:         buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL_IMM | idx);
 823:       } else {
 824:         buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL_ULEB);
 825:         encode_uleb(buf, idx);
 826:       }
 827:     }
 828: 
 829:     if (!last || last->sym->name != b.sym->name ||
 830:         last->sym->is_weak != b.sym->is_weak) {
 831:       i64 flags = (b.sym->is_weak ? BIND_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_IMPORT : 0);
 832:       buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_SYMBOL_TRAILING_FLAGS_IMM | flags);
 833: 
 834:       std::string_view name = b.sym->name;
 835:       buf.insert(buf.end(), (u8 *)name.data(), (u8 *)(name.data() + name.size()));
 836:       buf.push_back('\0');
 837:     }
 838: 
 839:     if (!last || last->seg_idx != b.seg_idx || last->offset != b.offset) {
 840:       assert(b.seg_idx < 16);
 841:       buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_SEGMENT_AND_OFFSET_ULEB | b.seg_idx);
 842:       encode_uleb(buf, b.offset);
 843:     }
 844: 
 845:     if (!last || last->addend != b.addend) {
 846:       buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_SET_ADDEND_SLEB);
 847:       encode_sleb(buf, b.addend);
 848:     }
 849: 
 850:     buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_DO_BIND);
 851:   }
 852: 
 853:   buf.push_back(BIND_OPCODE_DONE);
 854:   buf.resize(align_to(buf.size(), 8));
 855:   return buf;
 856: }
 857: 
 858: template <typename E>
 859: void BindSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 860:   std::vector<std::vector<BindEntry<E>>> vec(ctx.objs.size());
 861: 
 862:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
 863:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : ctx.objs[i]->subsections) {
 864:       if (subsec->is_alive) {
 865:         for (Relocation<E> &r : subsec->get_rels()) {
 866:           if (r.type == E::abs_rel && r.sym() && r.sym()->is_imported) {
 867:             OutputSegment<E> &seg = *subsec->isec->osec.seg;
 868:             vec[i].emplace_back(r.sym(), seg.seg_idx,
 869:                                 subsec->get_addr(ctx) + r.offset - seg.cmd.vmaddr,
 870:                                 r.addend);
 871:           }
 872:         }
 873:       }
 874:     }
 875:   });
 876: 
 877:   std::vector<BindEntry<E>> bindings = flatten(vec);
 878: 
 879:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got.syms)
 880:     if (sym->is_imported)
 881:       bindings.emplace_back(sym, ctx.data_const_seg->seg_idx,
 882:                             sym->get_got_addr(ctx) - ctx.data_const_seg->cmd.vmaddr,
 883:                             0);
 884: 
 885:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.thread_ptrs.syms)
 886:     if (sym->is_imported)
 887:       bindings.emplace_back(sym, ctx.data_seg->seg_idx,
 888:                             sym->get_tlv_addr(ctx) - ctx.data_seg->cmd.vmaddr, 0);
 889: 
 890:   contents = encode_bind_entries(ctx, bindings);
 891:   this->hdr.size = contents.size();
 892: }
 893: 
 894: template <typename E>
 895: void BindSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 896:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
 897: }
 898: 
 899: template <typename E>
 900: void LazyBindSection<E>::add(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> &sym, i64 idx) {
 901:   auto emit = [&](u8 byte) {
 902:     contents.push_back(byte);
 903:   };
 904: 
 905:   i64 dylib_idx = get_dylib_idx(ctx, sym);
 906: 
 907:   if (dylib_idx < 0) {
 908:     emit(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_SPECIAL_IMM | (dylib_idx & BIND_IMMEDIATE_MASK));
 909:   } else if (dylib_idx < 16) {
 910:     emit(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL_IMM | dylib_idx);
 911:   } else {
 912:     emit(BIND_OPCODE_SET_DYLIB_ORDINAL_ULEB);
 913:     encode_uleb(contents, dylib_idx);
 914:   }
 915: 
 916:   i64 flags = (sym.is_weak ? BIND_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_IMPORT : 0);
 917:   assert(flags < 16);
 918: 
 919:   emit(BIND_OPCODE_SET_SYMBOL_TRAILING_FLAGS_IMM | flags);
 920:   contents.insert(contents.end(), (u8 *)sym.name.data(),
 921:                   (u8 *)(sym.name.data() + sym.name.size()));
 922:   emit('\0');
 923: 
 924:   i64 seg_idx = ctx.data_seg->seg_idx;
 925:   emit(BIND_OPCODE_SET_SEGMENT_AND_OFFSET_ULEB | seg_idx);
 926: 
 927:   i64 offset = ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.addr + idx * sizeof(Word<E>) -
 928:                ctx.data_seg->cmd.vmaddr;
 929:   encode_uleb(contents, offset);
 930: 
 931:   emit(BIND_OPCODE_DO_BIND);
 932:   emit(BIND_OPCODE_DONE);
 933: }
 934: 
 935: template <typename E>
 936: void LazyBindSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
 937:   bind_offsets.clear();
 938: 
 939:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.stubs.syms.size(); i++) {
 940:     bind_offsets.push_back(contents.size());
 941:     add(ctx, *ctx.stubs.syms[i], i);
 942:   }
 943: 
 944:   contents.resize(align_to(contents.size(), 1 << this->hdr.p2align));
 945:   this->hdr.size = contents.size();
 946: }
 947: 
 948: template <typename E>
 949: void LazyBindSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
 950:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
 951: }
 952: 
 953: inline i64 ExportEncoder::finish() {
 954:   tbb::parallel_sort(entries, [](const Entry &a, const Entry &b) {
 955:     return a.name < b.name;
 956:   });
 957: 
 958:   // Construct a trie
 959:   TrieNode node;
 960:   tbb::task_group tg;
 961:   construct_trie(node, entries, 0, &tg, entries.size() / 32, true);
 962:   tg.wait();
 963: 
 964:   if (node.prefix.empty())
 965:     root = std::move(node);
 966:   else
 967:     root.children.emplace_back(new TrieNode(std::move(node)));
 968: 
 969:   // Set output offsets to trie nodes. Since a serialized trie node
 970:   // contains output offsets of other nodes in the variable-length
 971:   // ULEB format, it unfortunately needs more than one iteration.
 972:   // We need to repeat until the total size of the serialized trie
 973:   // converges to obtain the optimized output. However, in reality,
 974:   // repeating this step twice is enough. Size reduction on third and
 975:   // further iterations is negligible.
 976:   set_offset(root, 0);
 977:   return set_offset(root, 0);
 978: }
 979: 
 980: static i64 common_prefix_len(std::string_view x, std::string_view y) {
 981:   i64 i = 0;
 982:   while (i < x.size() && i < y.size() && x[i] == y[i])
 983:     i++;
 984:   return i;
 985: }
 986: 
 987: void
 988: inline ExportEncoder::construct_trie(TrieNode &node, std::span<Entry> entries,
 989:                                      i64 len, tbb::task_group *tg,
 990:                                      i64 grain_size, bool divide) {
 991:   i64 new_len = common_prefix_len(entries[0].name, entries.back().name);
 992: 
 993:   if (new_len > len) {
 994:     node.prefix = entries[0].name.substr(len, new_len - len);
 995:     if (entries[0].name.size() == new_len) {
 996:       node.is_leaf = true;
 997:       node.flags = entries[0].flags;
 998:       node.addr = entries[0].addr;
 999:       entries = entries.subspan(1);
1000:     }
1001:   }
1002: 
1003:   for (i64 i = 0; i < entries.size();) {
1004:     auto it = std::partition_point(entries.begin() + i + 1, entries.end(),
1005:                                    [&](const Entry &ent) {
1006:       return entries[i].name[new_len] == ent.name[new_len];
1007:     });
1008:     i64 j = it - entries.begin();
1009: 
1010:     TrieNode *child = new TrieNode;
1011:     std::span<Entry> subspan = entries.subspan(i, j - i);
1012: 
1013:     if (divide && j - i < grain_size) {
1014:       tg->run([=, this] {
1015:         construct_trie(*child, subspan, new_len, tg, grain_size, false);
1016:       });
1017:     } else {
1018:       construct_trie(*child, subspan, new_len, tg, grain_size, divide);
1019:     }
1020: 
1021:     node.children.emplace_back(child);
1022:     i = j;
1023:   }
1024: }
1025: 
1026: inline i64 ExportEncoder::set_offset(TrieNode &node, i64 offset) {
1027:   node.offset = offset;
1028: 
1029:   i64 size = 0;
1030:   if (node.is_leaf) {
1031:     size = uleb_size(node.flags) + uleb_size(node.addr);
1032:     size += uleb_size(size);
1033:   } else {
1034:     size = 1;
1035:   }
1036: 
1037:   size++; // # of children
1038: 
1039:   for (std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> &child : node.children) {
1040:     // +1 for NUL byte
1041:     size += child->prefix.size() + 1 + uleb_size(child->offset);
1042:   }
1043: 
1044:   for (std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> &child : node.children)
1045:     size += set_offset(*child, offset + size);
1046:   return size;
1047: }
1048: 
1049: inline void ExportEncoder::write_trie(u8 *start, TrieNode &node) {
1050:   u8 *buf = start + node.offset;
1051: 
1052:   if (node.is_leaf) {
1053:     buf += write_uleb(buf, uleb_size(node.flags) + uleb_size(node.addr));
1054:     buf += write_uleb(buf, node.flags);
1055:     buf += write_uleb(buf, node.addr);
1056:   } else {
1057:     *buf++ = 0;
1058:   }
1059: 
1060:   *buf++ = node.children.size();
1061: 
1062:   for (std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> &child : node.children) {
1063:     buf += write_string(buf, child->prefix);
1064:     buf += write_uleb(buf, child->offset);
1065:   }
1066: 
1067:   for (std::unique_ptr<TrieNode> &child : node.children)
1068:     write_trie(start, *child);
1069: }
1070: 
1071: template <typename E>
1072: void ExportSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1073:   auto get_flags = [](Symbol<E> &sym) {
1074:     u32 flags = 0;
1075:     if (sym.is_weak)
1076:       flags |= EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_WEAK_DEFINITION;
1077:     if (sym.is_tlv)
1078:       flags |= EXPORT_SYMBOL_FLAGS_KIND_THREAD_LOCAL;
1079:     return flags;
1080:   };
1081: 
1082:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1083:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms)
1084:       if (sym && sym->file == file && sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL)
1085:         enc.entries.push_back({sym->name, get_flags(*sym),
1086:                                sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr});
1087: 
1088:   if (enc.entries.empty())
1089:     return;
1090: 
1091:   this->hdr.size = align_to(enc.finish(), 8);
1092: }
1093: 
1094: template <typename E>
1095: void ExportSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1096:   if (this->hdr.size == 0)
1097:     return;
1098: 
1099:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset;
1100:   memset(buf, 0, this->hdr.size);
1101:   enc.write_trie(buf, enc.root);
1102: }
1103: 
1104: // LC_FUNCTION_STARTS contains function start addresses encoded in
1105: // ULEB128. I don't know what tools consume this table, but we create
1106: // it anyway by default for the sake of compatibility.
1107: template <typename E>
1108: void FunctionStartsSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1109:   std::vector<std::vector<u64>> vec(ctx.objs.size());
1110: 
1111:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1112:     ObjectFile<E> &file = *ctx.objs[i];
1113:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file.syms)
1114:       if (sym && sym->file == &file && sym->subsec && sym->subsec->is_alive &&
1115:           &sym->subsec->isec->osec == ctx.text)
1116:         vec[i].push_back(sym->get_addr(ctx));
1117:   });
1118: 
1119:   std::vector<u64> addrs = flatten(vec);
1120:   tbb::parallel_sort(addrs.begin(), addrs.end());
1121:   remove_duplicates(addrs);
1122: 
1123:   // We need a NUL terminator at the end. We also want to make sure that
1124:   // the size is a multiple of 8 because the `strip` command assumes that
1125:   // there's no gap between __func_starts and the following __data_in_code.
1126:   contents.resize(align_to(addrs.size() * 5 + 1, 8));
1127: 
1128:   u8 *p = contents.data();
1129:   u64 last = ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr;
1130: 
1131:   for (u64 val : addrs) {
1132:     p += write_uleb(p, val - last);
1133:     last = val;
1134:   }
1135: 
1136:   // Write the terminator
1137:   p += write_uleb(p, 0);
1138: 
1139:   this->hdr.size = align_to(p - contents.data(), 8);
1140:   contents.resize(this->hdr.size);
1141: }
1142: 
1143: template <typename E>
1144: void FunctionStartsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1145:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
1146: }
1147: 
1148: // The symbol table in an output file is sorted by symbol type (local,
1149: // global or undef).
1150: template <typename E>
1151: void SymtabSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1152:   std::string cwd = std::filesystem::current_path().string();
1153: 
1154:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> vec;
1155:   append(vec, ctx.objs);
1156:   append(vec, ctx.dylibs);
1157: 
1158:   // Compute the number of symbols for each symbol type
1159:   tbb::parallel_for_each(vec, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
1160:     file->compute_symtab_size(ctx);
1161:   });
1162: 
1163:   // Compute the indices in the symbol table
1164:   InputFile<E> &first = *vec.front();
1165:   InputFile<E> &last = *vec.back();
1166: 
1167:   // Add -add_ast_path symbols first
1168:   first.stabs_offset = ctx.arg.add_ast_path.size();
1169:   first.strtab_offset = strtab_init_image.size();
1170:   for (std::string_view s : ctx.arg.add_ast_path)
1171:     first.strtab_offset += s.size() + 1;
1172: 
1173:   // Add input file symbols
1174:   for (i64 i = 1; i < vec.size(); i++)
1175:     vec[i]->stabs_offset = vec[i - 1]->stabs_offset + vec[i - 1]->num_stabs;
1176: 
1177:   first.locals_offset = last.stabs_offset + last.num_stabs;
1178: 
1179:   for (i64 i = 1; i < vec.size(); i++)
1180:     vec[i]->locals_offset = vec[i - 1]->locals_offset + vec[i - 1]->num_locals;
1181: 
1182:   globals_offset = last.locals_offset + last.num_locals;
1183:   first.globals_offset = globals_offset;
1184: 
1185:   for (i64 i = 1; i < vec.size(); i++)
1186:     vec[i]->globals_offset = vec[i - 1]->globals_offset + vec[i - 1]->num_globals;
1187: 
1188:   undefs_offset = last.globals_offset + last.num_globals;
1189:   first.undefs_offset = undefs_offset;
1190: 
1191:   for (i64 i = 1; i < vec.size(); i++)
1192:     vec[i]->undefs_offset = vec[i - 1]->undefs_offset + vec[i - 1]->num_undefs;
1193: 
1194:   for (i64 i = 1; i < vec.size(); i++)
1195:     vec[i]->strtab_offset = vec[i - 1]->strtab_offset + vec[i - 1]->strtab_size;
1196: 
1197:   i64 num_symbols = last.undefs_offset + last.num_undefs;
1198:   this->hdr.size = num_symbols * sizeof(MachSym<E>);
1199:   ctx.strtab.hdr.size = last.strtab_offset + last.strtab_size;
1200: 
1201:   // Update symbol's output_symtab_idx
1202:   tbb::parallel_for_each(vec, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
1203:     i64 locals = file->locals_offset;
1204:     i64 globals = file->globals_offset;
1205:     i64 undefs = file->undefs_offset;
1206: 
1207:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->syms) {
1208:       if (sym && sym->file == file && sym->output_symtab_idx == -2) {
1209:         if (sym->is_imported)
1210:           sym->output_symtab_idx = undefs++;
1211:         else if (sym->visibility == SCOPE_GLOBAL)
1212:           sym->output_symtab_idx = globals++;
1213:         else
1214:           sym->output_symtab_idx = locals++;
1215:       }
1216:     }
1217:   });
1218: }
1219: 
1220: template <typename E>
1221: void SymtabSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1222:   // Create symbols for -add_ast_path
1223:   MachSym<E> *buf = (MachSym<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
1224:   u8 *strtab = ctx.buf + ctx.strtab.hdr.offset;
1225:   i64 stroff = strtab_init_image.size();
1226: 
1227:   memcpy(strtab, strtab_init_image.data(), strtab_init_image.size());
1228: 
1229:   for (std::string_view path : ctx.arg.add_ast_path) {
1230:     MachSym<E> &msym = *buf++;
1231:     msym.stroff = stroff;
1232:     msym.n_type = N_AST;
1233:     stroff += write_string(strtab + stroff, path);
1234:   }
1235: 
1236:   // Copy symbols from input files to an output file
1237:   std::vector<InputFile<E> *> files;
1238:   append(files, ctx.objs);
1239:   append(files, ctx.dylibs);
1240: 
1241:   tbb::parallel_for_each(files, [&](InputFile<E> *file) {
1242:     file->populate_symtab(ctx);
1243:   });
1244: }
1245: 
1246: template <typename E>
1247: void IndirectSymtabSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1248:   ctx.got.hdr.reserved1 = 0;
1249:   i64 n = ctx.got.syms.size();
1250: 
1251:   ctx.thread_ptrs.hdr.reserved1 = n;
1252:   n += ctx.thread_ptrs.syms.size();
1253: 
1254:   ctx.stubs.hdr.reserved1 = n;
1255:   n += ctx.stubs.syms.size();
1256: 
1257:   if (ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr) {
1258:     ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.reserved1 = n;
1259:     n += ctx.stubs.syms.size();
1260:   }
1261: 
1262:   this->hdr.size = n * ENTRY_SIZE;
1263: }
1264: 
1265: template <typename E>
1266: void IndirectSymtabSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1267:   ul32 *buf = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
1268: 
1269:   auto get_idx = [&](Symbol<E> &sym) -> u32 {
1270:     if (sym.is_abs && sym.visibility != SCOPE_GLOBAL)
1271:       return INDIRECT_SYMBOL_ABS | INDIRECT_SYMBOL_LOCAL;
1272:     if (sym.is_abs)
1273:       return INDIRECT_SYMBOL_ABS;
1274:     if (sym.visibility != SCOPE_GLOBAL)
1275:       return INDIRECT_SYMBOL_LOCAL;
1276:     return sym.output_symtab_idx;
1277:   };
1278: 
1279:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got.syms)
1280:     *buf++ = get_idx(*sym);
1281: 
1282:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.thread_ptrs.syms)
1283:     *buf++ = get_idx(*sym);
1284: 
1285:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.stubs.syms)
1286:     *buf++ = get_idx(*sym);
1287: 
1288:   if (ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr)
1289:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.stubs.syms)
1290:       *buf++ = get_idx(*sym);
1291: }
1292: 
1293: // Create __DATA,__objc_imageinfo section contents by merging input
1294: // __objc_imageinfo sections.
1295: template <typename E>
1296: std::unique_ptr<ObjcImageInfoSection<E>>
1297: ObjcImageInfoSection<E>::create(Context<E> &ctx) {
1298:   ObjcImageInfo info = {};
1299: 
1300:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
1301:     if (!file->objc_image_info)
1302:       continue;
1303: 
1304:     // Make sure that all object files have the same Swift version.
1305:     ObjcImageInfo &info2 = *file->objc_image_info;
1306:     if (info.swift_version == 0)
1307:       info.swift_version = info2.swift_version;
1308: 
1309:     if (info.swift_version != info2.swift_version && info2.swift_version != 0)
1310:       Error(ctx) << *file << ": incompatible __objc_imageinfo swift version"
1311:                  << (u32)info.swift_version << " " << (u32)info2.swift_version;
1312: 
1313:     // swift_lang_version is set to the newest.
1314:     info.swift_lang_version =
1315:       std::max<u32>(info.swift_lang_version, info2.swift_lang_version);
1316:   }
1317: 
1318:   // This property is on if it is on in all input object files
1319:   auto has_category_class = [](ObjectFile<E> *file) -> bool {
1320:     if (ObjcImageInfo *info = file->objc_image_info)
1321:       return info->flags & OBJC_IMAGE_HAS_CATEGORY_CLASS_PROPERTIES;
1322:     return false;
1323:   };
1324: 
1325:   if (std::all_of(ctx.objs.begin(), ctx.objs.end(), has_category_class))
1326:     info.flags |= OBJC_IMAGE_HAS_CATEGORY_CLASS_PROPERTIES;
1327: 
1328:   return std::make_unique<ObjcImageInfoSection<E>>(ctx, info);
1329: }
1330: 
1331: template <typename E>
1332: void ObjcImageInfoSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1333:   memcpy(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, &contents, sizeof(contents));
1334: }
1335: 
1336: // Input __mod_init_func sections contain pointers to global initializer
1337: // functions. Since the addresses in the section are absolute, they need
1338: // base relocations, so the scheme is not efficient in PIC (position-
1339: // independent code).
1340: //
1341: // __init_offset is a new section to make it more efficient in PIC.
1342: // The section contains 32-bit offsets from the beginning of the image
1343: // to initializer functions. The section doesn't need base relocations.
1344: //
1345: // If `-init_offsets` is given, the linker converts input __mod_init_func
1346: // sections into an __init_offset section.
1347: template <typename E>
1348: void InitOffsetsSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1349:   this->hdr.size = 0;
1350:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1351:     this->hdr.size += file->init_functions.size() * 4;
1352: }
1353: 
1354: template <typename E>
1355: void InitOffsetsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1356:   ul32 *buf = (ul32 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
1357: 
1358:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs)
1359:     for (Symbol<E> *sym : file->init_functions)
1360:       *buf++ = sym->get_addr(ctx) - ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr;
1361: }
1362: 
1363: template <typename E>
1364: void CodeSignatureSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1365:   std::string filename = filepath(ctx.arg.final_output).filename().string();
1366:   i64 filename_size = align_to(filename.size() + 1, 16);
1367:   i64 num_blocks = align_to(this->hdr.offset, E::page_size) / E::page_size;
1368:   this->hdr.size = sizeof(CodeSignatureHeader) + sizeof(CodeSignatureBlobIndex) +
1369:                    sizeof(CodeSignatureDirectory) + filename_size +
1370:                    num_blocks * SHA256_SIZE;
1371: }
1372: 
1373: // A __code_signature section contains a digital signature for an output
1374: // file so that the system can identify who created it.
1375: //
1376: // On ARM macOS, __code_signature is mandatory even if we don't have a key
1377: // to sign. The signature we append in the following function is just
1378: // SHA256 hashes of each page. Such signature is called the "ad-hoc"
1379: // signature. Although mandating the ad-hoc signature doesn't make much
1380: // sense because anyone can compute it, we need to always create it
1381: // because otherwise the loader will simply rejects our output.
1382: //
1383: // On x86-64 macOS, __code_signature is optional. The loader doesn't reject
1384: // an executable with no signature section.
1385: template <typename E>
1386: void CodeSignatureSection<E>::write_signature(Context<E> &ctx) {
1387:   Timer t(ctx, "write_signature");
1388: 
1389:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset;
1390:   memset(buf, 0, this->hdr.size);
1391: 
1392:   std::string filename = filepath(ctx.arg.final_output).filename().string();
1393:   i64 filename_size = align_to(filename.size() + 1, 16);
1394:   i64 num_blocks = align_to(this->hdr.offset, E::page_size) / E::page_size;
1395: 
1396:   // Fill code-sign header fields
1397:   CodeSignatureHeader &sighdr = *(CodeSignatureHeader *)buf;
1398:   buf += sizeof(sighdr);
1399: 
1400:   sighdr.magic = CSMAGIC_EMBEDDED_SIGNATURE;
1401:   sighdr.length = this->hdr.size;
1402:   sighdr.count = 1;
1403: 
1404:   CodeSignatureBlobIndex &idx = *(CodeSignatureBlobIndex *)buf;
1405:   buf += sizeof(idx);
1406: 
1407:   idx.type = CSSLOT_CODEDIRECTORY;
1408:   idx.offset = sizeof(sighdr) + sizeof(idx);
1409: 
1410:   CodeSignatureDirectory &dir = *(CodeSignatureDirectory *)buf;
1411:   buf += sizeof(dir);
1412: 
1413:   dir.magic = CSMAGIC_CODEDIRECTORY;
1414:   dir.length = sizeof(dir) + filename_size + num_blocks * SHA256_SIZE;
1415:   dir.version = CS_SUPPORTSEXECSEG;
1416:   dir.flags = CS_ADHOC | CS_LINKER_SIGNED;
1417:   dir.hash_offset = sizeof(dir) + filename_size;
1418:   dir.ident_offset = sizeof(dir);
1419:   dir.n_code_slots = num_blocks;
1420:   dir.code_limit = this->hdr.offset;
1421:   dir.hash_size = SHA256_SIZE;
1422:   dir.hash_type = CS_HASHTYPE_SHA256;
1423:   dir.page_size = std::countr_zero(E::page_size);
1424:   dir.exec_seg_base = ctx.text_seg->cmd.fileoff;
1425:   dir.exec_seg_limit = ctx.text_seg->cmd.filesize;
1426:   if (ctx.output_type == MH_EXECUTE)
1427:     dir.exec_seg_flags = CS_EXECSEG_MAIN_BINARY;
1428: 
1429:   memcpy(buf, filename.data(), filename.size());
1430:   buf += filename_size;
1431: 
1432:   // Compute a hash value for each block.
1433:   auto compute_hash = [&](i64 i) {
1434:     u8 *start = ctx.buf + i * E::page_size;
1435:     u8 *end = ctx.buf + std::min<i64>((i + 1) * E::page_size, this->hdr.offset);
1436:     sha256_hash(start, end - start, buf + i * SHA256_SIZE);
1437:   };
1438: 
1439:   for (i64 i = 0; i < num_blocks; i += 1024) {
1440:     i64 j = std::min(num_blocks, i + 1024);
1441:     tbb::parallel_for(i, j, compute_hash);
1442: 
1443: #if __APPLE__
1444:     // Calling msync() with MS_ASYNC speeds up the following msync()
1445:     // with MS_INVALIDATE.
1446:     if (ctx.output_file->is_mmapped)
1447:       msync(ctx.buf + i * E::page_size, 1024 * E::page_size, MS_ASYNC);
1448: #endif
1449:   }
1450: 
1451:   // A LC_UUID load command may also contain a crypto hash of the
1452:   // entire file. We compute its value as a tree hash.
1453:   if (ctx.arg.uuid == UUID_HASH) {
1454:     u8 uuid[SHA256_SIZE];
1455:     sha256_hash(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, this->hdr.size, uuid);
1456: 
1457:     // Indicate that this is UUIDv4 as defined by RFC4122.
1458:     uuid[6] = (uuid[6] & 0b00001111) | 0b01010000;
1459:     uuid[8] = (uuid[8] & 0b00111111) | 0b10000000;
1460: 
1461:     memcpy(ctx.uuid, uuid, 16);
1462: 
1463:     // Rewrite the load commands to write the updated UUID and
1464:     // recompute code signatures for the updated blocks.
1465:     ctx.mach_hdr.copy_buf(ctx);
1466: 
1467:     for (i64 i = 0; i * E::page_size < ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.size; i++)
1468:       compute_hash(i);
1469:   }
1470: 
1471: #if __APPLE__
1472:   // If an executable's pages have been created via an mmap(), the output
1473:   // file will fail for the code signature verification because the macOS
1474:   // kernel wrongly assume that the pages may be mutable after the code
1475:   // verification, though it is actually impossible after munmap().
1476:   //
1477:   // In order to workaround the issue, we call msync() to invalidate all
1478:   // mmapped pages.
1479:   //
1480:   // https://openradar.appspot.com/FB8914231
1481:   if (ctx.output_file->is_mmapped) {
1482:     Timer t2(ctx, "msync", &t);
1483:     msync(ctx.buf, ctx.output_file->filesize, MS_INVALIDATE);
1484:   }
1485: #endif
1486: }
1487: 
1488: template <typename E>
1489: void DataInCodeSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1490:   assert(contents.empty());
1491: 
1492:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
1493:     LinkEditDataCommand *cmd =
1494:       (LinkEditDataCommand *)file->find_load_command(ctx, LC_DATA_IN_CODE);
1495:     if (!cmd)
1496:       continue;
1497: 
1498:     std::span<DataInCodeEntry> entries = {
1499:       (DataInCodeEntry *)(file->mf->data + cmd->dataoff),
1500:       cmd->datasize / sizeof(DataInCodeEntry),
1501:     };
1502: 
1503:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : file->subsections) {
1504:       if (entries.empty())
1505:         break;
1506: 
1507:       DataInCodeEntry &ent = entries[0];
1508:       if (subsec->input_addr + subsec->input_size < ent.offset)
1509:         continue;
1510: 
1511:       if (ent.offset < subsec->input_addr + subsec->input_size) {
1512:         u32 offset = subsec->get_addr(ctx) + subsec->input_addr - ent.offset -
1513:                      ctx.text_seg->cmd.vmaddr;
1514:         contents.push_back({offset, ent.length, ent.kind});
1515:       }
1516: 
1517:       entries = entries.subspan(1);
1518:     }
1519:   }
1520: 
1521:   this->hdr.size = contents.size() * sizeof(contents[0]);
1522: }
1523: 
1524: template <typename E>
1525: void DataInCodeSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1526:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
1527: }
1528: 
1529: // Collect all locations that needs fixing on page-in
1530: template <typename E>
1531: std::vector<Fixup<E>> get_fixups(Context<E> &ctx) {
1532:   std::vector<std::vector<Fixup<E>>> vec(ctx.objs.size());
1533: 
1534:   tbb::parallel_for((i64)0, (i64)ctx.objs.size(), [&](i64 i) {
1535:     for (Subsection<E> *subsec : ctx.objs[i]->subsections) {
1536:       if (!subsec->is_alive)
1537:         continue;
1538: 
1539:       for (Relocation<E> &r : subsec->get_rels()) {
1540:         if (r.type == E::abs_rel && r.sym() && r.sym()->is_imported)
1541:           vec[i].push_back({subsec->get_addr(ctx) + r.offset, r.sym(),
1542:                             (u64)r.addend});
1543:         else if (needs_rebasing(r))
1544:           vec[i].push_back({subsec->get_addr(ctx) + r.offset});
1545:       }
1546:     }
1547:   });
1548: 
1549:   std::vector<Fixup<E>> fixups = flatten(vec);
1550: 
1551:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.got.syms)
1552:     fixups.push_back({sym->get_got_addr(ctx), sym->is_imported ? sym : nullptr});
1553: 
1554:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : ctx.thread_ptrs.syms)
1555:     fixups.push_back({sym->get_tlv_addr(ctx), sym->is_imported ? sym : nullptr});
1556: 
1557:   tbb::parallel_sort(fixups, [](const Fixup<E> &a, const Fixup<E> &b) {
1558:     return a.addr < b.addr;
1559:   });
1560:   return fixups;
1561: }
1562: 
1563: // Returns fixups for a given segment
1564: template <typename E>
1565: static std::span<Fixup<E>>
1566: get_segment_fixups(std::vector<Fixup<E>> &fixups, OutputSegment<E> &seg) {
1567:   auto begin = std::partition_point(fixups.begin(), fixups.end(),
1568:                                     [&](const Fixup<E> &x) {
1569:     return x.addr < seg.cmd.vmaddr;
1570:   });
1571: 
1572:   if (begin == fixups.end())
1573:     return {};
1574: 
1575:   auto end = std::partition_point(begin, fixups.end(), [&](const Fixup<E> &x) {
1576:     return x.addr < seg.cmd.vmaddr + seg.cmd.vmsize;
1577:   });
1578: 
1579:   return {&*begin, (size_t)(end - begin)};
1580: }
1581: 
1582: template <typename E>
1583: bool operator<(const SymbolAddend<E> &a, const SymbolAddend<E> &b) {
1584:   if (a.sym != b.sym)
1585:     return *a.sym < *b.sym;
1586:   return a.addend < b.addend;
1587: }
1588: 
1589: // A chained fixup can contain an addend if its value is equal to or
1590: // smaller than 255.
1591: static constexpr i64 MAX_INLINE_ADDEND = 255;
1592: 
1593: template <typename E>
1594: static std::tuple<std::vector<SymbolAddend<E>>, u32>
1595: get_dynsyms(std::vector<Fixup<E>> &fixups) {
1596:   // Collect all dynamic relocations and sort them by addend
1597:   std::vector<SymbolAddend<E>> syms;
1598:   for (Fixup<E> &x : fixups)
1599:     if (x.sym)
1600:       syms.push_back({x.sym, x.addend <= MAX_INLINE_ADDEND ? 0 : x.addend});
1601: 
1602:   sort(syms);
1603:   remove_duplicates(syms);
1604: 
1605:   // Set symbol ordinal
1606:   for (i64 i = syms.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
1607:     syms[i].sym->fixup_ordinal = i;
1608: 
1609:   // Dynamic relocations can have an arbitrary large addend. For example,
1610:   // if you initialize a global variable pointer as `int *p = foo + (1<<31)`
1611:   // and `foo` is an imported symbol, it generates a dynamic relocation with
1612:   // an addend of 1<<31. Such large addend don't fit in a 64-bit in-place
1613:   // chained relocation, as its `addend` bitfield is only 8 bit wide.
1614:   //
1615:   // A dynamic relocation with large addend is represented as a pair of
1616:   // symbol name and an addend in the import table. We use an import table
1617:   // structure with an addend field only if it's necessary.
1618:   u64 max = 0;
1619:   for (SymbolAddend<E> &x : syms)
1620:     max = std::max(max, x.addend);
1621: 
1622:   u32 format;
1623:   if (max == 0)
1624:     format = DYLD_CHAINED_IMPORT;
1625:   else if ((u32)max == max)
1626:     format = DYLD_CHAINED_IMPORT_ADDEND;
1627:   else
1628:     format = DYLD_CHAINED_IMPORT_ADDEND64;
1629: 
1630:   return {std::move(syms), format};
1631: }
1632: 
1633: template <typename E>
1634: u8 *ChainedFixupsSection<E>::allocate(i64 size) {
1635:   i64 off = contents.size();
1636:   contents.resize(contents.size() + size);
1637:   return contents.data() + off;
1638: }
1639: 
1640: // macOS 13 or later supports a new mechanism to apply dynamic relocations.
1641: // In this comment, I'll explain what it is and how it works.
1642: //
1643: // In the traditional dynamic linking mechanism, data relocations are
1644: // applied eagerly on process startup; only function symbols are resolved
1645: // lazily through PLT/stubs. The new mechanism called the "page-in linking"
1646: // changes that; with the page-in linking, the kernel now applies data
1647: // relocations as it loads a new page from disk to memory. The page-in
1648: // linking has the following benefits compared to the ttraditional model:
1649: //
1650: //  1. Data relocations are no longer applied eagerly on startup, shotening
1651: //     the process startup time.
1652: //
1653: //  2. It reduces the number of dirty pages because until a process actually
1654: //     access a data page, no page is loaded to memory. Moreover, the kernel
1655: //     can now discard (instead of page out) an read-only page with
1656: //     relocations under memory pressure because it knows how to reconstruct
1657: //     the same page by applying the same dynamic relocations.
1658: //
1659: // `__chainfixups` section contains data needed for the page-in linking.
1660: // The section contains the first-level page table. Specifically, we have
1661: // one DyldChainedStartsInSegment data structure for each segment, and the
1662: // data structure contains a u16 array of the same length as the number of
1663: // pages in the segment. Each u16 value represents an in-page offset within
1664: // the corresponding page of the first location that needs fixing. With that
1665: // information, the kernel is able to know the first dynamically-linked
1666: // location in a page when it pages in a new page from disk.
1667: //
1668: // Unlike the traiditional dynamic linking model, there's no separate
1669: // relocation table. Relocation record is represented in a compact 64-bit
1670: // encoding and directly embedded to the place where the relocation is
1671: // applied to. In addition to that, each in-place relocation record contains
1672: // an offset to the next location in the same page that needs fixing. With
1673: // that, the kernel is able to follow that chain to apply all relocations
1674: // for a given page.
1675: //
1676: // This mechanism is so-called the "chained fixups", as the in-place
1677: // relocations form a linked list.
1678: template <typename E>
1679: void ChainedFixupsSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
1680:   fixups = get_fixups(ctx);
1681:   if (fixups.empty())
1682:     return;
1683: 
1684:   // Section header
1685:   i64 hdr_size = align_to(sizeof(DyldChainedFixupsHeader), 8);
1686:   auto *h = (DyldChainedFixupsHeader *)allocate(hdr_size);
1687:   h->fixups_version = 0;
1688:   h->starts_offset = contents.size();
1689: 
1690:   // Segment header
1691:   i64 seg_count = ctx.segments.back()->seg_idx + 1;
1692:   i64 starts_offset = contents.size();
1693:   i64 starts_size = align_to(sizeof(DyldChainedStartsInImage) +
1694:                              seg_count * 4, 8);
1695:   auto *starts = (DyldChainedStartsInImage *)allocate(starts_size);
1696:   starts->seg_count = seg_count;
1697: 
1698:   // Write the first-level page table for each segment
1699:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments) {
1700:     std::span<Fixup<E>> fx = get_segment_fixups(fixups, *seg);
1701:     if (fx.empty())
1702:       continue;
1703: 
1704:     starts = (DyldChainedStartsInImage *)(contents.data() + starts_offset);
1705:     starts->seg_info_offset[seg->seg_idx] = contents.size() - starts_offset;
1706: 
1707:     i64 npages =
1708:       align_to(fx.back().addr + 1 - seg->cmd.vmaddr, E::page_size) / E::page_size;
1709:     i64 size = align_to(sizeof(DyldChainedStartsInSegment) + npages * 2, 8);
1710: 
1711:     auto *rec = (DyldChainedStartsInSegment *)allocate(size);
1712:     rec->size = size;
1713:     rec->page_size = E::page_size;
1714:     rec->pointer_format = DYLD_CHAINED_PTR_64;
1715:     rec->segment_offset = seg->cmd.vmaddr - ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr;
1716:     rec->max_valid_pointer = 0;
1717:     rec->page_count = npages;
1718: 
1719:     for (i64 i = 0, j = 0; i < npages; i++) {
1720:       u64 addr = seg->cmd.vmaddr + i * E::page_size;
1721:       while (j < fixups.size() && fixups[j].addr < addr)
1722:         j++;
1723: 
1724:       if (j < fixups.size() && fixups[j].addr < addr + E::page_size)
1725:         rec->page_start[i] = fixups[j].addr & (E::page_size - 1);
1726:       else
1727:         rec->page_start[i] = DYLD_CHAINED_PTR_START_NONE;
1728:     }
1729:   }
1730: 
1731:   // Write symbol import table
1732:   u32 import_format;
1733:   std::tie(dynsyms, import_format) = get_dynsyms(fixups);
1734: 
1735:   h = (DyldChainedFixupsHeader *)contents.data();
1736:   h->imports_count = dynsyms.size();
1737:   h->imports_format = import_format;
1738:   h->imports_offset = contents.size();
1739: 
1740:   if (import_format == DYLD_CHAINED_IMPORT)
1741:     write_imports<DyldChainedImport>(ctx);
1742:   else if (import_format == DYLD_CHAINED_IMPORT_ADDEND)
1743:     write_imports<DyldChainedImportAddend>(ctx);
1744:   else
1745:     write_imports<DyldChainedImportAddend64>(ctx);
1746: 
1747:   // Write symbol names
1748:   h = (DyldChainedFixupsHeader *)contents.data();
1749:   h->symbols_offset = contents.size();
1750:   h->symbols_format = 0;
1751: 
1752:   for (i64 i = 0; i < dynsyms.size(); i++)
1753:     if (Symbol<E> *sym = dynsyms[i].sym;
1754:         i == 0 || dynsyms[i - 1].sym != sym)
1755:       write_string(allocate(sym->name.size() + 1), sym->name);
1756: 
1757:   contents.resize(align_to(contents.size(), 8));
1758:   this->hdr.size = contents.size();
1759: }
1760: 
1761: // This function is a part of ChainedFixupsSection<E>::compute_size(),
1762: // but it's factored out as a separate function so that it can take
1763: // a type parameter.
1764: template <typename E>
1765: template <typename T>
1766: void ChainedFixupsSection<E>::write_imports(Context<E> &ctx) {
1767:   T *imports = (T *)allocate(sizeof(T) * dynsyms.size());
1768:   i64 nameoff = 0;
1769: 
1770:   for (i64 i = 0; i < dynsyms.size(); i++) {
1771:     Symbol<E> &sym = *dynsyms[i].sym;
1772: 
1773:     if (ctx.arg.flat_namespace)
1774:       imports[i].lib_ordinal = BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_FLAT_LOOKUP;
1775:     else if (sym.file->is_dylib)
1776:       imports[i].lib_ordinal = ((DylibFile<E> *)sym.file)->dylib_idx;
1777:     else
1778:       imports[i].lib_ordinal = BIND_SPECIAL_DYLIB_WEAK_LOOKUP;
1779: 
1780:     imports[i].weak_import = sym.is_weak;
1781:     imports[i].name_offset = nameoff;
1782: 
1783:     if constexpr (requires (T x) { x.addend; })
1784:       imports[i].addend = dynsyms[i].addend;
1785: 
1786:     if (i + 1 == dynsyms.size() || dynsyms[i + 1].sym != &sym)
1787:       nameoff += sym.name.size() + 1;
1788:   }
1789: }
1790: 
1791: template <typename E>
1792: void ChainedFixupsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
1793:   memcpy(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents.data(), contents.size());
1794: }
1795: 
1796: template <typename E>
1797: static InputSection<E> &
1798: find_input_section(Context<E> &ctx, OutputSegment<E> &seg, u64 addr) {
1799:   auto it = std::partition_point(seg.chunks.begin(), seg.chunks.end(),
1800:                                  [&](Chunk<E> *chunk) {
1801:     return chunk->hdr.addr < addr;
1802:   });
1803: 
1804:   assert(it != seg.chunks.begin());
1805: 
1806:   OutputSection<E> *osec = it[-1]->to_osec();
1807:   assert(osec);
1808: 
1809:   auto it2 = std::partition_point(osec->members.begin(), osec->members.end(),
1810:                                   [&](Subsection<E> *subsec) {
1811:     return subsec->get_addr(ctx) < addr;
1812:   });
1813: 
1814:   assert(it2 != osec->members.begin());
1815:   return *it2[-1]->isec;
1816: }
1817: 
1818: // This function is called after copy_sections_to_output_file().
1819: template <typename E>
1820: void ChainedFixupsSection<E>::write_fixup_chains(Context<E> &ctx) {
1821:   Timer t(ctx, "write_fixup_chains");
1822: 
1823:   auto page = [](u64 addr) { return addr & ~((u64)E::page_size - 1); };
1824: 
1825:   auto get_ordinal = [&](i64 i, u64 addend) {
1826:     for (; i < dynsyms.size(); i++)
1827:       if (dynsyms[i].addend == addend)
1828:         return i;
1829:     unreachable();
1830:   };
1831: 
1832:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments) {
1833:     std::span<Fixup<E>> fx = get_segment_fixups(fixups, *seg);
1834: 
1835:     for (i64 i = 0; i < fx.size(); i++) {
1836:       constexpr u32 stride = 4;
1837: 
1838:       u32 next = 0;
1839:       if (i + 1 < fx.size() && page(fx[i + 1].addr) == page(fx[i].addr))
1840:         next = (fx[i + 1].addr - fx[i].addr) / stride;
1841: 
1842:       u8 *loc = ctx.buf + seg->cmd.fileoff + (fx[i].addr - seg->cmd.vmaddr);
1843: 
1844:       if (Symbol<E> *sym = fx[i].sym) {
1845:         if (fx[i].addr % stride)
1846:           Error(ctx) << find_input_section(ctx, *seg, fx[i].addr)
1847:                      << ": unaligned relocation against `" << *sym
1848:                      << "; re-link with -no_fixup_chains";
1849: 
1850:         DyldChainedPtr64Bind *rec = (DyldChainedPtr64Bind *)loc;
1851: 
1852:         if (fx[i].addend <= MAX_INLINE_ADDEND) {
1853:           rec->ordinal = sym->fixup_ordinal;
1854:           rec->addend = fx[i].addend;
1855:         } else {
1856:           rec->ordinal = get_ordinal(sym->fixup_ordinal, fx[i].addend);
1857:           rec->addend = 0;
1858:         }
1859: 
1860:         rec->reserved = 0;
1861:         rec->next = next;
1862:         rec->bind = 1;
1863:       } else {
1864:         if (fx[i].addr % stride)
1865:           Error(ctx) << find_input_section(ctx, *seg, fx[i].addr)
1866:                      << ": unaligned base relocation; "
1867:                      << "re-link with -no_fixup_chains";
1868: 
1869:         u64 val = *(ul64 *)loc;
1870:         if (val & 0x00ff'fff0'0000'0000)
1871:           Error(ctx) << seg->cmd.get_segname()
1872:                      << ": rebase addend too large; re-link with -no_fixup_chains";
1873: 
1874:         DyldChainedPtr64Rebase *rec = (DyldChainedPtr64Rebase *)loc;
1875:         rec->target = val;
1876:         rec->high8 = val >> 56;
1877:         rec->reserved = 0;
1878:         rec->next = next;
1879:         rec->bind = 0;
1880:       }
1881:     }
1882:   }
1883: }
1884: 
1885: template <typename E>
1886: void StubsSection<E>::add(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
1887:   assert(sym->stub_idx == -1);
1888:   sym->stub_idx = syms.size();
1889: 
1890:   syms.push_back(sym);
1891:   this->hdr.size = syms.size() * E::stub_size;
1892: 
1893:   if (ctx.stub_helper) {
1894:     if (ctx.stub_helper->hdr.size == 0)
1895:       ctx.stub_helper->hdr.size = E::stub_helper_hdr_size;
1896: 
1897:     ctx.stub_helper->hdr.size += E::stub_helper_size;
1898:     ctx.lazy_symbol_ptr->hdr.size += sizeof(Word<E>);
1899:   }
1900: }
1901: 
1902: template <typename E>
1903: std::vector<u8>
1904: encode_unwind_info(Context<E> &ctx, std::vector<Symbol<E> *> personalities,
1905:                    std::vector<std::vector<UnwindRecord<E> *>> &pages,
1906:                    i64 num_lsda) {
1907:   // Compute the size of the buffer.
1908:   i64 size = sizeof(UnwindSectionHeader) +
1909:              personalities.size() * 4 +
1910:              sizeof(UnwindFirstLevelPage) * (pages.size() + 1) +
1911:              sizeof(UnwindSecondLevelPage) * pages.size() +
1912:              sizeof(UnwindLsdaEntry) * num_lsda;
1913: 
1914:   for (std::span<UnwindRecord<E> *> span : pages)
1915:     size += (sizeof(UnwindPageEntry) + 4) * span.size();
1916: 
1917:   // Allocate an output buffer.
1918:   std::vector<u8> buf(size);
1919: 
1920:   // Write the section header.
1921:   UnwindSectionHeader &uhdr = *(UnwindSectionHeader *)buf.data();
1922:   uhdr.version = UNWIND_SECTION_VERSION;
1923:   uhdr.encoding_offset = sizeof(uhdr);
1924:   uhdr.encoding_count = 0;
1925:   uhdr.personality_offset = sizeof(uhdr);
1926:   uhdr.personality_count = personalities.size();
1927:   uhdr.page_offset = sizeof(uhdr) + personalities.size() * 4;
1928:   uhdr.page_count = pages.size() + 1;
1929: 
1930:   // Write the personalities
1931:   ul32 *per = (ul32 *)(buf.data() + sizeof(uhdr));
1932:   for (Symbol<E> *sym : personalities) {
1933:     if (sym->file)
1934:       *per++ = sym->get_got_addr(ctx);
1935:     else
1936:       Error(ctx) << "undefined symbol: " << *sym;
1937:   }
1938: 
1939:   // Write first level pages, LSDA and second level pages
1940:   UnwindFirstLevelPage *page1 = (UnwindFirstLevelPage *)per;
1941:   UnwindLsdaEntry *lsda = (UnwindLsdaEntry *)(page1 + (pages.size() + 1));
1942:   UnwindSecondLevelPage *page2 = (UnwindSecondLevelPage *)(lsda + num_lsda);
1943: 
1944:   for (std::span<UnwindRecord<E> *> span : pages) {
1945:     page1->func_addr = span[0]->get_func_addr(ctx);
1946:     page1->page_offset = (u8 *)page2 - buf.data();
1947:     page1->lsda_offset = (u8 *)lsda - buf.data();
1948: 
1949:     for (UnwindRecord<E> *rec : span) {
1950:       if (rec->lsda) {
1951:         lsda->func_addr = rec->get_func_addr(ctx) - ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr;
1952:         lsda->lsda_addr = rec->lsda->get_addr(ctx) + rec->lsda_offset -
1953:                           ctx.mach_hdr.hdr.addr;
1954:         lsda++;
1955:       }
1956:     }
1957: 
1958:     std::unordered_map<u32, u32> map;
1959:     for (UnwindRecord<E> *rec : span)
1960:       map.insert({rec->encoding, map.size()});
1961: 
1962:     page2->kind = UNWIND_SECOND_LEVEL_COMPRESSED;
1963:     page2->page_offset = sizeof(UnwindSecondLevelPage);
1964:     page2->page_count = span.size();
1965: 
1966:     UnwindPageEntry *entry = (UnwindPageEntry *)(page2 + 1);
1967:     for (UnwindRecord<E> *rec : span) {
1968:       entry->func_addr = rec->get_func_addr(ctx) - page1->func_addr;
1969:       entry->encoding = map[rec->encoding];
1970:       entry++;
1971:     }
1972: 
1973:     page2->encoding_offset = (u8 *)entry - (u8 *)page2;
1974:     page2->encoding_count = map.size();
1975: 
1976:     ul32 *encoding = (ul32 *)entry;
1977:     for (std::pair<u32, u32> kv : map)
1978:       encoding[kv.second] = kv.first;
1979: 
1980:     page1++;
1981:     page2 = (UnwindSecondLevelPage *)(encoding + map.size());
1982:   }
1983: 
1984:   // Write a terminator
1985:   UnwindRecord<E> &last = *pages.back().back();
1986:   page1->func_addr = last.subsec->get_addr(ctx) + last.subsec->input_size + 1;
1987:   page1->page_offset = 0;
1988:   page1->lsda_offset = (u8 *)lsda - buf.data();
1989: 
1990:   assert((u8 *)page2 <= buf.data() + buf.size());
1991:   buf.resize((u8 *)page2 - buf.data());
1992:   return buf;
1993: }
1994: 
1995: // If two unwind records covers adjascent functions and have identical
1996: // contents (i.e. have the same encoding, the same personality function
1997: // and don't have LSDA), we can merge the two.
1998: template <typename E>
1999: static std::span<UnwindRecord<E> *>
2000: merge_unwind_records(Context<E> &ctx, std::vector<UnwindRecord<E> *> &records) {
2001:   auto can_merge = [&](UnwindRecord<E> &a, UnwindRecord<E> &b) {
2002:     // As a special case, we don't merge unwind records with STACK_IND
2003:     // encoding even if their encodings look the same. It is because the
2004:     // real encoding for that record type is encoded in the instruction
2005:     // stream and therefore the real encodings might be different.
2006:     if constexpr (is_x86<E>)
2007:       if ((a.encoding & UNWIND_MODE_MASK) == UNWIND_X86_64_MODE_STACK_IND ||
2008:           (b.encoding & UNWIND_MODE_MASK) == UNWIND_X86_64_MODE_STACK_IND)
2009:         return false;
2010: 
2011:     return a.get_func_addr(ctx) + a.code_len == b.get_func_addr(ctx) &&
2012:            a.encoding == b.encoding &&
2013:            a.personality == b.personality &&
2014:            a.fde == b.fde &&
2015:            !a.lsda && !b.lsda;
2016:   };
2017: 
2018:   assert(!records.empty());
2019: 
2020:   i64 i = 0;
2021:   for (i64 j = 1; j < records.size(); j++) {
2022:     if (can_merge(*records[i], *records[j]))
2023:       records[i]->code_len += records[j]->code_len;
2024:     else
2025:       records[++i] = records[j];
2026:   }
2027:   return {&records[0], (size_t)(i + 1)};
2028: }
2029: 
2030: // __unwind_info stores unwind records in two-level tables. The first-level
2031: // table specifies the upper 12 bits of function addresses. The second-level
2032: // page entries are 32-bits long and specifies the lower 24 bits of fucntion
2033: // addresses along with indices to personality functions.
2034: //
2035: // This function splits a vector of unwind records into groups so that
2036: // records in the same group share the first-level page table.
2037: template <typename E>
2038: static std::vector<std::vector<UnwindRecord<E> *>>
2039: split_records(Context<E> &ctx, std::span<UnwindRecord<E> *> records) {
2040:   constexpr i64 max_group_size = 200;
2041:   std::vector<std::vector<UnwindRecord<E> *>> vec;
2042: 
2043:   while (!records.empty()) {
2044:     u64 end_addr = records[0]->get_func_addr(ctx) + (1 << 24);
2045:     i64 i = 1;
2046:     while (i < records.size() && i < max_group_size &&
2047:            records[i]->get_func_addr(ctx) < end_addr)
2048:       i++;
2049:     vec.push_back({records.begin(), records.begin() + i});
2050:     records = records.subspan(i);
2051:   }
2052:   return vec;
2053: }
2054: 
2055: template <typename E>
2056: void UnwindInfoSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
2057:   std::vector<UnwindRecord<E> *> records;
2058: 
2059:   for (std::unique_ptr<OutputSegment<E>> &seg : ctx.segments)
2060:     for (Chunk<E> *chunk : seg->chunks)
2061:       if (OutputSection<E> *osec = chunk->to_osec())
2062:         for (Subsection<E> *subsec : osec->members)
2063:           for (UnwindRecord<E> &rec : subsec->get_unwind_records())
2064:             records.push_back(&rec);
2065: 
2066:   if (records.empty())
2067:     return;
2068: 
2069:   auto encode_personality = [&](Symbol<E> *sym) -> u32 {
2070:     for (i64 i = 0; i < personalities.size(); i++)
2071:       if (personalities[i] == sym)
2072:         return (i + 1) << std::countr_zero((u32)UNWIND_PERSONALITY_MASK);
2073: 
2074:     if (personalities.size() == 3)
2075:       Fatal(ctx) << "too many personality functions";
2076: 
2077:     personalities.push_back(sym);
2078:     return personalities.size() << std::countr_zero((u32)UNWIND_PERSONALITY_MASK);
2079:   };
2080: 
2081:   for (UnwindRecord<E> *rec : records) {
2082:     if (rec->fde)
2083:       rec->encoding = E::unwind_mode_dwarf | rec->fde->output_offset;
2084:     else if (rec->personality)
2085:       rec->encoding |= encode_personality(rec->personality);
2086: 
2087:     if (rec->lsda)
2088:       num_lsda++;
2089:   }
2090: 
2091:   tbb::parallel_sort(records,
2092:                      [&](const UnwindRecord<E> *a, const UnwindRecord<E> *b) {
2093:     return a->get_func_addr(ctx) < b->get_func_addr(ctx);
2094:   });
2095: 
2096:   std::span<UnwindRecord<E> *> records2 = merge_unwind_records(ctx, records);
2097:   pages = split_records(ctx, records2);
2098: 
2099:   this->hdr.size = encode_unwind_info(ctx, personalities, pages, num_lsda).size();
2100: }
2101: 
2102: template <typename E>
2103: void UnwindInfoSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2104:   if (this->hdr.size == 0)
2105:     return;
2106: 
2107:   std::vector<u8> vec = encode_unwind_info(ctx, personalities, pages, num_lsda);
2108:   assert(this->hdr.size == vec.size());
2109:   write_vector(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, vec);
2110: }
2111: 
2112: template <typename E>
2113: void GotSection<E>::add(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
2114:   if (sym->got_idx != -1)
2115:     return;
2116: 
2117:   sym->got_idx = syms.size();
2118:   syms.push_back(sym);
2119:   this->hdr.size = syms.size() * sizeof(Word<E>);
2120: }
2121: 
2122: template <typename E>
2123: void GotSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2124:   Word<E> *buf = (Word<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
2125: 
2126:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++)
2127:     if (!syms[i]->is_imported)
2128:       buf[i] = syms[i]->get_addr(ctx);
2129: }
2130: 
2131: template <typename E>
2132: void LazySymbolPtrSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2133:   Word<E> *buf = (Word<E> *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
2134: 
2135:   for (i64 i = 0; i < ctx.stubs.syms.size(); i++)
2136:     *buf++ = ctx.stub_helper->hdr.addr + E::stub_helper_hdr_size +
2137:              E::stub_helper_size * i;
2138: }
2139: 
2140: template <typename E>
2141: void ThreadPtrsSection<E>::add(Context<E> &ctx, Symbol<E> *sym) {
2142:   assert(sym->tlv_idx == -1);
2143:   sym->tlv_idx = syms.size();
2144:   syms.push_back(sym);
2145:   this->hdr.size = syms.size() * sizeof(Word<E>);
2146: }
2147: 
2148: template <typename E>
2149: void ThreadPtrsSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2150:   ul64 *buf = (ul64 *)(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset);
2151:   memset(buf, 0, this->hdr.size);
2152: 
2153:   for (i64 i = 0; i < syms.size(); i++)
2154:     if (!syms[i]->is_imported)
2155:       buf[i] = syms[i]->get_addr(ctx);
2156: }
2157: 
2158: // Parse CIE augmented string
2159: template <typename E>
2160: void CieRecord<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
2161:   u8 *aug = (u8 *)get_contents().data() + 9;
2162:   if (*aug != 'z')
2163:     return;
2164: 
2165:   u8 *data = aug + strlen((char *)aug) + 1;
2166:   read_uleb(data); // code alignment
2167:   read_uleb(data); // data alignment
2168:   read_uleb(data); // return address register
2169:   read_uleb(data); // augmentation data length
2170: 
2171:   for (i64 i = 1; aug[i]; i++) {
2172:     switch (aug[i]) {
2173:     case 'L': {
2174:       if ((*data & 0xf) == DW_EH_PE_sdata4)
2175:         lsda_size = 4;
2176:       else if ((*data & 0xf) == DW_EH_PE_absptr)
2177:         lsda_size = 8;
2178:       else
2179:         Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": __eh_frame: unknown LSDA encoding: 0x"
2180:                    << std::hex << (u32)*data;
2181:       data++;
2182:       break;
2183:     }
2184:     case 'P':
2185:       if (*data != (DW_EH_PE_pcrel | DW_EH_PE_indirect | DW_EH_PE_sdata4))
2186:         Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": __eh_frame: unknown personality encoding: 0x"
2187:                    << std::hex << (u32)*data;
2188:       data += 5;
2189:       break;
2190:     case 'R':
2191:       if ((*data & 0xf) != DW_EH_PE_absptr || !(*data & DW_EH_PE_pcrel))
2192:         Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": __eh_frame: unknown pointer encoding: 0x"
2193:                    << std::hex << (u32)*data;
2194:       data++;
2195:       break;
2196:     default:
2197:       Fatal(ctx) << *file << ": __eh_frame: unknown augmented string: "
2198:                  << (char *)aug;
2199:     }
2200:   }
2201: }
2202: 
2203: template <typename E>
2204: std::string_view CieRecord<E>::get_contents() const {
2205:   const char *data = file->mf->get_contents().data() + file->eh_frame_sec->offset +
2206:                      input_addr - file->eh_frame_sec->addr;
2207:   return {data, (size_t)*(ul32 *)data + 4};
2208: }
2209: 
2210: template <typename E>
2211: void CieRecord<E>::copy_to(Context<E> &ctx) {
2212:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + ctx.eh_frame.hdr.offset;
2213: 
2214:   std::string_view data = get_contents();
2215:   memcpy(buf + output_offset, data.data(), data.size());
2216: 
2217:   if (personality) {
2218:     if (!personality->file) {
2219:       Error(ctx) << "undefined symbol: " << *file << ": " << *personality;
2220:       return;
2221:     }
2222: 
2223:     i64 offset = output_offset + personality_offset;
2224:     *(ul32 *)(buf + offset) =
2225:       personality->get_got_addr(ctx) - (ctx.eh_frame.hdr.addr + offset);
2226:   }
2227: }
2228: 
2229: template <typename E>
2230: void FdeRecord<E>::parse(Context<E> &ctx) {
2231:   ObjectFile<E> &file = subsec->isec->file;
2232: 
2233:   auto find_cie = [&](u32 addr) {
2234:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : file.cies)
2235:       if (cie->input_addr == addr)
2236:         return &*cie;
2237:     Fatal(ctx) << file << ": cannot find a CIE for a FDE at address 0x"
2238:                << std::hex << input_addr;
2239:   };
2240: 
2241:   // Initialize cie
2242:   std::string_view data = get_contents();
2243:   i64 cie_offset = *(ul32 *)(data.data() + 4);
2244:   cie = find_cie(input_addr + 4 - cie_offset);
2245: 
2246:   // Initialize lsda
2247:   if (cie->lsda_size) {
2248:     u8 *buf = (u8 *)get_contents().data();
2249:     u8 *aug = buf + 24;
2250:     read_uleb(aug); // skip Augmentation Data Length
2251: 
2252:     i64 offset = aug - buf;
2253:     u64 addr = *(ul32 *)aug + input_addr + offset;
2254: 
2255:     lsda = file.find_subsection(ctx, addr);
2256:     if (!lsda)
2257:       Fatal(ctx) << file << ": cannot find a LSDA for a FDE at address 0x"
2258:                  << std::hex << input_addr;
2259:   }
2260: }
2261: 
2262: template <typename E>
2263: std::string_view FdeRecord<E>::get_contents() const {
2264:   ObjectFile<E> &file = subsec->isec->file;
2265:   const char *data = file.mf->get_contents().data() + file.eh_frame_sec->offset +
2266:                      input_addr - file.eh_frame_sec->addr;
2267:   return {data, (size_t)*(ul32 *)data + 4};
2268: }
2269: 
2270: template <typename E>
2271: void FdeRecord<E>::copy_to(Context<E> &ctx) {
2272:   u8 *buf = ctx.buf + ctx.eh_frame.hdr.offset + output_offset;
2273: 
2274:   // Copy FDE contents
2275:   std::string_view data = get_contents();
2276:   memcpy(buf, data.data(), data.size());
2277: 
2278:   // Relocate CIE offset
2279:   *(ul32 *)(buf + 4) = output_offset + 4 - cie->output_offset;
2280: 
2281:   // Relocate function start address
2282:   u64 output_addr = ctx.eh_frame.hdr.addr + output_offset;
2283:   *(ul64 *)(buf + 8) = (i32)(subsec->get_addr(ctx) - output_addr - 8);
2284: 
2285:   if (lsda) {
2286:     u8 *aug = buf + 24;
2287:     read_uleb(aug); // skip Augmentation Data Length
2288: 
2289:     i64 offset = aug - buf;
2290:     u64 addr = *(ul32 *)aug + input_addr + offset;
2291:     i64 val = lsda->get_addr(ctx) - output_addr - offset +
2292:               addr - lsda->input_addr;
2293: 
2294:     if (cie->lsda_size == 4) {
2295:       *(ul32 *)aug = val;
2296:     } else {
2297:       assert(cie->lsda_size == 8);
2298:       *(ul64 *)aug = val;
2299:     }
2300:   }
2301: }
2302: 
2303: template <typename E>
2304: void EhFrameSection<E>::compute_size(Context<E> &ctx) {
2305:   // Remove dead CIEs and FDEs
2306:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2307:     std::erase_if(file->fdes, [](FdeRecord<E> &fde) {
2308:       return !fde.subsec->is_alive;
2309:     });
2310: 
2311:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes)
2312:       fde.cie->is_alive = true;
2313: 
2314:     std::erase_if(file->cies, [](std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie) {
2315:       return !cie->is_alive;
2316:     });
2317:   });
2318: 
2319:   i64 offset = 0;
2320: 
2321:   for (ObjectFile<E> *file : ctx.objs) {
2322:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : file->cies) {
2323:       cie->output_offset = offset;
2324:       offset += cie->size();
2325:     }
2326: 
2327:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes) {
2328:       fde.output_offset = offset;
2329:       offset += fde.size();
2330:     }
2331:   }
2332: 
2333:   this->hdr.size = offset;
2334: }
2335: 
2336: template <typename E>
2337: void EhFrameSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2338:   tbb::parallel_for_each(ctx.objs, [&](ObjectFile<E> *file) {
2339:     for (std::unique_ptr<CieRecord<E>> &cie : file->cies)
2340:       cie->copy_to(ctx);
2341: 
2342:     for (FdeRecord<E> &fde : file->fdes)
2343:       fde.copy_to(ctx);
2344:   });
2345: }
2346: 
2347: template <typename E>
2348: SectCreateSection<E>::SectCreateSection(Context<E> &ctx, std::string_view seg,
2349:                                         std::string_view sect,
2350:                                         std::string_view contents)
2351:   : Chunk<E>(ctx, seg, sect), contents(contents) {
2352:   this->hdr.size = contents.size();
2353:   ctx.chunk_pool.emplace_back(this);
2354: }
2355: 
2356: template <typename E>
2357: void SectCreateSection<E>::copy_buf(Context<E> &ctx) {
2358:   write_string(ctx.buf + this->hdr.offset, contents);
2359: }
